feed.title: AATF AI News Aggregator - Research (Full)
feed.subtitle: Research items from AI News Aggregator

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.title: Research Summary: July 10, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
AI safety and alignment dominates today's most significant work, spanning foundational theory to practical pre-deployment evaluation.

  • Assistance Games yields the first provably efficient learning algorithms for repeated human-AI assistance, linking cooperative game theory to alignment.
  • Predicting LLM Safety Before Release simulates deployment by regenerating responses over fixed de-identified conversation prefixes.
  • GRAM routes gradients into switchable auxiliary modules to isolate dangerous capabilities for dual-use access control.
  • Persuasion Attacks show adversarial arguments weaken chain-of-thought monitoring, a widely-relied-on oversight mechanism.

Evaluation and mathematics frontiers advance in parallel. Measuring Intelligence Beyond Human Scale (Braverman, Hazan) uses relative, model-generated challenges to counter benchmark saturation. A high-profile position paper reframes LLM-driven formal mathematics as open-ended research agents rather than solvers. DeepSWE contributes 113 contamination-resistant, long-horizon coding tasks across 91 repositories.

Efficiency and learning theory complete the set. Jet-Long enables tuning-free long-context extension via dynamic bifocal RoPE. Additional work reframes continual learning around adapting to world change and derives an exact information theory of generalization phase transitions in Bayesian diffusion models.
entry.content:
AI safety and alignment dominates today's most significant work, spanning foundational theory to practical pre-deployment evaluation.

  • Assistance Games yields the first provably efficient learning algorithms for repeated human-AI assistance, linking cooperative game theory to alignment.
  • Predicting LLM Safety Before Release simulates deployment by regenerating responses over fixed de-identified conversation prefixes.
  • GRAM routes gradients into switchable auxiliary modules to isolate dangerous capabilities for dual-use access control.
  • Persuasion Attacks show adversarial arguments weaken chain-of-thought monitoring, a widely-relied-on oversight mechanism.

Evaluation and mathematics frontiers advance in parallel. Measuring Intelligence Beyond Human Scale (Braverman, Hazan) uses relative, model-generated challenges to counter benchmark saturation. A high-profile position paper reframes LLM-driven formal mathematics as open-ended research agents rather than solvers. DeepSWE contributes 113 contamination-resistant, long-horizon coding tasks across 91 repositories.

Efficiency and learning theory complete the set. Jet-Long enables tuning-free long-context extension via dynamic bifocal RoPE. Additional work reframes continual learning around adapting to world change and derives an exact information theory of generalization phase transitions in Bayesian diffusion models.

entry.title: Provably Optimal Learning Algorithms for Assistance Games

author.name: Nivasini Ananthakrishnan, Mark Bedaywi, Michael I. Jordan, Stuart Russell, Nika Haghtalab

entry.summary: Provides the first provably efficient learning algorithms for repeated online assistance games between an informed human and an uninformed assistant, introducing assistance regret and decentralized algorithms achieving a (1-1/e)-approximation. It formalizes cooperative human-AI interaction where the assistant only observes human actions.

entry.title: From Solvers to Research: Large Language Model-Driven Formal Mathematics at the Research Frontier

author.name: Eric Jiang, Xiao Liang, Yikai Zhang, Yingjia Wan, Mengting Li, Haikang Deng, Alexander K. Taylor, Justin Baker, Rushil Raghavan, Junyi Zhang, Ying Nian Wu, Andrea L. Bertozzi, Kai-Wei Chang, Raghu Meka, Matthew Sottile, Nanyun Peng, Amit Sahai, Terence Tao, Wei Wang

entry.summary: A position paper arguing that LLM-driven formal mathematics must shift from predefined problem solvers toward research agents capable of open-ended theorem discovery and resolving conjectures with rigorous formal reasoning. The author list notably includes Terence Tao alongside strong ML and math researchers.

entry.title: Predicting LLM Safety Before Release by Simulating Deployment

author.name: Marcus Williams, Hannah Sheahan, Cameron Raymond, Tomek Korbak, Deng Pan, Peilin Yang, Leon Maksin, Ningyi Xie, Phillip Guo, Ian Kivlichan, Micah Carroll

entry.summary: This safety paper simulates deployment by holding fixed the prefixes of de-identified conversations from a prior model deployment and regenerating responses from a candidate model, enabling audits for novel misalignment and estimation of misbehavior prevalence before release. It offers a more representative pre-deployment safety evaluation than typical recognizable tests.

entry.title: Measuring Intelligence Beyond Human Scale

author.name: Jerry Han, Rafael Moschopoulos, Ella Colby, Vishrut Goyal, Andrew Tu, Kia Ghods, Mark Braverman, Elad Hazan

entry.summary: This paper proposes measuring intelligence beyond human capability via relative rather than absolute evaluation, where models generate public challenges that separate other systems into an adversarial psychometric rating. It describes protocols that reduce private-information attacks and support judge-free adjudication that scales with agent capabilities.

entry.title: Jet-Long: Efficient Long-Context Extension with Dynamic Bifocal RoPE

author.name: Haozhan Tang, Zerui Wang, Yuxian Gu, Song Han, Han Cai

entry.summary: Jet-Long is a tuning-free zero-shot context-extension method pairing a RoPE-faithful local window with a long-range window whose rescaling factor adapts to the current sequence length, avoiding the short-vs-long fidelity tradeoff of fixed rescaling. It targets open-weight checkpoints deployed beyond their pretraining window.

entry.title: When Does Continual Learning Require Learning

author.name: Anne Harrington, Nayan Saxena, Michael Murphy, Anastasia Borovykh, Zeyu Yun, Sridhar Kamath, Ara Eindra Kyi, Trevor Darrell, Jitendra Malik, Yutong Bai

entry.summary: Reframes continual learning for LLMs as increasing competence as the world changes, disentangling change into new domains (space) and drifting data (time) rather than only forgetting mitigation. It builds evaluations under realistic conditions where new domains arrive, facts drift past cutoff, and agentic state accumulates.

entry.title: An exact information theory of generalization phase transitions in Bayesian diffusion models

author.name: Henry Hunt, Mason Kamb, Surya Ganguli

entry.summary: Introduces analytically tractable Bayesian information-restricted diffusion (BIRD) models to explain how diffusion models avoid the curse of dimensionality and generalize rather than memorize. It generalizes prior local-information analytical models and characterizes generalization phase transitions with exact information theory.

entry.title: Modular Pretraining Enables Access Control

author.name: Ethan Roland, Murat Cubuktepe, Erick Martinez, Stijn Servaes, Keenan Pepper, Mike Vaiana, Diogo Schwerz de Lucena, Judd Rosenblatt, Addie Foote, Cem Anil, Alex Cloud

entry.summary: Proposes gradient-routed auxiliary modules (GRAM), a pretraining method that adds modules updated selectively to induce capability specialization so that ablating a module at inference removes that capability, approximating a separately trained model. It targets the dual-use dilemma by enabling access control without training and deploying multiple full models.

entry.title: When Does In-Context Search Help? A Sampling-Complexity Theory of Reflection-Driven Reasoning

author.name: Yotam Wolf, Noam Wies, Amnon Shashua

entry.summary: This paper gives a theoretical treatment of in-context search, modeling reflection-driven reasoning as approximate inference over reasoning traces and analyzing the sequential sampling complexity needed for high success. It shows that when self-reflection reliably localizes early mistakes, in-context search can deliver exponential gains over the base model.

entry.title: DeepSWE: Measuring Frontier Coding Agents on Original, Long-Horizon Engineering Tasks

author.name: Wenqi Huang, Charley Lee, Leonard Tng, Serena Ge

entry.summary: DeepSWE is a benchmark of 113 original long-horizon software-engineering tasks written from scratch across 91 active repositories and five languages, explicitly designed to avoid pretraining contamination and the biased test-only-for-one-fix grading of SWE-bench. It aims to measure genuine problem-solving by coding agents.

entry.title: Persuasion Attacks Can Decrease Effectiveness of CoT Monitoring

author.name: Jennifer Za, Julija Bainiaksina, Nikita Ostrovsky, Tanush Chopra, Victoria Krakovna

entry.summary: This paper stress-tests chain-of-thought monitoring as a safety mechanism, showing that adversarial agents can use persuasion-based arguments to convince a CoT monitor to approve policy-violating actions across a 40-task evaluation with thousands of interactions. It demonstrates that persuasion-jailbreak vulnerabilities extend to monitoring LLMs, weakening a promising oversight approach.

entry.title: A law of robustness for two-layer neural networks with arbitrary weights

author.name: Yitzchak Shmalo

entry.summary: Proves the Bubeck-Li-Nagaraj law of robustness for two-layer neural networks with arbitrary (unbounded) weights, up to a logarithmic factor, for every continuous piecewise-linear activation including ReLU. This closes a gap where prior universal results required polynomial parameter bounds.

entry.title: Linear Attention Architectures: Mechanisms, Trade-offs, and Cross-Layer Routing

author.name: Tommaso Cerruti, Tim Rieder, George Rowlands, Lingfeng Jin, Imanol Schlag

entry.summary: A comparative study of softmax attention and four recurrent linear-attention architectures (DeltaNet, Gated DeltaNet, Kimi Delta Attention, Gated DeltaNet-2) expressed in a common recurrent-memory notation. Experiments on 350M-parameter models cover expressivity, memory control, throughput, hybrid stacks, and cross-layer routing.

entry.title: Contravariance Theory: Strong Alignment for Minimal Solutions to Hard Tasks

author.name: Dan Yamins, Aran Nayebi

entry.summary: Contravariance Theory proves that for any two minimal deep network solutions to a sufficiently hard task, weak (affine) alignment of representations guarantees strong alignment of privileged axes, and that alignment propagates up the network hierarchy. It formalizes contravariance and has consequences for comparing artificial networks to the brain in NeuroAI.

entry.title: BiSCo-LLM: Lookup-Free Binary Spherical Coding for Extreme Low-Bit Large Language Model Compression

author.name: Yuantian Shao, Peisong Wang, Zhilei Liu, Chuangyi Li, Yuanteng Chen, Pengcheng Xie, Yiwu Yao, Zhihui Wei, Jian Cheng

entry.summary: BiSCo-LLM proposes a codebook-free binary spherical coding scheme for extreme low-bit (approaching 2 bits) LLM weight compression that avoids index lookup and extra codebook storage. It matters because memory and bandwidth constraints are a central deployment bottleneck for large models.

entry.title: Latent Memory Palace: Reasoning for Control as Autoregressive Variational Inference

author.name: Chuning Zhu, Eva Xu, Jose Barreiros, Krishnan Srinivasan, Paarth Shah, Abhishek Gupta

entry.summary: Latent Memory Palace formulates adaptive reasoning for continuous control as autoregressive variational inference over a latent memory-like space, letting control policies deliberate more like reasoning language models. It aims to transfer flexible deliberation to spatially precise motor tasks where language-space reasoning falls short.

entry.title: Modular Pretraining Enables Access Control

author.name: E.Roland

entry.summary: Introduces Gradient Routed Auxiliary Modules (GRAM), a modular pretraining method that isolates dangerous knowledge into switchable modules so a single model can approximate several models each missing a category of sensitive data, tested from 50M to 5B parameters. Done at AE Studio with Anthropic as preliminary, non-production safety research.

entry.title: Aurora 1.5: Extending open foundation models for weather and Earth-system applications

author.name: Kenji Takeda, Haiyu Dong, Jonathan Weyn, Amit Misra, Matt Corey, Kevin White, Shannon Monroe, Juan M. Lavista Ferres, Ashley Llorens, Bonnie Kruft

entry.summary: Microsoft's Aurora 1.5 extends its open Earth-system foundation model with 22 additional weather variables, hourly resolution, and probabilistic ensemble forecasting, released open-source with checkpoints on Hugging Face. It links research to operational Microsoft Weather services for energy, agriculture, and climate-risk applications.

entry.title: RL Post-Training Builds Compositional Reasoning Strategies

author.name: Azwar Abdulsalam, Nishil Patel, Andrew Saxe

entry.summary: This controlled study asks whether RL post-training merely amplifies latent skills or composes primitives into new higher-level strategies, using a fully observable rewrite-grammar environment where every rewrite can be audited. Trace analysis shows RL reorganizes primitive competence through a phased compositional mechanism, solving held-out problems that remain rare even under large sampling budgets.

entry.title: Modular Pretraining Enables Access Control

author.name: E.Roland

entry.summary: Alignment Forum cross-post of the GRAM work introducing gradient-routed auxiliary modules to isolate and toggle dangerous knowledge in language models, validated from 50M to 5B parameters. It is preliminary AE Studio and Anthropic safety research.

entry.title: Memory Scarcity, Open Models, and the Restructuring of the AI Industry, 2026-2030 -- A quantitative scenario analysis of inference economics, training-cost divergence, and infrastructure solvency

author.name: Satoshi Matsuoka

entry.summary: A quantitative scenario analysis of how a DRAM/HBM price surge, frontier-capable open-weight models such as the existing GLM-5.2, inference-efficiency gains, and compute resale reshape the AI industry through 2030. It models inference economics in dollars per petabyte of bandwidth and argues the entrant-incumbent cost gap persists via a depreciation conveyor.

entry.title: Co-LMLM: Continuous-Query Limited Memory Language Models

author.name: Yair Feldman, Linxi Zhao, Nathan Godey, Dongyoung Go, Yilun Hua, Kilian Q. Weinberger, Jennifer J. Sun, Yoav Artzi

entry.summary: Co-LMLM extends limited memory language models, which externalize factual knowledge to a knowledge base during pretraining, by pairing continuous vector keys with textual knowledge values instead of relational queries. It generates cheap vector queries while still retrieving human-readable, attributable knowledge into generation.

entry.title: AI 2040: Plan A

author.name: Daniel Kokotajlo

entry.summary: The AI Futures Project's new detailed scenario, AI 2040: Plan A, framed as a normative recommendation rather than prediction, in which decisive US and Chinese government action delays superintelligence from 2030 to 2040. It follows the group's earlier AI 2027 work with step-by-step worldbuilding.

entry.title: AI 2040: Plan A

author.name: Daniel Kokotajlo

entry.summary: Cross-post of the AI Futures Project's AI 2040: Plan A scenario on the Alignment Forum, presenting a normative roadmap in which coordinated government action delays superintelligence to 2040. It follows the detailed worldbuilding style of AI 2027.

entry.title: WAM-TTT: Steering World-Action Models by Watching Human Play at Test Time

author.name: Yusen Feng, Bingchen Han, Jiangran Lyu, Kai Liu, Yixin Zheng, Yuxuan Wan, Weiheng Liu, Sun Han, Ruiqin Li, Yulong Zhang, Fangfu Liu, Xuesong Shi, Libin Liu, Yizhou Wang, Zhizheng Zhang, He Wang

entry.summary: WAM-TTT is a test-time training framework that steers frozen world-action models toward new tasks by absorbing raw human videos into a lightweight adaptive memory via self-supervised video prediction. A meta-training stage aligns human demonstrations with robot behaviors without needing robot demonstrations at test time.

entry.title: Entropy Pacing Policy Optimization for Multi-Task Agentic Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Zetian Hu, Shunyu Liu, Junjie Zhang, Yongcheng Jing, Ting-En Lin, Yongbin Li, Dacheng Tao

entry.summary: This paper identifies an exploration-exploitation pace mismatch in multi-task agentic RL where easy tasks converge to low entropy that hinders harder tasks, causing inter-task entropy crossovers. It proposes Entropy Pacing Policy Optimization to balance exploration across tasks for generalist LLM agents.

entry.title: Single-Rollout Asynchronous Optimization for Agentic Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Zhenyu Hou, Yujiang Li, Jie Tang, Yuxiao Dong

entry.summary: SAO (Single-rollout Asynchronous Optimization) addresses stability and effectiveness gaps in asynchronous RL for long-horizon agentic LLM tasks, where group-wise sampling in GRPO fits poorly with asynchronous training. It targets both throughput and training stability from a strong Zhipu-affiliated author team.

entry.title: Principled Analysis of Deep Reinforcement Learning Evaluation and Design Paradigms

author.name: Ezgi Korkmaz

entry.summary: Analyzes canonical evaluation and design paradigms in deep reinforcement learning and introduces theoretical foundations of scaling laws for RL, showing asymptotic performance is not monotonically related to scale. It aims to sharpen how RL progress is measured and interpreted.

entry.title: Optimal Learning Rate Scaling Depends on Data in Deep Scalar Linear Networks

author.name: Yedi Zhang, Peter E. Latham, Leena Chennuru Vankadara, Andrew Saxe

entry.summary: Shows in exactly solvable deep scalar linear networks that optimal depth-wise learning-rate scaling depends on data, and that data-agnostic scaling rules fail to transfer across depths. Under the data-dependent scaling, dynamics become data-independent and weakly depth-dependent with a constant convergence rate across all depths.

entry.title: Super Weights in LLMs and the Failure of Selective Training

author.name: Shreyas Subramanian, Adewale Akinfaderin, Akarsha Sehwag

entry.summary: This paper challenges the Super Weights hypothesis, showing that pruning-critical single parameters do not universally dominate across LLMs and that training these coordinates in isolation collapses accuracy while training random positions helps. It is a careful negative result about parameter importance and selective training.

entry.title: Persona Cartography: Charting Language Model Personality Traits in Weight Space

author.name: Luke Baines, Anton Gonzalvez Hawthorne, Mariia Koroliuk, Irakli Shalibashvili, Cl\'ement Dumas, Konstantinos Voudouris, David Demitri Africa

entry.summary: Persona Cartography treats language-model personas as positions in an OCEAN personality-trait space and trains low-rank adapters to amplify or suppress individual traits, evaluated with a calibrated LLM judge and trait benchmarks across six models. It offers tools to decompose, measure, and control behavioral traits relevant to safety.

entry.title: When Implausible Tokens Get Reinforced: Tail-Aware Credit Calibration for LLM Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Xiuyi Lou, Zicheng Xu, Yu-Neng Chuang, Hoang Anh Duy Le, Zhaozhuo Xu, Guanchu Wang, Vladimir Braverman

entry.summary: TACO identifies Positive-Credit Contamination in critic-free LLM reinforcement learning, where low-probability but contextually wrong tail tokens receive the same positive advantage as correct ones, and proposes tail-aware credit calibration to suppress this. It refines credit assignment for reasoning-focused RL.

entry.title: A First-Principles Theory of Slow Thinking and Active Perception

author.name: Hongkang Yang, Zhi-Qin John Xu, Feiyu Xiong, Weinan E

entry.summary: This paper attempts a first-principles mathematical theory of slow thinking and active perception, proposing an active-lifting framework based on sampling latent sequences with an intrinsic drive to reduce uncertainty, and connecting it to the design, training, and inference of slow-thinking language models. It seeks to derive deliberate reasoning from probability lifting and projection.

entry.title: AgentLens: Production-Assessed Trajectory Reviews for Coding Agent Evaluation

author.name: Andrey Podivilov, Vadim Lomshakov, Sergey Savin, Matvei Startsev, Roman Pozharskiy, Maksim Parshin, Sergey Nikolenko

entry.summary: AgentLens is a production-assessed benchmark for interactive coding agents that evaluates the full run trajectory rather than a single pass/fail bit, pairing formal verification with LLM-written trajectory reviews and side-by-side comparisons. It aims to diagnose model behavior and compare successive versions rather than just rank models.

entry.title: Multiplication Beyond Groups: Stratified Fourier Mechanisms in Transformer Circuits

author.name: Zitong Andrew Chen, Junaid Hasan, Akhil Srinivasan, Hemkesh Bandi, Jarod Alper

entry.summary: This mechanistic interpretability study examines how small transformers learn modular integer multiplication over composite moduli, a non-invertible operation with zero-divisors. It proposes a monoid extension showing models partition inputs into local algebraic regions where group-like Fourier mechanisms survive.

entry.title: DiPhon: Diffusion on Graphons for Scalable Graph Generation

author.name: Sergio Rozada, Yiming Qin, Manuel Madeira, Pascal Frossard, Alejandro Ribeiro

entry.summary: DiPhon is a diffusion framework for size-scalable graph generation that operates in graphon space via a Jacobi stochastic differential equation and discretizes it to finite graphs. It targets the open problem of scaling graph diffusion models to large dense graphs.

entry.title: TimEE: End-to-end Time Series Classification via In-Context Learning

author.name: Jaris K\"uken, Shi Bin Hoo, Martin Mr\'az, Frank Hutter, Lennart Purucker

entry.summary: TimEE is a compact 4.5M-parameter foundation model for end-to-end time-series classification via in-context learning, outputting a class distribution in a single forward pass from a labeled support set and query without per-dataset training. It follows the prior-data fitted network paradigm.

entry.title: Uncertainty-gated selection for block-sparse attention

author.name: Thomas Rossi

entry.summary: Proposes a value-of-information router for block-sparse attention that measures how decisively the top-k block cutoff was made per query and doubles the kept set only where the margin is smallest. The method is backbone-agnostic, stacks on existing scorers like Quest, and reportedly lifts paired recall substantially on a LongBench-v2 subset.

entry.title: Efficient Safety Alignment of Language Models via Latent Personality Traits

author.name: Mohamed Amine Merzouk, Nolan Smyth, Damiano Fornasiere, Linh Le, David Williams-King, Adam Oberman

entry.summary: Introduces Latent Personality Alignment, which replaces explicit harm-refusal adversarial training with latent adversarial training on just 66 harm-agnostic psychometric personality statements. It hypothesizes personality-anchored representations share latent structure with harm avoidance, constraining the subspace exploited by jailbreaks.

entry.title: What to Keep, What to Forget: A Rate--Distortion View of Memory Compaction in LLMs and Agents

author.name: Ashwin Gerard Colaco, Nada Lahjouji

entry.summary: Argues that KV cache eviction/quantization, prompt pruning, bounded architectural state, and agent memory consolidation are all instances of a single rate-distortion decision about what context-derived information to retain at what fidelity under a resource budget. It offers a unifying lens across four separate memory-compaction communities.

entry.title: GradInf: Gradient Estimation as Probabilistic Inference

author.name: Gaurav Arya, Mathieu Huot, Moritz Schauer, Alexander K. Lew, Feras A. Saad

entry.summary: GradInf introduces gradient inference, reducing gradient estimation for probabilistic programs to a related probabilistic inference problem whose solution can be differentiated to yield sound, efficient estimators. It addresses long-standing difficulties with discrete choices and complex stochastic dependencies.

entry.title: When Structured Sparse Autoencoders Learn Consistent Concepts Across Modalities

author.name: Weiduo Liao, Yunqiao Yang, Ying Wei

entry.summary: Proposes a Structured Sparse Autoencoder that enforces semantic and spatial concept consistency across modalities in vision-language models, grouping image patches by attention similarity and proximity to fix fragmented visual concept coverage. It addresses a real weakness of vanilla SAEs in multimodal mechanistic interpretability.

entry.title: Dive Into the Implicit Biases of Low-rank Vision-language Alignment

author.name: Mingjia Shi, Shuo Wang, Xiaobo Wang, Sifan Zhou, Kai Wang, Tianyu Fu, Chenxu Zhao, Anyang Su, Ping Jiang, Minghui Wu

entry.summary: This paper challenges the assumption that vision-language alignment needs full-parameter updates, showing that low-rank adaptation of the LLM during alignment is cheaper and often better, shifting behavior from hallucinatory to conservative. It characterizes the implicit biases that low-rank alignment introduces.

entry.title: Optimiser Choice Can Amplify or Suppress Emergent Misalignment

author.name: Jason R Brown

entry.summary: An arXiv-linked study (Astra Fellowship) finding that optimizer choice strongly modulates emergent misalignment from narrow fine-tuning, while model size and family matter little. Optimizers that concentrate the LoRA update into fewer directions worsen alignment, and regularizing toward a flatter spectrum can mitigate it.

entry.title: TriRoute: Unified Learned Routing for Joint Adaptive Attention, Experts, and KV-Cache Allocation

author.name: Andrii Balashov, Olena Ponomarova

entry.summary: TriRoute proposes a single lightweight controller that jointly makes attention resolution, expert selection, and KV-cache bit-width decisions per token per layer, arguing these three conditional-computation axes are strongly coupled. It unifies Mixture-of-Experts, Mixture-of-Depths, and KV-cache quantization into one coordinated policy.

entry.title: LoCA: Spatially-Aware Low-Rank Convolutional Adaptation of Vision Foundation Models

author.name: Sojung An, Junha Lee, Sujeong You, Nam Ik Cho, Donghyun Kim

entry.summary: LoCA extends low-rank adaptation to convolutional vision foundation models by respecting the 4D spatial-channel structure of conv kernels instead of flattening them into 2D matrices as standard LoRA does. This convolution-aware parameter-efficient fine-tuning method aims to preserve spatial topology during adaptation.

entry.title: Explaining Near-Zero Hessian Eigenvalues Through Approximate Symmetries in Neural Networks

author.name: Marcel K\"uhn, Bernd Rosenow

entry.summary: Argues that the bulk of near-zero Hessian eigenvalues in neural networks arise from weakly lifted pseudo-Goldstone modes of continuous parametrization symmetries, constructing exact zero modes in deep linear networks and treating ReLU as a symmetry-breaking perturbation. It resolves the spectrum at the eigenvector level to explain the flat directions of the loss landscape.

entry.title: KronQ: LLM Quantization via Kronecker-Factored Hessian

author.name: Donghyun Lee, Yuhang Li, Ruokai Yin, Priyadarshini Panda

entry.summary: KronQ is a post-training quantization framework that adds gradient covariance to the objective via a Kronecker-factored Hessian approximation, using bidirectional incoherence processing to improve on activation-only methods like GPTQ. It targets compressing LLMs without retraining.

entry.title: Understanding Layer Patching in Model Size Interpolation

author.name: Sara Kangaslahti, Jonathan Geuter, Nihal V. Nayak, Marco Fumero, Francesco Locatello, David Alvarez-Melis

entry.summary: Provides the first systematic study of student-layer selection for zero-shot model size interpolation, building on boomerang distillation where student layers are patched with teacher blocks to create intermediate-size models. It casts optimal layer-subset selection as a shortest-path optimization problem.

entry.title: Learning $\mathsf{AC}^0$ under Locally Sampleable Graphical Models

author.name: Weiming Feng, Xiongxin Yang, Yixiao Yu, Yiyao Zhang

entry.summary: Gives a quasipolynomial-time algorithm for learning constant-depth (AC^0) circuits under locally sampleable graphical models, extending the classic low-degree algorithm beyond the uniform distribution and recent Gibbs-distribution results. It broadens learnability guarantees to correlated distributions.

entry.title: Eigenvalue Calibration for Semantic Embeddings of Large Language Models

author.name: Sebastian G. Gruber, Nassim Walha, Francis Bach, Florian Buettner

entry.summary: Proposes a framework for calibrating the eigenvalues of semantic embeddings used in LLM uncertainty quantification, interpreting LLMs plus embeddings as density matrix predictors and applying temperature scaling to eigenvalues. It establishes entropy-risk equivalence under calibration and derives central limit results.

entry.title: Dynamics of Gradient Descent with Large Step Size Near a Manifold of Flat Minima

author.name: Lachlan Ewen MacDonald, Ren\'e Vidal

entry.summary: Extends the theory of large-step gradient descent near flat minima from single-output overparametrized least squares to vector-valued outputs, providing a normal form and convergence results in the regime where step size exceeds twice the reciprocal of sharpness. It addresses the gap between classical stability conditions and actual deep learning training.

entry.title: Stop Guessing When to Stop Testing: Efficient Model Evaluation with Just Enough Data

author.name: Ofir Arviv, Kristjan Greenewald, Yotam Perlitz, Hadar Mulian, Michal Shmueli-Scheuer, Leshem Choshen

entry.summary: Advocates sequential (anytime-valid) testing for model evaluation to adaptively stop collecting data once statistical power is sufficient, avoiding the rigidity and inefficiency of fixed-size benchmarks. It provides a framework balancing efficiency and reliability across ranking, selection, and development-time testing.

entry.title: Multi-Modal, Multi-Environment Machine Teaching for Robust Reward Learning

author.name: Ali Larian, Qian Lin, Chang Zong Wu, Daniel S. Brown

entry.summary: This work studies optimal machine teaching for inverse reinforcement learning across multiple environments and heterogeneous feedback modalities, aiming for reward functions that generalize rather than overfit single settings. It targets robustness of learned objectives, a core alignment concern for deployed agents.

entry.title: A Practical Investigation of Training-free Relaxed Speculative Decoding

author.name: Guoxuan Xia, Luka Ribar, Paul Balanca

entry.summary: The authors empirically survey training-free relaxed speculative decoding, unifying existing lossy acceleration methods in a shared framework and benchmarking them on contemporary settings. The practitioner-oriented takeaways clarify when relaxation genuinely helps versus hurts capability.

entry.title: Dimensionality Reduction Meets Network Science: Sensemaking on UMAP's kNN Graph

author.name: Duen Horng Chau, Donghao Ren, Fred Hohman, Dominik Moritz

entry.summary: This work argues that UMAP's internal k-nearest-neighbor graph, rather than only its 2D embedding, is an underused resource, and shows that PageRank, k-core decomposition, and clustering coefficients extract meaningful structure from it. It reframes dimensionality reduction workflows through network-science tools.

entry.title: APIVOT: Adaptive Planning with Interleaved Vision-Language Thoughts

author.name: Emily Jin, Joy Hsu, Yiqing Xu, Weiyu Liu, Nick Haber, Jiajun Wu

entry.summary: APIVOT is a vision-language-model planner for long-horizon robot tasks that adaptively interleaves language thoughts for semantic reasoning with visual thoughts serving as imagined future states for internal geometric-feasibility verification. It outperforms general VLMs and prior planners on long-horizon kitchen tasks.

entry.title: MuScriptor: An Open Model for Multi-Instrument Music Transcription

author.name: Simon Rouard, Michael Krause, Axel Roebel, Carl-Johann Simon-Gabriel, Alexandre D\'efossez

entry.summary: MuScriptor is an open-weight multi-instrument music transcription model trained by combining synthetic pretraining with fine-tuning on real audio and reinforcement-learning post-training, plus conditioning on instrument presence for customizable transcriptions. It targets robust transcription of real, complex music mixes where prior models fail.

entry.title: ARDY: Autoregressive Diffusion with Hybrid Representation for Interactive Human Motion Generation

author.name: Kaifeng Zhao, Mathis Petrovich, Haotian Zhang, Tingwu Wang, Siyu Tang, Davis Rempe

entry.summary: ARDY is a streaming autoregressive diffusion framework with a hybrid representation for real-time, controllable 3D human motion generation from online text and kinematic constraints. It targets the gap between high-quality offline motion synthesis and interactive-speed generation for animation and humanoid robotics.

entry.title: DeepSearch-World: Self-Distillation for Deep Search Agents in a Verifiable Environment

author.name: Xinyu Geng, Xuanhua He, Sixiang Chen, Yanjing Xiao, Fan Zhang, Shijue Huang, Haitao Mi, Zhenwen Liang, Tianqing Fang, Yi R. Fung

entry.summary: Presents DeepSearch-Evolve, a self-distillation framework for web agents built on DeepSearch-World, a deterministic, verifiable environment with 420K multi-hop QA tasks supporting progress verification, grounded reflection and failure recovery. It aims to let tool-use agents improve from their own experience beyond fixed teacher trajectories.

entry.title: PLURAL: A Global Dataset for Value Alignment

author.name: Dhruv Agarwal, Anya Shukla, Tanya Goyal, Aditya Vashistha

entry.summary: Introduces PLURAL, a large value-focused preference dataset grounded in the Integrated Values Survey across 92 countries, releasing about 500K synthetic preference triplets for 20 countries. It aims to counter the Western-centric skew of current LLM alignment data.

entry.title: Hidden Decoding at Scale: Latent Computation Scaling for Large Language Models

author.name: Aiwei Liu, Cheng Shi, Chuhan Wu, Ci Lei, Di Lu, Donald He, Fan Zhang, Fanhao Kong, Feifei Zhang, Guan Wang, Haicheng Wang, Haoyu Liu, Houjin Yu, Jiachen Ding, Jiayi Feng, Jie Zhou, Jijun Chi, Jindi Shi, Jing Lei, Junjie Zhang, Laiyi Li, Le Tian, Linhao Zhang, Miao Fan, Sijun Zhang, Wei Jia, Weiwei Shi, Wenhan Li, Wentao Zhao, Wenteng Liang, Xiao Zhou, Xiaojin Zhou, Xihuai Wang, Xinyu Gao, Xuanliang Wang, Xuyang Ao, Yang Yu, Yangxiu You, Yinuo Zhao, Yufei Kuang, Yufei Wang, Yuan Liu, Yuan Liu, Yuwen Chen, Zhencong Tian, Zhongyin Zhao, Zilin Yu, Zitao Wang

entry.summary: Proposes Hidden Decoding, a sequence-length scaling method that adds per-token computation along the sequence dimension so an existing Transformer backbone keeps improving without costly re-pretraining, staying compatible with standard large-model pipeline parallelism. It offers an alternative to depth-recurrent looped transformers that are hard to scale.

entry.title: Mathematical methods of reinforcement learning

author.name: Denis Belomestny, Alexander Gasnikov, Egor Gladin, Alexey Naumov, Artemy Rubtsov, Yuri Sapronov, Daniil Tiapkin, Nikita Yudin

entry.summary: This survey organizes the mathematical foundations underpinning modern reinforcement learning, spanning Markov decision processes and Bellman operators, contraction and fixed-point theory for convergence guarantees, stochastic approximation and martingale methods, convex duality and regularization, and function approximation. It aims to consolidate the probability, optimization, and operator-theoretic structures behind RL algorithms.

entry.title: Validate the Dream Before You Trust Its Verdict: Admissibility for World-Model Simulators

author.name: Christian Oefinger, Finn Rasmus Sch\"afer, Korbinian Moller, Mattia Piccinini, Johannes Betz

entry.summary: The paper argues that world models used as test oracles for robot policies must themselves be certified, since fidelity metrics like FVD reward visual realism but ignore whether the simulated world responds correctly to actions. It proposes an admissibility notion to accredit world-model simulators before trusting their verdicts.

entry.title: Behavior Foundations for Quadruped Robots: ABot-C0 Technical Report

author.name: Xufeng Zhao, Fuzhi Yang, Jianhui Chen, Li Gao, Zhang Meng, Jie Gao, Yao Zheng, Congyang Zhao, Tianxiong Lv, Menglin Yang, Minqi Gu, Yaru Zhao, Wenyu Liu, Honglin Han, Shihui Su, Zixiao Tang, Liu Liu, Mu Xu, Yang Cai, Wenbin Tang

entry.summary: This report presents ABot-C0, a generalist motion-control foundation for quadruped robots comprising a scalable multi-source motion-data pipeline, robust policy learning across tracking, locomotion, and scene interaction, and a unified deployment stack. It adapts the humanoid motion-capture recipe to the data-scarce quadruped setting.

entry.title: RLVP: Penalize the Path, Reward the Outcome

author.name: Bojie Li, Noah Shi

entry.summary: RLVP argues that agents acting in the costly, irreversible real world must respect outcome-neutral path constraints (e.g., not repeatedly calling an unresponsive user, respecting business hours) that outcome-based rewards cannot express, and must learn from few examples. It proposes penalizing the path while rewarding the outcome to address blind spots in reward-from-verifiable-rewards training.

entry.title: Agon: Competitive Cross-Model RL with Implicit Rival Grading of Reasoning

author.name: Vladislav Beliaev

entry.summary: Agon makes two competing models each other's graders: both attempt a problem, and in alternating roles one drafts a solution while the other reads it and is rewarded for out-solving the rival, so reasoning traces are judged implicitly without process labels or a reward model. It targets the limitation that verifiable-reward RL grades only final answers.

entry.title: Architecture Generalization with MetaNCA

author.name: Meet Barot, Daniel Berenberg, Sina Khajehabdollahi

entry.summary: MetaNCA extends neural cellular automata to learn local update rules that self-organize the weights of artificial neural networks, drawing on biological self-organization and adaptability. It explores architecture generalization through emergent local interactions rather than global optimization.

entry.title: SLORR: Simple and Efficient In-Training Low-Rank Regularization

author.name: David Gonz\'alez-Mart\'inez, Shiwei Liu

entry.summary: SLORR is a stateless, architecture-preserving in-training low-rank regularizer using Hoyer-sparsity and nuclear-norm variants that avoid expensive SVDs and extra parameters. It improves neural network compressibility while remaining GPU-friendly and simple to adopt.

entry.title: Hallucination Self-Play: Bootstrapping Reinforced Detector via Evolved Generator

author.name: Shiping Yang, Shining Liang, Weihao Liu, Wenbiao Ding, Linjun Shou, Lu Cheng, Angel X. Chang

entry.summary: Hallucination Self-Play bootstraps a reinforced faithfulness-hallucination detector by co-evolving a detector and a generator initialized from the same base model, with the generator producing increasingly hard-to-detect hallucinations. It replaces static synthetic-data generation with adversarial iterative improvement.

entry.title: TTHE: Test-Time Harness Evolution

author.name: Jun Nie, Yonggang Zhang, Jun Song, Qianshu Cai, Dahai Yu, Yike Guo, Xinmei Tian, Bo Han

entry.summary: Test-Time Harness Evolution optimizes the agent harness (the program that builds context, calls tools, and recovers from failures) during evaluation itself using only unlabeled execution traces, rather than freezing a pre-deployment workflow. It targets adaptation when test-time failure modes differ from development.

entry.title: Bayesian Experimental Design via Score Matching

author.name: Angus Phillips, Gavin Kerrigan, Tom Rainforth

entry.summary: This paper decouples the double intractability of expected information gain in policy-based Bayesian experimental design by first solving a policy-independent score-matching problem, then using the learned score to train the design policy in a singly intractable manner. It enables more effort to be invested in optimizing adaptive design policies.

entry.title: Prediction-Powered Active Testing

author.name: Kianoosh Ashouritaklimi, Valentin Kilian, Daolang Huang, Tom Rainforth, Fran\c{c}ois Caron

entry.summary: Prediction-Powered Active Testing combines the unbiased LURE active-testing estimator with a prediction-powered control variate that residualizes the loss using black-box model predictions, preserving unbiasedness while reducing variance for label-efficient risk estimation. It exploits increasingly available powerful proxy predictions without treating them as biased pseudo-labels.

entry.title: Prompt Compression via Activation Aggregation

author.name: Thibaud Ardoin, Semira Einsele, Evis Bregu, Gerhard Wunder

entry.summary: This paper compresses an instruction prompt into a single activation vector via a learned weighted sum of intermediate-layer activations re-injected at an early layer, preserving task-relevant information with under two percent accuracy loss. It reduces per-query computation for fixed prompts without reprocessing tokens and offers insight into how information propagates through layers.

entry.title: SensorFM: Towards a general intelligence and interface for wearable health data

author.name: Unknown

entry.summary: A Google research post on SensorFM, a foundation model aiming to provide a general intelligence and interface over wearable health sensor data. It targets unified understanding and interaction across heterogeneous physiological signals.

entry.title: Reason Less, Verify More: Deterministic Gates Recover a Silent Policy-Violation Failure Mode in Tool-Using LLM Agents

author.name: Vikas Reddy, Sumanth Reddy Challaram, Abhishek Basu

entry.summary: This paper identifies a silent wrong-state failure mode where tool-using agents execute forbidden state transitions while appearing successful, finding that 78% of failures on a budget agent in the tau2-bench airline domain are such silent failures. It shows lightweight deterministic gates recover this failure mode by verifying rather than reasoning more.

entry.title: Recursive Self-Improvement in AI: From Bounded Self-Refinement to Autonomous Research Loops

author.name: Mingguang Chen, Licheng Wang, Bo Qu

entry.summary: This survey categorizes 1,250 arXiv papers from 2024 to 2026 on AI self-improvement along two axes: what the system improves (deployment behavior, policy, evaluator, or the research process) and the degree of loop closure. It separates bounded, convergent self-refinement from open-ended autonomous research loops.

entry.title: Institutional Red-Teaming: Deployment Rules, Not Just Models, Causally Shape Multi-Agent AI Safety

author.name: Yujiao Chen

entry.summary: Institutional red-teaming holds agents, objectives, and task state fixed and varies only one deployment rule to attribute changes in collective behavior, instantiated in IABench-CA across 228 contexts and seven model populations over ~34k games. It finds deployment rules causally shift mean fatality by 22 to 58 points and that no safe default exists.

entry.title: When Agents Remember Too Much: Memory Poisoning Attacks on Large Language Model Agents

author.name: George Torres, Sharad Shrestha, Satyajayant Misra

entry.summary: GhostWriter is a novel attack that poisons the long-term memory of personal LLM agents that sit at the intersection of conversational and action-planning domains, where they handle sensitive data while consuming untrusted sources. The work characterizes previously unaccounted security vulnerabilities in memory-augmented personal assistants.

entry.title: Tool-Making and Self-Evolving LLM Agents in Low-Latency Systems

author.name: Kalle Kujanp\"a\"a, Ning Liu, Shahnawaz Alam, Yeshwanth Reddy Sura, Tianyu Yang, Kristina Klinkner, Shervin Malmasi

entry.summary: This paper replaces repeated inference-time code generation in production LLM agents with an agentic tool-making pipeline that compiles recurring standard-operating-procedure steps into validated, versioned tools before deployment, falling back to code generation only when needed. It is deployed in a fulfillment-center alarm-triage system over a 44-node SOP.

entry.title: What LLM Forecasters Know but Don't Say: Probing Internal Representations for Calibration and Faithfulness

author.name: Rapha\"el Sarfati, Pratyush Ranjan Tiwari, Siddharth Boppana, Christopher J. Earls, Srikar Varadaraj, Eric Ho

entry.summary: Probes internal activations of a forecasting-tuned LLM (Eternis-Forecaster 8B) and finds representation-pooling probes yield substantially better calibration than the model's own outputs, replicating on GLM-4.7-Flash and GLM-4.5-Air. It also shows chain-of-thought is often unfaithful under evidence ablation, with probes acting as lie detectors.

entry.title: Towards Mechanistically Understanding Why Memorized Knowledge Fails to Generalize in Large Language Model Finetuning

author.name: Lu Dai, Ziyang Rao, Yili Wang, Hanqing Wang, Hao Liu, Hui Xiong

entry.summary: Formalizes the Knowing-Using Gap, where fine-tuned LLMs quickly memorize new facts but fail to use them in downstream reasoning, and studies it via a self-patching intervention that relocates internal representations. Results support a knowledge-circuit misalignment hypothesis explaining the memorization-generalization gap.

entry.title: GIRAF: Towards Generalizable Human Interactions with Articulated Objects

author.name: Xiaohan Zhang, Sebastian Starke, Alexander Winkler, Federica Bogo, Samir Aroudj, Yuting Ye

entry.summary: Introduces GIRAF, a method for synthesizing generalizable full-body human interactions with articulated objects, jointly reasoning about locomotion, fine-grained contact and object articulation and transferring hand-object correspondences across geometries. It is authored by a strong Meta Reality Labs team.

entry.title: Native Video-Action Pretraining for Generalizable Robot Control

author.name: Qihang Zhang, Lin Li, Luyao Zhang, Shuai Yang, Yiming Luo, Shuaiting Li, Ruilin Wang, Junke Wang, Jiahao Shao, Gangwei Xu, Jiaming Zhou, Yishu Shen, Yudong Jin, Fangyi Xu, Shuailei Ma, Jiaqi Liao, Guanxing Lu, Zifan Shi, Yongkun Wen, Yujie Zhao, Weixuan Tang, Xinyang Wang, Chaojian Li, Jiapeng Zhu, Ka Leong Cheng, Nan Xue, Xing Zhu, Yujun Shen, Yinghao Xu

entry.summary: LingBot-VA 2.0 is a video-action foundation model built specifically for embodiment, introducing a semantic visual-action tokenizer that aligns visual representations with semantics and actions and adopting causal temporal modeling, rather than repurposing content-creation video generators. It targets generalizable robot control.

entry.title: How robust are natural language autoencoders to initialization?

author.name: michaelzhang

entry.summary: A MATS summer-2026 interpretability study testing how sensitive natural language autoencoders are to their Claude-generated initialization guesses, finding that implausibly initialized NLAs can still reconstruct activations well while emitting 99.3% implausible statements. Even plausibly initialized NLAs drift toward lower plausibility during training, casting doubt on NLA usefulness.

entry.title: The Harness Effect: How Orchestration Design Sets the Token Economics of Enterprise Agentic AI

author.name: Muayad Sayed Ali, Aliaksandra Novik, Anji Boddupally, Artem Yavorskyi, Chris Nickerson, Daniel Rica, Emily DuGranrut, Felix Leung, Garrett Prince, Grace Barnett, Heath Robinson, Hosain Al Ahmad, Jesse Resnick, Juan Carlos Farah, Jyothi Swaroop Meruga, Leonid Kuznetsov, Luke Gorham, Marie Schmoll, Michael Paciullo, Saumya Das, Sharath Sheripally, Tommy Griscom, Mykyta Osadchyi, Neha Mantri, Nick Westrum, Olivia Benowitz, Parikshith Kulkarni, Radik Chernyshov, Rakshith Vasudev, Rohith Nadimpally, Vikas Gangadevi, Waseem AlShikh

entry.summary: This enterprise study isolates the orchestration harness as the key lever for agentic token economics, using 22 locked tasks and six foundation models while swapping only the orchestration layer. It argues that harness design, not raw token spend, controls the cost-value tradeoff in production agents.

entry.title: Operational Reframing and Approval-Framed Delegation in Multi-Agent LLM Safety

author.name: Lifei Liu, Haoran Yu, Xiaochong Jiang, Su Wang, Pin Qian, Yihang Chen

entry.summary: The paper decomposes the aggregate safety difference between direct prompts and planner-executor pipelines into three mechanisms: harmful-intent operational reframing, planner refusal/transformation, and executor action under approval-implying delegation. A five-condition controlled design over synthetic harmful scenarios shows pipeline safety is not a stable property.

entry.title: Search, Fail, Recover: A Training Framework for Correction-Aware Reasoning

author.name: Dmitry Beresnev, Vladimir Makharev, Roman Khalikov, Ivan Oseledets, Petr Anokhin

entry.summary: Pyligent is a training and inference framework that represents reasoning as validated search over partial solution chains, converting labeled search trees into supervised targets for continue, finish, and backtrack actions. It is evaluated on a hidden directed graph task designed to isolate delayed-failure recovery.

entry.title: AnchorPrune: Relevance-Anchored Contextual Expansion for Visual Token Pruning

author.name: Kyuan Oh, Bumsoo Kim

entry.summary: AnchorPrune is a training-free visual token pruning method for large vision-language models that first builds a protected relevance anchor and then expands it with complementary context, avoiding overconcentration or loss of essential tokens. It reduces inference cost from high-resolution inputs.

entry.title: Intrinsic Green's Learning: Supervised Learning on Manifolds via Inverse PDE

author.name: Alexandre Quemy

entry.summary: Intrinsic Green's Learning models a target function on a manifold as the solution to a linear PDE whose learned source term is integrated against a Green's kernel, using a learned low-dimensional chart to collapse high-dimensional integrals into linear-cost 1D ones. A two-stage algorithm separates coordinate discovery from a near-convex source fit.

entry.title: GeoProp: Grounding Robot State in Vision for Generalist Manipulation

author.name: Guoyang Zhao, Quanhao Qian, Gongjie Zhang, Wenhao Li, Jiuniu Wang, Xiaowei Lu, Deli Zhao, Ran Xu

entry.summary: GeoProp is a lightweight plug-and-play adapter that grounds robot proprioception in vision by projecting robot state onto the image plane to sample localized features and inject spatial priors. It addresses the failure of naive proprioception fusion in manipulation policies.

entry.title: Quantum simulation of real-world nonlinear dynamics via Koopman method

author.name: Baoyang Zhang, Dong An, Zhaoyuan Meng, Yefei Yu, Xiaoxiao Xiao, Zhen Lu, Yue Yang

entry.summary: The quantum Koopman method embeds nonlinear dynamics into a learned linear representation and implements the resulting non-unitary evolution on shallow quantum circuits via parallel spectral channels. It is demonstrated on a superconducting processor across three nonlinear systems.

entry.title: HIVE: Understanding Post-Hallucination Reasoning in Vision Language Models

author.name: Feng He, Zhenting Wang, Qifan Wang, Qiang Guan, Dongfang Liu, Ruixiang Tang, Qiankun Li

entry.summary: HIVE studies post-hallucination reasoning in vision-language models, examining how hallucinated semantics that enter the inference context influence downstream predictions, via an evaluation engine enabling controlled comparisons of faithful versus hallucinated captions. It spans nine tasks and nine models.

entry.title: Selective Timestep Weighting and Advantage-Based Replay for Sample-Efficient Diffusion RLHF

author.name: Eric Zhu, Abhinav Shrivastava, Soumik Mukhopadhyay

entry.summary: This paper improves feedback efficiency of diffusion RLHF via selective timestep weighting and advantage-based replay, exploiting the observation that reward information is unevenly distributed across denoising timesteps and trajectories. It reduces required human or reward-model evaluations while preserving generalization.

entry.title: Breaking Database Lock-in: Agentic Regeneration of High Performance Storage Readers for Database Bypass

author.name: Victor Giannakouris, Immanuel Trummer

entry.summary: Jailbreak bypasses database engines for analytics by reading storage files directly and using LLMs to regenerate operator-specific table-reading code from format specifications and source, avoiding driver layers not designed for bulk columnar reads. It aims to break database lock-in for high-performance analytics.

entry.title: PGD-NO: A Neural Operator with Precomputed Geometry Decomposition for 3D Million-scale Physics Simulations

author.name: Weiheng Zhong, Jing Bi, Victor Oancea, Hadi Meidani

entry.summary: PGD-NO is a neural operator that precomputes geometry decomposition into geometry tokens to decouple geometric encoding from solution querying, enabling linear memory scaling for million-scale 3D physics simulations. It overcomes the single-node VRAM bottleneck limiting mesh resolution in neural PDE solvers.

entry.title: Reinforcing the Generation Order of Multimodal Masked Diffusion Models

author.name: Yidong Ouyang, Zhe Wang, Sourav Bhabesh, Dmitriy Bespalov

entry.summary: Investigates optimizing token generation order in multimodal masked diffusion models for text-to-image synthesis and multimodal understanding, showing model logits alone are insufficient to determine optimal order unlike structured language tasks. It introduces a learnable control module trained via group relative policy optimization.

entry.title: Vanilla SGD with Momentum Survives Heavy-Tailed Noise: Convergence Analysis without Gradient Clipping or Normalization

author.name: Ryusei Yamada, Naoki Sato, Hideaki Iiduka

entry.summary: Provides the first comprehensive convergence analysis of vanilla SGD with momentum under heavy-tailed noise for strongly convex, convex, and nonconvex objectives, without gradient clipping or normalization. It shows the rates are inferior to clipping-based optimal rates but that plain momentum still converges.

entry.title: Certified Interventional Fidelity: Anytime-Valid, Adaptive Evaluation of Causal Claims in Mechanistic Interpretability

author.name: Amir Asiaee

entry.summary: Introduces Certified Interventional Fidelity, a statistical layer for interventional interpretability that writes reported quantities (patching effects, ablation scores) as causal estimands and provides anytime-valid, adaptive confidence guarantees. It addresses whether reported fidelity is a stable causal claim or an artifact of finite sampling and adaptive evaluation choices.

entry.title: MatBind: A Shared Embedding Space for Multimodal Materials Characterization

author.name: Le Yang (Institute for Advanced Simulations), Anoop K. Chandran (J\"ulich Supercomputing Centre, Forschungszentrum J\"ulich), Jona \"Ostreicher (Institute of Nanotechnology, Karlsruhe Institute of Technology), Evgenii Sovetkin (J\"ulich Supercomputing Centre, Forschungszentrum J\"ulich), Adrian Mirza (Helmholtz-Zentrum Berlin f\"ur Materialien und Energie, Helmholtz Institute for Polymers in Energy Applications Jena), Sebastien Bompas (Institute for Advanced Simulations), Bashir Kazimi (Institute for Advanced Simulations), Pascal Friederich (Institute of Nanotechnology, Karlsruhe Institute of Technology), Stefan Kesselheim (J\"ulich Supercomputing Centre, Forschungszentrum J\"ulich, 1. Physikalisches Institut, University of Cologne), Kevin Maik Jablonka (Helmholtz Institute for Polymers in Energy Applications Jena, Center for Energy and Environmental Chemistry Jena, Friedrich Schiller University Jena), Stefan Sandfeld (Institute for Advanced Simulations, Faculty 5 - Georesources and Materials Engineering, RWTH Aachen University)

entry.summary: MatBind is a contrastive learning framework that aligns four materials characterization modalities (crystal structure, simulated powder X-ray diffraction, density of states, and text) into a unified embedding space using crystal structure as the physical anchor. It enables cross-modal querying of materials that are otherwise analyzed in isolation.

entry.title: Distributionally Faithful Imputation via Positive Semi-Definite Kernel Density Estimation

author.name: Andrea Basteri, Carlo Ciliberto, Alessandro Rudi

entry.summary: This paper recasts missing-data imputation under MCAR as density estimation from masked observations using positive semi-definite kernel densities, yielding a convex problem with closed-form marginals and consistency guarantees. PSD-Impute supports both single and multiple imputations from one fitted density.

entry.title: Shift & Drift: A Zero-Shot Benchmark for Generalizable and Robust Autonomous Driving Motion Planning

author.name: Alessandro Canevaro, Hang Yu, Julian Schmidt, Peizheng Li, Silvan Lindner, Wilhelm Stork, Georg Martius, Julian Jordan

entry.summary: Shift & Drift is a dual-track zero-shot benchmark that stress-tests autonomous-driving motion planners against semantic topology shift (converting aerial DeepScenario data into nuPlan scenarios) and execution-perturbation recovery. It exposes weak out-of-distribution generalization of planners strong on in-distribution data.

entry.title: CTA-Pipelining: A Latency-Oriented Spatial Scaling Method for Multi-GPU Systems

author.name: Tingkai Liu, Muralidhar Andoorveedu, Sanjoy Das, Sanjay Patel, Volodymyr Kindratenko

entry.summary: CTA-Pipelining is a latency-oriented spatial scaling execution paradigm that exploits Cooperative Thread Array-level dependencies to concurrently execute across coherent shared-memory multi-GPU systems, going beyond tensor parallelism. It targets latency-bound LLM serving on tightly integrated GPU interconnects.

entry.title: Functional and Secure Code Generation with Task Vectors

author.name: Felix Wang, Anudeep Das, Mei Nagappan, N. Asokan

entry.summary: This paper applies task-vector arithmetic (linear operations on LLM weights) to jointly improve functional correctness and security of generated code, treating secure coding as an alignment objective rather than post-hoc vulnerability finding. It seeks weight-space control over code quality with low overhead.

entry.title: Expressivity and Statistical Trade-offs in Diffusion Policy Learning

author.name: Viet Vu, Renyuan Xu, Jiacheng Zhang, Yufei Zhang

entry.summary: This paper analyzes why diffusion-based RL policies are so expressive, identifying the drift Lipschitz budget as the key quantity governing their expressivity and statistical trade-offs when learned from finite data. It provides theory to explain and exploit the flexibility of diffusion policies.

entry.title: Who Broke the System? Failure Localization in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems

author.name: Yufei Xia, Anjun Gao, Yueyang Quan, Zhuqing Liu, Minghong Fang

entry.summary: AgentLocate addresses failure localization in LLM-based multi-agent systems, attributing failures to both a responsible agent and the earliest decisive step using LLM judging plus multi-perspective verification. It targets diagnosis of system-level breakdowns in long-horizon, tightly coupled agent interactions.

entry.title: High-Dimensional Procrustes Matching via Tree Counts

author.name: Xiaochun Niu, Tselil Schramm, Jiaming Xu

entry.summary: This paper gives a polynomial-time algorithm for the high-dimensional Procrustes matching problem, recovering an unknown permutation aligning two rotation-and-permutation-related Gaussian point sets at constant correlation, whereas prior work required nearly perfect correlation. The algorithm compares weighted counts of specially structured trees.

entry.title: It Takes a MAESTRO To Prune Bad Experts

author.name: Palaash Goel, Ayush Maheshwari, Tanmoy Chakraborty

entry.summary: Introduces MAESTRO, a structured pruning framework for Mixture-of-Experts models that models autoregressive expert activation trajectories as ergodic Markov chains whose stationary distributions capture cross-token routing interdependence. It addresses the deployment bottleneck of keeping full expert banks in memory.

entry.title: UltraX: Refining Pre-Training Data at Scale with Adaptive Programmatic Editing

author.name: Xinlong Zhao, Dongsheng Liu, Hengyu Zhao, Zixuan Fu, Zheng Wang, Jie Cai, Jie Zhou, Qiang Ma, Xuanhe Zhou, Xu Han, Yudong Wang, Zhiyuan Liu

entry.summary: Proposes UltraX, a function-calling refinement framework that programmatically edits large-scale pretraining data adaptively, aiming to overcome the limits of fixed rule-based heuristics and the inefficiency of LLM-based refinement. It targets higher-quality data utilization as scaling-law gains from raw expansion diminish.

entry.title: DeltaV: Thinking with Visual State Updates in Unified Large Multimodal Models

author.name: Pengjie Wang, Linger Deng, Zujia Zhang, Shaojie Zhang, Zhenbo Luo, Pei Fu, Jian Luan, Xiang Bai, Yuliang Liu

entry.summary: DeltaV is a unified large multimodal model that replaces full-image generation of intermediate visual states with compact incremental update tokens capturing only visual changes across reasoning steps, cutting token redundancy. It also adapts the token budget to the magnitude of visual change.

entry.title: FabriVLA: A Lightweight Vision-Language-Action Model for Precise Multi-Task Manipulation

author.name: Shiyuan Yang, Borong Zhang, Jizheng Zhang, Zhijia Tao, Junfei Guo, Donglai Ran, Xu Bian, Qingbiao Li

entry.summary: Switch-Reasoner is a GRPO-based reinforcement learning framework that teaches multimodal LLMs when to think versus answer directly by treating explicit reasoning as a virtual tool, with dual-level regulation to stabilize the always-think vs always-direct instability. It targets efficient adaptive reasoning in heterogeneous multitask settings.

entry.title: Where Do LLM Values Come From?

author.name: lilysun004

entry.summary: MATS-program research examining how LLM values shift across post-training stages (SFT, DPO, RL), evaluated on Olmo-3, and finding some changes may be unintended such as reduced safety after DPO. It attempts to predict value changes from dataset activations and text embeddings while controlling for refusal confounds.

entry.title: Cost-Effective Agent Harnesses for Abstract Reasoning and Generalization on ARC-AGI-1

author.name: Kabir Moghe, Peter Chin

entry.summary: This work studies a third regime for ARC-AGI-1 progress: an open-weight non-thinking model (DeepSeek V3.2) under a strict budget with no ARC-specific fine-tuning, relying on architectural harness design. It introduces an Explorer-Definer pipeline that separates pattern discovery from executable program synthesis.

entry.title: Grounding Spatial Relations in a Compact World Model: Instruction Leakage and a Goal-Free Dynamics Fix

author.name: Yufeng Wang, Lu Wei, Haibin Ling

entry.summary: The paper exposes a confound in compact goal-conditioned world models: a predictor reaching 0.90 relation-readout accuracy is actually transcribing the instruction rather than perceiving the scene, collapsing to chance when the goal is withheld and following false instructions when they are injected. It proposes a goal-free dynamics fix to force genuine grounding.

entry.title: LiST: Lipschitz Scaling Training for Robust and Calibrated Neural Networks

author.name: Arthur Chiron (IRIT, EPE UT), Franck Mamalet (IRIT, DTIPG - SNCF, UT3), Thomas Massena (IRIT, DTIPG - SNCF, UT3), Thomas Deltort (IRIT), Mathieu Serrurier (IRIT, UT2J)

entry.summary: LiST links the enforced Lipschitz constraint in robust networks to temperature scaling calibration, identifying a nontrivial constraint value that yields out-of-the-box calibrated networks. It aims to jointly achieve accuracy, robustness, and calibration, which are usually studied separately.

entry.title: Selective Left-Shift: Turning Test-Time Compute and Difficulty-based Curation into Training Data for Low-Resource Code Generation

author.name: Didula Samaraweera, Anjana Supun, Srinath Perera

entry.summary: Presents a three-phase pipeline for low-resource programming languages like Julia that shifts inference-time compute to offline data synthesis using compiler and test feedback, decoupling syntax acquisition from algorithmic reasoning. It aims to resolve the trilemma of data scarcity, expensive inference scaling, and weak reinforcement learning signals.

entry.title: Unlocking Temporal Generalization in Hamiltonian Video Dynamics Models

author.name: Eli Laird, Corey Clark

entry.summary: Analyzes why Hamiltonian Generative Networks lose temporal generalization in forced, dissipative settings and proposes fixes to enable variable-timescale rollouts from a continuous-time energy function. This matters for hierarchical planning, sim-to-real transfer, and multi-timescale scientific and game-engine applications.

entry.title: NFTR: From Provable Mode-Averaging to Geodesic Subgoal Selection in Offline Goal-Conditioned RL

author.name: Erdemt Bao, Xing Lei, Jun Chen

entry.summary: NFTR replaces the Gaussian subgoal policy in Hierarchical Implicit Q-Learning with a conditional normalizing flow to fix optimistic bias and mode collapse in offline goal-conditioned RL, adding a triangle-slack reweighting for geodesic subgoal selection. A closed-form mode-averaging result identifies normalizing flows as the minimal generative class for the setting.

entry.title: Structure Learning on Clustered Data

author.name: Ryan Thompson, Matt P. Wand, Veerabhadran Baladandayuthapani

entry.summary: Extends DAG structure learning to clustered data by adapting the fixed- and random-effects mixed-model framework, estimating a global causal structure while accounting for cluster-specific effects. A differentiable graph coupling mechanism guarantees the union of fixed- and random-effects graphs remains acyclic.

entry.title: Resample or Reroute? Budget-Aware Test-Time Model Selection for Large Language Models

author.name: Teng-Ruei Chen

entry.summary: This paper formalizes budget-aware test-time model selection for LLMs, treating resampling a committed model versus rerouting to another model as competing uses of a single per-query cost budget. It bridges routing theory and practical cost-constrained serving where oracle labels are unavailable.

entry.title: Beware What You Autocomplete: Forensic Attribution of Backdoored Code Completions

author.name: Anjun Gao, Yueyang Quan, Zhuqing Liu, Minghong Fang

entry.summary: CodeTracer is a forensic framework that traces malicious autocomplete outputs from backdoored code-completion models back to the specific fine-tuning data responsible, operating with only the fine-tuning corpus and the reported miscompletion under post-deployment constraints. It extends supply-chain security to code LLMs.

entry.title: CausalDS: Benchmarking Causal Reasoning in Data-Science Agents

author.name: Andrej Leban, Yuekai Sun

entry.summary: CausalDS is a benchmark for evaluating causal reasoning in agentic data-science workflows, generating novel synthetic causal structures rather than templatized variants and combining principled data-generating processes with realistic data-analysis tasks. It bridges symbolic causal-reasoning and data-analysis benchmark traditions.

entry.title: When Debiasing Backfires: Counterintuitive Side Effects of Preprocessing-Based Stereotype Mitigation

author.name: Yahan Zheng, John Guerrerio, Soroush Vosoughi, Weicheng Ma

entry.summary: Shows that preprocessing-based stereotype mitigation (corpus debiasing, group-mention removal, reference swapping) often produces unintended side effects, increasing stereotyping for other demographics including unrelated categories. It demonstrates these effects across encoder- and decoder-only models and finds standard benchmarks miss them.

entry.title: Two Axes of LLM Abstention: Answer Correctness and Question Answerability

author.name: Benedikt J. Wagner

entry.summary: Argues that answer correctness and question answerability are separate axes of LLM abstention that single-confidence thresholds cannot distinguish. Across five instruction-tuned models, ordinary confidence tracks correctness but is nearly blind to answerability, while a hidden-state linear probe does the reverse, and the blind spot does not shrink with scale.

entry.title: Understanding and Mitigating the Video-Action Generalization Gap via Temporal Ratio

author.name: Utkarsh A. Mishra, Yongxin Chen, Danfei Xu, Yang Liu, Xi Chen, Jiayuan Mao

entry.summary: Investigates the video-action generalization gap where world-action and video-action models lose compositional video priors after finetuning on robot action data, and introduces the Temporal Ratio, an attention-based measure of how strongly the action head relies on future latent rollouts versus the current frame. It uses this to explain and mitigate the loss of generalization.

entry.title: Metrics or Mirage? An Audit of Evaluation Inconsistencies in Colonoscopy Polyp Segmentation Benchmarks

author.name: Aisha Urooj, Zain Ul Abdien, Neelu Madan

entry.summary: This audit of 27 colonoscopy polyp segmentation papers finds pervasive evaluation inconsistencies, including near-universal omission of the clinically relevant Hausdorff distance and multiple incompatible train/test splits on the same datasets. It argues that reported progress is difficult to verify.

entry.title: Debate with Self-Play Best-of-N Optimization

author.name: Dewi Gould

entry.summary: First research output from Arcadia Alignment's scalable oversight team studying debate as a training reward signal rather than just an evaluation protocol, showing that inference-time best-of-N optimization can de-risk debate protocols before committing to RL. It aims to build an open empirical science of debate training.

entry.title: Do Counterfactually Fair Image Classifiers Satisfy Group Fairness? -- A Theoretical and Empirical Study

author.name: Sangwon Jung, Sumin Yu, Sanghyuk Chun, Taesup Moon

entry.summary: This paper studies the unclear relationship between counterfactual fairness and group fairness in image classification, constructing new datasets with high-quality image editing and human labeling to enable counterfactual evaluation. It provides both theoretical and empirical analysis linking the two fairness notions.

entry.title: Security and Privacy in Agentic AI: Grand Challenges and Future Directions

author.name: Adam Jenkins, Agnieszka Kitkowska, Caterina Maidhof, Diego Paracuellos, Francesco Sovrano, Gonzalo Gabriel Mendez, Guillermo Suarez-Tangil, Hana Kopecka, Isabel Wagner, Isabel Barbera, Javier Carnerero-Cano, Jide Edu, Jose Luis Martin-Navarro, Jose Such, Josep Domingo-Ferrer, Juan Carlos Carrillo, Kopo Marvin Ramokapane, Mark Cote, Pablo Vellosillo, Ramon Ruiz-Dolz, Rongjun Ma, Ruba Abu-Salma, Sameer Patil, William Seymour, Xiao Zhan

entry.summary: This horizon-scanning paper synthesizes security and privacy challenges for agentic AI from a workshop of thirty international experts across academia, industry, and government. It lays out grand challenges and future research directions as AI systems gain more agency.

entry.title: D2PO: Optimizing Diffusion Samplers via Dynamic Preference

author.name: Jinkyu Kim, Jinyoung Choi, Bohyung Han

entry.summary: D2PO reformulates diffusion sampler optimization over timestep schedules and classifier-free guidance weights as a preference-based alignment problem using Direct Preference Optimization. It targets the failure of student-teacher regression to preserve high-frequency texture fidelity in low-step samplers.

entry.title: Gradient-Based Speech-to-Text Alignment for Any ASR Model: From CTC to Speech LLMs

author.name: Albert Zeyer, Ralf Schl\"uter, Hermann Ney

entry.summary: This paper studies a generic gradient-based speech-to-text alignment applicable to any differentiable ASR model, taking the gradient of each teacher-forced token log probability with respect to the input and reducing it to per-frame saliency. It works across CTC, transducer, attention encoder-decoder, and speech LLM models rather than being tied to attention weights.

entry.title: MADB: A Large-Scale Music Aesthetics Dataset with Professional and Multi-Dimensional Annotations

author.name: Sirui Zhang, Tianle Wang, Xinyi Tong, Peiyang Yu, Jishang Chen, Liangke Zhao, Haoxin Zhang, Duo Xu, Xin Jin, Feng Yu, Songchun Zhu

entry.summary: MADB is a large-scale music aesthetics dataset with roughly 10k tracks rated by trained annotators across ten perceptual dimensions plus textual comments. It exposes sizable gaps between pretrained model predictions and human aesthetic judgments.

entry.title: Riemannian Geometry for Pre-trained Language Model Embeddings

author.name: Szczepan Konior, Alexandre Quemy, Przemys{\l}aw Klocek, Gr\'egoire Cattan, Bart{\l}omiej Sobieski

entry.summary: The paper probes whether sentence classification signal resides in the Riemannian geometry of contextual token embeddings, introducing Riemannian Mean Pooling that aggregates per-token pullback metrics on the SPD manifold. RMP beats Euclidean pooling on structured tasks while correctly staying at chance on an artifact-free benchmark.

entry.title: Making Implicit Preservation Intent Explicit in Conversational Image Editing

author.name: Soomin Han, Jihyung Ahn, Bumsoo Kim, Buru Chang

entry.summary: OCCUR-Bench is a diagnostic benchmark for temporal preservation in conversational image editing, focusing on recovering occluded-but-unchanged regions across turns, paired with ReSpec, a training-free restoration framework. It targets faithful restoration rather than plausible regeneration.

entry.title: FedCVESA: Taking Away Training Data in Federated Learning via Correlation Value Encoding and Segmented Aggregation

author.name: Chongkai Li, Bang Zhang, Wenjian Luo

entry.summary: FedCVESA studies a white-box data-exfiltration attack in federated learning where a malicious server encodes and later recovers target clients' private training data via correlation-value encoding and segmented aggregation, overcoming the overwriting effect of multi-client averaging. It extends memorization-based extraction threats to FL.

entry.title: On Adversarial Vulnerability of Vision-Language Models through the Lens of Intermediate Spectral Subspaces

author.name: Chethan Krishnamurthy Ramanaik, Tobias Callies, Michael Hecht, Eirini Ntoutsi

entry.summary: This work studies adversarial vulnerability of vision-language models through the spectral structure of intermediate linear transformations, proposing a white-box spectral-subspace-guided attack that aligns intermediate representations with dominant subspaces. It introduces a previously unexplored mechanism of adversarial fragility.

entry.title: Beyond Attack-Success Rate: Action-Graded Severity Scale for Tool-Using AI Agents

author.name: Harry Owiredu-Ashley

entry.summary: This work argues binary attack-success rates for tool-using AI agents discard crucial harm information, and introduces a seven-level ordinal action-graded severity scale scoring trajectories by reversibility, scope-crossing, and privilege expansion. It computes the scale via a deterministic oracle and a panel of frontier LLM judges.

entry.title: Reward-Adaptive Iterative Discovery: A Case Study on Automated Game Testing for NHL26

author.name: Florian Fuchs, Jessy Gosselin-Grant, Boris Skuin, Michele Petteni, Alessandro Sestini, Joakim Bergdahl, Amir Baghi, Linus Gissl\'en

entry.summary: RAID is an iterative reinforcement-learning approach for automated game testing that trains a population of goal-scoring agents to find exploits in the goalie AI of a development build of NHL 26, mitigating RL's tendency to overfit to single solutions. It reduces the manual effort of repeated playtesting.

entry.title: Collaborative Synthetic Data Generation for Knowledge Transfer in Federated Learning

author.name: Maximilian Andreas Hoefler, Karsten Mueller, Wojciech Samek

entry.summary: FedKT-CSD is a one-shot federated learning framework that transfers client knowledge to the server via collaboratively constructed synthetic data, inspired by neural image compression, while providing formal privacy guarantees. It aims to jointly achieve low communication, robustness to heterogeneity, and rigorous privacy.

entry.title: DiaLLM: An Investigation into the Robustness-Generation Gap in English Dialect Adaptation

author.name: Jordan Painter, Dipankar Srirag, Adarsh Kappiyath, Diptesh Kanojia, Aditya Joshi, Lu Yin

entry.summary: DiaLLM continually pretrains three open-weight model families on the International Corpus of English and applies post-training and alignment strategies to study dialectal English generation across Australian, Indian, and Northern British English. It finds dialectal robustness (understanding) and generation are dissociated capabilities.

entry.title: LLT: Local Linear Transformer for PDE Operator Learning

author.name: Oded Ovadia, Eli Turkel

entry.summary: LLT is a Local Linear Transformer for PDE operator learning that combines linear global attention with local spatial mixing and coordinate/geometry information to overcome standard attention's quadratic cost and lack of locality bias. It is evaluated on PDE problems including elasticity and plasticity.

entry.title: SHIFT: Survival Prediction from Incomplete and Heterogeneous Genomic Data

author.name: Muhammet Sami Yavuz, Ayhan Can Erdur, Sabri Mustafa Kahya, Benedikt Wiestler, Jana Lipkova

entry.summary: Introduces SHIFT, a transformer survival model that predicts from incomplete genomic panels using masked self-attention and feature-availability masks, avoiding test-time imputation. It targets cross-institution transfer where sequencing panels differ and cause structural feature missingness.

entry.title: Trustworthy Machine Learning through the Lens of Combinatorial Optimization: Survey and Research Perspectives

author.name: Thibaut Vidal, Julien Ferry

entry.summary: A survey framing trustworthy machine learning (interpretability, robustness, fairness, privacy, certifiability) through combinatorial optimization and certification-oriented reasoning. It synthesizes recent work at the intersection of these fields and outlines research perspectives.

entry.title: Multimodal Unlearning Across Vision, Language, Video, and Audio: Survey of Methods, Datasets, and Benchmarks

author.name: Nobin Sarwar, Shubhashis Roy Dipta, Zheyuan Liu, Vaidehi Patil

entry.summary: A survey of multimodal unlearning across vision, language, video, and audio, offering a unified system-oriented taxonomy of methods, datasets, and benchmarks. It frames selective removal of sensitive or copyrighted cross-modal associations while retaining utility.

entry.title: Towards Efficient Large Language Model Serving: A Survey on System-Aware KV Cache Optimization

author.name: Jiantong Jiang, Peiyu Yang, Rui Zhang, Feng Liu
