entry.summary: A survey of system-aware KV cache optimization for LLM serving, organizing work into temporal (execution and scheduling), spatial (placement and migration), and structural (representation and retention) dimensions. It analyzes cross-behavior co-design and behavior-objective links to highlight future opportunities.

entry.title: Open-ended Multi-agent Autocurricula via Visual Inspection of Policies with Multi-modal LLMs

author.name: Lorenzo Pant\`e, Andrea Fanti, Roberto Capobianco

entry.summary: Introduces Visual Inspection of Policies (VIP), which uses a video language model to watch recorded episode videos and recommend curriculum tasks for open-ended multi-agent reinforcement learning. It assesses task difficulty relative to learning progress by directly inspecting policy behavior rather than scalar scores or text summaries.

entry.title: AutoAnchor: Stable Diffusion Unlearning Using Cross-Attention as a Manifold Surrogate

author.name: Siyuan Wen, Jiahao Zeng, Ningning Ding

entry.summary: AutoAnchor formalizes diffusion unlearning instability under the manifold hypothesis, proving that lacking a manifold-proximal anchor induces normal-space drift that degrades unlearning, and uses cross-attention as a manifold surrogate to stabilize updates. It aims for robust removal of harmful or copyrighted concepts from text-to-image models.

entry.title: Beyond Backpropagation: Monte Carlo Method Can Train Deep Neural Networks

author.name: Hong Zhao

entry.summary: Demonstrates that a simple Monte Carlo procedure (randomly mutate a parameter, keep if loss decreases, else retry) can train deep networks on a single GPU without backpropagation, batch normalization, or residual connections. It further shows the gradient-free method extends to pruning and discrete-weight training.

entry.title: Robust Bayesian Decision Making under Adversarial Uncertainty

author.name: Haripriya Harikumar, Sammie Katt, Yasir Zubayr Barlas, Samuel Kaski

entry.summary: Studies sequential adversarially robust decision-aware experimental design, where data acquisition maximizes information gain against plausible worst-case unexpected effects to keep downstream decisions reliable under hidden or weakly modeled influences. It moves beyond assuming well-specified outcome models and stable optimal decisions.

entry.title: Feedback Manipulation Regularization: Enabling Offline Agent Alignment for Imitation Learning

author.name: Benjamin Poole, Minwoo Lee

entry.summary: Feedback Manipulation Regularization is an algorithm-agnostic method that uses evaluative feedback as a corrective signal to align imitation-learning policies in single-stage offline sequential decision-making, rather than the multi-stage pipelines borrowed from language-model tuning. It aims to fuse demonstrations and feedback for better offline alignment.

entry.title: TRACE: A Two-Channel Robust Attribution Watermark via Complementary Embeddings for LLM-Agent Trajectories

author.name: Zheng Gao, Xiaoyu Li, Xiaoyan Feng, Jiaojiao Jiang, Yang Song, Yulei Sui, Zhenchang Xing, Liming Zhu

entry.summary: TRACE is presented as the first agent watermark that survives an adversary with full read/write access to the trajectory log used for detection, being distortion-free in action choices, self-synchronizing under deletion, and invariant under rewriting. It targets provenance disputes when resellers rebrand or substitute agents.

entry.title: FPGN: Redefining Ultra-Fast Programmable Gate-based Neural Acceleration with Differentiable LUTs

author.name: Jiawei Liang, Haotong Qin, Linfeng Du, Xingyu Liu, Shangkun Li, Hui Yu, Michele Magno, Xinyu Chen, Jiang Xu, Wei Zhang

entry.summary: FPGN advances LUT-native neural networks for FPGA acceleration by treating lookup tables as differentiable learnable neurons and translating their logic expressivity into high-performance nanosecond-scale accelerators, going beyond prior algorithm-only optimizations. It targets extreme low-latency inference for latency-critical applications.

entry.title: Statistical Efficiency and Inference of Quantile Distributional Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Zijie Cheng, Yang Peng, Zhihua Zhang

entry.summary: This paper studies the statistical efficiency of quantile-based distributional reinforcement learning for policy evaluation, constructing an estimator of the quantile fixed point from an empirical MDP with a generative model and establishing non-asymptotic supremum-norm error bounds. It provides finite-sample guarantees for characterizing return distributions.

entry.title: Cognitive-structured Multimodal Agent for Multimodal Understanding, Generation, and Editing

author.name: Feng Wang, Canmiao Fu, Zhipeng Huang, Chen Li, Jing Lyu, Ge Li

entry.summary: This paper proposes a cognitive-structured multimodal agent that externalizes visual information into an episodic visual memory and selectively reactivates relevant episodes, using perception, retrieval, and executive-control modules to overcome visual-token explosion in long-horizon multimodal dialogue. It targets unified understanding, generation, and editing across many turns.

entry.title: AI-guided stimuli discovery and generation to optimize facial emotion perception studies in autism

author.name: Kushin Mukherjee, Na Yeon Kim, Maren Wehrheim, Ralph Adolphs, Kohitij Kar

entry.summary: This work uses population-specific artificial neural network models to predict image-level facial-emotion judgments for autistic and neurotypical adults, then generates novel faces predicted to maximize group differences, showing that reported variability may reflect image-level sparsity concentrated in a few diagnostic expressions. It uses AI-guided stimulus optimization to improve behavioral-assay sensitivity.

entry.title: Score Accuracy Along the Forward Diffusion Does Not Certify Numerical Stability in Diffusion Sampling

author.name: Yiwei Zhou

entry.summary: A theoretical paper showing that small forward-marginal score error does not certify numerical stability of reverse-time diffusion samplers, constructing examples where Euler-Maruyama discretizations converge in probability yet every positive moment and all Wasserstein distances diverge. It challenges a common assumption behind score-matching guarantees.

entry.title: When the Judge Changes, So Does the Measurement: Auditing LLM-as-Judge Reliability

author.name: Zongyou Yang, Yinghan Hou, Xiaokun Yang

entry.summary: Treats LLM-as-judge evaluator replacement as a measurement-validity problem, showing across four datasets that judge upgrades are not interchangeable: scaling Qwen3 dense judges and moving across MiniMax M2-M2.7 releases yield inconsistent gains, and stronger judges reduce but do not remove position and verbosity bias. It cautions that structured debate can shift decisions without proper parsing controls.

entry.title: WebSwarm: Recursive Multi-Agent Orchestration for Deep-and-Wide Web Search

author.name: Xiaoshuai Song, Liancheng Zhang, Kangzhi Zhao, Yutao Zhu, Zhongyuan Wang, Guanting Dong, Jinghan Yang, Han Li, Kun Gai, Ji-Rong Wen, Zhicheng Dou

entry.summary: Introduces WebSwarm, a progressive recursive delegation framework for web search that jointly constructs task decomposition, recursive expansion and adaptive agent collaboration during inference. It targets the depth-versus-coverage limits of single ReAct agents and rigid multi-agent aggregation.

entry.title: Token-Flow Firewall: Semantic Runtime Auditing for Persistent AI Agents

author.name: Puji Wang, Yingchen Zhang, Ruqing Zhang, Jiafeng Guo, Xueqi Cheng

entry.summary: Proposes TokenWall, a Token-Flow Firewall that performs semantic runtime auditing of persistent AI agents by intercepting risky natural-language token flows (memory updates, tool arguments, retrieved files, inter-component messages) before they reach privileged sinks. It reframes agent safety as controlling unsafe semantic flows across an expanded attack surface.

entry.title: Remember When It Matters: Proactive Memory Agent for Long-Horizon Agents

author.name: Yifan Wu, Lizhu Zhang, Yuhang Zhou, Mingyi Wang, Bo Peng, Serena Li, Xiangjun Fan, Zhuokai Zhao

entry.summary: Identifies behavioral state decay in long-horizon agents, where decision-relevant information gets buried or pushed beyond the context window, and proposes a plug-and-play memory agent that runs alongside an unmodified action agent to inject memory-grounded reminders when needed. It treats memory as an active intervention rather than passive retrieval.

entry.title: LUMI: Tokenizer-Agnostic LLM-Based Lossless Image Compression

author.name: Chris Xing Tian, Chengkai Wu, Ziyu Wang, Rongqun Lin, Kecheng Chen, Xiandong Meng, Haoliang Li, Shiqi Wang, Siwei Ma

entry.summary: LUMI is a tokenizer-agnostic framework for lossless RGB image compression with frozen LLM backbones, replacing pixel-as-text tokenization with a pixel embedding module to decouple compression from vocabulary specifics. It broadens LLM-based compression across model families.

entry.title: OpenCoF: Learning to Reason Through Video Generation

author.name: Xinyan Chen, Ziyu Guo, Renrui Zhang, Dongzhi Jiang, Hongsheng Li

entry.summary: OpenCoF introduces a chain-of-frame reasoning paradigm where reasoning unfolds through temporally connected video frames, with a 17K-example dataset across 11 task families and a fine-tuned Wan-CoF model to study whether temporal supervision improves this behavior. It explores video generation as a reasoning substrate.

entry.title: EgoWAM: World Action Models Beyond Pixels with In-the-Wild Egocentric Human Data

author.name: Baoyu Li, Xinchen Yin, Mengying Lin, Yixin Zhang, Danfei Xu

entry.summary: EgoWAM studies whether world action models trained on in-the-wild egocentric human data transfer better to robots by requiring policies to predict scene evolution, hypothesizing that effective world targets abstract appearance, capture agent-invariant physical effects, and separate camera from environment motion. It introduces a controlled human-robot co-training framework.

entry.title: Natural Language Autoencoders are summarizers, but do they have to be?

author.name: Andrey Anurin

entry.summary: An interpretability investigation into natural language autoencoders (NLAs), which verbalize model activations, finding that verbalizations transfer across models and families but lean heavily on confabulated snippet quotes from prompts. The author argues these behaviors suggest NLAs summarize rather than faithfully report internal states, motivating architecture or training changes.

entry.title: From Atomic Actions to Standard Operating Procedures: Iterative Tool Optimization for Self-Evolving LLM Agents

author.name: Haipeng Ding, Yuexiang Xie, Zhewei Wei, Yaliang Li, Bolin Ding

entry.summary: EvoSOP lets LLM agents self-evolve by synthesizing granular atomic actions into reusable Standard Operating Procedures that act as higher-order callable tools, reducing repeated low-level reasoning. It extracts SOPs from execution trajectories to cut reasoning overhead and failure rates.

entry.title: The Blind Curator: How a Biased Judge Silently Disables Skill Retirement in Self-Evolving Agents

author.name: Xing Zhang, Yanwei Cui, Guanghui Wang, Ziyuan Li, Wei Qiu, Bing Zhu, Peiyang He

entry.summary: This paper shows that a biased LLM judge does not merely add noise but silently switches off skill retirement in self-evolving agents, letting a skill library drift below the no-skill baseline. Using a corrupted-reward analysis and injected corruption on a report-writing testbed, it demonstrates asymmetric bias breaks the retirement guarantee while symmetric noise does not.

entry.title: A Transdiagnostic Space of Disorder Like Phenotypes in Reinforcement Learning Agents

author.name: Hari Prasad

entry.summary: Recasts modeling of psychological disorders in RL agents as dose-controllable manipulation of cognitive appraisal signals in an appraisal-guided PPO agent, expressing seven disorders as single tunable knobs with preregistered assays. It positions this as a testbed for computational psychiatry and a lens on affective control failure modes.

entry.title: False Confidence: Automated Labels Confound Fairness Audits in Cervical Spine Segmentation

author.name: Linus Juni, Aasa Feragen, Aditya Parikh

entry.summary: This paper presents the first fairness audit of cervical-spine MRI segmentation and shows that although the model is demographically fair, the machine-generated silver reference labels used to judge it are themselves biased, confounding the audit. It warns that automated labels can distort fairness conclusions in medical imaging.

entry.title: When Synthetic Speech Is All You Have: Better Call GRPO

author.name: Shashi Kumar, Yanis Labrak, Hasindri Watawana, Sergio Burdisso, Esa\'u Villatoro-Tello, Kadri Hacio\u{g}lu, Petr Motlicek, Andreas Stolcke

entry.summary: Shows that Group Relative Policy Optimization extracts far more value from synthetic TTS speech than supervised fine-tuning for domain-adapted ASR, cutting WER by 40% relative to SFT and 45% when combined. It offers a privacy-friendly path to adapt ASR without real recordings.

entry.title: Playing ZendoWorld: Challenging AI Agents on Active Visual Concept Induction

author.name: Sophia Koehler, Antonia W\"ust, Inga Ibs, Wasu Top Piriyakulkij, Wolfgang Stammer, Constantin Rothkopf, Kevin Ellis, Kristian Kersting

entry.summary: ZendoWorld is an interactive environment for testing whether AI agents can jointly perceive scenes, form hypotheses about hidden logical rules, and design informative experiments to test them, evaluating VLM reasoning, Bayesian particle filtering, and neuro-symbolic methods. A key finding is that high label-prediction accuracy does not imply recovery of the underlying rule.

entry.title: Capturing token IDs during agentic interactions for better reinforcement learning

author.name: Unknown

entry.summary: An Amazon Science post explaining why capturing exact token IDs during agentic tool-use interactions (rather than reconstructed transcripts) is critical for correct RL parameter updates, since tokenization differences can silently corrupt training signals. It highlights a practical bookkeeping fix for training agents in harnesses.

entry.title: Does AI Understand Imaging? A Systematic Benchmark of Agentic AI for Computational Imaging Tasks

author.name: Ethan Chung, Chuanjun Zheng, Jasper Tan, Jingxi Li, Haopeng Zhang, Huaijin Chen

entry.summary: ImagingBench evaluates whether VLMs and agentic systems can handle the physics and inverse problems of computational imaging across 20 tasks in five categories, using Expert, Planner, and Forward settings. It benchmarks leading proprietary and open multimodal systems including Gemini and GPT variants.

entry.title: Reasoning Consistency Scanning: A Framework for Auditing Chain-of-Thought Validity in AI Safety Evaluations

author.name: Silvia Santano

entry.summary: This work introduces reasoning consistency scanning, a method to detect whether stated chain-of-thought is logically consistent with the accompanying answer, assessable from transcripts alone without interventions. It positions consistency as a more tractable proxy than faithfulness for auditing safety evaluation transcripts.

entry.title: Physics-Audited Agentic Discovery in Scientific Machine Learning

author.name: Diab W. Abueidda, Bilal Ahmed, Panos Pantidis, Mostafa E. Mobasher

entry.summary: PA-SciML is a verification-first workflow for agentic scientific machine learning that fixes a scoring evaluator before search and checks discovered surrogate models against machine-checkable physics requirements like boundary conditions and superposition. It separately searches input ranges for high-violation cases to expose failures a low error metric hides.

entry.title: Agentic Data Environments

author.name: Elaine Ang, Chenxi Huang, Georgios Liargkovas, Jerry Liu, Jinhui Liu, Nikos Pagonas, Charlie Summers, Haonan Wang, Jiakai Xu, Tianle Zhou, Yusen Zhang, Zhou Yu, Zhuo Zhang, Tianyi Peng, Kostis Kaffes, Eugene Wu

entry.summary: This talk-format position piece introduces Agentic Data Environments as the execution substrate spanning files, APIs, applications, and system state in which agents operate, arguing it can both amplify capability and enforce safety guarantees. It reframes data systems from passive stores into active substrates for safe, reliable agent execution.

entry.title: SkillCenter: A Large-Scale Source-Grounded Skill Library for Autonomous AI Agents

author.name: Tianming Sha, Yue Zhao, Lichao Sun, Yushun Dong

entry.summary: SkillCenter is presented as the largest open skill library for agents by count, with 216,938 structured skills across 24 domain bundles, combining source-grounded skills from peer-reviewed and technical literature with community skills from GitHub and a marketplace. A SkillGate quality filter and template-driven generation build the source-grounded subset.

entry.title: LipSSD: Lipschitz-Constrained Single-Shot Detection for Adversarially Robust Object Detection

author.name: Vincent L\'eb\'e (IRIT, DTIPG - SNCF, UT3), Yannick Prudent (IRIT, DTIPG - SNCF, UT3), Corentin Friedrich (IRIT, DTIPG - SNCF, UT3), Thomas Massena (IRIT, DTIPG - SNCF, UT3), Ronan Sicre (IRIT), Franck Mamalet

entry.summary: LipSSD introduces Lipschitz-constrained variants of single-shot object detectors as robust-by-design alternatives to adversarial training, aiming for robustness that transfers across attacks, budgets, and architectures. It provides a comprehensive evaluation of the constrained SSD detector for adversarially robust detection.

entry.title: Gimitest: A Comprehensive Tool for Testing Reinforcement Learning Policies

author.name: Dennis Gross, Quentin Mazouni, Helge Spieker, Arnaud Gotlieb

entry.summary: Gimitest is an open-source framework for testing single- and multi-agent reinforcement learning policies under varied conditions and gym environments. It supports modification of integrated components to probe policy reliability and safety.

entry.title: Progressive Crystallization: Turning Agent Exploration into Deterministic, Lower-Cost Workflows in Production

author.name: Arun Malik

entry.summary: Progressive crystallization is a lifecycle that treats AI-agent exploration as discovery, promoting repeatedly validated agent behaviors into cheaper deterministic workflows and demoting those that regress. It is evaluated on a production cloud-networking AIOps system handling tens of thousands of incidents.

entry.title: Tree-of-Thoughts Reasoning for Text-to-Image In-Context Learning

author.name: Stepanida Alekseeva, Jenifer Kalafatovich, Seong-Whan Lee

entry.summary: This work applies Tree-of-Thoughts reasoning to text-to-image in-context learning, generating, evaluating, and selecting among candidate hypotheses before constructing the final image prompt. It aims to mitigate compositional-reasoning failures and prompt sensitivity in multimodal models.

entry.title: Vision Foundation Models in Radiology: A Scoping Review of Data, Methodology, Evaluation and Clinical Translation

author.name: Alejandro Vergara-Richart (Quantitative Imaging Biomarkers in Medicine, Quibim S.L., Val\`encia, Spain, Universitat Polit\`ecnica de Val\`encia, Val\`encia, Spain), Xavier Rafael-Palou (Quantitative Imaging Biomarkers in Medicine, Quibim S.L., Val\`encia, Spain), Almudena Fuster-Matanzo (Quantitative Imaging Biomarkers in Medicine, Quibim S.L., Val\`encia, Spain), Ignacio Iborra Roncales (Quantitative Imaging Biomarkers in Medicine, Quibim S.L., Val\`encia, Spain), \'Angel Alberich-Bayarri (Quantitative Imaging Biomarkers in Medicine, Quibim S.L., Val\`encia, Spain), Ana Jim\'enez-Pastor (Quantitative Imaging Biomarkers in Medicine, Quibim S.L., Val\`encia, Spain)

entry.summary: A PRISMA-guided scoping review of vision foundation models trained exclusively on radiological imaging, mapping 67 studies across data scale, architectural and pretraining scalability, and downstream transfer. It documents heterogeneity in definitions, data (brain MRI, thoracoabdominal CT, chest X-ray), and evaluation.

entry.title: ORCAID: Oblique Rule-Based Continuous-Action Interpretation for Deep RL Policies

author.name: Ignacio D. Lopez-Miguel, Ezio Bartocci, Thomas Eiter, Martin Tappler

entry.summary: ORCAID extracts interpretable oblique rule-based policies from RL agents in mixed continuous-discrete environments with continuous actions, using an efficient oblique decision-tree algorithm with random initialization, local refinement, and backward elimination. Adjacent leaves are merged for concise rules.

entry.title: Hypergraph Neural Stochastic Diffusion: An SDE Framework for Uncertainty Estimation

author.name: Zhiheng Zhou, Mengyao Zhou, Dengyi Zhao, Xingqin Qi, Guiying Yan

entry.summary: HyperNSD is a stochastic differential equation framework for uncertainty estimation on hypergraph neural networks, capturing uncertainty from noisy attributes, ambiguous labels, and variable incidence structures during representation learning. It avoids the cost of ensembles or Bayesian inference.

entry.title: HumAIN: Human-Aware Implicit Social Robot Navigation

author.name: Daeun Song, Nhat Le, Jeffrey Chen, Mohammad Nazeri, Amirreza Payandeh, Rohan Chandra, Reuth Mirsky, Ross Mead, Ling Xiao, Xuesu Xiao

entry.summary: HumAIN fuses implicit social cues such as gait and body orientation into robot navigation planning via a transformer teacher distilled into a lightweight student for real-time deployment. It aims to anticipate conversational and pedestrian dynamics rather than merely react.

entry.title: SynthAVE: Scalable Synthetic Labeling for E-Commerce with LLM-Arena Validation

author.name: Andrea Scarinci, Virginia Negri, Brayan Impata, Suleiman Khan, Victor Martinez, Marcello Federico

entry.summary: SynthAVE is a large-scale human-validated benchmark for e-commerce attribute value extraction spanning thousands of products, attributes, and four languages, paired with a multi-LLM arena to validate synthetic labels at industrial scale. It addresses the prohibitive cost of human annotation for fine-tuning.

entry.title: Where to Intervene? Benchmarking Fairness-Aware Learning on Differentially Private Synthetic Tabular Data

author.name: Vin\'icius Gabriel Angelozzi, H\'eber H. Arcolezi

entry.summary: This paper presents a systematic evaluation of fairness interventions on differentially private synthetic tabular data, centered on the Adaptive Iterative Mechanism, examining where in the pipeline to intervene when privacy amplifies group disparities. It addresses the conflict between DP and fairness objectives.

entry.title: Stability of Flow Models for Graph Signals

author.name: Martin Schmidt, Gonzalo Mateos

entry.summary: This paper analyzes the stability of continuous normalized flow models parameterized by graph neural networks for graph-signal generation, proving permutation equivariance is preserved in both the ODEs and their discrete samplers and deriving explicit stability bounds under structural perturbations. It fills a theoretical gap on error propagation in generative flows on graphs.

entry.title: Creativity from Friction: Human-AI Interaction for Exploratory Structural Design

author.name: Ricardo Maia Avelino, Rita Sevastjanova, Tom Van Mele, Philippe Block, Mennatallah El-Assady

entry.summary: This position and interaction paper argues that generative AI's default goal of removing friction and delivering final solutions conflicts with creative fields like structural design, where friction from constraints stimulates novel ideas. It proposes interactive human-AI systems for exploratory structural design with credible architecture-engineering authorship.

entry.title: CARLA-GS: Decoupling Representation, Reasoning, and Physics Simulation for Autonomous Driving Corner-Case Synthesis

author.name: Kaicong Huang, Meng Ma, Ruimin Ke

entry.summary: CARLA-GS is a modular corner-case synthesis pipeline for autonomous driving that decouples visual representation, scene reasoning, and physics simulation to generate photorealistic safety-critical scenarios with spatiotemporal and physical consistency. It unifies aspects that prior scene-only, trajectory-only, or end-to-end diffusion methods handled in isolation.

entry.title: Towards the Explainability of Temporal Graph Networks via Memory Backtracking and Topological Attribution

author.name: Yazheng Liu, Xi Zhang, Sihong Xie, Hui Xiong

entry.summary: This paper makes temporal graph networks explainable by attributing predictions through a topology attribution tree and a memory backtracking tree, incorporating the memory module that prior methods overlook. It applies layer-wise relevance propagation to quantify how historical events shape node memory vectors.

entry.title: ReCoLoRA: Spectrum-Aware Recursive Consolidation for Continual LLM Fine-Tuning

author.name: Wentao Lu

entry.summary: ReCoLoRA is a spectrum-aware framework for continual LLM fine-tuning that initializes adapters from a randomized SVD of pretrained weights, selects per-layer ranks by an elbow criterion, and re-decomposes the current effective weight into frozen residual, slowly updated principal, and fresh components before each task. It aims to reduce overwriting of prior tasks by stacked low-rank updates.

entry.title: When Thinking Hurts: Epistemic Signals in the Reasoning Chains of Visual Language Models

author.name: Mayank Singal

entry.summary: Provides an empirical characterization of answer-entropy behavior in thinking-mode vision language models, finding three distinct patterns (complete collapse, no collapse, selective thinking) across models. It reports that reasoning-chain entropy outperforms answer entropy for uncertainty estimation on the subset where chains are generated.

entry.title: Deep Learning Method for Stationary Distribution of Reflected Brownian Motion

author.name: Jim Dai, Zhanhao Zhang

entry.summary: Provides a spectral analysis of dueling Q-learning, which decomposes the Q-function into value and advantage components, extending recent tabular analyses beyond regularized formulations toward the pure tabular setting. It aims to strengthen the theoretical understanding of this efficiency-improving RL variant.

entry.title: Workload-Preserving Differentially Private Synthetic Data for Causal Inference via Maximum-Entropy Calibration

author.name: Amir Asiaee, Kaveh Aryan

entry.summary: Proposes causal workloads for differentially private synthetic data: DP query sets designed around the orthogonal moments used by doubly robust causal estimators, so average treatment effect estimation is preserved. It provides theory decomposing ATE error into sampling, privacy, workload-approximation, and Monte Carlo terms.

entry.title: CASL-VAE: Learning Structured Latent Variables from Unpaired Data for Semi-supervised Clustering and Paired Sample Generation

author.name: Sai Spandana Chintapalli, Pratik Chaudhari, Christos Davatzikos

entry.summary: CASL-VAE is a contrastive latent variable model that learns structured generative factors from unpaired reference and target populations, factorizing variation into shared common factors and hierarchical salient factors modeling target-specific subtypes and within-subtype variation. It enables semi-supervised clustering and paired sample generation for clinical contrast problems.

entry.title: Write-Protected Discrete Bottlenecks for Language-Grounded World Models: A Structural Limitation and Sufficient Fix

author.name: Jiayi Fang

entry.summary: Analyzes how language should interface with a world model's discrete symbol system, showing that letting language gradients enter a Gumbel-softmax discrete bottleneck forces symbol collapse or fails to learn semantic labels across many anti-collapse variants. It proposes a write-protected bottleneck as a minimal architectural fix.

entry.title: Spectral Analysis of Dueling Q-Learning

author.name: Donghwan Lee

entry.summary: Develops a deep learning method to learn the Laplace transform of high-dimensional reflected Brownian motion stationary distributions using the basic adjoint relationship, targeting tail probabilities that are otherwise intractable. It combines tailored loss design, data sampling, and network architecture, validated against known ground-truth cases.

entry.title: Closing the Null Space: Guidance-Aware Quantization for Classifier-Free Diffusion

author.name: Abdullah Al Shafi, Sumaiya Rahim Suma

entry.summary: This work shows that post-training quantization for classifier-free-guidance diffusion models must account for the paired conditional/unconditional two-pass structure, exposing a hidden latency overhead and an exact null space where guidance-gap calibration alone can be satisfied without preserving quality. It proposes guidance-aware quantization to close this gap.

entry.title: Joint Discrete-Continuous Flow Matching for Open-Vocabulary Inverse Design of Multilayer Optical Coatings

author.name: Zhiyi Li, Yuheng Jin, Yidan Huang, Nan Chen, Hongyan Fu, Yikun Bu

entry.summary: IrisFlow is a query-based open-vocabulary flow-matching framework for inverse design of multilayer optical coatings, supplying target spectra, wavelength grids, candidate-material optical constants, and layer counts at query time so materials enter as wavelength-aware tokens rather than fixed learned identities. It jointly handles discrete material sequences and continuous thicknesses.

entry.title: Can We Trust LLM's Logic? Quantifying Uncertainty, Coherence, and Robustness via a Graph-Based Framework

author.name: Riccardo Revalor, Jalees Rehman, Debjit Pal

entry.summary: Presents GRAPHEVAL, a graph-based framework that reframes LLM reasoning uncertainty quantification as a holistic fidelity problem, evaluating intermediate-step logical validity rather than only final-answer agreement. It proposes a Graph Reasoning uncertainty metric and tests robustness under adversarial conditions.

entry.title: TypeProbe: Recovering Type Representations from Hidden States of Pre-trained Code Models

author.name: Giuliano Gorgone, Fausto Carcassi

entry.summary: Probes residual streams of pretrained code models and finds cross-lingual type representations emerge even from untyped code, with probes trained on one language inferring argument and result types in another. The structure is partly robust to lexical perturbations and syntactic variation.

entry.title: Cross-seed explainability using Procrustes-conditioned Joint End-to-end Top-K Sparse Autoencoders

author.name: Bendeg\'uz V\'aradi, Zolt\'an Kmetty

entry.summary: Introduces a Procrustes-conditioned joint end-to-end Top-K sparse autoencoder that extracts cross-seed universal features from independently trained BERT models by aligning activation spaces before joint SAE training. It tackles feature-space misalignment that undermines mechanistic-interpretability reproducibility.

entry.title: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks

author.name: Zhekai Chen, Chengqi Duan, Kaiyue Sun, Bohao Li, Yuqing Wang, Manyuan Zhang, Xihui Liu

entry.summary: Introduces UniClawBench, a capability-driven benchmark for evaluating proactive agents on dynamic real-world multi-turn tasks, moving beyond sandboxed single-turn paradigms and scenario-based taxonomies that mix capabilities. It aims to better localize root causes of agent failures.

entry.title: DreamCharacter-1: From 3D Generative Foundation Models to Product-Ready Character Generation

author.name: Weizhe Liu, Yunjie Wu, Xiangqian Shu, Guangwei Wang, Xiangyu Xu, Peng Li, Yujie Li, Hengkai Guo

entry.summary: Presents DreamCharacter-1, a lightweight post-adaptation framework that calibrates pretrained 3D foundation models toward production-ready character generation via geometry post-training, texture post-training and inference acceleration. It produces high-fidelity, structurally robust 3D character assets.

entry.title: Adversarial Decoys: Misdirecting Attention-Based Defenses in ViT

author.name: Giulia Marchiori Pietrosanti, Giulio Rossolini, Giorgio Buttazzo

entry.summary: Introduces adversarial decoys, independently optimized patches that redirect attention-based ViT defenses toward target tokens by decoupling misclassification from defense evasion, so the original adversarial region misleads prediction while decoys absorb attention-suppression defenses. It exposes a weakness in test-time attention-suppression defenses.

entry.title: LightCrafter: PBR-Conditioned Video Diffusion Refinement for Controllable and Consistent Relighting

author.name: Zixin Guo, Yehonathan Litman, Yifeng He, John Miller, Chuhan Chen, Deva Ramanan

entry.summary: Introduces LightCrafter, a PBR-conditioned video diffusion refinement method for controllable, temporally consistent relighting that combines physically-based rendering conditioning with generative refinement to overcome noisy inverse-rendering reconstructions and limited control of pure generative approaches. It targets long-form temporal consistency plus physically grounded light transport.

entry.title: Attention-Based Segmentation of WMHs and Differentiation of Vascular vs. Demyelinating Lesions

author.name: Aina Tur-Serrano, Gabriel Moy\`a-Alcover, Francisco J. Perales L\'opez

entry.summary: A survey of Continual Test-Time Adaptation in computer vision that formalizes the problem, categorizes continual domain-shift patterns and evaluation protocols, and analyzes methods mitigating catastrophic forgetting and pseudo-label error accumulation. It consolidates benchmarks and outlines future directions.

entry.title: HSA: Hierarchical Slot Attention for Multi-granularity Scene-Decomposition

author.name: Neelu Madan, Rongzhen Zhao, Andreas Mogelmose, Juho Kannala, Joni Pajarinen, Graham W. Taylor, Thomas B. Moeslund

entry.summary: HSA introduces hierarchical slot attention to decompose scenes into multiple semantic granularities (foreground/background, category, instance) rather than a flat set of appearance-based slots, using supervision to inject semantic hierarchy. It aligns object-centric learning more with human scene understanding.

entry.title: Progression as Latent Drift: Generative Forecasting of Slow-Evolving Pathologies

author.name: Yuxiang Feng, Juncheng Wang, Chao Xu, Wenlong Hou, Huihan Wang, Yijie Qian, Yang Liu, Baigui Sun, Yong Liu, Shujun Wan

entry.summary: This work frames neurodegenerative disease progression as latent drift and provides a theoretical account of two failure modes (identity collapse and continuous interpolation trap) when applying generative sequence models to subtle longitudinal MRI change. It proposes a generative forecasting approach for slow-evolving pathologies.

entry.title: Texture Representations in Deep Vision Models: Comparing CNNs, Vision Transformers, and Human Perception

author.name: Ludovica de Paolis, Marco Baroni, Alessandro Laio, Eugenio Piasini

entry.summary: This study compares how CNNs, vision transformers, and humans represent visual texture (as opposed to object recognition), using rank-based statistics to quantify information in internal representations across textures of varying complexity. It probes the limits of deep networks as models of biological vision.

entry.title: ZipDepth: Bringing Lightweight Zero-Shot Monocular Depth Anywhere, on Any Device

author.name: Fabio Tosi, Luca Bartolomei, Matteo Poggi, Stefano Mattoccia

entry.summary: ZipDepth is a compact 6.1M-parameter monocular depth network that achieves zero-shot generalization by distilling from a foundation depth model over a large multi-domain dataset, running in real time from server GPUs to power-constrained devices. It bridges the gap between heavy foundation models and lightweight single-domain methods.

entry.title: In vivo feasibility study of humanoid robots in surgery

author.name: Zekai Liang, Nikita Thareja, Peihan Zhang, Calvin Joyce, Soofiyan Atar, Florian Richter, Garth Jacobsen, Shanglei Liu, Ryan Broderick, Michael Yip

entry.summary: This feasibility study explores using humanoid robots for surgical tasks in vivo, motivated by healthcare staffing shortages and the embodied nature of hospital work that current automation neglects. It examines the potential of humanoid form factors versus purpose-built surgical platforms.

entry.title: Tiny robot boats build floating structures

author.name: Rachel Gordon | MIT CSAIL

entry.summary: MIT CSAIL's FloatForm is a swarm of small square robotic boats that autonomously self-assemble into larger floating structures, disassemble, and reconfigure on water with minimal human direction. The team envisions on-demand adaptive waterfront infrastructure such as temporary bridges, platforms, and stages.

entry.title: PRoVeFL: Private Robust and Verifiable Aggregation in Federated Learning

author.name: Harsh Kasyap, Anil Kumar Pradhan, Ugur Ilker Atmaca, Graham Cormode, Carsten Maple

entry.summary: PRoVeFL is a modular federated learning framework aiming to be simultaneously privacy-preserving, Byzantine-robust, and verifiable, tackling the usual trade-off among these goals and the overhead of cryptographic primitives. It addresses vulnerabilities from centralized aggregation and honest-but-curious client assumptions.

entry.title: Large Language Models (LLMs) and Generative AI in Cybersecurity and Privacy: A Survey of Dual-Use Risks, AI-Generated Malware, Explainability, and Defensive Strategies

author.name: Kiarash Ahi, Saeed Valizadeh

entry.summary: A survey of dual-use risks of LLMs and generative AI in cybersecurity, covering AI-generated malware, explainability, and defensive strategies. It cites a striking claimed surge in LLM-generated malware and calls for next-generation security frameworks.

entry.title: Beyond wheelchairs and blindfolds: Investigating disability stereotypes in T2I models with INCLUDE-BENCH

author.name: Sophia Lichtenberg, Albert Gatt, Judith Masthoff

entry.summary: INCLUDE-BENCH is a large-scale benchmark (119K generated images) for evaluating disability-related bias in text-to-image models, grounded in sociological definitions of stereotyping that prior benchmarks lack. It addresses a systematically underexplored bias dimension.

entry.title: DexVerse: A Modular Benchmark for Multi-Task, Multi-Embodiment Dexterous Manipulation

author.name: Yunchao Yao, Zhuxiu Xu, Tianqi Zhang, Zixian Liu, Sikai Li, Zhenyu Wei, Feng Chen, Dihong Huang, Kechang Wan, Chenyang Ma, Shuqi Zhao, Shenghua Gao, Masayoshi Tomizuka, Yi Ma, Mingyu Ding

entry.summary: DexVerse is a large-scale modular benchmark for multi-task, multi-embodiment dexterous manipulation with 100 tasks spanning grasping, articulated-object interaction, tool use, bimanual coordination, non-prehensile control, and contact-rich behaviors under controllable visual variation. It targets systematic study of cross-task and cross-embodiment generalization.

entry.title: Announcing our $160M grant from Coefficient Giving

author.name: Geoffrey Irving

entry.summary: Resolution (formerly Sequent), led by Geoffrey Irving, announces a $160M grant from Coefficient Giving to scale rigorous, semiautomated alignment research toward higher-confidence guarantees. The grant includes a conditional tranche tied to hiring and compute, plus a small regranting budget for the wider community.

entry.title: Evaluating SageMath-Augmented LLM Agents for Computational and Experimental Mathematics

author.name: Pavel Snopov, German Magai

entry.summary: The paper proposes a ReAct-style agent that couples LLM reasoning with verifiable SageMath feedback and up-to-date documentation retrieval for research-level math problems from RealMath. It also refines the RealMath benchmark with multi-step post-processing and validation.

entry.title: Learning social norms enhances compatibility in dynamic human-AI coordination

author.name: Yi Yang, Siyuan Liu, Xin Gao, Huamu Sun, Chao Liu, Qing Zhou, Bingbing Nie

entry.summary: This work argues that human-AI coordination failures stem from aligning to demonstrations without explicitly quantifying the underlying social norms, using pedestrian-vehicle interaction as a testbed. It proposes learning explicit norms to improve compatibility in dynamic interactions.

entry.title: Can Reinforcement Learning Efficiently Discover Price Manipulation?

author.name: Ioanna-Yvonni Tsaknaki, Andrea Macr\`i, Fabrizio Lillo

entry.summary: This paper investigates whether a model-free RL agent can identify and exploit price-manipulation opportunities more effectively than a model-based approach with noisy parameter estimates, in an Almgren-Chriss market with nonlinear permanent and linear temporary impact. It establishes the existence of manipulative strategies and compares finite-sample learning approaches.

entry.title: When Prompts Ignore Structure: Graph-Based Attribute Reasoning for Calibrated VLMs

author.name: Tanay Sodha, Aditya Sharma, Ramya Hebbalaguppe, Vinti Agarwal, Pranav Murthy Yeluripaty

entry.summary: ARGTCA improves calibration of test-time-adapted vision-language models by representing class-attribute pairs as nodes in a symbolic attribute graph and training a graph attention network with contrastive objectives to capture inter-attribute dependencies. It offers diversity- and relevance-oriented attribute selection strategies.

entry.title: Heterogeneity-Adaptive Diffusion Schrodinger Bridge for PET-Guided Whole-Body MRI Translation

author.name: Chengbo Wang, Jiacheng Yu, Linjie Bian, Ming Qi, Xiaosheng Liu, Tongtong Che, Jichang Zhang, Shuyu Li, Shaoli Song, Xiuying Wang

entry.summary: HA-DSB is a heterogeneity-adaptive diffusion Schrodinger bridge for PET-guided whole-body MRI translation, explicitly modeling heterogeneous feature distributions across anatomical regions and pathological tissues to reduce PET-MR acquisition time. It tackles whole-body scans that region-specific models handle poorly.

entry.title: Future Confidence Distillation in Large Language Models

author.name: Sahil Kale

entry.summary: This work studies LLM confidence from a temporal perspective, comparing pre-solution feeling-of-knowing and post-solution judgment-of-learning estimates across frontier and open-source models, and finds post-solution confidence is better calibrated and more discriminative. It proposes distilling this future confidence signal.

entry.title: Omni-Sleep: A Sleep Foundation Model via Hierarchical Contrastive Learning of CNS--ANS Dynamic

author.name: Zhoujie Hou, Song Wang, Kexin Lou, Mo Wang, Chen Wei, Quanying Liu

entry.summary: Omni-Sleep is a sleep foundation model that uses the central/autonomic nervous system partition as a physiological prior for topology-constrained representation learning over multimodal polysomnography signals. It learns structured representations via intra-system consistency, inter-system synchronization, and hierarchical contrastive objectives.

entry.title: The Importance of Encoder Choice:A Tabular-Image Study

author.name: Ilia Koloiarov, Diego Coello de Portugal Mecke, Vijaya Krishna Yalavarthi, Tom Hanika, Lars Schmidt-Thieme

entry.summary: Evaluates state-of-the-art tabular models as encoders in image-tabular multimodal learning for the first time, addressing the challenge that in-context-learning tabular models require labels to embed instances consistently. It argues plain MLP encoders undersell the tabular modality.

entry.title: Evaluating the Generalizability of Foundation Models for Extreme Environmental Events: Case Study of California Wildfire PM2.5

author.name: Yongcan Huang, Li Jiang, Ze Yu Liu

entry.summary: Benchmarks six time series foundation model configurations against fully trained baselines and naive persistence for forecasting extreme wildfire PM2.5 spikes over a 12-year dataset. It provides an early systematic study of foundation model behavior under extreme out-of-distribution conditions.

entry.title: Collate: Collaborative Neural Network Learning for Latency-Critical Edge Systems

author.name: Shuo Huai, Di Liu, Hao Kong, Xiangzhong Luo, Weichen Liu, Ravi Subramaniam, Christian Makaya, Qian Lin

entry.summary: Collate is a federated learning training framework that collaboratively learns heterogeneous models to satisfy differing latency constraints across edge systems, using a dynamic zeroizing-recovering method to adjust local architectures. It targets speeding up inference under latency budgets, not just training efficiency.

entry.title: Rethinking Small VLM Quantization: From Component-Wise Analysis to Hardware-Aware Edge Deployment

author.name: Hyeju Shin, Chorwon Kim, Ryangsoo Kim, Hark Yoo, Jaein Kim

entry.summary: A systematic component-wise quantization study of sub-3B vision language models on Jetson Orin hardware, separating vision encoder, projector, and LLM backbone. Findings include that MoE backbones tolerate INT4 noise better than dense ones and that SigLIP encoders are disproportionately sensitive.

entry.title: Who Analyses the Analyser? Self-Validating LLM Hazard Analysis with Constitutional Meta-STPA

author.name: Samuel Tetteh, Udip Shrestha, Joshua R. Waite, Cody Fleming

entry.summary: Presents Constitutional Meta-STPA, an LLM-assisted hazard analysis tool that turns systems-theoretic process analysis on the LLM-assisted analyzer itself, addressing the blind spot that the analyzing tool is a safety-relevant system that can hallucinate standards and leave no audit trail. It self-validates LLM-drafted safety artifacts.

entry.title: PIT-SUN: A Deployable Empirical Marginal Transform Framework with Expectation-Consistent Recovery for Regression in Recommender Systems

author.name: Mingyu Zhao, Zhaohan Li, Zhenxiong Miao, Xu Zhang, Dewei Leng, Yanan Niu, Kun Gai

entry.summary: PIT-SUN is an empirical marginal transform framework for regression in recommender systems (dwell time, GMV, LTV) that preserves expectation consistency lost under nonlinear target transforms, addressing unstable MSE gradients on heavy-tailed, zero-inflated, multimodal targets. It provides expectation-consistent recovery deployable at industrial scale.

entry.title: Holographic Neural PCFG for Unsupervised Parsing

author.name: Ryosuke Yamaki, Daichi Mochihashi, Nobutaka Shimada, Tadahiro Taniguchi

entry.summary: Holographic Neural PCFG recasts probabilistic-context-free-grammar rule scoring for unsupervised parsing as algebraic relation modeling among grammar-symbol embeddings using circular-correlation holographic embeddings. It seeks interpretable rule probabilities instead of opaque black-box scoring networks.

entry.title: BACH: A Bayesian Admixture of Contrastive Heads for Multi-Interest Two-Tower Retrieval

author.name: Quoc Phong Nguyen, Paul Albert, Long Vuong, Vuong Le, Julien Monteil

entry.summary: BACH casts multi-interest two-tower retrieval as a per-user variational mixture over heads, softly training all heads to mitigate routing collapse and producing per-user interest weights reusable at serving, with a shared global-codebook variant for precomputable retrieval. It improves diversity-aware recommendation retrieval.

entry.title: Best-of-$N$ TTS Evaluation is Confounded by ASR Family Alignment

author.name: Taehyung Yu, Seongjae Kang

entry.summary: This paper identifies an evaluation confound in Best-of-N text-to-speech: the apparent quality of an ASR verifier depends strongly on which ASR family judges it, with same-family verifier-evaluator pairs recovering far more oracle headroom despite near-identical representations. It proposes cross-family evaluation corrections to avoid inflated results.

entry.title: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery

author.name: Ali Motahharynia, Mohammadreza Ghaffarzadeh-Esfahani, Mahsa Sheikholeslami, Navid Mazrouei, Matin Irajpour, Yousof Gheisari, Hajar Sirous

entry.summary: DrugGen-2 is a generative model that designs small molecules conditioned on both disease ontology and target protein sequence, built by fine-tuning GPT-2 with supervised training followed by GRPO reinforcement learning guided by drug-likeness reward functions. It incorporates disease context often neglected by target-only molecule generators.

entry.title: Structural Bottlenecks on Frequency Representation in End-to-End Audio Models

author.name: Nicole Cosme-Clifford

entry.summary: This paper analyzes whether state-of-the-art strided convolutional audio encoders preserve access to time-frequency-localized primitives like pitch and timbre, showing through theory and controlled experiments that they impose two architecture-predictable structural bottlenecks on frequency representation. It cautions against reading high fidelity as evidence of interpretable feature representation.

entry.title: MulTTiPop: A Multitrack Transcription Dataset for Pop Music

author.name: Nathan Pruyne, Benjamin Stoler, William Chen, Chien-yu Huang, Shinji Watanabe, Chris Donahue

entry.summary: Releases MulTTiPop, a 3.5-hour multitrack MIDI-audio dataset of pop music spanning the 1930s-2000s for benchmarking automatic music transcription. Evaluation shows state-of-the-art transcribers achieve only 38% onset F1, revealing large headroom.

entry.title: Scalable and Culturally Specific Stereotype Dataset Construction via Human-LLM Collaboration

author.name: Weicheng Ma, John Guerrerio, Soroush Vosoughi

entry.summary: Introduces a cost-efficient human-LLM collaborative annotation framework and applies it to build EspanStereo, a Spanish-language stereotype dataset spanning multiple Spanish-speaking countries. It captures culturally specific biases absent from English-centric resources.

entry.title: A Reliability Assessment of LALM Audio Judges for Full-Duplex Voice Agents

author.name: A. Sayyad, J. Emmons, S. Jones, T. Lin, H. Krishnan

entry.summary: Assesses the reliability of Gemini models (2.5 Flash, 3.5 Flash, 3.1 Pro) as audio judges that score full-duplex agent conversations directly from raw stereo waveforms, validated against calibrated human raters across accents and adversarial defect-injected clips. It reports strong LALM-human agreement on most production dimensions.

entry.title: Echoes Across Vietnam's Highlands, Delta, and Coast: A Multilingual Corpus for Cham, Khmer, and Tay-Nung

author.name: Anh Trac Duc Dinh, Khang Nhat Hoang Vo, Vinh Cong Doan, Tai Tien Ta, Khoa Duc Anh Lam

entry.summary: Introduces CKTN, the first corpus and benchmark for Vietnam's Cham, Khmer and Tay-Nung minority languages (44K documents, 24M subword tokens), covering continued pretraining, classification and retrieval. It shows existing multilingual encoders fragment these languages and that common adaptation metrics can mislead.

entry.title: Do You Need a Frontier Model as a Citation Verifier? Benchmarking Rubric LLMs for Deep-Research Source Attribution

author.name: Ethan Leung, Elias Lumer, Corey Feld, Austin Huber, Vamse Kumar Subbiah, Kevin Paul

entry.summary: Benchmarks whether a frontier model is necessary to verify citations in deep-research systems, scoring eight off-the-shelf LLM judges from three families against gold labels on 1,248 rubric decisions for source relevance and factual support. It studies how capable and biased a reward-model judge must be for RL training on citation quality.

entry.title: The complexities of patient-centred conversational artificial intelligence

author.name: Jo\~ao Matos, Olivia Buege, Donny Cheung, Gary S. Collins, Paula Dhiman, Nan Li, Bingyu Mao, Benjamin W. Nelson, Michail Ouroutzoglou, Paul Varghese, Jonathan Amar

entry.summary: Analyzes 2,053 real patient-chatbot conversations to show wide variation in communication and emotional expression, then builds a patient simulator modeling clinical content, emotional state, conversational strategy and style separately. In a Turing-inspired evaluation, 15 human graders distinguished simulated from real conversations at only 55% accuracy.

entry.title: LEEVLA: Seeing What Matters in Latent Environment Evolution for Vision-Language-Action

author.name: Qi Lyu, Baicheng Liu, Xudong Wang, Jiahua Dong, Lianqing Liu, Zhi Han

entry.summary: LEEVLA is a vision-language-action architecture that guides robot policies toward task-critical visual regions using drift-guided dynamic prioritization while preserving a structured latent world representation. It aims to handle complex dynamic scenes better than approaches that treat all visual tokens uniformly.

entry.title: WCog-VLA: A Dual-Level World-Cognitive Vision-Language-Action Model for End-to-End Autonomous Driving

author.name: Xuerun Yan, Zhexi Lian, Nuoheng Zhang, Shiyu Fang, Haoran Wang, Chen Lv, Jia Hu, Binyang Song

entry.summary: WCog-VLA is a dual-level vision-language-action framework for end-to-end autonomous driving that combines semantic world forecasting (with 3D perception and game-theoretic chain-of-thought) and generative world evolution to enable proactive rather than reactive driving. It targets richer world cognition for driving policies.

entry.title: Track2Map: Online Deformable SLAM with Motion-Aware Pose Optimization in Robotic Surgery

author.name: Tianyi Song, Sierra Bonilla, Xinwei Ju, Evangelos Mazomenos, Danail Stoyanov, Adam Schmidt, Omid Mohareri, Sophia Bano, Francisco Vasconcelos

entry.summary: Track2Map is an online 3D Gaussian splatting SLAM pipeline for robotic surgery that jointly optimizes camera trajectory and deformable scene reconstruction directly from video, removing dependence on accurate kinematic priors. It enables robust reconstruction when trajectory priors are missing or noisy.

entry.title: SAM-MT: Real-Time Interactive Multi-Target Video Segmentation

author.name: Ruiqi Shen, Chang Liu, Henghui Ding

entry.summary: SAM-MT extends Segment Anything 2 into a real-time interactive multi-target video segmentation framework using per-target explicit queries plus a shared global-context representation and decoupled masked attention. It avoids the frame-rate collapse of running single-target models per object.

entry.title: Physics-Guided Biomechanical Gait Adaptation for Humanoid Locomotion on Extreme Sloped Terrains

author.name: Xuanyu Chen, Mohan Liu, Dengchen Mei, Zhihao Gu, Haitian Zhang, Kaimin Mao, Haiyue Zhu, Shijun Yan, Lin Wang

entry.summary: HumoSlope is a two-stage physics-guided reinforcement learning framework for humanoid locomotion on steep slopes, using a slope-adaptive Zero Moment Point regularizer to establish a terrain-consistent balance prior and avoid conservative crouched gaits. It targets the largely unexplored problem of control on steep inclines.

entry.title: Harness VLA: Steering Frozen VLAs into Reliable Manipulation Primitives via Memory-Guided Agents

author.name: Yixian Zhang, Huanming Zhang, Feng Gao, Xiao Li, Zhihao Liu, Chunyang Zhu, Jiaxing Qiu, Yuchen Yan, Jiyuan Liu, Wenhao Tang, Zhengru Fang, Yi Nie, Changxu Wei, Yu Wang, Wenbo Ding, Chao Yu

entry.summary: Harness VLA is a memory-augmented agentic framework that exposes a frozen vision-language-action model as a retryable contact-rich primitive steered by LLM coding agents, combining local visuomotor skills with compositional reasoning to survive deployment perturbations like semantic retargeting and layout shifts. It targets robustness gaps of end-to-end VLAs.

entry.title: Learning Adaptive Solvers for Distributed Factor Graph Optimization on Matrix Lie Groups

author.name: Jaeho Shin, Maani Ghaffari, Yulun Tian

entry.summary: DeepCORD is a learning-augmented framework for distributed factor graph optimization on general matrix Lie groups that unfolds a parallel accelerated Riemannian optimizer into differentiable iterations and learns a self-supervised feedback policy to adapt solver parameters by optimization phase and communication status. It generalizes beyond rigid-body pose graphs and supports synchronous and asynchronous settings.

entry.title: ContactMimic: Humanoid Object Interaction via Contact Control

author.name: Xinyao Li, Xialin He, Runpei Dong, Saurabh Gupta

entry.summary: ContactMimic is a learning framework for humanoid object interaction that tracks explicit part-level binary contact commands alongside keypoint trajectories, using contact-following rewards and trajectory augmentation to decouple contact behavior from keypoint geometry. It enables precise physical contact and contact-controllability for tasks like sitting, wiping, and pushing.

entry.title: Your Prompt-Injection Defense Metric Might Be Lying to You

author.name: sahilraut

entry.summary: Independent research proposing a benchmark for indirect prompt-injection defenses that scores clean refusal and resistance rather than relying on potentially gameable LLM-judge success metrics used by existing benchmarks. It draws on the author's jailbreak dataset from a Gray Swan challenge and releases code.

entry.title: Imputation Meets Clustering: Exploiting Latent Subgroup Structure for Missing Data Recovery

author.name: Chuyao Zhang, E Li, Taochen Chen, Yiqun Zhang, Yuzhu Ji, Shuping Zhao, Peng Liu, Yiu-ming Cheung

entry.summary: CAGI is a cluster-aware generative imputation method that jointly recovers missing data while modeling latent subgroup structure, resolving the circular dependency between clustering and completion. It targets heterogeneous real-world datasets where generic imputation blurs subgroup boundaries.

entry.title: End-to-End LLM Flight Planning with RAG-based Memory and Multi-modal Coach Agent

author.name: Amin Tabrizian, Arsyi Aziz, Aarifah Ullah, Mahyar Ghazanfari, Pouria Razzaghi, Peng Wei

entry.summary: FRAMe is an end-to-end LLM flight-planning tool for eVTOL aircraft combining a planner LLM, a multi-modal coach agent, and retrieval-augmented memory to align flight plans with operator preferences. It is demonstrated across scenarios of varying difficulty.

entry.title: Physics-guided spatiotemporal neural models for fuel density prediction

author.name: Tolga Caglar, Jaynil Jaiswal, Saqib Azim, Yudhir Gala, Mai H. Nguyen, Ilkay Altintas

entry.summary: A physics-guided ML framework predicts fuel density for wildfire modeling by embedding mass-conservation and rate-of-spread constraints into the loss across ConvLSTM, AFNO, and ViViT architectures. It outperforms purely data-driven baselines.

entry.title: On the Principles of Deep Feedforward ReLU Networks

author.name: Changcun Huang

entry.summary: This theoretical paper analyzes the mechanism of deep feedforward ReLU networks, using the concept of paths and their relationships to explain solutions found by backpropagation. It argues units form piecewise-linear manifolds partitioning the input space.

entry.title: Scalable and Trustworthy Earth Observation Foundation Models

author.name: Syed Usama Imtiaz, Mitra Nasr Azadani, Nasrin Alamdari

entry.summary: A review chapter on Earth observation foundation models that synthesizes design principles arising from measurement physics and operational constraints, arguing remote sensing foundation models need domain-specific adaptation to transfer reliably. It covers scalability and trustworthiness considerations for satellite and airborne data.

entry.title: Stochastic Order Learning: An Approach to Rank Estimation Using Noisy Data

author.name: Chaewon Lee, Seon-Ho Lee, Chang-Su Kim

entry.summary: Reformulates rank estimation under noisy ordinal labels as a stochastic ordering problem where each instance maps to multiple plausible ranks, and proposes stochastic order learning with a discriminative loss and a stochastic order loss. It captures structured ordinal label uncertainty rather than treating noise as simple corruption.

entry.title: Contrastive Order Learning: A General Framework for Ordinal Regression

author.name: Chaewon Lee, BeomJun Shim, Kwang Pyo Choi, Chang-Su Kim

entry.summary: Introduces contrastive order learning (ConOrd) for ordinal regression, combining contrastive learning's use of all batch samples with explicit modeling of rank ordinality via soft affinity and disparity weights based on rank differences. It aims to capture global ordinal structure across all sample pairs.

entry.title: DeepPySR -- A Symbolic Regression Framework with Dynamic Pruning, Pareto Selection, and Hierarchical Composition for Real-World Scientific Discovery

author.name: Fuling Chen, Kevin Vinsen, Phillip Melton, Rae-Chi Huang

entry.summary: DeepPySR is a symbolic regression framework adding dynamic variable pruning, an exponential Pareto selection criterion, and hierarchical multi-layer composition to produce interpretable equations from high-dimensional, irregular data. It targets glass-box modeling for clinical and social science discovery.

entry.title: Systematic Evaluation of Learning Rate Scheduling Strategies Across Heterogeneous Architectures

author.name: Hafsa Mateen, Radu Timofte, Dmitry Ignatov

entry.summary: A systematic evaluation of 25 learning-rate scheduler configurations across nine PyTorch families on 30 architectures and nearly 4,000 model variants on CIFAR-10, using automated source-code injection. It quantifies how much scheduler choice affects classification accuracy across heterogeneous architectures.

entry.title: CAAD: Causality-Aware Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection via Multi-Scale Alignment and Structural Causal Consistency

author.name: Xin Wang, Yunshi Wen, Yanan He, Haotian Xu, Youlan Zhao, Michel Ferreira Cardia Haddad, Tengfei Ma

entry.summary: CAAD reframes multivariate time series anomaly detection as continuous verification of Granger causality consistency, modeling exogenous variables as residuals and flagging significant deviations from external interventions. It uses multi-scale alignment and structural causal consistency to catch failures that disrupt internal causal relationships.

entry.title: The Regularization Parameter: Sparse Precision Matrix Estimation

author.name: Aryan Eftekhari, Daniel Sergio Vega, Ernst-Jan Camiel Wit, Olaf Schenk

entry.summary: The authors derive a closed-form, matrix-valued regularization parameter for L1-penalized Gaussian precision matrix estimation from the sampling distribution of the optimality conditions, eliminating cross-validation. It targets principled sparsity selection in high-dimensional, low-sample settings.

entry.title: ConRad: Efficient Conformal Prediction for Radiomics

author.name: Matt Y. Cheung, Ashok Veeraraghavan, Guha Balakrishnan

entry.summary: ConRad is a conformal-prediction framework for scalar radiomic targets computed from predicted segmentation masks, using covariates from image appearance, mask geometry, and segmentation uncertainty to produce more efficient distribution-free prediction intervals. It addresses overconfident, poorly calibrated derived clinical measurements.

entry.title: Tubular Neighbourhoods of Pfaffian Sets and Applications to Neural Networks

author.name: Paul Lezeau, Martin Lotz

entry.summary: This paper derives volume bounds for tubular neighborhoods of smooth Pfaffian hypersurfaces, generalizing algebraic-variety results, and applies them to tail bounds on a condition-number robustness measure for neural network classifiers with Pfaffian activations. It yields polynomial-in-width bounds for single-hidden-layer sigmoid networks.

entry.title: XALPHA: A Memory-Driven AI Quant Researcher for Hypothesis-to-Code Alpha Discovery

author.name: Fengyuan Liu, Yuchen Fu, Yuqi Wang, Qi Liu

entry.summary: Introduces XAlpha, a memory-driven LLM agent that functions as an end-to-end quant researcher, closing the hypothesis-to-code validation loop and learning from accumulated discovery feedback for trading-signal alpha discovery. It goes beyond automating isolated factor-generation steps.

entry.title: Detecting Ladder Logic Bombs in IEC 61131-3 PLC Programs using ESBMC-PLC+: A Formal Verification Approach with Trigger Synthesis

author.name: Pierre Dantas, Lucas Cordeiro, Waldir Junior

entry.summary: Presents ESBMC-LLB, a formal-verification approach that detects Ladder Logic Bombs in IEC 61131-3 PLC programs by exposing function-block logic and recasting bomb detection as safety and non-termination verification with trigger synthesis. It targets malicious dormant control logic invisible to prior verifiers.

entry.title: DominoTree: Conditional Tree-Structured Drafting with Domino for Speculative Decoding

author.name: Saw S. Lin (Zhiqi Zhang), Jyh-Shing Roger Jang

entry.summary: Presents DominoTree, a training-free best-first draft-tree method for speculative decoding that scores candidates using Domino's conditional, path-dependent correction rather than factorized per-position marginals. It restricts per-node correction to a top-M candidate set for practicality and is evaluated on Qwen3-4B.

entry.title: Improving Ad-hoc Search Effectiveness for Conversational Information Retrieval via Model Merging

author.name: Ahmed Rayane Kebir, Jose G. Moreno, Lynda Tamine

entry.summary: Introduces model merging as a training-free strategy to build a single retrieval model that handles both ad-hoc and conversational information retrieval, avoiding costly retraining and catastrophic forgetting of foundational retrieval ability. It targets topic shifts and coreference across conversation turns.

entry.title: Beyond Thermal Imaging: Inferring Thermophysical Properties from Time-Resolved Thermal Observations

author.name: Chenghao Xu, Malcolm Mielle, Olga Fink

entry.summary: Proposes inferring spatially resolved thermophysical properties from time-resolved thermal observations, going beyond temperature-field reconstruction by leveraging heat-transfer physics encoded in thermal evolution. It targets applications from digital twins and infrastructure monitoring to robotics and scientific imaging.

entry.title: SAGA: Stable Acceleration Guidance for Autoregressive Video Generation

author.name: Thanh-Nhan Vo, Trong-Thuan Nguyen, Trung-Hoang Le, Tam V. Nguyen, Minh-Triet Tran

entry.summary: Proposes SAGA, a training-free stable acceleration guidance for autoregressive video generation that analyzes discrete latent acceleration as a spectral signal to detect and suppress unstable high-frequency temporal perturbations causing flicker, jitter and drift. It uses a Slepian-based acceleration-domain spectral guidance objective.

entry.title: Post-Training in End-to-End Autonomous Driving

author.name: Ruining Yang, Muxing Wang, Yixiao Chen, Tongfei Guo, Yi Xu, Can Cui, Zichong Yang, Yitian Zhang, Ziran Wang, Yun Fu, Lili Su

entry.summary: Surveys and analyzes post-training in end-to-end autonomous driving, covering vision-language-action models and trajectory-generative planners, and argues that open-loop imitation is insufficient for safety-critical, interaction-intensive environments where errors accumulate and recovery data is scarce. It motivates post-training approaches for long-horizon safety and comfort objectives.

entry.title: TMI: Text-to-Image Meets Image-to-Image for Complementary Data Synthesis to Boost Long-Tailed Instance Segmentation

author.name: Hyeonseop Song, Seokhun Choi, Hoseok Do

entry.summary: TMI couples text-to-image generation with context-aware image-to-image editing to synthesize training data for long-tailed instance segmentation, using a teacher-student scheme to keep labels reliable. It aims to combine diversity from T2I with contextual realism.

entry.title: SkelGen4D: Weakly-Supervised Skeleton-Based 4D Generation for Text-Driven Mesh Animation

author.name: Hao Feng, Zhi Zuo, Jia-Hui Pan, Ka-Hei Hui, Zhengzhe Liu, Dian Zhang, Haoran Xie, Bin Sheng, Jingyu Hu

entry.summary: SkelGen4D is a weakly-supervised feed-forward framework for text-driven mesh animation that generates explicit skeleton motions without per-frame skeleton annotations, aligning with standard industry animation pipelines. It first recovers pseudo-skeletons then generates text-conditioned motion.

entry.title: On the Design of Mixture-of-Experts for Dynamic Gaussian Splatting

author.name: In-Hwan Jin, Hyeongju Mun, Joonsoo Kim, Kugjin Yun, Kyeongbo Kong

entry.summary: This paper studies mixture-of-experts designs for dynamic Gaussian splatting, treating multi-deformation modeling as combining deformation experts under different integration constraints. It aims to improve robustness across diverse real-world motion in dynamic scene reconstruction.

entry.title: VEGAS: Human-Aligned Video Caption Evaluation via Gaze

author.name: Shenghui Chen, Po-han Li, Ximeng Sun, Shijia Yang, Emad Barsoum, Zicheng Liu, Sandeep Chinchali, Ufuk Topcu

entry.summary: VEGAS is a training-free, information-theoretic metric that uses test-time gaze to select personalized, attention-aligned video captions via rejection sampling, without model retraining. A curated egocentric gaze dataset supports evaluation.

entry.title: Whareformer: Learning to Track What is Where in Long Egocentric Videos

author.name: Jacob Chalk, Saptarshi Sinha, Dima Damen, Yannis Kalantidis, Diane Larlus

entry.summary: Whareformer is the first learning-based solution to the OSNOM task of tracking objects moved by an egocentric camera wearer, maintaining instance locations even through occlusion or leaving view. It jointly reasons over appearance and 3D location with a memory of tracks and a new-track token.

entry.title: OPSD-V: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Post-Training Few-Step Autoregressive Video Generators

author.name: Hongyu Liu, Chun Wang, Feng Gao, Xuanhua He, Yue Ma, Ziyu Wan, Yong Zhang, Xiaoming Wei, Qifeng Chen

entry.summary: OPSD-V is an on-policy self-distillation method for post-training few-step autoregressive video diffusion models, using real long-video data as temporal context and dense trajectory-level supervision to reduce long-horizon error accumulation while preserving fast inference. It targets degradation during long autoregressive rollouts.

entry.title: AnyDexRT: Calibration-Free Dexterous Hand Retargeting with Few-Shot Human Guidance

author.name: Chenxi Wang, Ying Feng, Hongjie Fang, Shangning Xia, Lixin Yang, Chuan Wen, Cewu Lu

entry.summary: AnyDexRT is a calibration-free dexterous hand retargeting method that combines self-supervised fingertip correspondence learning with few-shot human guidance to map operator hand motions to diverse robot hands without hand-crafted objectives or precise calibration. It targets intuitive teleoperation across different dexterous hands.

entry.title: SkillPlug: Unsupervised Skill Mining for Few-Shot Adaptation in Robotic Manipulation

author.name: Zi-han Ding, Ziwei Wang

entry.summary: SkillPlug is a plug-in framework that augments an existing visuomotor policy with a skill-conditioning module and mines a shared, transferable skill library from raw multi-task demonstrations via self-supervised objectives promoting compact, reusable behaviors. It targets data-efficient few-shot adaptation to new manipulation tasks.

entry.title: Early to Share, Late to Save: Synchronisation-Driven Communication Gating in Bandwidth-Constrained Cooperative VLN

author.name: Arav Gupta, Nivedan Yakolli, Avinash Gautam

entry.summary: This work introduces bandwidth-constrained cooperative vision-language navigation and proposes hindsight gating, a lightweight supervised gate that labels communication-critical steps post-hoc from navigation failures. It reports a counter-intuitive finding that trained gates fire mostly early in episodes and when agents are confident.

entry.title: Because 8 ≈ e², Anthropic's researcher uplift is plausibly >2x

author.name: Thomas Kwa

entry.summary: A METR-affiliated modeling exercise arguing that Anthropic's reported 8x increase in code merged per day (Q2 2026 vs 2021-2024) implies, under Cobb-Douglas-style economic assumptions, a serial researcher productivity uplift plausibly exceeding 2x from coding agents alone. The author flags the assumptions as personal and contested internally.

entry.title: Transformers Resist Their Own Architecture

author.name: Zach Baker

entry.summary: The first in a series comparing a mathematical theory of attention (Geshkovski et al.) to trained transformers, testing what happens to token-cluster formation when Q/K/V matrices are random or learned rather than identity. It explores how architecture-induced clustering interacts with training, with code released.

entry.title: Large Behavior Model: A Promptable Digital Twin of the Retail Customer

author.name: Wachiravit Modecrua, Krittin Pachtrachai, Touchapon Kraisingkorn

entry.summary: The Large Behavioral Model learns retail customer decision-making from large-scale transactions using a Person-Environment formulation, with behavioral profiles plus retrieval-augmented product context. It trains through continued pretraining, supervised fine-tuning, and RL with verifiable rewards to create a promptable digital twin of shoppers.

entry.title: Do LLM-Generated Skills Make Better AI Data Scientists? A Component Ablation Across Data-Science Workflows

author.name: Wei-Jung Huang

entry.summary: This ablation study tests whether LLM-generated reusable skill files improve data-science agent performance over the task prompt alone across four lifecycle stages. It reports no reliable gain from a single generated skill per stage, questioning the low-curation skill hypothesis.

entry.title: NEST: Tackling Dataset-Level Distribution Shifts via Regime-Oriented Mixture-of-Experts

author.name: Lanhao Li, Bingshu Xie, Lijun Sun, Xin Xue, Haoyi Zhou, Jianxin Li

entry.summary: NEST is a two-phase dense Mixture-of-Experts framework for long-term time-series forecasting under dataset-level distribution shifts, partitioning data into operational regimes via unsupervised clustering before recomposing them. It addresses global structural shifts that local-shift methods miss.

entry.title: Computing with Stochastic Oracles in AI-Augmented Computation

author.name: Jie Wang

entry.summary: SpaCellAgent is an autonomous LLM multi-agent framework automating end-to-end spatial and single-cell trajectory inference, with strategic workflow planning, dynamic tool orchestration, and a self-evolution module. It targets the manual-intervention barrier in transcriptomics trajectory analysis.

entry.title: Complexity-Budgeted, Interaction-Aware Interpretable Model for Tabular Data

author.name: Srikumar Krishnamoorthy

entry.summary: IAIML is an inherently interpretable tabular classifier that recovers predictive variables whose value emerges only through feature interactions, via adaptive discretization, pairwise interaction scoring, and a partitioned explanation budget. It addresses the blind spot of marginal feature screening.

entry.title: Navigating Hierarchy: Hyperbolic Learning on Brain Graphs for Disorder Diagnosis

author.name: Yapeng Li, Bo Jiang, Ziyan Zhang, Dongdong Chen, Zhengzheng Tu

entry.summary: HLBG applies hyperbolic learning to functional brain graphs to capture their hierarchical organization across region, community, and whole-brain levels for disorder diagnosis. It aims to better model ROI-community interactions than existing graph methods.

entry.title: Bayesian Optimization of Genetic Algorithm Hyperparameters in a Multi-Fidelity Framework for Efficient Lattice Material Design

author.name: Sergei Zorkaltsev, Maciej Haranczyk, Christina Schenk

entry.summary: This paper presents a multi-fidelity framework using Bayesian optimization to tune genetic-algorithm hyperparameters for lattice material design, combining FFT homogenization, a 3D CNN surrogate, and a Gaussian-process surrogate. A noise-aware acquisition function enables efficient short GA runs.

entry.title: CarbonCLIP: Enhance Carbon Prediction from Satellite Imagery via Integrated Street-View Semantics and Temporal Context Training

author.name: Zeru Yang, Fang-Ying Gong, Steve H. L. Yim, Chau Yuen

entry.summary: CarbonCLIP is a multimodal distillation framework that improves satellite-based urban carbon-emission prediction by transferring street-view semantics and temporal context into a unified satellite representation via dual-branch contrastive learning. It bridges top-down satellite views with ground-level human activity.

entry.title: Multimodal Voice Activity Projection for Turn-Taking in Social Robots with Voice-Activity-Related Pretrained Encoders

author.name: Antonio Cano, Guillermo P\'erez, Luis Merino, Randy Gomez

entry.summary: MM-VAP extends audio-only voice-activity projection to synchronized audio-visual inputs for turn-taking prediction in social robots, adapting pretrained speech backbones via low-rank adaptation with inter-speaker attention. It targets mediator-style human-robot interaction.

entry.title: HAJJv2-CrowdCount: Zero-Shot Benchmark for Dense Crowd Counting

author.name: Reem AlYabis, Fares AlTuwaim, AlJawharh AlOtaibi, Mohamed Eltahir

entry.summary: HAJJv2-CrowdCount adds per-second human-annotated crowd counts to the HAJJv2 dataset and benchmarks three zero-shot counting paradigms on extremely dense, steep-angle, heavily occluded Hajj footage. It exposes limitations of current counting models under these challenging conditions.

entry.title: Towards Agentic AI Governance: A Preliminary Assessment

author.name: Mubarak Raji, Masooda Bashir

entry.summary: This paper presents a systematic review of the emerging literature on agentic AI governance, identifying features that distinguish agentic systems from traditional AI and synthesizing governance priorities, mechanisms, and stakeholder roles. It offers preliminary groundwork toward a governance roadmap.

entry.title: QCNN with Rough Path Signature Kernels

author.name: Leonardo Nogueira Falabella, Vasily Sazonov

entry.summary: This paper proposes a hybrid quantum-classical architecture for time-series classification that integrates quantum neural networks with path-signature kernels to mitigate the time-reparameterization invariance of temporal data. It uses feature layers computing signature kernels between input pairs.

entry.title: ALER-TI: Aligned Latent Embedding Retrieval for Time Series Imputation

author.name: Xuan-Thong Truong, Trung-Kien Le, Tung Kieu, Thi-Thu Nguyen, Nhat-Hai Nguyen

entry.summary: ALER-TI is a retrieval-augmented time-series imputation framework that supplements degraded local context with historical patterns via latent embedding alignment, targeting non-stationary series with weak temporal correlations. It moves beyond reliance on localized context within the corrupted sequence.

entry.title: Physics-Informed Machine Learning Under Small-Data Constraints: Lessons from Abrasive Waterjet Milling

author.name: Sarah Grewe, J\"org Frochte

entry.summary: Uses a small abrasive waterjet milling dataset to make methodological points about physics-informed ML in small-data regimes, separating physics-based cleaning from statistical curation and showing model rankings are unstable under different validation schemes. It highlights that data curation and evaluation design can matter as much as the learning algorithm.

entry.title: Ensemble Diversity Optimization for Subjective Supervision

author.name: Xia Cui, Ziyi Huang, N. R. Abeynayake

entry.summary: Ensemble Diversity Optimization jointly optimizes ensemble weights, effective cardinality, and calibration through a differentiable objective for subjective NLP tasks with annotator disagreement. A signed diversity regularizer lets it either preserve or suppress disagreement, preventing ensemble collapse.

entry.title: Federated Deep Learning for Privacy-Preserving Cardiovascular Disease Risk Prediction

author.name: Hyunho Mo, Djura Smits, Mahlet A. Birhanu, Maarten J. G. Leening, Daniel Bos, Pim van der Harst, Esther E. Bron
