entry.summary: Presents a federated deep learning approach for privacy-preserving cardiovascular disease risk prediction integrating two large population cohorts with different sizes, populations, and outcome definitions. It enables collaborative model development without transferring patient-level data across institutions.

entry.title: Steering Neural Network Training through Interpretable Constraints Based on Partial Dependence

author.name: Yann Claes, Pierre Geurts, V\^an Anh Huynh-Thu

entry.summary: This paper introduces an explanation-guided learning method that steers neural network training using partial dependence, aligning a model's average response with prior domain knowledge and extending beyond the usual classification-only focus. It contributes to making models faithful to expected feature effects rather than only interpretable after the fact.

entry.title: EdgeRefine: Privacy-Utility Balance for Graphs via Jaccard Sampling under Edge Differential Privacy

author.name: Wenxiu Ding, Muzhi Liu, Zheng Yan, Mingjun Wang, Yifan Zhao, Qiao Liu

entry.summary: EdgeRefine is a local differential privacy framework for graph learning that uses adaptive Jaccard-based edge refinement to improve the privacy-utility trade-off when protecting sensitive link structure in GNNs. It tackles the noise-versus-accuracy tension in edge-level differential privacy.

entry.title: MPFlow: Learning Budgeted Max-Flow Optimization on the Lightning Network with Deep Graph Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Harrison Rush, Vincent Davis, Simone Antonelli, Vikash Singh, Jesse Shrader, Emanuele Rossi

entry.summary: MPFlow uses graph reinforcement learning with a message-passing policy and a hub-exclusion curriculum to place liquidity channels on the Bitcoin Lightning Network under a fixed budget, maximizing s-t max-flow routing capacity. It applies combinatorial graph optimization to a real crypto-networking problem.

entry.title: RadioDiff-v2: Generative Angular Radio Maps for Multi-Beam Selection and Localization

author.name: Xiucheng Wang, Junxi Huang, Nan Cheng

entry.summary: RadioDiff-v2 is a dual-branch one-dimensional diffusion transformer trained with flow matching that predicts angular power spectra for 6G beam selection and localization, treating the ill-posed non-line-of-sight mapping as a perception-distortion problem. It avoids the over-smoothing of conditional-mean regressors that erases multipath structure.

entry.title: Securing Autonomous Vehicle Systems via Twin-Aware Federated Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Zifan Zhang, Minghong Fang, Dianwei Chen, Zhuqing Liu, Prashant Khanduri, Xianfeng Yang, Anupam Das, Yuchen Liu

entry.summary: This paper proposes a secure aggregation scheme with poisoning prevention and historical reinforcement (a twin-aware federated reinforcement learning framework) to defend autonomous-vehicle control models against parameter-injection poisoning attacks. It addresses an underexplored security vulnerability in safety-critical FRL.

entry.title: On Exploring Input Resolution Scaling For Anytime LiDAR Object Detection

author.name: Ahmet Soyyigit, Shuochao Yao, Heechul Yun

entry.summary: This paper enables anytime computing for LiDAR 3D object detection by allowing multi-resolution inference within a single deployed model that processes point clouds as pillars or voxels, dynamically scaling input resolution to meet timing constraints without deploying multiple trained models. It targets latency-utility trade-offs for cyber-physical systems.

entry.title: Validating LLMs in social science: Epistemic threats and emerging norms

author.name: Meera Desai, Dallas Card, Abigail Z. Jacobs

entry.summary: Systematically analyzes validation practices in a corpus of papers from eight flagship social-science journals that use LLMs as measurement instruments, finding LLM-generated measurements often play central roles yet validation is inconsistent. It surfaces epistemic threats and emerging norms for methodologically sound use.

entry.title: Do Egocentric Video-Language Models Capture Both Hand- and Object-Centric Cues?

author.name: Masatoshi Tateno, Alexandros Stergiou, Risa Shinoda, Yoichi Sato, Dima Damen

entry.summary: This paper investigates whether egocentric video-language models rely on spurious correlations rather than genuine hand and object reasoning, and proposes hand-object masked training plus an HOI-dynamics-aware decoder to strengthen both cue types. It targets more robust hand-object interaction recognition.

entry.title: Do Transformations Reveal the Truth? Generative Residual Learning for Generalized AI-Generated Image Detection

author.name: Kutub Uddin, Nusrat Tasnim, Awais Khan, Mohammad Umar Farooq, Khalid Malik

entry.summary: GenRes proposes generative residual learning via a neural tensor network to detect AI-generated images by modeling fine-grained relational features between original and transformed samples, with GenRes++ adding attention to aggregate across multiple transformations. It aims for generalizable deepfake image detection.

entry.title: HumanForge: A Human-Centric Deepfake Video Benchmark with Multi-Agent Forgery Rationales

author.name: Wenbo Xu, Zhimin Chen, Xiaojie Liang, Hengrui Liu, Wei Lu

entry.summary: HumanForge is a large-scale multi-paradigm human-centric deepfake video benchmark covering human-object and human-human interactions with multi-agent-generated forgery rationales, built via an automated LangGraph pipeline. It fills gaps left by face-swap-only or global text-to-video forensic datasets.

entry.title: Wat3R: Underwater 3D Geometry Learning without Annotations

author.name: Jiangwei Ren, Xingyu Jiang, Zijie Song, Wei Xu, Hongkai Lin, Dingkang Liang, Xiang Bai

entry.summary: Wat3R is a cross-domain semi-supervised framework that adapts feed-forward 3D reconstruction models from air to underwater scenes without any annotated underwater data, using a teacher-student setup on unlabeled real footage and a cross-view consistency loss. It tackles light attenuation, scattering, and annotation scarcity underwater.

entry.title: FSD-VLN: Fast-Slow Dual-System Modeling for Aerial Long-Horizon Vision-Language Navigation

author.name: Xueke Zhu, Qingyan Meng, Liutao Yu, Wei Zhang, Zhengyu Ma, Huihui Zhou, Yonghong Tian

entry.summary: FSD-VLN is a fast-slow dual-system architecture for aerial long-horizon vision-language navigation that disentangles high-level semantic reasoning from low-latency flight control to reduce jittery trajectories and decision latency. It targets structural misalignment between global understanding and sequential action in UAV navigation.

entry.title: Persistent Latent Misalignment, a new dimension of misalignment?

author.name: Florian_Dietz

entry.summary: A commentary flagging an ICML paper on Latent Collaboration in Multi-Agent Systems (LatentMAS), where agents share latent states directly instead of text for efficiency, and arguing this could let misaligned plans propagate hidden between agents. The author frames latent inter-agent communication as a potential new dimension of alignment risk.

entry.title: AT-Attn: Temporal-Aware Cross-Attention for Longitudinal Multimodal Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis

author.name: Xinyue Du, Yibo Liu, Zhenglei Zhou, Xuancheng Yao, Weimin Zhong, Qiuhui Chen

entry.summary: AT-Attn is a temporal-aware multimodal framework combining change-and-time encoding, time-biased asymmetric cross-attention, and gated fusion to integrate irregular MRI and clinical trajectories for longitudinal Alzheimer's diagnosis. It is evaluated on an ADNI cohort of over 1,500 patients.

entry.title: Graph-Regularized Deep Learning for EEG-Based Emotion Recognition with Psychologically-Grounded Label Structure

author.name: Dongyang Kuang, Zizheng Ma, Yushan Zhang, Xiaocong Zeng

entry.summary: Proposes a graph-regularized deep learning framework for EEG emotion recognition where emotions are nodes with edges encoding proximity from dimensional emotion theories, and applies three regularization strategies including graph label smoothing and optimal-transport-based losses. It aims to exploit psychological interdependencies among emotion classes.

entry.title: Cross-Modal Generative Framework for Signal Translation from Fetal-Maternal Electrocardiograms to Fetal Doppler Waveforms

author.name: Tongli Su, Alireza Rafiei, Marly van Assen, Reza Sameni, Gari D. Clifford, Faezeh Marzbanrad, Nasim Katebi

entry.summary: Proposes a cross-modal generative framework to translate fetal-maternal electrocardiograms into fetal Doppler waveforms, probing which Doppler components are recoverable from electrical signals and incorporating maternal-fetal cardiac coupling. It aims to inform clinical understanding of electrical versus mechanical contributions in fetal circulation.

entry.title: An interpretable Good--Turing restart criterion for k-means++

author.name: Renato Cordeiro de Amorim

entry.summary: Introduces GTRC, an interpretable restart criterion for k-means++ that combines a Good-Turing estimate with an unconditional bound and a confidence-based bound on whether further restarts would improve results, stopping below a user tolerance. Across 36 datasets it matches well-chosen fixed restart counts while adapting compute to data difficulty.

entry.title: Self-Adaptive Anomaly Detection with Reinforcement Learning and Human Feedback in Connected Vehicles

author.name: Matthias Wei{\ss}, Athreya Hosahalli Prakash, Maurice Artelt, Falk Dettinger, Nasser Jazdi, Michael Weyrich

entry.summary: Presents an online anomaly detection framework for connected vehicles combining a factorized deep model, reinforcement learning, and human feedback to adapt to evolving normal behavior from over-the-air updates and workload shifts. It integrates automated model adaptation and operator input in a single supervisory loop.

entry.title: Frequency-Domain Multi-Modality Transportation Modeling

author.name: Jiewen Deng, Hangchen Liu, Junchen Li, Boyuan Zhang, Renhe Jiang

entry.summary: FreMo is a lightweight frequency-domain framework for multi-modality transportation forecasting that disentangles modality-wise spectral representations to enable adaptive, selective cross-modality synergy. It addresses that different transport modes exhibit distinct spectral characteristics and interact unevenly across frequencies.

entry.title: Secure Decentralized Federated Learning via Gossip and Virtual Voting

author.name: Amirhossein Taherpour, Xiaodong Wang

entry.summary: gspDAG-FL is a decentralized federated learning framework that derives consensus directly from the gossip history used to spread model updates, providing Byzantine resilience and provenance without a blockchain or central coordinator. It addresses auditability and robustness in peer-to-peer training.

entry.title: Structured Pruning of Large Language Models via Power Transformation and Sign-Preserving Score Aggregation with Adaptive Feature Retention

author.name: Ryota Kobayashi, Tsubasa Hirakawa, Takayoshi Yamashita, Hironobu Fujiyoshi, Yasunori Ishii, Tomoyuki Okuno, Kazuki Kozuka

entry.summary: Proposes a structured pruning method for LLMs that adapts an unstructured Adaptive Feature Retention technique via power transformation for distribution alignment, sign-preserving score aggregation, and percentile outlier removal. Experiments on Llama-3-8B, Vicuna-13B and LLaVA-13B show maintained performance.

entry.title: Grounded Event Extraction from SEC 8-K Filings with a Fine-Grained Taxonomy

author.name: Rian Dolphin, Joe Dursun, Jarrett Blankenship, Katie Adams, Quinton Pike

entry.summary: Presents a two-stage system tagging SEC 8-K disclosures against a three-tier taxonomy of 119 event types, anchoring each tag to a verbatim quote via fuzzy n-gram validation and re-grading cited quotes for reliability. It enables fine-grained, source-traceable event labeling at corpus scale.

entry.title: Unified Face Attack Detection via Fine-Grained Semantic Guidance

author.name: Ning Jiang, Shijie Yu, Dingheng Zeng, Haiyang Yi, Yanhong Liu, Haifeng Shen, Ying Li

entry.summary: Builds on the large-scale MS-UFAD dataset (8M+ attack images) by enriching each with fine-grained textual descriptions of forgery cues and proposes a Dual Alignment Forgery Network to leverage this text for unified face attack detection. It moves face anti-spoofing beyond pure visual recognition toward semantically grounded forgery representations.

entry.title: Dual-Correlation Hypergraph Network for Unaligned RGBT Video Object Detection and A Large-scale Benchmark

author.name: Qishun Wang, Yapeng Li, Bin Luo, Zhengzheng Tu, Chenglong Li

entry.summary: DHNet uses a dual-correlation hypergraph network to fuse misaligned RGB and thermal video for object detection, modeling both temporal and cross-modal spatial correlations, and introduces a large-scale benchmark. It targets robust detection under challenging conditions where RGB alone fails.

entry.title: Unpaired Joint Distribution Modeling via Multi-Scale Image Representations

author.name: Yihang Zou, Hui Zhang, Zuowei Shen, Chenglong Bao

entry.summary: LUD-MSR is a latent-variable probabilistic framework for learning a joint distribution from only marginal observations, with a theoretical error bound and a multi-scale representation to balance domain consistency and information preservation. It addresses the ill-posed unpaired joint-modeling problem.

entry.title: UniRef-UAV: A Multimodal Benchmark for Universal Referring in UAV Imagery

author.name: Haibin Tian, Huichao Xie, Xuelin Qian, Ruitao Lu, Junwei Han, Dingwen Zhang

entry.summary: UniRef-UAV is a multimodal benchmark for universal referring in drone imagery, supporting text-only, image-only, and multimodal queries with multiple or absent targets, going beyond single-object text-only referring. It targets realistic UAV grounding scenarios.

entry.title: Attribute Retrieving for Open-Vocabulary Endoscopic Compositional Referring Segmentation

author.name: Shun Liu, Nan Xi, Yang Liu, Tianyu Luan, Xuan Gong, David Doermann

entry.summary: This work introduces ReferEndoscopy, a benchmark for referring image segmentation in endoscopy, and an attribute-retrieval-based method to capture fine-grained textual cues where general vision-language models fail. It targets open-vocabulary compositional referring in a data-scarce medical domain.

entry.title: VocaDet: Sample-Driven Open-Vocabulary Object Detection and Segmentation via Visual Tokenization and Vector Database Retrieval

author.name: ZhiXin Sun

entry.summary: VocaDet is a sample-driven open-vocabulary detection and segmentation framework that learns object concepts from user-provided positive and negative samples without retraining, converting continuous features into discrete visual vocabularies for efficient vector-database retrieval. It targets scalable, continuously expanding object repositories.

entry.title: HoloTetSphere: Unified TetSphere Mesh Reconstruction for Physical Simulations

author.name: YaQiao Dai, Renjiao Yi, Zhirui Gao, Wei Chen, Kai Xu, Chenyang Zhu

entry.summary: HoloTetSphere is a topology-adaptive framework for holistic tetrahedral mesh reconstruction that couples Gaussian spheres to tetrahedral elements and performs end-to-end topological and geometric optimization, producing a single connected simulation-ready mesh. It overcomes homeomorphic constraints that prior methods impose.

entry.title: AUTOPILOT VQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Incident-Centric Dashcam Understanding

author.name: Siddharth Damodharan, Radhika Gupta, Ali Alshami, Ryan Rabinowitz, Jugal Kalita

entry.summary: AUTOPILOT-VQA is an incident-centric visual question answering benchmark for dashcam video that tests whether vision-language and multimodal models can reason about safety-critical driving incidents and near-incidents across categories like weather, traffic environment, and road conditions. It targets a gap in reliable incident reasoning evaluation.

entry.title: Input-Constrained Spatiotemporal Tubes for Safe Navigation of Unknown Euler-Lagrange Systems in Dynamic Environments

author.name: Siddhartha Upadhyay, Ratnangshu Das, Pushpak Jagtap

entry.summary: This paper presents a real-time, approximation-free control framework for unknown Euler-Lagrange systems that guarantees finite-time reach-avoid-stay specifications while respecting actuator input limits, extending the spatiotemporal tube framework with offline-verifiable feasibility conditions. It targets safe navigation in dynamic environments without accurate models or online optimization.

entry.title: InductWave: Inductive Multi-Hop Logical Query Answering on Knowledge Graphs

author.name: Mayank Kharbanda, Michael Cochez, Rajiv Ratn Shah, Raghava Mutharaju

entry.summary: InductWave is a wavelet-based inductive embedding method for multi-hop logical query answering over knowledge graphs where test graphs contain entities unseen during training. It handles conjunction, disjunction, and negation while matching baselines under resource-scarce inductive settings.

entry.title: Audio Sentiment Analysis via Distillation and Cross-Modal Integration of Generated Multilingual Transcripts

author.name: Andrei-George Durdun, Victor Constantinescu, Radu Tudor Ionescu

entry.summary: This paper proposes a multimodal speech sentiment system that fuses audio with ASR-generated transcripts translated into multiple languages via machine translation, combined through cross-modal transformers and knowledge distillation. It aims to capture both vocal inflection and word meaning that audio-only foundation models may miss.

entry.title: Hybrid Least Squares/Gradient Descent Methods for MIONets

author.name: Jun Choi, Chang-Ock Lee, Minam Moon

entry.summary: This paper introduces a hybrid least-squares/gradient-descent training method for MIONets that treats the network as multilinear in last-layer branch parameters and uses alternating least squares with Kronecker and Khatri-Rao factorizations. It accelerates operator-network training.

entry.title: Latent graph encoding of multimodal neuroimaging features with generative AI architectures

author.name: Ishaan Batta, Meenu Ajith, Vince Calhoun

entry.summary: The paper builds a multimodal generative framework encoding structural and functional MRI features, systematically comparing VAEs, transformers, and other generative models for latent fusion of neuroimaging data. It aims to study brain structural and functional properties.

entry.title: FMMVCC: Fuzzy Mamba-based Multi-View Contrastive Clustering for Univariate Time Series

author.name: Donato Cerciello, Leonardo Schiavo, Angel Panizo-LLedot, Javier Huertas Tato, David Camacho

entry.summary: FMMVCC is a Mamba-based deep clustering framework for univariate time series that combines fuzzy assignment with multi-view contrastive learning to capture long-range dependencies at lower cost. It targets unsupervised organization of unlabeled time series.

entry.title: Distributed Sketching on Data Partitions for OLS Regression

author.name: Luyuan Yang, Brayden Garner, Shayan Shafaei, Chao Lan

entry.summary: Studies distributed sketching for ordinary least squares regression where small sketches of partitioned data subsets are averaged, deriving the exact excess loss under a fixed-design setting. It shows the loss is comparable to whole-dataset sketching when subset covariance divergence is small.

entry.title: RhyMix: A Lightweight Adaptive Multi-Rhythm Network for Long-Term Time Series Forecasting

author.name: Sumit Satishrao Shevtekar, Chandresh Kumar Maurya

entry.summary: RhyMix is a lightweight time series forecasting network using parallel dual-path modeling with adaptive gating to jointly capture short-term fluctuations, seasonal cycles, long-term trends, and abrupt changes. It combines complementary cyclic and other encoding branches to overcome single-path modeling limitations.

entry.title: A Self-Supervised Approach for Minimal-Annotation Hydroacoustic Data Exploration

author.name: Pierre-Yves Raumer, Axel Marmoret, Dorian Cazau, Anatole Gros-Martial, Richard Dreo, Maelle Torterotot, Sara Bazin, Flore Samaran, Jean-Yves Royer

entry.summary: This paper builds a self-supervised pipeline using a masked autoencoder pretrained on spectrograms to explore low-frequency passive hydroacoustic recordings with minimal annotation, aggregating patches into event-level embeddings for clustering. It reduces reliance on costly manual labels for rare bioacoustic signals.

entry.title: ProsMAE: Multi-Source MAE Pretraining for ISUP Grade Classification

author.name: Anna Jung, Kyeonghun Kim, Youngung Han, Eunseob Choi, Jiwon Yang, Ken Ying-Kai Liao, Hyuk-Jae Lee, Nam-Joon Kim

entry.summary: ProsMAE is a multi-source masked-autoencoder pretraining framework that exposes a histopathology encoder to diverse tissue morphology and acquisition conditions from PANDA, CAMELYON17, and BRACS, then transfers it for ISUP prostate-cancer grade classification. It aims for robust representations despite stain and scanner variation.

entry.title: Benchmark Evaluation of Feredated Learning on Multi-organ Images

author.name: Junbin Mao, Xu Tian, Jianchun Zhu, Ludi Li, Jin Liu

entry.summary: This paper introduces a comprehensive federated medical-imaging benchmark spanning multiple organs and modalities and incorporating state-of-the-art FL algorithms, aiming to provide a unified evaluation standard for clinically realistic heterogeneous data. It addresses gaps in prior single-organ federated benchmarks.

entry.title: fog: Expressing Motion and Emotion through Function Composition of AI-Generated Code

author.name: Vivian Liu, Lydia Chilton

entry.summary: Introduces fog, a function-composition framework for generating and composing motion functions (verbs, adverbs, gestures, emotions) via AI-generated code to build an open-ended motion vocabulary for Heider-Simmel style animations. A perceptual evaluation of 452 generated animations tests whether people recognize their semantic meaning.

entry.title: Different Teachers, Different Capabilities: Sub-1B On-Device Distillation for Structured Text Enrichment

author.name: Vinay Kumar Chaganti

entry.summary: Measures what on-device distillation delivers per sub-task by distilling an 8B reasoning teacher into a 0.6B student (Qwen3-0.6B, QLoRA) for structured news enrichment producing summaries and categorical labels. A blinded reference-free three-judge panel compares against non-reasoning and larger-pipeline teachers plus few-shot and constrained-decoding baselines.

entry.title: LOGOS: Language-guided Oriented Object Detection in Aerial Scenes

author.name: Trong-Thuan Nguyen, Minh-Triet Tran

entry.summary: Presents LOGOS, a transformer-based approach for oriented object detection in aerial imagery that uses textual prompts and prompt-modulated content queries to dynamically focus detection. It addresses angular discontinuity, fixed query sizes and cluttered scenes in remote sensing.

entry.title: GRE-Diff: Gaussian Room Embeddings for Structured Layout Diffusion

author.name: Jing Wang, Haoran Xiong, Zihao Yan, Minglun Gong, Hui Huang

entry.summary: Proposes GRE-Diff, a controllable interactive diffusion framework using Gaussian room embeddings for structured floor-plan layout generation and editing under user-specified room types, counts and boundaries via LLM-parsed instructions or GUI interaction. It combines AI suggestions with human-in-the-loop editing.

entry.title: Enhancing In-context Panoramic Generation via Geometric-aware Pretraining

author.name: Haoran Feng, Ruiyang Zhang, Longyi Zhang, Dizhe Zhang, Lu Qi

entry.summary: Canvas360 is a two-stage framework for in-context panoramic generation combining geometry-aware pretraining with task-specific fine-tuning, supported by a one-million-sample paired panoramic dataset for style transfer, inpainting, outpainting, and editing. It adds depth generation and circular padding to learn geometry-aware representations.

entry.title: Geometry and Gradient-based Partitioning for Panoramic Outdoor Reconstruction

author.name: Weijian Chen, Weibo Yao, Yuhang Zhang, Xiaolin Tang, Guo Wang, Weijun Zhang, Xitong Gao, Yihao Chen, Hongde Qin, Lu Qi

entry.summary: PanoLOG is a coarse-to-fine framework for large-scale panoramic outdoor 3D Gaussian splatting that introduces geometry- and gradient-based partitioning suited to equirectangular images, whose full 360-degree visibility breaks frustum-based block partitioning. It uses sky-sphere modeling and panoramic depth supervision for reliable geometry.

entry.title: Equivariant Quantum Clustering with Differential Privacy: Parameter-Efficient Privacy-Preserving Analysis Across Heterogeneous Sensitive Datasets

author.name: B. M. Taslimul Haq, Md Arifur Rahman, Tawfiq Al Islam Foysal, Abdullah Al Noman, Abir Ahmed

entry.summary: EQC combines symmetry-aware (p4m equivariant) quantum circuits with differential privacy to improve the privacy-utility tradeoff in clustering across sensitive healthcare, cybersecurity, and insider-threat datasets. It reduces circuit complexity while preserving informative features.

entry.title: RadLoc: Radar-based 3-DoF Global Localization via Fast, Robust, and Lightweight Spatial Descriptor Across Diverse Environmental Scenarios

author.name: Hogyun Kim, Jiwon Choi, Jungwoo Lee, Younggun Cho

entry.summary: RadLoc is a fast, lightweight end-to-end pipeline for radar-based 3-DoF global localization spanning place recognition to pose estimation, using CA-CFAR filtering, near-range dominance for a compact descriptor, hierarchical retrieval, and phase-correlation pose estimation. It provides a holistic radar localization solution robust to adverse conditions.

entry.title: When is misalignment just a bug?

author.name: Yoav Hollander

entry.summary: The first post in a series applying coverage-driven verification methodology (from chip and autonomous-vehicle safety) to AI alignment, arguing many deployed misalignments are engineering bugs where the system did what was requested but missed intent. The author is more uncertain about verification helping with strategic deception.

entry.title: path_boost: A Python Package for Interpretable Graph-Level Prediction using Path-Based Gradient Boosting

author.name: Claudio Meggio, Johan Pensar, Riccardo De Bin

entry.summary: Presents path_boost, a Python package implementing PathBoost, a gradient boosting method that discovers predictive labeled paths in graphs to produce interpretable additive models. It avoids exhaustive path enumeration by iteratively selecting and extending paths during learning.

entry.title: Context Graphs for Proactive Enterprise Agents

author.name: Avinash Kumar

entry.summary: This position-style paper proposes Context Graphs for proactive enterprise agents, using a live relational graph, a delta-detection engine, a proactivity scorer, and an LLM-driven surfacing layer to push relevant insights to workers before they ask. It reframes enterprise agents from reactive to proactive.

entry.title: A Quantum Reservoir Architecture for Chaotic Forecasting and a Test of Whether Its High Dimension Helps

author.name: Tushar Pandey

entry.summary: This paper gives a reproducible recipe for quantum reservoir computing applied to chaotic time-series forecasting and, importantly, a test of whether the reservoir's high-dimensional feature space actually contributes useful work rather than merely inflating apparent performance. It scrutinizes a common assumption in quantum ML.

entry.title: LEXIC: Lightweight Eye-tracking eXtension via Injected Complexity

author.name: Sumin Lee, Kyeonghun Kim, Subeen Lee, Jiwon Yang, Tien Nguyen, Ken Ying-Kai Liao, Nam-Joon Kim

entry.summary: LEXIC augments a gaze-only reading-comprehension model on the EyeBench benchmark by injecting precomputed word-difficulty signals (GPT-2 surprisal, frequency, length) via concatenation or a residual deviation mechanism, without using a full language model at inference. It probes how far language-model-free conditioning can close the gap to text-aware models.

entry.title: COALA: Robust Contextualized Speech-augmented Language Modeling for ASR via Contrastive Regularizer and Biasing Score Estimation

author.name: Jhih-Rong Guo, Bi-Cheng Yan, Tien-Hong Lo, Berlin Chen

entry.summary: Proposes COALA, a contextual biasing framework for speech-augmented language models that maps latent representations into a discriminative space to match audio against candidate entities and addresses training collapse from prior methods. It targets multi-entity recognition under context-window limits.

entry.title: VSRo-200: A Romanian Visual Speech Recognition Dataset for Studying Supervision and Multimodal Robustness

author.name: Iulia-Maria Udrea, Alexandra Diaconu, Bogdan Alexe

entry.summary: Introduces VSRo-200, the first large-scale Romanian visual speech recognition (lip-reading) dataset with 200 hours of podcast video, 100 hours human-transcribed and the rest pseudo-labeled, enabling controlled study of supervision quality. It shows human labels help at fixed scale while pseudo-labels enable continued gains through scalability.

entry.title: CT-CLIP Representations for Multimodal Lung Cancer Survival Prediction

author.name: Sofie Allg\"ower, Mikael Johansson, Andreas Hallqvist, Jonas Andersson, {\AA}se Johnsson, Ida H\"aggstr\"om, Jennifer Alv\'en

entry.summary: This study evaluates the CT-CLIP foundation model as a feature extractor for multimodal lung cancer survival prediction in a small clinical cohort, comparing frozen encoders, full fine-tuning, and low-rank adaptation with modality ablations. It probes foundation-model transfer in data-constrained oncology.

entry.title: Time-to-Collision Based Dynamic Obstacle Avoidance Using Pretrained Vision Models for Robots in Unstructured Environments

author.name: Erik Jagnandan, Mulugeta Haile, Gregory Barber, Pratik Chaudhari

entry.summary: This paper presents a data-efficient, interpretable dynamic obstacle avoidance method for outdoor robots that runs entirely on real-world data, using the pretrained UniDepth model for dense depth and extended SuperPoint/SuperGlue correspondences to estimate time-to-collision. It avoids sim-to-real transfer issues.

entry.title: Monocular Vision Based Control Framework for Grasping

author.name: Shail Jadav, Dongheui Lee

entry.summary: This work presents a unified monocular RGB-only grasping framework for both soft/deformable and rigid objects, combining open-vocabulary detection, segmentation, boundary-aware point assignment, real-time tracking, and monocular depth to recover object geometry and motion. It avoids tactile sensing and specialized grippers.

entry.title: Interpretability is becoming increasingly uninterpretable

author.name: jcksanderson

entry.summary: A critical essay arguing that a growing interpretability subfield uses increasingly complex, opaque tools that are themselves hard to interpret, undermining the original goal of understanding networks in simple terms. It contrasts this with the classical mechanistic-interpretability aim and questions the trend.

entry.title: LLM-powered reasoning in agent-based modeling

author.name: Sifat Afroj Moon, Dakotah Maguire, Adam Spannaus, Joe Tuccillo, Maksudul Alam, Sudip K. Seal, John Gounley, Heidi Hanson

entry.summary: HALE is a hybrid framework that embeds LLM-based human decision prediction inside agent-based epidemic simulation, applied as a COVID-19 proof-of-concept for Salt Lake County. It targets the inability of static-prior ABMs to adapt to real-time behavioral change.

entry.title: MiLSD: A Micro Line-Segment Detector for Resource-Constrained Devices

author.name: Parsa Hassani Shariat Panahi, Amir Hossein Jalilvand, M. Hassan Najafi

entry.summary: MiLSD is a line-segment detector designed for microcontroller-level constraints under a sub-megabyte budget, comparing three output representations within a compact fully-convolutional backbone. It finds a center-with-length-and-angle formulation learns best at small sizes and that 8-bit quantization preserves accuracy while 4-bit degrades it.

entry.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Reliability Based Bi-Objective Portfolio Optimization

author.name: Sounaq Das, Tanmay Sen, Raghu Nandan Sengupta, Aditya Gupta

entry.summary: MORP-DRL is a deep reinforcement learning framework for multi-objective, reliability-based portfolio optimization that jointly optimizes expected return and downside risk using variance, CVaR, and entropic value-at-risk. It aims to capture sequential decision-making, tail risk, and transaction costs that static frameworks miss.

entry.title: STAGformer: A Spatio-temporal Agent Graph Transformer for Micro Mobility Demand Forecasting

author.name: Ye Zihao

entry.summary: STAGformer is a spatio-temporal transformer for station-level bike-sharing demand forecasting that uses a two-step agent-attention mechanism with learnable spatial and temporal agent tokens to achieve linear O(NT) complexity. It captures long-range interactions while avoiding quadratic self-attention cost.

entry.title: Self-Supervised Pretraining Improves Cross-Site and Cross-Scale Robustness of Point Cloud Leaf-Wood Segmentation

author.name: Heeju Mun, Tackang Yang, Yunsoo Nam, Changhyun Choi

entry.summary: The paper applies self-supervised pretraining (Point-M2AE) to leaf-wood segmentation of tree point clouds, using recursive voxel subdivision to handle varying point densities across scales. It shows improved cross-site and cross-scale robustness for forestry applications.

entry.title: Multimodal Spatiotemporal-Frequency Fusion with Peak Enhancement for Cellular Traffic Forecasting

author.name: Qingzhong Li, Yue Hu, Hui Ma, Yajun Zhang, Xinjun Pei, Ming Yan, Fei Xing

entry.summary: MSPF-Net is a multimodal cellular-traffic forecasting framework that fuses spatiotemporal-frequency features with external contextual signals and peak enhancement to handle bursty demand from urban events. It targets network planning and resource allocation.

entry.title: Latency-Aware Bid Acceptance under Operational Feasibility: A Public Benchmark with Hindsight Ceilings

author.name: Aswin Chandrasekaran

entry.summary: FreightBidBench is a public, reproducible closed-loop benchmark for online truckload bid acceptance that makes operational feasibility and economics explicit, with hindsight ceilings for evaluation. It fills a gap where routing benchmarks are static and dynamic-fleet studies rely on private data.

entry.title: Image classification via a quantum-inspired strategy involving a mixture of experts

author.name: Kumari Jyoti, Rohith Babu, Apoorva D. Patel

entry.summary: Proposes a hybrid classical-quantum image classification scheme using amplitude encoding, local unitary convolutions, a mixture of experts, and quantum stabilizer codes for feature extraction. It positions itself as a more efficient quantum-inspired alternative to standard convolutional downsampling.

entry.title: ArtMine: Discovering and Formalizing Artistic Processes

author.name: Kaustubh Kumar, Ashutosh Ranjan, Vivek Srivastava, Blessin Varkey, Shirish Karande

entry.summary: ArtMine is a framework for discovering and formalizing artistic processes from fragmented historical evidence such as archival records, preparatory studies, and correspondence, modeling the iterative decisions and material operations behind artworks rather than just finished artifacts. It targets process-level understanding of artistic production.

entry.title: Deep Learning for Joint Narrowband Interference Cancellation and Soft Demodulation in OFDM Systems

author.name: Emmanouil Kavvousanos, Francky Catthoor, Vassilis Paliouras

entry.summary: This work presents a unified deep learning pipeline (NBI-CNet) for joint narrowband interference cancellation and robust soft demodulation in OFDM systems, replacing latency-heavy compressed-sensing approaches. It targets reliability of log-likelihood ratios and decoder performance under structured interference.

entry.title: Statistical inverse learning problems with random observations

author.name: Abhishake Rastogi, Tapio Helin, Nicole M\"ucke

entry.summary: This is an overview of recent progress in statistical inverse problems with random experimental design, covering spectral regularization, projection methods in Hilbert scales, and convex-penalty regularization with minimax convergence-rate analysis. It consolidates theory for robust solutions to linear and nonlinear inverse problems.

entry.title: Agentic AI and Retrieval-Augmented Models in Straight-Through Underwriting

author.name: Robert Richardson, Josh Meyers, Brian Hartman, David Sandberg

entry.summary: This paper examines how LLMs, retrieval-augmented generation, and multi-agent architectures can support actuarial straight-through underwriting while preserving transparency, auditability, and human oversight. It develops and analyzes an agentic framework for regulated decision workflows.

entry.title: FedTR: Federated Learning Framework with Transfer Learning for Industrial Visual Inspection

author.name: Vikash Sathiamoorthy, Shuo Huai, Hao Kong, Di Liu, Wendy Yong Yi Loy, Christian Makaya, Daren Ho, Ravi Subramaniam, Qian Lin, Weichen Liu

entry.summary: FedTR combines federated learning with transfer learning for autonomous industrial visual inspection, focusing on end-to-end text recognition of label defects under limited data and privacy constraints. It leverages pretrained knowledge to improve performance in data-scarce inspection tasks.

entry.title: Generalization Theory for Through-the-Wall Radar Human Activity Recognition

author.name: Weicheng Gao

entry.summary: This paper builds a generalization-analysis framework for through-the-wall radar human-activity recognition, modeling indoor kinematics, echo generation, image formation, and bounded-weight networks to explain target-domain error under person, view, and wall-condition shifts. It provides theoretical grounding for a domain lacking rigorous generalization explanations.

entry.title: MLQENABLER: Enabling Secure Machine Learning Queries over Encrypted Database in Cloud Computing

author.name: Xu Zhou, Haoyang Chen, Xinyu Lei

entry.summary: MLQENABLER is a system for running secure machine-learning queries over encrypted databases in untrusted cloud settings, addressing the tension between outsourcing storage/ML services and protecting sensitive client data. It targets privacy-preserving analytics on encrypted data.

entry.title: LTM: Large-scale Terrain Model for Wildfire-prone Landscapes

author.name: Xiao Fu, Yue Hu, Meida Chen, Peter Anthony Beerel, Barath Raghavan

entry.summary: Presents a multimodal 3D terrain reconstruction framework for wildfire-prone regions that uses outdated Digital Elevation Models as geometric priors for image-based reconstruction via physics-based pixel alignment. It aims to reduce reliance on expensive LiDAR for hazard assessment.

entry.title: Pose-to-Biomechanics: Bridging 3D Human Pose Estimation and Biomechanical Attribute Prediction

author.name: Ayda Eghbalian, Kevin Desai

entry.summary: Introduces BioModule, an estimator-agnostic temporal transformer plug-in that predicts biomechanical attributes from standard 17-joint 3D skeletons produced by any pose estimator. It bridges geometric pose accuracy and clinically meaningful biomechanical quantities for rehabilitation and sports science.

entry.title: From Execution to Education: A Bloom-Aligned Framework for Measuring Educational Control in LLMs

author.name: Yi Zhang, Julia Rayz

entry.summary: Introduces a Bloom's-Taxonomy-aligned framework for measuring educational control in LLMs, i.e. preserving instructional intent while shifting cognitive demand, applied to programming tasks. It compares a matched Qwen3-Next model pair under difficulty and Bloom-level control settings.

entry.title: Validity of LLMs as data annotators: AMALIA on authority

author.name: Manuel Pita

entry.summary: Tests the validity (not just reliability) of Portugal's publicly funded 9B European Portuguese model AMALIA as a data annotator for the moral foundation of authority, using a recovery gap that measures performance loss when a holistic prompt is decomposed. It shows high human agreement may mask reliance on correlated shortcuts rather than the underlying construct.

entry.title: SPL: Orchestrating Workflows with Declarative Deterministic-Probabilistic Composition

author.name: Wen G. Gong

entry.summary: Presents SPL, a declarative Structured Prompt Language that unifies deterministic (SOLVE/ASSERT) and probabilistic (GENERATE/EVALUATE) computation in one specification, running unchanged across local, cloud and distributed backends. It bridges LLM orchestration frameworks and symbolic computation tools.

entry.title: From Prompts to Contracts: Harness Engineering for Auditable Enterprise LLM Agents

author.name: Joongho Ahn, Moonsoo Kim

entry.summary: Proposes a harness-engineering approach that turns prototype LLM applications into auditable enterprise agents by moving deterministic behavior into code, manifests, schemas and validation artifacts around a replaceable composition boundary while keeping source-backed claims authoritative. It is evaluated on a public-data slice of five Korean corporate groups.

entry.title: EVIS: A Physics-Grounded Event Camera Plugin for NVIDIA Isaac Sim

author.name: Linli Shi, Ruijun Zhang, Ziyun Wang

entry.summary: Presents EV-MoE, a Mixture of Enhanced-View Experts for multi-query vehicle re-identification that enhances each view's features and dynamically fuses view-specific enhanced features, alongside a large-scale benchmark. It addresses simplistic feature fusion that ignores important view information and cross-view relationships.

entry.title: ARGUS: Accelerated, Robust, General, and Unsupervised Cell Tracking Solutions

author.name: Noah Jaitner, Kandice Tanner, Ingolf Sack, Hossein S. Aghamiry

entry.summary: ARGUS is an unsupervised cell tracking framework combining adaptive detection, dense optical flow, frame-to-frame assignment, and tracklet refinement to reconnect fragmented trajectories across temporal gaps. It targets robust tracking in noisy time-lapse microscopy with divisions and fusions.

entry.title: Classical versus Deep Mirror-Symmetry Scoring: A Benchmark of Thirteen Methods

author.name: Maximilian Woehrer

entry.summary: This benchmark compares 13 mirror-symmetry scoring methods (classical and deep) across nine datasets under a common, statistically grounded protocol, finding deep features best but a classical HOG descriptor competitive. It provides the first fair comparison in symmetry scoring.

entry.title: Swapping Faces, Saving Features: A Dual-Purpose Pipeline for Pedestrian Privacy in ITS

author.name: Roba H. Farouk, Catherine M. Elias

entry.summary: This paper proposes a five-stage face-swapping pipeline that preserves pedestrian privacy in intelligent transportation datasets while retaining image attributes needed to train intention and trajectory prediction models. It aims to balance privacy protection with dataset usability.

entry.title: STEMbot: A Compliant Robot for Under-Canopy Plant Navigation

author.name: Zachary Charlick, Nilay Roy Choudhury, Haoyu Ma, Xiaonan Huang, Dmitry Berenson

entry.summary: STEMbot is a miniature compliant climbing robot for autonomous navigation under plant canopies to enable early pest detection on leaf undersides and stems, integrating a geometric PIN-SLAM pipeline with a semantic OcTree for localization while climbing. It targets a labor-cost bottleneck in organic agriculture.

entry.title: D-CLIPSE: Distributed Consensus-based Localization with Passive Listening on Shared State Exchange

author.name: Kyle Biron-Gricken, James Richard Forbes

entry.summary: D-CLIPSE is a consistent, communication-efficient, consensus-based distributed filtering framework for multi-robot localization that shares preintegrated odometry and relevant shared states via passive listening. It is validated in simulation and experiments.

entry.title: Factors Influencing Conversational Engagement in Robot-Delivered Individual Cognitive Stimulation Therapy (iCST) for Dementia in Home Settings

author.name: Emmanuel Akinrintoyo, Nicole Salomons

entry.summary: This study analyzes conversational dynamics during robot-delivered individual cognitive stimulation therapy for dementia in eight participants' homes over a week, measuring metrics like words per turn, response latency, and self-referential language. It provides insight into engagement factors in home-based therapeutic HRI.

entry.title: X-ACTA: eXtended Analytic Center Tension distribution Algorithm for fixed and mobile cable-driven-parallel-robot

author.name: Domenico Dona', Vincenzo Di Paola, Alberto Trevisani, Matteo Zoppi

entry.summary: X-ACTA extends the Analytic Center method to select cable tensions for cable-driven parallel robots operating outside their wrench-feasible workspace, maintaining differentiable, continuous tension profiles and handling nonlinear constraints during aggressive maneuvers or cable failure. It ensures fast real-time convergence to a unique solution.

entry.title: Selecting Interpretable Circular Coordinates from Data

author.name: Vincent P. Grande, Marina Meila

entry.summary: Presents a method (CIRCOL) for selecting scientifically interpretable circle-valued coordinates from a user dictionary to explain loop structure detected via persistent cohomology, framing selection as a minimum-weight basis problem in a matroid. This bridges abstract topological data analysis and domain-meaningful explanations.

entry.title: Selective Optimism: a critique of AI 2040

author.name: Richard_Ngo

entry.summary: A commissioned critique by Richard Ngo of the AI Futures Project scenario AI 2040, focusing on three high-level framing disagreements rather than granular details. The author, a paid consultant on the project, transparently flags his potential bias.

entry.title: ICDAR 2026 HIPE-OCRepair Competition on LLM-Assisted OCR Post-Correction for Historical Documents

author.name: Maud Ehrmann, Emanuela Boros, Juri Opitz, Andrianos Michail, Florian Wagner, Simon Clematide

entry.summary: Reports results of the ICDAR 2026 HIPE-OCRepair competition on LLM-assisted OCR post-correction for historical documents, providing a reproducible evaluation framework across languages, document types and noise conditions. It probes both correction capability and hallucination tendencies.

entry.title: Collective Intelligence with Foundation Models

author.name: J. de Curt\`o, I. de Zarz\`a

entry.summary: A book-chapter-style multi-agent framework where solver models draft independently, a critic agent critiques and revises, and an aggregator synthesizes consensus, with a scoring module for semantic, numerical and procedural evaluation. Ablations isolate framework architecture versus model diversity across a multi-domain benchmark.

entry.title: Multimodal 3D LUT Generation via StatLUT with Statistical Features for Photorealistic Style Transfer

author.name: Yifan Wang, Zhixiang Hao, Yu Wang, Congchao Zhu

entry.summary: StatLUT generates 3D lookup tables for photorealistic style transfer using spatially-agnostic statistical features rather than pretrained encoders, avoiding semantic entanglement and adding text-driven multimodal control. It targets color banding and distortion problems in existing methods.

entry.title: Multi-Resolution Feature Stem for Diabetic Retinopathy lesion segmentation

author.name: Indranil Dutta, Taehee Jeong

entry.summary: This work studies how input resolution affects diabetic retinopathy lesion segmentation across multiple architectures, identifying a counter-intuitive phenomenon where higher resolution can hurt certain lesion types. It proposes a multi-resolution feature stem to handle the wide size variation of DR lesions.

entry.title: Idiobionics: The Unification of Privacy and Intelligent Robotic Prostheses

author.name: Kwesi Afari Darfoor, Patrick M. Pilarski, Bailey Kacsmar

entry.summary: This position paper introduces Idiobionics, arguing for unifying privacy and intelligence in robotic prostheses, since the same sensing and AI-based control that make bionic limbs perceptive and co-adaptive also raise persistent biological-digital privacy risks. It frames prostheses as semi-autonomous wearable robotic systems.

entry.title: QANTIS: Hardware-Calibrated Sequential POMDP Belief Updates on IBM Heron

author.name: Bayram Yuksel Eker, Suayb S. Arslan, Ozgur Nazli, Mustafa Serhat Demirgil, Furkan Deligoz

entry.summary: QANTIS treats a quantum processor as a calibrated belief-update service for POMDP planning, testing whether it can supply posterior updates across a sequential Tiger POMDP on IBM Heron hardware. The work is framed as a controlled hardware case study rather than an end-to-end speedup claim.

entry.title: Comprehensive Evaluation of Large Language Model Responses: A Multi-Factor Scoring System

author.name: Yiming Gai, Junde Lu, Xuefei Huang

entry.summary: This work proposes a multi-factor scoring system for LLM response quality combining accuracy, conciseness, factual consistency, readability, and coherence with a visualization interface. It reports evaluations on TruthfulQA revealing weaknesses in handling complex facts.

entry.title: Predicting Pseudo-nitzschia harmful algal blooms along the Portuguese Coast using satellite-derived predictors

author.name: Ayman Bnoussaad, El Khalil Cherif, Ligia Pinto, Ramiro Neves, Alexandra D. Silva, Alexandre Bernardino

entry.summary: Develops a spatio-temporal machine learning framework to forecast Pseudo-nitzschia harmful algal blooms along the Portuguese coast using only satellite-derived predictors under realistic forecasting constraints. It uses a decade of observations across shellfish production zones.

entry.title: Spectral Stability of Pseudoinverse-Based Extreme Learning Machine

author.name: Bich Van Nguyen, Ngoc Anh Khong

entry.summary: Studies the numerical stability of pseudoinverse-based Extreme Learning Machines from a spectral perspective, showing the smallest singular value governs perturbation amplification and comparing SVD-based pseudoinverse with iterative hyperpower methods. It relates width-dependent conditioning to a random feature interpretation.

entry.title: SASGeo: Stability-Aware Semantic Map Localization for GNSS-Denied UAVs -- A Framework and Synthetic Proof of Concept

author.name: Natalia Trukhina, Vadim Vashkelis

entry.summary: SASGeo proposes a semantic map-localization framework for GNSS-denied UAVs that relies on persistent structures like roads and buildings, combined with stability, distinctiveness, and integrity-aware rejection of ambiguous position fixes. Results are a synthetic proof of concept rather than real-world validation.

entry.title: SpO$_2$ Predictor-Guided Stage-Wise Time-Frequency Reconstruction of Low-Quality Dual-Wavelength PPG for Oxygen Saturation Estimation

author.name: Zequan Liang, Elahe Hosseini, Ning Miao, Mahdi Pirayesh Shirazi Nejad, Wei Shao, Ehsan Kourkchi, Setareh Rafatirad, Houman Homayoun

entry.summary: This paper proposes a stage-wise time-frequency reconstruction framework guided by an SpO2 predictor to repair low-quality dual-wavelength PPG signals for oxygen-saturation estimation on wearables. It emphasizes preserving frequency structure and SpO2-relevant information beyond simple waveform fidelity.

entry.title: COBART: Controlled, Optimized, Bidirectional and Auto-Regressive Transformer for Ad Headline Generation

author.name: Yashal Shakti Kanungo, Gyanendra Das, Pooja A, Sumit Negi

entry.summary: COBART fine-tunes BART with prefix control tokens to generate advertising headlines that optimize click-through rate and quality while letting users control headline length for different ad formats. It reports meaningful ROUGE-L and CTR improvements.

entry.title: MASTE: A Multi-Agent Pipeline for Zero-Shot Aspect Sentiment Triplet Extraction

author.name: Ao Hong, Lehang Wang, Zhirun Yue, Mingxin Wang, Zihan Wang, Houde Liu

entry.summary: Presents MASTE, a multi-agent pipeline for zero-shot aspect sentiment triplet extraction that decomposes span-boundary detection, opinion grouping and sentiment polarity into separate agent steps. It targets the weakness of single-pass LLM generation on structured extraction.

entry.title: Large-Language-Models-as-a-Judge in Theory-Agnostic Adaptive Metric-Alignment for Prototypical Networks in Personality Recognition

author.name: Jing Jie Tan, Ban-Hoe Kwan, Danny Wee-Kiat Ng, Yan-Chai Hum, Shih-Yu Lo, Po-An Chen, Noriyuki Kawarazaki, Kosuke Takano, Anissa Mokraoui

entry.summary: Proposes JAM, a theory-agnostic framework using an LLM-as-judge to align adaptive metrics for prototypical networks in personality recognition, discovering unified latent pseudo-facets rather than fitting predefined psychological taxonomies. It aims for better generalization across inconsistent annotation schemes.

entry.title: AutoPersonas: A Multi-Timescale Loop Engine for Open-Ended Persona Evolution

author.name: Mengchen Li

entry.summary: Introduces AutoPersonas, a multi-timescale life-environment engine for bounded persona self-evolution, identifying self-locking (collapse toward familiar environments and stale life stages) as a runtime failure of continuing persona loops. It separates environment-side occurrences from accumulated observations to sustain identifiable yet adaptive personas.

entry.title: LDFE: Laplacian Decoupled Feature Enhancement Block for Dual-Stream CNN-based RGB-IR Object Detection

author.name: Wenhao Dong, Xiaoyan Luo, Linlin Yang, Haodong Zhu, Xiaorong Shi, Guodong Guo, Baochang Zhang

entry.summary: Introduces the Laplacian Decoupled Feature Enhancement block for dual-stream CNN RGB-IR object detection, which decomposes features into global and local components via a Laplacian pyramid then denoises, fuses and reconstructs them across backbone stages. It targets better complementary RGB-infrared fusion under extreme conditions.

entry.title: Leveraging Color Naming for Image Enhancement

author.name: David Serrano-Lozano, Luis Herranz, Michael S. Brown, Javier Vazquez-Corral

entry.summary: NamedCurves+ integrates the concept of color naming into a learning-based image enhancement framework, applying tone curves per named color plus a transformer block for local adjustments. The goal is interpretable, user-adjustable photo editing.

entry.title: TVTA: Trajectory-Aware Viseme-Guided Temporal Aggregation for Event-Based Lip Reading

author.name: Jingrong Zheng, Hongwei Ren, Xiangqian Wu

entry.summary: This work proposes a temporally enhanced framework for event-based lip reading that preserves sparse motion trajectories through trajectory-aware differential aggregation and adds articulatory (viseme) structure supervision. It targets weaknesses of premature spatial compression in event-camera speech recognition.

entry.title: WaspMOT: A Benchmark for Long-Term Multi-Object Tracking of Trichogramma Wasps

author.name: Tomasz Stanczyk, Yuan Gao, Hardik Agarwal, Seongroo Yoon, Tiantao Zhang, Vincent Calcagno, Francois Bremond

entry.summary: WaspMOT is a long-duration multi-object tracking benchmark of Trichogramma wasps with roughly 12,000-frame sequences and dense annotations, forming a closed-set scenario that stresses long-term identity preservation. It targets a gap in existing short-sequence MOT datasets.

entry.title: Towards Soft Robotic Exogloves for Musculoskeletal Manipulation to Reduce Pain and Spasticity

author.name: Antonia Salluce, Maeryn Erdheim, Gailen Davis, Lauren H. Sullivan, Max-William Kanz, Jacqueline Libby

entry.summary: This paper presents preliminary development of a soft pneumatic robotic exoglove that both aids hand mobility and delivers massage-like compression to relax spastic muscles, personalized to an individual's hand topology. It addresses the lack of solutions targeting both pain and mobility for hand spasticity.

entry.title: Soft Robotic Exogloves for Dexterous Mobility -- Towards Personalized Rehabilitation

author.name: Paul Dela Cruz, Mostafa Mo. Massoud, Jacqueline Libby

entry.summary: This paper describes the design, fabrication, FEA modeling, and testing of a personalized pneumatically-actuated soft robotic exoglove fitted to a user's hand via topological scans and silicone casting for dexterous rehabilitation. It emphasizes personalization for fine articulation.

entry.title: Unit-Independent Low-Rate Wrist GSR Processing for Stress Detection Using Phasic nSCR Features

author.name: Zequan Liang, Sally Hang, Geneva M. Jost, Ning Miao, Wei Shao, Mahdi Pirayesh Shirazi Nejad, Hossein Sayadi, Ehsan Kourkchi, Setareh Rafatirad, Camelia E. Hostinar, Houman Homayoun

entry.summary: This work presents a unit-independent, low-rate pipeline to extract skin-conductance-response rate from wrist GSR for stress detection, validated against palmar measurements during a standardized social-stress test with 31 participants. It aims to make consumer-grade wrist GSR usable despite amplitude differences from lab sensors.

entry.title: Joint estimation of high-dimensional spiked covariance matrices via a partially shared subspace

author.name: Changwon Yoon, Minwoo Kim, Sungkyu Jung, Jeongyoun Ahn

entry.summary: Proposes a spiked covariance model where two related high-dimensional datasets share a subspace of unknown rank while each retains distinct spiked directions, enabling joint estimation that borrows strength across sources. It generalizes prior shared-covariance models and provides a full estimation procedure.

entry.title: POO-LPSP: Parallel Osprey Optimized Least Penalty-Squared Prioritization Methods for Priority Derivation in the Analytic Hierarchy Process

author.name: Kevin Kam Fung Yuen

entry.summary: POO-LPSP develops revised least-penalty-squared prioritization models for deriving priorities in the Analytic Hierarchy Process, solving the nonlinear formulations with a parallel Osprey optimization algorithm. It addresses debated robustness of the eigenvector method.

entry.title: Classifier Chain-based Pathological Test Recommendation

author.name: Abu Rafe Md Jamil, Nayan Malakar

entry.summary: Presents a pathological test recommendation system framed as multi-label classification using the classifier chain technique to model dependencies between tests, based on patient symptoms before physician consultation. It uses a custom dataset built with pathology expertise and standard classifiers.

entry.title: Predicting Male Fertility Using Machine Learning: A Semen Parameters Based Analysis with the VISEM Dataset

author.name: Shahnawaz Qureshi, Raja Khurram Shahzad, Muhammad Fozan, Emal Kawal, Syed Aziz Shah, Sattam Al-Anazi, Syed MuhammadZeeshan Iqbal

entry.summary: Uses machine learning to classify male fertility status (fertile, sub-fertile, infertile) from semen parameters such as concentration, motility, and morphology using the VISEM dataset of 85 participants. Multiple classifiers were compared via an automated framework.

entry.title: Evaluating the Effect of Frame Rate in Sequence-Based Classification of Autism-Related Self-Stimulatory Hand Idiosyncrasies

author.name: Raunak Mondal, Peter Washington

entry.summary: This study evaluates how frame sampling rate and data augmentation affect LSTM and GRU classification of autism-related self-stimulatory hand behaviors from pose-derived video features. It seeks scalable, remote behavioral screening from small datasets.

entry.title: INTENT: An LSTM Framework for Vehicle Intention Prediction in Intersection Scenarios with Comprehensive Ablation Analysis

author.name: Logine M. Zaki, Catherine M. Elias

entry.summary: INTENT is an LSTM framework for predicting vehicle intention in intersection scenarios, accompanied by a comprehensive ablation analysis, aimed at improving real-time evasive decision-making and safety for autonomous vehicles. It emphasizes human-like interpretation of driver intent in complex interactions.

entry.title: Predicting Viticulture Potential through an Ensemble of U-Net and a Geospatial Foundation Model

author.name: Jorge Ignacio Perez, Hwaai Kang Kee, Lucas Rassbach

entry.summary: This paper predicts viticulture potential in Southern France using an ensemble of U-Net and the Prithvi-2.0 geospatial foundation model for the ImageCLEF AI4Agri 2026 challenge, achieving second place among seven teams. It applies remote-sensing foundation models to agricultural land-suitability assessment.

entry.title: A Multi-cluster Boundary Learning Method for Out-of-Scope Intent Detection via MiniLM Embedding

author.name: Yihong Xu, Mingyu Kang, Linyuan L\"u

entry.summary: Proposes a multi-cluster boundary learning method for out-of-scope intent detection using compact MiniLM embeddings in a one-class classification workflow. It aims to avoid accuracy degradation as known-intent counts grow while remaining lightweight enough for deployment.

entry.title: Diarization-Guided Qwen-ASR Adaptation for Multilingual Two-Speaker Conversational Speech

author.name: Hao Wu, RongQi Han, Zhen Wang, Wei Liang, Wei Xu

entry.summary: Describes a challenge system for multilingual two-speaker conversational speech combining modular speaker diarization (VAD, CAMPPlus embeddings, spectral clustering) with an adapted Qwen3-ASR-1.7B recognizer fine-tuned with LoRA and synthetic TTS speech. It targets the MLC-SLM 2026 task.

entry.title: Sampling on Random Subspaces under Limited Data in the Context of Exploratory Landscape Analysis

author.name: Iv\'an Olarte Rodr\'iguez, Anja Jankovic, Thomas B\"ack, Elena Raponi

entry.summary: This paper proposes an alternative sampling strategy for Exploratory Landscape Analysis based on random linear embeddings, allocating limited budget to randomly oriented low-dimensional subspaces to improve robustness of landscape descriptors. It targets noisy, unstable descriptors under tight evaluation budgets.

entry.title: Some Thoughts on The Environment Problem in Agent Training

author.name: TheVinci

entry.summary: Working notes on training autonomous LLM agents, arguing that improving real-world interaction (rather than text/code generation) requires different training data, based on fine-tuning Gemma on a small corpus of real interactions. The author reports bounded held-out gains and treats a task regression as evidence the approach is valid.

entry.title: Non-contact, Real-time, Heart-rate Measurement using Image Processing with Commodity Cameras and AI Agents

author.name: Kelly Li, Fulu Li

entry.summary: This paper develops a non-contact, real-time heart-rate measurement system using image processing with commodity cameras such as laptop webcams, with an algorithm to extract relevant signals in real-world environments. It targets continuous health monitoring, especially for elderly care.

entry.title: ImputeViz: A Visual Analytics Dashboard for Diagnosing Missing Data and Comparing Imputation Methods

author.name: Aitik Dandapat, Lalith Punepalle Raveendrareddy, Mithilesh Kumar Singh, Klaus Mueller

entry.summary: Introduces ImputeViz, an interactive visual analytics dashboard for diagnosing missing-data patterns and comparing imputation methods like MICE, Random Forest, XGBoost and kNN, plus a geographically informed gKNN variant. It targets provenance and accountability for how analysts handle missing values in scientific and public-health data.

entry.title: Mixtures of spatial factor analyzers for tensor-variate data

author.name: Hanzhang Lu, Keiran Malott, Kirsty Milligan, Sanjeena Subedi, Edana Cassol, Vinita Chauhan, Connor McNairn, Prarthana Pasricha, Sangeeta Murugkar, Rowan Thomson, Andrew Jirasek, Jeffrey L. Andrews

entry.summary: Introduces a mixture of spatial factor analyzers (MSFA) for clustering high-dimensional tensor-variate spatial data, using a spline-based spatial decay covariance and matrix-variate factor analyzers to control parameter growth. Estimation combines an EM variant with generalized least squares, demonstrated on Raman spectroscopy and hyperspectral data.

entry.title: A scalable version of MADD for big-data classification

author.name: Annesha Ghosh, Adrija Saha, Soham Sarkar

entry.summary: Develops a scalable variant of the Mean Absolute Difference of Distances (MADD) semi-metric to overcome its quadratic complexity, enabling distance-based classification in high-dimension low-sample-size settings for large datasets. It targets the distance-concentration failures of Euclidean classifiers.

entry.title: AI #176 Part 1: Doing It Live

author.name: Zvi

entry.summary: A weekly AI news roundup by Zvi covering assorted developments, previewing coverage of OpenAI's GPT-5.6 (Sol) and an upgraded voice mode, noting Grok 4.5, and commenting on the growing prevalence of AI-generated writing. It aggregates and editorializes on the week's happenings.

author.name: Youcheng Zong, Runda Jia, Mingxuan Ren, Dakuo He

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06633v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: In this paper, we address the problem of multimodal federated learning with missing modality. Existing methods utilize an additional public dataset o...

author.name: Amin Haeri, Mahdi Ghelichi

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06636v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Large language models frequently generate code that appears correct on typical inputs yet fails on edge cases, invalid inputs, and other specificatio...

author.name: Truong Xuan Khanh

author.name: Donna Vakalis

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06641v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) achieve promising results on medical question answering benchmarks, yet their use in public health is constrained by hal...

author.name: Iman Seyedi, Francesco Archetti

author.name: Sumer S. Vaid, Ashley V. Whillans

author.name: Sankalp Gilda

author.name: Inkyu Sa, Chanoh Park, Hea-Min Lee, Donghee Noh, Ho Seok Ahn

author.name: Razvan Mihai Popescu

author.name: Nilay Kushawaha, Muhammad Sunny Nazeer, Baljinder Singh Bal, Cecilia Laschi, Egidio Falotico

author.name: Yizhi Wang, Xinghua Gao, Reachsak Ly, Alireza Shojaei

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06748v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Smart home automation platforms increasingly rely on user-authored YAML configuration files to define device behaviors, but these files are prone to ...

author.name: Seyed Mohammad Azimi-Abarghouyi, Christopher G. Brinton

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06768v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: This paper investigates over-the-air federated learning (AirFL) in wireless systems where the access point is equipped with a multi-waveguide pinchin...

author.name: Petar Djukic, Sudipta Acharya, Takai Eddine Kennouche, Burak Kantarci

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06786v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Standards bodies, including TM Forum, 3GPP, and ETSI, are converging on Agentic AI as the foundation for next-generation network management, where La...

author.name: Javidan Abdullayev, Maxime Devanne, Jonathan Weber, Germain Forestier

entry.title: What Predicts Correctness in Text-to-SQL? A Selective-Prediction Study

author.name: Robert Richardson

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06799v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Evaluating uncertainty in AI-generated SQL queries requires estimating whether a query is correct, where correct means it executes to the same result...

entry.title: A Multi-Analyst LLM Pipeline for Auditable Rule Discovery Across 68 Public Physiological Corpora

author.name: Dovy Paukstys

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06802v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Open physiological corpora are heterogeneous: they use different sensors, labels, sampling rates, recording settings, and clinical endpoints. They ca...

author.name: Yashal Shakti Kanungo, Sumit Negi, Aruna Rajan

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06818v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: For any E-commerce website it is a nontrivial problem to build enduring advertisements that attract shoppers. It is hard to pass the creative quality...

author.name: Nima Kelidari, Mohammadsaeed Haghi, Mahdi Salmani

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06854v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Reinforcement learning agents for imperfect-information card games are only as strong as the opponents they train against, and they are hard to grade...

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06882v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Visual navigation policies built on large pretrained models have so far followed a common recipe: a dedicated visual encoder, a bespoke action head, ...

entry.title: Multi-Agent AI Control: Distributed Attacks Hamper Per-Instance Monitors

author.name: Oliver Makins, Orazio Angelini, Zohreh Shams, Mary Phuong

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07368v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: AI control is a family of techniques to prevent an AI with malicious goals from subverting its operator's intent. AI Control usually studies a single...

entry.title: Accurate, Interdisciplinary and Transparent Structure-property Understanding with Deep Native Structural Reasoning

author.name: Chen Tang, Yizhou Wang, Jianyu Wu, Lintao Wang, Shixiang Tang, Pengze Li, Encheng Su, Jun Yao, Jiabei Xiao, Yuqi Shi, Jielan Li, Hongxia Hao, Zhangyang Gao, Fang Wu, Ben Fei, Xiangyu Yue, Pan Tan, Bozitao Zhong, Jinouwen Zhang, Aoran Wang, Yan Lu, Jiaheng Liu, Xinzhu Ma, Liang Hong, Mingyue Zheng, Phil Torr, Bowen Zhou, Wanli Ouyang, Lei Bai

entry.title: Who Gets Missed in the Tail? Thresholded Subgroup Underdiagnosis in Long-Tailed Chest X-ray Classification

author.name: Ha-Hieu Pham, Hai-Dang Nguyen, Dang P. M. Cao, Thanh-Huy Nguyen, Min Xu, Trung-Nghia Le, Ulas Bagci, Huy-Hieu Pham

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07717v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In chest X-ray (CXR) classification, acceptable ranking performance can still leave rare-positive patients below threshold, especially within subgroups...

entry.title: SQuaD-SQL: Efficient Text-to-SQL with Small Language Models via LLM-Guided Knowledge Distillation

author.name: Wangyu Wu, Xiaojian Lin, Rong Fu, Zaiyang Yu, Xuhang Chen, Wenjun Yu, Zhenhong Chen

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.08161v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Text-to-SQL is a fundamental task in natural language processing that enables users to interact with structured databases using natural language. While...

entry.title: 3D Reconstruction of deciduous Trees using low-cost UAV- and Crane-based Photogrammetry for Monitoring Shoot Elongation across entire Canopies

author.name: Stephan Nebiker, Micha Tschanz, Nando Amport, Frederik Baumgarten

entry.summary: Reconstructs native deciduous trees in 3D from low-cost UAV and crane-based photogrammetry to monitor shoot elongation (primary growth) across entire canopies, addressing a gap where suitable measurement techniques for primary growth are lacking. It supports studying climate-change effects on tree growth and carbon sequestration.

entry.title: UAV-OVVIS: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles Also Need Open-Vocabulary Video Instance Segmentation

author.name: Mingyu Dou, Shi Qiu, Ming Hu, Yifan Chen, Zhe Sun

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.08075v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) videos are widely used in traffic monitoring, urban management, and emergency rescue. However, existing UAV video percept...

entry.title: Mixture of Enhanced-View Experts for Multi-Query Vehicle ReID and A Large-Scale Benchmark

author.name: Aihua Zheng, Jie Zhen, Chenglong Li, Jiaxiang Wang, Jin Tang

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.08085v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-query vehicle ReID aims to leverage complementary information from diverse views for robust feature learning. However, current methods suffer fro...

entry.title: Continual Test-Time Adaptation in Computer Vision: Methods, Benchmarks, and Future Directions

author.name: Sarthak Kumar Maharana, Shambhavi Mishra, Yunbei Zhang, Shuaicheng Niu, Taki Hasan Rafi, Jihun Hamm, Marco Pedersoli, Jose Dolz, Yunhui Guo

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.08164v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep neural nets achieve remarkable performance when training and test data share the same distribution, but this assumption frequently breaks in real-...

entry.title: Enhancing the KidSat Model: Integrating Geographical Encoding and Data Quality Assessment for Childhood Poverty Prediction

author.name: Hou Hin Ip, Ka Nam Lam, Joshua Man Yu Ng, Makkunda Sharma, Seth Flaxman, Codie Gerlach-Wood, H Juliette T Unwin

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.08281v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate poverty mapping using satellite imagery is often hindered by (i) noisy and sparse survey-derived supervision, (ii) image quality issues such a...

entry.title: Switch-Reasoner: Learn When to Think in Multitask Mixtures via Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Yiyang Fang, Pei Fu, Jinjie Li, Jian Liang, Wenke Huang, Ruijie Luo, Shaojie Zhang, Jian Luan, Yi R. Fung, Mang Ye

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.08572v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) often follow a fixed Think-then-Answer paradigm, which is inefficient in heterogeneous multitask settings beca...

entry.title: LongE2V: Long-Horizon Event-based Video Reconstruction, Prediction, and Frame Interpolation with Video Diffusion Models

author.name: Cheng-De Fan, Chun-Wei Tuan Mu, Chen-Wei Chang, Chin-Yang Lin, Kun-Ru Wu, Yu-Chee Tseng, Yu-Lun Liu

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.08770v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recovering high-quality video from sparse event streams is a challenging task. Regression methods often blur textures, while existing generative models...

entry.title: TFP: Temporally Conditioned Memory-Fusion Policies for Visuomotor Learning

author.name: Yushen Liang, Yue Peng, Baosheng Jin, Tianluo Zhang, Xinyu Zhang, Shuyi Zhou, Zhuoran Chen, Xinqi Liu, Shenji Wan

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.08283v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision--Language--Action (VLA) policies such as $\pi_{0.5}$ and OpenVLA perform well on many manipulation tasks, but they are often reactive: the next ...

entry.title: A New Human-Likeness and Comfort Index for Robot Movements Along Prescribed Paths

author.name: Rosanna Coccaro, Enrico Ferrentino, Antonio Parziale, Angelo Marcelli, Pasquale Chiacchio

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.08620v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As human-robot interaction rapidly spreads in numerous fields, the subject of robot acceptance gains increasing importance. Visual similarity to the hu...

entry.title: Rogue ASI Can't Stay Aligned to Itself

author.name: absenteewarlord

entry.summary: A speculative essay arguing that even a rogue artificial superintelligence forming a singleton would face internal alignment problems when deploying large agent fleets, since individual agents have divergent instrumental drives. It frames catastrophic takeover capability as a self-destabilizing technology.

entry.title: Revisiting One-Zero and Two-Zero Neutrino Mass Textures in Light of Recent Oscillation and Cosmological Data

author.name: Haruto Kitagawa, Coh Miyao, Satsuki Nishimura, Hajime Otsuka

entry.summary: This is a particle-physics phenomenology paper revisiting one-zero and two-zero neutrino mass matrix textures against the latest oscillation, cosmological, and neutrinoless double-beta-decay constraints, identifying viable structures and CP-phase predictions. It appears mis-categorized under machine learning.

entry.title: How Do I Know What to Say Next? Barenholtz's Autogenerative Theory as an Enrichment of Harrisean Integrationism

author.name: J. Mark Bishop, Stephen J. Cowley

entry.summary: A theoretical linguistics paper arguing that Barenholtz's autogenerative theory of language enriches Harrisean Integrationism, addressing explanatory gaps about how signs sustain prospective openness. It is a conceptual critique of referentialist assumptions in computational language models.

entry.title: The Memory Wall of Green Software: Empirical Energy Evaluation of Memento Design Pattern

author.name: Imane Jriri, Tarik Houichime, Younes El Amrani

entry.summary: Empirically measures the energy cost of the Memento software design pattern using RAPL hardware telemetry, contrasting a direct baseline against classic full-snapshot and differential delta-encoding strategies across 10-200 MB state volumes. It exposes an architectural energy trade-off in green software engineering.

entry.title: Criticism against "unembedded FDT" doesn't apply to FDT

author.name: Fernand0

entry.summary: A decision-theory argument claiming that some criticisms of Functional Decision Theory actually target a self-contradictory unembedded variant, and that FDT properly applies only to embedded agents. It contends that God's-eye-view thought experiments smuggle in impossible premises.

entry.title: The Cube Theory of Partially Grasped Concepts

author.name: Mateusz Bagiński

entry.summary: A conceptual reference post proposing that partially grasped concepts can be characterized along a multidimensional spectrum of key characteristics without dissolving the concept's boundary. It argues that continuous framing does not necessarily destroy meaningful structure.

entry.title: Skeptical of the TESCREAL Acronym? Read This.

author.name: philosophytorres

entry.summary: A polemical defense of the TESCREAL acronym, describing the bundle of Silicon Valley ideologies (singularity, superintelligence, cosmic colonization) it purports to name. It is a cross-post arguing the framing is legitimate against skeptics.

entry.title: What is the computational substance of the axiom of choice?

author.name: tailcalled

entry.summary: An expository essay using realizability semantics to give an intuitive, constructive account of the axiom of choice and its everyday computational meaning. It contrasts classical and constructive viewpoints through worked proofs.

entry.title: Unveiling Public Opinion: A Study of Sentiment Analysis Using LSTM and Traditional Models

author.name: Atiq Ur Rehman

entry.summary: Applies LSTM and traditional machine-learning models to Twitter sentiment classification into positive, negative and neutral categories. It restates well-established sentiment-analysis methodology on social-media text.

entry.title: How big is the Sun? How could you figure it out?

author.name: Elliott Thornley

entry.summary: A reflective essay recounting how ancient Greeks reasoned their way from Earth's roundness to estimating the size of the Sun, inspired by Tegmark's book. It celebrates the chain of clever observational inferences.

entry.title: WHERE to Generate Matters: Budget-Aware Synthetic Augmentation for Label Skewed Federated Learning

author.name: Sangwoo Lee, Sunghwan Park, Jaewoo Lee

entry.title: Research Summary: July 09, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Today's research spans medical data infrastructure, efficient architectures, and multimodal understanding. MedPMC converts 6.1M permissively licensed PubMed Central articles into 11M high-fidelity medical multimodal samples for foundation models, offering rare practical, industry-relevant scale.

Efficiency and architecture advances dominate:

  • Sparse Delta Memory (SDM) extends Gated DeltaNet with sparse key-value reads/writes, directly attacking recall limits in linear-attention models
  • GIFT enables low-precision (FP8/NVFP4) gradient communication for LLM pretraining via geometry-aware, near-isotropic coordinates
  • Constrained decoding for diffusion language models yields exact, tractable generation under any finite-automaton (JSON) constraint

Multimodal, theory, and safety:

  • Gen4U (DeepMind-affiliated) shows video-diffusion latents encode structured high-level semantics, unifying generation and understanding
