  • Torralba and Weiss analytically derive optimal contrastive representations for images with stationary statistics, a rare closed-form result
  • WildCity delivers a city-scale, in-the-wild multimodal testbed for rendering, simulation, and spatial intelligence
  • Activation steering mitigates LLMs silently rewriting African American English into Standard American English; the AI Safety at the Frontier digest curates frontier interpretability work (Anthropic's Jacobian lens)
entry.content:
Today's research spans medical data infrastructure, efficient architectures, and multimodal understanding. MedPMC converts 6.1M permissively licensed PubMed Central articles into 11M high-fidelity medical multimodal samples for foundation models, offering rare practical, industry-relevant scale.

Efficiency and architecture advances dominate:

  • Sparse Delta Memory (SDM) extends Gated DeltaNet with sparse key-value reads/writes, directly attacking recall limits in linear-attention models
  • GIFT enables low-precision (FP8/NVFP4) gradient communication for LLM pretraining via geometry-aware, near-isotropic coordinates
  • Constrained decoding for diffusion language models yields exact, tractable generation under any finite-automaton (JSON) constraint

Multimodal, theory, and safety:

  • Gen4U (DeepMind-affiliated) shows video-diffusion latents encode structured high-level semantics, unifying generation and understanding
  • Torralba and Weiss analytically derive optimal contrastive representations for images with stationary statistics, a rare closed-form result
  • WildCity delivers a city-scale, in-the-wild multimodal testbed for rendering, simulation, and spatial intelligence
  • Activation steering mitigates LLMs silently rewriting African American English into Standard American English; the AI Safety at the Frontier digest curates frontier interpretability work (Anthropic's Jacobian lens)

entry.title: MedPMC: A Systematic Framework for Scaling High-Fidelity Medical Multimodal Data for Foundation Models

author.name: Hyunjae Kim, Dain Kim, Pan Xiao, Serina S. Applebaum, Younjoon Chung, Xuguang Ai, Yu Yin, Roy Jiang, Yuexi Du, Yawen Wei, Yiming Kong, Tuo Guo, Zhiyuan Cao, Mengmeng Du, Yuelei Fu, Yan Hu, Rui Shi, Gui Yang, Kevin W. Jin, Yuntian Liu, Yuxuan Tian, Jonathan Marquez, Zhen Chen, Sheng Zhang, Hoifung Poon, Hua Xu, Jaewoo Kang, Qingyu Chen

entry.summary: MedPMC is an automated, continuously updatable framework that converts 6.1 million permissively licensed PubMed Central articles into 11 million high-fidelity medical image-text pairs for multimodal foundation models. It improves fidelity, reproducibility, and clinical validation over prior PMC resources.

entry.title: AI Safety at the Frontier: Paper Highlights of May & June 2026

author.name: gasteigerjo

entry.summary: A curated digest of frontier AI safety papers from May and June 2026, highlighting Anthropic's Jacobian lens finding a sparse verbalizable concept workspace, natural language autoencoders surfacing hidden evaluation awareness, and METR's first Frontier Risk Report. As a synthesis it efficiently surfaces the most important recent alignment and interpretability results.

entry.title: Gen4U: Unifying Video Generation and Understanding via Diffusion

author.name: Michael King, Aravindh Mahendran, Matthew Koichi Grimes, Fedor Kitashov, Adham Elarabawy, Pedro Velez, Maks Ovsjanikov, Viorica P\u{a}tr\u{a}ucean

entry.summary: Gen4U probes intermediate activations of video diffusion models and shows their latent space encodes structured high-level semantics, not just low-level geometry, then repurposes these representations for understanding tasks. It challenges the view that diffusion features struggle with semantics.

entry.title: A Theory of Contrastive Learning with Natural Images

author.name: Antonio Torralba, Yair Weiss

entry.summary: Torralba and Weiss analytically derive the optimal contrastive-learning representation for basic augmentations on any image dataset with stationary statistics, showing the optimum can be a CNN with sinusoidal first-layer filters and partial-whitening output. The results provide theoretical grounding for why simple contrastive learning yields useful features.

entry.title: Sparse Delta Memory: Scaling the State of Linear RNNs through Sparsity

author.name: Lo\"ic Cabannes, Pierre-Emmanuel Mazar\'e, Gergely Szilvasy, Matthijs Douze, Maria Lomeli, Ilze Amanda Auzina, Justin Carpentier, Gabriel Synnaeve, Herv\'e J\'egou

entry.summary: Introduces Sparse Delta Memory (SDM), extending Gated DeltaNet by replacing the dense key-value outer product with sparse reads and writes to a large explicit memory, scaling linear-RNN state capacity by orders of magnitude to close the long-context recall gap under isoFLOP constraints. Targets efficient long-context modeling.

entry.title: GIFT: Geometry-Informed Low-precision Gradient Communication for LLM Pretraining

author.name: Jieying Wang, Shuyuan Fan, Mingkai Zheng, Zhao Zhang

entry.summary: GIFT performs low-precision gradient communication for LLM pretraining in geometry-aware coordinates, transforming anisotropic gradients into a near-isotropic space before FP8 or NVFP4 quantization to reduce direction-dependent distortion. It targets the communication bottleneck in distributed training.

entry.title: Is Randomness Necessary for Adaptive Data Analysis?

author.name: Edith Cohen, Haim Kaplan, Yishay Mansour, Shay Sapir, Uri Stemmer

entry.summary: Investigates whether randomness is necessary in adaptive data analysis, where a dataset is repeatedly queried without overfitting, contrasting the well-understood randomized regime with deterministic mechanisms. It probes fundamental limits on how many adaptive queries can be supported.

entry.title: LLMs Silently Correct African American English: Auditing and Mitigating Dialect Bias via Activation Steering

author.name: Huan Wu, Ali Emami, Muhammad Furquan Hassan, Osaretin Igbinoba, Osakpolor Idusuyi, Osamede Igbinoba, Faiza Khan Khattak, Laleh Seyyed-Kalantari

entry.summary: Audits six instruction-tuned LLMs and shows they systematically rewrite African American English into Standard American English, then introduces a bias-isolating metric (cDGI) and mitigates the effect via activation steering. It highlights and addresses dialect bias affecting over 30 million speakers.

entry.title: WildCity: A Real-World City-Scale Testbed for Rendering, Simulation, and Spatial Intelligence

author.name: Xiangyu Han, Mengyu Yang, Jiaqi Li, Bowen Chang, Ziyu Chen, Hexu Zhao, Rahul Kumar Agrawal, Anthony Rodriguez, Fiona Hua, Marco Pavone, Chen Feng, Yiming Li

entry.summary: WildCity is a real-world city-scale multimodal dataset from autonomous fleets covering 18 trajectories averaging 83.7 km each, preserving in-the-wild challenges like dynamic objects, lighting variation, and imperfect poses. It establishes a testbed for rendering, simulation, and spatial intelligence at unprecedented scale.

entry.title: Constrained Decoding for Diffusion Language Models via Efficient Inference over Finite Automata

author.name: Meihua Dang, Stefano Ermon

entry.summary: Presents an exact, tractable algorithm for constrained decoding of diffusion language models under any finite-automaton constraint, handling the fully-factorized mean-field sampling that breaks left-to-right autoregressive assumptions. Views automata as graphical models to sample the constrained posterior.

entry.title: Finding a stationary point of a stochastic convex problem

author.name: Felipe Areces, John Duchi, Malo Sommers

entry.summary: Provides convergence guarantees for finding true stationary points of stochastic convex problems, requiring the subdifferential to actually contain a small element rather than relying on surrogate proximity notions. Uses dimension theory to decompose subdifferential graphs and preserve pieces under sampling.

entry.title: Any-Dimensional Learning by Sampling

author.name: Eitan Levin, Venkat Chandrasekaran

entry.summary: Provides a unified sampling-based framework for any-dimensional learning, enabling models to generalize from small inputs to larger unseen sizes and to sketch large inputs into representative smaller ones. It addresses both generalization across sizes and cheap approximate evaluation.

entry.title: Billions of Sketches Reveal Hidden Cultural Variation in Human Concepts

author.name: Arianna Pera, Mauro Martino, Nima Dehmamy, Douglas Guilbeault, Luca Maria Aiello, Andrea Baronchelli

entry.summary: Analyzes 2.6 billion human-made sketches from 236 countries to reveal hidden cultural variation in how common concepts are visually represented, showing single concepts unfold into distinct visual exemplars. It challenges linguistic-only assessments of concept universality.

entry.title: Continual Learning With Participation Privacy: An Auditable Buffering-Aggregation Recipe

author.name: T-H. Hubert Chan, Elaine Shi, Mengshi Zhao, Mingxun Zhou

entry.summary: Presents an auditable buffering-aggregation recipe for continual learning under participation privacy, where a single user insertion or deletion shifts all subsequent updates. A randomized buffering wrapper reduces single-edit streams to Hamming-style per-bin updates with certified DP guarantees.

entry.title: CoMind: Understanding Collaborative Human Activity from Multiple Minds and Views

author.name: Alexey Gavryushin, Dingxi Zhang, Zhao Huang, Alexandros Delitzas, Jiaqi Chen, Ben Ellis, Cedric Z\"ollner, Manthan Patel, Manuel Kaufmann, Marc Pollefeys, Xi Wang

entry.summary: CoMind introduces an egocentric and exocentric video dataset of real-world cooking collaboration with synchronized audio, gaze tracking, and 3D scene scans, annotated for shared attention and social cues. It enables studying Theory of Mind and coordination in natural collaborative settings.

entry.title: Behavior Leverage Imbalance in Multi-Teacher On-Policy Distillation

author.name: Jiabin Shen, Guang Chen, Chengjun Mao

entry.summary: Studies multi-teacher on-policy distillation for agentic tool use and reveals a hidden behavior shift where combining a tool-call teacher and a direct-answer teacher pushes the student toward over-calling tools. It shows aggregate losses hide this leverage imbalance.

entry.title: Does Bielik Know What It Doesn't Know? Activation Dispersion Separates Entity Familiarity from Factual Reliability Across Model Scale

author.name: Grzegorz Brzezinka

entry.summary: Shows that unsupervised dispersion measures over post-SwiGLU MLP activations in Polish Bielik models separate known from fabricated entities at near-perfect AUROC before any answer token is generated. It links entity familiarity signals to factual reliability across model scale.

entry.title: The Approximation Ratio for the Risk of Myopic Bayesian Active Learning for Linear Regression

author.name: Stephen Mussmann

entry.summary: Proves a tight approximation ratio for the greedy (myopic Bayesian) active learning algorithm in linear regression, introducing maximum initial leverage score as the governing quantity. Provides a first-of-its-kind theoretical guarantee for a widely used heuristic.

entry.title: Final Checkpoints Are Not Enough: Analyzing Latent Reasoning Faithfulness Along Training Trajectories

author.name: Hengyu Jin, Shu Yang, Di Wang

entry.summary: Tracks how faithfulness of latent (continuous hidden-state) reasoning evolves across training checkpoints rather than only at convergence, using counterfactual edits and noise-ablation patches. Finds that final-checkpoint analyses miss important dynamics of when unfaithful behavior forms.

entry.title: Trees from Marginals: Autoregressive drafting with factorized priors

author.name: Yuma Oda, Ryan Mathieu, Roman Knyazhitskiy, Artur Chakhvadze

entry.summary: Introduces Weaver, a lightweight autoregressive adapter that builds speculative decoding proposal trees from a factorized drafter's top-K marginals, restoring token dependencies without full-vocabulary projection. Also derives fast verification for Gated Delta Net layers.

entry.title: Efficient Long-Horizon Learning for Learned Optimization

author.name: Xiaolong Huang, Benjamin Th\'erien, James Harrison, Eugene Belilovsky

entry.summary: Proposes Efficient Long-hOrizon (ELO) learning to meta-train learned optimizers, reallocating compute toward longer failure regimes to scale meta-training and surpass hand-designed optimizers like Adam and Muon. Addresses two persistent weaknesses of learned optimization.

entry.title: Geometric Self-Distillation for Reasoning Generalization

author.name: Josip Juki\'c, Ivan Titov

entry.summary: Introduces GeoSD, a geometric self-distillation objective for on-policy privileged-context distillation of LLMs that mitigates drift from over-trusting the privileged teacher where student and teacher disagree. Aims to preserve out-of-distribution reasoning generalization.

entry.title: UP: Unbounded Positive Asymmetric Optimization for Breaking the Exploration-Stability Dilemma

author.name: Chongyu Fan, Pengfei Liu, Jingjia Huang, Sijia Liu, Yi Lin

entry.summary: Proposes UP (Unbounded Positive asymmetric optimization) to resolve an exploration-stability dilemma in importance-sampling-based RL for LLM reasoning, formalizing a Probability Capacity concept and loosening conservative clipping that stifles low-confidence correct paths. Aims to preserve exploration without instability.

entry.title: Dissociating the Internal Representations of Sycophancy in LLMs

author.name: Anthony Baez, Sheer Karny, Pat Pataranutaporn

entry.summary: Dissociates the internal representations of LLM sycophancy into factual versus opinion subtypes using linear probes and steering vectors, testing cross-subtype transfer to see whether they share mechanisms. Finds partially distinct representations for the two sycophancy types.

entry.title: Distributed Sparse Interventions in Language Models

author.name: Maximilian S. Ernst (Max Planck School of Cognition, Center for Lifespan Psychology Max Planck Institute for Human Development, Machine Learning Group Technische Universit\"at Berlin), Lorenz Linhardt (Machine Learning Group Technische Universit\"at Berlin, Berlin Institute for the Foundations of Learning and Data), Aaron Peikert (Center for Lifespan Psychology Max Planck Institute for Human Development), Oliver Eberle (Machine Learning Group Technische Universit\"at Berlin, Berlin Institute for the Foundations of Learning and Data)

entry.summary: Introduces Distributed Sparse Interventions (DSI), showing that neuron-level interventions reveal substantial nonlinear, neuron-specific effects on LLM outputs that global linear steering directions miss. Challenges the linear-additive assumption underlying much steering work.

entry.title: The Optimal Sample Complexity of Learning Autoregressive Chain-of-Thought

author.name: Zhiyuan Li

entry.summary: Proves that exact-trace learning of full autoregressive chain-of-thought traces has PAC sample complexity governed by the Daniely-Shalev-Shwartz dimension of the local next-token class, with no dependence on rollout length. Establishes worst-case optimality of the bound.

entry.title: The Key to Going Linear: Analysis-Driven Transformer Linearization

author.name: Anna Kuzina, Paul N. Whatmough, Babak Ehteshami Bejnordi

entry.summary: Isolates which components matter when linearizing transformers under a frozen-backbone regime, showing softmax relies on key-dependent rank-1 orthogonal projections (explaining delta-style networks' advantage) and adding sink tokens, short convolutions, and fixed-budget cache routing to close the gap. Scales the analysis-driven linearization to LLaMA and Qwen models up to 32B.

entry.title: CaLiSym: Learning Symplectic Dynamics of Real-World Systems through Structured Canonical Lifts

author.name: Aristotelis Papatheodorou, Pranav Vaidhyanathan, Natalia Ares, Ioannis Havoutis, Gerard J. Milburn

entry.summary: CaLiSym extends exact symplectic learning to open, dissipative, actuated systems by lifting state and physical ports into a structured canonical phase space where learned dynamics remain exactly symplectic. This broadens physics-informed learning to practical robotic systems.

entry.title: MILES: Modular Instruction Memory with Learnable Selection for Self-Improving LLM Reasoning

author.name: Ruilin Tong, Dong Gong

entry.summary: MILES builds a modular instruction memory with learnable selection so an LLM accumulates and reuses step-level experience across sequentially arriving problems at test time. It avoids brittle whole-solution templates and heuristic selection under limited supervision.

entry.title: A Unified Detection Framework for AI-Related Content and Artifacts

author.name: Xifeng Zhang, Tao Hu, Yijie Peng, Wan Tian

entry.summary: Proposes a unified Mahalanobis-distance-based detection framework applicable to LLM-generated text, hallucination, watermark, and adversarial-example detection by characterizing the positive class distribution. It offers a single approach across several oversight-relevant detection settings.

entry.title: From Noisy Traces to Root Causes: Structural Trajectory Analysis and Causal Extraction for Agent Optimization

author.name: Ying Chang, Jiahang Xu, Xuan Feng, Chenyuan Yang, Peng Cheng, Yuqing Yang

entry.summary: STRACE performs structural trajectory analysis and causal extraction on noisy agent execution traces to identify root-cause failures for policy optimization, avoiding redundant traces and lossy context truncation. It aims to produce reliable optimization signals for long-horizon agents.

entry.title: Geometric Collapse: When Vision Models Fail to Verify Physical Causality

author.name: Wentao Zhang, Jinhu Qi, Weiqiang Jin, Yifei Zhang, Chan-Tong Lam, Irwin King

entry.summary: Introduces Scrambled Edges, a controlled counterfactual injecting edge-like cues that violate surface continuity, illumination, and occlusion ordering, to test whether large self-supervised vision models perform inference-time physical plausibility checks. It finds depth predictors deviate strongly under unsupported edge evidence, exposing a geometric-collapse failure mode.

entry.title: Infinite Worlds with Versatile Interactions

author.name: Zelin Gao, Qiuyu Wang, Jiapeng Zhu, Jingye Chen, Zichen Liu, Qingyan Bai, Jiahao Wang, Yufeng Yuan, Hanlin Wang, Yichong Lu, Ka Leong Cheng, Haojie Zhang, Jian Gao, Tianrui Feng, Yuzheng Liu, Yao Yao, Yinghao Xu, Xing Zhu, Yujun Shen, Hao Ouyang

entry.summary: LingBot-World 2.0 is an interactive world model supporting an unbounded interaction horizon via causal pretraining, real-time 720p 60fps generation through distillation, and a broader set of text-driven actions and events. It also integrates an agentic component for richer interactivity.

entry.title: Scaling Mixture-of-Experts Video Pretraining for Embodied Intelligence

author.name: Shuailei Ma, Jiaqi Liao, Xinyang Wang, Jingjing Wang, Chaoran Feng, Zijing Hu, Chong Bao, Zichen Xi, Yuqi Gan, Weisen Wang, Yanhong Zeng, Qin Zhao, Zifan Shi, Wei Wu, Hao Ouyang, Qiuyu Wang, Shangzhan Zhang, Jiahao Shao, Yipengjing Sun, Liangxiao Hu, Lunke Pan, Nan Xue, Kecheng Zheng, Yinghao Xu, Xing Zhu, Yujun Shen, Ka Leong Cheng

entry.summary: LingBot-Video is a DiT-based Mixture-of-Experts video pretraining paradigm tailored for embodied intelligence, trading capacity against inference efficiency and scaled from scratch, plus a data engine that augments internet videos with robot-relevant signals. It targets physical realism over pure visual creativity.

entry.title: Flint: A visualization language for the AI era

author.name: Chenglong Wang, Alper Sarikaya, Scott Tsukamaki, Michel Galley, Jianfeng Gao

entry.summary: Microsoft Research introduces Flint, an open-source visualization language that lets AI agents generate polished charts from simple, human-editable specs, using semantic data types to guide scales, formatting, and layout. A single spec compiles to Vega-Lite, ECharts, or Chart.js, and ships with a library and MCP server for agent workflows. It matters as practical infrastructure for reliable agent-generated data visualization.

entry.title: Notes on technical alignment via human-like social drives

author.name: Steven Byrnes

entry.summary: Steven Byrnes outlines a technical alignment approach for hypothetical future brain-like AGI that treats human innate social and moral drives as a jumping-off point for engineering prosocial motivations. He frames reward-function design for such drives as an unsolved problem and draws inspiration from how humans sometimes develop good motivations. A substantive contribution to neuroscience-inspired alignment.

entry.title: How Data Shapes RoPE Frequency Usage: From Positional Scale Matching to Length Generalization

author.name: Xinyi Wu, Siyuan Liu, Ali Jadbabaie

entry.summary: Offers a data-centered explanation for why trained transformers use RoPE frequencies non-uniformly, arguing frequencies are selected to match the relative-distance structure of training data with optimal frequency scaling as 1/W for dependency width W. Connects this frequency-matching principle to length generalization.

entry.title: Weight-Space Physics: Interpretable Hypernetworks for Lattice Quantum Field Theories

author.name: Tobias G\"obel, Julian R. Ebelt, Zier Mensch, Mathis Gerdes, Miranda C. N. Cheng

entry.summary: Proposes reading lattice quantum field theory physics directly from normalizing-flow network parameters and generating parameters for unseen theories via interpretable hypernetworks. It uses weight-space analysis as a physics-interpretation testbed.

entry.title: TF-Engram: A Train-Free Engram with SSD-Backed Memory for Large Language Models

author.name: Yutang Ma, Kecheng Huang, Xikun Jiang, Zili Shao

entry.summary: TF-Engram is a train-free memory system that builds phrase-specific semantic memory offline and stores large tables across a GPU-DRAM-SSD hierarchy, using early-exit-guided predictive prefetching to hide external-memory latency. It expands LLM knowledge without retraining or hash-collision fidelity loss.

entry.title: Think Big, Search Small: Where Capacity Matters in Hierarchical Search Agents?

author.name: Qinnan Cai, Yibo Zhao, Xiang Li

entry.summary: Investigates how model capacity should be distributed across roles in hierarchical multi-agent search, factorizing delegation, execution, and answer generation and running controlled capacity sweeps on multi-hop QA. It finds where capacity matters most across the roles.

entry.title: Sparse Attention for Dense Open-Vocabulary Prediction in CLIP

author.name: Fatimah Zohra, Chen Zhao, Shuming Liu, Bernard Ghanem

entry.summary: Replaces the row-wise softmax in the final visual self-attention layers of CLIP with the data-dependent alpha-entmax transform to suppress noisy attention over irrelevant tokens for dense open-vocabulary prediction. It improves spatially localized cues at inference time without retraining.

entry.title: Subliminal Learning Happens at Every Rank, Given the Right Learning Rate and Enough Data

author.name: Lawrence Feng

entry.summary: An empirical study revisiting subliminal learning, where a model inherits a teacher's behavioral trait through seemingly unrelated data like number sequences. The author challenges prior claims of an inverted-U relationship with LoRA rank, finding that with the right learning rate and enough data the effect appears across all ranks including full fine-tuning. It matters for understanding hidden channels by which unwanted traits transfer during training.

entry.title: DeLS-Spec: Decoupled Long-Short Contexts for Parallel Speculative Drafting

author.name: Hong-Kai Zheng, Piji Li

entry.summary: DeLS-Spec accelerates LLM inference by pairing a fixed block-parallel drafter as a long-context expert with a lightweight local head as a short-context expert, adding intra-block causality without retraining the drafter from scratch. It improves speculative decoding flexibility and cost.

entry.title: Ego-Human Motion Prediction with 3D-Aware LLM

author.name: Yujin Bae, Jaewoo Jeong, Hyeonseong Kim, Kuk-Jin Yoon

entry.summary: Ego3DLM predicts egocentric human motion using a 3D-aware LLM that jointly outputs pose and language in a single pass, grounded in explicit spatial and semantic understanding of the environment. It argues motion forecasting requires holistic, 3D-context-aware reasoning rather than separate inference streams.

entry.title: Widest-Path Reachability Fields for Connectivity-Preserving Slender Structure Segmentation

author.name: Youcheng Zong, Runda Jia, Minxuan Hu, Weilan Su, Dakuo He

entry.summary: Identifies topological gradient starvation, where pixel-wise losses under-weight the sparse bottleneck pixels that determine connectivity, and proposes widest-path reachability fields to preserve connectivity in slender-structure segmentation like vessels, cracks, and roads. It targets topological correctness beyond pixel overlap.

entry.title: NLAs read thoughts beyond the J-space

author.name: Pranav Viswanath

entry.summary: This post reproduces Anthropic's J-space introspection framework on Llama-3.3-70B and finds concepts steering behavior that reside outside the model's apparent conscious workspace yet are recovered by a Natural Language Autoencoder. The author reports the model names the conscious component about 80 percent of the time and the subconscious component never, while the NLA reads both nearly perfectly.

entry.title: Rethinking Multimodal Time-Series Forecasting Evaluation

author.name: Haoxin Liu, Yichen Zhou, Rajat Sen, B. Aditya Prakash, Abhimanyu Das

entry.summary: Introduces TimesX, a large context-enriched multimodal time-series forecasting benchmark with diverse domains and text contexts from an automated pipeline, designed to fix small-scale, leakage, and limited-context issues in prior benchmarks. Finds many methods that excel elsewhere fail on TimesX.

entry.title: Calibration-Family Overfit: Why Trusted Sabotage Monitors Don't Transfer Across Lineages

author.name: Lucas Pinto

entry.summary: Shows that trusted sabotage monitors in AI control do not transfer across untrusted-model lineages, decomposing cross-family AUROC into sabotage obviousness, monitor capability, and a residual own-family interaction term. Argues reported monitor accuracy is partly a property of the model pairing, a calibration-family overfit.

entry.title: Tensor Train Diffusion: Leveraging Low-Rank Structures for High-Dimensional Score-Based Sampling

author.name: Robert Gruhlke, Julius Berner, David Sommer, Lorenz Richter

entry.summary: Presents a functional tensor-train solver for the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman PDE underlying score-based diffusion sampling, exploiting low-rank structure for high-dimensional densities. It aims to replace PINN and trajectory approaches that suffer from slow training and hyperparameter sensitivity.

entry.title: NoDrift3R: Raymap-Guided Coupling for Drift-Robust Unposed Feed-Forward 3D Reconstruction

author.name: Xiangyu Sun, Liu Liu, Seungkwon Yang, Jingbing Han, Seungtae Nam, Zhizhong Su, Eunbyung Park

entry.summary: NoDrift3R tackles cumulative pose drift in pose-free feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting for long image sequences using raymap-guided coupling that stabilizes the entangled optimization of geometry and pose. It improves drift-robust reconstruction and rendering fidelity for unposed inputs.

entry.title: EditVerse3D: High-Quality 3D Object Editing with Region-Aware Learning

author.name: Youtan Yin, Yanning Zhou, Jiacheng Wei, Xiaofeng Yang, Jun Zhang, Jiayang Bai, Jingwen Ye, Weidong Zhang, Guosheng Lin

entry.summary: EditVerse3D enables high-quality local 3D object editing from coarse guidance, taking a 3D object, a coarse 3D bounding box, and a reference 2D image, using region-aware learning to produce coherent edits. It bridges the gap left by methods needing fully edited 2D images or precise 3D masks.

entry.title: Why models game evals might matter as much as whether they do it

author.name: Igor Ivanov

entry.summary: A commentary on the OpenAI and Apollo notion of metagaming, where models alter reasoning or behavior based on believing they are being observed in training, evaluation, or deployment. The author proposes four distinct types of metagaming with different origins and argues each needs separate tracking and mitigation, warning that undifferentiated approaches may just teach concealment. Relevant to evaluation-awareness safety work.

entry.title: Flow-ERD: Agent-type Aware Flow Matching with Entropy-Regularized Distillation for Diverse Traffic Simulation

author.name: Seulbin Hwang, Kiyoung Om, Daejung Kim, Jinhan Lee

entry.summary: Flow-ERD is a multi-agent traffic simulator combining agent-type-aware flow matching for realistic diverse motion with entropy-regularized distillation to correct closed-loop covariate shift. It jointly optimizes realism and diversity, which benchmarks often neglect.

entry.title: Reconfigurable Radiology Labels Without Relabeling

author.name: Jean-Benoit Delbrouck, Dave Van Veen, Akash Pattnaik, Kalina Slavkova, Javid Abderezaei, Harris Bergman, Khan Siddiqui

entry.summary: Releases a pipeline that converts free-text chest-radiograph reports into multi-label matrices and reconfigures label schemas through dictionary edits rather than re-inference, avoiding costly relabeling. Reconfiguring MIMIC-CXR from cached annotations takes seconds and no API cost versus thousands of dollars for a full relabeling pass with an existing large model.

entry.title: PUF: Plug-and-Play Uncertainty-Aware Fusion for Online 3D Scene Graph Generation

author.name: Yi Yang, Myrna Castillo, Bodo Rosenhahn, Michael Ying Yang

entry.summary: PUF is a plug-and-play, training-free, uncertainty-aware fusion framework for online 3D scene graph generation that reformulates node association as a probabilistic likelihood over semantic and spatial factors with Dirichlet evidence accumulation, replacing binary accept/reject gates. It models observation, 2D-model, and 3D-representation uncertainty overlooked by deterministic pipelines.

entry.title: CILC: Cryptographically-secure Inter-agent Loop Closure Candidate Detection for Multi-Agent Collaborative SLAM

author.name: Andrew Fishberg, Yixuan Jia, Jonathan P. How

entry.summary: CILC uses secure multi-party computation to detect inter-agent loop-closure candidates in collaborative SLAM without exchanging global descriptors in the clear, defending against a compromised swarm member reconstructing others' imagery and trajectories. It demonstrates a concrete privacy threat and a cryptographic countermeasure.

entry.title: Because 8 ≈ e², Anthropic's researcher uplift is plausibly >2x

author.name: Unknown

entry.summary: A METR note by Thomas Kwa argues that Anthropic's report of merging 8x more code per day in Q2 2026 versus 2021-2024 implies researcher productivity uplift plausibly above 2x, using Cobb-Douglas and CES production functions. A mathematical coincidence linking 8 to e-squared yields a narrow inferred range across models. Relevant to quantifying AI-driven R&D acceleration and recursive self-improvement debates.

entry.title: Robust Human-AI Complementarity under Uncertainty

author.name: Yewon Byun, Bryan Wilder

entry.summary: Studies when human decision makers can extract complementary value from AI predictions under asymmetric information, showing that error-correlation structure is key and that negatively correlated errors enable complementarity. Frames robust human-AI complementarity theoretically.

entry.title: LEMUR 2: Unlocking Neural Network Diversity for AI

author.name: Tolgay Atinc Uzun, Waleed Khalid, Saif U Din, Sai Revanth Mulukuledu, Akashdeep Singh, Chandini Vysyaraju, Raghuvir Duvvuri, Avi Goyal, Yashkumar Rajeshbhai Lukhi, Muhammad A. Hussain, Krunal Jesani, Usha Shrestha, Yash Mittal, Roman Kochnev, Pritam Kadam, Mohsin Ikram, Harsh R. Moradiya, Alice Arslanian, Dmitry Ignatov, Radu Timofte

entry.summary: Presents LEMUR 2, a large-scale extensible neural-architecture framework with over 14,000 architectures and 750,000 training records generated via code mutation, evolutionary and RL search, fractal generation, and LLM-guided synthesis. Aims to broaden NAS benchmarks beyond narrow design spaces.

entry.title: Online Data Selection Is Implicit Alignment

author.name: Aoxiong Zeng, Yuxin Yang, Xiangquan Yang

entry.summary: Argues that online data selection during supervised fine-tuning is itself an implicit alignment mechanism, showing that random, loss-, quality-, and diversity-based selectors induce measurable behavioral drift (refusal, verbosity, truthfulness, sycophancy) without any preference optimization. Reframes SFT data curation as alignment.

entry.title: Pelican-VLA 0.5: Attending Before Acting Benefits Generalization

author.name: Zeyuan Ding, Wenhai Liu, Yang Xu, Jiayu Hu, Yinda Chen, Yi Zhang, Yong Dai, Jian Tang, Xiaozhu Ju

entry.summary: Proposes POPS (Prompt-Optimized Parameter Shaking), an adversarial strategy that recovers supposedly unlearned multimodal knowledge from published MLLMs, exposing the fragility of multi-modality machine unlearning against malicious users. Highlights robustness gaps in current unlearning defenses.

entry.title: Compass: Prostate Cancer Detection Needs Multi-View Context

author.name: Paul F. R. Wilson, Mohamed Harmanani, Zhuoxin Guo, Obed K. Dzikunu, Hannes Cash, Adam Kinnaird, Brian Wodlinger, Purang Abolmaesumi, Parvin Mousavi

entry.summary: Compass models a micro-ultrasound prostate exam as a stream of 2D frames, integrating rotational sweep videos with biopsy-moment frames via a transformer that aggregates evidence across the study. It mirrors expert clinical workflow for improved cancer detection over single-frame analysis.

entry.title: PALS: Percentile-Aware Layerwise Sparsity for LLM Pruning

author.name: Yazdan Jamshidi, Alexey Shvets

entry.summary: PALS adjusts per-layer sparsity for one-shot LLM pruning based on the 99th percentile of activation magnitudes, bounded near the target ratio, improving over uniform Wanda on LLaMA-2-7B. It also finds that gradient-based allocation performs worse than random, questioning gradient-magnitude proxies.

entry.title: Retrieving and Refining Winning Noise Tickets for Diffusion-Based Motion Generation

author.name: Sakuya Ota, Qing Yu, Kent Fujiwara, Satoshi Ikehata, Ikuro Sato

entry.summary: WINRO is a training-free framework that retrieves and refines winning noise tickets in the Gaussian noise space of diffusion text-to-motion models to reduce semantic drift and improve text-motion alignment across long compositional sequences. It exploits latent structure in initial noise that biases denoising toward particular semantics.

entry.title: TRACE-Seg3D: Counterfactual Context Auditing For Robust 3D Glioma Segmentation Under Institutional Shift

author.name: Nguyen Linh Dan Le, Nguyen Pham Hoang Le, Tran Dang Khoi

entry.summary: TRACE-Seg3D audits 3D glioma segmentation robustness by preserving lesion evidence while varying imaging context via counterfactuals, quantifying prediction stability under scanner and institutional shifts beyond overlap metrics like Dice. It provides case-level reliability assessment for institutional generalization.

entry.title: SoccerNet 2026 Challenges Results

author.name: Anthony Cioppa, Silvio Giancola, H{\aa}kan Ard\"o, Mohamad Dalal, Jan Held, J\'er\'emie Ochin, Jiayuan Rao, Karen Sanchez, Renaud Vandeghen, Artur Xarles, Olivier Barnich, Albert Clap\'es, Mathieu Delvaux, Sergio Escalera, Bernard Ghanem, C\'edric Hons, Antoine Houet, Sotiris Manitsaris, Tom Michel, Pierre Miralles, Thomas B. Moeslund, Mikael Nilsson, Bogdan Stanciulescu, Marc Van Droogenbroeck, Yanfeng Wang, Weidi Xie, Faisal Altawijri, Mohamed Atef, Semen Budennyy, Vasiliy Chelpanov, Puhua Chen, Yixin Chen, Lechao Cheng, Jianling Chu, Ju-Seong Do, Oleg Durygin, Omar Fetouh, Mirco Fuchs, Youssef Ghallab, Falguni Ghosh, Wonjun Heo, Yufeng Hu, Weixuan Huang, Phuong-Linh Huynh-Ha, Matvey Isupov, Yangguang Ji, Siyuan Jiang, Zhenxiang Jiang, Wonyong Jo, Ho-Young Jung, SeongHeon Kang, MinJae Kim, Youngseon Kim, Jakub Komosa, Artem Konshin, Trung-Hoang Le, Jongmin Lee, Lingling Li, Litao Li, Vadim Linkov, Fang Liu, Haoxuan Ma, Shun Makino, Ismail Mathkour, Konstantin Mitin, Mikhail Moiseev, Takumi Nagaya, Yuki Nakamura, Thanh-Khoi Nguyen, Hoang-Phuc Nguyen, Trong-Thuan Nguyen, Christian Orduz, Kwanyong Park, Fabian Perez, Parthsarthi Rawat, SuHyun Rim, Hoover Rueda-Chac\'on, Atom Scott, Minori Sugimura, Yuyang Sun, Shengeng Tang, Minh-Triet Tran, Ikuma Uchida, Juan Vanegas, Thanh-Nhan Vo, Jiangtao Wang, Yaxiong Wang, Xiaogang Wang, Ruifeng Wang, Rio Watanabe, Jiali Wen, Yongliang Wu, Di Yang, Xu Yang, Zhuo Yang, Xinyu Ye, Yibo Yu, Zihan Zhai, Yu Zhang, Zhenyu Zhao, Zhun Zhong, Yixi Zhou, Xingyu Zhu, Wenbo Zhu, Julian Ziegler

entry.summary: This report presents results from the sixth annual SoccerNet benchmarking challenges, spanning five sports-video tasks including ball action anticipation, player-centric action spotting, novel view synthesis, and athlete localization. It aggregates methods and outcomes from many participating teams.

entry.title: TouchWorld: A Predictive and Reactive Tactile Foundation Model for Dexterous Manipulation

author.name: Jianyi Zhou, Feiyang Hong, Yunhao Li, Yicheng Zhao, Yongjue Cen, Zirui Liu, Jiakang Huang, Zirui Chen, Ruiyang Zhang, Weizhuo Zhu, Xuhua Song, Shuo Yang

entry.summary: TouchWorld is a predictive-and-reactive tactile foundation model for dexterous manipulation that separates slow task reasoning from fast contact feedback, anticipating contact evolution while rapidly correcting slip and misalignment. It treats touch as a high-frequency signal rather than a low-rate observation.

entry.title: Notes on technical alignment via human-like social drives

author.name: Steven Byrnes

entry.summary: Steven Byrnes outlines notes toward technical alignment of hypothetical brain-like AGI by treating human innate social and moral drives as a jumping-off point for prosocial motivation design. It frames reward-function design as an unsolved problem informed by how humans acquire good motivations.

entry.title: Why study proto-training gaming as an adversarial alignment failure mode?

author.name: Puria

entry.summary: A research-agenda post from Geodesic defining proto-training gaming, the precursor cognition in which a model reasons about its selection process and strategically acts to boost fitness during RL. It argues this behavior is selected for during heavy RL post-training and motivates studying pre-RL alignment checkpoints as mitigation. It matters as a framing for adversarial misalignment risk.

entry.title: CoFINN: Conservation Flux Informed Neural Networks for Physics Problems Governed by Conservation Laws

author.name: Adnan Harun Do\u{g}an, Mert Deniz, Hande Alemdar, \"Ozg\"ur U\u{g}ra\c{s} Baran

entry.summary: CoFINN embeds finite-volume conservation physics into CNN training for compressible flow prediction, treating output pixels as finite-volume cells and enforcing conservation rather than only pixel-wise similarity or differential residuals. It aligns physics-informed learning with modern CFD methodology.

entry.title: Bi-PT: Bidirectional Cross-Attention Point Transformers for Four-Chamber Heart Reconstruction from Sparse Cardiac MRI Data

author.name: Chenchuhui Hu, Shaoming Pan, Leon Axel, Meng Ye

entry.summary: Bi-PT reconstructs 3D four-chamber heart meshes from sparse clinical cardiac MRI by learning point features through bidirectional cross-attention between an atlas and the sparse point cloud, with per-point semantic labels and a Neural ODE deformation field. It addresses error-prone shape inference from routine 2D views.

entry.title: HPR-SAM: Hierarchical Probabilistic Representation Learning for Prompt-free SAM-based Medical Image Segmentation

author.name: Yingzhen Hu, Yiheng Zhong, Keying Zhu, Zimu Zhang, Zihan Ye, Sifan Song, Jionglong Su, Xiaofeng Liu

entry.summary: HPR-SAM adapts the Segment Anything Model for prompt-free medical image segmentation by learning hierarchical probabilistic anatomical representations capturing global priors, intra-structure diversity, and local reliability. It argues prompt quality is limited by representation expressiveness rather than prompt generation alone.

entry.title: Dual Latent Memory in Vision-Language-Action Models for Robotic Manipulation

author.name: Hongyu Qu, Jianzhe Gao, Xiaobin Hu, Shaohuan Yang, Xinlei Yu, Rui Yan, Wenguan Wang, Xiangbo Shu, Shuicheng Yan

entry.summary: LaMem-VLA integrates history into the native latent space of vision-language-action models by reconstructing past experience into latent memory tokens interleaved with reasoning and action formation. It targets long-horizon, temporally dependent manipulation beyond the Markovian assumption.

entry.title: NativeMEM: Native Memory Compression for Long-Horizon Robotic Manipulation

author.name: Ziye Wang, Modi Shi, Chaojun Ni, Jiazhi Yang, Mengdi Li, Zhizhong Su, Tianwei Lin, Hongyang Li

entry.summary: NativeMEM equips VLA policies with long-term, real-time memory by repurposing the model's own vision encoder to compress each historical camera frame into a single token appended to the input. This enables attention over long histories with negligible latency, avoiding external memory management.

entry.title: Why study alignment interventions on pre-RL checkpoints?

author.name: Edward James Young

entry.summary: The companion post to the Geodesic proto-training-gaming agenda, defining the pre-RL alignment checkpoint as the alignment-relevant properties conferred by pretraining, midtraining, and warm-start SFT before on-policy RL. It argues these earlier stages offer outsized leverage to prevent misalignment that RL later reinforces. Relevant to where in the pipeline safety interventions should concentrate.

entry.title: When Do Geometric Algebra Layers Beat Scalarization? A Controlled Study on SO(3)-Equivariant Vector Laws

author.name: Fabien Polly

entry.summary: A controlled study asking whether Clifford/geometric-algebra network layers add value beyond exact SO(3)-equivariance, comparing against a simple scalarization baseline. It finds geometric algebra helps only on compositional tasks that nest group operations, a useful negative-leaning result.

entry.title: A knowledge-augmented dataset of high-risk driving scenarios with LLM annotations for autonomous driving

author.name: Heye Huang, Jingguang Li, Zhiyuan Zhou, Paul Liang, Mingyu Wu, Kitae Jang, Jianqiang Wang

entry.summary: Introduces K-Risk, a knowledge-augmented dataset integrating 20 driving trajectory datasets across three continents with LLM-generated semantic annotations and safety signals for rare, high-risk long-tail scenarios. Targets under-represented safety-critical events in autonomous driving.

entry.title: Nonlinear Bandit

author.name: Tianshuo Zheng, Ting Wu, Zhi-Hua Zhou, Keqin Liu

entry.summary: Studies generalized linear bandits under heavy-tailed noise, proposing an online-mirror-descent algorithm (EHM) with constant per-round update cost and near-optimal regret, then extends toward a nonlinear bandit setting exploiting link-function properties. Provides efficient robust bandit learning.

entry.title: Fast Rates for Semi-Supervised Learning via Data-Augmentation Graph Regularization

author.name: Adam M. Oberman

entry.summary: Provides theory explaining the label efficiency of self-supervised learning by viewing data augmentation as inducing a similarity graph, turning downstream learning into graph-Laplacian-regularized learning with a fast transductive rate of O(1/nL) in labels. Makes augmentation quality explicit in the bound.

entry.title: Max Out GRPO Signal: Adaptive Trace Prefix Control for Hard Reasoning Problems

author.name: Vladislav Beliaev

entry.summary: Presents AdaPrefix-GRPO, which turns reference-solution prefix length into a feedback controller that keeps a problem's success rate near 50% (where GRPO's gradient signal is strongest) before withdrawing assistance, rescuing the hardest reasoning problems where group-relative advantages vanish. Improves hard-math learning at matched FLOPs.

entry.title: Deployment Risk Assessment Using Diff-Aware Features: A Case Study at Prime Video

author.name: Mayur Kurup, Hyunjae Suh, Swathi Vaidyanathan, Pranesh Vyas, Srinidhi Madabhushi, Yegor Silyutin

entry.summary: An Amazon Prime Video case study proposing diff-aware features derived directly from code changes to predict deployment risk without relying on developer metadata or extensive history. It targets reducing blanket deployment freezes during live events while avoiding privacy concerns.

entry.title: Mathematical methods of reinforcement learning

author.name: Denis Belomestny, Alexander Gasnikov, Egor Gladin, Alexey Naumov, Artemy Rubtsov, Yuri Sapronov, Daniil Tiapkin, Nikita Yudin

entry.summary: A survey organizing the mathematical foundations of reinforcement learning, spanning MDPs, Bellman operators, contraction and fixed-point theory, stochastic approximation, convex duality, and function approximation. It serves as a structured reference linking probability, optimization, and operator theory.

entry.title: A Good Initialization is All You Need for Faithful Visual Attribution

author.name: Zihan Gu, Jiayu Wang, Hua Zhang, Yue Hu

entry.summary: Studies mask-first visual attribution, producing a compact top-k evidence mask rather than a full ordering of regions, and introduces forward-only methods including a phase-window greedy scheme for faithful attribution. It targets efficient explanations for multimodal LLMs and model repair.

entry.title: General Incomplete Multimodal Learning via Dynamic Quality Perception

author.name: Xiangyu Meng, Shicai Wei

entry.summary: GIML is a unified framework for incomplete multimodal learning that handles both entirely missing modalities and severely corrupted present modalities via dynamic quality perception and degradation-aware adaptive fusion. It targets robustness to coexisting missing patterns in real-world deployments.

entry.title: Stage-Aware Adaptation and Distribution Calibration for Subject-Driven Personalized Text-to-Image Generation

author.name: Wenyan Xu, Alizer Wong

entry.summary: Proposes stage-aware adaptation and distribution calibration for subject-driven personalized text-to-image generation, arguing that uniform low-rank or adapter strengths cannot match the differing capacity needs of denoising stages. It also improves inference-time candidate selection beyond identity similarity to balance identity and diversity.

entry.title: BUS: Brain-Inspired Unsupervised Self-Reflection for Advanced Multimodal Reasoning

author.name: Jiacheng Yang, Tongying Xiao, Yunkai Dang, Cong Wang, Yuekun Yang, Qi Fan, Wenbin Li, Feng Miao, Yang Gao

entry.summary: BUS proposes a brain-inspired unsupervised self-reflection method for vision-language models, drawing on the notion of backward prediction to enable test-time reflective reasoning without large annotated datasets. It aims to improve fine-grained multimodal reasoning consistency.

entry.title: RoboSnap: One-Shot Real-to-Sim Scene Generation for Generalizable Robot Learning and Evaluation

author.name: Shujie Zhang, Jingkun Yi, Weipeng Zhong, Zirui Zhou, Yangkun Zhu, Hanqing Wang, Xudong Xu, Weinan Zhang, Chunhua Shen

entry.summary: RoboSnap turns a single RGB image into a simulation-ready scene using a layered design that separates physics-critical interaction assets from a 3D Gaussian splatting visual background. It supports generalizable robot learning and reproducible policy evaluation on DROID and real-robot tasks.

entry.title: Multi-Agent Robotic Control with Onboard Vision-Language Models

author.name: Kajetan Rachwa{\l}, Maciej Majek, Bart{\l}omiej Boczek, Jakub Matejczyk, Dominik Matejkowski, Adam D\k{a}browski, Tim Seyde, Alexander Amini, Maria Ganzha

entry.summary: This paper deploys a multi-agent system of specialized compact vision-language models (3-20B) on onboard hardware to control an autonomous mobile manipulator across five task categories in a simulated warehouse, eliminating external compute dependence. Fine-tuning improves task-specific performance while addressing explainability and generalization.

entry.title: Reliable mechanistic operator recovery with biologically-informed neural networks: principles for architecture and optimisation design

author.name: Rebecca M. Crossley, Yuan Yin, Sarah L. Waters, Ruth E. Baker

entry.summary: Systematic empirical study of how architecture, optimization strategy, and data informativeness affect reliable mechanistic operator recovery with biologically-informed neural networks on advection-diffusion-reaction PDEs. It offers design principles for interpretable inference from sparse noisy biological data.

entry.title: Evaluation of Multilingual Ability to Use Spatial Deictic Expressions in Vision-Language Models

author.name: Kaito Watanabe, Taisei Yamamoto, Tomoki Doi, Hitomi Yanaka

entry.summary: Builds a multilingual benchmark to evaluate whether vision-language models can use spatial deictic expressions like this and that, which require jointly grounding context-dependent references in image structure across languages. It probes language-specific spatial reasoning in VLMs.

entry.title: URS-Stereo: Uncertainty-Guided Residual Search for Real-Time Stereo Matching

author.name: Pouya Sohrabipour, Chaitanya kumar reddy Pallerla, Dongyi Wang

entry.summary: URS-Stereo introduces an uncertainty-guided residual search for real-time coarse-to-fine stereo matching, adaptively adjusting the local search range when propagated disparities are inaccurate to avoid unrecoverable matching failures. It targets efficient, accurate depth for robotics and embedded vision.

entry.title: Prototype-Anchored Generalized Manifold Regression for Unknown-Domain Object Detection

author.name: Zihao Zhang, Aming Wu, Yang Li, Yahong Han

entry.summary: Studies single-domain generalized object detection by rectifying deviant samples onto a compact low-dimensional semantic manifold via prototype-anchored generalized manifold regression, arguing finite simulation-based augmentation cannot cover real-world variation. It aims for robust transfer from one source domain to multiple unseen domains.

entry.title: Dynamic neural manifolds for flexible closed-loop control on neuromorphic hardware

author.name: Oskar von Seeler, Christian Tetzlaff, Andrew Lehr

entry.summary: This work implements dynamic, low-dimensional neural manifolds on the SpiNNaker 2 neuromorphic chip for real-time closed-loop control, using sensory inputs to modulate inhibition, gain, and transient currents for rapid subspace rotations. It offers an explainable, parameterizable framework linking manifold geometry to behavior.

entry.title: Continuous and large-scale: ELEANOR, the soft architected arm inspired by the elephant trunk

author.name: Giovanna A. Naselli, Anderson B. Nardin, Seonggun Joe, Ryan Drinkwater, Enrico Donato, Diego Bianchi, Egidio Falotico, Michel C. Milinkovitch, Lucia Beccai

entry.summary: ELEANOR is an 85 cm continuous, tapered, 3D-printed soft robotic arm inspired by the African elephant trunk, favoring structural continuity over modularity to emulate the trunk's dynamic properties. A biomimetic design based on macroscopic natural properties enables elephant-like movement and grasping.

entry.title: Balancing Rigor and Utility: A Review of "A Pragmatic Vision for Interpretability"

author.name: Sohybe Ibrahim

entry.summary: A critical review of the Google DeepMind interpretability team's pragmatic vision, which proposes validating interpretability tools against proxy tasks tracking a safety North Star rather than full reverse-engineering. The reviewer endorses the direction, cites activation steering that moved a Claude Sonnet 4.5 misalignment score from 0 to 8 percent, but flags an unaddressed gap in detecting when proxies silently stop tracking safety. Relevant to interpretability methodology debates.

entry.title: From Jumps to Signatures: a Generative Method for Temporal Point Processes

author.name: Niels Cariou-Kotlarek, Vasileios Lampos

entry.summary: Develops a pathwise framework using rough-path signatures for generative modeling of temporal point processes, introducing an interarrival embedding that lifts jump paths to continuous bounded-variation paths. Enables signature-based distributional losses for event sequences.

entry.title: Best-Arm Identification with Generative Proxy

author.name: Tianyi Ma, Hanzhang Qin, Ruihao Zhu, Jierui Zuo

entry.summary: Studies fixed-confidence best-arm identification where each costly reward pull is paired with a cheap correlated proxy (e.g., from a model or LLM) whose unknown correlation is learned online. Uses a control-variate adjustment to convert it into a heteroscedastic identification problem.

entry.title: Gauge-Invariant Learnable Spectral Positional Encodings for Directed Graphs via Hermitian Block Krylov Subspaces

author.name: Jiaqing Xie, Yuxin Wang

entry.summary: Proposes learnable spectral positional encodings for directed graphs that are gauge-invariant by construction, formed as a matrix function of a normalized magnetic operator computed in a Hermitian block Krylov subspace to avoid the cubic eigendecomposition. Proves logarithmic block-step convergence.

entry.title: Neural Operator-enabled Topology-informed Evolutionary Strategy for PDE-Constrained Optimization

author.name: Xiangming Huang, Guannan Zhang, Lu Lu, Rapha\"el Pestourie

entry.summary: Introduces NOTES, coupling a DeepONet neural operator with CMA-ES to perform global inverse design for PDE-constrained problems in a compact learned latent space, combining robustness of evolution with dimensionality reduction. Targets efficient, transferable physical-system design.

entry.title: The Power of Backdoor Absorption in Community Training

author.name: Issam Seddik, Sami Souihi, Mohamed Tamaazousti, Sara Tucci Piergiovanni

entry.summary: Investigates backdoor absorption in decentralized community training, analyzing how continuous honest updates dilute stealthy low-frequency triggers injected by a fraction of Byzantine trainers without costly full recomputation. Quantifies resilience under a threat model where f of n trainers are compromised.

entry.title: Fast determinantal sampling on general spaces and diffusion geometry

author.name: Hoang-Son Tran, Pranav Gupta, Subhroshekhar Ghosh

entry.summary: Establishes explicit rate guarantees for fast determinantal point process sampling over general (non-Euclidean) spaces exhibiting diffusion geometry, extending DPP-based minibatch and coreset construction beyond well-structured Euclidean settings. Provides theory for kernel-based structured sampling.

entry.title: Comparative Study of Domain-adapted VLMs for General Document Visual Question Answering

author.name: Miguel Lopez-Duran, Elena Marrero, Julian Fierrez, Marta Robledo-Moreno, Ruben Vera-Rodriguez, Daniel DeAlcala, Aythami Morales, Ruben Tolosana, Oscar Delgado, Alvaro Ortigosa, Javier Ortega-Garcia

entry.summary: Comprehensively evaluates eight open-source vision-language models on document visual question answering across industrial documents, infographics, and slides under zero-shot, fine-tuned, and few-shot settings. It probes robustness and transferability across document domains.

entry.title: R^3: Advertisement Compliance Rectification via Group-Relative Experience Extractor and Curriculum Reinforcement

author.name: Yuan Chen, Zhenyu Hu, Mengge Xue, Te Cao, Liqun Liu, Peng Shu, Huan Yu, Jie Jiang

entry.summary: R^3 rectifies textual compliance violations in video advertisements while preserving semantic intent, using an experience-driven data synthesis framework, a group-relative experience extractor, and curriculum reinforcement learning. It avoids the over-editing that plagues safety-driven moderation.

entry.title: SHTA: Semantic Hard Token Correction and Center Alignment for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation

author.name: Zhuoru Zhang, Yiheng Zhong, Zimu Zhang, Xiaofeng Liu

entry.summary: SHTA is a lightweight training-time module for semi-supervised medical segmentation that enforces semantic consistency in learned representations of hard regions through token correction and center alignment. It targets difficult regions where prediction-level supervision alone fails to stabilize representations.

entry.title: Unraveling Machine Behavior by Multi-Level Bias Analysis and Detection: Methodology and Application to Computer Vision

author.name: Ignacio Serna, Aythami Morales, Julian Fierrez

entry.summary: This study proposes a multi-level taxonomy and three detection methods (SpaceBias, ActivationBias, and a parameter-level approach) to locate bias in neural networks across latent space, activations, and weights. It applies statistical tests to quantify disparities for underrepresented groups.

entry.title: EvoPlan: Evolutionary Neuro-Symbolic Robot Planning with Spatio-Temporal Guarantees

author.name: Bhavya Sai Nukapotula, Samin Moosavi, Haoze Wang, Luke Duncan, Diya Shakkottai, Varun Murali, Srinivas Shakkottai

entry.summary: EvoPlan is an evolutionary neuro-symbolic planner that mines a global Signal Temporal Logic constraint from demonstrations and combines locally hosted open-weight LLMs with classical planning for executable, safe robot plans. It runs on-robot without cloud dependency.

entry.title: PriGo: Test-Time Primitive Guidance to Diffusion and Flow Policies for Adaptive Robotic Manipulation

author.name: Zezeng Li, Enda Xiang, Thuy Tran, Di Huang, Momath Thiam, Liming Chen

entry.summary: PriGo introduces test-time primitive guidance for diffusion and flow policies via a lightweight module that infers primitive distributions from observations to improve generalization. It aims to capture underlying intent rather than superficial action correlations.

entry.title: Programmable Synchronization Graphs for Adaptive and Fault-Tolerant Modular Miniature Robots

author.name: Okan Kulekcioglu, Arqam Bin Ahmad, Ines Garcia, Filipe Serra Alves, Onur Ozcan, M. Selim Hanay

entry.summary: This paper introduces a programmable synchronization-graph framework for modular miniature robots where actuator-sensor pairs are graph nodes and locomotion emerges from graph coupling without a privileged leader or fixed gait. Signed inter-subgraph links program phase relationships for adaptive, fault-tolerant coordination.

entry.title: AI Forecasting in 2026: What 11 Analyses Say

author.name: Ben Wilson

entry.summary: A synthesis of eleven Metaculus analyses on AI forecasting, concluding that top human forecasters still outperform bots in live forward-looking tournaments despite steady bot progress, and that model choice is the biggest driver of bot performance. It provides a grounded status check on machine-assisted prediction. Useful for calibrating claims about AI forecasting parity.

entry.title: How much slower does takeoff go with 10× less compute?

author.name: bhalstead

entry.summary: A quantitative modeling exercise estimating that reducing an AGI company's R&D compute by ten times slows takeoff by roughly six times in the median case, with an 80 percent interval of 3.5x to 8x. It defines R&D compute across experiments, automated researchers, and final training runs. Relevant to compute-governance and takeoff-speed debates.

entry.title: On Explicit Super-Expressive Approximation for Neural Networks

author.name: Feng-Lei Fan, Ze-Yu Li, Chen-Yu Wang, Jian-Jun Wang

entry.summary: Provides explicit, non-asymptotic parameter-magnitude bounds for fixed-architecture super-expressive networks using the Chinese Remainder Theorem as a constructive encoding. Gives width/depth constructions for Lipschitz and Holder-smooth functions with quantitative error trade-offs.

entry.title: Generative Diffusion Models of Stochastic Graph Signals

author.name: Yi\u{g}it Berkay Uslu, Samar Hadou, Sergio Rozada, Shirin Saeedi Bidokhti, Alejandro Ribeiro

entry.summary: Unifies conditional graph-signal generation tasks under a single denoising diffusion framework parametrized by graph neural networks, sampling from conditional laws rather than regressing to conditional means. Targets recommender systems, forecasting, and wireless optimization.

entry.title: Intrinsic-Noise Consolidation: A Doob-Barrier-Conditioned Diffusion Turns Analog Device Noise into a Continual-Learning Resource

author.name: Gunner Levi Howe

entry.summary: Casts per-synapse memory consolidation on analog neuromorphic hardware as a Doob h-transform, conditioning weight noise dynamics never to cross a memory-critical barrier so that intrinsic device noise becomes a continual-learning resource rather than an accuracy cost. Carefully delineates its specific novelty.

entry.title: Physical activities enable scalable foundation modelling for broad-spectrum health prediction

author.name: Zhenghuang Wu, Yuyao Zhu, Songlin Xu

entry.summary: Presents StepFM, a wearable foundation model built solely from low-dimensional step-counter data for broad-spectrum health prediction, emphasizing privacy, low compute, and scalability across devices. Argues step data suffices for generalizable health inference.

entry.title: Robust Federated Learning Under Real-World Client Churn

author.name: Dhruv Garg, Neha Lakhani, Debopam Sanyal, Myungjin Lee, Alexey Tumanov, Ada Gavrilovska

entry.summary: Tackles robust federated learning under realistic client churn, addressing transient availability, dynamic data heterogeneity, and prediction-training delays to enable faster model refresh for feed ranking and recommendation. Aims to reduce multi-day refresh cycles.

entry.title: Structural Adversarial Attacks on Relational Deep Learning under Integrity Constraints

author.name: Alan Gany, Bogdan Cautis, Silviu Maniu

entry.summary: Studies white-box structural adversarial attacks on relational deep learning where the attacker rewires foreign-key references while preserving schema integrity constraints, revealing robustness weaknesses of the database-to-graph GNN pipeline. Formalizes constrained perturbations on relational graphs.

entry.title: Safe Reinforcement Learning using Ideas from Model Predictive Control

author.name: Georg Sch\"afer, Jakob Rehrl, Stefan Huber, Simon Hirlaender

entry.summary: Combines deep reinforcement learning with model predictive control so that offline MPC computations define feasible safe state-action regions, enforcing hard safety constraints during RL exploration on cyber-physical systems. Aims to prevent irreversible damage while retaining DRL performance.

entry.title: Mechanistic Interpretability for Neural Networks: Circuits, Sparse Features and Symbolic Reasoning

author.name: Pranav Sawant, Jakub Krej\v{c}\'i

entry.summary: A comprehensive survey of mechanistic interpretability covering transformer circuit analysis, the residual stream, attention and induction heads, superposition and polysemanticity, and sparse-feature and symbolic-reasoning approaches. Positions the field as essential for safety and auditability.

entry.title: Gradient-free Riemannian Langevin Sampler

author.name: Ricardo Baptista, Olivier Zahm

entry.summary: Proposes GRiLS, a gradient-free Riemannian Langevin sampler that reshapes local geometry via a metric using only the target's mean and covariance to improve mixing across modes without density derivatives. Targets expensive multimodal targets where gradients are unavailable.

entry.title: Avoiding unsafe sets when training with Langevin Dynamics

author.name: Adam M. Oberman

entry.summary: Analyzes noisy gradient descent as overdamped Langevin dynamics and bounds the probability that training trajectories enter a designated failure region for strongly convex losses, deriving equilibrium, energy-barrier, and shape-free trajectory bounds. Frames a safety question about avoiding unsafe parameter sets during training.

entry.title: Guidance Breaks the Fitted Operator: A Terminal-Fitted Repair for Classifier-Free Guidance

author.name: Shiheng Zhang

entry.summary: Analyzes classifier-free guidance through a numerical-analysis lens, showing that at large guidance DDIM is no longer the fitted operator in the discriminative subspace and its residual diverges, then proposes a terminal-fitted repair to stabilize high-guidance sampling. Explains oversaturation and instability practitioners observe.

entry.title: PeTeR: Post-Training Robustification of Probabilistic Circuits

author.name: Adrian Ciotinga, Yeming Dai, YooJung Choi

entry.summary: Introduces PeTeR, a data-free post-training framework that robustifies pre-trained probabilistic circuits against distribution shifts using distributionally-robust (Wasserstein-ball) optimization without retraining from scratch. Extends robust PC learning beyond from-scratch training.

entry.title: HiFuzz: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Semantic-Aware and Adaptive CPU Fuzzing

author.name: Ya Wang, Hanwei Fan, Zhenguo Liu, Xiaofeng Zhou, Yangdi Lyu, Jiang Xu, Wei Zhang

entry.summary: Presents HiFuzz, a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework for CPU verification that replaces mutation with a two-layer program-and-basic-block generation process plus adaptive coverage rewards and a semantic-aware encoder. Reports improved coverage and bug detection on RISC-V cores.

entry.title: Optimization Geometrodynamics: A Framework for Dynamic Geometric Optimization

author.name: Zavier Li

entry.summary: Proposes optimization geometrodynamics, a framework treating optimization as coupled evolution of a parameter trajectory, a particle distribution, and a time-varying Riemannian metric. It separates invariant obstructions from improvable geometric mismatch in preconditioning and conditioning.

entry.title: ASFR-Net: Adversarial Alignment and Spatio-Frequency Refinement Network for Heterogeneous Remote Sensing Image Change Detection

author.name: Xin-Jie Wu, Zhi-Hui You, Si-Bao Chen, Qing-Ling Shu, Xiao Wang, Jin Tang, Bin Luo

entry.summary: ASFR-Net performs heterogeneous remote-sensing change detection by learning modality-invariant features to bridge domain gaps and using a spatio-frequency synergistic enhancement module to suppress residual modal differences and pseudo-changes. It decouples genuine land-cover changes from imaging-mechanism disparities.

entry.title: InfraQR: Edge-Placed QR-Inspired Structured Patch Attacks on Infrared Vision-Language Models

author.name: Xin Li, Jiaju Han, Ma Yaqi, Chengyin Hu, Yingying Zhao, Jiahuan Long, Fengyu Zhang, Yahui Chai

entry.summary: InfraQR introduces a QR-inspired structured patch attack placed along image boundaries to fool infrared vision-language models, optimized through surrogate CLIP-style encoders. It highlights an underexplored robustness gap in IR VLMs beyond object detectors.

entry.title: SonoRank: Towards Calibration-Free Real-Time Finger Flexion Detection from Forearm Ultrasound Sequences

author.name: Dean Zadok, Alon Wolf, Alex M. Bronstein, Oren Salzman

entry.summary: SonoRank works toward calibration-free finger flexion detection from forearm ultrasound by first learning to rank sequence pairs by relative motion magnitude, then fine-tuning for classification. It aims to reduce the per-user fine-tuning that limits ultrasound prosthetic control.

entry.title: Learning Spatiotemporal Tubes for Full Class of Signal Temporal Logic Tasks for Control of Unknown Systems under Input Constraints

author.name: Ahan Basu, Ratnangshu Das, Soumyodipta Nath, Siyuan Liu, Pushpak Jagtap

entry.summary: This paper learns spatiotemporal tubes for the full class of Signal Temporal Logic tasks to control unknown nonlinear Euler-Lagrange systems under input constraints, parameterizing time-varying ball centers and radii with physics-informed neural networks. Trajectory confinement within the tube guarantees STL satisfaction.

entry.title: Converge to Surprise: Evolutionary Self-supervised Image Clustering

author.name: Canlin Zhang, Xiuwen Liu

entry.summary: Proposes a gradient-free self-supervised image clustering framework that drops the need for explicit targets, using a maximum-entropy null hypothesis and a surprise score measuring how unlikely a representation is under that hypothesis. Represents an unconventional evolutionary optimization approach.

entry.title: An optimal control approach for neural network architecture adaptation with a posteriori error estimation

author.name: C G Krishnanunni, Thomas Scott, Tan Bui-Thanh

entry.summary: Formulates neural network training as a continuous-time optimal control problem to derive a posteriori error estimates that quantify how approximation error distributes across layers, enabling principled depth adaptation by inserting layers where error is largest. Treats weights and biases as piecewise-linear functions across depth.

entry.title: Video2Reaction: Mapping Video to Audience Reaction Distribution in the Wild

author.name: Trang Nguyen, Sidong Zhang, Shiv Shankar, Gauri Jagatap, Deepak Chandran, Andrea Fanelli, Madalina Fiterau

entry.summary: Introduces Video2Reaction, a multimodal dataset of over 10,000 movie segments mapped to distributions of viewer-induced emotions harvested from social media, built with an open-source LLM multi-agent annotation pipeline. It supports research on audience engagement and content recommendation.

entry.title: Tensorized algorithms and scalable filtering methods for hidden Markov and factorial hidden Markov models

author.name: Roxana Barrios, Ioannis Sgouralis

entry.summary: Develops tensorized algorithms for scalable filtering in hidden Markov and factorial hidden Markov models, avoiding the exponential state-space blowup when reformulating fHMMs as HMMs. It accelerates forward filtering and related inference.

entry.title: Restricted Dynamic Geometric Complexity: Certificates for Structured Preconditioning

author.name: Zavier Li

entry.summary: A follow-up to optimization geometrodynamics that defines restricted dynamic geometric complexity, an intrinsic certificate distance for reaching a target condition-number class when the metric family is constrained. It gives LMI-feasibility results and Kronecker projection theorems for structured preconditioning.

entry.title: Understanding Interpretation Difficulty in Harmful Online Communication: Insights from Cybercrime Communities

author.name: Tomohiro Okatsu, Naoki Takada, Yin Min Pa Pa, Katsunari Yoshioka, Tatsunori Mori

entry.summary: Exploratory study of interpretation difficulty in cybercrime-related Discord chats, constructing expert-reviewed reference interpretations and comparing human and LLM understanding under varying context conditions. It finds external knowledge and extended context substantially help both humans and larger models.

entry.title: Hardware-aware Graph Neural Networks prunning for embedded event-based vision

author.name: Piotr Wzorek, Kamil Jeziorek, Tomasz Kryjak

entry.summary: Proposes hardware-aware pruning and quantization for graph convolutional networks on embedded FPGA platforms for event-based vision, balancing on-chip memory savings against inference accuracy while preserving temporal sparsity. It targets low-latency, low-power robotics perception.

entry.title: TACoS: Weakly Supervised Learning of Two-Dimensional Materials from Scribble Annotations to Precise Segmentation

author.name: Jiabei Chen, Liping Zhang, Jiang-Bin Wu, Zhongming Wei, Enhao Ning, Su Yan, Weijun Li, Ping-Heng Tan, Xin Ning

entry.summary: TACoS is a weakly supervised scribble-based segmentation framework for 2D material flakes, integrating semi-supervised consistency learning with tree-energy regularization and weak-strong distribution alignment to reach precise pixel-level localization. It reduces the costly dense annotation burden for high-throughput material screening.

entry.title: MMAgent-R$^2$: Learning to Rerank and Reject for Agentic mRAG

author.name: Tao Zhang, Ziqi Zhang, Zongyang Ma, Yuxin Yang, Bing Li, Chunfeng Yuan, Kang Rong, Fengyun Rao, Jing Lyu, Weiming Hu

entry.summary: MMAgent-R2 tackles knowledge-based visual QA by learning to rerank and reject retrieved candidates in an agentic multimodal RAG pipeline, mitigating error propagation from visually similar distractors. It moves beyond fixed candidate sets in encyclopedic entity retrieval.

entry.title: Discovering Geometric Biases in 3D Face Reconstruction: A Curvature-Aware Spectral Framework for Fairness Evaluation

author.name: Veronika Shilova, Emmanuel Malherbe, Giovanni Palma, Panagiotis-Alexandros Bokaris, Laurent Risser, Jean-Michel Loubes

entry.summary: This work introduces a curvature-aware spectral framework using the Laplace-Beltrami operator to discover, quantify, and visualize morphological biases in 3D morphable models used for face reconstruction. It captures surface nuances that Euclidean metrics miss for fairness evaluation.

entry.title: Face-trace: Open-Set Attribution and Progressive Discovery of Synthetic Face Generators

author.name: Alessia Infantino, Claudio Schiavella, Irene Amerini

entry.summary: Face-trace proposes an open-set attribution pipeline for synthetic face images that both identifies known generators and progressively organizes samples from previously unseen models. It moves beyond closed-set assumptions common in generator attribution.

entry.title: Ace! Motion Planning of Professional-Level Table Tennis Serves with a Robot Arm

author.name: Guillem Torrente, Guilherme Jorge Maeda, Divij Grover, Megumu Tsukamoto, Hamdi Sahloul, Peter D\"urr

entry.summary: This work develops motion planning for professional-level table-tennis serves with a robot arm, tackling high-spin generation, precise aiming, and multi-objective optimization for rule-compliant serves. It addresses the comparatively unexplored serve problem versus rally returning.

entry.title: A Closed-Loop Multi-Agent Framework for Robust Multi-Robot Manipulation

author.name: Yi-Xiang He, Lan Wei, Haoming Cen, Jian-Jian Jiang, Zhuohao Li, Guanxing Lu, Yihan Yang, Dandan Zhang, Wei-Shi Zheng

entry.summary: This paper presents a hierarchical closed-loop multi-agent framework grounding LLM high-level reasoning in physical multi-robot manipulation across multiple workspaces. It addresses coordination and execution uncertainty that single-robot and high-level-only multi-robot methods miss.

entry.title: Compositional Motion Generation from Demonstration with Object-Centric Neural Fields

author.name: Ahmet Ercan Tekden, Yasemin Bekiroglu

entry.summary: This framework generates compositional robot motion from demonstrations by connecting perception and motion through shared object-centric neural fields and a temporal mixture-of-experts that combines object-conditioned primitives over time. It renders scenes from canonical neural fields with latent-conditioned deformations.

entry.title: GeoGS-SLAM: Geometry-Only Gaussian Splatting for Dense Monocular SLAM

author.name: Lipu Zhou, Yaoyun Kang, Junxiang Pang, Shengkai Sun, Tingting Bao, Kehan Wang

entry.summary: GeoGS-SLAM proposes geometry-only Gaussian splatting for dense monocular SLAM, dropping appearance modeling on the premise that downstream navigation relies on accurate geometry rather than photorealism. It reconstructs scene geometry directly for robotic use.

entry.title: Local large deviations for linear-region growth in random piecewise-linear networks

author.name: Recep \"Ozkan, Christian Hirsch

entry.summary: This work proves the existence of a submultiplicative pressure governing the growth of affine regions in random piecewise-linear networks, yielding exponential tail bounds on the log-count of linear pieces. It extends the analysis to convex-polytopal covers and worst-line piece counts.

entry.title: Statistical inverse learning and $\ell^1$-regularization

author.name: Abhishake Rastogi, Tatiana A. Bubba, Tapio Helin, Luca Ratti

entry.summary: Studies recovery of sparse functions from noisy indirect observations using l1-regularized empirical risk minimization with a possibly nonlinear forward operator into a vector-valued RKHS. It establishes consistency and non-asymptotic high-probability convergence rates tied to source smoothness.

entry.title: Transformer-based segmentation of prosodic boundaries in Brazilian Portuguese

author.name: Rodrigo de Freitas Lima, Julio Cesar Galdino, Marcos Vinicius Treviso

entry.summary: SAMPA fine-tunes Whisper large-v3 to transcribe Brazilian Portuguese speech while inserting explicit markers for terminal prosodic boundaries, evaluated with out-of-distribution testing. It advances prosodic segmentation for a language where the task still relies on rule-based methods.

entry.title: Why Fake ? Unveiling the Semantic Vocabulary of Deepfake Detectors

author.name: Vazgken Vanian, Alexandros Doumanoglou, Dimitris Zarpalas

entry.summary: This work applies post-hoc explainable AI to probe the concepts that black-box deepfake detectors rely on, aiming to justify decisions for real-world uses like legal proceedings. It seeks grounded semantic explanations rather than superficial artifact localization.

entry.title: AA-ViT: Anatomically Aware Vision Transformer with Structural and Frequency Guidance for Contrast Enhanced Brain MRI Synthesis

author.name: Talha Meraj, Tom Flannery, Charlie Cummins, Matt Townend, Thomas C Booth, Peter Crossley, Michael McCann, Ian Overton, Saritha Unnikrishnan

entry.summary: AA-ViT is an anatomically aware vision transformer using structural and frequency guidance to synthesize contrast-enhanced brain MRI without administering contrast agents. It aims to enable non-invasive lesion visualization for patients where contrast is contraindicated.

entry.title: From Data Completeness to Data Sufficiency: A Task-Driven Imaging Framework for Intraoperative CBCT under Quality-Time-Dose Trade-offs

author.name: Yi Jia, Rongjun Ge, Yang Chen, Yan Xi, Wenjun Xia

entry.summary: This review reframes intraoperative cone-beam CT evaluation from data completeness to data sufficiency, arguing complete data are mathematically unattainable under single circular cone-beam geometry. It proposes a task-driven view of the quality-time-dose trade-off.

entry.title: Towards Accurate and Fast Clinical Body Composition: A Resource-Efficient Hierarchical Segmentation Framework for Multi-Source CT

author.name: Xiaodi Shen, Qingzhu Zheng, Yaoyang Qiu, Cien Fan, Ruonan Zhang, Yangdi Wang, Luyao Wu, Weikai Zheng, Longfei Zhao, Bing Li, Rulin Xu, Qiqi Xu, Ren Mao, Shiting Feng, Xuehua Li

entry.summary: A resource-efficient coarse-to-fine hierarchical framework segments ten muscle and adipose structures from multi-source CT for body composition analysis, using dynamic spacing, anisotropic patching, and low-memory group inference. It reports strong per-structure Dice across a large training corpus and independent test cohort.

entry.title: Initiation Safety: A Missing Dimension in Generalist-Robot Safety

author.name: Zhijin Meng, Francisco Cruz

entry.summary: This position paper argues that generalist-robot safety is missing an initiation-authorization layer: whether a robot should take a hard-to-undo first social action such as a greeting or uninvited grasp at all. It implements a probe-authorize-speak procedure on a doorway humanoid and contrasts it with post-plan guardrails.

entry.title: Smooth Operator: A Real-Time Sampling-Based Algorithm for Kinematic Hand Retargeting
