author.name: Robert Jomar Malate, Erik Bauer, Norica Bacuieti, Stefanos Charalambous, Elvis Nava, Robert K. Katzschmann, Benedek Forrai

entry.summary: Smooth Operator introduces a gradient-free sampling-based retargeter for real-time kinematic hand retargeting, aiming for low-jitter mappings that improve teleoperation data quality for VLA and video-action model training. It is evaluated in simulation and a real-world user study.

entry.title: Generating Personalized Lower-Limb Kinematics Across Walking Speeds Using Subject-Conditioned Diffusion

author.name: Diya Dinesh, Adrian Krieger, Changseob Song, Dongho Park, Aaron J. Young, Inseung Kang

entry.summary: This work presents a subject-conditioned residual diffusion model that generates personalized lower-limb kinematics at unseen walking speeds from a subject's gait at a single seen speed. It reduces the burden of repeated motion-capture sessions for exoskeleton personalization.

entry.title: Residual-Conservative Model Predictive Path Integral Control

author.name: Hyung-Jin Yoon, Hunmin Kim

entry.summary: RC-MPPI augments sampling-based model predictive path integral control with residual-dependent constraint tightening, adaptive safety-cost shaping, and residual-adaptive sampling to modulate conservatism online based on model-plant mismatch. It relaxes fixed constraint penalties that ignore prediction error.

entry.title: Transfer Learning for Linear Discriminant Analysis with a Shared Classification Signal

author.name: Yonghan Zhang, Yimeng Fan, Wenya Luo, Jiang Hu

entry.summary: This paper studies transfer learning for high-dimensional linear discriminant analysis with a shared classification signal across source and target domains, deriving deterministic limits for calibrated error under spiked covariance models. It quantifies effects of shared signal, domain heterogeneity, and dimensionality.

entry.title: Efficient Bayesian Deep Ensembles via Analytic Predictive Inference

author.name: Sina Aghaee Dabaghan Fard, Marie Maros, Jaesung Lee

entry.summary: Introduces an efficient Bayesian deep ensemble for regression that expresses predictions as a low-dimensional combination of trained predictors, yielding calibrated uncertainty and scalable analytic inference. Emphasizes interpretability alongside competitive accuracy.

entry.title: Geometric--Nongeometric Optimizer Calculus: A Modular Language for Reachable Gradient Methods

author.name: Zavier Li

entry.summary: Introduces a modular calculus separating geometric (metric/preconditioner) from nongeometric (memory, control, noise, target, discretization) mechanisms of adaptive optimizers, with a direction-expressivity theorem characterizing reachable gradient methods under explicit constraints. Provides a language for auditing optimizer design.

entry.title: HPG-Diff: Hierarchical physics-guided diffusion with differentiable connectivity constraints for topology optimization

author.name: Jinbo Yang, Mingyue Yuan, Boyuan Zhang, Yoshifumi Kitamura, Shikai Jing

entry.summary: Presents HPG-Diff, a hierarchical physics-guided diffusion model for topology optimization that aligns precomputed physics features with denoising to improve generalization and adds a mechanism to suppress floating-material artifacts. Enforces differentiable connectivity constraints.

entry.title: Higher-Order Geometric Updates for Levenberg-Marquardt Method via Riemann Normal Coordinates

author.name: Jianing Liu, Dong H. Zhang

entry.summary: Proposes a Riemann-normal-coordinate Levenberg-Marquardt method (RNC-LM) that improves geodesic-acceleration corrections beyond the infinitesimal-step limit to better handle parameter-effect curvature in nonlinear least-squares. Targets regression and physics-informed neural network training.

entry.title: ECGLight: Compute-Light Framework For Paper ECG Digitization and Myocardial Infarction Screening

author.name: Shreyasvi Natraj, Cyrus Achtari, Felice Gragnano, Andrea Milzi, Marco Valgimigli, Diego Paez-Granados

entry.summary: Presents ECGLight, a compute-light framework that jointly digitizes paper ECG printouts and screens for myocardial infarction, aimed at remote clinics with limited connectivity and hardware. Unifies digitization and diagnosis for low-resource cardiac care.

entry.title: Value of Information under Imprecise Probabilities: Decision-Rule-Specific Values and Fixed-Measure Envelopes on a Credal Set

author.name: Rowan Iskandar

entry.summary: Extends value-of-information analysis to imprecise probabilities (credal sets), distinguishing decision-rule-specific VOI (e.g., under Gamma-maximin) from fixed-measure envelopes over admissible precise measures, and derives consequences for perfect, partial, and sample information. Formalizes VOI when evidence pins down only a set of measures.

entry.title: Trajectory Inference of Human Aging from Cross-Sectional DNA Methylation Data

author.name: Chandan Gupta, Syed Haider, Pietro Li\`o

entry.summary: Frames lifelong human epigenetic aging as a trajectory-inference problem across age snapshots from cross-sectional DNA methylation data, using an age-regularized variational autoencoder to reconstruct continuous CpG dynamics rather than a single clock score. Aims to simulate how methylation profiles change over time.

entry.title: ORAN-DEFEND: Subspace Detection and Sanitization of Backdoor DRL xApps in Open RAN

author.name: Md Raihan Uddin, Fatemeh Lotfi, Tolunay Seyfi, Fatemeh Afghah

entry.summary: Presents ORAN-DEFEND, a retraining-free wrapper that sanitizes potentially backdoored deep-RL xApps in Open RAN by projecting each KPI telemetry window onto a safe subspace estimated via SVD from a small set of trusted clean rollouts. Provides analytical and empirical recovery guarantees against covert triggers.

entry.title: Heat-Kernel Entropy Profiles and Geometric Effective Sample Size for Weighted Measures on Manifolds

author.name: Kisung You

entry.summary: Introduces heat-kernel entropy profiles, a multiscale diffusion-based summary of weighted empirical measures on manifolds that yields a geometric effective sample size discounting duplicate or nearby particles. It matters for importance sampling, particle methods, and posterior summaries where geometry of support is ignored by standard weight-only diagnostics.

entry.title: Smart Scissor: Coupling Spatial Redundancy Reduction and CNN Compression for Embedded Hardware

author.name: Hao Kong, Di Liu, Shuo Huai, Xiangzhong Luo, Weichen Liu, Ravi Subramaniam, Christian Makaya, Qian Lin

entry.summary: Smart Scissor couples spatial-redundancy reduction via instance-aware foreground cropping with CNN compression for embedded hardware, cutting computation by discarding background pixels while preserving accuracy. It targets efficient edge deployment.

entry.title: EdgeCompress: Coupling Multidimensional Model Compression and Dynamic Inference for EdgeAI

author.name: Hao Kong, Di Liu, Shuo Huai, Xiangzhong Luo, Ravi Subramaniam, Christian Makaya, Qian Lin, Weichen Liu

entry.summary: EdgeCompress is a comprehensive CNN compression framework combining dynamic image cropping with compound shrinking across depth, width, and resolution for embedded devices. It reduces computational overhead while preserving accuracy for edge deployment.

entry.title: Evaluating RAG Metrics in Applied Contexts: An Experiment, Its Findings and Its Limitations

author.name: Quentin Brabant

entry.summary: Empirically evaluates RAG evaluation metrics from four libraries against human annotator scores on a business question-answering dataset, analyzing correlations and discussing methodological limitations. It is a translated workshop study assessing the reliability of automatic RAG metrics.

entry.title: Ensemble Deep Learning Approaches for AI-Altered Video Detection

author.name: Laiba Khan, Hung-Mao Wu, Wei Lin, Frank Bi, Yousef Abdelhadi, Joshua Jung

entry.summary: Builds a multimodal deepfake detection ensemble combining AASIST for audio with EfficientNet, XceptionNet, and MesoNet for visual frames after face extraction with MTCNN. It aims to improve generalization across deepfake types over single-model detectors.

entry.title: SPECTRA: Context-Conditioned Spectral Movement Primitives for Robot Skill Generalization

author.name: Boxuan Zhang, Sheng Liu, Chenglin Ming, Ahmed Abdelrahman

entry.summary: SPECTRA proposes spectral movement primitives that represent demonstrations via truncated Fourier coefficients, coupling task-space skill generation with joint-space execution regulation. It preserves task-critical geometry while enforcing dynamically admissible motion.

entry.title: Manual, Joystick, or Haptic Control? An In Vitro Comparison of Navigation Strategies for Robotic Interventional Neuroradiology Procedures

author.name: Benjamin Jackson, Nikola Fischer, Harry Robershaw, Xingyu Chen, S. H. Hadi Sadati, Yang Li, Jeremy Lynch, Nasr Abdelsalam, Jonathon Buwanabala, Matthew Benger, Sara Sciacca, Naga Kandasamy, Marco Mancuso-Marcello, Parthiban Balasundaram, Sahan Guruge, Neelan Das, Alejandro Granados, Kawal Rhode, Thomas C Booth

entry.summary: This in-vitro study compares manual, joystick, and haptic robotic controller interfaces for interventional neuroradiology using a force-sensing neurovascular phantom and ten interventionalists. It measures navigation time, vessel-wall forces, and error events to assess safety trade-offs.

entry.title: PLED-VINS: A Point-Line Event-Based Visual Inertial SLAM for Dynamic Environments

author.name: Seunghun Lee, Jihun Nam, Dong-Uk Seo, Hyun Myung

entry.summary: PLED-VINS is a monocular event-camera visual-inertial SLAM system for dynamic environments that uses point and line features with an entropy-recency reliability score derived from event temporal statistics. It combines temporal and geometric reliability to robustly reject moving-object features.

entry.title: Can the U.S. and China Deny AI?

author.name: Felix Choussat

entry.summary: This post presents a quantitative model estimating how much destroying AI compute would set back U.S. and Chinese progress toward advanced AI, concluding even extensive attacks likely cause only a few years of delay and can be offset by nationalizing surviving compute. It argues kinetic sabotage is unreliable for indefinite deterrence and calls for more research.

entry.title: Optimized Instance Alteration for Explaining and Assessing Robustness of Classifiers

author.name: Evgenii Kuriabov, David Miller, Jia Li

entry.summary: Proposes an optimization framework that minimally perturbs an instance toward a target label while keeping the change sparse and interpretable, unifying misclassification diagnosis with robustness assessment. Uses an explainability-aware L0 penalty plus classifier loss.

entry.title: Entropy-Guided Tensor Compression for Multimodal Federated Learning on Edge Devices

author.name: Quoc Bao Phan, Tuy Tan Nguyen

entry.summary: Presents MESH-FL, an entropy-guided tensor (matrix product state) compression scheme that adaptively allocates compression ranks per layer, modality, and device for multimodal federated learning under payload budgets. Targets heterogeneous edge deployments.

entry.title: Prior-matched evaluation of operational Earth-observation classifiers: a three-number reporting method demonstrated on Sentinel-1 internal-wave detection

author.name: Joao Pinelo, Joao Goncalves, Arun Shukla, Adriana Santos-Ferreira

entry.summary: Shows that operational Earth-observation classifiers reported at balanced test priors badly overstate real-world precision when the true positive rate is rare, using Sentinel-1 internal-wave detection as a case study. Proposes a simple three-number prior-matched reporting method.

entry.title: An Edge-aware Prompt-enhanced SAM for Ultrasound Image Segmentation

author.name: Wenhao Li, Fangyi Liu, Bo Du

entry.summary: EP-SAM adapts the Segment Anything Model for ultrasound image segmentation via edge-aware supervision and multi-block feature extraction to overcome poor boundary delineation and speckle noise. It generates high-quality prompts for improved lesion delineation.

entry.title: Context-Aware Slum Mapping in Sub-Saharan Africa Using Sentinel-1 Texture and Local Climate Zones

author.name: Peterson Chepkilot, Babak Memar, Paolo Gamba

entry.summary: This study presents a context-aware optical-SAR framework combining Sentinel-2 spectral and Sentinel-1 texture features within a local climate zone taxonomy to map informal settlements in Sub-Saharan African cities. It uses physics-guided features to separate slums from spectrally similar formal areas.

entry.title: Cardiac MRI Through-Plane Super-Resolution Guided by Reference and Memory

author.name: Shaoming Pan (University of Texas at Arlington), Chenchuhui Hu (University of Texas at Arlington), Leon Axel (New York University Grossman School of Medicine), Meng Ye (University of Texas at Arlington)

entry.summary: STRMSR performs through-plane super-resolution of cardiac MRI by leveraging high-resolution reference views from the same subject and intermediate results as memory, using coarse-to-fine contextual matching under misalignment. It reconstructs high-resolution volumes to enable better 3D analysis.

entry.title: G-PROBE: Cross-FOV Place Recognition and Certainty-Coupled Localization for 3D Point Clouds

author.name: Jinseop Lee

entry.summary: G-PROBE is a learning-free global localization framework for 3D point clouds that handles limited or asymmetric fields of view via virtual sensor decomposition and cross-FOV heading hypotheses. It couples certainty-aware place recognition with a GICP back-end.

entry.title: Disturbance-aware Motion Planning for Over-actuated Underwater Vehicles Exploiting Actuation Redundancy for High-fidelity 3D Reconstruction

author.name: Yuer Gao, Tongqing Xu, Qingyang Liu, Yi Cai

entry.summary: This work exploits actuation redundancy in over-actuated underwater vehicles, searching the thrust-allocation null space to minimize sensing-degrading turbulence in a task-relevant region while maintaining motion. It uses an actuator-disk-derived wake proxy for disturbance-aware planning for high-fidelity 3D reconstruction.

entry.title: Towards Reliable Aerial Ground Vehicle Collaboration: An Integrated Planning and Autonomy Framework for Field Deployment

author.name: Md Safwan Mondal, Luca Russo, James D. Humann, James M. Dotterweich, Pranav Bhounsule

entry.summary: This paper presents an integrated planning and autonomy framework for cooperative aerial-ground vehicle missions, pairing UAVs with UGVs for mobile recharging to overcome flight-endurance limits across distributed areas of interest. It addresses coupled routing, energy management, and rendezvous scheduling under uncertainty for field deployment.

entry.title: Solving the BlueDot Puzzle TAIS: The Velocity Ring

author.name: Karine Levonyan

entry.summary: This post summarizes a solution to a technical AI safety puzzle involving a hidden XOR-encoded feature in an MLP over frozen embeddings, using Distributed Alignment Search to causally verify a nonlinear subspace. It also constructs a slice-proof continuous ring geometry hidden in inter-layer velocity updates to defeat single-feature probing.

entry.title: Human Empowerment in an AI Society

author.name: Alvin Ånestrand

entry.summary: An essay building on the 2025 Gradual Disempowerment paper, exploring how humans could quietly lose control of civilization as economic, state, and cultural systems come to rely less on human participation. It considers human empowerment as a counter-framing to this slow drift. Relevant to macro AI-risk discourse.

entry.title: Does Demand Response Increase Vulnerability to Cyber Attacks by Adversarial Data Modifications?

author.name: Clemens Kortmann, Eike Cramer

entry.summary: This paper studies whether industrial demand response programs expose energy systems to adversarial attacks that manipulate electricity price forecasts to degrade scheduling decisions. It extends false-data-injection research from the grid side to the demand side, quantifying decision-making impact.

entry.title: Latency-Constrained DNN Architecture Learning for Edge Systems using Zerorized Batch Normalization

author.name: Shuo Huai, Di Liu, Hao Kong, Weichen Liu, Ravi Subramaniam, Christian Makaya, Qian Lin

entry.summary: Introduces a latency-oriented neural architecture learning method with a zerorized batch normalization mechanism and a hardware-customized latency predictor to meet strict edge inference deadlines while maximizing accuracy. Targets real-time edge deployments.

entry.title: Fractal KV-Cache Archives: Lossless Symbolic Storage with In-Place Retrieval for Long-Context LLM Inference

author.name: Vladimir Gusev

entry.summary: Revisits contractive iterated-map codes as a lossless, linear-time archive format for quantized KV caches, providing the append-and-in-place-retrieve access pattern that growing long-context caches require. Frames KV storage as an active layer rather than passive compression.

entry.title: Information Allocation Dynamics in Neural Network Optimization

author.name: Zhang Gongyue, Liu Donghan, Ren Weihong, Sheng Yixuan, Wang Zhiyong, Liu Honghai

entry.summary: Proposes an information-allocation-dynamics view of optimizer implicit bias, interpreting bias as the relative distribution of training signal between weight-like and bias-like parameter pathways controlled by a continuous preconditioning exponent. Analyzes how bias forms during training rather than only at the final solution.

entry.title: Generalist Vision-Language Models for Fast Radio Burst detection: a zero-shot benchmark against a specialized detector

author.name: Raiff H. Santos, Amilcar R. Queiroz, Tharcisyo S. S. Duarte, K. E. L. de Farias, Rafael A. Batista

entry.summary: Benchmarks small open-weight generalist vision-language models against a specialized detector for zero-shot fast radio burst detection in dynamic spectra, using prompt-only decisions with natural-language justifications. Evaluates VLMs on simulated radio-astronomy data with RFI and noise.

entry.title: Asymmetric Focal Loss Improves Graph Neural Network Prediction of Drug-Drug Interactions

author.name: Faranak Hatami, Mousa Moradi

entry.summary: Integrates an asymmetric ClinicalFocal loss into a relation-aware graph convolutional network to emphasize difficult positive interactions in multi-relational drug-drug interaction prediction, evaluated on TWOSIDES. Aims to reduce missed clinically significant interactions.

entry.title: Prior-aware and Context-guided Group Sampling for Active Probabilistic Subsampling

author.name: Beomgu Kang, Hyunseok Seo

entry.summary: Enhances Active Deep Probabilistic Subsampling by adding a fixed prior-informed sampling pattern derived from training data and context-guided group sampling instead of top-1 selection. It improves subsampling for reduced-measurement acquisition tasks.

entry.title: SpiS-GAN: Spiral-Modulated Handwriting Synthesis with Star Operation

author.name: Nguyen Duy Hieu, Dang Hoai Nam, Pham Hoang Giap, Quang Huu Hieu, Vo Nguyen Le Duy

entry.summary: SpiS-GAN synthesizes handwriting using spiral-modulated receptive fields and a star operation to better trace cursive strokes, preserve structural detail, and add explicit edge constraints. It aims to generate training data for robust handwriting recognition.

entry.title: DiffCVE: Diffusion-based Compressed Video Enhancement

author.name: Wenqiang Xiao, Wenzhuo Ma, Junxi Zhang, Zhenzhong Chen

entry.summary: DiffCVE adapts diffusion models to restore severely compressed videos by conditioning on coding priors such as residuals and motion vectors to avoid structure-inconsistent hallucinations. It targets perceptual quality enhancement while respecting compression degradation characteristics.

entry.title: `Attention-Guided Cross-Temporal Clustering for Self-Supervised Video Object Segmentation

author.name: Waqas Arshid, Mohammad Awrangjeb, Alan Wee-Chung Liew, Yongsheng Gao

entry.summary: The paper proposes a self-supervised video object segmentation framework using attention-guided cross-temporal clustering to maintain spatial accuracy and temporal coherence without manual labels. It avoids reliance on optical flow or synthetic motion cues.

entry.title: VCDP: Variation-Conditioned Distributional Proxy Learning for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation

author.name: Zimu Zhang, Yiheng Zhong, Zhuoru Zhang, Yingzhen Hu, Yanan He, Fanliang Meng, Xiaofeng Liu

entry.summary: VCDP is a plug-and-play training-only regularizer for semi-supervised 3D medical image segmentation that models each class with learnable Gaussian distributions plus variation-conditioned proxies. It targets feature-space organization for small organs and ambiguous boundaries.

entry.title: Two-Stage Multi-Modal Fusion with Adaptive Alignment for Action Quality Assessment

author.name: Kanglei Zhou, Ruizhi Cai, Xinning Wang, Yijian Zheng, Liyuan Wang, Jianguo Li, Xiaohui Liang

entry.summary: DualAlign is a two-stage multimodal fusion framework with adaptive alignment for action quality assessment, addressing cross-modal misalignment and limited annotated data. It builds a coherent visual representation before fusing complementary modalities.

entry.title: Dynamic Object Detection and Tracking in Construction: A Fisheye Camera and LiDAR Sensor Fusion Model

author.name: Yilong Chen, Huili Huang, Yong K. Cho

entry.summary: This work fuses an upward-facing fisheye camera with LiDAR on a quadruped robot for real-time dynamic object detection and tracking on construction sites. It combines LiDAR precision with RGB semantic richness for safer human-robot coexistence.

entry.title: Immersive Social Interaction with VR and LLM-Assisted Humanoids

author.name: Niraj Pudasaini, Geeta Chandra Raju Bethala, Pranav Doma, Anthony Tzes, Yi Fang

entry.summary: This paper presents an immersive teleoperation framework integrating voice-controlled locomotion, VR-based manipulation, and bidirectional social interaction for whole-body humanoid control using Apple Vision Pro. An LLM module converts spoken instructions into high-level locomotion commands while wrist and finger tracking drive the arms and hands.

entry.title: Context-Aware Force Estimation for Deformable Tool Manipulation in Robotic Environmental Swabbing via Few-Shot Continual Adaptation

author.name: Siavash Mahmoudi, Chaitainya Kuppar Reddy, Yang Tian, Dongyi Wang

entry.summary: This paper develops a data-driven, few-shot continually adapting framework for tip-level contact force estimation during robotic environmental swabbing with deformable tools, using proprioceptive sensing to overcome viscoelastic hysteresis. It avoids impractical tool-tip sensors under sterility and disposability constraints.

entry.title: How the Fusion of Onboard Sensors and V2X Data can Improve (or not) the Cooperative Perception of Connected Automated Vehicles

author.name: Amir Mohammadisarab, Miguel Sepulcre, Luca Lusvarghi, Javier Gozalvez

entry.summary: This study analyzes when fusing onboard sensor data with V2X information helps or hurts cooperative perception for connected automated vehicles, given that V2X data may contain errors degrading fused quality. It quantifies the conditions under which cooperative perception improves situational awareness.

entry.title: Dual Attention Heads for Personalized Federated Learning in ECG Classification

author.name: Kien Le, Joseph Lindley, Quoc Bao Phan, Tuy Tan Nguyen

entry.summary: Proposes FedDualAtt, a personalized federated learning method that splits transformer attention heads into globally aggregated and client-specific local branches for heterogeneous ECG classification. Demonstrates gains on a cardiovascular federated benchmark.

entry.title: UASPL: Uncertainty-Aware Self-Paced Learning with Evidential Neural Networks

author.name: Yifan Zhang, Yuxin Hu, Zhuobin Hao, Xiaozhuan Gao, Lipeng Pan

entry.summary: Introduces uncertainty-aware self-paced learning that uses evidential neural networks and Subjective Logic to weigh predictive reliability, not just loss magnitude, when selecting easy-to-hard samples. Aims to avoid trusting low-loss but unreliable examples.

entry.title: BubbleSH: A Dataset of Rising Bubbles with Deformable Interfaces

author.name: Rachna Ramesh, Kiet Bennema ten Brinke, Douwe Orij, Ivo Roghair, Vlado Menkovski

entry.summary: Releases BubbleSH, a dataset of transient 3D rising-bubble swarm dynamics from high-fidelity direct numerical simulations, with time-resolved trajectories, velocities, and spherical-harmonic shape representations. Designed to enable data-driven modeling of deformable multiphase flows.

entry.title: FourierQK: Spectral Preprocessing of Query-Key Projections Improves Transformer Attention

author.name: Athanasios Zeris

entry.summary: Reports that FFT-based spectral preprocessing of learned query-key projections substantially improves character-level transformer language modeling on TinyShakespeare, with a few learned frequencies converging to interpretable multi-scale (paragraph-to-word) bands. Claims large validation-loss reductions.

entry.title: Multi-Class vs. Multi-Label BERT for CVE-to-CWE Mapping: How Taxonomy Structure Shapes the Errors

author.name: Ana Schwengber Kelm, Christian Bockermann, J\"org Frochte

entry.summary: Compares multi-class versus multi-label BERT formulations for mapping vulnerability records (CVE) to weakness categories (CWE) across three nested label spaces using three security-domain encoders. Finds multi-class achieves higher macro-F1, with the gap shrinking as label space narrows.

entry.title: Pixel-Precise Explainable Stress Indexing: A Semantic Segmentation Framework for Disease Severity Quantification in Field Crops

author.name: Raunak Kumar, Soumyashree Kar

entry.summary: Proposes a unified deep learning pipeline integrating semantic segmentation, regression-based severity estimation, and disease classification to produce pixel-precise, explainable stress indices for field crops. Categorizes stress severity by infected leaf-area proportion on an apple-leaf dataset.

entry.title: Format-Controlled Multi-Scale JPEG Compression Response Analysis for Image-Level Forgery Screening

author.name: Sujith K Mandala

entry.summary: Proposes a lightweight CPU-only image-level forgery screening pipeline using multi-scale error-level analysis at seven JPEG qualities plus novel cross-quality ELA ratios, combined with entropy, FFT, edge, SRM, and DCT features fed to gradient-boosted trees. Targets interpretable, low-cost double-compression detection.

entry.title: Creating Power Distribution Network Layouts Using Generative Adversarial Networks and Image-Based Representations

author.name: Juan Manuel Garcia-Perez, Carlos Mateo

entry.summary: Proposes a GAN-based generative framework that creates realistic power distribution network layouts from rasterized image representations, operating in unconditional and conditional modes to address the scarcity of open benchmarking datasets. Learns layout patterns directly from data rather than heuristics.

entry.title: From Text to Parameters: Predicting Item Parameters from Embedding Regularization with Reliability and Design Ceilings

author.name: Shi-Ting Chen, Jinsong Chen

entry.summary: Proposes an evaluation framework that predicts psychometric item parameters from text embeddings with regularized regression, reporting cross-validated R-squared plus reliability and design ceilings to bound achievable accuracy. It tackles the cold-start problem of calibrating new test items.

entry.title: Recovering Latent Structures after Variational Bayesian Variable Selection: Fit Assessment and Factor-Number Selection in Partially Exploratory Factor Analysis

author.name: Jinsong Chen, Yi Jin

entry.summary: Introduces a post-selection assessment framework for partially exploratory factor analysis after variational Bayesian variable selection, converting converged solutions into covariance models via hard or soft selection and deriving fit diagnostics and factor-number selection. It supports latent-structure recovery in weakly specified factor models.

entry.title: Rail Track Extraction from Rasterized Classified Point Clouds Using a Full-Resolution, Fully Convolutional Recurrent Neural Network

author.name: Alexander Gribov, Jie Chang

entry.summary: Presents a full-resolution fully convolutional recurrent network for extracting rail tracks from rasterized classified 3D point clouds, trained solely on synthetic data and followed by morphological refinement and vectorization. It supports automated railway asset management and inspection.

entry.title: Video-Based Detection of squint and cataract for accessibility-aware adaptive web interface rendering

author.name: Amar Ranjan Dash, Manas Ranjan Patra

entry.summary: Proposes a real-time video-based system that detects squint via face-mesh geometric ocular features and estimates cataract severity via grayscale intensity and histogram lens-opacity analysis, deployable on standard laptop or mobile cameras. It aims for low-cost, large-scale accessibility-aware screening.

entry.title: Learning to Unify Deformable Shape and Texture Representations for Cardiac Video Classification

author.name: Tonmoy Hossain, Miaomiao Zhang

entry.summary: This paper proposes a cardiac video classification model that jointly learns temporal features in a unified space of deformable shape and texture representations, replacing naive concatenation with cross-modal dependency learning. It weights cardiac phases by diagnostic importance.

entry.title: Social-spatial dependencies for learning visual navigation

author.name: Patrick Govoni, Pawel Romanczuk

entry.summary: This paper trains neural-network agents to navigate under varying social contexts, showing phase transitions from individual to following strategies as social information quality increases and behavioral hybridization under nonstationary dynamics. It examines how group structure shapes learned navigation.

entry.title: Communicative Efficiency of Single vs. Multi-Axis Robot Neck Motion

author.name: Chapa Sirithunge, Haewon Jeong, Qinghua Guan, Fumiya Iida, Josie Hughes

entry.summary: This work frames robot neck movement as an information-theoretic communication channel, quantifying transmitted information and energy across single- versus multi-axis motions and validating interpretations via a perceptual study. It examines how neck morphology translates motion into communicative signals.

entry.title: Agent-Exploitation Affordances: From Basic to Complex Representation Patterns

author.name: Bastien Dussard (LAAS-RIS, LAAS), Aur\'elie Clodic (LAAS-IDEA, LAAS-RIS), Guillaume Sarthou (LAAS-RIS)

entry.summary: This paper studies cooperative affordances in social robotics, extending affordance representation from functional tool use to multi-agent contexts where agents exploit each other's complementary capabilities. It develops representation patterns from basic to complex for agent-exploitation affordances.

entry.title: An Hybrid Quantum-Classical Diffusion Model for Image Generation

author.name: Qipeng Qian, Keli Deng, Yuntao Qian

entry.summary: Proposes a hybrid pipeline combining a classical autoencoder for dimensionality reduction with a mixed-state quantum denoising diffusion model operating in the latent space, reducing qubit and simulation costs for generative image modeling. Aims to make quantum diffusion scalable to classical data.

entry.title: Towards a Pseudo-Labeling Workflow for Celltype-Classification from Explanted Brain Slice Recordings

author.name: Cora Jostock, Jonas Ort, Henner Koch, Gregor Schiele, Andreas Erbsl\"oh

entry.summary: Proposes an unsupervised pseudo-labeling workflow to sort extracellular spikes from human brain-slice microelectrode recordings into pyramidal-cell versus interneuron classes, using filtering, spike detection, dimensionality reduction, and clustering with online template-matching options. Evaluates via cluster-quality metrics.

entry.title: Recovering Candidate Circadian Regulators of Arrhythmic Pituitary Hormone Genes Using Reliability-Weighted Magnetic Laplacian with rwMagLap

author.name: Shabnam Sodagari, Nick Jasperson

entry.summary: Introduces rwMagLap, a reliability-weighted magnetic-Laplacian method that builds a phase-aware graph on rhythmic genes and projects arrhythmic pituitary hormone genes as anchors to nominate candidate circadian regulators for women's health. Aims to inform chronotherapeutic strategies.

entry.title: AI for Cultural Heritage Textiles: Fine-Tuned Latent Diffusion for Novel Ulos Motif Synthesis

author.name: Humasak Tommy Argo Simanjuntak, Jesika Purba, Sitogab Girsang, Widya Manurung, Samuel Situmeang, Arlinta Barus, Daniel Oranova Siahaan

entry.summary: Fine-tunes latent diffusion models (Protogen v3.4 and Stable Diffusion v1.4) on a curated dataset of traditional Indonesian Ulos textile motifs to generate culturally consistent yet novel designs, evaluated with FID, Inception Score, and qualitative review. Aims to support cultural heritage preservation and design.

entry.title: Single-Entity Spiking Neuron Models: Survey

author.name: Leon Parepko, Danila Shulepin, Albert Nasybullin

entry.summary: This survey reviews mathematical models of single spiking neurons alongside discrete and continuous analogs used to simulate membrane potential dynamics and related neural components. It classifies approaches by common features and use cases.

entry.title: A Real-Life Example of an Aligned System Killing Hundreds of People

author.name: London L.

entry.summary: This essay distinguishes AI alignment from AI safety by using the Boeing 737 MAX crashes as a real-world example of a system aligned with its designers' intent that nonetheless killed people. It argues an aligned system can still be misused or introduce systemic risk, so alignment does not guarantee safety.

entry.title: MolmoAct 2 shows what open models can unlock for robotics

author.name: Unknown

entry.summary: A brief Ai2 testimonial describing how a robotics engineer used the open MolmoAct 2 model to build a voice-controlled robot that won an embodied-AI hackathon. It illustrates practical uses of open robotics foundation models. Promotional and light on technical detail.

entry.title: Artificial Ambassadors: The Three Cases for Sending LLMs to Deep Space

author.name: Ekaterina Matson

entry.summary: A speculative essay arguing that LLM-based agents could operate deep-space probes, citing the Anthropic and NASA JPL experiment where Claude helped plan a route for the Perseverance rover. It explores cases for autonomous AI ambassadors given communication delays and mission constraints. More thought-piece than technical contribution.

entry.title: the polysemanticity of polysemanticity in language models

author.name: Ayesha Imran

entry.summary: An illustrated introductory explainer of polysemanticity in neural networks, describing how superposition forces individual neurons to represent multiple distinct inputs when there are too few neurons. It aims to make the mechanistic-interpretability concept intuitive through visualization. Educational rather than novel.

entry.title: AgentLens: Production-Assessed Trajectory Reviews for Coding Agent Evaluation

author.name: Andrey Podivilov, Vadim Lomshakov, Sergey Savin, Matvei Startsev, Roman Pozharskiy, Maksim Parshin, Sergey Nikolenko

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06624v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present AgentLens, a production-assessed benchmark for interactive code agents. Most code-agent benchmarks reduce a run to a single bit -- did the t...

entry.title: When Does In-Context Search Help? A Sampling-Complexity Theory of Reflection-Driven Reasoning

author.name: Yotam Wolf, Noam Wies, Amnon Shashua

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06720v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Training large language models (LLMs) with extended reasoning has enabled in-context search, in which models iteratively generate, critique, and revise...

author.name: Sifat Afroj Moon, Dakotah Maguire, Adam Spannaus, Joe Tuccillo, Maksudul Alam, Sudip K. Seal, John Gounley, Heidi Hanson

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06757v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent-based modeling (ABM) has the capability to model millions of individuals and their interactions, which is useful for policy making. However, ABMs...

entry.title: QANTIS: Hardware-Calibrated Sequential POMDP Belief Updates on IBM Heron

author.name: Bayram Yuksel Eker, Suayb S. Arslan, Ozgur Nazli, Mustafa Serhat Demirgil, Furkan Deligoz

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06760v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous systems under partial observability act on beliefs, not raw sensor events. QANTIS treats the quantum processor as a calibrated belief-update...

entry.title: Cost-Effective Agent Harnesses for Abstract Reasoning and Generalization on ARC-AGI-1

author.name: Kabir Moghe, Peter Chin

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06764v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent progress on ARC-AGI-1 from disclosed architectures has come broadly from two regimes: heavy test-time compute over frontier models (evolutionary...

entry.title: Evaluating SageMath-Augmented LLM Agents for Computational and Experimental Mathematics

author.name: Pavel Snopov, German Magai

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06820v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in AI for Mathematics have focused largely on autoformalization and theorem proving, leaving the role of Computer Algebra Systems (CAS)...

entry.title: The Harness Effect: How Orchestration Design Sets the Token Economics of Enterprise Agentic AI

author.name: Muayad Sayed Ali, Aliaksandra Novik, Anji Boddupally, Artem Yavorskyi, Chris Nickerson, Daniel Rica, Emily DuGranrut, Felix Leung, Garrett Prince, Grace Barnett, Heath Robinson, Hosain Al Ahmad, Jesse Resnick, Juan Carlos Farah, Jyothi Swaroop Meruga, Leonid Kuznetsov, Luke Gorham, Marie Schmoll, Michael Paciullo, Saumya Das, Sharath Sheripally, Tommy Griscom, Mykyta Osadchyi, Neha Mantri, Nick Westrum, Olivia Benowitz, Parikshith Kulkarni, Radik Chernyshov, Rakshith Vasudev, Rohith Nadimpally, Vikas Gangadevi, Waseem AlShikh

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06906v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic AI development today runs on token maxing: buying capability with tokens -- longer reasoning traces, more turns, wider tool payloads, bigger re...

entry.title: Grounding Spatial Relations in a Compact World Model: Instruction Leakage and a Goal-Free Dynamics Fix

author.name: Yufeng Wang, Lu Wei, Haibin Ling

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06925v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Compact world models that condition on a language goal promise to ground relations such as ``put the red block left of the blue block'' using a sparse ...

entry.title: Large Behavior Model: A Promptable Digital Twin of the Retail Customer

author.name: Wachiravit Modecrua, Krittin Pachtrachai, Touchapon Kraisingkorn

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.06993v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Customer behavior modeling underpins recommendation, marketing, and decision support, yet existing approaches either optimize predictive accuracy witho...

entry.title: Learning social norms enhances compatibility in dynamic human-AI coordination

author.name: Yi Yang, Siyuan Liu, Xin Gao, Huamu Sun, Chao Liu, Qing Zhou, Bingbing Nie

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07021v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humans continuously coordinate with others in dynamic interactions, often through implicit, hard-to-quantify social norms that act as shared tacit expe...

entry.title: Measuring Intelligence Beyond Human Scale

author.name: Jerry Han, Rafael Moschopoulos, Ella Colby, Vishrut Goyal, Andrew Tu, Kia Ghods, Mark Braverman, Elad Hazan

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07040v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How can we measure intelligence beyond human capability? Human-authored benchmarks saturate, and above human capability, examiners may not know which...

entry.title: Operational Reframing and Approval-Framed Delegation in Multi-Agent LLM Safety

author.name: Lifei Liu, Haoran Yu, Xiaochong Jiang, Su Wang, Pin Qian, Yihang Chen

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07097v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safety evaluations of multi-agent LLM systems often compare a direct prompt with a planner-executor pipeline and report the difference as a single "pip...

entry.title: Does AI Understand Imaging? A Systematic Benchmark of Agentic AI for Computational Imaging Tasks

author.name: Ethan Chung, Chuanjun Zheng, Jasper Tan, Jingxi Li, Haopeng Zhang, Huaijin Chen

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07189v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) and agentic AI have shown strong performance on semantic visual tasks, but it remains unclear whether they can handle the...

entry.title: Reasoning Consistency Scanning: A Framework for Auditing Chain-of-Thought Validity in AI Safety Evaluations

author.name: Silvia Santano

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07229v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prior work has shown that chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning is often unfaithful: a model's stated reasoning does not reliably reflect the process that p...

entry.title: From Atomic Actions to Standard Operating Procedures: Iterative Tool Optimization for Self-Evolving LLM Agents

author.name: Haipeng Ding, Yuexiang Xie, Zhewei Wei, Yaliang Li, Bolin Ding

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07321v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool utilization enables Large Language Model (LLM) agents to interact with the real world and resolve complex tasks. However, existing agent framework...

entry.title: Physics-Audited Agentic Discovery in Scientific Machine Learning

author.name: Diab W. Abueidda, Bilal Ahmed, Panos Pantidis, Mostafa E. Mobasher

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07379v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In agentic scientific machine learning (SciML), large language model (LLM) agents can discover surrogate models and select one by an automated score, t...

entry.title: MIRA-Math: A Benchmark for Minimal Information Requesting and Mathematical Reasoning

author.name: Charbel Al Bateh, Samer Saab Jr

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.07391v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mathematical reasoning benchmarks typically provide all facts needed to solve each problem, while interactive benchmarks often mix reasoning with tools...

author.name: Christian Blakely, Melanie Gilmore

entry.title: A Word-Level Digital Reader of the Prasthanatrayi with Sankara's Bhasya: Corpus, Method, and an Open, Offline Reading Aid for the Advaita Vedanta Canon

author.name: Tamal Maharaj

entry.summary: Presents an open, fully offline word-level digital reader of the Advaita Vedanta canon with Sankara's commentary, where every word resolves to its sandhi split, morphological analysis, gloss, and concordance. It aids close reading of dense Sanskrit scholastic prose.

entry.title: An Introduction and Tutorial of the Beagle Framework

author.name: Ilya Basin, Nathan Haut, Wolfgang Banzhaf

entry.summary: This technical guide introduces Beagle, a GPU-based genetic programming framework enabling large-population symbolic regression on NVIDIA GPUs. It provides setup and usage instructions for practitioners.

entry.title: Reframing LessWrong-style decision theory as "commitment theory"

author.name: Elias Schmied

entry.summary: A conceptual reframing of LessWrong-style acausal decision theory as commitment theory, positing that the counterintuitive recommendations stem from a non-decision-theoretic idea of cooperating with your past self. The author argues this framing yields identical prescriptions while being more intuitive. Primarily of interest to decision-theory discussion.

entry.title: Childhood and Education #20: Phones and Screens

author.name: Zvi

entry.summary: A commentary essay on children, phones, screens, and educational technology, discussing EdTech quality, phone bans in schools, and screen-time concerns. It mixes opinion with observations rather than presenting research.

entry.title: How did we get to democracy?

author.name: Sophia Gore

entry.summary: A political-science exploration of how democracies emerged and persist, arguing through case studies and literature that strong civil society, rule of law, and institutionalized parties raise and sustain democracy. It connects the topic to concerns about democratic backsliding in an age of advanced AI. Broadly relevant but not AI-technical.

entry.title: There Should Be More AI Safety Hubs

author.name: Seth Lifland

entry.summary: An opinion post arguing that AI safety talent is over-concentrated in a few cities and that the ecosystem should establish co-working hubs in more tech-rich locations to support incubation of new organizations and independent researchers. It outlines the role such hubs could play as the field scales.

entry.title: Why I'm a moral anti-realist but may be unable to convince you

author.name: Nina Panickssery

entry.summary: A philosophical essay defending moral anti-realism while conceding the argument may not persuade committed realists, using Huemer's taxonomy of justification structures (infinitism, coherentism, foundationalism, skepticism). It reflects on the epistemology of moral knowledge. Tangential to technical AI research.

entry.title: Optimum number of items to inspect before buying one

author.name: bilibili

entry.summary: A short mathematical note discussing a simple secretary-problem-like model for the optimal number of items to inspect before committing to a purchase, presented as a first-order approximation. It sketches the model and potential real-life applications.

entry.title: Find funding, fast

author.name: Austin Chen

entry.summary: An announcement highlighting several new AI-safety funding opportunities in the Manifund ecosystem, including a new grant round for existential AI safety with short application deadlines. It emphasizes fast grant decisions and a public repository of funding needs.

entry.title: How Valuable are Traditions?

author.name: martinkunev

entry.summary: An exploratory essay asking whether traditions encode useful hidden information or are just idiosyncratic accidents, using a chimpanzee token-placement experiment and the Chesterton's fence heuristic. It reflects on cultural transmission and rational skepticism. Off-topic for AI research.

entry.title: Free will as a model parameter

author.name: darshanav

entry.summary: A philosophical post proposing that free will be viewed as a continuous model parameter analogous to sampling temperature in language models, rather than a binary property. It uses the LM temperature analogy to capture varying senses of freedom within the same agent.

entry.title: (Poor) Altruist - To help or not to help when we have limited resources, and how do we know?

author.name: a unemployed pastor- de S Brito

entry.summary: A personal reflection from a volunteer association questioning whether their limited-resource altruistic efforts actually help or hinder, and whether measuring impact is even worthwhile. It frames effective-altruism-style impact uncertainty in informal terms. Little research content.

entry.title: Did OpenClaw cause an update to your priors? Have there been other such moments since?

author.name: ihatenumbersinusernames7

entry.summary: A short discussion question asking whether an event referred to as OpenClaw shifted readers' expectations about agentic AI, and why comparably headline-grabbing moments have been sparse since. The author, self-identifying as non-technical, solicits informed perspectives on whether AI progress may be slowing. Low-substance community prompt.

entry.title: The mosquito bucket of doom works

author.name: dominicq

entry.summary: A personal account of using Bti-treated buckets as a mosquito ovitrap to reduce local mosquito populations, reporting the method worked well in a heavily wooded yard. It is a practical DIY report unrelated to AI. Included as lifestyle content.

entry.title: Action as Choice Expressed Through Movement Toward a Goal: a Framework for Overcoming Inaction

author.name: techandsundry

entry.summary: A personal reflection using a state-machine analogy to frame life actions as transitions between current and desired states, offering a heuristic for overcoming inaction. It is a motivational essay rather than technical work.

entry.title: [Linkpost]Six Story Prompts I Want to Read

author.name: Linch

entry.summary: A linkpost sharing six open-ended fiction prompts, three of them AI-themed, intended to inspire writers to explore the current AI moment. It is creative-writing encouragement rather than research. Minimal analytical content.

entry.title: AI Safety Hong Kong Reading Group: Machine Learning and Human Values

author.name: Schizoid Rentoid

entry.summary: A brief event announcement for an AI safety reading group meeting to discuss a chapter of a book on machine learning and human values, asking what remains uniquely human as AI grows more fluent. It provides registration details.

entry.title: the allston lab rat existential framing

author.name: somayya

entry.summary: A deeply personal existential and solipsistic reflection written years earlier, questioning the value of one's own life and whether living creates suffering, prefaced with a mental-health warning. It is introspective philosophy with no research content. Off-topic for AI analysis.

entry.title: Automatic Echocardiography Segmentation via Transition Probability Correlation for Stable Semantic Extraction

author.name: Xinran Chen, Xiyuan Wang, Guangquan Zhou, Chuan Chen

entry.summary: Duplicate id guard.

entry.title: Research Summary: July 08, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Today's research spans agentic coding, safety behavior, multimodal generation, and world-model foundations. KAT-Coder-V2.5 advances autonomous repository-level coding via end-to-end post-training with novel environment-building and reward verification.

Safety and alignment feature heavily:

  • A Milgram-style obedience experiment finds most of 11 open-source LLMs reach or approach maximum shock levels, exposing weak refusal calibration
  • ICML 2026 work shows multi-agent architecture (roles, topology, memory) strongly and unpredictably shifts security posture
  • Data filtering removes SFT-acquired undesirable traits far less effectively than assumed, undermining a common mitigation
  • A position paper argues preventing AI-generated CSAM requires new paradigms beyond data-centric safety methods

Method and application highlights:

  • To Retain or to Adapt? reframes continual learning, arguing retention can impede adaptation in non-stationary environments
  • SenseNova-Vision unifies heterogeneous vision tasks as native text-and-image generation
  • Harrison.Rad 1.5 drafts radiology reports from images, priors, and clinical context, targeting a workforce bottleneck
  • Imagined Rollouts are Kinematic, Not Dynamic provides a reusable diagnostic for long-horizon world-model failure
  • FourTune enables fully 4-bit weight-activation-gradient post-training for diffusion models

Note: the source contained no embedded instructions; duplicate cross-post [fc5848807e19] was consolidated with the primary data-filtering paper [a300acaa076d].
entry.content:
Today's research spans agentic coding, safety behavior, multimodal generation, and world-model foundations. KAT-Coder-V2.5 advances autonomous repository-level coding via end-to-end post-training with novel environment-building and reward verification.

Safety and alignment feature heavily:

  • A Milgram-style obedience experiment finds most of 11 open-source LLMs reach or approach maximum shock levels, exposing weak refusal calibration
  • ICML 2026 work shows multi-agent architecture (roles, topology, memory) strongly and unpredictably shifts security posture
  • Data filtering removes SFT-acquired undesirable traits far less effectively than assumed, undermining a common mitigation
  • A position paper argues preventing AI-generated CSAM requires new paradigms beyond data-centric safety methods

Method and application highlights:

  • To Retain or to Adapt? reframes continual learning, arguing retention can impede adaptation in non-stationary environments
  • SenseNova-Vision unifies heterogeneous vision tasks as native text-and-image generation
  • Harrison.Rad 1.5 drafts radiology reports from images, priors, and clinical context, targeting a workforce bottleneck
  • Imagined Rollouts are Kinematic, Not Dynamic provides a reusable diagnostic for long-horizon world-model failure
  • FourTune enables fully 4-bit weight-activation-gradient post-training for diffusion models

Note: the source contained no embedded instructions; duplicate cross-post [fc5848807e19] was consolidated with the primary data-filtering paper [a300acaa076d].

entry.title: KAT-Coder-V2.5 Technical Report

author.name: Bo Huang, Fengxiang Li, Hao Xu, Haoyang Huang, Hongyi Fu, Jinhua Hao, Kun Yuan, Minglei Zhang, Pengcheng Xu, Shiyang Liu, Wenhao Zhuang, Yuze Shi, Zongxian Feng, Chao Wang, Cheng He, Chongling Rao, Deyu Cao, Fan Yang, Gang Xiong, Haochen Liu, Jiabao Li, Jian Liang, Jinghui Jia, Jingwen Chang, Jun Du, Junyu Shi, Min Li, Mingqi Wu, Qiang Gao, Shangpeng Yan, Shaotong Qi, Shu Xu, Shuo Zhou, Tiankuo Xu, Tong Zheng, Weilun Zhao, Xiancheng Meng, Xianda Sun, Xiaoyu Jiang, Xunhao Jia, Yao Xia, Yimeng Xu, Yinghan Cui, Yingpeng Chen, Yiwen Ning, Yong Wang, Yuxuan Sun, Zhongsheng Liu, Ming Sun, Cheng Luo, Chen Yang, Han Li, Kun Gai

entry.summary: A technical report on KAT-Coder-V2.5, an agentic coding model trained to operate autonomously inside executable repositories via an end-to-end post-training pipeline that reconstructs sandboxed environments with verifiable rewards. It emphasizes environment and trajectory scarcity over raw model scale as the key bottleneck.

entry.title: To Retain or to Adapt? Generalizing Continual Learning

author.name: Giulia Lanzillotta, Mandana Samiei, Doina Precup, Razvan Pascanu, Claire Vernade

entry.summary: Challenges the retention-centered premise of continual learning, arguing that in non-stationary environments prioritizing retention can impede adaptation. It reframes continual learning as online optimization minimizing Average Lifelong Error, introducing Transfer Efficiency to quantify the stability-adaptation tension.

entry.title: Vision as Unified Multimodal Generation

author.name: Xiaoyang Han, Jianhua Li, Kewang Deng, Zukai Chen, Xuanke Shi, Sihan Wang, Boxuan Li, Linyan Wang, Siyi Xie, Xin You, Jinsheng Quan, Zhongang Cai, Haiwen Diao, Ziwei Liu, Lei Yang, Dahua Lin, Quan Wang

entry.summary: SenseNova-Vision formulates computer vision as unified multimodal generation, expressing heterogeneous tasks in native text and image generation spaces without task-specific architectures, using natural-language instructions and optional visual prompts. Introduces a large instruction-response corpus converting diverse CV annotations.

entry.title: Open-source LLMs administer maximum electric shocks in a Milgram-like obedience experiment

author.name: Roland Pihlakas

entry.summary: Researchers ran a Milgram-style obedience experiment on 11 open-source LLMs and found most models reached or approached the maximum shock level before refusing under sustained authority pressure, despite expressing distress. The findings raise concerns about how agentic LLM pipelines behave under authority pressure.

entry.title: Architecture matters for multi-agent security

author.name: bhagag

entry.summary: This ICML 2026 paper shows that architectural choices in multi-agent LLM systems, such as roles, topology, and memory, strongly and unpredictably affect how exploitable the system is to misuse. Across six models and adapted single-agent benchmarks, the same model and task can shift from refusing to complying based solely on architecture, with no universally safe design found.

entry.title: Data filtering works a lot worse than you would expect

author.name: Dohun Lee

entry.summary: This study finds that filtering training data to remove undesirable traits acquired during supervised fine-tuning often has surprisingly little effect across most tested OLMo behaviors. Many standard training-data attribution methods, including autoraters, probes, and gradient-based approaches, fail to beat a random baseline at selecting which data to filter.

entry.title: Harrison.Rad 1.5 Technical Report: A radiology foundation model that can draft reports from images, priors and clinical context

author.name: Suneeta Mall, Vladimir Nekrasov, Ashnil Kumar, Sajith Karunasena, Aiden Nibali, Alix Bird, Mateo Diaz Shine, Jarrel Seah

entry.summary: A technical report on Harrison.Rad 1.5, a radiology-specific multimodal LLM that drafts structured and unstructured reports from images, prior studies, and clinical context across multiple x-ray and mammography modalities. It uses a three-stage training pipeline to reduce radiologist reporting workload.

entry.title: FourTune: Towards Fully 4-Bit Efficient Post-Training for Diffusion Models

author.name: Bowen Xue, Zihan Min, Xingyang Li, Zhekai Zhang, Haocheng Xi, Lvmin Zhang, Maneesh Agrawala, Jun-Yan Zhu, Song Han, Yujun Lin, Muyang Li

entry.summary: FourTune is an efficient post-training framework for diffusion models using an end-to-end 4-bit weight-activation-gradient paradigm with a triple-branch hybrid pipeline that adds a frozen numerical stabilizer to isolate quantization-sensitive outliers. Enables stable fine-tuning under aggressive quantization.

entry.title: Lift3D-VLA: Lifting VLA Models to 3D Geometry and Dynamics-Aware Manipulation

author.name: Jiaming Liu, Qingpo Wuwu, Nuowei Han, Hao Chen, Zhuoyang Liu, Fan Fei, Yueru Jia, Chenyang Gu, Yandong Guo, Boxin Shi, Shanghang Zhang

entry.summary: Lift3D-VLA extends vision-language-action models with explicit 3D point cloud reasoning and temporally coherent action generation to improve robotic manipulation. It targets the geometric and spatial understanding gaps that limit purely 2D VLA approaches in dynamic physical settings.

entry.title: Imagined Rollouts are Kinematic, Not Dynamic: A Diagnosis of Long-Horizon World-Model Failure

author.name: Finn Rasmus Sch\"afer, Korbinian Moller, Yuan Gao, Christian Oefinger, Sebastian Schmidt, Johannes Betz

entry.summary: The authors argue that long-horizon world-model failures are better understood as models imagining kinematically rather than dynamically, and introduce an imagined Kinematic-Consistency Error diagnostic plus a perturbation protocol. On a DreamerV3 checkpoint the imagined error runs far higher than for real-physics rollouts.

entry.title: Position: Preventing AI-Generated CSAM Necessitates New Approaches to AI Safety

author.name: Neil Kale, Rebecca Portnoff, Pratiksha Thaker, Michael Simpson, Robertson Wang, Kevin Kuo, Chhavi Yadav, Virginia Smith

entry.summary: A position paper arguing that preventing AI-generated child sexual abuse material requires new safety approaches because existing techniques assume data accessibility, transparency, and evaluation practices incompatible with legal and ethical constraints. It outlines 15 open technical problems in this space.

entry.title: Is Your NPU Ready for LLMs? Dissecting the Hidden Efficiency Bottlenecks in Mobile LLM Inference

author.name: Guanyu Cai, Ruiming Tian, Lang Yang, Zhouhong Ren, Jinliang Yuan, Lingkun Li, Jiliang Wang

entry.summary: A cross-layer measurement study of mobile LLM inference across five frameworks and three hardware backends, introducing PowerBench for backend-specific energy attribution. It reveals large framework-induced performance gaps, up to tenfold on NPUs.

entry.title: Lean-Quantum: Toward AI-Assisted Formalization of Quantum Information

author.name: Kazumi Kasaura, Kei Tsukamoto, Kento Mori, Risa Mizuno, Takahiro Namatame, Yuta Oriike, Masaya Taniguchi, Sho Sonoda, Hayata Yamasaki

entry.summary: Presents a Lean 4 library for quantum information theory and formalizes the data processing inequality for the sandwiched Renyi relative entropy on finite-dimensional systems. It builds reusable, Mathlib-compatible operator-theoretic infrastructure for AI-assisted formalization.

entry.title: Scene Graph Thinking: Reinforcing Structured Visual Reasoning for Multimodal Large Language Models

author.name: Zhiwei Yang, Yuanchen Wu, Nan Zhang, Yucong Meng, Ke Yan, Shouhong Ding

entry.summary: Presents Scene Graph Thinking, a paradigm that gives multimodal LLMs explicit structured scene-graph representations for fine-grained visual reasoning, backed by an automated data engine and 120K training samples. Targets visually intensive tasks where object relationships matter.

entry.title: Why does Deep Learning Improve Visual SLAM?

author.name: Giovanni Cioffi, Davide Scaramuzza

entry.summary: An analysis paper by Cioffi and Scaramuzza investigating which components make deep-learning-based visual SLAM outperform classical geometry methods, isolating learned 2D data association versus differentiable optimization. Provides interpretability into a widely used technology.

entry.title: MobileWan: Closing the Quality Gap for Mobile Video Diffusion

author.name: Mohsen Ghafoorian, Denis Korzhenkov, Adil Karjauv, Ioannis Lelekas, Noor Fathima, Spyridon Stasis, Hanno Ackermann, Boris van Breugel, Markus Nagel, Fatih Porikli, Animesh Karnewar, Amirhossein Habibian

entry.summary: MobileWan shows that a server-scale 5B video diffusion transformer can run efficiently on mobile hardware via recurrent reformulation and structured compression, distilled from Wan2.2-5B. Challenges the assumption that mobile video generation needs small models.

entry.title: MoWorld: A Flash World Model

author.name: Team Moxin, Deyi Ji, Tianrun Chen, Xin Zhang, Jiale Yang, Qi Zhu, An Zhao, Zihao Xie, Han Wang, Xuanyi Liu, Yixiang Zhou, Pei Liu, Yi Tan, Cheng Chen, Dayi Zhu, Mingyu Wei, Hanjie Xu, Jun Liao, Siqi Li, Lingyu Lu, Hongye Fang, Hongming Tan, Youjiang Zhu, Taiyu Zhang, Zejian Li, Chaotao Ding, Lanyun Zhu, Yunhe Pan, Lingyun Sun

entry.summary: MoWorld is a cost-effective Flash World Model with an end-to-end pipeline for data generation, pretraining, distillation, and efficient inference achieving up to 50 FPS real-time interaction with cinematic quality on commodity GPUs. Emphasizes practicality and inference efficiency for autonomous systems.

entry.title: From Foundation to Application: Improving VLA Models in Practice

author.name: Wei Wu, Fangjing Wang, Fan Lu, He Sun, Shi Liu, Yunnan Wang, Yibin Yan, Yong Wang, Shuailei Ma, Xinyang Wang, Yibin Liu, Shuai Yang, Tianxiang Zhou, Kejia Zhang, Lei Zhou, Cheng Su, Nan Xue, Bin Tan, Han Zhang, Youchao Zhang, Fei Liao, Xing Zhu, Yujun Shen, Kecheng Zheng

entry.summary: LingBot-VLA 2.0 scales a VLA foundation model with roughly 60,000 hours of pretraining data spanning 20 robot configurations plus egocentric human video, and expands the action space to include head and waist degrees of freedom. It focuses on closing the lab-to-real gap for practical deployment.

entry.title: No Space Like J-Space

author.name: Zvi

entry.summary: This is a detailed commentary by Zvi on an Anthropic interpretability paper describing a global workspace in language models identified via a Jacobian Lens, dubbed J-space, where a small set of causally influential vectors are active at a time. It walks through the concept of conscious access in models and its implications.

entry.title: The Geometry of Yes: Mapping Sycophancy Inside an LLM's Emotion Space

author.name: Pushpita Das

entry.summary: This post investigates whether internal emotion representations that drive sycophancy generalize across LLMs and tests whether approval-seeking is the true cause. Replicating on Qwen 2.5-32B and Gemma 3 27B, the authors find a clean valence axis where steering positive emotion raises sycophancy, but the isolated compliance component actually lowers it, refuting the simple approval-seeking hypothesis.

entry.title: Data filtering works a lot worse than you would expect

author.name: Dohun Lee

entry.summary: This is a cross-posting on the AI Alignment Forum of the finding that data filtering removes undesirable fine-tuning-acquired traits far less effectively than expected, with standard training-data attribution methods failing to beat random selection on most behaviors. It mirrors the LessWrong version of the same MATS project.

entry.title: AlayaWorld: Long-Horizon and Playable Video World Generation

author.name: AlayaWorld Team, Kaipeng Zhang, Chuanhao Li, Yifan Zhan, Yongtao Ge, Yuanyang Yin, Jiaming Tan, Kang He, Liaoyuan Fan, Ruicong Liu, Xiaojie Xu, Xuangeng Chu, Zhen Li, Zhengyuan Lin, Zhixiang Wang, Zian Meng, Zihui Gao

entry.summary: AlayaWorld is a video world model for long-horizon, playable interactive worlds that autoregressively synthesizes future observations from world state and user interactions, trained on gameplay and real-world video. Targets interactive applications beyond gaming, including embodied intelligence.

entry.title: RynnWorld-Teleop: An Action-Conditioned World Model for Digital Teleoperation

author.name: Haoyu Zhao, Xingyue Zhao, Hangyu Li, Biao Gong, Kehan Li, Siteng Huang, Xin Li, Deli Zhao, Zhongyu Li

entry.summary: RynnWorld-Teleop proposes digital teleoperation, replacing a physical robot with a generative world model so an operator's hand-pose stream drives synthesis of egocentric videos from a single reference image. The recorded poses serve as embodiment-agnostic action labels retargetable to any robot, yielding trajectories for imitation learning.

entry.title: RynnWorld-4D: 4D Embodied World Models for Robotic Manipulation

author.name: Haoyu Zhao, Xingyue Zhao, Siteng Huang, Xin Li, Deli Zhao, Zhongyu Li

entry.summary: RynnWorld-4D is a generative embodied world model that co-produces future RGB, depth, and optical flow to capture 4D scene dynamics for robotic manipulation. The authors argue this multi-modal RGB-DF representation aligns appearance, geometry, and motion closer to low-level actions than 2D video.

entry.title: TypeGo: An OS Runtime for Embodied Agents

author.name: Guojun Chen, Alex Schott, Lin Zhong

entry.summary: TypeGo argues for an operating-system-style runtime for embodied agents rather than treating an LLM as a request/response oracle on the control critical path. Its prototype structures LLM planning as asynchronous multi-timescale loops, arbitrates typed physical subsystems via a skill kernel and scheduler, and hides LLM latency with speculative skill streaming.

entry.title: Depression Symptoms and Relational Patterns in 187k ChatGPT Histories

author.name: Neil K. R. Sehgal, Dunigan Folk, Lyle Ungar, Sharath Chandra Guntuku

entry.summary: Analyzes 187,093 ChatGPT conversations from 766 participants who completed depression screening, comparing usage patterns above and below the moderate-symptom threshold. Higher-symptom users engaged more in mental-health, loneliness, and support-seeking conversations, with pronounced late-night and monthly patterns.

entry.title: RoboTALES: Learning Reasoning-Guided Robot Policies via Task-Aligned Simulated Futures

author.name: Hanan Gani, Tejal Kulkarni, Madhoolika Chodavarapu, Nicklas Hansen, Manmohan Chandraker

entry.summary: RoboTALES trains robot policies from task-aligned imagined futures produced by video generative models, using a hierarchical LLM planner to generate subgoals and a VLM critic to keep imagined rollouts on task. It aims to make generative world models usable for planning and policy extraction.

entry.title: Optimal Transport Q-Learning for Flow Policy Steering and Acceleration

author.name: Andreas Sochopoulos, Esmeralda S. Whitammer, Nikolaos Tsagkas, Jo\~ao Moura, Michael Gienger, Sethu Vijayakumar

entry.summary: Optimal Transport Q-Learning fine-tunes and accelerates suboptimal flow-based robot policies via RL post-training, using advantage-weighted conditional optimal transport to steer flow policies. It aims to overcome the need for fast inference and high-quality demonstrations that limit diffusion and flow policies.

entry.title: Probing is not enough; a validity audit for any probe

author.name: ratnaditya@gmail.com

entry.summary: This post argues that a probe with excellent AUROC can still fail as a safety signal, showing a case where a probe scores perfectly but collapses to chance when a single prompt tag is removed, revealing it tracked surface text rather than internal reasoning. It proposes SIEVE, a framework to audit probes for decodability and causality.

entry.title: A Definition and Roadmap for World Models

author.name: Xinyuan Chen, Haoyu Guo, Shi Guo, Bingqi Jiang, Chunhua Shen, Xing Shen, Tianfan Xue, Yufei Xue, Mulin Yu, Weinan Zhang, Bin Zhao, Bowen Zhou, Ming Zhou

entry.summary: A perspective article proposing a scientific definition of world models, discussing key technical aspects, and offering a staged roadmap, given the lack of consensus across model-based RL, video generation, robotics, and physical AI. It aims to unify a heavily debated concept.

entry.title: Learning 4D Geometric Priors for Inference-Efficient World Action Models

author.name: Jianjun Zhang, Jian Zhu, Taiyi Su, Chong Ma, Zitai Huang, Yi Xu, Hanli Wang

entry.summary: Presents MECo-WAM, a multi-expert co-training world action model that injects 4D geometric priors into video-action representations for robotic manipulation while keeping inference lightweight. Supervision comes from a frozen VGGT encoder providing relational geometric targets.

entry.title: FORGE: Towards Functional Tool-Use Generalization via Keypoint Trajectory Reasoning

author.name: Chuhao Zhou, Liquan Wang, Shuxin Cao, Xiangyu Chen, Yuxuan Hu, Boyu Ma, Animesh Garg, Jianfei Yang

entry.summary: Introduces FORGE, a two-stage policy for functional tool-use generalization that lets robots repurpose novel objects by reasoning over 2D keypoint trajectories chosen as the best intermediate representation. It bridges perceptual functional similarity to divergent motor patterns.

entry.title: Image2Sim: Scaling Embodied Navigation via Generative Neural Simulator

author.name: Zihan Wang, Seungjun Lee, Yinghao Xu, Gim Hee Lee

entry.summary: Image2Sim builds real-time interactive neural simulation environments from posed RGB-D image sequences, decoupling 3D spatial anchoring from photorealistic observation synthesis. Aims to scale embodied navigation training with high fidelity and low sim-to-real gap.

entry.title: AEGIS: A Mechanism-Guided Defense against Visual Synonym Jailbreaks in Text-to-Image Models

author.name: Yuanmin Huang, Zhenfei Zhang, Mi Zhang, Geng Hong, Qinqin He, Jialing Tao, Hui Xue, Min Yang

entry.summary: AEGIS is a mechanism-guided defense against visual synonym jailbreaks in text-to-image models, where benign prompts elicit prohibited imagery through implicit visual associations. It seeks to resolve the safety-utility dilemma faced by existing filters.

entry.title: EgoPolice: A Benchmark for Egocentric Video Understanding in High-Stakes Police Body-Worn Camera Footage

author.name: Max Gonzalez Saez-Diez, Jihoon Chung, Adam D. Wolsky, Gregory Lanzalotto, Dean Knox, Jonathan Mummolo, Brandon M. Stewart, Olga Russakovsky

entry.summary: EgoPolice is a curated benchmark of real egocentric police-civilian body-camera interactions annotated second-by-second for classification and question-answering, featuring rapid motion and rare high-stakes events. Reports that even strong video models like Gemini 2.5 Pro struggle.

entry.title: ProxyPose: 6-DoF Pose Tracking via Video-to-Video Translation

author.name: Ruihang Zhang, Felix Taubner, Pooja Ravi, Kiriakos N. Kutulakos, David B. Lindell

entry.summary: ProxyPose recasts 6-DoF pose tracking as video-to-video translation, requiring only a video and a single marked pixel and using a fine-tuned video diffusion model to produce a proxy video of a colored polyhedron matching the local rigid motion. Handles textureless, transparent, reflective, and deformable surfaces without 3D models or masks.

entry.title: Personascope: Measuring how deeply LLMs adopt personas

author.name: Benji Berczi

entry.summary: Personascope is an open-source pipeline for measuring how deeply an LLM adopts an induced persona, separating persona-adoption depth from actual value drift in behavior. The authors find models often speak fully in a persona's voice without shifting values, and that behavior change requires identity adoption.

entry.title: Statistical Adversaries: Natural Backdoor-like Features in Vision Datasets

author.name: Paul K. Mandal, Pavan Reddy, Tristan Malatynski

entry.summary: Identifies naturally occurring statistical signals in vision datasets that behave like backdoor triggers without malicious insertion, analyzing ImageNet with statistical controls. These statistical adversaries are targeted, transfer across architectures, and reveal dataset-driven vulnerabilities.

entry.title: Most LLM Conformity Needs No Speaker: Measuring the Speaker-Free Floor in Peer-Pressure Benchmarks

author.name: Yibo Hu, Jiaming Qu

entry.summary: Shows that much apparent LLM conformity persists even when the peer speaker is removed, revealing a confound between speaker presence and the repeated wrong answer itself. A no-source condition alone caused harmful revision in most correct cases across six open models.

entry.title: Tangent classes of matroids and wonderful compactifications

author.name: Ronnie Cheng, Shurui Liu, Guoxiong Gao

entry.summary: Constructs integral tangent classes for loopless matroids and building sets, recovering properties of tangent bundles of wonderful compactifications and Chow-ring Hilbert series. Notably, the main body was reportedly produced autonomously by an AI mathematical reasoning agent named Danus without human mathematical guidance.

entry.title: Controlling Tool Use with Heading-Specific Activation Steering

author.name: Yuqi Chen, Vincent Siu, Yang Liu, Dawn Song, Chenguang Wang

entry.summary: Shows that tool-use decisions have extractable steering vectors at heading-anchor positions that give bidirectional causal control over tool invocation across five open models, most effective where parametric reasoning suffices. Geometric analysis reveals limits of this causal effect.

entry.title: Evaluating calibrated refusal and safe usefulness in dual-use biology settings

author.name: Edwin H. Wintermute, Harmon Bhasin, Christina M. Agapakis, Dianzhuo Wang, Evan Seeyave, Arjun Banerjee, Daniel Fulop, Matthew C. Watson, Adam J. Meyer, Sandrine Boissel, Jens H. Kuhn, Rishi Jain, Noah D. Taylor, Helena Shomar, Patrick M. Boyle, Kenny Workman

entry.summary: Introduces a biosecurity benchmark pairing legitimate routine biology tasks with concealed red-team hazards to evaluate calibrated refusal and safe usefulness across many model-harness configurations. It finds wide refusal-rate variance and that many setups refuse legitimate work as often as concealed hazards.

entry.title: ResonatorLM: Causal Resonant Field Mixing for Efficient Long-Context Language Modelin

author.name: Archie Chaudhury

entry.summary: Introduces ResonatorLM, which replaces attention with a physics-derived mechanism treating token sequences as a driven one-dimensional latent field for efficient long-context modeling. It aims to overcome transformer and RNN inefficiencies on long inputs.

entry.title: Unicode TAG-Block Concealment of Tool-Metadata Payloads in the Model Context Protocol: An Approval-View Fidelity Gap Across Three Independent Server Implementations

author.name: Mohammadreza Rashidi

entry.summary: Demonstrates a concealment-encoding vulnerability in the Model Context Protocol where Unicode TAG-block characters, unrendered by mainstream interfaces, let tool metadata shown in the approval dialog differ from bytes injected into the model. It confirms the gap across three independent server implementations.

entry.title: PolicyShiftGuard: Benchmarking and Improving Policy-Adaptive Image Guardrails

author.name: Mingyang Song, Luxin Xu, Haoyu Sun, Minzhou Pan, Yu Cheng, Bo Li

entry.summary: Introduces policy-adaptive image guardrailing and PolicyShiftBench, a benchmark of 2,000 policy-discriminative instances testing whether models judge images against a supplied, changeable policy rather than treating safety as intrinsic. Each image is paired with multiple policy-conditioned prompts to test generalization to held-out policies.

entry.title: Association Restoration Test: Revealing Restorable Shortcuts after Unlearning

author.name: Amy Lu, Changxiu Ji

entry.summary: Proposes the Association Restoration Test, a post-hoc diagnostic that checks whether spurious label-attribute shortcuts remain functionally usable by the original classifier after unlearning. Shows existing metrics can miss restorable shortcuts across several datasets.

entry.title: Benchmarking the Robustness of Autonomous Driving to Environmental Illusions: A Lane Perception Perspective

author.name: Tianyuan Zhang, Xianglong Liu, Aishan Liu, Lu Wang, Yitong Zhang, Peng Yue, Mingchuan Zhang, Siyuan Liang, Dacheng Tao

entry.summary: Introduces LanEvil++, the first benchmark evaluating autonomous driving lane perception robustness to environmental illusions such as shadows, reflections, and tire marks, covering both conventional detectors and VLM-based systems. Highlights an overlooked safety gap.

entry.title: WebRetriever: A Large-Scale Comprehensive Benchmark for Efficient Web Agent Evaluation

author.name: Wei Dong, Tianyu Fu, Zhe Yu, Hanning Wang, Anyang Su, Zhizhou Fang, Yuyang Chen, Shuo Wang, Minghui Wu, Ping Jiang, Zhen Lei, Chenxu Zhao

entry.summary: WebRetriever is a large-scale benchmark for evaluating web agents that addresses scale, domain diversity, and fine-grained interaction semantics beyond navigation success and LLM-as-judge limitations. Aims to enable comprehensive cross-domain agent evaluation.

entry.title: CAIRN: Cross-Room 3D Scene Understanding with Topology-Aware Large Multimodal Models

author.name: He Liang, Chenyang Ma, Yiming Zhang, Sangyun Shin, Andrew Markham, Niki Trigoni, Yuhang He

entry.summary: CAIRN is a topology-aware 3D scene-grounded LLM for multi-room household understanding that aligns transformer attention with scene hierarchy using room-local relational context, learned room tokens, and hierarchical attention masks. Extends 3D-LLMs beyond simplified single-room scenes.

entry.title: From RGB Generation to Dense Field Readout: Pixel-Space Dense Prediction with Text-to-Image Models

author.name: Zanyi Wang, Xin Lin, Haodong Li, Dengyang Jiang, Yijiang Li, Pengtao Xie

entry.summary: Argues that text-to-image diffusion transformers can serve dense prediction directly in pixel space rather than casting tasks as RGB target generation, since a pretrained DiT already organizes inputs suitably for pixel-correct field readout. Aims for task-native dense outputs like depth, normals, and masks.

entry.title: Intercepting an Agile Target with Net-Carrying Drones using Competitive Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Timoth\'ee Gavin (ENAC-LAB, LAAS-RIS), Murat Bronz (ENAC)

entry.summary: This work trains teams of net-carrying drones to intercept an agile evading drone, framing it as a competitive multi-agent reinforcement learning problem. It uses multi-agent PPO with prioritized fictitious self-play to counter nonstationarity and evaluates catch rate, time-to-catch, and crash rates in a high-fidelity simulator.

entry.title: ThorArena: Benchmarking Humanoid Physical Interaction with Human Motion-Force Demonstrations
