author.name: Chenhao Yu, Hongwu Wang, Weitao Zhang, Youhao Hu, Jiachen Zhang, Gangyang Li, Alois Knoll, Shaqi Luo

entry.summary: ThorArena is a benchmark for evaluating force-aware humanoid physical interaction using human demonstrations with synchronized motion and interaction forces. It fills a gap where existing datasets focus on kinematics and ignore how external forces affect tracking and stability.

entry.title: Hilti-Trimble-Oxford Dataset: 360 Visual-Inertial Benchmark with Floor Plan Priors for SLAM and Localization

author.name: Samuele Centanni, Yuhao Zhang, Yifu Tao, Julien Kindle, Frank Neuhaus, Tilman Ko{\ss}, Aryaman Patel, Michael Helmberger, Emilia Szyma\'nska, Torben Gr\"aber, Maurice Fallon

entry.summary: This paper releases a high-quality 360-degree visual-inertial dataset with floor plan priors collected at an active construction site, aimed at SLAM and floor-plan-referenced localization for progress monitoring. It positions consumer 360 cameras with embedded IMUs as a cost-effective alternative to LiDAR.

entry.title: Hypothesis-driven Model Expansion under Uncertainty for Open-World Robot Planning

author.name: Anxing Xiao, Hanbo Zhang, Tianrun Hu, David Hsu

entry.summary: This work proposes an open-world planning framework where service robots automatically generate, verify, and update hypotheses about their abstract world models under uncertainty, integrating hypothesis verification into goal-reaching planning. It leverages foundation models to handle unexpected objects and tasks.

entry.title: UniLM-Nav: A Unified Framework for Zero-Shot Last-Mile Navigation

author.name: Zhuofan Zhang, Tianxu Wang, Guoxi Zhang, Yixiong Lin, Xilin Wang, Hongming Xu, Qing Li, Song-Chun Zhu, Lifeng Fan

entry.summary: UniLM-Nav is a zero-shot, open-vocabulary framework for last-mile navigation, decomposing the problem into view selection, task-conditioned affordance grounding, and geometry-aware base-pose reasoning, all resolved with a shared multimodal LLM. It targets reaching a manipulation-ready base pose without pose annotation or task-specific training.

entry.title: Calibrating alignment evals

author.name: darshanav

entry.summary: This post examines why current alignment evaluations may be miscalibrated, focusing on failure modes like evaluation awareness where models detect that they are being tested and behave differently. It argues that high pass rates do not tell us how robust or sensitive the benchmarks really are.

entry.title: CanvasAgent: Enabling Complex Image Creation and Editing via Visual Tool Orchestration

author.name: Hairui Zhu, Yiying Yang, Tengjin Weng, Ziyu Lu, Xiao Yao, Xiaoyang Ye, Lin Ma, Wenhao Jiang

entry.summary: Introduces CanvasAgent and a large-scale dataset for orchestrating multiple visual tools (generation, segmentation, editing, compositing) to handle complex image creation tasks. It shifts multimodal agents from perception to actively manipulating visual state.

entry.title: aiAuthZ: Off-Host, Identity-Bound Authorization for AI Agents

author.name: Sai Varun Kodathala

entry.summary: Presents aiAuthZ, an off-host authorization gateway that binds tool-call decisions to verified caller identity via per-message signed nonces, evaluated against eight attack scenarios on fifteen models. It finds refusal rates vary widely and uncorrelated with model price.

entry.title: Diagnosing Semantic Handoff Failures in Agent-Orchestrated Vision-Language-Action Skill Composition

author.name: Ke Rui, Yushen Zuo, Jiawei Wang, Haoran Jia, Jinming Ma, Weitao Zhou, Minglei Li

entry.summary: This paper studies semantic handoff failures in long-horizon robot tasks, where one skill satisfies its own postcondition but leaves the system in a state the next skill cannot start from. Using an agent-orchestrated harness with pi-0.5 skill checkpoints and multi-view VLM verification, it isolates handoff problems from individual skill competence in BEHAVIOR-1K.

entry.title: Neural-ESO: A Dual-Pathway Architecture for Provably Robust Learning-Based Control

author.name: Fan Zhang, Richie Suganda, Jinfeng Chen, Wenhua Liu, Hantao Fu, Bin Hu, Qin Lin

entry.summary: Neural-ESO is a dual-pathway disturbance-rejection controller pairing a neural feedforward disturbance estimate with a conventional extended state observer that corrects prediction errors, avoiding over-reliance on the learned model. Lyapunov and small-gain analysis with a Lipschitz bound provide stability guarantees.

entry.title: Information Limits and Attractor Dynamics in Economies of Frontier LLM Agents: A Pre-Registered Test

author.name: Cheng Qian

entry.summary: Runs a pre-registered two-part experiment on small economies of frontier LLM agents, testing an information-theoretic capacity region for wealth growth and a mean-field scaling law for population misalignment, with all predictions frozen in a public git chain. Results are re-derivable from cached outputs at near-zero cost.

entry.title: KVpop -- Key-Value Cache Compression with Predictive Online Pruning

author.name: Lukas Hauzenberger, Niklas Schmidinger, Anamaria-Roberta Hartl, David Stap, Thomas Schmied, Sebastian B\"ock, G\"unter Klambauer, Sepp Hochreiter

entry.summary: Introduces a learned fixed-budget KV eviction policy that directly supervises keep-or-drop decisions against a future-attention target computed without dense attention maps, plus a delayed scorer that defers decisions to exploit near-future context. It targets brittle heuristic-based eviction.

entry.title: The Jagged Global Economy: Frontier AI Unevenly Exposes National Economies

author.name: Arul Murugan, Tom\'as Aguirre, Abhishek Nagaraj, Rishi Bommasani

entry.summary: Introduces a national AI-exposure metric combining occupation-level exposure scores with international employment data for 141 countries, finding high-income nations far more exposed and a gender gap where women are more exposed in most countries. It broadens labor-market evidence beyond a few high-income economies.

entry.title: What Do AI Agents Actually Change? An Empirical Taxonomy of Mutation Patterns in Performance-Improving Pull Requests

author.name: Illia Dovhoshliubnyi, Nima Soroush, Ashkan Sami, Alexander Brownlee

entry.summary: Empirically classifies what AI coding agents actually change by analyzing 1,254 performance-relevant diff hunks from performance-improving pull requests across five agent systems using a syntactic mutation taxonomy. It treats agents as black boxes inspectable through their code changes for search-based software engineering.

entry.title: LEGATO 2: Toward Multimodal Sheet Music Recognition and Understanding

author.name: Guang Yang, Brian Siyuan Zheng, Victoria Ebert, Noah A. Smith

entry.summary: Presents Legato 2, an optical music recognition pipeline that processes sheet music system-by-system with an autoregressive vision-language model, scaling to long inputs and embedding textual content like titles. It combines system-level segmentation with score-structure modeling.

entry.title: Segmentation before Answering: Pixel Grounding for MLLM Visual Reasoning

author.name: Yake Wei, Yuan Wang, Fengyun Rao, Jing Lyu, Di Hu

entry.summary: Proposes SegAnswer, which shifts the zoom-in unit in multimodal reasoning from bounding boxes to pixel-level segmentation masks to isolate targets from clutter before answering. This yields more precise regions of interest and filters interfering background.

entry.title: Beyond Refusal: A Same-Lineage Study of Aligned and Abliterated LLMs for Vulnerability Analysis

author.name: Mingchen Li, Meikang Qiu, Zifan Peng, Heng Fan, Song Fu, Junhua Ding, Yunhe Feng

entry.summary: Studies safety state in same-lineage LLMs, comparing aligned versus refusal-ablated (abliterated) variants for defensive vulnerability-analysis tasks. By isolating safety state from architecture and scale confounds, it examines how refusal ablation affects defensive utility in software-security workflows.

entry.title: Differentially Private Natural Gradient Descent

author.name: Pan Li, Kai Chen, Shuai Chang, Shengzhi Zhang, Peizhuo Lv, Jinwen He

entry.summary: Proposes differentially private natural gradient descent, preconditioning gradients with curvature to overcome the zigzagging inefficiency of DP-SGD in ill-conditioned landscapes under fixed privacy budgets. It aims to extract more efficient signal per privacy expenditure.

entry.title: Propose and Attend: Training-free MLLM Grounding Confidence via Multi-Token Localized Attention

author.name: Daniel Shalam, Emanuel Ben Baruch, Avi Ben Cohen, Tal Remez

entry.summary: Proposes Multi-Token Localized Attention, a training-free post-hoc score measuring how strongly a prediction's tokens attend to the region they claim, to detect hallucinated bounding boxes and temporal windows in multimodal LLMs. It generalizes prior attention-based detectors as special cases.

entry.title: PluraMath: Extending Mathematical Reasoning Evaluation Beyond High-Resource Languages

author.name: Daryna Dementieva, Nikolay Babakov, Kathy H\"ammerl, Ilseyar Alimova, Jind\v{r}ich Libovick\'y, Shu Okabe, Miras Baisbay, Lukas Edman, Abrorkhon Inomkhujaev, Antonia Karamolegkou, Mateusz Lango, Volkan \"Ozer, Nikola Selic, Subhankar Swain, Tsedeniya Kinfe Temesgen, Galit Bary Weisberg, Alexander Fraser

entry.summary: Introduces PluraMath, extending the PolyMath benchmark with 18 additional underrepresented languages across six families via a human-curated, native-speaker pipeline. It addresses the heavy English and Chinese bias in mathematical-reasoning evaluation.

entry.title: RoME: Robust Mixture of Low-Rank Experts against Multiple Adversarial Perturbations

author.name: Woo Jae Kim, Kyle Min, Suhyeon Ha, Joonsung Jeon, Sung-eui Yoon

entry.summary: Proposes RoME, a robust mixture of low-rank experts that routes different adversarial perturbation types through distinct model pathways to overcome robustness trade-offs in multi-perturbation adversarial training. It addresses expert redundancy and threat-agnostic routing that plague naive mixture-of-experts.

entry.title: ARMS: Anchor-Relational Motion Streaming for Seamless Solo-Social Motion Transitions

author.name: Huakun Liu, Qing Yu, Kent Fujiwara, Hideaki Uchiyama, Kiyoshi Kiyokawa

entry.summary: ARMS is a streaming human motion generation framework that unifies solo and human-human interaction in a single causal process, using an anchor-relational representation to handle transitions into and out of interactions. Targets long-horizon, socially coherent motion synthesis from text.

entry.title: GaussFusion: Towards Multimodal 3D Gaussian Pretraining

author.name: Zhixuan You, Jihua Zhu, Yiding Sun, Zihao Guo, Haozhe Cheng, Dongxu Zhang, Lin Chen, Hainan Luo

entry.summary: GaussFusion is a multimodal pretraining framework for 3D Gaussian representations that injects image and text supervision into masked Gaussian modeling via cross-modal semantic alignment. Aims to add semantic richness missing from geometry-only pretraining.

entry.title: Unlearnable Faces: Privacy Protection Surviving Extraction Pipeline

author.name: Byunghoon Oh, Sunghwan Park, Jaewoo Lee

entry.summary: LPID crafts unlearnable perturbations for shared face photos that survive the crop-and-resize extraction pipeline attackers use before training, by confining energy to preserved frequency bands. Addresses a failure mode where prior protections collapse under realistic extraction.

entry.title: Tuning-Free Latent Diffusion Models for Ultrahigh-Resolution Image Editing

author.name: Wanglong Lu, Lingming Su, Kaijie Shi, Minglun Gong, Xiaogang Jin, Hanli Zhao, Xianta Jiang

entry.summary: UltraDiffEdit is a tuning-free framework that extends off-the-shelf latent diffusion models to ultrahigh-resolution image editing up to 8K without requiring high-resolution training data. Addresses the gap between sub-1K model capabilities and real device resolutions.

entry.title: HoloCount: A Holistic Visual Counting Benchmark for MLLMs

author.name: Jinhong Deng, Limeng Qiao, Guanglu Wan

entry.summary: HoloCount is a holistic visual counting benchmark for multimodal LLMs organized around a three-level hierarchical taxonomy that probes counting under logical constraints and adversarial conditions. Targets persistent numerical hallucinations missed by simpler benchmarks.

entry.title: Training-Free Acceleration for Vision-Language-Action Models with Action Caching and Refinement

author.name: Ryuji Oi, Hikari Otsuka, Kosuke Matsushima, Yuki Ichikawa, Masato Motomura, Tatsuya Kaneko, Daichi Fujiki

entry.summary: ActionCache is a training-free, plug-and-play external cache that reuses past intermediate actions to warm-start flow-matching-based Vision-Language-Action model generation, cutting the iterative denoising latency that bottlenecks real-time robot deployment. Refines the reused actions for accuracy.

entry.title: Co-STAR: Cognitive Stimulation Therapy by an Autonomous Robot for Dementia -- A One-Week In-Home Study

author.name: Emmanuel Akinrintoyo, Nicole Salomons

entry.summary: Co-STAR deploys a social robot that autonomously delivers cognitive stimulation therapy to people with dementia in their homes over a one-week study. Adherence exceeded typical caregiver-led sessions, and the study highlights the important supporting role of family members.

entry.title: EAGOR: Embodied Reasoning in Omni-direction

author.name: Shriram Damodaran, Soumyaratna Debnath, Yan Wu, Wei-Yun Yau, Addison Lin Wang

entry.summary: EAGOR addresses directional reasoning for embodied agents using 360-degree cameras, arguing that standard VLMs mishandle the spherical geometry when using equirectangular projections. It targets consistent direction estimation for map-free navigation under agent motion.

entry.title: LAMP: Latent Motion Prior-Guided Real-World Learning for Dexterous Hand Manipulation

author.name: Xinye Yang, Zhiyuan Ma, Hongze Yu, Yuanpei Chen, Yaodong Yang, Xiaojie Chai, Xinlei Chen, Chao Yu

entry.summary: LAMP introduces a latent motion prior that maps recent hand-action histories into a compact latent space to make real-world dexterous hand learning more stable, reducing risky exploration in high-dimensional action spaces. It sits within a three-stage framework combining imitation and online RL.

entry.title: SIEVE: Structure-Aware Data Selection for Imitation Learning with VLA Models

author.name: Changti Wu, Bin Yu, Zhaolong Shen, Shijie Lian, Xiaopeng Lin, Cong Huang, Zhirui Zhang, Lei Zhang, Kai Chen

entry.summary: SIEVE is a structure-aware data selection method for VLA imitation learning that treats demonstrations as compositions of reusable visuo-motor primitives and allocates selection budgets to maximize reuse across composition patterns. It addresses the observation that more data does not always yield better policies.

entry.title: Prior-First, Condition-Second: Scalable and Controllable Hand Motion Completion

author.name: Mingyi Shi, Xuelin Chen, Taku Komura

entry.summary: This paper presents a prior-first, condition-second framework for body-conditioned hand motion completion that first learns a generic body-hand kinematic prior from large unlabeled motion data, then adds semantic control via lightweight adaptation on the frozen prior. It centers on a streaming autoregressive body-hand prior.

entry.title: Beyond the Leaderboard: A Synthesis of Tool-Use, Planning, and Reasoning Failures in Large Language Model Agents

author.name: Wael Albayaydh, Rui Zhao, Ivan Flechais

entry.summary: Synthesizes 27 benchmark, taxonomy, and audit papers into a unified taxonomy of LLM-agent failure modes spanning tool use, planning, long-horizon reasoning, multi-agent coordination, safety, and measurement validity. It identifies six recurring failure clusters obscured by leaderboard gains.

entry.title: When Should LLMs Search? Counterfactual Supervision for Search Routing

author.name: Minho Kim

entry.summary: Formulates instance-level search routing for LLMs, deciding when external search actually improves task success by comparing no-search and forced-search outcomes to build a NO-SEARCH/SEARCH/UNSOLVED oracle. This counterfactual supervision trains routers and serves as an evaluation criterion.

entry.title: WristMimic: Full-Body Humanoid Control with Wrist-Guided Manipulation

author.name: Wongyun Yu, Youngwoon Kim, Minsu Cho

entry.summary: WristMimic is a wrist-guided whole-body humanoid control framework that separates contact-free body motion from contact-rich hand manipulation, guiding body and wrist with kinematic targets while fingers learn grasping from object tracking rather than direct hand-pose supervision. Reproduces object motion and contacts for successful manipulation retargeting.

entry.title: Memory in the Loop: In-Process Retrieval as ExtendedWorking Memory for Language Agents

author.name: Yusuf Khan, Carlo Lipizzi

entry.summary: Argues that agent memory should be read and written every reasoning step by moving from networked stores to in-process retrieval, cutting latency by roughly three orders of magnitude. It reframes in-loop retrieval latency as a property of store location rather than the access pattern.

entry.title: SearchEyes: Towards Frontier Multimodal Deep Search Intelligence via Search World Simulation

author.name: Zhengbo Jiao, Yiming Cheng, Yilei Jiang, Kaituo Feng, Rui Huang, Tianyi Jiang, Juanxi Tian, Jiapeng li, Qunzhong Wang, Tailai Chen, Qianshan Wei, Chuan Xiao, Shanyu Rong, Yangfu Li, Yanhan Zhou, Yunpu Ma, Yifan Zhang, Xiangyu Yue

entry.summary: Builds a simulated multimodal search world backed by a typed knowledge graph to unify training data, environment, and reward for multi-hop search agents, sampling constrained visual-knowledge paths over a Wikidata-derived corpus. It targets the disconnect among independently constructed pipeline components.

entry.title: Information Gain-based Rollout Policy Optimization: An Adaptive Tree-Structured Rollout Approach for Multi-Turn LLM Agents

author.name: Yijun Zhang, Fan Xu, Jiaxin Ding, Yule Xie, Shiqing Gao, Xin Ding, Haoxiang Zhang, Luoyi Fu, Xinbing Wang

entry.summary: Proposes a policy-optimization framework for multi-turn search agents that allocates rollout budget by intermediate-state informativeness using an adaptive tree-structured rollout rather than uniform sampling. It targets wasted compute on low-value branches.

entry.title: Danus: Orchestrating Mathematical Reasoning Agents with Fact-Graph Memory

author.name: Jihao Liu, Guoxiong Gao, Zeming Sun, Bin Wu, Shurui Liu, Jiedong Jiang, Haocheng Ju, Leheng Chen, Ronnie Cheng, Xiping Zhang, Bin Dong

entry.summary: Introduces an orchestration system for research-level mathematical reasoning centered on a shared fact graph as global memory, with a planning main agent, parallel proof-search workers, and a stateless verifier that checks claims before acceptance. It targets coordinating parallel proof search reliably.

entry.title: Doomed from the Start: Early Abort of LLM Agent Episodes via a Recall-Controlled Probe Cascade

author.name: Kai Ruan, Zihe Huang, Ziqi Zhou, Qianshan Wei, Xuan Wang, Hao Sun

entry.summary: Shows that lightweight per-round probes on agent hidden activations can predict eventual episode failure as early as the first round, far better than behavior-only scorers, and turns this into a calibrated abort cascade with per-round recall budgets. It saves inference compute on doomed trajectories.

entry.title: Bridging Physical Reasoning and Task Generalization via Visual Action Outcome Reasoning Alignment

author.name: Han-Jun Ko, Jr-Jen Chen, Haobo Yuan, Hsin-Ying Lee, Tiancheng Shen, Ming-Hsuan Yang, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang

entry.summary: Proposes a reward design for VLMs in interactive physical reasoning with a visual alignment reward that anchors reasoning to visual context and a visual-action alignment reward that grounds reasoning in the visual outcome of actions. It targets hallucinated chain-of-thought and reasoning-action misalignment.

entry.title: CCBENCH: Assessing LLM Cultural Competence via Implicitly Signaled Norms using Health Queries

author.name: Vasudha Varadarajan, Akhila Yerukola, Mona T. Diab, Maarten Sap

entry.summary: Introduces a framework and health-focused benchmark for evaluating LLM cultural competence, treating culture as a continuum of norm-adherence states rather than fixed demographics, with theoretically grounded personas across cultures answering real healthcare questions. It measures adaptation to implicitly signaled cultural values.

entry.title: Rendering-Aware Bayesian 3D Gaussian Splatting with Native Uncertainty and Adaptive Complexity Control

author.name: Gaoxiang Jia, Vikram Appia, Junzhou Huang, Xinlei Wang

entry.summary: Introduces a rendering-aware Bayesian 3D Gaussian Splatting framework with a Normal-Inverse-Wishart posterior over Gaussian geometry, giving native uncertainty and principled complexity control. An optional Dirichlet-process extension provides component-usage signals, useful under sparse views.

entry.title: The yes-no bias of large language models reflects answer order and wording, not shifts in moral judgment

author.name: Haonan Huang

entry.summary: Argues that the reported yes-no bias of LLMs on moral dilemmas reflects answer order and wording rather than genuine shifts in moral judgment. A psychometric battery separating logical verdict, lexical token, and option position recovers a nearly format-invariant internal moral scale in frontier models.

entry.title: The Balkanization of Execution-Security Research for AI Coding Agents: Isolation, Access Control, and Time-of-Check-to-Time-of-Use Vulnerabilities

author.name: Mohammadreza Rashidi

entry.summary: A systematization of 39 papers from 2023-2026 on execution security for AI coding agents, organizing scattered work on sandbox isolation, access control, TOCTOU races, and MCP threats into 17 verified categories. It highlights the fragmentation and non-cross-citation across this literature.

entry.title: AbICL: In-Context Learning for Antigen-Specific Antibody Affinity Ranking

author.name: Zhiyuan Chen, Jing Hu, Junzhe Wang, Yueyang Huang, Xinyi Yang, Zhaoyang Wang, Feng Zhu

entry.summary: Proposes AbICL, casting antigen-specific antibody affinity ranking as in-context learning that exploits a few labeled affinity comparisons to infer antigen-specific ranking patterns. It moves beyond treating affinity comparisons independently.

entry.title: CMDR: Contextual Multimodal Document Retrieval

author.name: Ryota Tanaka, Taku Hasegawa, Kyosuke Nishida

entry.summary: Introduces CMDR and its benchmark for contextual multimodal document retrieval, plus CMDR-Embed, which jointly encodes multiple pages to capture cross-page document context. It targets queries that aggregate information across pages, which page-independent encoders miss.

entry.title: TRIG: Trajectory-Rig Decoupled Metric Geometry Learning

author.name: Lizhou Liao, Wentao Xu, Handong Wang, Lirong Yang, Shuai Yang, Weiwei Liu, Chang Huang

entry.summary: TRIG factorizes multi-camera vehicle poses into ego-trajectory and static camera-rig components to better exploit rigid geometric priors for autonomous driving. Improves metric geometry and ego-motion estimation from synchronized cameras.

entry.title: SparseCtrl-HOI: Sparse Temporal Control for Human-Object Interaction Video Generation

author.name: Shenbo Xie, Mingrui Cai, Xu Yang, Yifei Liu, Changxing Ding

entry.summary: SparseCtrl-HOI generates human-object interaction videos using sparse temporal control instead of dense frame-wise pose guidance, reducing annotation cost while preserving motion diversity. Targets e-commerce live-streaming applications.

entry.title: PhyMRI-SR: Toward Physics-Aware MRI Image Super-Resolution

author.name: Lihua Wei, Huatong Gao, Jia Gong, Zhiyu Tan, Hao Li, Jun Liu, Zhihua Ren

entry.summary: PhyMRI-SR reframes MRI super-resolution as a physics-aware reconstruction problem, exploiting the coupling between spatial resolution and signal-to-noise ratio to identify an optimal resolution-SNR configuration before super-resolving. Treats resolution as dynamic rather than fixed.

entry.title: Point as Skeleton: Accumulated Point Cloud Enhanced Autoregressive Generation for Closed-Loop Autonomous Driving Simulation

author.name: Songbur Wong, Xiaosong Jia, Junqi You, Bo Zhang, Pei Xu, Renqiu Xia, Yuping Qiu, Shaofeng Zhang, Zelin Zhao, Xuechao Yan, Yuchen Zhou, Yurui Chen, Wen Guo, Hang Xu, Junchi Yan

entry.summary: Point as Skeleton is a generative sensor-simulation framework for closed-loop autonomous driving that autoregressively synthesizes video from step-wise updated ego and actor states plus point-cloud skeleton conditions. Introduces Reset-and-Roll to stabilize rolling diffusion during closed-loop rollout.

entry.title: $\mathbf{\lambda}$-VAE: Variance Equalization for Posterior Collapse

author.name: Girum Demisse

entry.summary: Proposes lambda-VAE with variance equalization to address posterior collapse, formalizing two coupled causes: gradient imbalance where the reconstruction signal vanishes faster than KL pressure, and information gap from stochastic sampling discarding encoder representation. Offers a unified account of a long-open VAE failure mode.

entry.title: GEM-Occ: From Visual Geometry Evidence to Embodied Semantic Occupancy Memory

author.name: Hu Zhu, Bohan Li, Xianda Guo, Hongsi Liu, Baorui Peng, Mingqi Yuan, Xin Jin, Wenjun Zeng, Chang Wen Chen

entry.summary: GEM-Occ and its HIOcc benchmark unify ScanNet, ScanNet++, and Matterport3D into a common sparse semantic occupancy format for long-horizon indoor mapping across connected spaces, supporting local, cross-view, and global evaluation regimes. Addresses the gap left by single-view or room-level occupancy work.

entry.title: LLM-Driven Neural Network Generation with Same-Family Architecture Guidance: Disentangling Transfer and Adaptation

author.name: Kabir Dev Paul Baghel, Radu Timofte, Dmitry Ignatov

entry.summary: Studies LLM-driven neural network generation constrained to improving a weak target using a stronger same-family source from a model database, with a source-guided candidate protocol and no-LLM ablation under equal budgets. Reports large accuracy advantages for source-guided candidates on CIFAR-10.

entry.title: Uncertainty-Aware Velocity Correction for Proprioceptive Vehicle Localization using Evidential Mamba

author.name: Abinav Kalyanasundaram, Karthikeyan Chandra Sekaran, Wolfgang Utschick, Michael Botsch

entry.summary: EVC-Mamba turns onboard vehicle sensor data into a virtual velocity sensor for correcting inertial navigation drift in GPS-denied conditions, using a Mamba selective state-space model with evidential deep learning for uncertainty. It avoids extra hardware or infrastructure for localization.

entry.title: Delay-Aware Active Triangulation with Uncertainty-Driven Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Counter-UAS

author.name: Seungwook Lee, David Hyunchul Shim

entry.summary: This paper proposes a delay-aware, uncertainty-driven multi-agent RL framework for triangulating and localizing aerial targets in counter-UAS scenarios, using an Age-of-Information augmented Dec-POMDP. It reports that modeling latency and using perception-consistent rewards improves triangulation validity.

entry.title: Calf-Integrated Arms for Bimanual Quadruped Loco-Manipulation

author.name: Yan Pan, Yuanchuan Ren, Chipui Chan, Jingcheng Sun, Chengxu Zhou

entry.summary: This paper integrates calf-mounted manipulators with slider, revolute joints, and grippers into a Unitree Go2 quadruped, enabling two-handed ground-level manipulation while all four legs remain planted. A vision-language model sequences skills from a predefined library.

entry.title: TurnOPD: Making On-Policy Distillation Turn-Aware for Efficient Long-Horizon Agent Training

author.name: Yuhang Zhou, Kai Zheng, Haoling Li, Dengyun Peng, Can Xu, Jingjing Chen

entry.summary: Introduces a turn-aware on-policy distillation method that budgets KL supervision away from noisy tail turns and toward under-trained deep decision turns for long-horizon agents. It improves training efficiency over vanilla trajectory-level distillation.

entry.title: When do prophets profit in prediction markets?

author.name: Anri Gu, Nicole Kagan, Alec Sun, Jibang Wu, Haifeng Xu

entry.summary: Establishes a formal equivalence between forecasting accuracy and trading profitability in central-limit-order-book prediction markets, constructing a proper betting strategy for any strictly proper scoring rule. It resolves the discrepancy where informed forecasters can lose money under real exchange designs.

entry.title: Breaking Structural Isolation: Scalable Graph Clustering via Community-Aware Sampling and Structural Entropy

author.name: Jingyun Zhang, Hao Peng, Jianxin Li, Angsheng Li, Philip S. Yu

entry.summary: Proposes SCISE, a scalable graph clustering framework combining community-aware sampling with a structural entropy constraint to avoid the structural isolation problem in mini-batch contrastive training. It aims to preserve global topology in large networks.

entry.title: Self-Review Reinforcement Learning (SRRL) with Cross-Episode Memory and Policy Distillation

author.name: Muhammad Zain Amin, Kibele Sebnem Yildirim

entry.summary: Proposes Self-Review Reinforcement Learning, which embeds an explicit self-review step into each RL episode so failed first attempts inform an improved second attempt. It combines cross-episode memory and policy distillation to address sparse, delayed feedback.

entry.title: Harnessing Generative Image Models for Training-Free Primitive Shape Abstraction

author.name: Gregor Kobsik, Tim Elsner, Leif Kobbelt

entry.summary: Presents a training-free pipeline that harnesses pretrained generative image models to abstract 3D shapes into geometric primitives by rendering multi-view images, using a vision-language model to identify parts, and painting primitives back. It avoids task-specific fine-tuning.

entry.title: Safe Bayesian Optimization with Counterfactual Policies

author.name: Katherine Avery, Bruno Castro da Silva, David Jensen

entry.summary: Proposes safe Bayesian optimization where safety is defined relative to an unobserved counterfactual baseline policy, using conformal prediction to bound the baseline outcomes. It targets settings like clinical medicine where new interventions must not fall below an established standard.

entry.title: SAMPLe: SAM-based Optimizer for Prompt Learning in VLMs

author.name: Hossein Rajoli, Fatemeh Lotfi, Niloufar Alipour Talemi, Hossein Kashiani, Xiaolong Ma, Fatemeh Afghah

entry.summary: SAMPLe applies sharpness-aware minimization to prompt learning in vision-language models like CLIP to reduce overfitting on seen distributions. It seeks to preserve generalization to unseen data as a plug-in optimizer.

entry.title: DeSeG: Decoupling Semantic Intent and Geometric Constraints for Physically Plausible Human-Scene Interaction

author.name: Jiakun Li, Zhe Li, Wenqiang Wu, Zheng Chang, Mingqi Gao, Jinyu Yang, Feng Zheng

entry.summary: DeSeG is a hierarchical framework for synthesizing physically plausible human-scene interactions that explicitly decouples semantic intent from geometric constraints. It aims to reduce shortcut bias and physical hallucinations like body-scene penetration.

entry.title: Breaking Spurious Correlations via Generative Randomization and Cross-Variant Self-Supervised Learning

author.name: Suraj Yadav, Anjaneya Sharma, Siddharth Yadav

entry.summary: Proposes a two-stage framework that isolates foreground objects and generates context-shifted variants with a structure-preserving diffusion model, then uses cross-variant self-supervision to learn background-invariant representations. Targets distribution-shift robustness against spurious correlations.

entry.title: Bridging Diffusion Pruning and Step Distillation with Teacher-Aligned Repair

author.name: Jincheng Ying, Li Wenlin, Minghui Xu, Yinhao Xiao

entry.summary: Investigates replacing post-pruning retraining with step distillation for diffusion models, finding direct replacement fails and introducing teacher-aligned repair to bridge pruning and distillation. Attacks both network-size and step-count inference costs together.

entry.title: Generalized Synthetic Image Detection with Enhanced RGB-Noise Representation Learning

author.name: Zhen Li, Gang Cao, Tian Zhang, Lifang Yu, Shaowei Weng

entry.summary: RNSIDNet is a forensic framework for generalized synthetic image detection using enhanced RGB-Noise representation learning, where CLIP-based global semantics modulate high-frequency noise artifacts. Targets cross-model generalization and real-world degradation robustness.

entry.title: PIPBench: A Profile-Inclusive Framework for Personalized Image Generation Evaluation

author.name: Yuhang Wu, Shuxiang Zhang, Wee Hian Ching, Chi Zhang, Miao Liu

entry.summary: PIPBench is a profile-inclusive benchmark for personalized image generation that evaluates alignment with a user's implicit visual preferences from a few historical images and a short prompt. It includes a data pipeline using psychological and demographic profiling for real and synthetic data.

entry.title: APVI-SLAM: Real-Time Acoustic-Pressure-Visual-Inertial Localization and Photorealistic Mapping System in Complex Underwater Environment

author.name: Hanwen Zhang, Yipeng Zhu, Xiaopeng Guo, Huajian Huang, Sai-Kit Yeung

entry.summary: APVI-SLAM is a real-time multi-sensor fusion SLAM system for complex underwater environments that combines acoustic, pressure, visual, and inertial data with reliability-aware reweighting and a sliding-window recovery strategy. It targets accurate localization plus photorealistic mapping under severe feature degradation.

entry.title: Fork Around and Find Out: Part 1: Part 1: A Fork Snaps Into Existence at Block 5

author.name: dl

entry.summary: This is the first part of a mechanistic interpretability project on Maia 3, a chess transformer trained to imitate human play, aiming to locate and causally describe the knight-fork tactic. It presents correlational evidence that the knight-fork policy logit forms after a specific attention layer, with later parts promising causal and compositional analysis.

entry.title: Narrative World Model: Narratology-Grounded Writer Memory for Long-Form Fiction

author.name: Mohammad Saifullah, Thomas Kornmaier, Taaha Kazi, Vasu Sharma, Aditya Sanjiv Kanade, Aanand Kumar Yadav

entry.summary: Introduces a writer-memory system that pairs a narratology-grounded typed temporal-state graph with query-conditioned hybrid retrieval to answer multi-hop questions about evolving story state. It isolates memory quality by using a fixed reader model for all systems.

entry.title: Akashic: A Low-Overhead LLM Inference Service with MemAttention

author.name: Yang Liu, Zhaokai Luo, Huayi Jin, Ruozhou He, Chenchen Hong, Zhiyong Wang, Yifei Liu, Yunfei Gu, Chentao Wu, Junhao Hu

entry.summary: Presents a low-overhead memory system for multi-turn agents that organizes context into bounded chunks with cross-chunk semantic relationships and hardware-aware placement, avoiding full-history replay. It targets prefill cost and evidence dilution in long agentic contexts.

entry.title: DT-Guard: Intent-Driven Reasoning-Active Training for Reasoning-Free LLM Safety Guardrail

author.name: He Liu, Changtao Miao, Xinjie Yang, Tianle Song, Yin Wu, Junchi Chen, Bintao He, Xinyuan Zhang, Bo Zhang, Shi Yan, Wei Lu, Wei Wang, Danyang Xu, Jiansheng Cai, Zhe Li

entry.summary: Presents a content-safety guardrail trained with reasoning supervision but emitting only structured safety labels at inference, aiming to combine the judgment quality of reasoning guards with the latency of classifier guards. It targets concealed intent and borderline decisions.

entry.title: ExplAIner: A Declarative Query Language for Explaining Classification Models

author.name: Marcelo Arenas, Pablo Barcel\'o, Diego Bustamante, Jose Caraball, Mar\'ia Alejandra Schild, Bernardo Subercaseaux

entry.summary: Develops a declarative query language for explaining Boolean classification models, revisiting an existing interpretability language, proving its limitations for optimality-based queries and hardness over decision trees, and extending its vocabulary and layering. It brings a data-management perspective to XAI notions.

entry.title: RMISC: A Large-scale Real-world Multivariate Corpus for Time Series Foundation Models

author.name: Qian Sun, Yong-Ming Tian, Jia-Wei Huang, Cheng Feng, Shao-Qun Zhang

entry.summary: Establishes a large, openly accessible real-world multivariate time-series corpus (around 200 datasets) to test whether time-series foundation models trained on real data outperform those trained on synthetic data. It probes a key pretraining data question for multivariate TSFMs.

entry.title: Modality Relevance is not Modality Utility: Post-hoc Selective Modality Escalation for Cost-Aware Multimodal RAG

author.name: Xue Li, Yiming Gai

entry.summary: Shows via oracle analysis on a multimodal QA dataset that a modality's relevance to a question weakly predicts whether it is actually needed to answer correctly, and proposes post-hoc selective modality escalation instead of pre-retrieval modality selection. It reframes when to invoke expensive vision-language processing in multimodal RAG.

entry.title: Prompt Robustness Is Task-Dependent: Comparing Objective and Belief-Style Questions in LLM Evaluation

author.name: Sadia Kamal, Arefa Patwary, Anthony Marchiafava, Atriya Sen, Sagnik Ray Choudhury

entry.summary: Investigates whether prompt robustness differs between objective questions with fixed answers and subjective belief or value questions, across four model families and six datasets. It finds robustness is task-dependent, cautioning against treating survey-style responses as stable measures of model values.

entry.title: RPAM: A Principled Metric for Evaluating Associations in Language Models with High Predictive Validity in Downstream Outputs

author.name: Damian Hodel, Jevin West, Aylin Caliskan

entry.summary: Proposes RPAM, an upstream metric analyzing language-model associations at the level of embeddings or continuation probabilities that shows strong predictive validity for downstream stereotyped outputs. It aims for generalizable bias measurement across models without dataset-specific downstream metrics.

entry.title: SCOReD: Student-Aware CoT Optimization for Recommendation Distillation

author.name: Haz Sameen Shahgir, Yufei Li, Frank Shyu, Luke Simon, Sandeep Pandey, Xi Liu, Yue Dong

entry.summary: Presents SCOReD, a chain-of-thought distillation framework tailored to recommendation that parses verbose, high-uncertainty teacher traces into typed components to train concise, revising student models. It addresses teacher traces being out-of-distribution for small student LLMs.

entry.title: Complementary Roles of Image Classification and Vessel Segmentation in AI-Based Screening for Retinopathy of Prematurity Plus Disease in a Kenyan Preterm Cohort

author.name: Fred Mutisya, Oscar Onyango, Sarah Sitati, Syokau Ilovi, Aeesha NJ Malik, Brenda W'mosi, Brian Makini, Jalemba Aluuvala, Josiah Onyango, Rachael Kanguha Mmene, Steven Wanyee

entry.summary: Evaluates AI-based screening for retinopathy of prematurity plus disease in a Kenyan preterm cohort of 121 infants, comparing image classification and vessel segmentation approaches across eleven configurations. It shows complementary roles of the two approaches for automated detection in a low-resource setting.

entry.title: Unsupervised Anomaly Detection of Information Operations Users via Behavioral and Language Patterns

author.name: Sishun Liu, Sajal Halder, Ke Deng, Yan Wang, Xiuzhen Zhang

entry.summary: Introduces TENSOR, an unsupervised approach that frames detection of social-media information-operations users as anomaly detection over multimodal temporal, behavioral, and language signals. It avoids oversimplified coordination assumptions used by prior unsupervised methods.

entry.title: Few-Medoids: An Embarrassingly Simple Coreset Selection Method for Few-Shot Knowledge Distillation

author.name: Cemil-Andrei Dilmac, Florinel-Alin Croitoru, Radu Tudor Ionescu

entry.summary: Presents few-medoids, a simple coreset selection strategy that picks samples closest to each class centroid for few-shot knowledge distillation, tested across four datasets and three teacher-student pairs. It aims to reliably surpass the stubborn random-selection baseline.

entry.title: Progressive Reasoning with Primitive Correction for Compositional Zero-Shot Learning

author.name: Ziyi Chen, Haoyan Shi, Sunhan Xu, Congyan Lang

entry.summary: PRPC is a progressive reasoning framework for compositional zero-shot learning that models bidirectional attribute-object dependency via step-wise Q&A-style chain-of-thought inference with primitive correction. It suppresses error propagation in earlier predictions.

entry.title: KOAL: Knowledge-Driven Prostate Cancer Grading with Ordinal-Aware Learning

author.name: Zheng Guo, Jiaqi Cui, Haocheng Xiong, Jize Han, Bo Liu, Qianwen Zhang, Rui Chen, Yan Wang

entry.summary: KOAL predicts prostate cancer Gleason Grade Group from multiparametric MRI using clinical context modulation and ordinal-aware learning that respects the hierarchy of primary and secondary Gleason patterns. Aims to reduce unnecessary biopsies non-invasively.

entry.title: Revisiting Scene Graph Generation from the Perspective of Detector-Conditioned Reachability

author.name: Runfeng Qu, Pia K Bideau, Ole Hall, Julie Ouerfelli-Ethier, Klaus Obermayer, Olaf Hellwich

entry.summary: Analyzes prediction discrepancies between detector-based and query-based scene graph generation through detector-conditioned reachability, then proposes a Dual-SGG method combining both mechanisms via dual queries. Leverages their complementary behaviors.

entry.title: EeveeDark: A Binary Neural Framework for Low-Light Video Enhancement via Event-Guided Sensor-Level Fusion

author.name: Onur Eker, Erkut Erdem, Aykut Erdem

entry.summary: EeveeDark is a binary neural network framework for low-light video enhancement that fuses sensor-level RAW data with event streams using modality-specific binary encoders and event-guided gating. Targets high-quality restoration under tight compute budgets.

entry.title: MAC-XA: Multi-view Anatomy-Correspondence Fusion for Coronary Stenosis Reporting from X-ray Angiography

author.name: Chen Jia, Baochang Zhang, Fatia Kusuma Dewi, Amir Yousefi, Heribert Schunkert, Reza Ghotbi, Nassir Navab

entry.summary: MAC-XA generates coronary stenosis reports from multi-view X-ray angiography by learning anatomically consistent cross-projection fusion despite the fact that cross-view alignment is not directly observable. Addresses projection-dependent overlap and foreshortening in single-view modeling.

entry.title: Straight-Path Flow Matching for Incomplete Multi-View Clustering

author.name: Yiteng Yuan, Junyan Wang, Zheyuan Liu, Hong Jia, Lei Fan, Zhulin Tao, Lianbo Guo

entry.summary: Introduces a flow-matching framework with linear interpolation paths between paired view representations for incomplete multi-view clustering, replacing stochastic diffusion denoising with deterministic ODE flows. Provides theoretical analysis tailored to clustering rather than generic generation.

entry.title: Synthetic-to-Real Translation for Class-Agnostic Motion Prediction

author.name: Yizheng Wu, Hongwei Fan, Kewei Wang, Ruibo Li, Xingyi Li, Xiao Song, Zhe Wang, Chenjing Ding, Dongliang Wang, Zhiguo Cao, Guosheng Lin

entry.summary: Proposes a synthetic-to-real translation framework for class-agnostic motion prediction in autonomous driving, addressing sensitivity of naive motion regression to domain shift with objectness-aware components. Aims to transfer motion knowledge from synthetic to real data cheaply.

entry.title: Andha-Dhun: A First Look at Audio Descriptions in Hindi

author.name: Ritabrata Chakraborty, Divy Kala, Nisheeth Bhooshan Gupta, Ganji Sreeram, Pailla Balakrishna Reddy, Makarand Tapaswi

entry.summary: Andha-Dhun presents the first systematic study of audio descriptions in Hindi for blind and low-vision audiences, including a dataset from eight movies and comparisons of direct generation versus translation. Motivated by Indian film certification mandates to expand beyond English.

entry.title: MonoIR-RS: Infrared Remote Sensing Vision-Language Learning with CLIP and VLM Adaptation

author.name: Jiaju Han, Ma Yaqi, Yahui Chai, Xuemeng Sun, Xin Li, Qike Zhang, Yingying Zhao, Xiang Chen, Luwei Yang, Chengyin Hu, Jiahuan Long

entry.summary: MonoIR-RS is a large-scale infrared remote-sensing vision-language dataset and benchmark pairing IR-aware data with CLIP-style contrastive adaptation and VLM instruction tuning, comprising 600K synthesized IR images. Addresses the visible-band bias of existing remote-sensing VL resources.

entry.title: BitFair: A 12nm Bit-Serial CNN Accelerator with Learnable Early Termination and Adaptive Bit Ordering for Ultra-Low-Power XR Vision

author.name: Ang Li, Chang Gao

entry.summary: BitFair is a 12nm bit-serial CNN accelerator with learnable bit-level early termination and adaptive bit ordering that exploits dynamic bit-level sparsity by learning per-layer thresholds. Targets always-on XR perception under a few-watt power and sub-20ms latency budget.

entry.title: SSA-3DGS: Unsupervised Removal of Screen-Space Artifacts for 3D Gaussian Splatting

author.name: Kristof Overdulve, Lode Jorissen, Nick Michiels

entry.summary: SSA-3DGS unsupervisedly removes screen-space artifacts (sensor defects, lens obstructions, watermarks, UI overlays) that get baked into 3D Gaussian Splatting reconstructions as floaters. Handles occlusions fixed to the 2D image plane rather than the 3D world.

entry.title: GraspIT: A Dataset Bridging the Sim-to-Real gap and back for Validated Grasping SE(3) Pose Generation

author.name: Paul Koch. Adem Karakurt, Andr\'e Sers

entry.summary: GraspIT is a grasping dataset providing photorealistic RGB-D observations, physically validated grasp quality via a four-stage slip-test on parallel Franka Panda instances, and a real-to-sim loop, yielding graded hard negatives. Aims to bridge the sim-to-real gap for SE(3) grasp pose generation.

entry.title: Learning to Throw Objects Safely in Multi-Obstacle Environments

author.name: Mohammadreza Kasaei, Klemen Voncina, Hamidreza Kasaei

entry.summary: Addresses robotic throwing into a target basket while avoiding randomly placed obstacles, introducing a potential-field state representation encoding basket attraction and obstacle repulsion on a fixed grid to let RL policies generalize across obstacle configurations. Extends prior obstacle-free throwing approaches.

entry.title: Assessing the Operational Impact of Poisoning Attacks over Augmented 3D Point Cloud Public Datasets for Connected and Autonomous Vehicles

author.name: Marwan Lazrag, Badis Hammi, Lorena Gonzalez-Manzano, Joaquin Garcia-Alfaro

entry.summary: This paper studies how data poisoning attacks against public 3D point cloud datasets behave when the poisoned data is expanded through data augmentation, in the context of connected and autonomous vehicles. It examines whether augmentation reduces or amplifies misclassification and backdoor risks in trained perception models.

entry.title: RoboVAST: Automated Scenario-Based Validation of Robots at Scale

author.name: Frederik Pasch, Samuel Wiest, Argentina Ortega, Nico Hochgeschwender

entry.summary: RoboVAST is a framework for automated, scenario-based validation of robots at scale, modeling scenarios compositionally and executing declarative test campaigns in containerized environments with integrated analysis. It is demonstrated on a large navigation dataset with thousands of scenario configurations.

entry.title: Superhuman Articulacy as an LLM Safety Target

author.name: Dylan Bowman

entry.summary: This essay argues that current LLMs are poor communicators relative to their agentic capabilities and that improving articulacy, distinct from truthfulness, is useful or even necessary for AI safety. It proposes a path toward superhuman articulacy as a safety target.

entry.title: From Passive Retrieval to Active Memory Navigation: Learning to Use Memory as a Structured Action Space

author.name: Yue Xu, Yutao Sun, Yihao Liu, Mengyu Zhou, Jiayi Qiao, Lu Ma, Kai Tang, Wenjie Wang, Xiaoxi Jiang, Guanjun Jiang

entry.summary: Proposes treating long-term user memory as a structured action space rather than passive retrieval, organizing history into a multi-granularity pyramid with provenance links and training the agent to actively select memory. It targets personalized conversational agents.

entry.title: PCBWorld: A Benchmark Environment for Engine-Grounded PCB Design Automation

author.name: Hyungseok Song, Junseok Park, Won-Seok Choi, Seohui Bae, Han-Seul Jeong, Youngjoon Park, Soonyoung Lee

entry.summary: Introduces an open-source engine-grounded PCB routing environment built on KiCad where agents route boards through native operations and DRC feedback, supporting both RL policies and tool-using LLM agents. It ships datasets including hundreds of real open-source boards.

entry.title: Learning to Control LLM Agent Harnesses with Offline Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Haiwen Yi, Xinyuan Song

entry.summary: Reframes the agent execution harness as a learnable control layer, formalizing harness operation as a finite-horizon MDP where a lightweight controller selects structural execution actions while the LLM stays frozen, trained via advantage-weighted regression on terminal rewards. It also introduces a harness-maturity score separate from final task quality.

entry.title: PatchOptic for Shared-State LLM Workflows with Projected Views and Verified Structured Updates

author.name: Zhaoyu Bai, Jiaqi Cai

entry.summary: Introduces PatchOptic, an optic-inspired interface defining a contract between locally proposed rewrites and global validity in shared-state agentic LLM workflows. It targets the correctness gap left by progressive-disclosure views built via search, RAG, and AST queries.

entry.title: Whose fairness? Structural concentration in AI bias research

author.name: Abhash Shrestha, Subigya Gautam, Anu Sapkota, Sanju Tiwari, Tek Raj Chhetri

entry.summary: A bibliometric and semantic-clustering study of 692 fairness publications showing that AI bias research is structurally concentrated geographically, especially in the domain the rest of the field inherits from. It questions the presumed universality of fairness definitions and benchmarks.

entry.title: Hierarchical Classification via Cascading Feature Elimination: Application to Human Phenotype Ontology-Aligned Facial Phenotyping (FaceMesh2HPO)

author.name: Fabio Hellmann, Alexander Hustinx, Benjamin D. Solomon, GestaltMatcher Database Consortium, Tzung-Chien Hsieh, Peter Krawitz, Elisabeth Andr\'e

entry.summary: Presents FaceMesh2HPO, a hierarchical PointNet-based pipeline classifying facial phenotypes aligned to the Human Phenotype Ontology from 3D meshes, using annotations from 124 clinicians across ten disorders. It reaches AUROCs from about 0.55 to 0.89 with better performance at ontology parent nodes.

entry.title: Beyond Accuracy: How Humans Evaluate Legally Correct but Socially Controversial Legal Advice from Machines

author.name: Benjamin Minhao Chen, Zhiyu Li

entry.summary: A preregistered survey experiment with 3,348 Chinese adults on how people evaluate identical legal advice attributed to AI versus human lawyers, with or without reasoning. It finds no net algorithm aversion but reveals opposing psychological pathways, with AI advice seen as more objective.

entry.title: Structured-Condensed Prompt Tuning in Vision-Language Models for Fine-grained Image Recognition

author.name: Xinda Liu, Qinyu Zhang, Weiqing Min, Guohua Geng, Shuqiang Jiang

entry.summary: Proposes structured-condensed prompt tuning for fine-grained recognition in CLIP-style VLMs that models semantic relationships and hierarchical dependencies among class labels rather than treating them as isolated. Aims to capture inter-class correlations for visually similar categories.

entry.title: WING: A Window-Prior-Based Generative Network with Gated Inception for Cross-Modality CT Synthesis

author.name: Siyuan Mei, Yan Xia, Yipeng Sun, Siming Bayer, Zirong Li, Chengze Ye, Daiqi Liu, Fuxin Fan, Yixing Huang, Andreas Maier

entry.summary: WING synthesizes CT volumes from MRI and CBCT by reformulating regression into multiple windowed representations that smooth the high-dynamic-range, long-tailed intensity distribution before structured fusion. Aims to improve adaptive radiotherapy planning without extra radiation.

entry.title: FADRA: Frequency-Aware Diffusion with Residual Adaptation for Video Face Restoration

author.name: Jin Jiang, Jia Wang, Panwen Hu, Weiran Zhao, Shengcai Liao

entry.summary: FADRA is a frequency-aware diffusion framework for video face restoration using LoRA adapters and pixel-alignment feature fusion atop a pretrained text-to-video model, plus iterative residual adaptation. Aims to balance spatial fidelity and temporal coherence under complex degradations.

entry.title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Calcification Classification in Mammography Across Multi-Site Datasets

author.name: Xuan Liu, Derek L. Nguyen, Emily C. Barre, Jennifer Thomas, Thomas Lynch, Jeffrey R. Marks, E. Shelley Hwang, Marc D. Ryser, Joseph Y. Lo, Lars J. Grimm

entry.summary: Proposes a calcification classification framework for mammography that uses style-transfer-based unsupervised domain adaptation (AdaIN and CycleGAN) to generate vendor- and technique-specific samples, improving malignant versus benign classification across multi-site datasets. Addresses domain shift without extra annotation.

entry.title: DexTele: A Dual-Arm Dexterous Teleoperation System Based on Motion Retargeting and Adaptive Force Control

author.name: Yuanchuan Lai, Qing Gao, Ziyan Liang, Xianfeng Cheng, Junjie Hu, Zhaojie Ju

entry.summary: DexTele is a dual-arm dexterous teleoperation system combining vision-based motion retargeting with adaptive force control to support cross-platform generalization and compliant grasping. It uses a motion-graph encoder and latent optimization to map human images to robot motions.

entry.title: Entanglement Between an AI and Its Environment

author.name: VojtaKovarik

entry.summary: This post introduces entanglement, a proposed measure of how much information an AI has about its environment, distinguishing actual entanglement of a specific instance from the minimum entanglement needed to solve a task. The authors argue it can lower-bound the fidelity that test environments need to be credible, affecting evaluation cost.

entry.title: Foundation Models for Automatic CAD Generation

author.name: J de Curt\`o, Victoria Guill\'en, I. de Zarz\`a

entry.summary: An empirical study of foundation models for text-to-CAD generation of mechanical parts, introducing a multi-model framework with schema validation, analytic scoring, and iterative refinement over a 97-problem benchmark. It compares critique regimes including a ray-traced visual metric approach.

entry.title: ArtisanCAD: An Industrial-Level CAD Agent with Expert-Grounded Knowledge Distillation

author.name: Yunhan Xu, Qifeng Wu, Xunjin Li, Yuanwei Bin, Qingsong Yao, Jianghang Gu, Guan Wang, Weihao Lv, Huiyu Yang, Wenfa Luo, Jiao Xiang, Yuntian Chen, Shiyi Chen

entry.summary: Introduces a skill-guided industrial CAD agent that distills expert procedural knowledge from CATIA recordings, macro logs, and drawing notes to handle ambiguous, high-level design intent with editable parametric geometry. It aims at production-grade B-Rep execution.

entry.title: FreqDepthKV: Frequency-Guided Depth Sharing for Robust KV Cache Compression in Long-Context LLM Inference

author.name: Anna C\'ordoba, Adam Puente Tercero, Nerea Angulo Hijo, Mar Linares Tercero, Julia Barrientos, Ainhoa Miranda, Jes\'us Olivera

entry.summary: Introduces an inference-time KV cache compression method that factorizes adjacent-layer KV states into shared low-frequency depth components and sparse high-frequency residuals, with an online probe assigning heads to shared, residual, or exact modes. It adapts compression to prompt structure without retraining.

entry.title: Lingering Authority: Revocable Resource-and-Effect Capabilities for Coding Agents

author.name: Igor Santos-Grueiro

entry.summary: Introduces a reference monitor that compiles a task contract into revocable, epoch-bound capabilities for coding agents so temporary tool access is closed once its subgoal ends, addressing lingering authority after an episode. It uses a request-grant-invoke lifecycle with closure predicates and deny rules.

entry.title: Benchmarking KV-Cache Optimizations across Task Quality and System Performance for Long-Context Serving

author.name: Nikita Agrawal, Ruben Mayer

entry.summary: Provides a workload-aware benchmark comparing representative KV-cache optimization methods across quantization, pruning, and merging on multiple long-context task types, measuring quality, throughput, time-to-first-token, and realized compression. It addresses inconsistent evaluation conditions across prior methods.

entry.title: CANONIC: Governance Is Compilation

author.name: Dexter Hadley

entry.summary: Introduces a benchmark of dozens of multilingual long-horizon workplace tasks where reasoning, tool invocation, and outputs may span multiple languages, challenging the monolingual assumption of existing agent benchmarks. It probes the interaction between multilinguality and agentic execution.

entry.title: PORTS: Preference-Optimized Retrievers for Tool Selection with Large Language Models

author.name: Lorenzo Molfetta, Giacomo Frisoni, Nicol\`o Monaldini, Gianluca Moro

entry.summary: Introduces an odds-ratio preference-optimization method for training tool-selection retrievers, using a perplexity-inspired preference signal from a frozen LLM to align retrieval with tool-calling behavior. It addresses the misalignment between separately trained retrievers and tool-using LLMs.

entry.title: Scientific Code Search at Scale: A Multi-Domain Dataset and Benchmark

author.name: Nishan Pantha, Pranath Reddy Kumbam, Sajil Awale, Pushwitha Krishnappa, Muthukumaran Ramasubramanian, Nidhi Jha, Emily Foshee, Ankur Kumar, Rachel Slank, Ashkbiz Danehkar, Rahul Ramachandran

entry.summary: Presents a curated corpus of over 5,000 domain-classified scientific software repositories across five NASA science divisions, enriched with cleaned documentation and topics, plus two information-retrieval tasks for scientific code search. It targets the gap between general code-search benchmarks and domain-specific scientific computing needs.

entry.title: Empirical Minimal-Realisation Compression of Deep Neural Networks via Controllability-Observability Tests

author.name: Anis Hamadouche, Amir Hussain

entry.summary: Proposes a control-theoretic framework for compressing deep networks by viewing a trained network as a depth-indexed nonlinear dynamical system and building data-driven controllability, observability, and balanced Gramians to estimate reducible hidden-state ranks. These ranks guide state-order reduction beyond weight- or neuron-based pruning.

entry.title: Decision-Focused Scenario Generation and Selection for Efficient and Robust Grid Dispatch

author.name: Yangze Zhou, Yihong Zhou, Thomas Morstyn, Yi Wang

entry.summary: Proposes a decision-focused generative framework for correlated scenario generation in distributionally robust power grid dispatch, optimizing scenarios for downstream operation rather than pure distribution fitting. It addresses neglected spatial correlations that make statistically plausible scenarios operationally suboptimal.

entry.title: Think Before You Grid-Search: Floor-First Triage for LLM Serving

author.name: Yihua Liu

entry.summary: Presents Floor First, a residual-driven triage workflow for LLM serving that models each decode step as a five-dimensional resource vector to compute optimistic and pessimistic performance floors before invoking heavy profilers. It argues estimation should precede grid-search benchmarking.

entry.title: PVCap: Towards Accurate 3D Dense Captioning via PseudoCap and VoxelCapNet

author.name: Xiaopei Wu, Chenshu Hou, Liang Peng, Dan Xu, Binbin Lin, Xiaoshui Huang, Yuenan Hou, Yu Li, Wenxiao Wang, Haifeng Liu, Deng Cai, Wanli Ouyang

entry.summary: Presents PVCap for 3D dense captioning, addressing limited spatial-layout augmentation and simplistic backbone/detection design in prior work via PseudoCap augmentation and VoxelCapNet. It aims to better capture spatial relations between objects when describing 3D scenes.

entry.title: SpecTrack: Spectral Prompt Guided Adaptive Experts for Multispectral Object Tracking

author.name: Xingyu Tan, Yunrong Qin, Mengjie Hu

entry.summary: SpecTrack is a complexity-aware multispectral and hyperspectral object tracker that adapts capacity based on frame difficulty rather than using a fixed spectral-spatial path. Uses spectral prompts and adaptive experts to handle ambiguous or spectrally similar regions.

entry.title: OBBSeg: Irregular Lesion Segmentation under Oriented Bounding Box Annotations

author.name: Jun Wei, Xinchang Liu, Yu Liu, Chuhua Yang, Shuhui Wang, Hui Huang

entry.summary: OBBSeg uses oriented bounding box annotations as intermediate supervision for medical lesion segmentation, bridging full and weak supervision with a differentiable mask-to-OBB consistency loss. Aims to reduce pixel-level annotation cost for elongated lesions.

entry.title: High-Resolution Artwork Outpainting with Global Blueprint Guidance and Layout Control

author.name: Junha Kim, Hyunjoon Park, Donghyeon Cho

entry.summary: Introduces a high-resolution artwork outpainting method with a global blueprint planning mechanism and layout control to reduce structural instability and error accumulation during progressive expansion. Adds spatial controllability beyond text prompts.

entry.title: Temporal Modeling of Optically Variable Devices in Identity Documents

author.name: Glen Pouliquen (IDnow Research Center, France, EPITA Research Lab), Joseph Chazalon (EPITA Research Lab), Guillaume Chiron (IDnow Research Center, France), Oriol Ramos Terrades (Computer Vision Center), Thierry G\'eraud (EPITA Research Lab), Ahmad Montaser Awal (IDnow Research Center, France)

entry.summary: Presents temporal modeling approaches for verifying optically variable devices (holograms) in identity documents from user-captured videos, exploiting their dynamic nature to resist swapping attacks. Addresses the impracticality of frame-by-frame annotation.

entry.title: Verification of Dynamic Holographic Behavior in Identity Documents

author.name: Glen Pouliquen (IDnow Research Center, France, EPITA Research Lab), Joseph Chazalon (EPITA Research Lab), Guillaume Chiron (IDnow Research Center, France), Thierry G\'eraud (EPITA Research Lab), Ahmad Montaser Awal (IDnow Research Center, France)

entry.summary: Addresses remote verification of holograms on identity documents including detection of dynamic handcrafted fraud, contributing methods and evaluation where prior work lacked public datasets and generalization to unseen attacks. Focuses on optically variable devices over cardholder photos.

entry.title: Mitigating Domain Shift in Conditioned Floor Plan Generation: Synthetic Pre-training for Data-Efficient Adaptation

author.name: Matthieu Ospici, Arnaud Gueze, Luc Bourrat, Adrien Bernhardt

entry.summary: Studies robustness to domain shift in conditioned floor plan generation across three datasets and two generative paradigms, showing large performance drops on new domains and proposing synthetic pretraining for data-efficient adaptation. First to examine this robustness for floor plans.

entry.title: Abductive Corroboration of Probabilistic AI Models for Forensic Synthetic Media Detection

author.name: Junade Ali

entry.summary: Applies abductive reasoning to corroborate outputs from multiple probabilistic AI models for forensic synthetic media detection, aiming to disproportionately lower false-positive risk in forensic settings. Frames this as an alternative to infeasible deductive reasoning with AI models.

entry.title: OrchardBench: A Physically-Grounded, GPU-Parallel Apple-Orchard Simulation Benchmark for Agricultural Robotics

author.name: Humphrey Munn

entry.summary: OrchardBench is a physically grounded, GPU-parallel apple-orchard simulation on the Newton engine where trees are grown by stochastic L-systems and instantiated as fully articulated compliant bodies. Addresses the gap between geometrically detailed but inert tree models and physically plausible robot-learning simulators.

entry.title: TMF-RSE: Tri-Modal Fusion with Regional Semantics and Evidential Uncertainty for Lung Severity Scoring

author.name: Fadi Abdeladhim Zidi, Salah Eddine Bekhouche, Abdellah Zakaria Sellam, Gaby Maroun, Fadi Dornaika, Cosimo Distante

entry.summary: TMF-RSE is a tri-modal deep learning framework for lung disease severity scoring that fuses appearance features, lung segmentation structure, and VLM semantics, using evidential regression for both predictions and uncertainty estimates. Targets clinical decision-making and resource allocation.

entry.title: Efficient Transfer Learning of Robot Dynamic Models Using Morphological Similarity

author.name: Pavlo Kupyn, Yuya Hamamatsu, Roza Gkliva, Asko Ristolainen, Maarja Kruusmaa

entry.summary: This paper introduces an autoencoder-based domain adaptation method that transfers a learned dynamics model from a larger soft, fin-actuated underwater robot to a smaller morphologically similar one with little labeled data. Experiments on two real robots show accurate velocity estimation on the target platform without target labels.

entry.title: Clustering-Embedded Model Predictive Path Integral Control: Avoiding Averaging-Induced Failure and Enabling Efficient Cluster Selection for Dynamic Obstacles

author.name: Zidong Liu, Kaixin Chang, Xu Chen

entry.summary: CE-MPPI adds clustering to Model Predictive Path Integral control to avoid averaging-induced failures where incompatible rollouts are blended, causing hesitation or collision in cluttered non-convex environments. It enables efficient cluster selection for handling dynamic obstacles.

entry.title: Driving the Wrong Way: Leveraging Interpretability in End2End Autonomous Driving Models

author.name: Franz Motzkus, Sebastian Bernhard

entry.summary: Integrates unsupervised dictionary learning as a post-hoc interpretability module in end-to-end autonomous driving models to decompose behavior into semantic concepts and demonstrate their causal influence on trajectory predictions. It also explores targeted interventions.

entry.title: Privilege and confidentiality in generative AI workflows

author.name: V\'aclav Jane\v{c}ek, Thomas Melham

entry.summary: A legal-technical analysis of how generative AI stores client data in model parameters, context windows, and RAG knowledge bases, mapping distinct confidentiality and legal-privilege risks for each mode. It draws on early English and American court decisions on privilege and GenAI.

entry.title: BaFCo: A Document Understanding Benchmark for Complex Bangla Form Comprehension

author.name: Abu Tyeb Azad, Ishita Sur Apan, Fahim Ahmed, Sumaiya Karim Katha, Ezharuddin Jubaer, Armun Alam, Pranjal Kumar Nandi, Amin Ahsan Ali, Aman Chadha, Md Mofijul Islam, AKM Mahbubur Rahman

entry.summary: Introduces BaFCo, a benchmark of 200 complex multi-page Bangladeshi government forms for document layout analysis and key information extraction in Bangla. It addresses the scarcity of annotated data for low-resource document understanding by multimodal LLMs.

entry.title: IMR: Iterative Mode-World Weighted Regression for Multi-Agent Trajectory Prediction

author.name: Honglin Wang, Shiyao Pan, Yun-Fu Liu

entry.summary: Proposes IMR, an iterative mode-world weighted regression approach for multi-agent trajectory prediction that mitigates mode collapse while improving world ranking and top-1 confidence. An iterative decoder generates trajectories recurrently and segmentally to improve accuracy for automated vehicles.

entry.title: Data-dependent Evaluations for Budgeted Submodular Maximization

author.name: Lejian Zhang, Xueyan Tang, Jing Tang

entry.summary: Develops data-dependent upper bounds for budgeted submodular maximization under a knapsack constraint that provably dominate the optimal solution. These bounds let practitioners certify how close a produced solution is to optimal on given instances.

entry.title: NegROI: Click-Centric Uncertainty-Guided Refinement with Scene-Conditioned Negative Prompts for Robust Interactive 3D Segmentation

author.name: Shuheng Zhang, Feng Wu

entry.summary: Presents NegROI, a transformer-based interactive 3D segmentation framework coupling click-centric multi-resolution refinement with scene-conditioned negative prompts to handle coarse voxel resolution and hard false positives. It aims to generalize across dense RGB-D and sparse LiDAR density shifts.

entry.title: Decoupled Single-Mask Annotation Noise Detection via Cross-Sectional Patch Self-Consistency

author.name: Yinheng Zhu, Xiaowei Xu

entry.summary: Introduces a decoupled framework for detecting single-mask annotation noise in vascular CT by exploiting cross-sectional patch self-consistency, producing interpretable, auditable noise evidence without costly multi-rater fusion or coupling to training. Anatomically similar patches with disagreeing masks flag likely labeling errors.

entry.title: Prompt-to-Paper: Agentic AI System for Bioinformatics

author.name: Ramsha Kamran, Maheera Amjad, Zartasha Mustansar, Arsalan Shaukat, Salma Sherbaz, Muhammad U. S. Khan

entry.summary: Proposes a multi-agent framework for automated bioinformatics manuscript generation that grounds claims in retrieved literature, executes rather than fabricates results, and adds a multi-dimensional quality assessment. It targets three known failure modes of end-to-end paper-writing systems.

entry.title: Beyond Static Evaluation: Building Simulation Environments for Scalable Agentic Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Akshay Arora, Ishan Nigam, Ashutosh Aggarwal, Shefali Bansal, Krishna Singh, Sweta Kumari, Nikhil Mittal, Shariq Farhan, Siddarth Malreddy

entry.summary: Describes an API- and UI-driven RL gym that decouples environment creation from scalable execution, using verifiable outcomes, multi-dimensional reward shaping, and internal state validation to curb reward hacking. It is illustrated with a customer-support agent case study.

entry.title: StateFuse: Deterministic Conflict-Preserving Memory for Multi-Agent Systems

author.name: Sergey Volkov, Yang Li, Ye Luo

entry.summary: Introduces a conflict-aware replicated memory contract for multi-agent systems built on CRDT merge, preserving disagreement via immutable history, explicit conflict objects, and projection-time resolution instead of overwrite rules. It provides deterministic, inspectable correction handles.

entry.title: Demonstrating TOFFEE: A Learned System for Synthesizing Data Agent Trajectories at Scale

author.name: Ziting Wang, Yin Li, Zuhao Yang, Xiuchang Li, Jiale Bai, Gao Cong

entry.summary: Demonstrates a system for synthesizing high-quality data-agent trajectories that generalize across heterogeneous enterprise data environments, usable as fine-tuning data or in-context demonstrations. It targets the poor generalization of data agents to unseen analytical workflows.

entry.title: FootsiesGym: A Fighting Game Benchmark for Two-Player Zero-Sum Imperfect-Information Games

author.name: Chase McDonald, Nathan Tsang, Wesley N. Kerr

entry.summary: Releases an open-source vectorized environment based on a minimalist 2D fighting game to study two-player zero-sum imperfect-information learning with cyclic, non-transitive strategies. It benchmarks several RL algorithms and enables high-throughput training on standard hardware.

entry.title: DepthWeave-KV: Token-Adaptive Cross-Layer Residual Factorization for Long-Context KV Cache Compression

author.name: Anna Cordoba, Adam Puente Tercero, Nerea Angulo Hijo, Mar Linares Tercero, Julia Barrientos, Ainhoa Miranda, Jesus Olivera

entry.summary: Presents a token-adaptive KV cache compression method that factorizes key and value states across neighboring layers with shared low-rank bases plus token-specific residuals, routed by a depth router that gives more rank to instruction-bearing tokens. It aims to preserve retrieval quality under compression.

entry.title: Proof of Execution: Runtime Verification for Governed AI Agent Actions

author.name: James Rhodes, George Kang

entry.summary: Formalizes runtime proof of execution for governed agent actions as a contract, causal event stream, and replay context, with well-formedness and validator-checkable invariants guaranteeing authorization, tamper-evidence, and deterministic reconstruction. It targets correctness of effectful agent steps beyond plausible final outputs.

entry.title: Catalyst Papers in Artificial Intelligence Research: A Landscape on ICLR from 2017 to 2025

author.name: Fan Huang

entry.summary: Analyzes over 36,000 ICLR submissions from 2017 to 2025 to test whether public reviewer scores and decisions can identify trajectory-changing catalyst papers at submission time, comparing four disruptiveness measures. It examines whether review signals predict long-run influence.

entry.title: Decision Protocols in Multi-Agent Large Language Model Conversations

author.name: Lars Benedikt Kaesberg

entry.summary: A thesis introducing the MALLM framework to implement and compare decision protocols (voting, consensus, judge mechanisms) in multi-agent LLM conversations. It studies how collaboration structure affects task performance versus test-time cost.

entry.title: Full-range Binary Classifier Calibration for Stable Model Updates in Production

author.name: Konstantin Berlin

entry.summary: Proposes a calibration method that enforces a consistent false-positive-rate meaning across the full score range for security detection models that are frequently retrained. It addresses breakage of downstream users when adversarial distributions drift.

entry.title: Physics-Regularized Machine Learning for Proprioceptive Vehicle Localization Using Onboard Sensors

author.name: Abinav Kalyanasundaram, Karthikeyan Chandra Sekaran, Wolfgang Utschick, Michael Botsch

entry.summary: Introduces PRML2, a physics-regularized hybrid framework combining Kalman filtering with data-driven learning to estimate vehicle pose from onboard proprioceptive sensors during satellite-signal outages. It leverages IMU data for robust autonomous-vehicle localization.

entry.title: Plainbook: Data Science, in Plain Language

author.name: Luca de Alfaro, Mathis Aubert, Ranjit Jhala, Eliana Pastor, Elena Baralis

entry.summary: Introduces Plainbook, notebooks centered on natural-language descriptions from which code is generated automatically, aiming to make reproducible data-science analysis accessible to non-coders. It preserves descriptions rather than code and adopts linear top-to-bottom execution semantics.

entry.title: InfluMatch: Frontier-Quality KOL Search at 4B-Model Cost

author.name: Krittanon Kaewtawee, Petmongkon Pornpichitsuwan, Natchaya Temyingyong, Nutnicha Laplamoon, Wachiravit Modecrua, Krittin Pachtrachai, Touchapon Kraisingkorn

entry.summary: Presents InfluMatch, a low-cost three-stage retrieval-rerank-reason cascade built entirely from small open-weight models for matching influencers to free-form Thai marketing criteria. It claims frontier-quality matching at roughly 4B-model cost by filtering candidates before expensive reasoning.

entry.title: Faithful or Findable? Evaluating LLM-Generated Metadata for RDF Dataset Search

author.name: Riccardo Terrenzi, Serkan Ayvaz

entry.summary: Studies six settings for generating LLM metadata for RDF datasets, evaluating both retrieval effectiveness and faithfulness. It finds unconstrained rewriting maximizes retrieval gains but is least faithful, while profile-grounded rewriting offers the most balanced trade-off.

entry.title: From Blueprint to Reality: Modeling and Applying Putnam's Social Capital Theory with LLM-based Multi-agent Simulations

author.name: Shiyi Ling, Zhi Zheng, Hui Zheng, Wenjun Xue, Feng Ye, Tong Xu

entry.summary: Introduces SocaSim, an LLM-based multi-agent simulation framework to operationalize Putnam's Social Capital Theory with an environment integrating social-network evolution, trust dynamics, and norm propagation. Agents run repeated collective-action experiments to analyze the theory's core dimensions.

entry.title: LLM-Guided Measurement Credibility Correction for Trustworthy Industrial Process Inference

author.name: Youcheng Zong, Runda Jia, Dakuo He

entry.summary: Proposes an LLM-guided approach to correct measurement credibility for industrial process inference, flagging biased, delayed, or stale inputs that still look plausible without relying on numerical correlation, alarms, or explicit process equations. It targets failures that occur before the forecasting model itself becomes the limitation.

entry.title: Clustered Codebook Quantization for 2D Gaussian-based Image Compression

author.name: Runze Cheng, Yicheng Zhan, Josef Spjut, Kaan Ak\c{s}it

entry.summary: Introduces Cluster-Guided Vector Quantization for compressing 2D Gaussian-based image representations by grouping primitive parameters into homogeneous clusters before quantization. Reports around 20 percent bit-per-pixel reduction over the baseline while preserving fidelity.

entry.title: Robust Face Super-Resolution and Recognition Through Multi-Feature Aggregation in Diffusion Models

author.name: Marcelo dos Santos, Rayson Laroca, Jo\~ao Carlos Raposo Neves, David Menotti

entry.summary: Proposes a diffusion-based face super-resolution method that aggregates multiple low-quality surveillance images to preserve identity during restoration. Aims to improve downstream recognition robustness under pose, illumination, and occlusion variation.

entry.title: Realistic Compound-Lens Defocus Blur Synthesis

author.name: Yunkyu Lee, Woohyeok Kim, Sunghyun Cho

entry.summary: Presents a pipeline for synthesizing realistic defocus deblurring training data across diverse compound lenses using wave-optics PSF computation, depth-aware rendering, and ISP simulation. Aims to improve deblurring generalization across cameras.

entry.title: AVA-VLM: Adaptive Visual Attention-Vision Language Model for In-the-Wild Construction Site Monitoring

author.name: Younggun Kim, Taeheon Kim, Youngseo Kim, Seunghee Park

entry.summary: AVA-VLM is a construction-site monitoring vision-language model using a human-inspired coarse-to-fine strategy that requests high-resolution crops only when needed. Improves operational range, reliability at low resolution, and inference efficiency.

entry.title: MSA-DCNN: A Data-Efficient Multi-Scale Deformable CNN for Medical Image Classification

author.name: Hamza Hussaini, Shahana Bano, Eyad Elyan, Carlos Francisco Moreno-Garc\'ia
