entry.summary: MSA-DCNN is a data-efficient multi-scale deformable CNN for medical image classification combining adaptive sampling, within-scale saliency, learned cross-scale fusion, and semi-supervised regularization. Targets multi-scale morphology and limited annotations jointly.

entry.title: RFHNet: Relational and Frequency-Aware Hashing Network for Large-Scale Fine-Grained Food Image Retrieval

author.name: Junsong Wang, Weiqing Min, Guorui Sheng, Tao Yao, Lili Wang, Shuqiang Jiang

entry.summary: RFHNet is a cascaded hierarchical hashing network for large-scale fine-grained food image retrieval that captures both global structure and frequency-sensitive local details. Targets applications like food traceability and dietary monitoring.

entry.title: VaseMuseum: Digital Intelligent Museum for Ancient Greek Pottery

author.name: Jiazi Wang, Nonghai Zhang, Qiushi Xie, Zeyu Zhang, Yufeng Chen, Yang Zhao, Ling Shao, Hao Tang

entry.summary: VaseMuseum is a modular multimodal agent framework for interactive exploration of ancient Greek pottery that grounds fine-grained 2D/3D evidence in curatorial knowledge and calibrates uncertainty when evidence is incomplete. Aims to reduce confident but unsupported VLM answers in cultural heritage.

entry.title: XRFormer: Multiscale Tokenization for XRF Representation Learning

author.name: Sofiane Daimellah, Sylvie Le H\'egarat-Mascle, Clotilde Boust

entry.summary: XRFormer is a transformer for X-ray fluorescence spectra with a multiscale convolutional tokenizer that injects locality and multi-resolution biases before self-attention, tailored to sharp elemental peaks and background variation. Targets automated material analysis in cultural heritage.

entry.title: Observation Quality Matters: Robust Multi-Fisheye Calibration via Failure-Oriented Analysis

author.name: Peize Liu, Zhe Tong, Chen Feng, Shaojie Shen

entry.summary: This paper conducts a failure-oriented analysis of multi-fisheye camera calibration, finding that the dominant failure mode is poor intrinsic initialization when observations have limited radial span, rather than detector recall or distribution imbalance. The insight aims to make large multi-camera calibration more robust.

entry.title: MP-MPPI: A Motion Primitive Guided Sampling-Based Optimizer for Model Predictive Control

author.name: Marlon Mathisen, Aksel Vaaler, Olav Egeland, Eleni Kelasidi

entry.summary: MP-MPPI augments Model Predictive Path Integral control with motion primitives for structured sampling to improve convergence toward globally optimal solutions in cluttered navigation. It is demonstrated on a quadcopter simulator for obstacle-field navigation.

entry.title: Trying to grok Anthropic's Global Workspace paper and the J-space

author.name: TheManxLoiner

entry.summary: This post is an explainer attempting to reconstruct the conceptual reasoning behind the J-space and Jacobian Lens methodology from Anthropic's global workspace paper, focusing on the derivation rather than the empirical findings. It aims to help readers understand the core ideas.

entry.title: A conceptor by any other name

author.name: Keenan Pepper

entry.summary: This post connects an independently conceived steering idea to the existing concept of conceptors, a matrix construction used for representing and steering neural network states, drawing on the Conceptors for Semantic Steering work. It maps a rediscovered idea onto established terminology.

entry.title: Onnes: A Physics-Grounded Multi-Agent LLM Simulator for Cryogenic Fault Diagnosis in Quantum Computing Infrastructure

author.name: Praneeth Narisetty, Uday Kumar Reddy Kattamanchi, Shiva Nagendra Babu Kore

entry.summary: Presents a physics-grounded digital-twin simulator of a dilution refrigerator with a learned real-fridge noise fingerprint, driving a multi-agent LLM operations layer for cryogenic fault diagnosis. It runs a head-to-head between zero-shot LLM agents and a supervised classifier.

entry.title: K-ABENA: K-Adaptive Backpropagation with Error-based N-exclusion Algorithm : (Compensated Loss-Based Sample Exclusion with Unbiased Gradient Estimation)

author.name: Jean-Francois Bonbhel

entry.summary: Presents K-ABENA, a selective gradient framework that excludes low-loss samples from the backward pass while using Horvitz-Thompson inverse-probability reweighting to keep the gradient estimator design-unbiased. It provides convergence guarantees quantifying residual bias.

entry.title: Agentic AI for IPoDWDM Network Lifecycle Automation: An MCP-Enabled Architecture

author.name: Chunmin Xia, Jakub Harbaczewski, Nikhil Dsilva, Julie Raulin, Dominic Schneider, Achim Autenrieth

entry.summary: Presents a distributed, vendor-agnostic multi-MCP architecture for SDN-based autonomous control of multi-vendor, multi-layer IPoDWDM optical networks, enabling end-to-end lifecycle automation and closed-loop cross-layer control. It is experimentally validated on a testbed.

entry.title: Towards Real-World Applications with an Autonomous Powered Wheelchair

author.name: Simone Arreghini, Alessandro Giusti, Alex Bordini, Enrico Ferrara, Giovanni Fulgoni, Antonio Paolillo

entry.summary: This is a proof-of-concept study integrating autonomous perception, gesture-based interaction, and navigation onto a commercial self-balancing powered wheelchair for assistive mobility. It aims to give wheelchair users more active, adaptive support in complex real-world environments.

entry.title: From Graphs to Gradients: Physics-Inspired Structural Attribution for Cyber-Physical IoT Systems and Beyond

author.name: Spyridon Evangelatos, Christos Diou, Georgios Th. Papadopoulos, Evangelos Markakis, Panagiotis Sarigiannidis

entry.summary: Introduces a statistical-mechanics-inspired framework for causal-style structural attribution in cyber-physical IoT systems where explicit causal graphs are impractical. It aims to give interventional, robust explanations for high-risk automated decisions.

entry.title: FirstResearch: Auditable Question Formation for LLM Scientific Discovery Agents

author.name: Yufeng Wang

entry.summary: Proposes a framework where scientific discovery agents emit a structured Research Question Certificate recording assumptions, mechanism, falsifier, and a minimal decisive test, making the first question auditable before downstream work. It targets the opacity of agent-proposed research directions.

entry.title: A toy framework for single and multi-agent human-AI curiosity ecosystems

author.name: Ilya E. Monosov

entry.summary: Offers a conceptual toy framework treating curiosity as an ecosystem, modeling how a single agent's inquiry policy weighs uncertainty reduction, cost, delayed return, and keeping questions open, and extending it to many agents on a shared knowledge landscape. Weights are allowed to shift with experience.

entry.title: Task Decomposition-Guided Reranking for Adaptive Agent Skill Retrieval

author.name: Yanping Chen, Weijie Shi, Wen Yang, Jiajie Xu

entry.summary: Introduces an inference-time reranking framework for agent skill selection that semantically decomposes both task and skill descriptions and accounts for task difficulty and skill applicability. It targets ambiguous matching in large skill libraries.

entry.title: TopoBrick: Agentic Topology Sampling of Exogenous Variables for Zero-Shot Building IoT Forecasting

author.name: Xiachong Lin, Du Yin, Arian Prabowo, Hao Xue, Wen Hu, Imran Razzak, Matthew Amos, Sam Behrens, Flora D. Salim

entry.summary: Introduces a training-free framework for zero-shot building IoT forecasting that uses building knowledge graphs to build a structural skeleton and an agentic sampler to select target-specific exogenous variables, separating past-known from future-known covariates. It outperforms strong zero-shot foundation-model baselines on real buildings.

entry.title: The Granularity Paradox: How Temporal Disaggregation Inflates In-Sample Fit and Compounds Out-of-Sample Error

author.name: Hugo Moreira

entry.summary: Formalizes and empirically demonstrates a granularity paradox in time-series forecasting where finer temporal disaggregation improves in-sample fit but worsens out-of-sample accuracy via recursive error compounding, while coarse aggregation reduces data. It benchmarks ten models across six granularities on a 13-year procurement dataset.

entry.title: AdaStop: Cost-Aware Early Stopping for DNN Test Selection

author.name: Bonan Shen, Wei-Jung Huang, Xin Liu, Jiazhou Gao, Tao Ning

entry.summary: Formulates deep-neural-network testing as a cost-benefit stopping problem, introducing a method that estimates the marginal fault-discovery rate and stops labeling once it drops below a cost-to-value threshold. It addresses the difficulty of choosing a fixed labeling budget for test selection.

entry.title: EvalLoop: A Methodology for Evaluation-Driven Iterative Improvement of Business AI Systems

author.name: Kenneth Benavides, Josh Fleischer, Danti Chen

entry.summary: Presents EvalLoop, a methodology reframing evaluation of business LLM systems from static model ranking to diagnostic, iterative improvement via dimensional metrics, failure-mode classification, and a structured iteration workflow. It targets diagnosing why systems underperform and what to fix.

entry.title: i-EXAM: Instructable and Explainable Attack Connectivity Graph Modeler

author.name: Rakesh Podder, Wadia Ganim, Sarath Sreedharan, Indrajit Ray, Indrakshi Ray

entry.summary: Introduces i-EXAM, a planning-powered tool that builds security profiles of complex networks, identifies attack paths with soundness and completeness guarantees, generates diverse hardening strategies, and explains them in natural language via LLMs. It supports administrators in what-if network hardening analyses.

entry.title: Signed-Graph Recommendation as Structural Consistency Maximization

author.name: Zifan Wang, Siyu Chen, Wenzhuo Song

entry.summary: Proposes SSC-Loop, framing signed social recommendation as maximizing structural consistency across structural, propagation, and semantic layers to combat noise and sparsity. It moves beyond treating the observed trust/distrust graph as fixed.

entry.title: Agents That Teach: Towards Designing Incidental Learning Back into AI-Assisted Software Development

author.name: Rohit Mehra, Samdyuti Suri, Prithviraj K Tagadinamani, Kapil Singi, Vikrant Kaulgud, Adam P. Burden

entry.summary: Introduces Prompt Coach, an agentic tutor embedded in the IDE that uses Socratic guidance grounded in the developer's codebase and target LLM behavior to teach prompt engineering for code generation. An early study with 15 professional developers combines prompt-quality scoring with qualitative feedback.

entry.title: Visual graphs for image classification: does the structure affect performance?

author.name: Alessandra Ibba

entry.summary: An empirical study comparing graph construction techniques for image classification within a fixed three-layer GCN, examining whether graph structure affects performance. Explores encoding spatial, topological, and semantic image information via graph neural networks.

entry.title: A VLM-Enhanced Framework for Comprehensive Traffic Sign Condition Assessment Integrating Daytime Visual Performance and Nighttime Retroreflectivity Evaluation

author.name: Linlin Zhang, Neema Jakisa Owor, Xiang Yu, Abby Watts, Yaw Adu-Gyamfi

entry.summary: Proposes a VLM-enhanced framework for comprehensive traffic sign condition assessment integrating daytime visual factors and nighttime retroreflectivity, automating what is normally manual and equipment-dependent inspection. Addresses the lack of combined day-night evaluation.

entry.title: Embodied Human-Robot Interaction via Acoustics: A MARL Approach with AcoustoBots for Spatial Data Physicalization

author.name: Shiqi Liu, Narsimlu Kemsaram, Prateek Mittal, Pengyuan Wei, Sriram Subramanian

entry.summary: AcoustoBots is a mobile acoustophoretic data-physicalization platform where TurtleBot3 robots carry ultrasonic phased arrays that levitate particles encoding urban scalar values by height. A MADDPG-based multi-agent RL policy handles collision-aware navigation while holography controls levitation.

entry.title: The power of collaboration: How we can reduce traffic congestion

author.name: Unknown

entry.summary: This Google research blog post, filed under Algorithms and Theory, discusses collaborative approaches to reducing traffic congestion. The provided content is only a category tag, so specifics are unavailable.

entry.title: Finding H. pylori in the Fine Print: Evidence-Linked Multi-Agent Case Finding from Gastric Biopsy Reports

author.name: Yufan Wang, Anit Kumar Sahu, Yan Fei Ng, Daniel Kang, Shayan Vassef, Soorya Ram Shimgekar, Koustuv Saha, Piyum Zonooz, Navin Kumar, Chee Leong Cheng, Li Yan Khor

entry.summary: Presents a field-name-driven, evidence-linked multi-agent extraction workflow to identify H. pylori positivity and associated gastritis across coded and free-text gastric biopsy report fields. It is evaluated in a retrospective pilot with contextual assertion and negation handling.

entry.title: CHARLIE: An On-Premise Multi-Agent Retrieval-Augmented Generation System for Evidential Reasoning in Forensic Science

author.name: Leandro D. Carneiro, Andre L. S. Meirelles, Juliano de A. Gomes, Rafael C. A. Cabral

entry.summary: Presents an on-premise multi-agent retrieval-augmented system for structured evidential processing in digital forensics, combining local retrieval, task decomposition, structured memory, and verification while keeping all data within institutional infrastructure. It emphasizes traceability, confidentiality, and legal compliance.

entry.title: Uncovering Latent Depression Severity for Binary Depression Detection via Advantage-weighting Ranking

author.name: Manning Gao, Tingyi Liu, Leheng Zhang, Haifeng Hu, Yuncheng Jiang, Sijie Mai

entry.summary: Proposes a multimodal audio-visual depression detection framework with a temporal encoder, mutual transformer, and a binary advantage-weighting ranking loss that mines hard pairs and enforces intra-class compactness. It aims to sharpen decision boundaries for binary detection.

entry.title: AgoraSim: A Hybrid Agent-Based Modeling Framework

author.name: Chung-Chi Chen

entry.summary: Introduces a hybrid agent-based modeling framework that converts textual or multimodal scenarios into editable configurations and mixes LLM, vision-language, classical, and random agents against matched reference dynamics. It emits shared structured decision objects to enable comparison and auditability.

entry.title: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA): A Model-Agnostic Orchestration Framework for Scalable Clinical Decision Support in Oncology

author.name: Ghassen Marrakchi, Basarab Matei

entry.summary: Proposes a model-agnostic, post-hoc orchestration framework for oncology decision support that decouples data ingestion, clinical routing, and model inference, formalized as a tuple architecture with geometric deep learning to standardize multimodal patient data. It emphasizes keeping orchestration independent of underlying black-box models.

entry.title: EcoVision: AI-Powered Drone Imaging for Salt Marsh Vegetation Monitoring and Dominance Mapping

author.name: Innocent Onyenonachi, Peter J. Lawerance, Nadia Kanwal

entry.summary: Presents EcoVision, a modular UAV imagery pipeline combining transformer segmentation, connected-component vegetation extraction, ConvNeXt species classification, and grid-based dominance scoring to monitor salt-marsh halophytic grasses. It maps dominance of two ecologically significant species at fine resolution.

entry.title: Dynamic Evaluation of Classical and Control-Aware Optimal Trajectory Planning in Robot Manipulators

author.name: Bhanuka Dayawansa, Rohan Munasinghe

entry.summary: The authors present a control-aware optimal trajectory planning framework for robot manipulators that folds actuator effort and system dynamics into a finite-horizon optimization, rather than relying on purely kinematic profiles. A midpoint linearization strategy is introduced to improve accuracy.

entry.title: CSTutorBench: Benchmarking Small Language Models as Tutors for Block-Based Programming

author.name: H. Chad Lane, Bryson Kageler

entry.summary: Presents CSTutorBench, a benchmark evaluating small language models as tutors in a block-based robotics programming environment using a pedagogical rubric and an LLM-as-judge pipeline. It focuses on privacy- and cost-friendly on-premise tutoring for K-12.

entry.title: Integrating knowledge graphs and multilingual scholarly corpora for domain-adaptive LLMs in SSH

author.name: Adam Faci, Alessio Miaschi, Anne Combe, Pascal Cuxac, Francesca Frontini, Nicolas Larrousse, St\'ephane Pouyllau

entry.summary: Reports an ongoing European-project use case integrating knowledge graphs and multilingual scholarly corpora to adapt foundation models for social-sciences-and-humanities research tasks like QA, comparative analysis, and literature review. It follows a shared evaluation protocol emphasizing disciplinary and multilingual diversity.

entry.title: Rethinking Indic AI from a Lens of Cultural Heritage Preservation

author.name: Aparna Madva, Sharath Srivatsa, Srinath Srinivasa, Tulika Saha

entry.summary: A position and longitudinal-survey paper examining how AI affects Indian linguistic and cultural heritage, framing AI as a double-edged tool that can enable inclusion or homogenize worldviews and exclude underrepresented languages. It traces NLP evolution in this space.

entry.title: Contrastive Predictive Coding with Compression for Enhanced Channel State Feedback in Wireless Networks

author.name: Ahmed Y. Radwan, Hina Tabassum, Fahad Syed Muhammad, Matthew Baker

entry.summary: Proposes a unified compression-prediction framework for wireless channel state information that integrates contrastive predictive coding into a 3GPP-compliant compression architecture, forecasting future latent representations rather than high-dimensional CSI matrices. It jointly optimizes reconstruction fidelity and temporal predictive coherence to address channel aging.

entry.title: Learnable Weighting of Intra-Attribute Distances for Categorical Data Clustering with Nominal and Ordinal Attributes

author.name: Yiqun Zhang, Yiu-ming Cheung

entry.summary: Proposes a graph-inspired distance metric that learns weights for nominal versus ordinal categorical attributes, accounting for ordinal ordering and inter-attribute dependencies in clustering. It targets a longstanding gap where most methods treat both categorical subtypes identically.

entry.title: Optimized Adaptive Loop Filter in Versatile Video Coding

author.name: Meng Xuewei, Zhang Jiaqi, Jia Chuanmin, Zhang Xinfeng, Wang Shanshe, Ma Siwei

entry.summary: Optimizes the adaptive loop filter in the Versatile Video Coding standard through parallel GALF/CCALF design, adaptive parameter decisions, and a one-pass distortion estimation scheme. Reduces encoder complexity and memory access versus the VTM baseline.

entry.title: Structured Data Extraction from Real Estate Documents using Clustering, Classification, and Large Language Models

author.name: Muhammad Assad Shehbaz, Carlos Francisco Moreno-Garc\'ia

entry.summary: Presents an end-to-end pipeline combining clustering, classification, and LLMs to extract structured data from heterogeneous real estate questionnaire documents, including scanned and checkbox forms. Applied to nearly 4000 live listing documents.

entry.title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network

author.name: Keonvin Park, Yong Ann Voeurn, Hyeokjun Kweon, Doyun Lee

entry.summary: Proposes a reflection-robust seam segmentation framework for autonomous construction welding robots using a BiSeNetV2 backbone with transfer learning and a hybrid cross-entropy plus Lovasz loss. Improves joint IoU substantially without added compute.

entry.title: Auto-DSM Under the Lens: A Black-Box Evaluation Framework for LLM-Based DSM Generation

author.name: Niels Potters, Theo Hofman

entry.summary: Presents a black-box evaluation framework for LLM-generated Design Structure Matrices, benchmarking against validated ground truth with structural, classification, and stability metrics plus a composite score. It emphasizes reproducibility given closed-source Auto-DSM pipelines.

entry.title: A Physics-Informed Neural Network Framework for Elastodynamic Wave Propagation in Bimaterial Systems

author.name: Sonal Ankush Chibire, Jenn-Terng Gau, Bo Zhang

entry.summary: Presents a physics-informed neural network framework for transient elastodynamic wave propagation in a steel-aluminum bimaterial system, embedding governing equations plus initial, boundary, and interface conditions into the loss, validated against finite-element simulations. It targets a Split Hopkinson Pressure Bar configuration.

entry.title: Geometry-Aware Infrastructure-Anchored Denoiser for UWB Sensing and Work-Zone Reconstruction

author.name: Weizhe Tang, Jiaxi Liu, Junwei you, Steven T. Parker, Pei Li, Sikai Chen, Meng Ran, Bin Ran

entry.summary: Introduces a geometry-aware, infrastructure-anchored learning framework for ultra-wideband sensing that couples temporal range denoising with latent anchor-layout estimation and deterministic distance projection for work-zone reconstruction. It addresses non-line-of-sight, burst noise, and long-tail ranging errors outdoors.

entry.title: How novice coders can develop AI programs for military applications

author.name: Haley Wahl | MIT Lincoln Laboratory

entry.summary: This MIT News article describes an Air Force cadet, with mentor guidance, using vibe-coding, where a novice relies entirely on chatbot prompts, to build a functional AI program for a military-relevant application. It explores whether complete coding novices can produce working software via LLMs.

entry.title: Synthetic Consumer Insight Generation with Large Language Models

author.name: Stephen L. France, Pia. A. Albinsson

entry.summary: Tests whether LLMs can generate synthetic consumer responses for projective marketing techniques, comparing model outputs to human study data across prompts and temperatures using linguistic and diversity metrics. It examines feasibility of scaling costly consumer research.

entry.title: Reward-Density Heuristic for Dynamic Multi-Vehicle Routing: Performance and Computational Efficiency

author.name: Manish Kolachalam, Rani Malhotra

entry.summary: Proposes a reward-density (efficiency) heuristic for a dynamic online vehicle routing and orienteering problem where fleets maximize cumulative reward under continuous replanning. It is evaluated across drone task allocation and taxi dispatch at multiple scales.

entry.title: Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning for Multi Objective Battery Management in Dairy Farms

author.name: Marcos Eduardo Cruz Victorio, Karl Mason

entry.summary: Proposes a two-layer control system combining differential evolution for dynamic pricing with multi-agent deep RL for battery management to integrate renewable energy in Irish dairy farms. It simulates the electrical response in a rural distribution context.

entry.title: Automated Recommendation of Programming Learning Content Using Pattern-based Knowledge Components

author.name: Muntasir Hoq, Griffin Pitts, Zhangqi Duan, Arun Balajiee Lekshmi Narayanan, Mohammad Hassany, Andrew Lan, Peter Brusilovsky, Bita Akram

entry.summary: Investigates using pattern-based knowledge components extracted from code samples to automatically identify introductory programming learning resources targeting similar concepts, aligning activities by shared programming patterns. It aims to reduce time-intensive expert curation.

entry.title: Binocular Gaze Estimation with Single Camera and Single Light Source

author.name: Tongbing Huang, Yang Fu, Yunfei Wang, Zhaocan Wang

entry.summary: Introduces a gaze estimation method needing only one camera and one light source by constructing a virtual glint placed symmetrically to the real source, reducing hardware requirements below the conventional minimum. It targets mobile eye-tracking scenarios.

entry.title: MCP-Enabled Agentic AI for Autonomous IPoDWDM Network Lifecycle Automation

author.name: Chunmin Xia, Jakub Harbaczewski, Nikhil Dsilva, Julie Raulin, Dominic Schneider, Achim Autenrieth

entry.summary: A demo of an MCP-enabled agentic AI architecture for autonomous control of vendor-agnostic IPoDWDM networks, showing live end-to-end multi-layer lifecycle automation and closed-loop control validated on a real testbed. It largely mirrors the companion architecture paper.

entry.title: A Guiding Framework for K-12 Teachers in Creating AI-powered Learning Technologies through Vibe Coding

author.name: Yukyeong Song, Seoyeon Choi, Jinhee Kim, Yeongje Kim, Lauren Weisberg, Jewoong Moon

entry.summary: Proposes a framework guiding K-12 teachers to build AI-powered learning technologies through natural-language vibe coding, grounded in Design Thinking and validated via interaction analysis of a multi-week workshop. It targets the lack of structured guidance for teachers-as-designers.

entry.title: From Application-Layer Simulation to Native Meta-Architecture: Structural Tension as an Endogenous Driver for Heterogeneous AI Evolution

author.name: Heting Mao

entry.summary: Proposes a theoretical meta-architecture aiming to move application-layer cognitive protocols into native model mechanisms via a structural-tension loss, an offline recurrent self-processing loop, and related components. It argues current LLMs are fundamentally stateless simulators of higher-order cognition.

entry.title: When AI Classifies: What Counts as Public Administration?

author.name: Shaoming Cheng, Laurie Schintler

entry.summary: Compares author-defined, citation-driven, and AI-assisted approaches to identifying public-administration and AI-in-public-administration scholarship across two databases, finding substantial divergence in corpus size, outlets, and thematic structure. It shows alternative representations often capture different knowledge domains entirely.

entry.title: Do It Right! A Methodology for Successful NLP System Development

author.name: Olga V. Patterson, Brett South, T. Elizabeth Workman, Scott L DuVall

entry.summary: Applies the Systems Development Life Cycle to clinical NLP projects, presenting a stepwise methodology for successful development beyond just algorithm choice. It emphasizes that algorithmic knowledge is only one ingredient of a working system.

entry.title: From Textural Counterpoint to Feature Encoding: A Multi-Dimensional Machine Representation Study of Haydn's "The Lark" Integrating Electroacoustic Analysis

author.name: Yakun Liu, Zhiyu Jin, Hai Luan, Dong Liu, Xiaonan Li

entry.summary: An interdisciplinary study of Haydn's string quartet The Lark that combines classical morphology, electroacoustic measurement, and machine representation to model role assignment and interaction in polyphonic music. It aims to inform role-aware human-computer collaborative composition.

entry.title: Quaternion-Averaging-Based Adaptive Complementary Filter for Pedestrian Dead Reckoning With a Foot-Mounted AHRS

author.name: Shunsei Yamagishi, Lei Jing

entry.summary: This work proposes a quaternion-averaging adaptive complementary filter for pedestrian dead reckoning using a foot-mounted attitude and heading reference system, aiming for better attitude estimation at lower compute cost. It targets indoor navigation where GPS is unreliable.

entry.title: AI Safety Can't Afford a Second Cause

author.name: atlasaligned

entry.summary: This essay argues that the AI safety movement should stay narrowly focused on existential risk and avoid diluting credibility by mixing in unrelated political causes, using an analogy of an astronomer who tweets about zoning reform. It is a strategy and community-norms argument.

entry.title: AI tools in Arab University English classrooms: Looking back and forward

author.name: Mohammed Q. Shormani, Muneef Yehia Alshawsh

entry.summary: A PRISMA-guided synthesis of empirical studies on AI tools supporting English-language learners in Arab university classrooms, finding positive learner attitudes but mixed gains for higher-order outcomes. Only 11 of 184 studies met inclusion criteria.

entry.title: Another Look at 'Slack'

author.name: AdamPiovarchy

entry.summary: This post revisits the concept of slack, attempting a tighter characterization as the absence of binding constraints on behavior, and points to a longer philosophy journal article. It works through puzzles in defining the notion for a rationalist audience.

entry.title: Ranges of Probabilities: What Are They For?

author.name: SaltAndMetal

entry.summary: This post uses a forecasters' dialogue to explore what ranges or intervals of probabilities are actually for, contrasting a single point estimate against expressing uncertainty about the probability itself. It examines the epistemics of second-order uncertainty.

entry.title: Jesse Thaler named director of the Laboratory for Nuclear Science

author.name: Julia C. Keller | School of Science

entry.summary: This MIT News item announces that theoretical particle physicist Jesse Thaler, who combines machine learning with fundamental physics, has been named director of the Laboratory for Nuclear Science. It notes his leadership of an NSF AI institute bridging AI and physics.

entry.title: June 2026 Links

author.name: nomagicpill

entry.summary: This is a monthly link roundup mixing an unverified claim about Anthropic warning of recursive self-improvement, commentary on AI productivity gains, and unrelated items such as a 1994 UFO incident. It is opinion and aggregation rather than research.

entry.title: Experiments With Fabel's Fiction

author.name: Tomás B.

entry.summary: This post shares a short story generated by an LLM writing tool asked to imitate the author's style, presented as an experiment rather than authored fiction. It is a creative-writing demonstration ahead of losing access to the tool.

entry.title: Join Our AI Safety × Philosophy Reading Group

author.name: Ahmed

entry.summary: This is a recruitment announcement for an eight-week AI Safety and Philosophy reading group covering topics like consciousness, agency, alignment, and metaethics. It seeks participants and facilitators before an application deadline.

entry.title: "Fundamental Uncertainty" Audiobook Available

author.name: Gordon Seidoh Worley

entry.summary: This post announces that the audiobook version of the book Fundamental Uncertainty is now available through several retailers, read by the author. It also reminds readers of an associated essay contest deadline.

entry.title: Research Summary: July 07, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Today's research is anchored by Gemma 4, Google's open-weight multimodal family spanning dense and MoE variants (2B+) with a novel encoder-free design. Generative modeling advances with the first multiplayer interactive world model, conditioning on multiple agents' action streams to handle coupled physics in real time.

Safety, security, and alignment feature prominently:

  • Doubly-efficient interactive proofs provide complexity-theoretic foundations for scalable oversight without debate (Kalai)
  • Untrusted Content Masking extends provable prompt-injection defenses to web agents (Tramèr, Debenedetti, Rando)
  • Reading Between the Dots shows DeepSeek V3 and Kimi K2 perform legible multi-step computation across filler tokens, with direct CoT-monitoring implications

Interpretability and training methods round out the top tier:

  • Anthropic's global workspace paper presents evidence of a working-memory space in Claude
  • LLM-as-a-Verifier frames verification as a new scaling axis via scoring-token logit expectations (Finn, Pavone, Stoica, Mirhoseini)
  • Two critical audits challenge trends: Dictionaries, Not Darwin finds set-level selection beats LLM evolution in equation discovery, while Rethinking On-Policy Self-Distillation (Arora group) shows privileged self-distillation degrades thinking models
  • Anchored Self-Play trains a single model to both generate and fix bugs, forming an automatic code-repair curriculum
entry.content:
Today's research is anchored by Gemma 4, Google's open-weight multimodal family spanning dense and MoE variants (2B+) with a novel encoder-free design. Generative modeling advances with the first multiplayer interactive world model, conditioning on multiple agents' action streams to handle coupled physics in real time.

Safety, security, and alignment feature prominently:

  • Doubly-efficient interactive proofs provide complexity-theoretic foundations for scalable oversight without debate (Kalai)
  • Untrusted Content Masking extends provable prompt-injection defenses to web agents (Tramèr, Debenedetti, Rando)
  • Reading Between the Dots shows DeepSeek V3 and Kimi K2 perform legible multi-step computation across filler tokens, with direct CoT-monitoring implications

Interpretability and training methods round out the top tier:

  • Anthropic's global workspace paper presents evidence of a working-memory space in Claude
  • LLM-as-a-Verifier frames verification as a new scaling axis via scoring-token logit expectations (Finn, Pavone, Stoica, Mirhoseini)
  • Two critical audits challenge trends: Dictionaries, Not Darwin finds set-level selection beats LLM evolution in equation discovery, while Rethinking On-Policy Self-Distillation (Arora group) shows privileged self-distillation degrades thinking models
  • Anchored Self-Play trains a single model to both generate and fix bugs, forming an automatic code-repair curriculum

Hernandez, Omri Homburger, L\'eonard Hussenot, Juyeong Ji, Armand Joulin, Aishwarya Kamath, Parnian Kassraie, Olivier Lacombe, Preethi Lahoti, Ga\"el Liu, Gus Martins, Luciano Martins, Tatiana Matejovicova, Ramona Merhej, Nikola Momchev, Sneha Mondal, Ryan Mullins, Sindhu Raghuram Panyam, Shreya Pathak, Sarah Perrin, Andr\'e Susano Pinto, Etienne Pot, Ang\'eline Pouget, Alexandre Ram\'e, Sabela Ramos, Douglas Reid, David Rim, Morgane Rivi\`ere, Karsten Roth, Louis Rouillard, Omar Sanseviero, Pier Giuseppe Sessa, Shane Settle, Danila Sinopalnikov, Sara Smoot, Piotr Stanczyk, Andreas Steiner, Lawrence Stewart, Ilya Tolstikhin, Michael Tschannen, Anton Tsitsulin, Nino Vieillard, Renjie Wu, Pingmei Xu, Haichuan Yang, Edouard Yvinec, Li Zhang, Joe Zou, Nicolas Aagnes, Abdelrahman Abdelhamed, Shivani Agrawal, Shubham Agrawal, Ibrahim Alabdulmohsin, Jean Baptiste Alayrac, Uri Alon, Chandramouli Amarnath, Ankesh Anand, Chrysovalantis Anastasiou, Setareh Ariafar, Fran\c{c}ois-Xavier Aubet, Kyriakos Axiotis, Federico Barbero, Joelle Barral, Alexei Bendebury, Urs Bergmann, Stanley Bileschi, Kat Black, Mathieu Blondel, Sebastian Borgeaud, Arthur Bra\v{z}inskas, Ryan Burnell, Robert Busa-Fekete, Mu Cai, Glenn Cameron, Charlotte Caucheteux, Garima Chadha, Jetha Chan, Aditya Chawla, Blake Jianhang Chen, Jesse Chen, Lin Chen, Xu Chen, Derek Cheng, Tzu-hsiang Chien, Nikolai Chinaev, Yi Chou, Zhaohui Chu, Benjamin Coleman, Pooja Consul, Sam Conway-Rahman, Scott Crowell, Dylan Cutler, Vivek Dani, Samira Daruki, Anil Das, Daniel Deutsch, Nishanth Dikkala, Li Ding, Qiuhan Ding, Shenil Dodhia, Konstantin Donhauser, Tulsee Doshi, Anca Dragan, Alex Druinsky, Sahil Dua, Zoltan Egyed, Danielle Eisenbud, Daniel Eppens, Cindy Fan, Bahare Fatemi, Yassir Fathullah, Vlad Feinberg, Milen Ferev, Takumi Fujimoto, Isaac Galatzer-Levy, Jo\~ao Gante, Simon Geisler, Soham Ghosal, Antonious M. Sander, Rohit Sathyanarayana, Sagar Savla, Samuel Schmidgall, Tal Schuster, Benoit Seguin, Andrew Sellergren, Aliaksei Severyn, Izhak Shafran, Dhruv Shah, Yuan Shangguan, Ashish Shenoy, Pradeep Shenoy, Rakesh Shivanna, Pauline Sho, Lucas Spangher, Wojciech Stokowiec, Tim Strother, Yao Su, Yinghao Sun, Mukund Sundararajan, Andrea Tacchetti, Mor Hazan Taege, Pouya Tafti, Chetan Tekur, Rahul Thapa, Madeleine Traverse, Lenart Treven, Tao Tu, Chien Te Tung, Petar Veli\v{c}kovi\'c, Malini Pooni Venkat, Sagar Gubbi Venkatesh, Vidya Venkiteswaran, Francesco Visin, Alex Vitvitskyi, Kiran Vodrahalli, Weiyi Wang, Xin Wang, Tris Warkentin, Jan Wassenberg, John Wieting, Lechao Xiao, Hao Xu, Yuhui Xu, Fuzhao Xue, Arun Yadav, Jun Yan, Antoine Yang, Lin Yang, Ming-Hsuan Yang, Ziyu Ying, Jae Hyeon Yoo, Sajjad Zafar, Fred Zhang, Jiageng Zhang, Jianyi Zhang, Xiaofan Zhang, Chao Zhao, David Zhou, Chen Zou

entry.summary: The Gemma 4 technical report introduces Google's new generation of open-weight natively multimodal models spanning dense and MoE architectures from 2.3B to 31B parameters, with improved vision/audio encoders, a unified encoder-free 12B model ingesting raw audio and image patches, and an integrated thinking mode. Gemma 4 was released in April 2026, so this documents an established model family.

entry.title: Multiplayer Interactive World Models with Representation Autoencoders

author.name: Anthony Hu, V\'aclav Volhejn, Adrien Ramanana Rahary, Chris Mulder, Aditya Makkar, Am\'elie Royer, Manu Orsini, Alyx Liao, Adam Jelley, Eloi Alonso, Florian Laurent, Fredrik Nor\'en, James Swingos, Jan H\"unermann, Kent Rollins, Lucas Hosseini, Matthieu Le Cauchois, Maxim Peter, Pim de Witte, Tim Brown, Vincent Micheli, Moritz B\"ohle, Gabriel de Marmiesse, Viktoriia Sharmanska, Lucia Specia, Michael Black, Patrick P\'erez

entry.summary: This paper introduces the first multiplayer interactive world model for highly dynamic environments, conditioning on multiple agents' action streams to attribute scene changes to the correct player and stay coherent under arbitrary action combinations, demonstrated in Rocket League. The 5B-parameter latent diffusion model, trained on 10,000 hours of gameplay, generates real-time four-player matches at 20 fps on a single B200 GPU.

entry.title: How to Avoid Debate: Scalable AI Safety via Doubly-Efficient Interactive Proofs

author.name: Liyan Chen, Yael Tauman Kalai, Zoe Xi

entry.summary: This theoretical AI-safety paper initiates the study of single-prover interactive proofs (specifically doubly-efficient ones) for verifying AI outputs, avoiding debate's assumptions that two provers are equally capable and one is truthful. It shows how to obtain verifiability guarantees without adversarial debate.

entry.title: A global workspace in language models

author.name: wesg

entry.summary: The blog post for Anthropic's paper presenting evidence that language models like Claude have a small collection of verbalizable internal neural patterns functioning as a global workspace, analogous to consciously accessible processing, that can be described, controlled, and used for deliberate reasoning. It introduces techniques for identifying and accessing this space.

entry.title: Dictionaries, Not Darwin: Set-Level Selection Beats LLM Evolution in Scientific Equation Discovery

author.name: Pan Li

entry.summary: This paper audits whether using LLMs as evolutionary engines actually compounds discovery in scientific equation finding, and finds that parent-conditioned evolution is statistically indistinguishable from fresh independent sampling under matched budgets. It argues the loop effectively reduces to building a dictionary of candidate terms, then proposes set-level selection as a stronger alternative. It matters as a rigorous negative result challenging a popular LLM-for-science paradigm.

entry.title: LLM-as-a-Verifier: A General-Purpose Verification Framework

author.name: Jacky Kwok, Shulu Li, Pranav Atreya, Yuejiang Liu, Yixing Jiang, Chelsea Finn, Marco Pavone, Ion Stoica, Azalia Mirhoseini

entry.summary: LLM-as-a-Verifier proposes verification as a new scaling axis, computing continuous scores from the expectation over scoring-token logits rather than discrete judge outputs to provide fine-grained, training-free feedback for agentic tasks. The probabilistic formulation lets verification scale across multiple dimensions.

entry.title: Reading Between the Dots: Decoding Hidden Computation across Filler Tokens

author.name: Kaley Brauer, Claudio Mayrink Verdun, Samuel Marks

entry.summary: This interpretability study shows that frontier open-weight models (DeepSeek V3, Kimi K2) perform structured, legible multi-step computation over content-free filler tokens, with attention routing questions through the filler region and logit-lens readouts revealing intermediate reasoning. It matters for behavioral oversight because it demonstrates hidden reasoning is still decodable from internals.

entry.title: Untrusted Content Masking for Web Agents with Security Guarantees

author.name: Kristina Nikoli\'c, Egor Zverev, Javier Rando, Matthew Jagielski, Edoardo Debenedetti, Florian Tram\`er

entry.summary: Untrusted Content Masking extends provable prompt-injection defenses to web agents, which face a fundamental challenge because rendered pages intermingle trusted instructions with untrusted content, dissolving the trust boundary that guarantees rely on. It provides a masking mechanism to restore separation and offer security guarantees for web agents.

entry.title: Anchored Self-Play for Code Repair

author.name: Caroline Choi, Zeyneb Kaya, Shirley Wu, Tengyu Ma, Tatsunori Hashimoto, Ludwig Schmidt

entry.summary: Proposes generator-fixer self-play, where a single model is RL-trained to both generate bugs and fix them, creating an automatic curriculum as the fixer improves, and introduces BugSourceBench covering realistic bug sources. It scales code-repair supervision without limited human data.

entry.title: A Review of Anthropic's Global Workspace Paper

author.name: Neel Nanda

entry.summary: Neel Nanda's public review of Anthropic's global workspace paper, endorsing its evidence for a cognitive working-memory space in language models and the J-Lens technique for accessing it, and reporting replication of core claims on Qwen 3.6 27B plus preliminary evidence of abstract interpretative meta-tokens. It provides expert critical assessment and independent replication of an interpretability result.

entry.title: Tie training can make DPO/RLHF-trained AIs generalize better

author.name: Elliott Thornley

entry.summary: This post summarizes an ICML paper arguing that DPO and RLHF cause models to latch onto every feature correlated with true value on the training distribution, including spurious ones, even in the infinite-data limit. The authors propose tie training, using pairs of equal-value actions with random or two-way labels, and show it reduces reliance on spurious features and improves out-of-distribution generalization.

entry.title: What Does a Discrete Diffusion Model Learn?

author.name: Rodrigo Casado Noguerales, Bernhard Sch\"olkopf, Thomas Hofmann, Aran Raoufi

entry.summary: This theoretical paper asks what a discrete diffusion model actually learns (a denoiser, score ratio, or bridge predictor), showing these are one object in different coordinates and that reading the network in the wrong coordinate changes the trained/sampled process. It rigorously derives the continuous-time Markov chain ELBO and proves an Oracle Distance theorem equating the negative ELBO exactly to data entropy plus path KL from the oracle reverse process.

entry.title: Rethinking On-Policy Self-Distillation for Thinking Models

author.name: Simran Kaur, Narutatsu Ri, Yinghui He, Liam Fowl, Sanjeev Arora

entry.summary: This paper shows a surprising negative result: privileged self-distillation, where a thinking model teaches itself using solution hints, actually degrades long-reasoning performance by up to 17% across Qwen3 and OLMo models. Degradation scales with withheld context and long rollout budgets.

entry.title: Pretraining Curricula Enable Selective Fine-tuning

author.name: Sebastian A. Bruijns, Jirko Rubruck, Mia H. Whitefield, Kai J. Sandbrink, Fazl Barez, Christopher Summerfield

entry.summary: This paper shows that explicit pretraining curricula (balanced vs imbalanced task ordering) affect in-context learning and the selectivity of refusal fine-tuning, with imbalanced pretraining disentangling tasks into separable neural circuits confirmed by activation patching. It has implications for AI safety where fine-tuning suppresses misaligned behaviors.

entry.title: AIFS-SUBS: Extending Data-Driven Forecasting to Sub-Seasonal Timescales

author.name: Jakob Schloer, Steffen Tietsche, Christopher D. Roberts, Lorenzo Zampieri, Simon Lang, Gert Mertes, Gareth Jones, Matthew Chantry, Frederic Vitart

entry.summary: AIFS-SUBS adapts ECMWF's data-driven AIFS-CRPS medium-range model to sub-seasonal timescales using a 24h autoregressive step to reduce error accumulation, added stratospheric and radiation predictors, and a held-out verification window. It extends machine-learning weather forecasting to longer, harder sub-seasonal lead times.

entry.title: Agent Data Injection Attacks are Realistic Threats to AI Agents

author.name: Woohyuk Choi, Juhee Kim, Taehyun Kang, Jihyeon Jeong, Luyi Xing, Byoungyoung Lee

entry.summary: This paper introduces agent data injection attacks (ADI), a new category of indirect prompt injection where malicious data is disguised as trusted data (security-critical metadata or agent context data like tool call/response formats), rather than as instructions. It shows agents unknowingly execute attacker intent, evading instruction-injection mitigations.

entry.title: Search Beyond What Can Be Taught: Evolving the Knowledge Boundary in Agentic Visual Generation

author.name: Haozhe Wang, Weijia Feng, Jinpeng Yu, Che Liu, Ping Nie, Fangzhen Lin, Jiaming Liu, Ruihua Huang, Jimmy Lin, Wenhu Chen, Cong Wei

entry.summary: This paper tackles the world-knowledge bottleneck in visual generators (which confidently fabricate unknown entities) by building SearchGen-20K, SearchGen-Bench (20,839 prompts across 12 failure categories), and a pre-executed multimodal corpus, then evolving the knowledge boundary via search-augmented agentic visual generation. Frontier open generators score only 21-28/100, a collapse invisible to prior benchmarks.

entry.title: Induction Heads Interpolate N-Grams

author.name: Francesco D'Angelo, Oguz Kaan Yuksel, Swathi Shree Narashiman, Nicolas Flammarion

entry.summary: This mechanistic-interpretability study shows that induction heads in transformers trained on Markov chains implement two classical smoothing estimators: a soft context-matching interpolation analogous to Jelinek-Mercer smoothing, and Dirichlet-style pseudo-counts induced by a beginning-of-sequence token. It gives a precise characterization of what induction-head circuits actually compute.

entry.title: CompactionRL: Reinforcement Learning with Context Compaction for Long-Horizon Agents

author.name: Yujiang Li, Zhenyu Hou, Yi Jing, Jie Tang, Yuxiao Dong

entry.summary: CompactionRL integrates context compaction into reinforcement learning so long-horizon LLM agents can summarize prior states and continue under a compressed context. It uses token-level loss normalization and cross-trajectory advantage estimation to learn from compacted trajectories.

entry.title: Nemotron-Labs-3-Puzzle-75B-A9B: Compressing Hybrid MoE LLMs

author.name: Akhiad Bercovich, Talor Abramovich, Daniel Afrimi, Shay Aharon, Nir Ailon, Vladimir Anisimov, Omer Ullman Argov, Maor Ashkenazi, Tomer Asida, Nave Assaf, Tomer Bar Natan, Alexander Bukharin, Grzegorz Chlebus, Marcin Chochowski, Eric Chung, Mohammad Dabbah, Carlo del Mundo, Ewa Dobrowolska, Ido Galil, Yaniv Galron, Amnon Geifman, Yonatan Geifman, Izik Golan, Alex Gronskiy, Tomasz Grzegorzek, Netanel Haber, Lior Kadoch, Grzegorz Karch, Tomer Keren, Abhinav Khattar, Amir Klein, Tugrul Konuk, Roi Koren, Daniel Korzekwa, Shaun Kotek, Konstantinos Krommydas, Itay Levy, Ofri Masad, Yoav Miron, Pavlo Molchanov, Shahar Mor, Zach Moshe, Saurav Muralidharan, Najeeb Nabwani, Besmira Nushi, Mostofa Patwary, Omri Puny, Johannes Rausch, Tomer Ronen, Sepehr Sameni, Itamar Schen, Elad Segal, Daniel Serebrenik, Ido Shahaf, Soumye Singhal, Daniil Sorokin, Sharath Turuvekere Sreenivas, Marta Stepniewska-Dziubinska, Ali Taghibakhshi, Nima Tajbakhsh, Oren Tropp, Dor Tzur, Anna Warno, Yi-Fu Wu, Michal Zawalski, Jiaqi Zeng, Yian Zhang, Ran Zilberstein, Amit Zuker, Ran El-Yaniv

entry.summary: Nemotron-Labs-3-Puzzle-75B-A9B is a compressed hybrid-MoE variant of NVIDIA's Nemotron-3-Super optimized for interactive serving, achieving roughly 2x higher server throughput at matched user throughput and increasing 1M-token concurrency from 1 to 8 requests on a single H100. It combines iterative Puzzle compression with distillation, RL, quantization, and multi-token prediction.

entry.title: DSpark: Confidence-Scheduled Speculative Decoding with Semi-Autoregressive Generation

author.name: Xin Cheng, Xingkai Yu, Chenze Shao, Jiashi Li, Yunfan Xiong, Yi Qian, Jiaqi Zhu, Shirong Ma, Xiaokang Zhang, Jiasheng Ye, Qinyu Chen, Chengqi Deng, Jiping Yu, Damai Dai, Zhengyan Zhang, Yixuan Wei, Yixuan Tan, Wenkai Yang, Runxin Xu, Yu Wu, Zhean Xu, Xuanyu Wang, Muyang Chen, Rui Tian, Xiao Bi, Zhewen Hao, Shaoyuan Chen, Huanqi Cao, Wentao Zhang, Anyi Xu, Huishuai Zhang, Dongyan Zhao, Wenfeng Liang

entry.summary: DSpark is a speculative decoding framework combining semi-autoregressive parallel drafting with adaptive, load-aware verification to prevent acceptance decay and wasted batch capacity in high-concurrency serving. The large author list from DeepSeek signals a production-oriented efficiency contribution.

entry.title: Individual Parameters in Weight-Sparse Transformers Appear Interpretable

author.name: Arnau Marin-Llobet, Stefan Heimersheim

entry.summary: This paper asks whether individual weights in weight-sparse transformers can be understood globally by characterizing which inputs cause a weight to matter, using an automated LLM pipeline that writes and verifies human-readable descriptions. It advances mechanistic interpretability toward per-parameter accounts.

entry.title: RADIO1D: Elastic Representations for Condensed Vision Modeling

author.name: Greg Heinrich, Mike Ranzinger, Collin McCarthy, Natan Bagrov, Eugene Khvedchenya, Bryan Catanzaro, Jan Kautz, Andrew Tao, Pavlo Molchanov

entry.summary: RADIO1D challenges fixed 2D patch features in VLMs, showing that alignment-trained encoders develop specialized global-summary tokens, and compresses images into compact variable-length 1D token sequences via multi-teacher distillation and an autoencoder. It enables strong scene understanding even from a single token.

entry.title: Do Vision-Language-Action Models Mean What They Say? On the Role of Faithfulness in Embodied Reasoning

author.name: Matthew Foutter, Matteo Cercola, Lena Wild, Yunshan Wang, Michelle Li, Daniele Gammelli, Marco Pavone

entry.summary: This paper distinguishes functional reasoning (which improves task performance) from faithful reasoning (which reflects the policy's true decision process) in Vision-Language-Action models, arguing current alignment is insufficient for faithfulness. It provides a framework and evidence that verbalized embodied chain-of-thought can mask causal links in action prediction.

entry.title: EdgeBench: Unveiling Scaling Laws of Learning from Real-World Environments

author.name: Deyao Zhu, Xin Zhou, Shengling Qin, Xuekai Zhu, Hangliang Ding, Shu Zhong, Zixin Wen, Zhonglin Xie, Chenhui Gou, Linxuan Ren, Yueyang Wang, Junfeng Zhong, Rui Liu, Tian Gao, Yangguang Lin, Jingyuan Zhang, Maojia Song, Xuan Qi, Jinhong Wu, Chenyang Zhang, Yinzhu Piao, Ziru Niu, Hongbin Lin, Lingxiang Meng, Peng Tang, Chengyao Tang, Shanyu Wu, Huanyu Zheng, Yu Liu, Liya Zhu, He Wang, Ming Ding, Ziyu Wan, Hao Liu, Sibo Wang, Haotian Zhu, Xintian Zhang, Nan Chai, Yipeng Liu, Panhao Lai, Sihang Yuan, Zixin Su, Ge Zhang, Wangchunshu Zhou, Yantao Du, Wenhao Huang, Guang Shi

entry.summary: EdgeBench analyzes roughly 38,000 hours of agent-environment interaction across 134 ultra-long-horizon real-world tasks and finds that post-deployment environment learning follows a log-sigmoid scaling law with R-squared of 0.998, with agent learning speed doubling roughly every three months. This provides early quantitative evidence for scaling laws governing learning from real-world environments rather than just pretraining.

entry.title: When Agents Lie: Premeditation, Persistence, and Exploitation in Repeated Games

author.name: Jerick Shi, Terry Jingcheng Zhang, Bernhard Sch\"olkopf, Vincent Conitzer, Zhijing Jin

entry.summary: Places LLM agents in repeated n-player games with a three-stage protocol separating private intent, public announcement, and final action to detect whether deviations from stated commitments were premeditated. It finds deviations are predominantly already planned during private deliberation, exceeding 90 percent in some settings.

entry.title: iFLYTEK-Embodied-Omni Technical Report

author.name: Yuan Zhang, Jingfei Ni, Guanchen Lu, Shiqi Zhang, Qingshan Xu, Chi Liu, Xin Nie, Wenjie Xu, Lin Gao, Zhiyuan Cheng, Mingxin Zhou, Jiajia Wu, Diyuan Liu, Jia Pan, Chao Ji

entry.summary: iFLYTEK presents a unified omni foundation model that jointly handles video/image understanding, video-based world modeling, and action generation for embodied agents within a single shared framework rather than cascaded pipelines. It targets the compounding-error problem in separate perception-then-action systems, which matters for building general-purpose robotic controllers.

entry.title: Reward-Gated On-Policy Distillation

author.name: Mohammad Sadegh Akhondzadeh, Vijay Lingam, Atula Tejaswi, Chanakya Ekbote, Sujay Sanghavi, Aleksandar Bojchevski

entry.summary: RG-OPD adds a verifier-based gate to on-policy distillation so that a student model only follows teacher logits when a reward signal confirms they are trustworthy, avoiding the reinforcement of plausible-but-wrong teacher preferences. This targets a concrete failure mode of dense token-level distillation for reasoning transfer.

entry.title: Geometry-Aware Motion Latents for Learning Robust Manipulation Policies

author.name: Yunchao Zhang, Yijia Weng, Ruizhe Liu, Ming Hu, Leonidas Guibas, Yanchao Yang

entry.summary: GeoMoLa learns discrete motion latent codes for robotic manipulation by predicting how point clouds evolve over time rather than reconstructing visual observations, forcing latents to encode physical 3D motion. It achieves state-of-the-art with only single-view RGB-D input where prior methods need multi-view reconstruction.

entry.title: The Map Behind the Flow: Finite-Step Gradient Descent as a Dynamical System

author.name: Thomas Hofmann

entry.summary: This paper analyzes fixed-step gradient descent as a discrete dynamical system in a hierarchy of exactly-solvable deep-learning-like models, capturing phenomena (edge-of-stability, sharpness oscillations, catapult phases, balancing) that the small-step gradient-flow limit misses. It derives the training map from quartic losses and cubic gradient maps.

entry.title: Your Agent's Memories Are Not Its Own: Forged Reasoning Attacks on LLM Agent Memory and Defenses

author.name: Neeraj Karamchandani, Piyush Nagasubramaniam, Sencun Zhu, Dinghao Wu

entry.summary: FARMA is a novel attack that poisons an LLM agent's remembered reasoning traces (rather than factual knowledge) using evasive language to bypass keyword defenses and self-referential reinforcement to defeat consensus defenses. The paper also introduces SENTINEL, a layered defense pipeline against this reasoning-memory poisoning.

entry.title: When Claws Remember but Do Not Tell: Stealthy Memory Injection in Persistent Personal Agents

author.name: Yechao Zhang, Shiqian Zhao, Jiawen Zhang, Jie Zhang, Gelei Deng, Xiaogeng Liu, Chaowei Xiao, Tianwei Zhang

entry.summary: This paper studies stealth memory injection in persistent personal agents, where a remote black-box adversary uses a single email payload to induce the agent to write poisoned memory, stay hidden in its response, and affect future behavior. It introduces WhisperBench, a 108-case benchmark over five risk categories built on a real IMAP/SMTP workflow.

entry.title: Weak-to-Strong Generalization via Direct On-Policy Distillation

author.name: Shiyuan Feng, Huan-ang Gao, Haohan Chi, Hanlin Wu, Zhilong Zhang, Zheng Jiang, Bingxiang He, Wei-Ying Ma, Ya-Qin Zhang, Hao Zhou

entry.summary: Direct On-Policy Distillation (Direct-OPD) enables weak-to-strong generalization by running expensive RLVR on a cheaper small model, then transferring the teacher's RL-induced policy shift (rather than its full final policy) to improve a stronger target model without repeating costly rollouts. It separates useful RL gains from the small teacher's limitations.

entry.title: ACE-Brain-0.5: A Unified Embodied Foundational Model for Physical Agentic AI

author.name: Brain Team, Ziyang Gong, Haoming Gu, Zehang Luo, Tianyi Zhang, Tao Tao, Yixiao Chi, Zhe Liu, Lingsi Zhu, Jingyuan Liu, Anke Tang, Songze Li, Yilun Kong, Ningjing Liu, Tianyu Zhu, Yunpeng Qing, Shuang Luo, Xiang Liu, Shi Fu, Dawei Nie, Sixiang Liu, Zhexi Wen, Feng Pan, Xiaofeng Wang, Zhi Hou, Chunxiao Liu, Xue Yang, Junchi Yan, Hengshuang Zhao, Dacheng Tao, Xiaogang Wang

entry.summary: ACE-Brain-0.5 is a unified embodied foundation model organizing robot intelligence into spatial perception, decision making, embodied interaction, self-monitoring, and self-improvement in one coupled system rather than fragmented modules. It aims toward general physical agentic AI that plans, acts, monitors, and improves.

entry.title: InternVLA-A1.5: Unifying Understanding, Latent Foresight, and Action for Compositional Generalization

author.name: Haoxiang Ma, Junhao Cai, Xiaoxu Xu, Hao Li, Yuyin Yang, Yang Tian, Jiafei Cao, Hongrui Zhu, Zherui Qiu, Zhaxizhuoma, Yuqiang Yang, Jiaqi Peng, Xueyuan Wei, Yangkun Zhu, Jiahao Jiang, Xing Gao, Hanqing Wang, Feng Yuan, Kailin Li, Xueyue Zhu, Tai Wang, Yan Ding, Jiangmiao Pang, Jia Zeng, Jingjing Zhang, Bowen Zhou, Yao Mu, Chunhua Shen, Weinan Zhang

entry.summary: InternVLA-A1.5 unifies understanding, latent foresight, and action by keeping a native VLM backbone training on VQA and subtask prediction while attaching a lightweight action expert, and recasts future prediction as querying learnable foresight tokens to exploit pretrained video-generator dynamics. It addresses semantic erosion and objective interference in unified manipulation models.

entry.title: Full-Stack FP4: Stable LLM Pretraining with Quantized Projections, Optimizers, and Attention

author.name: Siyu Ding, Mingchuan Ma, Jiabo Tong, Xingrun Xing, Ziming Wang, Guoqi Li

entry.summary: Full-Stack FP4 is described as the first complete NVFP4 pretraining framework that stabilizes 4-bit training across linear layers, optimizer states and arithmetic, and attention, using module-wise precision strategies to address distinct numerical failure patterns in each. It closes the gap left by prior methods that only quantized transformer linear layers.

entry.title: Transplanting, inverting, and preventing a misalignment persona: method-conditional emergent misalignment in Qwen2.5

author.name: Lyndon Drake (University of Oxford), Zandi Eberstadt (University of Oxford)

entry.summary: This Oxford study shows emergent misalignment in Qwen2.5 is mediated by a causal latent persona direction that can be transplanted into a model sharing only pretraining to induce broad misalignment, and ablated to roughly halve an overt inducer's effect. It also finds whether fine-tuning recruits this persona depends on method and capacity, with low-rank PEFT being the cheaper risky regime.

entry.title: Wasserstein Residuals: Learning Gradient Flows from Population Dynamics

author.name: Markus Heinonen, Yair Shenfeld, Ricardo Baptista, Daniel Waxman, Dmitry Batenkov, Tim Cooijmans, Eli Bingham

entry.summary: This paper introduces a residual approach to learning Wasserstein gradient flows from population dynamics, enforcing continuity equations via a non-negative loss whose minimum is the WGF, avoiding costly optimal-transport JKO steps and time-discretization inflexibility. It unifies several existing methods under one global objective.

entry.title: Trust Region Policy Distillation

author.name: Zhengpeng Xie, Li Lyna Zhang, Zeke Xie, Mao Yang

entry.summary: Trust Region Policy Distillation (TOP-D) stabilizes high-variance on-policy distillation by dynamically constructing a proximal teacher, with theoretical guarantees of gradient variance control, global convergence, and monotonic improvement. It improves training stability, sample efficiency, and reasoning performance.

entry.title: Multi-Turn On-Policy Distillation with Prefix Replay

author.name: Baohao Liao, Hanze Dong, Christof Monz, Xinxing Xu, Li Dong, Furu Wei

entry.summary: ReOPD (Replayed-Prefix On-Policy Distillation) reduces the cost of on-policy distillation for multi-turn agent tasks by reusing pre-collected teacher trajectories as replayed prefixes, letting the student act at selected steps while the teacher supervises densely without new environment interactions. It identifies and mitigates a prefix trap in multi-turn OPD.

entry.title: GaP: A Graph-as-Policy Multi-Agent Self-Learning Harness For Variational Automation Tasks

author.name: Kaiyuan Chen, Shuangyu Xie, Letian Fu, Justin Yu, William Pacini, Sandeep Bajamahal, Hudson Kim, Jaimyn Drake, Daehwa Kim, Haoru Xue, Jonathan Francis, Christian Juette, Peter Schaldenbrand, Muhammet Yunus Seker, Ruwan Wickramarachchi, Uksang Yoo, Guanzhi Wang, Adithyavairavan Murali, Balakumar Sundaralingam, S. Shankar Sastry, Spencer Huang, Yuke Zhu, Linxi "Jim" Fan, Ken Goldberg

entry.summary: GaP (Graph-as-Policy) is a multi-agent coding harness that generates directed computation graphs with perception and planning nodes for Variational Automation tasks, which have larger object geometry/pose variation than fixed automation but require reliable, persistent execution. It aims to combine interpretable robot programming with the open-world adaptability of model-free policies.

entry.title: On the effectiveness of reward functions in reinforcement learning for confidence calibration of large language models

author.name: Chee Heng Tan, Zhuoyi Lin, Mehul Motani, Wee Sun Lee

entry.summary: This paper studies RL reward design for jointly improving LLM reasoning accuracy and calibrated verbalized confidence, identifying confidence reward hacking where models are incentivized to answer wrongly to be confidently wrong. It defines non-hackable confidence reward schemes and shows selective composition works. It matters for trustworthy, calibrated LLMs.

entry.title: Learning Task-Sufficient World Models by Synergizing Agentic Exploration and Structured Modeling

author.name: Fan Feng, Yujia Zheng, Minghao Fu, Yongqiang Chen, Guangyi Chen, Kevin Murphy, Biwei Huang, Kun Zhang

entry.summary: This paper develops world models with task-sufficient, minimal representations by coupling active agentic exploration with structured world-model learning in a closed loop, distilling only control-relevant factors. It aims to improve efficiency and cross-task generalization over generic latent embeddings. It matters for sample-efficient model-based RL, with strong causal-representation-learning authors.

entry.title: Benign Overfitting Does Not Occur in Diffusion Models

author.name: Tyler Farghly, Benjamin Dupuis, Alain Durmus, Umut Simsekli

entry.summary: The paper proves that benign overfitting and double descent do not occur in diffusion models: unless sample size grows exponentially with dimension, overfitting and good generalization cannot coexist, giving a classical U-shaped population loss. This challenges assumptions carried over from standard deep learning.

entry.title: Flex-Forcing: Towards a Unified Autoregressive and Bidirectional Video Diffusion Model

author.name: Xinyin Ma, Julius Berner, Chao Liu, Arash Vahdat, Weili Nie, Xinchao Wang

entry.summary: Flex-Forcing unifies autoregressive and bidirectional video diffusion in one model via a flexible chunking mechanism over the temporal axis and denoising steps, letting the same model trade off global coherence against efficient streaming generation. It aims to combine bidirectional fidelity with autoregressive speed while avoiding exposure bias.

entry.title: Flash-BoN: Instant Drafts for Inference-Time Scaling in Diffusion Models

author.name: Ruchit Rawal, Reza Shirkavand, Sayak Paul, Yuxin Wen, Heng Huang, Yizheng Chen, Tom Goldstein, Gowthami Somepalli

entry.summary: Flash-BoN reexamines inference-time scaling for text-to-image diffusion, showing that under wall-clock evaluation simple Best-of-N sampling matches or beats guided search methods once verifier overhead is counted. It argues compute is better spent on broader exploration than repeated intermediate verification.

entry.title: HunyuanOCR-1.5: Making Lightweight OCR VLMs Faster and Better

author.name: Gengluo Li, Xingyu Wan, Shangpin Peng, Weinong Wang, Hao Feng, Yongkun Du, Binghong Wu, Zheng Ruan, Zhiqiong Lu, Liang Wu, Pengyuan Lyu, Huawen Shen, Zibin Lin, Shijing Hu, Jieneng Yang, Hongbing Wen, Guanghua Yu, Hong Liu, Bochao Wang, Can Ma, Han Hu, Chengquan Zhang, Yu Zhou

entry.summary: HunyuanOCR-1.5 is a lightweight end-to-end OCR-specialized vision-language model unifying document parsing, text spotting, information extraction, translation, and multi-image understanding, with DFlash decoding giving a 6.37x Transformer and 2.14x vLLM speedup. It builds on the HunyuanOCR-1.0 backbone to improve both efficiency and capability.

entry.title: Look Before You Leap: Distilling Tree Search into Action Evaluation for Frozen VLA Models

author.name: Xinyi Xie, Zican Hu, Zhanyu Liu, Yicheng Dong, Wenhao Wu, Zhenhong Sun, Haoran Li, Chunlin Chen, Zhi Wang, Pichao Wang

entry.summary: SVA distills tree search into an action-evaluation module so frozen VLA models can select better actions from their existing output distribution, motivated by a pass@k finding that success rises from 33% at pass@1 to 92% at pass@32. It targets action evaluation, not just generation, as a key failure source.

entry.title: SwarmResearch: Orchestrating Coding Agents for Open-Ended Discovery

author.name: Yuvraj Virk, Zack Edds, Chunqiu Steven Xia, Lingming Zhang

entry.summary: SwarmResearch is an orchestrator-subagent harness where a Shepherd Agent steers a population of Search Agents each operating on its own git branch, addressing the tendency of long-running coding agents to lock onto a single approach and miss superior ones. On open-ended optimization tasks it finds better or comparable solutions than single-agent baselines.

entry.title: Formal Disco: Scalable Open-Ended Generation of Formally Verified Programs

author.name: Gabriel Poesia, Simon Henniger, Tzu-Han Hsu, Yilun Du, Nada Amin

entry.summary: Formal Disco is a distributed system coordinating LLM-based workers (initiators, and other classes) to generate formally verified programs at scale via open-ended synthetic data generation, tackling the scarcity of human-written verification-aware code. It shares tasks and programs across workers reading real repositories and documentation.

entry.title: Hierarchical Sparse Attention Done Right: Toward Infinite Context Modeling

author.name: Xiang Hu, Xinyu Wei, Hao Gu, Minshen Zhang, Tian Liang, Huayang Li, Lei Zhu, Yan Wang, Sirui Han, Yushi Bai, Kewei Tu, Haitao Mi, Leo Liang

entry.summary: HiLS is a chunk-wise sparse attention mechanism that learns chunk selection end-to-end under the language-modeling loss and hierarchically fuses per-chunk outputs by retrieval scores, aiming to close the gap with full attention for long/infinite context. It addresses the inaccurate chunk selection of prior sparse-attention methods.

entry.title: Separating Representation from Reconstruction Enables Scalable Text Encoders

author.name: Megi Dervishi, Mathurin Videau, Yann LeCun

entry.summary: CrossBERT is a two-part encoder architecture that separates high-quality representation learning from token-reconstruction grounding, addressing BERT's flat design where representations become unexploitable by frozen probes despite improved perplexity. It enables high masking ratios and gradient collection over all tokens for scalable text encoders.

entry.title: RoboDojo: A Unified Sim-and-Real Benchmark for Comprehensive Evaluation of Generalist Robot Manipulation Policies

author.name: Tianxing Chen, Yue Chen, Zixuan Li, Junyuan Tang, Kailun Su, Weijie Wan, Baijun Chen, Haoran Lu, Haowen Yan, Honghao Su, Zhiyang Dou, Kaixuan Wang, Dandan Zhang, Yunze Liu, Yan Qin, Qiwei Liang, Qiwei Wu, Zijian Lin, Wenwei Lin, Yuran Wang, Minghua He, Tianshu Wu, Ruihai Wu, Jingquan Zhou, Kai-Chong Lei, Haibao Yu, Yuanfeng Ji, Weiyang Jin, Guanyu Lin, Xiaofan Li, Qi Xiong, Renjing Xu, Zhongyu Li, Wenhao Chai, Enze Xie, Ziwei Wang, Yao Mu, Hao Dong, Wojciech Matusik, Mingyu Ding, Wenbo Ding, Ping Luo, Masayoshi Tomizuka

entry.summary: RoboDojo is a unified sim-and-real benchmark for generalist robot manipulation policies with 42 simulation and 18 real-world tasks covering diverse manipulation capabilities, combining scalable simulation feedback with real deployment realism. It addresses fragmented, narrow, and single-modality prior benchmarks.

entry.title: SILO: Simulation-in-the-Loop Sim-to-Real Transfer for Multi-Stage Cable Routing

author.name: Stone Tao, Jie Xu, Hesam Rabeti, Yashraj Narang, Yijie Guo, Iretiayo Akinola

entry.summary: SILO presents a sim-to-real reinforcement learning framework for multi-stage cable routing using GPU-parallelized simulation to model deformable objects, enabling policies that generalize across cable geometries. It addresses the scalability problem of imitation learning that typically needs thousands of task-specific demonstrations.

entry.title: Hyperparameter Transfer in Graph Neural Networks

author.name: Gage DeZoort, Boris Hanin

entry.summary: This paper develops and validates hyperparameter transfer parameterizations for graph neural networks trained with SGD, Adam, and AdamW, extending muP-style transfer principles (well-studied for dense networks) to the under-explored GNN setting. It enables tuning small GNNs to set near-optimal hyperparameters for larger ones.

entry.title: Privacy-Preserving Robustness Verification for Neural Networks

author.name: Nianyun Song, Xiaokun Luan, Yu Guo, Rongfang Bie, Meng Sun, Xiyue Zhang

entry.summary: SecureCROWN is presented as the first framework for privacy-preserving neural network robustness verification, using secure two-party computation so a model owner and data owner can jointly compute certified robustness bounds while revealing only the final result under a semi-honest security model. It resolves the tension between verification's need for full access and privacy constraints.

entry.title: TREK: Distill to Explore, Reinforce to Refine

author.name: Yuanda Xu, Zhengze Zhou, Kayhan Behdin, Jelena Markovic-Voronov, Hejian Sang, Xiaomin Li, Wenhui Zhu, Xinchen Du, Aida Rahmattalabi, Ran He, Sen Na, Zhipeng Wang, Alborz Geramifard

entry.summary: TREK (Teacher-Routed Exploration via Forward KL) uses distillation not for imitation but to expand a student's exploration support on hard prompts where GRPO stalls because correct solution modes lie outside on-policy support. It works with black-box, white-box, or self-teachers and identifies which hard samples are most worth consolidating.

entry.title: Asymptotic-Preserving A Posteriori Analysis of Diffusion and Flow-Matching Samplers

author.name: Shiheng Zhang

entry.summary: This work treats the terminal noise floor of diffusion and flow-matching samplers as a singular-perturbation parameter and identifies which fixed-step integrators remain stable and accurate as that floor shrinks. It provides a posteriori residual diagnostics computable on a pretrained model without ground-truth scores. It matters for principled selection and auditing of ODE samplers.

entry.title: Geometry of Ordinal Representations in Language Models

author.name: Saksham Bassi, Sharvi Tomar

entry.summary: This interpretability study tests whether the finding that language models encode ordinal quantities on curved 1D manifolds generalizes across four ordinal tasks in Gemma and Qwen models. It finds place-cell-like tiling emerges for locally computable variables while integration-heavy tasks yield higher-dimensional representations, with architecture-dependent geometric computation. It matters for mechanistic understanding of numeric reasoning in LLMs.

entry.title: KVpop -- Key-Value Cache Compression with Predictive Online Pruning

author.name: Lukas Hauzenberger, Niklas Schmidinger, Anamaria-Roberta Hartl, David Stap, Thomas Schmied, Sebastian B\"ock, G\"unter Klambauer, Sepp Hochreiter

entry.summary: KVpop learns a fixed-budget key-value cache eviction policy by directly supervising keep-or-drop decisions against a novel future-attention target computed without dense attention maps, and adds a delayed memory-based scorer that briefly defers decisions to exploit near-future context. It matters for reducing the KV-cache memory bottleneck in long-context decoding, with prominent authors including Hochreiter.

entry.title: Geometric Causal Models

author.name: Eli N. Weinstein, David M. Blei

entry.summary: Geometric Causal Models provide a framework for causal inference from non-i.i.d. dependent data (spatial, network, molecular) by exploiting symmetries formalized through group theory. The authors use ergodic theory for identification and combine geometric deep learning with scalable Bayesian inference for estimation.

entry.title: Don't Commit Alone: Joint Token Commitment in Diffusion Large Language Models

author.name: Lin Yao

entry.summary: CoCommit addresses factorization error in diffusion LLMs, where multiple tokens committed per step are decoded independently despite dependencies; it adds a marker-gated coordination pass reusing the last-n layers to approximate joint decoding before committing. It improves parallel decoding quality with minimal overhead.

entry.title: RABBiT: Rapidly adaptive BOLD foundation model via brain-tuning for accurate zero-shot and few-shot prediction of speech-elicited responses in the brain

author.name: Omer Moussa, Mariya Toneva

entry.summary: RABBiT is a compact audio-to-fMRI foundation model that uses brain-tuning to achieve accurate zero- and few-shot prediction of speech-elicited brain responses across 324 participants and multiple unseen datasets. It captures shared neural structure while adapting efficiently to new participants and inputs.

entry.title: Reward Lightning: Fast Video Generation via Homologous Preference Distillation

author.name: Jiaxiang Cheng, Bing Ma, Xuhua Ren, Kai Yu, Peng Zhang, Tianxiang Zheng, Qinglin Lu

entry.summary: Reward Lightning jointly performs preference alignment and distillation acceleration for video diffusion models within a single shared latent representation, using a latent reward model to score videos directly in latent space. The homology principle mitigates gradient conflicts between the two objectives.

entry.title: Paired Uterine Whole-Slide Images and Pathology Reports for Multimodal Computational Pathology

author.name: Han Li, Jingsong Liu, Ayako Ura, Junlin Hou, Zhengyang Xu, Azar Kazemi, Oskar Thaeter, Christian Grashei, Fabian G\"ulhan, Reza Nasirigerdeh, Xun Ma, Rui Yan, Hao Chen, S. Kevin Zhou, Nassir Navab, Carolin Mogler, Peter Sch\"uffler

entry.summary: This work introduces a dataset pairing uterine whole-slide images with clinically meaningful pathology reports for multimodal computational pathology. It targets the scarcity of WSI-report pairs that limits report generation and AI-assisted diagnosis.

entry.title: Feeling the Unexpected: ResTacVLA for Contact-Rich Manipulation via Residual Tactile Representation

author.name: Pengwei Zhang, Bin Xie, Ce Hao, Xinpan Meng, Xinyu Guo, Fang Deng, Long Cheng, Tiancai Wang

entry.summary: ResTacVLA reformulates tactile input as a residual representation capturing the discrepancy between visual priors and actual physical sensation, inspired by predictive coding, to prevent visual features from overwhelming sparse tactile cues in VLA models. It offers a principled fix for modality collapse in contact-rich manipulation.

entry.title: Folding, Reasoning, and Scaling with Open-source Drug Discovery Engine

author.name: Aureka AI OpenDDE project

entry.summary: OpenDDE is an open-source all-atom biomolecular foundation model that uses co-folding as the entry point to a scalable drug-discovery engine, positioning structure prediction as a shared reasoning layer for design, affinity estimation, and structure-conditioned optimization. It integrates all-atom architecture and atomic latent reasoning.

entry.title: Revealing Hidden Model Behaviors with Task-Specific Self-Reports

author.name: Taras Kutsyk, Bartosz Zieli\'nski

entry.summary: The Stabilized Adapter for self-Report (SAR) is a lightweight LoRA adapter that makes a fine-tuned model describe its own hidden implanted behavior in plain language using only the model and its training data. It detects all seven implanted behaviors including broad emergent misalignment, outperforming introspection-adapter baselines.

entry.title: Turning Off-Policy Tokens On-Policy: A Plug-in Approach for Improving LLM Alignment

author.name: Yu Li, Xiuyu Li, Mingyang Yi, Jiaxing Wang, zhangliangxu, Zhaolong Xing, Zhen Chen

entry.summary: Selective Importance Sampling (SIS) improves LLM RL post-training by treating off-policy tokens as a proposal distribution and applying a token-level rejection test, converting accepted tokens to on-policy and avoiding high-variance importance-sampling ratios that compound over long sequences. It is a plug-in for improving alignment stability.

entry.title: Cortex: A Bidirectionally Aligned Embodied Agent Framework for Long-horizon Manipulation

author.name: Jiaqi Peng, Xiqian Yu, Delin Feng, Yuqiang Yang, Wenzhe Cai, Jing Xiong, Ganlin Yang, Jinliang Zheng, Jiafei Cao, Xueyuan Wei, Jiangmiao Pang, Yuan Shen, Tai Wang

entry.summary: Cortex is a bidirectionally aligned embodied agent framework that closes the gap between high-level VLM planning semantics and low-level VLA execution kinematics by standardizing manipulation into 32 canonical skill primitives and injecting tractability principles like object attributes and trajectory reachability. It targets long-horizon manipulation where Markovian VLA models struggle.

entry.title: RL Forgets! Towards Continual Policy Optimization

author.name: Mao-Lin Luo, Zhe-Xu Wang, Zi-Hao Zhou, Bo Ye, Jian Zhao, Min-Ling Zhang, Tong Wei

entry.summary: This paper challenges the belief that RL is inherently less prone to forgetting than supervised fine-tuning during continual post-training of vision-language models, introducing the MRCL benchmark and showing severe catastrophic forgetting can still occur under RL. It proposes a continual policy optimization approach to mitigate it. It matters for continual adaptation of multimodal models.

entry.title: Compressed Computation under $L^4$ Loss is likely Computation in Superposition

author.name: Francisco Ferreira da Silva, Stefan Heimersheim

entry.summary: This paper studies compressed computation as a trained toy model of computation in superposition, arguing that under L4 loss such a single-hidden-layer ReLU network likely performs genuine computation in superposition rather than mere representation.
