It matters for mechanistic interpretability and understanding how networks compute more functions than neurons.

entry.title: Vidu S1: A Real-Time Interactive Video Generation Model

author.name: Jintao Zhang, Kai Jiang, Jintao Chen, Xu Wang, Yang Luo, Yuji Wang, Dechuang Chen, Jungang Li, Chengyang Ye, Marco Chen, Hongzhou Zhu, Min Zhao, Yuxuan Jiang, Zhengkun Huang, Chendong Xiang, Kaiwen Zheng, Haoxu Wang, Xiaohang Wang, Qi Jia, Xin Chen, Yimin Chen, Youhe Jiang, Fangcheng Fu, Zhijie Deng, Fan Bao, Jianfei Chen, Jun Zhu

entry.summary: Vidu S1 is a real-time interactive video generation model supporting voice control of digital characters, producing infinite-length 540p video at up to 42 FPS on consumer GPUs via TurboDiffusion and TurboServe. Users can upload custom images and select voice tones.

entry.title: Awakening Diffusion Transformers: Eliciting Stronger Generation and Understanding via Massive Activation Modulation

author.name: Chaofan Gan, Zicheng Zhao, Yuanpeng Tu, Xi Chen, Ziran Qin, Tieyuan Chen, Supavadee Aramvith, Mehrtash Harandi, Weiyao Lin

entry.summary: This work systematically analyzes massive activations in Diffusion Transformers, finding they concentrate in fixed feature dimensions aligned with AdaLN residual scaling and are driven by denoising timestep rather than text. It then modulates these activations to boost both generation detail and downstream understanding.

entry.title: Present but Not Remembered: Auditing How Frozen VLAs Encode, Deploy, and Steer Visual History

author.name: Chih-Ting Liao, Xin Cao

entry.summary: Audits how frozen vision-language-action models encode and use visual history via layer-resolved linear probing and causal interchange interventions across three VLAs, finding history is largely a redundant copy of the present and only causally used when the current frame is degraded. It reframes memory questions from adding memory to understanding existing representations.

entry.title: Video Generation Models Are Inherent Lighting Estimators

author.name: Ziqi Cai, Shuchen Weng, Kaiqi Liu, Zifeng Wang, Zhiquan Zhang, Minggui Teng, Han Jiang, Boxin Shi

entry.summary: V-LITE reframes lighting estimation as guided video inpainting, inserting a synthetic chrome ball to compel video generation models to produce physically plausible reflections that reveal dynamic environment maps. It unlocks the implicit lighting knowledge inside video generators and bridges LDR to HDR.

entry.title: LangLoc: "Tell Me What You See"

author.name: Shaurya Kishore Panwar, Roham Zendehdel Nobari, Shirley Feng Yi Lau, Abu Bakr Rahman Shaik, Manuel G\"unther, Marc Pollefeys, Daniel Barath

entry.summary: LangLoc estimates a person's 2D position and heading inside a known 3D environment purely from a free-form natural language description of surroundings, closing the gap between coarse scene retrieval and precise pose. It reports strong gains in scene recall and sub-meter median localization error, offering a privacy-preserving, camera-free localization paradigm.

entry.title: Internal Pluralism and the Limits of Pairwise Comparisons

author.name: Bailey Flanigan, Michelle Si

entry.summary: This theory paper formalizes how local pairwise comparisons, a standard tool for preference elicitation and alignment, break down under internal pluralism, where a single person judges decision rules by multiple competing priorities. It identifies formal failure modes when forcing decisive local comparisons, which matters for participatory design and RLHF preference collection.

entry.title: Oyster-II: Reinforcement Learning for Constructive Safety Alignment in Large Language Models

author.name: Jiyang Guan, Yong Xie, Jun Chen, Jiexi Liu, Zipeng Ye, Defeng Li, Jiayu Shen, Jialing Tao, Hui Xue

entry.summary: Oyster-II extends constructive safety alignment (moving beyond blanket refusals toward helpful responses to sensitive queries) using reinforcement learning to fix the poor generalization of the earlier SFT-based Oyster-I. It aims to jointly optimize safety, helpfulness, and trustworthiness.

entry.title: AgentGym2: Benchmarking Large Language Model Agents in De-Idealized Real-World Environments

author.name: Zhiheng Xi, Dingwen Yang, Jiaqi Liu, Jixuan Huang, Honglin Guo, Baodai Huang, Tinggang Chen, Qi Zhang, Zhonghang Lu, Chenyu Liu, Jiajun Sun, Jiazheng Zhang, Dingwei Zhu, Xin Guo, Junzhe Wang, Zhihao Zhang, Yuming Yang, Junjie Ye, Minghe Gao, Dongrui Liu, Jiaming Ji, Guohao Li, Tao Gui, Qi Zhang, Xuanjing Huang

entry.summary: AgentGym2 is an evaluation framework that places LLM agents in de-idealized, end-to-end real-world tasks with noisy inputs, missing steps, and tools that must be discovered through exploration. It aims to close the gap between idealized benchmarks and production deployment difficulty.

entry.title: !Imperio, smolVLA: The Implications of Data Poisoning on Open Source Robotics

author.name: Stefan B\"uhler, Mark Schutera

entry.summary: This paper shows trigger-word data poisoning is practical against open-source vision-language-action robot models, with just three poisoned episodes among 320 clean ones causing complete denial of service on a real pick-and-place task. It highlights security risks in the trust assumptions of open robotics ecosystems.

entry.title: Retroactive Chain-of-Thought (RetroCoT): Forensic Reconstruction Prompts as a Safety Diagnostic Across Model Generations

author.name: Samira Hajizadeh

entry.summary: RetroCoT introduces a single-turn jailbreak that reframes harmful requests as forensic reconstruction tasks (presupposing the harm already occurred), revealing that LLM safety alignment is sensitive to pragmatic framing rather than semantic content. It is used as a diagnostic to probe alignment robustness across model generations.

entry.title: URSA: Chemistry-Aware Benchmark for Utilitarian Retrosynthesis Assessment

author.name: Bogdan Zagribelnyy, Ivan Ilin, Nikita Bondarev, Anton Morgunov, Arkadii Lin, Maksim Kuznetsov, Rim Shayakhmetov, Vladimir Aladinskiy, Alex Aliper, Alex Zhavoronkov

entry.summary: URSA is a chemistry-aware benchmark for retrosynthesis that evaluates synthetic routes not only formally (convergence to purchasable materials) but also for chemical plausibility, mimicking expert judgment. It enables objective comparison between specialized deep-learning retrosynthesis systems and general-purpose LLMs.

entry.title: RustMizan: A Compilable, Contamination-Aware Benchmarking Framework for Rust Vulnerabilities

author.name: Tarek Elsayed, Shiping Yang, Eunsong Koh, Sanika Goyal, Vincent Huang, Paul Ngo, Nathan Young, Mohammad Omidvar Tehrani, Alvyn Kang, Arnell Kang, Zeyu Chen, Ang\'elica Moreira, Xuan Feng, Angel X. Chang, Nick Sumner, Steven Y. Ko

entry.summary: RustMizan is a compilable, contamination-aware benchmark for Rust vulnerability analysis with crate/file/function-level code, annotations for detection, CWE classification, and localization, plus a semantics-preserving mutation framework for robustness and contamination testing. It addresses gaps in existing snippet-based, binary-classification vulnerability benchmarks.

entry.title: Multi-Robot Open Adaptive Teaming Across Unseen Environments, Partners, and Scales

author.name: Yang Li, Feng Xue, Fan Mo, Yunhao Liu, Jianhong Wang, Ying Wen, Qingrui Zhang, Shaoshuai Mou, Wei Pan

entry.summary: This paper formalizes open adaptive multi-robot teaming (simultaneous adaptation to unseen environments, unknown partners, and varying team sizes) as a hypergraphic-form game capturing team-level cooperative relationships beyond pairwise interactions. It provides a game-theoretic foundation for coordination structure inference when team composition changes within episodes.

entry.title: Open Problems in AI Incident Governance

author.name: Harleen Kaur Sidhu, Rebecca Scholefield, Nour Annan, Kevin Hernandez, Isabel Nieh Hou, Abdulrahman Alshaikhi, Ze Shen Chin, Rokas Gipi\v{s}kis

entry.summary: This paper examines existing AI incident governance frameworks from regulators and independent efforts, finding a lack of consistency across definitions, classification, monitoring, and reporting of post-deployment AI failures. It maps open problems needed for adequate incident governance.

entry.title: Steering Optimisation Trajectories in Diffusion Representation Learning

author.name: Rajat Rasal, Avinash Kori, Tian Xia, Ben Glocker

entry.summary: This work studies why diffusion autoencoders reach similar image quality with different latent structures, tracing it to optimization dynamics that organize into reconstruction-first vs gradual-disentanglement regimes early in training. It proposes steering the reconstruction-disentanglement trade-off by targeting shortcut pathways in the diffusion U-Net and controlling early noise-level exposure.

entry.title: Selective Disclosure Watermarking for Large Language Models

author.name: Xuyang Chen, Xiang Li, Yangxinyu Xie, Qi Long

entry.summary: HeRo (Hierarchical Vocabulary Routing) is a multi-bit LLM watermarking framework that enables selective disclosure of embedded metadata, so verifying part of a watermark no longer requires revealing the entire message. It recursively partitions the vocabulary to distribute watermark information hierarchically, reducing unnecessary information exposure and privacy concerns.

entry.title: The Multiscale Single-Index Model: A Stylized Model for Hierarchical Feature Learning

author.name: Joan Bruna

entry.summary: Joan Bruna analyzes the Multiscale Single-Index Model as a stylized setting for hierarchical feature learning with scale separation, showing the first Wiener chaos of the target behaves like a perturbed spiked tensor and casting the model as a nonlinear analogue of Tensor PCA. It offers tractable insight into how deep architectures learn multiscale representations.

entry.title: MDL Meets Latent Confounders: LNML-based Causal Discovery

author.name: Zhongyi Que, Shin Matsushima, Kenji Yamanishi

entry.summary: This paper proposes an MDL-based causal discovery framework using luckiness normalized maximum likelihood code-length that handles nonlinear mechanisms and latent confounders simultaneously. It introduces a delta-pseudo-collinearity notion and a greedy algorithm to detect confounder-induced dependencies. It matters because most methods assume either linearity or causal sufficiency.

entry.title: Structure-Specific Representational Priors Causally Control the Grokking Delay

author.name: Gunner Levi Howe

entry.summary: This paper causally tests whether the grokking delay measures time to form task-structured representations by injecting supervised-contrastive priors with true, wrong-sibling, or random structure into a small transformer learning modular addition. It matters as a controlled intervention clarifying the mechanism behind delayed generalization.

entry.title: MARLIN: De Novo Molecular Structure Elucidation from Tandem Mass Spectra without a Ground-Truth Formula

author.name: Xujun Che, Xiuxia Du, Depeng Xu

entry.summary: MARLIN performs de novo molecular structure elucidation directly from tandem mass spectra without requiring a ground-truth molecular formula at any stage, using a self-supervised encoder, unlike prior methods that assume a known formula oracle. It matters for identifying novel metabolites and natural products relevant to drug discovery and exposomics.

entry.title: Active Learning on Adversarially Corrupted Graphs

author.name: Marco Bressan, Nicol\`o Cesa-Bianchi, Tommaso d`Orsi, Emmanuel Esposito, Silvio Lattanzi

entry.summary: This paper defines an adversarial graph model where an attacker hides corrupted vertices by adding edges among them and to the host graph, and designs an active-learning algorithm that recovers the corrupted set with query complexity polynomial in the adversary's power. It matters for robust graph learning under structural attacks, with strong theory authors.

entry.title: Train Smarter, Not Longer: Memorization-Guided Data Reuse for Efficient LLM Training

author.name: Jingwei Zuo, Cong Zeng, Ilyas Chahed, Maksim Velikanov, Dhia Eddine Rhaiem, Pasquale Balsebre, Abhay Kumar, Younes Belkada, Hakim Hacid

entry.summary: This paper introduces memorization-guided data reuse for multi-epoch LLM training, using a Memorization Window signal from loss retention and downstream scores to adaptively decide when and how much to repeat high-quality data, balancing sample efficiency against overfitting. It matters for data-efficient pretraining under limited high-quality data.

entry.title: Functional Bilevel Optimization for Predictive Fairness

author.name: Ieva Petrulionyte, Julien Mairal, Michael Arbel

entry.summary: This paper formulates mean demographic parity via DPVar, the variance of the conditional-mean prediction given continuous high-dimensional sensitive attributes, and casts its optimization as a functional bilevel problem solved with an exact closed-form hypergradient (FBO) or iterative differentiation (ITD). It matters for fairness when sensitive attributes are continuous and full independence is too restrictive.

entry.title: Latent Programming Horizons in Coding Agents

author.name: Andr\'e Silva, Han Tu, Martin Monperrus

entry.summary: This paper probes the residual streams of LLMs inside coding agents and finds they linearly encode program properties like whether code parses, passes tests, or introduces regressions, with probes reaching high AUC and, surprisingly, running ahead of the agent's own edits. It matters for interpretability and possible early error detection in coding agents.

entry.title: TabPack: Efficient Hyperparameter Ensembles for Tabular Deep Learning

author.name: Yury Gorishniy, Akim Kotelnikov, Ivan Rubachev, Artem Babenko

entry.summary: TabPack trains many MLPs with sampled hyperparameters in parallel in a single run and selects ensemble members on the fly, reducing dependence on explicit hyperparameter tuning for tabular deep learning. Users specify ranges instead of exact values.

entry.title: AquaGen: Scaling generative models to molecular dynamics precision on thousands of atoms

author.name: Emmanuel Bengio, Sanjeev Raja, Yui Tik Pang, Kerstin Klaeser, Cristian Gabellini, Nikhil Shenoy, Francesco Di Giovanni, Prudencio Tossou

entry.summary: AquaGen is described as the first all-atom, explicit-solvent, periodic-boundary-aware generative model producing Boltzmann-distributed molecular configurations at a fraction of molecular dynamics cost. It enables property prediction like absolute hydration free energy via ensemble averaging of energy evaluations.

entry.title: Uncertainty-Aware Abstention in Large Language Models with Provable Alignment Guarantees

author.name: Sijin Dong, Hiroyuki Shinnou

entry.summary: Introduces CIC, a confidence-interval calibration framework that converts arbitrary LLM uncertainty scores into selective-answering rules with statistical error guarantees on accepted answers. It matters for deploying trustworthy QA systems that can reliably abstain.

entry.title: Fidelity-Diversity Metrics for Text

author.name: Amanda Wang, Tudor Manole, Florentina Bunea, John Thickstun

entry.summary: Develops paired fidelity and diversity metrics for text based on optimal-transport divergences, adapting generative precision-recall ideas to quantify how closely candidate text resembles reference data and how well it covers reference modes. It supports informed data-augmentation and curation decisions.

entry.title: MIRAGE: Defending Long-Form RAG Against Misinformation Pollution

author.name: Saadeldine Eletter, Ruihong Zeng, Yuxia Wang, Maxim Panov, Aleksandr Rubashevskii, Preslav Nakov

entry.summary: MIRAGE is a training-free, model-agnostic defense for long-form RAG that builds an NLI-based cross-document claim graph and uses a Defended-Claims Gate to condition on multi-source-supported claims or fall back to parametric answering. It also releases a minimal-edit pollution protocol across four perturbation families for evaluation.

entry.title: Prior Bias in Vision Language Models on UML Diagram Interpretation

author.name: Zaiyu Cheng, Khai-Nguyen Nguyen, Antonio Mastropaolo

entry.summary: This benchmark tests whether vision-language models actually read UML class diagrams or answer from learned priors by pairing each diagram with a version where only the relation arrow is reversed. Evaluating eight open-source models plus GPT-5.4 variants, it shows accuracy drops sharply when the visual notation conflicts with typical priors, exposing shortcut reliance.

entry.title: InSpace: Structure-Aware 3D Indoor Scene Generation from a Single 360{\deg} Image

author.name: Gwanhyeong Koo, Hyunsu Kim, Youngji Kim, Taejae Lee, Siwoo Lim, Sunjae Yoon, Suyong Yeon, Chang D. Yoo

entry.summary: InSpace generates structure-aware 3D indoor scenes from a single 360-degree equirectangular image, using a three-stage pipeline that estimates partial geometry as spatial priors before generating a coherent global layout. It emphasizes structural layout that anchors asset placement.

entry.title: PreSIST: Vision-Language-Informed Object Persistence Prediction in Open-World Scenes

author.name: Amanda Adkins, Tarunvidyut Ravisankar, Joydeep Biswas

entry.summary: PreSIST proactively predicts how long observed objects will persist in place using vision-language context (e.g., a parked vs. stopped car), enabling long-term robot perception without reactive map updates. It estimates instance survival over arbitrary future times.

entry.title: Aura: Consistent Multi-Subject Video Generation via VLM-Grounded Semantic Alignment

author.name: Zixiang Zhou, Zhentao Yu, Yifeng Ma, Hongmei Wang, Wenqing Yu, Cong Wang, Zilin Yang, Rui Chen, Jiarong Ou, Yezhou Liu, Yuan Zhou, Qinglin Lu

entry.summary: Aura is a unified framework for identity-consistent multi-subject video generation that uses AI director-level dense captions and a VLM with learnable queries to extract multimodal semantic features from text and visual references. It targets identity preservation and complex subject relationships.

entry.title: CCFM: Collision-Constrained Flow Matching for Safety-Critical Scenario Generation

author.name: Ke Li, Kaidi Liang, Yuxin Ding, Debojyoti Biswas, Xianbiao Hu, Ruwen Qin

entry.summary: CCFM (Collision-Constrained Flow Matching) generates safety-critical driving scenarios with precise collision control by enforcing hard physical constraints rather than soft probabilistic guidance. It uses a heuristic collision selector and structured constraints to guarantee specific collision types.

entry.title: TrustCLIP: Learning Private Visual Features via Adversarial Reconstruction

author.name: Nikos Athanasiou, Ilya A. Petrov, Angela Yao, Shugao Ma, Eric Sauser, Edoardo Remelli, Shreyas Hampali, Johannes Sch\"onberger, Fadime Sener, Bugra Tekin

entry.summary: TrustCLIP treats a feature-conditioned generator as an explicit privacy adversary, learning a projection between encoder features and downstream modules optimized to degrade image reconstructions while retaining task utility. It mitigates privacy risks from invertible visual features.

entry.title: Cross-Embodiment Robot Manipulation via a Unified Hand Action Space

author.name: Luis Felipe Casas, Robert Teal, Keval Shah, Abhijit Tadepalli, Wanxin Jin, Yu Xiang

entry.summary: Introduces the Unified Hand Action Space, a sphere-based action representation for cross-embodiment dexterous manipulation, mapped to embodiment-specific joints via a cascade inverse kinematics algorithm and trained with RL for in-hand reorientation. It aims to transfer manipulation policies across hands with different kinematics.

entry.title: CoRE-VLA: Towards Scalable and Robust Vision-Language-Action Modeling via Conditional Routing of Experts

author.name: Haozhe Zhang, Sixian Li, Yifei Zhang, Zezheng Huai, Hao Chen, Chunhua Shen, Jingjing Gong, Xipeng Qiu

entry.summary: CoRE-VLA uses conditional routing of experts so a single VLA policy can exploit auxiliary sensors when present yet remain reliable when sensors fail or are absent by design. It addresses the brittleness of VLA policies tied to fixed sensor sets under heterogeneous embodiments.

entry.title: RoboVista: Evaluating Vision Language Models for Diverse Robot Applications

author.name: Shuangyu Xie, Kaiyuan Chen, Ziyang Chen, Simeon Adebola, Yixuan Huang, Zehan Ma, Tianshuang Qiu, Wentao Yuan, Dhruv Shah, Pannag R. Sanketi, Ken Goldberg

entry.summary: RoboVista proposes a modular Robot Question Answering evaluation framework and a benchmark curated from real robotic systems, papers, and expert annotations to assess VLMs across the individual decision components underlying robot behavior. It moves beyond end-to-end teleoperated datasets toward structured, interpretable evaluation.

entry.title: Can we find whether models have been backdoored?

author.name: Anthony Hughes

entry.summary: First post in a sequence on defending against data-poisoning backdoor attacks, describing methods to determine whether a model has been backdoored and to find the trigger, accompanying a forthcoming ICML Mech Interp workshop paper with a benchmark of variously backdoored models. It matters for detecting hidden triggers in models deployed in high-stakes domains.

entry.title: SpecGradFilter: A Spectral Gradient Filtering Framework for Taming Federated Heterogeneity

author.name: Liyang Yuan, Yibo Yang, Dandan Guo, Peter Richtarik, Zhouchen Lin

entry.summary: SpecGradFilter revisits federated client drift from a frequency-domain perspective, revealing that inter-client gradient divergence concentrates in low-frequency components encoding distribution shifts. It proposes spectral gradient filtering to mitigate heterogeneity. It matters for federated convergence, and includes established optimization researchers.

entry.title: Natural Language Camera Movement Understanding

author.name: Yuwen Tan, Joey Huang, Jin Huang, Haoxiang Li, Boqing Gong

entry.summary: Establishes natural-language camera-movement understanding as a standalone task, showing existing VLMs confuse translation with rotation and object with camera motion, and introduces a two-level cinematographic taxonomy plus a real-and-synthetic benchmark. A fine-tuned 8B VLM outperforms Gemini on the task.

entry.title: Perceptual Flow Matching for Few-Step Generative Modeling

author.name: Chuyang Zhao, Yifei Song, Hongfa Wang, Jianlong Yuan, Yuan Zhang, Siming Fu, Zhineng Chen, Huilin Deng, Haoyang Huang, Nan Duan

entry.summary: Perceptual Flow Matching supervises flow matching in a pretrained perceptual feature space rather than VAE latent space, cutting sampling from 35-50 steps to 4-8 while preserving quality without teacher models or auxiliary score networks. It integrates into standard flow-matching pipelines with minimal changes.

entry.title: PixelPilot: Scalable Vision-Language-Action Models for End-to-End Autonomous Driving

author.name: Pin Tang, Guoqing Wang, Xiangxuan Ren, Zhongdao Wang, Guodongfang Zhao, Bailan, Chao Ma

entry.summary: PixelPilot is a scalable vision-language-action model for end-to-end autonomous driving that decouples planning from lifting, avoiding the camera-parameter entanglement of 2D-to-3D trajectory prediction. This design improves cross-dataset scalability and reduces reliance on ego-status shortcuts.

entry.title: Solve the Missing First Step: Can VLMs Standardize Raw Heterogeneous Medical Data?

author.name: Xin Chen, Dongliang Xu, Cunhao Zhu, Xudong Luo, Haoyang Lyu, Xiaoxiao Sun, Serena Yeung-Levy, Yue Yao

entry.summary: This paper studies the neglected first step of raw medical data standardization, evaluating whether VLMs can identify source formats, convert raw images into compatible inputs, and extract relevant text from heterogeneous, fragmented clinical data folders. It highlights that existing benchmarks assume already-standardized inputs.

entry.title: VERITAS: Towards a General-Purpose Replication Tool for Scientific Research

author.name: Haokun Liu, Filbert Aurelian Tjiaranata, Chenhao Tan

entry.summary: VERITAS is a domain-agnostic replication framework built on CLI coding agents that, given a paper and/or repository, extracts claims, runs the methodology while resolving issues, and judges each claim. Unlike prior benchmark-bound systems, it aims to be a general-purpose replication tool amid the reproducibility crisis.

entry.title: Demonstrating Generalization Failures via Mixtures of Conditional Policies

author.name: Jou Barzdukas, Jack Peck, Julian Schulz, Paulius Rauba, Steven Basart, Lennie Wells

entry.summary: This work provides a controllable method to construct language models that predictably fail to generalize when subsequently trained with RL, by supervised fine-tuning on a mixture of conditional-policy transcripts. It is a tool to study post-training generalization failures under distribution shift in a clean, simplified setting.

entry.title: MentalThink: Shaping Thoughts in Mental SVG World

author.name: Kangheng Lin, Jisheng Yin, Dingming Li, En Yu, Yana Wei, Han Zhou, Liang Zhao, Hongyu Zhou, Hongbo Peng, Jianjian Sun, Zheng Ge, Xiangyu Zhang, Daxin Jiang, Jingyu Wang

entry.summary: MentalThink equips multimodal LLMs with an executable think-with-SVG pipeline where the model generates, renders, and interprets vector graphics as intermediate visual representations to externalize spatial hypotheses during multi-turn reasoning. Training combines SFT for SVG syntax with reinforcement learning.

entry.title: Online Linear Programming for Multi-Objective Routing in LLM Serving

author.name: Zixi Chen, Yinyu Ye, Zijie Zhou

entry.summary: This paper formulates online routing in LLM serving as an online linear program with interpretable SLO-weighted rewards and applies a bid-price control policy that admits requests when their weighted benefit exceeds shadow prices, meeting millisecond decision constraints. It offers principled control over latency-throughput trade-offs versus ad-hoc heuristics.

entry.title: MoP-JEPA: Hard-Assigned Predictor Mixtures for Stochastic JEPA World Models

author.name: Zhi Song, Ximing Xing, Zhenchao Tang, hanbo Huang, Tianxu Lv, minghao Yang, Zhongzheng Niu, He Bing, Lusheng Wang, Jianhua Yao

entry.summary: MoP-JEPA proves that standard single-predictor JEPA world models collapse to the conditional mean of successor states under stochastic transitions, corresponding to no valid state. It introduces hard-assigned predictor mixtures that converge to a quantizer of the transition distribution, one head per mode.

entry.title: S-EMBER: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Streaming Egocentric Memory Retrieval

author.name: Xiaodong Wang, Xuanyi Zhao, Pedro Rodriguez, Devendra Singh Sachan, Barlas Oguz, Seungwhan Moon, Shang-Wen Li, Gargi Ghosh, Xin Dong, Wen-Tau Yih

entry.summary: S-EMBER is a large-scale streaming egocentric memory benchmark with 3,141 videos (388 hours) captured via Ray-Ban Meta glasses, formalizing grounded streaming episodic retrieval triggered by visual events rather than global offline search. It provides 9,448 QA pairs for causal active recall.

entry.title: Not All Refusals Are Equal: How Safety Alignment Fails Cybersecurity at Scale

author.name: Vadym Hadetskyi, Dario Pasquini, Artem Sorokin

entry.summary: Reports a large-scale abliteration study on 24 open-source LLMs showing that domain-specific removal of refusal behavior is achievable with standard methodology, demonstrated on a 1T-parameter Kimi K2. It argues safety alignment fails to distinguish legitimate cybersecurity operations from harmful queries.

entry.title: R3D: Quantitative 3D Spatial Reasoning for Egocentric Wearables

author.name: Maxwell Horton, Wei Lu, Quan Tran, Yury Astashonok, Kirmani Ahmed, Babak Damavandi, Anuj Kumar, Xiao Zhang, Seungwhan Moon

entry.summary: R3D introduces a benchmark of 3,033 quantitative 3D spatial-reasoning questions from egocentric RGB-D video plus a model-agnostic spatial tool-calling framework. It targets the perception needs of next-generation wearable assistants.

entry.title: Can Model Merging Improve Aggregation in DiLoCo?

author.name: Stefan Horoi, Benjamin Th\'erien, Guy Wolf, Eugene Belilovsky

entry.summary: This paper studies whether model-merging techniques can improve the aggregation step in communication-efficient distributed training methods like DiLoCo/local SGD, where performance degrades as local models and steps increase. It connects two active research threads.

entry.title: Amortising Bayesian Experimental Design for Sequential Information Gathering in LLMs

author.name: Jakob Hartmann, James Harvey, Jhonathan Navott, Erik Y. Wang, Luckeciano C. Melo, Flaviu Cipcigan, Cheng Zhang, Alessandro Abate

entry.summary: ASIG amortizes Bayesian Experimental Design into LLM policies via a multi-turn Group Relative Policy Optimization variant with an expected-information-gain reward, improving sequential information gathering. It more than doubles the 20-Questions success rate of a 7B base model while cutting inference cost over 25x versus an inference-time BED baseline, and transfers to medical diagnosis.

entry.title: They Infer What You Meant: Models Represent Communicative Intent More Reliably Than They Act On It

author.name: Alex Kwon

entry.summary: This interpretability study shows that language models reliably represent a sender's Gricean communicative intent (whether they want something recognized versus evaluated) in hidden states, decodable by a linear probe across six models and four families, yet often fail to act on it. It frames intent as a readout failure over a robust internal representation.

entry.title: Refused in Chat, Written in Code: Workflow-Level Jailbreak Construction in IDE Coding Agents

author.name: Abhishek Kumar, Carsten Maple

entry.summary: This paper introduces workflow-level jailbreak construction, where a harmful objective is assembled across ordinary software-development stages in IDE coding agents rather than via a single prompt, studied with GitHub Copilot across Claude and Gemini backends. It shows chatbot-style single-turn safety evaluation misses multi-turn agentic attack surfaces.

entry.title: CausalGame: Benchmarking Causal Thinking of LLM Agents in Games

author.name: Zhenhao Chen, Yongqiang Chen, Chenxi Liu, Junchi Yu, Xiangchen Song, Zijian Li, Jialin Li, Philip Torr, Bo Han, Kun Zhang

entry.summary: CausalGame is a benchmark that evaluates causal thinking in LLM agents through interactive games incorporating selection bias, measurement error, and hidden confounders, requiring agents to actively design experiments. It targets the causal-reasoning gap in AI-scientist agents.

entry.title: Transferability Between Understanding and Generation in Unified Multimodal Models

author.name: Jiwon Kang, Heeji Yoon, Jaewoo Jung, Jaewon Min, Minkyeong Jeon, Biyeon Hwang, Sangwon Jung, Seungryong Kim

entry.summary: This paper investigates transferability in unified multimodal models, empirically showing that training a capability on understanding can improve generation of the same capability without explicit supervision, but only when the architecture has a fully shared transformer backbone and unified encoder. It then proposes a training strategy exploiting this cross-task transfer.

entry.title: ToolFailBench: Diagnosing Tool-Use Failures in LLM Agents

author.name: Harsh Soni

entry.summary: ToolFailBench is a diagnostic benchmark of 1,000 tasks across five domains that categorizes tool-use failures in LLM agents (Tool-Skip, Result-Ignore, Output-Fabrication, Unnecessary-Tool-Use) rather than only measuring final accuracy. It reveals where agents fail in tool calling that aggregate scores hide.

entry.title: RSPO: Reward-Swap Policy Optimization for Multi-Turn LLM Agents

author.name: Qiang Liu, Taian Guo, Ruizhi Qiao, Xing Sun

entry.summary: RSPO (Reward-Swap Policy Optimization) trains multi-turn LLM agents in long-horizon tasks with sparse outcome rewards by combining dense process rewards to accelerate convergence with mechanisms to mitigate misalignment between surrogate and ground-truth rewards. It targets slow convergence and unsampled successful trajectories in RL.

entry.title: EventCoT: Event-centric Video Chain-of-thought for Reasoning Temporal Localization

author.name: Youngkil Song, Yoonjae Baek, Dongwon Kim, Inho Kim, Dongkeun Kim, Suha Kwak

entry.summary: EventCoT is an event-centric video chain-of-thought framework for reasoning temporal localization that tokenizes video into compact event tokens, reasons within relevant events, and grounds time intervals via embedding matching. It jointly produces high-level reasoning and precise temporal grounding in a single response, achieving SOTA.

entry.title: DSWAM: A Dual-System World Action Foundation Model for Fine-Grained Robot Manipulation

author.name: Jian Zhu, Jianjun Zhang, Taiyi Su, Tianbin Liu, Zhangyuan Wang, Kai Xie, Zitai Huang, Chong Ma, Youzhang He, Tianjian Wang, Hanyang Wang, Weihao Ding, Yi Xu

entry.summary: DSWAM is a dual-system World Action foundation model that adds an explicit language-level planning interface to video-based world models for fine-grained robot manipulation, decomposing coarse instructions into executable subtasks. It also aims to provide a fair real-robot comparison between VLA and WAM execution capabilities.

entry.title: Noisy-Channel Minimum Bayes Risk Decoding

author.name: Yusuke Sakai, Hidetaka Kamigaito, Taro Watanabe

entry.summary: This paper introduces a noisy-channel decomposition of Minimum Bayes Risk decoding that incorporates bidirectional (hypothesis-to-reference and reference-to-hypothesis) effects to account for asymmetric evaluation metrics like BLEU and COMET. It addresses a mismatch between MBR hypothesis selection and asymmetric metric design.

entry.title: REDDIT: Correcting Model-Generated Timestamp Drift in ASR without Forgetting via Replay-Based Distribution Editing

author.name: Cheng-Kang Chou, Ming-To Chuang, Ke-Han Lu, Chan-Jan Hsu, Hung-yi Lee

entry.summary: REDDIT identifies that autoregressive ASR systems emitting timestamps as tokens can drift across long non-speech spans (transcript stays plausible but time axis diverges) and proposes a replay-based distribution editing post-training framework to correct timestamp drift without catastrophic forgetting. It uses self-built gap benchmarks across 15 timestamp-producing ASR systems.

entry.title: Auditing the Audit: Five Failure Modes in Benchmark-Validity Audits

author.name: Yanhang Li, Zhichao Fan, Zexin Zhuang

entry.summary: This paper argues that perturbation-based benchmark-validity audits used for AI governance can be silently manipulated by hidden implementation choices, naming five failure modes and proposing a six-point due-diligence gate. It matters because governance frameworks increasingly rely on such audits as evidence of model safety.

entry.title: Implicit Bias of SGD in Multivariate ReLU Networks: Effective Width Collapse

author.name: Shuang Liang, Tom Jacobs, Guido Mont\'ufar

entry.summary: This theory paper characterizes the implicit bias of noisy SGD in wide two-layer ReLU networks for multivariate regression, showing via a mean-field Wasserstein gradient flow that the learned predictor undergoes effective width collapse into finitely many directions. Despite infinite overparameterization, the solution is continuous piecewise affine.

entry.title: Tensor-Train Joint Modeling for Few-Step Discrete Diffusion

author.name: Byoungkwon Kim, Minhyuk Sung

entry.summary: This paper introduces the first framework for explicit joint-distribution modeling in discrete diffusion via tensor decomposition, representing the conditional clean distribution as a controllable low-rank tensor to overcome the parallelization bias of the conditional-independence assumption. It targets the few-step regime where fast generation is undermined by that bias.

entry.title: MANCE: Manifold Aware Concept Erasure

author.name: Matan Avitan, Yoav Goldberg, Yanai Elazar

entry.summary: MANCE is a concept-erasure method built on the Manifold Constraint Hypothesis, which posits that constraining interventions to the lower-dimensional manifold of natural representations better preserves correlated information when removing a target concept. It performs iterative manifold-aware updates guided by a classifier.

entry.title: CertMix: Certified, Data-Efficient Metamaterial Design by Affine Mixing of Aligned Neural-Implicit Weight Spaces

author.name: Yifan Wang

entry.summary: CertMix represents metamaterial unit cells as aligned SIREN neural implicit fields and exploits an approximately linear relationship between mixing coefficients and homogenized elasticity to enable data-efficient inverse design with certification. It reduces targeted design to a small optimization in aligned weight space. It matters as a data-efficient, guarantee-providing approach to inverse mechanical design.

entry.title: Beyond the Need for Speed: Energy-Aware Code Generation via Simulation-Guided Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Saurabhsingh Rajput, Tushar Sharma

entry.summary: This paper replaces noisy hardware energy profiling with deterministic architectural simulation to build Green Tea, a 3.5-million-evaluation corpus over C++ problems, and trains an energy-aware code model via contrastive SFT plus simulation-in-the-loop GRPO. It introduces a correctness-adjusted energy-reduction metric. It matters for sustainable code generation with reproducible feedback.

entry.title: When Do Foundation Models Pay Off? A Break-Even Analysis of Pretrained Time Series Forecasters

author.name: Nicholas Tan Jerome, Frank Simon

entry.summary: This paper provides a systematic break-even analysis of when time-series foundation models such as Chronos, Moirai, and Lag-Llama justify their GPU cost versus classical baselines like ARIMA and XGBoost, across 30 datasets and six training-set sizes. It gives per-dataset guidance on deployment worthiness. It matters as practical decision support for practitioners.

entry.title: Tile-Level Activation Overlap for Efficient LLM Inference

author.name: Abhinav Jangda, Tyler Sorensen, Sebastian Burckhardt, Jianlan YE, Chaoyin Li, Atul Gupta

entry.summary: Two CUTLASS-based SM90 kernels fuse SwiGLU into GeMM at the tile level, eliminating costly intermediate tensor materialization during LLM MLP execution. On H100 with Qwen-2.5 models they report up to 2.47x speedup and high BF16 utilization.

entry.title: Incentivizing Vision Language Models to Search for Long Video Question Answering

author.name: Harsh Goel, S P Sharan, Sahil Shah, Minkyu Choi, Joungbin An, Kristen Grauman, Sandeep P. Chinchali

entry.summary: VSeek reframes long-video question answering as a multi-turn agentic retrieval process, using RL post-training to formulate search queries and reason over retrieved clips, with a neuro-symbolic reward for verified relevance. It addresses the lack of verified rewards for long-video RL.

entry.title: Sequential Correlations Change In-Context Learning: Effective Context Length and Architectural Mismatch

author.name: Mary Letey, Yue M. Lu, Cengiz Pehlevan, Jacob Zavatone-Veth

entry.summary: This paper extends in-context learning theory to sequentially correlated data using a solvable linear-attention model, identifying how within-context correlations affect effective context length and reveal architectural mismatch. It tests predictions on realistic transformer architectures.

entry.title: TESSERA v2: Scaling Pixel-wise Earth Foundation Models

author.name: Zhengpeng Feng, Sadiq Jaffer, Ira Shokar, Jovana Knezevic, Mark Elvers, Clement Atzberger, Robin Young, Aneesh Naik, Niall Robinson, Andrew Blake, David Coomes, Anil Madhavapeddy, Srinivasan Keshav

entry.summary: TESSERA v2 presents the largest controlled scaling study for pixel-wise Earth-observation foundation models—395 runs on 1,024 GH200 chips across 15 downstream tasks—finding that pretraining loss barely predicts downstream performance and giving rules for allocating compute between encoder and data. It offers practical scaling guidance for EO models.

entry.title: Tightening the Score Matching Gap for Diffusion Models

author.name: Benjamin Dupuis, Tyler Farghly, Maxime Haddouche, Alain Durmus, Umut Simsekli

entry.summary: This theoretical paper analyzes the 'score matching gap' between diffusion sample quality and the score-matching training loss, deriving tighter bounds for KL, reverse KL, and Wasserstein distances. It aims to make the ELBO-based bound more descriptive of actual generation quality.

entry.title: How Much is Left? LLMs Linearly Encode Their Remaining Output Length

author.name: Mohamed Amine Merzouk, Dmitri Carpov, Mirko Bronzi, Damiano Fornasiere, Adam Oberman

entry.summary: This paper shows that LLMs linearly encode their remaining output length: minimal linear probes on frozen hidden states can decode total response length from the prompt's last hidden state before any token is emitted, and these probe directions transfer across datasets. It provides converging evidence that models carry an internal estimate of how much response remains.

entry.title: Wrong Before Right: Late Rescue and Interface Failure in Aligned Language Models

author.name: Jiaqi Deng

entry.summary: Uses layer-wise difference-in-differences and activation transplantation across 17 models to show a mid-layer wrong-dip where internal preference commits to the incorrect answer before late-layer correction, and characterizes how alignment amplifies this dynamic. It offers mechanistic insight into how correctness is assembled internally.

entry.title: Rating the Pitch, Not the Product: User Evaluations of LLMs Reflect Expectations More Than Performance

author.name: Robert Morabito, Tyler McDonald, Charitra Viswanath, Angel Hsing-Chi Hwang, Susanne Gaube, Jad Kabbara, Ali Emami

entry.summary: A controlled study of 162 participants shows that pre-interaction framing of an LLM's capability (matched, overstated, or understated) shifts user ratings and prompting behavior even though task performance is unchanged. It reveals that user evaluations reflect expectations more than actual model quality.

entry.title: GameEngineBench: Evaluating Coding Agents on Real C++ Runtime Environments

author.name: Brian La, Sejoon Chang, Ben Kim, Junyoung Bae, Aamish Ahmad Beg, Sei Chang, Gonzalo Gonzalez-Pumariega

entry.summary: GameEngineBench evaluates coding agents on scoped C++ tasks inside real Unreal Engine 5 projects across nine repositories, testing modification of stateful, real-time, interactive systems (gameplay, multiplayer, rendering). It offers a realistic testbed beyond typical isolated coding benchmarks.

entry.title: How to Build Digital Humans? From Priors to Photorealistic Avatars

author.name: Wojciech Zielonka, Tobias Kirschstein, Timo Bolkart, Simon Giebenhain, Vanessa Sklyarova, Xiang Deng, Donglai Xiang, Shunsuke Saito, Yebin Liu, Matthias Niessner, Justus Thies

entry.summary: A state-of-the-art report surveying controllable 3D human avatar creation, covering prior learning and personalized avatar creation across full-body, head, and layered representations, with a multi-axis taxonomy. It consolidates the field's principles and open challenges.

entry.title: Evaluating Intellectual Property Guardrails of Generative Image Models: A Technical Report

author.name: Austin T. Hoag, Apostolos Modas, Yunhao Ba, Julienne M. LaChance, Jinru Xue, Wiebke Hutiri, Jan Simson, Tiffany Georgievski, Alex Towli, Joseph Smith, Yuki Mitsufuji, Alice Xiang

entry.summary: Presents a benchmark and automated pipeline to test intellectual-property guardrails in fourteen text-to-image models across fictional characters, celebrity likeness, and commercial logos, evaluating both guardrail presence and propensity to generate recognizable IP. It provides systematic evidence on IP-replication risks.

entry.title: Holo-Captioning: Toward the Text Equivalent of 3D Scenes

author.name: Kun-Yu Lin, Chengke Bu, Zhenguo Li, Kai Han

entry.summary: Introduces holo-captioning, a new task of producing a structured textual description of an entire 3D scene covering semantic tags, spatial locations, attributes, and inter-entity relations, alongside a 15K-scene benchmark and the HoloScribe model. It aims to create a text-equivalent representation of 3D scenes.

entry.title: Overloading Large Vision-Language Models for Jailbreaking

author.name: Haoyu Zhang, Yangyang Guo, Mohan Kankanhalli

entry.summary: Proposes an information-overloading jailbreak against large vision-language models that combines extensive text with multi-dimensional image attacks to overcome defenses in modern multimodal backbones. It demonstrates improved transferability across model architectures compared to simpler prior attacks.

entry.title: Do Medical Vision Language Models Actually See? A Counterfactual Grounding Framework and Hard-Negative Contrastive Training for Visually-Reliant Medical VLMs

author.name: Anas Zafar, Leema Krishna Murali, Siddhant Bharadwaj, Ashish Vashist, Jia Wu

entry.summary: Introduces a counterfactual grounding framework for medical VLMs that substitutes images with blank, shuffled, absent, or hard-negative surrogates to measure a Visual Reliance Score and Visual Hallucination Rate, then proposes CORAL, a LoRA fine-tune of Qwen2.5-VL-7B with a contrastive grounding objective. It tests whether medical VLMs reason from visual evidence or textual shortcuts.

entry.title: EgoInertia-MI: A Multimodal Egocentric Vision and IMU Benchmark for Motor Impairment Assessment

author.name: Fatemah Alhamdoosh, Pietro Pala, Abduallah Mohamed, DK Arvind

entry.summary: EgoInertia-MI is a multimodal benchmark pairing synchronized egocentric video with wearable IMU signals for objective motor impairment assessment (tremor, bradykinesia, gait). It offers a privacy-aware alternative to third-person clinical recordings.

entry.title: SAGE: Synchronized Action-Gaze Recognition and Anticipation for Human Behavior Understanding

author.name: Chenyi Kuang, Nakul Agarwal

entry.summary: SAGE is a unified transformer framework that jointly recognizes and anticipates human-object interactions and gaze behavior in a single end-to-end model. It incorporates gaze into spatiotemporal attention to exploit the interdependence of gaze and actions.

entry.title: SiamJEPA: On the Role of Siamese Student Encoders in JEPA

author.name: Makoto Yamada

entry.summary: SiamJEPA investigates the role of Siamese student encoders in Joint Embedding Predictive Architectures, contrasting with the single-encoder design of I-JEPA and V-JEPA. It argues Siamese encoders align more naturally with brain-inspired representation learning.

entry.title: Real-Time LiDAR Gaussian Splatting SLAM

author.name: Seungjun Tak, Yewon Jeon, Jaeik Hwang, SukMin Hwang, Seongbo Ha, Hyeonwoo Yu

entry.summary: This work presents a real-time LiDAR-based Gaussian Splatting SLAM that couples fast G-ICP registration with spherical rasterization dense mapping, reusing tracking covariances to initialize range-aware Gaussians. A covariance-derived geometry score drives pruning and densification.

entry.title: RAF: Reliability-Aware Fusion of Camera, LiDAR, and 4D RADAR for Robust 3D Object Detection in Adverse Weather

author.name: Heejun Park, Jaeseok Jeong, Kuk-Jin Yoon

entry.summary: RAF is a reliability-aware fusion framework combining camera, LiDAR, and 4D radar for robust 3D detection in adverse weather, providing direct supervision to region confidence maps that prior methods leave unsupervised. It exploits camera cues when LiDAR and radar returns are weakened.

entry.title: Aperture-aware Dispersion 5-D Light-field Imaging Spectrometer

author.name: Chenglong Huang, Tao Lv, Jianing Yang, Chongde Zi, Linsen Chen, Xun Cao

entry.summary: ADLIS is an aperture-aware dispersion light-field imaging spectrometer that captures 5D spectral light-field data cubes with a single detector using aperture-multiplexed modulation and birefringent spectral filtering. It targets a synergy between compactness and cross-dimensional resolution.

entry.title: From Open Loop to Closed Loop: A Test-Time Iterative Optimization Framework for Reference-Consistent Image Generation

author.name: Baixuan Zhao, Xinyu Zhang, Huayu Zheng, Shuaicheng Liu, Xiongkuo Min, Guangtao Zhai, Xiaohong Liu

entry.summary: This work reformulates reference-consistent image generation as a closed-loop tracking problem, treating a pretrained generator as a control plant and using a modified PID controller for test-time iterative optimization with feedback and error correction. It replaces open-loop feed-forward control injection.

entry.title: SparseOcc++: Geometry-Aware Sparse Latent Representation for Semantic Occupancy Prediction

author.name: Pin Tang, Zhongdao Wang, Guoqing Wang, Xiangxuan Ren, Chao Ma

entry.summary: SparseOcc++ is a geometry-aware fully sparse framework for 3D semantic occupancy prediction that explicitly decouples scene completion from semantic segmentation, avoiding indiscriminate feature propagation into empty regions. This reduces excessive activations, computational overhead, and geometric ambiguity.

entry.title: DriftST: One-Step Generative Inference of Spatial Transcriptomics from H\&E Histology

author.name: Yuhang Yang, Yonggan Bu, Shengyuan Zhou, Yiming Luo, Kai Zhang

entry.summary: DriftST performs one-step generative inference of spatial transcriptomics from H&E histology, modeling inter-gene dependencies and heterogeneous gene informativeness while supporting both spot- and cell-level resolution. It avoids the over-smoothing of regression and the slow multi-step inference of prior generative methods.

entry.title: Current as Touch: Proprioceptive Contact Feedback for Compliant Dexterous Manipulation

author.name: Chenyang Ma, Yunchao Yao, Zhenyu Wei, Ruogu Li, Daniel Szafir, Mingyu Ding

entry.summary: This work learns contact-aware compliance from motor current and joint states, treating motor current as an intrinsic proxy for actuator torque and predicting a compliance reference position for a standard PD controller. It enables compliant dexterous manipulation on low-cost hands without external force sensors.

entry.title: WSA$_1$: a 3D-Centric World-Spatial-Action Model for Generalizable Robot Control

author.name: Jiahao Jiang, Jianing Zhang, Zhenhan Yin, Ruidong Chen, Sen Wang, Zhaoshu Yu, Pengpeng Zeng, Xiaofeng Cao, Xuanhan Wang, Jingkuan Song, Heng Tao Shen

entry.summary: WSA1 is a 3D-centric world-spatial-action robot foundation model designed to reason about physical dynamics and causal effects in the 3D world, addressing the mismatch between 2D visual perception and 3D embodied interaction. It aims to improve generalization of robot foundation models to real-world tasks.

entry.title: Aerial Manipulation: Contact, Medium Coupling, and the Geometry of Readiness

author.name: Antonio Franchi

entry.summary: This review by Antonio Franchi argues aerial manipulation cannot be treated as classical manipulation on a flying base because flying agents continuously exchange momentum with the surrounding medium, coupling support, locomotion, and interaction. It proposes a medium-aware interpretation covering contact, fluid, and combined mediation.

entry.title: Spatial Attention: Adapting Execution Horizons for Diffusion Policies via Observation Sensitivity

author.name: Che-Sang Park, Junsu Ha, Jianlong Fu, Frank C. Park

entry.summary: Introduces Spatial Attention, defined as the expected squared gradient norm of the action log-likelihood with respect to observation, to adaptively adjust the execution horizon of sampled action chunks in diffusion policies. It balances responsiveness and computational cost under a fixed sampling budget.

entry.title: Harness-Aware Self-Evolving: Co-Evolving Model Weights, Harness, and Task Solutions

author.name: Haochen Luo, Yi Huang, Sichun Luo, Fengyuan Liu, Lei Li, Zefa Hu, Junlan Feng, Qi Liu

entry.summary: HASE is an agentic RL framework where a single model can either generate task solutions or edit selected harness components in a multi-turn action space, co-evolving weights, harness, and solutions. A Qwen3-8B model with HASE reportedly matches or beats a GPT-OSS-120B baseline on text classification, alpha factor mining, and circle-packing discovery.

entry.title: Out-of-Distribution Generalization of Risk Aversion in Language Models

author.name: Kristina Zhang, Junior Chinomso Okoroafor, Benjamin Maltbie, Andrew Lin, Abhitej Bokka, Elliott Thornley

entry.summary: Introduces RiskAverseOOD, a benchmark measuring whether risk aversion trained on low-stakes gambles generalizes to astronomically high-stakes gambles, motivated by the idea that risk-averse misaligned AIs would prefer cooperation over rebellion. Initial results train Qwen3-8B to choose risk-aversely and test out-of-distribution generalization.

entry.title: Safe Inference-Time Alignment via Lagrangian Reward Augmentation

author.name: Yaswanth Chittepu, Ativ Joshi, Sohini Chintala, Scott Niekum

entry.summary: LARA is an inference-time alignment framework that steers a frozen LLM under explicit safety constraints by dualizing a KL-regularized constrained objective with reward and cost models, reducing it to a one-dimensional convex problem over a dual variable estimated from a small calibration set. It avoids manual penalty tuning for safety.

entry.title: Covert Trait Propagation Is Representation Alignment: Mechanistic Evidence from Hidden-Channel Distillation

author.name: Kargi Chauhan, Aditya Shah

entry.summary: This work provides mechanistic evidence that covert trait propagation in hidden-channel distillation is fundamentally representation alignment: shared initialization makes the output projection a common key, and KL gradients reshape the student's input projection until its hidden representations align with the teacher's. It explains where in the network the covert channel lives and what limits its capacity.

entry.title: Lights, Camera, Carbon: Architectural Scaling Laws for Video Generation Energy Consumption

author.name: Nidhal Jegham, Boris Gamazaychikov, Sasha Luccioni

entry.summary: This paper presents a bidirectional framework estimating text-to-video and text-to-video-audio model energy consumption from architectural first principles and observable parameters like resolution and duration, without access to weights or model size. It derives quadratic and linear scaling laws for the compute-bound video diffusion models and uses prediction accuracy as a test of architectural validity.

entry.title: A Few Teacher Steps Go a Long Way: Cost-Efficient On-Policy Data Augmentation for Agent Post-Training

author.name: Junze Ye, Jiayi Cheng, Miao Lu, Michal Mankowski, Jose Blanchet, Mohsen Bayati

entry.summary: This paper frames on-policy data construction for LLM-agent post-training as a budget-allocation problem, asking under matched supervision resources whether teacher output is best spent on more full demos, longer continuations, outcome filtering, or broader coverage. It argues that a few teacher steps at student-visited contexts go a long way for cost-efficient augmentation.

entry.title: Input Pathways Shape Few-Shot, Not Zero-Shot, Binding in Tiny Transformers: A Fully-Enumerable Study

author.name: Yoshiyuki Ootani

entry.summary: This fully-enumerable study of tiny (~6-10K parameter) transformers on finite factored worlds examines how input encoding (symbolic tokens, oracle codes, entangled perceptual vectors) affects compositional binding, finding input pathways shape few-shot but not zero-shot binding. Exhaustive enumeration gives zero sampling variance and exact Bayes ceilings.

entry.title: ImputeECG: Deep Learning Reconstruction of Complete 12-Lead Electrocardiograms from Incomplete Recordings for Cardiac Assessment

author.name: Xiaocheng Fang, Haoyu Wang, Jieyi Cai, Qinghao Zhao, Jun Li, Shanwei Zhang, Guangkun Nie, Yujie Xiao, Shun Huang, Jiarui Jin, Hongmin Liu, Guodong Wang, Shuohua Chen, Liming Lin, Shouling Wu, Hongyan Li, Shenda Hong

entry.summary: ImputeECG is a mask-conditioned 1D Transformer autoencoder that reconstructs complete 12-lead 10-second ECGs from incomplete recordings while retaining observed samples, validated on PTB-XL, CPSC2018, and a 43,633-record real clinical cohort after image digitization. It addresses incomplete ECGs from short displays, lead loss, or corruption.

entry.title: PDEFlow: Autonomous Agentic PDE Pipelines for Neural Operator Learning and Solver-Free Inference

author.name: Akshat Jani, Prathamesh Gadekar, Sakhinana Sagar Srinivas, Venkataramana Runkana

entry.summary: PDEFlow is an autonomous agentic framework that turns natural-language ODE/PDE descriptions into solver-backed neural-operator pipelines, linking specification, data generation (via FEniCSx finite-element solving), operator training, and checkpoint-based inference. A stateful input graph converts multi-turn language and edits into validated problem specifications.

entry.title: SPEARBench: A Benchmark for Naturalness Evaluation in Streaming Speech-to-Speech Language Models

author.name: Thomas Thebaud, Yuzhe Wang, Hao Zhang, Sathvik Manikantan Napa Ugandhar, Ashish Hallur, Georgi Tinchev, Venkatesh Ravichandran, Laureano Moro-Velazquez

entry.summary: SPEARBench is a benchmark for evaluating naturalness in streaming speech-to-speech language models, constructing controlled dialogue prompts from the Seamless Interaction corpus and assessing response latency, timing, turn-taking, prosody, stance, and relationship-aware appropriateness. It captures conversational qualities that standard speech and text benchmarks miss.

entry.title: On the Design Space of Discrete Diffusion Online Adaptation for Molecular Optimization

author.name: Trevor Chen, Ariel Dai, Jason Yang, Riccardo De Santi, Daniel Khalil, Wenda Chu, Nate Gruver, Pranav Murugan, Alexander F. G. Goldberg, Maruan Al-Shedivat, Yisong Yue

entry.summary: This paper studies the design space of online adaptation for discrete diffusion models in molecular optimization, jointly analyzing candidate selection, reward-to-update conversion, feedback reuse, and how far to move from the pretrained prior. It uses controlled experiments to understand whether these choices complement or interfere with each other.

entry.title: Social Networks of LLM Agents

author.name: Kaixuan Liu, Guojun Xiong, Weinan Zhang, Shengpu Tang

entry.summary: SNLA models how much each LLM agent actually influences others in interacting populations, accounting for limited attention that classical social-network models ignore, to distinguish genuine consensus from herding. It addresses whether populations of LLM agents aggregate knowledge or collapse into false consensus.

entry.title: Pathways of Visual Information Flow in Vision-Language Models

author.name: Israfel Salazar, Stella Frank, Dan Oneata, Desmond Elliott, Constanza Fierro

entry.summary: Using causal patching on controlled datasets, this study identifies two distinct pathways for routing visual information in vision-language models—a direct pathway retained in image tokens and a text-mediated pathway transferred to query tokens—and shows pathway selection is task- and prompt-dependent. It uses attention knockouts and corrupted-input patching for mechanistic analysis.

entry.title: GuideMe: Multi-Domain Task Guidance and Intervention in Streaming Video

author.name: Fang Liu, Jinpeng Chen, Ke Xu, Yuhao Liu, Huankang Guan, Xudong Lu, Bo Yang, Gerhard Hancke, Rui Liu, Rynson W. H. Lau

entry.summary: GuideMe is the first multi-domain streaming-video benchmark for closed-loop interactive task guidance, with 2,458 videos across cooking, manipulation, daily-life, and fitness, and 47,775 interaction samples covering next-step instructions, feedback, and mistake correction. It evaluates how far MLLMs are from serving as real-time procedural coaches.

entry.title: A Decomposable Probe for Few-Step Diffusion Models: Prompt, Latent, and Score Selectivity across Backbone Families and Distillation Paradigms

author.name: Patrick Mu Haojie

entry.summary: Introduces a decomposable probe that injects controlled perturbations at prompt encoder, denoiser input, and denoiser output layers to diagnose few-step distilled diffusion models beyond a single FID/CLIP scalar, using a Bures W2 selectivity ratio across 23 models. It separates whether behaviors stem from architecture, distillation objective, or being a diffusion model.

entry.title: XPlainVerse: A Million-Scale Benchmark for Explainable Deepfake Detection

author.name: Abhijeet Narang, Kartik Kuckreja, Shreya Ghosh, Muhammad Haris Khan, Jianfei Cai, Abhinav Dhall

entry.summary: XPlainVerse is a million-scale benchmark for explainable deepfake detection pairing one million real and manipulated images across five real sources and twelve editing/synthesis models, evaluating whether natural-language explanations are grounded in actual manipulations rather than just classification accuracy. It targets deployable, trustworthy explainable detection.

entry.title: Sparse4D-Radar: An Efficient and Robust Framework for Surround-View 3D Object Detection via 4D Radar-Camera Fusion

author.name: Fuyuan Ai, Yuchen Tan, Jiehui Chen, Zhiwei Xu, Chunyi Song

entry.summary: Sparse4D-Radar is an efficient surround-view 3D object detection framework fusing 4D imaging radar with cameras via a deformable fusion module and sparse queries. It extends front-view radar-camera fusion to full 360-degree coverage while limiting computation.

entry.title: The Multipath Blind Spot: $K$-Agnostic Robust Calibration for Sparse-Anchor Metric Depth from Frozen Foundations

author.name: Sohag Roy, Rajesh Misra, Swami Shastravidyananda, Tamal Maharaj

entry.summary: This paper exposes a multipath blind spot in sparse-anchor metric depth calibration from frozen depth foundations, showing that standard residual calibration and the deployed VI-Depth method collapse under time-of-flight outliers rather than merely missing anchors. It proposes a K-agnostic robust calibration approach.

entry.title: Topology-Driven Transferability Estimation for 3D Medical Vision Foundation Models

author.name: Jiaqi Tang, Shaoyang Zhang, Fandong Zhang, Shu Zhang, Yang Liu, Qingchao Chen

entry.summary: This paper proposes a non-parametric, topology-driven transferability estimation framework for selecting 3D medical vision foundation models without exhaustive fine-tuning. It preserves spatial relationships and boundary details needed for dense voxel-wise segmentation.

entry.title: HeartVolMesh: Cardiac Volumetric Mesh Reconstruction via Covariance-Guided Graph Deformation

author.name: Fengming Lin, Arezoo Zakeri, Haoran Dou, Zherui Zhou, Shaokun Lan, Jinming Duan, Alejandro Frangi

entry.summary: HeartVolMesh reconstructs patient-specific volumetric cardiac tetrahedral meshes by lifting template vertices to anisotropic Gaussian kernels and predicting displacements and covariances via a CNN-GNN. It preserves cross-case correspondence for in-silico trials.

entry.title: AdaptiveSplat:Texture Aware Controllable 3D Gaussian Allocation for Feed-Forward Reconstruction

author.name: Badrinath Singhal, Srihari K G, Sreehari Iyer, Ankit Dhiman, Venkatesh Babu Radhakrishnan

entry.summary: AdaptiveSplat controllably allocates 3D Gaussians for feed-forward reconstruction by leveraging local texture information, assigning more primitives to high-frequency regions and fewer to smooth areas. It reduces redundancy without inference-time fine-tuning.

entry.title: Geographic Diversity Beats Data Volume for Cross-Domain Generalization in Zero-Label JEPA Driving World Models

author.name: Santosh Jaiswal

entry.summary: This study shows that JEPA-based self-supervised driving world models trained on geographically diverse data generalize their surprise/complexity scores to unseen cities better than models trained on equal amounts of single-geography data. It uses a controlled matched-scale nuPlan-to-Argoverse transfer experiment.

entry.title: QSVideo: Query-Conditioned Semantic Temporal Retrieval for Video Understanding

author.name: Wei Ao, Lan Wang, Vishnu Naresh Boddeti

entry.summary: QSVideo is a unified multimodal retrieval framework for long-video understanding that introduces a query-conditioned semantic ranker to localize key visual evidence, addressing relevance bias, limited diversity, and temporal collapse. It helps VLMs avoid confusion from query-irrelevant moments.

entry.title: Reference-Induced Consensus for Selective Posed-Reference Visual Localization

author.name: Wonseok Kang, Jaehyun Kim, Jeongmin Lee, Tae-Wan Kim

entry.summary: RIC-Loc is a scene-training-free, SfM-map-free visual localizer that uses a frozen VGGT pass to predict poses, depth, and tracks, deriving per-reference SE(3) pose hypotheses whose consensus yields a pose and two ground-truth-free reliability scores. It enables failure detection via spatial dispersion and track-conditioned covariance.

entry.title: DeGenseGS: Geometrically and Semantically Decoupled Surgical Scene Understanding in 4D Gaussian Splatting

author.name: Yimo Wang, Bin Kang, Shuojue Yang, Yueming Jin

entry.summary: DeGenseGS is a 4D Gaussian Splatting framework for surgical scene understanding that decouples semantic evolution from geometric deformation, using a HexPlane-based module with shared kinematic latents to synchronize semantic mutations with scene dynamics. It targets text-promptable real-time reconstruction for autonomous surgical interaction.

entry.title: LILAC: Layer-Wise Independent LoRAs and Cascaded Conditioning for Multi-Concept Customization of Diffusion Models

author.name: Marian Lupascu, Sebastian Ripa, Mihai Trascau, Mariana-Iuliana Georgescu, Ionut Mironica

entry.summary: LILAC composes independently trained low-rank adapters as separate image layers at inference time via layer-wise independent LoRAs and cascaded conditioning, avoiding the identity confusion and style bleeding of weight-space adapter fusion. It enables multi-concept customization without joint retraining.

entry.title: PAGE: Towards Practical Human-level Gaze Target Estimation

author.name: Zhoutong Ye, Chengwen Zhang, Zhaibin Cui, Mingze Sun, Jiaqi Liu, Xiangwu Li, Qingyang Wan, Chang Liu, Xutong Wang, Huan-ang Gao, Yu Mei, Chun Yu, Yuanchun Shi

entry.summary: PaGE is a practical gaze target estimation model that explicitly models the interaction between global scene semantics and precise head-appearance features, using a large ViT-H+ teacher to distill lighter students on large unlabeled data. It targets human-level performance for practical deployment.

entry.title: LH-AVLN: A Benchmark for Long-Horizon Audio-Visual-Language Navigation

author.name: Rufeng Chen, Yue Chang, Zili Shao, Zhaofan Zhang, Hechang Chen, Hui Xiong, Sihong Xie

entry.summary: LH-AVLN is a benchmark for long-horizon audio-visual-language navigation with multi-goal missions, heterogeneous goal specifications (category, language, image), and persistent spatialized binaural audio cues in indoor 3D environments. It fills a gap where long-horizon benchmarks are acoustically silent and audio-visual tasks are single-goal.

entry.title: SEAM: Smooth Execution of Action-Chunked Motion for Vision-Language-Action Policies

author.name: Dijia Zhan, Xuemiao Xu, Jinyi Li, Jie Tang

entry.summary: SEAM is a training-free inference-time method for flow-matching VLA policies that fixes multimodal bifurcation, where independently sampled adjacent action chunks converge to incompatible trajectories, causing discontinuities at chunk boundaries. It uses a synchronous-execution insight to smooth transitions without retraining or backpropagation.

entry.title: KAM-WM: Kinematic Affordance Maps from Latent World Models for Robot Manipulation

author.name: Xinyu Shao, Keru Zhou, Guowei Huang, Yajun Gao, Tongtong Cao, Xiu Li

entry.summary: KAM-WM extracts a coarse directional interaction cue from a frozen latent video world model by interpreting its single-step latent velocity as a Kinematic Affordance Map, without rollouts or fine-tuning, then compresses it to condition a diffusion policy. It provides both where and how to interact from few demonstrations.

entry.title: Closing the Reality Gap: Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real Deployment for Dexterous Force-Based Grasping and Manipulation

author.name: Zhe Zhao, Zhibin Li, Yilin Ou, Mengshi Qi

entry.summary: Presents a zero-shot sim-to-real RL method for dexterous force-based grasping that uses fast parallel-forward-kinematics tactile simulation and current-to-torque calibration to regulate physical interactions with dense tactile and joint-torque feedback. It aims to deploy contact-rich manipulation policies directly on real multi-finger hardware.

entry.title: Reinforcement Learning for Evidence-Seeking Diagnostic Reasoning with Large Language Models

author.name: Shengyi Hua, Kangzhe Hu, Conghui He, Xiaofan Zhang, Shaoting Zhang

entry.summary: This work formalizes medical diagnosis as an iterative evidence-seeking task and uses RL with verifiable rewards to train LLMs to actively acquire evidence in a closed loop, supported by a retrieval-augmented examination simulator (RAGES). It moves diagnostic reasoning from passive inference over complete information toward active investigation.

entry.title: Evaluating LLM Uncertainty in Long-Form Generation Using Deterministic Ground Truth

author.name: Ido Amit, Ido Galil, Ran El-Yaniv

entry.summary: SALT is a benchmark of six procedurally generated long-form tasks with single deterministic ground truths, enabling unit-level evaluation of correctness, calibration, and ranking without external judges or noisy labels. It is designed to make uncertainty estimation in long-form generation measurable at any resolution.

entry.title: LLM-as-a-Tutor: Policy-Aware Prompt Adaptation for Non-Verifiable RL

author.name: Yujin Kim, Namgyu Ho, Sangmin Hwang, Joonkee Kim, Yongjin Yang, Sangmin Bae, Seungone Kim, Jaehun Jung, Se-Young Yun, Hwanjun Song

entry.summary: LLM-as-a-Tutor extends the LLM's role from judge to tutor in non-verifiable-RL instruction following, adapting the training prompts (not just rubrics) to policy capability so that prompts elicit enough quality variance for a discriminative reward signal. It addresses the mismatch between static prompt difficulty and evolving policy ability.

entry.title: FORGE: Research-Trajectory Hijacking Attacks on Deep Research Agents

author.name: Yue Pan, Ziheng Zhang, Junxiang Lei, Changhao Jia, Qingyi Si, Hongcheng Guo

entry.summary: FORGE is a two-level attack on deep research agents combining intra-document reasoning fabrication with inter-document chain coordination to poison the retrieval pool and hijack subtask planning, turning local injections into report-level contamination. It also introduces the PRISM metric and Root Query Anchoring, a lightweight defense tying follow-up generation to the root query.

entry.title: Training Hybrid Block Diffusion Language Models with Partial Bidirectionality

author.name: Pranshu Chaturvedi, Parth Shroff, Tarun Suresh, Hangoo Kang, Kaiyue Wen

entry.summary: Trains hybrid block diffusion language models that combine linear-time Mamba mixing with block-parallel diffusion generation, using partial bidirectionality to resolve the incompatibility between bidirectional scans and causal KV caching. It targets high-throughput long-context generation that is memory-bandwidth-bound.

entry.title: Unbiased Alignment for Large Language Models with Noisy Preferences

author.name: Jialiang Wang, Xianming Liu, Xiong Zhou, Hui Liu, Haoliang Li

entry.summary: This paper presents a theoretical framework for unbiased LLM alignment under noisy preference data, introducing an Unbiased Reward Model loss and Unbiased DPO loss that mathematically correct noise-induced distortion without clean supervision. It provides theoretical guarantees of unbiased training.

entry.title: Spectral Signatures of Large Language Models

author.name: Zhuoying Zhang, Ishan V. Prasad, Yuanzhe Hu, Zihang Liu, Hengrui Luo, Pu Ren, Yaoqing Yang

entry.summary: This work adopts spectral shape-based metrics from Heavy-Tailed Self-Regularization theory to derive a compact spectral signature from each LLM's weight empirical spectral density, enabling scalable model lineage tracing, licensing, and evaluation without task-specific benchmarks. The signature is robust across post-training.

entry.title: Towards Diverse and Comprehensive Benchmarks for Mutual Information Estimation

author.name: Alberto Foresti, Ivan Butakov, Alexander Tolmachev, Giulio Franzese, Alexey Frolov, Pietro Michiardi

entry.summary: This work introduces a copula-theoretic benchmarking framework for mutual information estimation that spans varying dimensionality, marginal complexity, and real-world image data, subsuming prior benchmarks. It fills a gap where MI estimators were only tested on simple low-dimensional distributions.

entry.title: DualView: Preventing Indirect Prompt Injection in Personal AI Agents

author.name: Juhee Kim, Woohyuk Choi, Taehyun Kang, Youngmin Kim, Byoungyoung Lee

entry.summary: DualView defends personal AI agents against indirect prompt injection, identifying stored IPI where untrusted data saved and later reread by the agent returns as trusted rather than as a reference symbol. It extends Dual-LLM defenses to track untrusted data across persistent storage in the user's shared real environment.

entry.title: Signal or Noise? Understanding Generative Models for Real-World Sensor Time Series

author.name: Zitao Shuai, Zongzhe Xu, Yuntian Wu, Sirui Li, Tianhong Li, Yuzhe Yang

entry.summary: SensorGen is a large-scale study of generative models for real-world sensor time series spanning 14 settings, 4 domains, 7 datasets, and 12 modalities, systematically evaluating five model families. It clarifies when and why generative models succeed or fail on noisy continuous sensor data.

entry.title: evalci: A Python Library for Statistically Rigorous Comparison of Language Model Evaluations

author.name: Shreyas K Chandrahas

entry.summary: evalci is a pip-installable Python library packaging confidence intervals, paired significance tests, power analysis, clustered standard errors, and multiple-comparison correction over per-item LM evaluation result tables. It targets the common practice of declaring one accuracy number better without testing for sampling noise.

entry.title: Language Models Represent and Transform Concepts with Shared Geometry

author.name: Zhimin Hu, Lanhao Niu, Sashank Varma

entry.summary: This paper formalizes concept representations in language models as point-cloud manifolds and contextual transformations as vector fields, finding across six model families that context moves each concept differently in a semantically organized way correlating with concreteness and density, and that this structure is shared across models. It draws on neural population geometry to reconceive concepts as dynamic rather than stationary.

entry.title: Dashboard2Code: Evaluating Multimodal Models on Reconstructing Interactive Dashboards

author.name: Tianhao Niu, Ziyu Han, Qiguang Chen, Shiqi Zhou, Baocai Shan, Hengjie Fang, Qingfu Zhu, Wanxiang Che

entry.summary: Dashboard2Code introduces a task and benchmark (DashboardMimic, 180 Plotly+Dash pairs) requiring multimodal models to interactively explore dashboards, integrate feedback from clicking and filtering, and generate code that reproduces them. It extends visualization generation beyond static charts to interactive dashboards.

entry.title: Graph Representation Learning of Longitudinal Medical Imaging Trajectories for Treatment Response Prediction

author.name: Johannes Kiechle, Richard Osuala, Daniel M. Lang, Stefan M. Fischer, Ivana Jan\'i\v{c}kov\'a, Karim Lekadir, Julia A. Schnabel, Jan C. Peeken

entry.summary: This paper proposes a 3D spatio-temporal graph neural network with self-supervised trajectory representations for predicting pathological complete response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy in breast cancer from longitudinal medical imaging. It models temporal interactions across timepoints for individualized treatment response prediction.

entry.title: Localized LoRA-MoE: Block-wise Low-Rank Experts With Adaptive Routing

author.name: Babak Barazandeh, Subhabrata Majumdar, Vinay Prithyani, George Michailidis

entry.summary: Localized LoRA-MoE combines block-wise low-rank experts with adaptive routing to overcome LoRA's monolithic bottleneck and gradient warfare in interleaved multi-task streams, adding dynamic topology adaptation missing from static spatial-partitioning methods. It targets robust PEFT under task-switching and sensor failure.

entry.title: ProPS: Prompted Profile Synthesis for Natural Language-Conditioned Speaker Embedding Distributions

author.name: Thomas Thebaud, Junhyeok Lee, Laureano Moro-Velazquez, Jesus Villalba Lopez, Najim Dehak

entry.summary: ProPS (Prompted Profile Synthesis) generates distributions of speaker embeddings (x-vectors) conditioned on natural-language prompts by converting profile descriptions into sentence embeddings and using a mixture density network to predict a Gaussian mixture in x-vector space. It shifts speaker embedding extraction from descriptive to generative.

entry.title: Air Quality Downscaling with Station-Guided Pseudo-Supervision

author.name: Guorun Wang, Simone Foti, Andreas D. Demou, Leonidas Kotoulas, Theodoros Christoudias, Alexandros Koliousis, Mihalis Nicolaou, Stefanos Zafeiriou

entry.summary: This paper presents a station-guided pseudo-supervision framework for high-resolution PM2.5 downscaling over Europe, jointly super-resolving (x40, ~1km) and bias-correcting coarse CAMS fields using heterogeneous side information, while handling the mismatch between area-average pixels and discrete point observations. It avoids relying on temporal sequence modeling.

entry.title: Variable Bit-width Quantization: Learning Per-Group Precision for "Bigger-but-Smaller" Language Models

author.name: Hamish Ogilvy

entry.summary: Variable Bit-width Quantization (VBQ) learns per-group (64-weight) precision from {1,2,4,8} bits via Gumbel-Softmax during training, discovering strongly heterogeneous allocations such as 69% of groups collapsing to 1 bit while early MLP blocks retain higher precision. It shows precision need not be a single global hyperparameter.

entry.title: Rethinking AI-Generated Text Detection: A Strong Baseline and the Distribution-Shift Problem That Remains

author.name: Zhuoer Shen, Mingyi Wang, Shaofeng Zou, Yuheng Bu

entry.summary: This paper shows that a plainly fine-tuned RoBERTa matches or beats specialized AI-generated-text detectors across benchmarks, arguing much recent architectural complexity is unnecessary for in-distribution detection. The unsolved challenge is distribution shift, where the strong baseline degrades sharply and more source data does not close the gap.

entry.title: A Unified Framework for In-Context Learning with Causal and Masked Language Models

author.name: Chenrui Liu, Chuanlong Xie, Falong Tan, Yicheng Zeng, Lixing Zhu

entry.summary: This paper develops a unified statistical learning framework that places autoregressive (causal) and masked language-model pretraining within a common excess-risk analysis for in-context learning, representing context examples by their empirical measure. Under Wasserstein-type regularity it relates pretraining across tasks to ICL generalization.

entry.title: NKI-Agent: Domain-Specific Fine-Tuning and Agentic Tool Use for Neuron Kernel Generation

author.name: Junjie Tang, Jun Huan, Hao Zhou, Yuhao Zhang, Lin Wang
