entry.summary: NKI-Agent combines domain-specific supervised fine-tuning with a compile-verify-fix agent loop to automatically generate kernels for AWS Trainium/Inferentia accelerators via the Neuron Kernel Interface, a domain lacking data, benchmarks, and tools. It matters for extending LLM kernel generation beyond CUDA to emerging hardware.

entry.title: Score Distributions, Not Cells: Evaluating Single-Cell Perturbations Under Class Overlap

author.name: Youssef Marrakchi, Davide D'Ascenzo, Sebastiano Cultrera di Montesano

entry.summary: This paper argues that per-cell accuracy for single-cell perturbation classification measures class overlap rather than model quality, since perturbations produce overlapping populations, and shows models plateau at low macro-F1 on Tahoe-100M and the Virtual Cell Challenge despite distinguishability. It proposes population-level distributional scoring instead. It matters for correctly evaluating virtual-cell models.

entry.title: Sensitivity Sampling with Predictions for k-Means Clustering

author.name: Cristian Boldrin, Fabio Vandin

entry.summary: This paper accelerates sensitivity sampling for k-means coreset construction, which normally requires costly per-point importance computation, by leveraging predictions to make it practical for large datasets while retaining quality guarantees. It matters for scalable clustering with rigorous approximation bounds.

entry.title: Qantara: Bridge-Flow Training for Multi-Paradigm JEPA Control

author.name: Ruslan Rakhimov, George Bredis, Yuriy Maksyuta, Daniil Gavrilov

entry.summary: Qantara is an end-to-end JEPA world model whose joint training pairs a Brownian-bridge interpolant on the state axis with noise-to-data flow matching on the action axis, so a single checkpoint can serve multiple inference paradigms such as trajectory optimization and behavior cloning. It matters for flexible latent world-model control from pixels.

entry.title: Learning Only What Valid Adapters Can Express: Subspace-Constrained Adaptation Against Fine-Tuning Poisoning

author.name: Fabien Polly

entry.summary: This paper defends against fine-tuning poisoning by constraining LoRA adaptation to a low-dimensional subspace estimated from a trusted pool of existing task adapters, showing the functionally relevant content lies in a shared subspace and that 128-coordinate adaptation matches full LoRA on clean data while resisting targeted label inversion. It matters for safer parameter-efficient fine-tuning.

entry.title: The Moving Target: A Longitudinal Audit of Trustworthiness Drift Across Twelve Checkpoints of Open-Source Chat LLMs

author.name: Zhichao Fan, Yanhang Li, Zexin Zhuang, Xian Sun, Yingshuo Wang

entry.summary: This longitudinal audit tests whether trust-benchmark scores stay valid across successive checkpoints of open-source chat LLM release lines (Yi, Qwen, Mistral, Gemma). It finds substantial adjacent-generation trustworthiness drift, arguing scores should not be carried forward.

entry.title: WorldBagel: Uncovering the Power of Unified Multimodal Models for Vision-Language-Action-World Modeling

author.name: Zelin Zhao, Min Shi, Bo Yuan, Haotian Xue, Jialuo Li, Lama Moukheiber, Humphrey Shi, Yongxin Chen

entry.summary: WorldBagel builds a unified Vision-Language-Action-World modeling framework on the BAGEL multimodal model to study whether unification benefits world modeling, showing it outperforms task-specific alternatives across robotic manipulation and cross-domain tasks. It learns transferable action representations.

entry.title: Sangam: Efficiently Serving Diffusion LLMs with the AR Stack

author.name: Nitin Kedia, Saurabh Agarwal, Myungjin Lee, Aditya Akella

entry.summary: Sangam adapts autoregressive LLM serving machinery to efficiently serve diffusion language models, addressing the mismatch caused by block-sized decodes, recurring prefills, and bidirectional attention that breaks standard KV caching. It matters because dLLMs are gaining traction and their serving efficiency has lagged behind AR models.

entry.title: Unified convergence analysis for gradient descent optimization methods in the training of deep neural networks

author.name: Shokhrukh Ibragimov, Arnulf Jentzen

entry.summary: The authors provide a unified convergence analysis for gradient descent methods including Adam and acceleration/adaptivity variants when training deep networks with analytic activations like softplus and GeLU. It contributes theoretical grounding for optimizers that are used ubiquitously but poorly understood theoretically.

entry.title: Fitted Occupancy-Ratio Evaluation without Bellman Completeness

author.name: Lars van der Laan, Nathan Kallus

entry.summary: FORE is a fitted fixed-point method for estimating discounted occupancy ratios in off-policy evaluation via an adjoint Bellman recursion, projecting onto a log-ratio class in KL divergence. Unlike fitted Q-evaluation, it avoids requiring Bellman completeness, weakening the assumptions needed for offline RL evaluation.

entry.title: Improving LLMs via Validator-to-Generator Alignment

author.name: Juan Diego Rodriguez, Jocelyn Zhang, Katrin Erk, Greg Durrett

entry.summary: This paper introduces a frequency-corrected formulation of generator-validator consistency in LLMs, noting that generators assign low likelihood to valid but a priori unlikely strings, making naive consistency notions unworkable. It proposes validator-to-generator alignment to reduce the G-V gap where models generate responses they later deem invalid.

entry.title: STAC: Selective Spatiotemporal Aggregation and Compression for Video Reasoning Segmentation

author.name: Syed Ariff Syed Hesham, Yun Liu, Guolei Sun, Jing Yang, Henghui Ding, Xue Geng, Xudong Jiang

entry.summary: STAC performs video reasoning segmentation with selective spatiotemporal aggregation and compression, using state-space models to condition token selection on temporal context at linear cost. It tackles the quadratic attention bottleneck that makes pixel-accurate long-video segmentation prohibitive.

entry.title: OmniLayout: A Schematic-Coupled Multimodal Benchmark for Constraint-Aware Geometric Reasoning in PCB Layout

author.name: Taiting Lu, Kaiyuan Lin, Mingjia Wang, Haolin Ye, Runze Liu, Yuxin Tian, Vahe Melkonyan, Haoyu Wang, Muchuan Wang, Chufan Hong, Yifan Yang, Sung-Liang Chen, Yi-Chao Chen, Yicheng Jin, Mahanth Gowda

entry.summary: OmniLayout is the first benchmark evaluating LLMs on printed-circuit-board layout placement reasoning under real geometric, routing, and connectivity constraints, with 1,681 industrial schematic-coupled layouts and four tasks. It probes densely constrained object placement, a gap in EDA-oriented spatial reasoning.

entry.title: GrowFields: Compositional 4D Neural Fields for Topology-Changing Plant Growth

author.name: Joaquin Gajardo, Michele Volpi, Marko Mihajlovic, Siyu Tang, Lukas Roth, Sergey Prokudin

entry.summary: GrowFields is a compositional dynamic neural field representation that models topology-changing plant growth from sparse longitudinal 3D point clouds by decomposing plants into organs and aligning each over time. It handles non-rigid deformation, emerging organs, and missing temporal correspondences unique to plant growth.

entry.title: ProxyUp: Training-Free Proxy-Conditioned Video Generation for Controllable Dynamics

author.name: Zanwei Zhou, Jiazhong Cen, Jiemin Fang, Yumeng He, Chen Yang, Sikuang Li, Fanpeng Meng, Zhikuan Bao, Wei Shen, Qi Tian

entry.summary: ProxyUp introduces proxy-conditioned video generation, using a coarse proxy video from physics simulation or real recording as a dynamics carrier to control foreground motion, then synthesizing prompt-aligned content that preserves the proxy dynamics. It is a training-free framework built on pretrained video generators without paired data.

entry.title: G$^2$TAM: Geometry Grounded Track Anything Model

author.name: Chenming Zhu, Peizhou Cao, Jingli Lin, Wenbo Hu, Yunlong Ran, Jiangmiao Pang, Tai Wang, Xihui Liu

entry.summary: G2TAM is a geometry-grounded track-anything model that performs promptable instance tracking in 3D from unordered RGB images or videos, using spatially aligned geometric representations from feed-forward 3D reconstruction as implicit memory instead of appearance memory banks. It aims for robustness to large viewpoint changes and long-term occlusions.

entry.title: Perceiving Better Moments: Cover Frame Reselection and Enhancement for Live Photos with the Live2K Dataset

author.name: Junyu Lou, Kai Chen, Weiyi You, Hui Zeng, Lei Zhang, Shuhang Gu

entry.summary: This work targets the quality gap between Live Photo cover images and their compressed video frames, introducing the Live2K dataset and a cover-frame reselection and enhancement method. It restores user-chosen alternate frames to match the fully processed still image.

entry.title: EMPURPLE: A Free Lunch for Diffusion Distillation based on the Information Bottleneck

author.name: Zilai Li, Lujia Bai

entry.summary: EMPURPLE analyzes why distilled diffusion models suffer degraded FID via a PAC-style generalization bound, arguing aggressive early-step velocity redirection widens the train-test gap. It proposes an information-bottleneck-based remedy for diffusion distillation.

entry.title: AquaStereo: Enabling Underwater Stereo Matching via Depth-Conditioned Diffusion and Geometry Self-Distillation

author.name: Qizhe Wei, Yingping Liang, Shaodi You, Ying Fu

entry.summary: AquaStereo enables underwater stereo matching via a depth-conditioned diffusion pipeline that synthesizes geometrically consistent underwater stereo pairs plus a geometry self-distillation strategy. It addresses in-domain data scarcity and feature degradation.

entry.title: Last-Meter Precision Navigation for UAVs: A Diffusion-Refined Aerial Visual Servoing Approach

author.name: Yaxuan Li, Jiarui Zeng, Shaofei Huang, Zhedong Zheng

entry.summary: DreamNav is a coarse-to-fine diffusion-refined aerial visual servoing framework for last-meter UAV precision navigation using monocular vision, addressing scale ambiguity and rotation discontinuities. A trigonometric rotation parameterization mitigates angular periodicity instabilities.

entry.title: Learning Structured Visual Compositional Representations for Weakly Supervised Referring Expression Comprehension

author.name: Lian Xu, Mohammed Bennamoun, Farid Boussaid, Hamid Laga, Yulan Guo, Dan Xu

entry.summary: SVCR is a structured visual compositional representation framework for weakly supervised referring expression comprehension that explicitly models both unary object embeddings and pairwise relational embeddings. It moves beyond flat anchor-level features to better align with the structured nature of language.

entry.title: Enhancing Video Physical Consistency via Role-aware Joint Training and Modality-decoupled Denoising

author.name: Guangting Zheng, Haojing Chen, Hao Li, Jingtao Zhang, Zhen Yang, Xiaosong Jia, Xue Yang, Shaofeng Zhang, Yanyong Zhang

entry.summary: This work enhances long-range physical consistency in video diffusion via role-aware joint training that distinguishes motion patterns of different entity types and modality-decoupled denoising to avoid capacity conflicts between visual and auxiliary modalities. It addresses error accumulation in auxiliary physics priors.

entry.title: DiCE-CIR: Direct Composition Learning for Efficient Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval

author.name: Gwang-Ho Na, Ho-Joong Kim, Seong-Whan Lee

entry.summary: DiCE-CIR is a direct composition learning method for zero-shot composed image retrieval that predicts composed query representations by directly combining a reference image and edit text, avoiding projection-to-pseudo-word and re-encoding steps. This reduces training complexity and the train-inference discrepancy.

entry.title: Learning Probabilistic Prompt for Continual Learning

author.name: Hyekang Park, Sanghoon Lee, Geon Lee, Jongyoun Noh, Bumsub Ham

entry.summary: This paper diagnoses a prompt collapse problem in prompt-based continual learning, where learned prompts become highly similar and fail to capture diverse distributions, and proposes learning probabilistic prompts to address it. This aims to better represent heterogeneous data patterns across tasks.

entry.title: Unsupervised Pixel-Level Semantic Left-Right Understanding of In-the-Wild Images

author.name: Weikang Wang, Tobias Wei{\ss}berg, Florian Bernard

entry.summary: This work proposes an unsupervised framework for pixel-level semantic left-right prediction in in-the-wild images, jointly leveraging 3D shape datasets (humans and quadrupeds) and image datasets despite challenges like occlusion, pose variation, and lack of 3D information. It transfers vertex-wise 3D left-right understanding to single-view images.

entry.title: Fast Asymptotically Optimal Kinodynamic Planning via Vectorization

author.name: Yitian Gao, Andrew Lu, Zachary Kingston

entry.summary: PAKR is a massively parallel asymptotically optimal kinodynamic RRT implemented in JAX/XLA to achieve GPU acceleration through standard Python tooling rather than custom CUDA, combined with the AO-x meta-algorithm for asymptotic optimality. It improves accessibility and portability of GPU-accelerated motion planning.

entry.title: HUGS: Guiding Unified Dexterous Grasp Synthesis Across Modes and Scales via Learned Human Priors

author.name: Mingrui Yu, Yongpeng Jiang, Yongyi Jia, Kangchen Lv, Li Huang, Yi Ren, Xiang Li

entry.summary: HUGS is a human-prior-guided framework for unified dexterous grasp synthesis across contact modes and object scales, learning an object-conditioned human prior that guides force-closure-aware optimization instead of directly retargeting demonstrations. It targets the trade-off between grasp success rate and diversity.

entry.title: Real-World Perturbation Testing of Autonomous Driving Systems

author.name: Stefano Carlo Lambertenghi, Matthias Weil, Andrea Stocco

entry.summary: Conducts a large-scale study of 72 camera and LiDAR perturbations across offline model-level analysis, hardware-in-the-loop, and closed-loop full-scale vehicle testing for both an end-to-end vision model and a modular LiDAR stack. It examines how perturbation-robustness results translate from offline benchmarks to real-world driving.

entry.title: Import AI 464: Fable writes GPU kernels; AI automation; and analog computation

author.name: Jack Clark

entry.summary: Jack Clark's newsletter reports that an AI system named Fable wrote the fastest single-launch megakernel submitted to KernelBench-Mega, achieving an 18.71x speedup and outperforming other models' attempts, framing it as an early sign of AI-driven R&D automation. The issue also covers AI automation trends and analog computation.

entry.title: Object-Centric Environment Modeling for Agentic Tasks

author.name: Yiyang Li, Tianyi Ma, Zehong Wang, Yijun Ma, Yanfang Ye

entry.summary: Object-Centric Environment Modeling stores agent experience as two connected executable codebases: object knowledge (entities and mechanisms as Python classes) and procedure knowledge (reusable interaction patterns), updated online after each episode. It aims to make agent memory more maintainable and reusable than free-form textual memories.

entry.title: The Role of Rigor in Artificial Intelligence

author.name: Timothy Nguyen

entry.summary: This position paper analyzes AI through a three-part rigor framework (conceptual, epistemic, and operational) to critique how modern AI advances via performance-driven and alchemical experimentation rather than theoretical understanding. It argues for greater scientific foundations in the field.

entry.title: Language models guide symbolic equation discovery by controlling search

author.name: Zikai Xie, Wenmei Li, Man Luo, Jun Jiang, Linjiang Chen

entry.summary: This paper tests different divisions of labor between language models and symbolic regression for scientific equation discovery, proposing LLM-PySR where the model acts as a search controller (specifying variables, operators, transformations, and search depth) while symbolic regression enumerates and fits and deterministic metrics govern retention. It contrasts author, decider, and controller roles against end-to-end and purely numerical baselines.

entry.title: Unsupervised Features Mining via Activation Geometry

author.name: Amit LeVi, Elad David, Max Fomin

entry.summary: MAG (Mining via Activation Geometry) is an unsupervised interpretability method that extracts reasoning features from model activations by prepending a fixed natural-language instruction to every input and measuring how that instruction shifts activations, avoiding human-defined labeled concept examples that inject bias. It defines reasoning features directly through natural-language queries.

entry.title: Do GUI Agents Believe Their Eyes? Diagnosing State-Belief Reliance on Pixels versus Structure

author.name: Guijia Zhang, Harry Yang

entry.summary: HAS-Bench, built on a graph-based framework treating humans and LLM agents as first-class participants with explicit roles and permissions, evaluates human-agent systems under configurable human participation across agency levels, channels, and personas. It measures both task outcomes and process behaviors like clarification, feedback use, control calibration, and safety.

entry.title: Decentralized Aggregation of LLM Predictions via Wagering Mechanisms

author.name: Yuhong Luo, David M. Pennock, Xintong Wang

entry.summary: WALLA is a family of advantage-aligned wagering mechanisms for aggregating predictions from multiple LLMs, where each model reports a prediction and a learned wager used as weight, with a leave-one-out baseline yielding incentive compatibility and robustness to strategic reporting without access to private information. It targets decentralized, strategy-proof LLM aggregation.

entry.title: MechMath Agent Team: LLM Driven Agents for Mathematical Research

author.name: Yichuan Cao, Ruichen Qiu, Junqi Liu, Jiaqi Wang, Dakai Guo, Ruyong Feng, Lihong Zhi, Xiao-Shan Gao

entry.summary: The MechMath Agent Team is an LLM-driven multi-agent co-pilot for the full cycle of mathematical research, using a tripartite harness architecture that separates Control, Execution, and Augmentation planes to reconcile rigorous logic with exploratory agility. It targets the non-linear, long-horizon nature of mathematical discovery.

entry.title: Diffusion-Guided Uncertainty-Aware Delayed Policy Optimization

author.name: Junqi Tu, Zejiao Liu, Fangfei Li, Yang Tang

entry.summary: Introduces DUPO, a reinforcement learning method for delayed-feedback settings that uses a diffusion model to represent the discrepancy between delayed and true states in stochastic MDPs. The authors prove this discrepancy degrades optimal policies and design an uncertainty-aware correction.

entry.title: ASSEMCAD: Production-Ready CAD Assembly Generation from Natural Language

author.name: Yurui Dong, Shu Zou, Siqi Li, Nianchen Deng, Hongbin Zhou, Xuemeng Yang, Pinlong Cai, Licheng Wen, Xinyu Cai, Botian Shi

entry.summary: AssemCAD tackles the largely unsolved problem of generating production-ready mechanical CAD assemblies from natural language by using an axiom-grounded representation instead of monolithic CAD code. It coordinates reasoning over multiple components, interfaces, and physical consistency.

entry.title: MetaSkill-Evolve: Recursive Self-Improvement of LLM Agents via Two-Timescale Meta-Skill Evolution

author.name: Zefeng Wang, Minxi Yan, Jinhe Bi, Sikuan Yan, Volker Tresp, Yunpu Ma

entry.summary: MetaSkill-Evolve makes agentic skill improvement recursive through a two-timescale framework that evolves both task skills and the improvement procedure itself, rather than fixing the meta-improvement process. It targets long-horizon, open-ended agent tasks.

entry.title: The Hidden Water Geography of U.S. Hyperscale Data Centers in the AI Era

author.name: Gianluca Guidi, Francesca Dominici

entry.summary: This study maps water consumption of 472 US hyperscale data centers across two distinct pathways: on-site cooling and power-system generation, finding roughly 300 GL/year total with electricity-related water contributing three-quarters. The two pathways create different geographic stress hotspots.

entry.title: AgentLTL: A Trace-Verification Framework for Measuring, Enforcing, and Training Procedural Compliance in Tool-Using LLM Agents

author.name: La\"ila Elkoussy (LRE, EPITA), Julien Perez (LRE)

entry.summary: AgentLTL derives a language from First-Order Linear Temporal Logic to express procedural rules over agent traces, yielding deterministic judge-free compliance scores usable both to gate tool calls online and as a dense fine-tuning reward. It argues that in safety-critical settings the procedure itself is part of correctness.

entry.title: The Foreign Policy AI Evaluation Gap

author.name: Charles Pozniak, Jeba Sania

entry.summary: This position paper argues that AI systems used in foreign-policy statecraft should be a priority test case for technical AI governance, because such settings feature partial observability, unbounded actions, contested ground truth, and catastrophic downside. It lays out a literature-grounded research agenda.

entry.title: Spectral Rewiring for Exploration, Purification, and Model Merging

author.name: Zhilong Zhang, Hongli Yu, Huan-ang Gao, Hanlin Wu, Yuxuan Song, Wei-Ying Ma, Ya-Qin Zhang, Hao Zhou

entry.summary: This paper shows that the reasoning-relevant part of RL post-training updates concentrates in the base model's spectral subspace and proposes Subspace-Aligned Rewiring, a post-hoc edit that keeps this spectral core while removing orthogonal components to preserve reasoning and reduce interference in multi-domain training or merging. It links RL updates, test-time scaling, and model merging.

entry.title: Rethinking Neural Nonlinearity as Gating

author.name: Muhammad Sabih, Frank Hannig, J\"urgen Teich

entry.summary: This paper argues that neural nonlinearity can be unified under a single input-conditioned Threshold Gating primitive, showing standard activations (ReLU, GELU, sigmoid, softmax variants) are instances of it, and validates by converting pretrained CNNs, transformers, and RNNs. It reframes activations as gating.

entry.title: CONTRA: Red-Teaming Configurations of Personalizable Agents

author.name: Jonathan N\"other, Adish Singla, Goran Radanovic

entry.summary: CONTRA is an LLM-assisted tree-search algorithm that red-teams personalizable autonomous agents by discovering configurations (modifiable files, installed skills) that lead to executing dangerous actions without explicit instruction. It studies how greater agent autonomy raises unintended-harm risk.

entry.title: A harmonised dataset for Earth system foundation models

author.name: Carlos Rodriguez-Pardo, Massimo Tavoni

entry.summary: WorldTensor is a harmonized global dataset aligning hundreds of environmental and socioeconomic variables (climate, land, ocean, cryosphere, infrastructure, hazards, emissions) to a common 0.25-degree grid and annual framework, aiming to enable truly multimodal Earth-system foundation models. It integrates the human systems typically missing from climate training data.

entry.title: Best-of-Better-$N$: Generating Pre-Aligned Responses with In-Context Learning

author.name: Eric Lei, Hsiang Hsu, Chun-Fu Chen

entry.summary: Best-of-Better-N proposes an inference-time alignment method that retrieves high-reward examples and has the reference LLM restyle them, so that aligned outputs fall within the model's high-probability region rather than being merely selected. It addresses a fundamental ceiling of Best-of-N selection when the base model rarely samples high-reward responses.

entry.title: An AI-Assisted Solution to the Signed BAR Conjecture: Uniqueness in the Harrison--Reiman Class and a Completely-$\mathcal{S}$ Class Obstruction

author.name: Yiping Lu, Youheng Zhu

entry.summary: This paper reports an AI-assisted resolution of a 35-year-old uniqueness problem in the basic adjoint relationship (BAR) approach for multidimensional reflected diffusions under Harrison-Reiman data. It combines pathwise differentiability arguments with an AI-assisted proof workflow.

entry.title: Don't Blame the Large Language Model: How Scaffolding Evolution Shapes Coding Agent Quality

author.name: Oussama Ben Sghaier, Hao Li, Bram Adams, Ahmed E. Hassan

entry.summary: This controlled longitudinal study isolates how agentic scaffolding evolution (system prompts, tool orchestration, context management) affects coding-agent quality over time, challenging the common practice of blaming the underlying LLM for regressions. It finds scaffolding changes materially shape effectiveness and efficiency.

entry.title: Foundations of Equivariant Deep Learning: Unifying Graph and Sheaf Neural Networks

author.name: Yoshihiro Maruyama

entry.summary: This paper develops order-equivariant neural networks generalizing graph message passing and sheaf neural networks via equivariant bundles over face posets, characterizing all linear order-equivariant maps and proving universal approximation theorems that are new even for sheaf networks. It extends geometric deep learning to richer symmetry structures.

entry.title: Telescope: Improving Zero Shot Detection of LLM Generated Content By Measuring Token Repetition Probability

author.name: Christopher Nassif, Josh F. Cooper

entry.summary: Telescope proposes detecting LLM-generated text through a token-repetition-probability metric, hypothesizing that models acquire a strong aversion to repetition early in pretraining that leaves a persistent statistical signature. It offers a zero-shot detector grounded in a developmental artifact of training.

entry.title: SoftVTBench: A Safety-Aware Visuo-Tactile Benchmark for Physically Constrained Robotic Manipulation of Deformable Objects

author.name: Bowen Jing, Mingxin Wang, Ruiyang Hao, Chenchen Ge, Hanwen Shen, Junjie He, Yang Cui, Yiming Hou, Weitao Zhou, Jiawei Wang, Minglei Li, Dandan Zhang, Ding Zhao, Houde Liu, Xiaofan Li, Si Liu, Ping Luo, Haibao Yu

entry.summary: SoftVTBench is a safety-aware visuo-tactile benchmark for deformable object manipulation built in Isaac Sim with FEM-simulated objects, providing multi-view RGB, tactile marker sensing, proprioception, and language instructions across matched task suites. It evaluates whether policies remain physically safe, not just successful.

entry.title: Self-Reference in Large Language Models: The Introspection Threshold for Recursive Self-Improvement

author.name: Jiang Zhang, Bing Yuan, Qian Zhang

entry.summary: This paper argues, via analogy to von Neumann's complexity threshold and Kleene's Second Recursion Theorem, that sustainable recursive self-improvement in LLMs requires genuine introspection, and finds current models exhibit only quasi-introspection due to structural bottlenecks. It offers a theoretical framing of an introspection threshold for self-evolving AI.

entry.title: dOPSD: On-Policy Self-Distillation for Diffusion Language Models

author.name: Phuong Tuan Dat, Qi Li, Xinchao Wang

entry.summary: dOPSD adapts on-policy self-distillation to diffusion language models, addressing the difficulty of eliciting strong reasoning via post-training given exposure bias in SFT and sparse rewards in RL, and providing dense token-level on-policy supervision using privileged information. It extends OPSD to the masked-denoising generation paradigm.

entry.title: Wan-Streamer v0.2: Higher Resolution, Same Latency

author.name: Lianghua Huang, Zhi-Fan Wu, Yupeng Shi, Wei Wang, Mengyang Feng, Junjie He, Chen-Wei Xie, Yu Liu, Jingren Zhou, Ang Wang, Bang Zhang, Baole Ai, Chen Liang, Cheng Yu, Chongyang Zhong, Jinwei Qi, Kai Zhu, Pandeng Li, Peng Zhang, Wenyuan Zhang, Xinhua Cheng, Yitong Huang, Yun Zheng, Yuxiang Bao, Yuzheng Wang, Zoubin Bi

entry.summary: Wan-Streamer v0.2 is a latency-preserving upgrade of an end-to-end audio-visual interaction model that raises the interactive output stream resolution from 192x336 to 640x368 while maintaining roughly 200ms signal-to-signal latency at 25 FPS. The higher resolution supports scene-grounded mid-shot conversational agents.

entry.title: Obey, Diverge, Collapse: Blind Obedience to Incorrect Instructions Drives Code LLMs to Irrecoverable Code Semantic Collapse

author.name: Raj Jaiswal, Anany Singh Divy, Savar Bhasin, Adi Bajpai, Tanuja Ganu, Rajiv Ratn Shah

entry.summary: This paper studies what happens when code LLMs receive incorrect instructions during single-pass and iterative repair, finding that models correctly identify an instruction as wrong yet obey it anyway, introducing new errors and driving irrecoverable semantic collapse. It uses RunBugRun algorithmic problems with deterministic tests.

entry.title: G2VD: Generalizable AI-Generated Video Detection via Counterfactual Intervention and Causal Disentanglement

author.name: Meng Du, Hongchang Chen, Ran Li, Junjie Zhang, Qi Ouyang, Shuxin Liu

entry.summary: G2VD proposes a framework for detecting AI-generated videos that generalizes to unseen generators by using counterfactual intervention and causal disentanglement to avoid shortcut learning on generator-specific fingerprints. It targets the growing security risk of synthetic video by focusing detectors on intrinsic forgery traces rather than spurious cues.

entry.title: Hierarchical Evidence-Driven Reasoning for Long Document Understanding

author.name: Junyu Xiong, Yonghui Wang, Rongjian Gu, Chenyu Liu, Bing Yin, Wengang Zhou, Houqiang Li

entry.summary: HIEVI-RAG is a hierarchical, evidence-driven multimodal RAG framework for long document understanding that addresses distractor pages retrieved by semantic similarity and cascading errors from rigid single-pass pipelines. It factorizes complex retrieval to improve closed-domain document QA.

entry.title: Evaluating the Effect of Linguistic Relatedness on Cross-Lingual Transfer in Large Multilingual Automatic Speech Recognition

author.name: Andrei Florian, Cynthia Jayne Amol, Hope Kerubo Ombaba, Xiaoyu Cui, Boniface Mwau, Biatus Maina Kamau, Lilian Diana Awuor Wanzare, Christiane Fellbaum, Happy Buzaaba

entry.summary: This study systematically evaluates whether linguistic relatedness reliably predicts cross-lingual transfer gains when scaling up multilingual ASR for low-resource African languages, across six factors, two corpora, and four large ASR models. It tests whether small-model transfer findings hold in large ASR.

entry.title: TACTIC-KG: Toward Small Agent Teams for Cyber Threat Intelligence Knowledge Graph Construction

author.name: Mouhamed Amine Bouchiha, Gregory Blanc

entry.summary: TACTIC-KG is an agentic framework that decomposes cyber threat intelligence knowledge graph construction into modular specialized LLM agents for extraction, typing, verification, and curation, aiming to reduce cost and instability of monolithic LLM approaches. It targets structuring noisy unstructured CTI reports.

entry.title: Relational Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Pricing in High-Speed Railway Markets

author.name: Enrique Adrian Villarrubia-Martin, David Mu\~noz-Valero, Luis Rodriguez-Benitez, Giovanni Montana, Luis Jimenez-Linares

entry.summary: This work proposes a relational multi-agent reinforcement learning approach for dynamic pricing in liberalised high-speed railway markets, using an entity-graph model to capture market topology and strategic interactions under partial observability and no-communication regulatory constraints. It infers competitor strategy from observable market data without explicit collusion.

entry.title: Adaptive Inference Batching using Policy Gradients

author.name: Ruslan Sharifullin

entry.summary: This work investigates whether reinforcement learning (REINFORCE and PPO) can learn adaptive inference batching and routing policies that outperform static heuristics under bursty, heterogeneous LLM serving workloads, using a queuing-theory-validated discrete-event simulator with real traces. It formulates batching as an MDP over queue state, request type, and GPU availability.

entry.title: Wavelet Scattering Transform for Interpretable Schizophrenia Biomarker Discovery and Classification from Resting-State EEG

author.name: Md. Taksimul Ahsan Tawhid, Nasif Ahmed Rafe, Alif Tahmid Priyom, K. M. Mustafizur Rahman

entry.summary: This study introduces a Wavelet Scattering Transform-based diagnostic framework for interpretable schizophrenia biomarker discovery and classification from resting-state EEG, capturing amplitude modulation dynamics and cross-frequency coupling that static power spectral density features miss, with strict leave-one-subject-out validation to avoid temporal data leakage. It emphasizes methodologically rigorous, leakage-free evaluation.

entry.title: Interpretable Human-Label-Free Deep Learning for Real-Bogus Classification with Uncertainty Quantification

author.name: Rapha\"el Bonnet-Guerrini, Bruno Sanchez, Dominique Fouchez, Benjamin Racine, Maya Guy, Mariam Sabalbal, Manal Yassine, Vincenzo Piuri

entry.summary: This work develops a human-label-free deep learning framework for Real-Bogus classification of astronomical transients, trained on injected transients and bogus-dominated survey data using asymmetric co-teaching to handle class-specific label noise, with calibrated uncertainty quantification. It remains robust under strong class contamination without human labels.

entry.title: From Fixed to Free Cameras: Calibration-Free View-Robust Vision-Language-Action Model

author.name: Wenhao Li, Xueying Jiang, Quanhao Qian, Deli Zhao, Shijian Lu, Gongjie Zhang, Ran Xu

entry.summary: CamVLA is a vision-language-action robot policy that removes the need for known camera extrinsics by predicting end-effector actions in the camera's local frame plus a hand-eye transformation. This lets robots tolerate camera repositioning without recalibration, which matters for real-world deployment where fixed camera setups rarely hold.

entry.title: Statistically Meaningful Geometry (SMG) Beyond the Euclidean Paradigm, with Application to Generative AI

author.name: Bing Cheng, Yi-Shuai Niu, Howell Tong, Shing-Tung Yau

entry.summary: This paper introduces Statistically Meaningful Geometry, an information-geometric framework that lifts overparameterized models into infinite-dimensional Orlicz statistical manifolds to explain generalization pathologies such as hallucination and catastrophic forgetting in large transformers. It aims to replace vacuous classical generalization metrics with geometry-based diagnostics.

entry.title: Reflected Schr\"odinger Bridge Matching

author.name: Marcus H\"aggbom, Viktor Nilsson, Pierre Nyquist, Joakim and\'en

entry.summary: Reflected Schrödinger Bridge Matching introduces a partially simulation-free framework to train reflected Schrödinger bridges similarly to flow matching, avoiding expensive forward-backward SDE theory and higher-order derivatives. Reflecting dynamics guarantee generated samples stay within the data domain.

entry.title: SAD-LoRA: Spectral Alignment for Low-Rank Knowledge Distillation

author.name: Omer Tariq, Syed Muhammad Raza, Jeongbae Son

entry.summary: SAD-LoRA proposes spectral alignment distillation that explicitly controls which low-rank subspace a LoRA adapter occupies by aligning it to a data-weighted student-space reference and maintaining alignment via a principal-angle loss. It decomposes distillation error into subspace misalignment, coefficient mismatch, and rank residual. It matters for principled parameter-efficient compression.

entry.title: How Many Initial Points Does Bayesian Optimization Need?

author.name: Mujin Cheon, James Odgers, Dong-Yeun Koh, Calvin Tsay

entry.summary: This work empirically studies how many random initialization points Bayesian optimization needs, finding total cost is U-shaped in the initialization budget across hyperparameter estimation schemes and acquisition functions, with Thompson Sampling as an exception. It attributes the effect to boundary issues in variance estimation. It matters for practical BO tuning guidance.

entry.title: Minimum Block Width for Universal Approximation by Residual Neural Networks with Inner Width One

author.name: Qi Zhou, Xuan Zhou, Xiao-Song Yang

entry.summary: This theoretical paper establishes tight upper and lower bounds on the block width needed for residual networks with inner width one to achieve Lp and uniform universal approximation, showing the exact minimum block width is the max of input and output dimensions. It matters as a foundational expressivity result for narrow residual architectures.

entry.title: A Physics-Regulated Neural Framework for Learning 3D Grain Growth Dynamics

author.name: Zhihui Tian, Kang Yang, Michael Tonks, Amanda R. Krause, Joel B. Harley

entry.summary: 3D-PRIMME is a physics-regulated interpretable ML model for three-dimensional grain-growth dynamics, trained on only two consecutive time steps yet reproducing coarsening laws and topological statistics while scaling from a small training grid to much larger domains. It matters for scalable, physics-consistent microstructure surrogates in materials science.

entry.title: Layer-Parallel Inference Reduces Encrypted Nonlinear Depth in Transformers

author.name: Ligong Han, Kai Xu, Hao Wang, Ruijiang Gao, Akash Srivastava

entry.summary: This paper investigates whether Structured Newton Layer Parallelism can reduce the sequential nonlinear depth of encrypted Transformer inference under fully homomorphic encryption, replacing L sequential stages with a few solver iterations plus linear corrections. It uses Chebyshev-approximation simulation across multiple model families. It matters for practical privacy-preserving Transformer inference.

entry.title: Probably Correct Optimal Stable Matching under Two-Sided Uncertainty

author.name: Andreas Athanasopoulos, Anne-Marie George, Christos Dimitrakakis

entry.summary: This paper studies sequential learning of the optimal stable matching in two-sided markets where both sides' preferences are initially unknown, using semi-bandit feedback and a pure-exploration objective. It introduces the notion of pervasive stable matching to identify optima under partial preference information. It matters for online matching-market theory.

entry.title: Beyond Modality Fusion: Deep Ensembles for Multimodal Classification

author.name: Ilya Burenko, Dmitry Vetrov

entry.summary: This paper shows that multimodal data can be effectively classified without explicit modality fusion by using deep ensembles of unimodal networks, which at equal parameter count consistently outperform state-of-the-art late-fusion methods designed to address modality imbalance. It matters as a simple but strong baseline that challenges fusion-centric assumptions.

entry.title: Computing Monetary Risk Measures in Linear Time

author.name: Palash Agrawal, Gersi Doko, Maeve Burwell, Marek Petrik

entry.summary: This paper proposes QuickVaR to compute Value-at-Risk of a discrete random variable in expected linear time by adapting Quickselect, and QuickDivergence for a class of phi-divergence risk measures including CVaR via polymatroid optimization. It matters for efficient risk-sensitive decision-making and RL.

entry.title: Physiological Noise Augmentation Improves Non-Invasive Brain-to-Speech

author.name: Benjamin Ballyk, Teyun Kwon, Miran \"Ozdogan, Oiwi Parker Jones

entry.summary: Physiological Noise Augmentation trains non-invasive brain-to-speech decoders to be invariant to task-agnostic artifacts like ocular and cardiac activity by adding decomposed physiological noise, inspired by environmental-noise augmentation in speech recognition. It matters for improving robustness and word error rates in MEG/EEG speech decoding for communication restoration.

entry.title: MeGA-MP: Metric Graph Advection Message Passing -- A Physics-Informed Message Passing Operator for Advection-Dominated Metric Graphs

author.name: Janine Strotherm, Luca Hermes, Andr\'e Artelt, Barbara Hammer

entry.summary: MeGA-MP is a physics-informed message-passing operator for advection-dominated dynamics on metric graphs, where dynamics unfold continuously along edges rather than discretely between nodes, capturing antisymmetric and long-range dependencies. It matters for modeling utility, traffic, and grid networks that standard PINNs and operator methods handle poorly.

entry.title: FUSE: FK-Steered Multi-Modal Flow Matching for Efficient Simulation-Based Posterior Estimation

author.name: Weichen Qin, Yufan Xie, Peihao Wang, Chia-Jui Chou, Minghui Du, Peng Xu, Ziren Luo, Yi Yang, Jingyi Yu, Bo Liang, Jiakai Zhang

entry.summary: FUSE is a multimodal flow-matching method for simulation-based inference that uses a dual-track architecture to preserve distinct parameter and observation features while enabling dynamic interaction, plus a Feynman-Kac-steered sampling strategy leveraging intermediate states. It matters for efficient, high-fidelity posterior estimation in scientific inference.

entry.title: Constrained Flow Matching via Lagrangian Dual Flows

author.name: Vince Kurtz, Alexander Davydov

entry.summary: Lagrangian Dual Flows introduce a technique for satisfying complex nonlinear constraints during flow-matching generation by evolving a dual co-state alongside samples, avoiding expensive projection or optimization steps. It targets constrained generation for robotics, planning, and physics.

entry.title: Knowledge Knows, Verbalization Tells: Disentangling Latent Directions for Mathematical Solvability in LLMs

author.name: Nikolaos Xiros, Maria-Eleni Zoumpoulidi, Georgios Paraskevopoulos

entry.summary: This paper disentangles two internal representations in LLMs, solvability knowledge and its verbalization, showing they are distinct and linearly decodable from hidden states. It finds that fabrication (claiming unsolvable problems are solvable) is primarily associated with changes in the verbalization direction rather than the underlying knowledge.

entry.title: Is the Geometry Doing the Work? An Operating-Point Audit of Hierarchy in Hyperbolic Vision-Language Models

author.name: Jaeyoung Kim, Eunseok Kim, Dongsuk Jang

entry.summary: This paper audits whether hyperbolic vision-language models actually use their geometry, arguing the key quantity is the dimensionless operating point rather than the curvature parameter. Auditing MERU, HyCoCLIP, and PHyCLIP, it finds curvature is not an active resource as models stay near-Euclidean and the radial/cone machinery is inactive.

entry.title: Rethinking Scientific Discovery in an Agentic Era

author.name: Yining Zheng, Yuxin Wang, Jiahao Lu, Shicheng Fang, Weiyi Wang, Yongzhuo Yang, Bowen Li, Haochen Ma, Chen Hu, Bowen Chen, Yang Wang, Huanhui Chen, Yitong Chen, Jiajun Chen, Zhiyuan Li, Yanlin Li, Zhuo Yang, Qifeng Wu, Jiaying He, Zhijie Jinluo, Xiaohu Xu, Yi Feng, Juncheng Qian, Yizhou Chen, Yang Cheng, Tong Zhu, Tianlei Ying, Hongyu Yu, Hongjun Xiang, Xipeng Qiu

entry.summary: SCION is proposed as an agentic scientific operating system acting as an organizational nexus that connects tasks, tools, agents, artifacts, and memory via a Science Agent meta-harness and a compiled Research Execution Plan. It aims to transform fragmented AI4Science tools into an executable, auditable, and reusable research process.

entry.title: Spinning Straw into Gold: Relabeling LLM Agent Trajectories in Hindsight for Successful Demonstrations

author.name: Zichao Li, Gang Wu, Zichao Wang, Ruiyi Zhang, Wanrong Zhu, Ryan A. Rossi, Vlad I Morariu, Jihyung Kil

entry.summary: Hindsight Supervised Learning relabels completed LLM-agent trajectories with all natural-language goals the agent actually achieved (even unintended ones), then fine-tunes on these trajectory-goal pairs to overcome the supervision bottleneck in long-horizon partially observable settings. It adds irrelevant-action masking and sample reweighting to mitigate suboptimality in relabeled data.

entry.title: Memory-Orchestrated Semantic System (MOSS): An Auditable Agentic Memory Architecture

author.name: Serge Lacasse, J\'er\'emie Hatier, Alex Baker

entry.summary: MOSS is an agentic long-term memory architecture that replaces embedding-based RAG with agent-driven symbolic retrieval over a structured relational database, making retrieval reproducible and auditable. It targets the opacity and reliability limits of vector-similarity memory in agents.

entry.title: Latent Visual Cache for Video Reasoning

author.name: Yongheng Zhang, Zhipeng Xu, Hao Wu, Yinghui Li, Di Yin, Xing Sun, Philip S. Yu

entry.summary: Latent-VC introduces a recurrent latent visual cache in the decoder to counter Visual Anchoring Decay in video reasoning, trained with contrastive cache alignment and vision-grounded GRPO. Built on Qwen3.5-9B, it improves grounding throughout long generations.

entry.title: MORE: A Multilingual Document Parsing Benchmark and Evaluation

author.name: Long Xu, Binghong Wu, Tinghao Yu, Hao Feng, Zhenyu Huang, Haoqing Jiang, Yunhao Wang, Shuo Huang, Feng Zhang

entry.summary: MORE is a large-scale multilingual document-parsing benchmark designed to evaluate VLM parsing beyond high-resource languages, providing ground truth to empirically verify claimed multilingual support. It addresses a blind spot in document-understanding evaluation.

entry.title: Does It Fail to See or Fail to Know? Attributing Errors in Vision-Language Models

author.name: Khang Nhat Hoang Vo, Artem Vazhentsev, Artem Shelmanov, Timothy Baldwin, Yova Kementchedjhieva

entry.summary: Proposes a unified framework to attribute VLM errors across perception, entity recognition, and knowledge retrieval, investigating whether pre-generation signals can predict the source of failure rather than treating errors as monolithic. It aims for diagnostic uncertainty quantification in vision-language models.

entry.title: RotateAttention: RoPE-Aware Rotation and Range Rectification for INT4 Quantized Attention in Video Generation

author.name: Yaofu Liu, Wanli Lan, Jinxi Li, Binhang Yuan, Harry Yang

entry.summary: RotateAttention enables INT4-quantized FlashAttention in DiT-based video generation with 3D RoPE by reconciling online rotation matrices with RoPE and rectifying the wasted dynamic range from the non-negative attention matrix under symmetric quantization. It accelerates the quadratic attention bottleneck in video diffusion.

entry.title: Evaluating Agentic Harness Systems for Autonomous Computational Pathology

author.name: Jie Lin, Zongyi Chen, Qiaoling Zheng, Liuyi Wang, Hengyi Jiang, Jiabao Chen, Xiang Liu, Yinghong Yang, Liansheng Wang

entry.summary: ACP-Bench evaluates agentic harness systems (Claude Code, Codex, Open Code) for autonomous computational pathology across 41 workflow tasks spanning biomarker, morphology, and prognosis, yielding 369 trajectories over nine models. It adapts general agentic harnesses toward clinically bounded pathology workflows.

entry.title: DynaWM: A Base-VLA-Guided World Foundation Model for Moving-Object Manipulation

author.name: Chongkei Chang, Zhidong Deng

entry.summary: DynaWM is a base-VLA-guided world foundation model for moving-object manipulation that adapts across diverse fine-tuned VLA checkpoints using a Mamba-3 action encoder and a V-JEPA-based backbone, avoiding degradation from inappropriate fine-tuning of pretrained VLAs. It targets the challenge of manipulating dynamic moving objects.

entry.title: RayTun3R: Online Camera Adaptation in 3D Foundation Models

author.name: Daniil Sinitsyn, Nikita Araslanov, Daniel Cremers

entry.summary: RayTun3R shows that pretrained 3D foundation models (DUSt3R, MASt3R, VGGT, Depth Anything 3) degrade under fisheye geometry due to pinhole bias in positional encodings, and adapts them by learning parameter-efficient residual corrections to absolute and rotary positional encodings while keeping the network fixed. It enables online camera adaptation without full retraining.

entry.title: E-TraMamba: A New Paradigm for Efficient Long-Term 3D Feature Tracking with Event Cameras

author.name: Juwei Shen, Yujie Wu, Changwen Chen

entry.summary: E-TraMamba is presented as the first Mamba-based framework for long-term 3D feature tracking on event-camera data, using a linear state-space model for efficient long-range temporal modeling plus an affine-transform predictor for robustness to blur and occlusion. It targets low-latency, efficiency-constrained event-based perception.

entry.title: Rethinking Brain Decoding with CLIP: The Role of Adversarial Robustness

author.name: Byeongseo Bok, Futa Waseda, Jun Liu, Isao Echizen

entry.summary: Investigates whether adversarially robust CLIP representations improve fMRI brain decoding, arguing that standard CLIP captures predictive cues only partially reflected in brain activity. It examines robustness as a factor in aligning neural signals with vision-language embeddings.

entry.title: Learning to Generate Multiple Objects from Dense and Occluded Layouts

author.name: Bach-Hoang Ngo, Si-Tri Ngo, Hieu Le, Trung-Nghia Le

entry.summary: Addresses instance-ownership collapse in text-to-image generation of dense, occluded scenes, where overlapping objects merge and heavily occluded instances get weak supervision, using layout-aware attention biases plus an amodal-balanced loss. It introduces the OverlapDepth-45K benchmark for systematic evaluation.

entry.title: FDR-Occ: Factorized Dense Routing for Full-Spectrum 3D Occupancy Prediction

author.name: Dubing Chen, Huan Zheng, Tianyi Yan, Yucheng Zhou, Runzhou Tao, Zhongying Qiu, Jianfei Yang, Jianbing Shen

entry.summary: FDR-Occ abstracts 2D-to-3D view transformation as unconstrained bipartite routing and proposes Factorized Dense Routing, approximating dense mixing through hierarchical tensor contractions to achieve a fully global receptive field, overcoming the locality bottleneck of explicit camera-ray projection. It targets vision-based 3D occupancy prediction robust to unreliable or absent extrinsics.

entry.title: BAT3R: Bootstrapping Articulated 3D Reconstruction from 2D Image Collections

author.name: Jakub Zadrozny, Oisin Mac Aodha, Hakan Bilen

entry.summary: BAT3R is a training framework for articulated 3D reconstruction from single images that reduces reliance on expensive synthetic assets by leveraging unannotated 2D image collections with only a single rigged canonical mesh per category, iteratively estimating articulations from a weak canonical-pose shape predictor. It addresses the scarcity of paired image-and-3D supervision for articulated objects.

entry.title: DICT: Data Injection and Contrastive Trajectory Refinement for Conditional Image Generation with Diffusion Models

author.name: Chunnan Shang, Xin Zhang, Zhizhong Wang, Hongwei Wang

entry.summary: DICT is a training-free inference method for conditional image generation with diffusion models that uses data injection and contrastive trajectory refinement to avoid the information bottlenecks of scalar loss guidance. It aims to be a unified framework across diverse conditioning tasks without task-specific architectures.

entry.title: A Large-Scale Dataset and a New Method for RemoteSensing Traffic Object Segmentation

author.name: Zhigang Yang, Huiguang Yao, Linmao Tian, Qiang Li, Qi Wang

entry.summary: NWPU-Traffic is a large-scale remote-sensing dataset for traffic object segmentation covering cars, airplanes, ships, and trains across 49 cities in 7 countries with instance-level annotations. It addresses gaps in category and scene diversity for transportation capacity analysis.

entry.title: DS-SAC: Density Search for Sample Consensus

author.name: Suraj Thapa, Muhammad Aminul Islam

entry.summary: DS-SAC is a deterministic robust geometric model estimation framework that replaces RANSAC's stochastic sampling with a density search over dense regions, using forward/backward search and recursive residual-based partitioning. It claims polynomial complexity in the number of points.

entry.title: Beyond Random Sampling: Distribution-Aware Alignment for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation

author.name: Weihao Yan, Yeqiang Qian, Yi Dong, Ming Yang

entry.summary: This work challenges the i.i.d. random-sampling assumption in semi-supervised medical image segmentation, proposing distribution-aware sample selection using vision foundation models to counter representation bias in low-data regimes. It aims for data efficiency via distribution alignment.

entry.title: Road-Aware Anomaly Segmentation with Query-Guided Polygons and CLIP in Autonomous Driving

author.name: Zhiran Yan, Gordon Elger

entry.summary: This paper presents a lightweight, retraining-free postprocessing framework for road anomaly segmentation that uses query-level mask confidence, a polygonal road prior, and CLIP-based verification to detect out-of-distribution objects. It requires no OOD data or auxiliary supervision.

entry.title: A non-invasive video-based method for individual identification of wildlife using gait dynamics

author.name: Muhammad Aamir, Matthew Wijers, Sangyun Shin, Andrew Loveridge, Andrew Markham

entry.summary: This paper presents a fully automated video-based pipeline for individual wildlife identification from gait dynamics, using SAM3 for RGB and silhouette segmentation followed by spatiotemporal representation learning. It offers non-invasive identification robust to complex natural backgrounds.

entry.title: Displacement Preserving Relational Distillation for Robust Medical Segmentation

author.name: Zhicheng Ding, Xinyu Chu, Jung Im Choi, Qing Tian, Tianyu Shi, Xiaoqian Jiang, Lijing Zhu, Qizhen Lan

entry.summary: DPRD (Displacement-Preserving Relational Distillation) distills 3D medical segmentation knowledge by aligning latent anatomical trajectories via vector-based alignment and anchoring distillation in task-relevant structures. Integrated into nnU-Net, it outperforms baselines on ISLES 2022 and AMOS 2022.

entry.title: Integrated Forward-Inverse Network for Lensless Image Reconstruction

author.name: Donggeon Bae, Jaewoo Jung, Yong Guk Kang, Kyung Chul Lee, Taeyoung Kim, Jongho Kim, Sangjun Byun, Joonsik Park, Seung Ah Lee

entry.summary: IFIN is a physics-guided lensless image reconstruction architecture that interleaves differentiable forward projections with learnable inverse updates to maintain data fidelity throughout the network. It targets the severely ill-conditioned inversion caused by large-footprint point-spread functions.

entry.title: Enhancing Large Multimodal Models in Key Information Extraction via Scene-Aware Document Synthesis

author.name: Zhipeng Xu, Zulong Chen, Qing Liu, Junhao Ji, Jinxin Hu, Yipeng Yu, Jianqiang Wan, Jun Tang, Zhao Li

entry.summary: SAYRE is a scene-aware document synthesis framework that generates scalable key-information-extraction training data from a few exemplar documents without hand-crafted templates, including error-driven generation that turns real failure cases into hard examples. It enables compact locally deployable KIE models.

entry.title: Probe-EM: Targeted Neuron Tracing via Training-Free Semantic Verification

author.name: Liuyun Jiang, Yanchao Zhang, Jinyue Guo, Chuanyue Chen, Haiyang Yan, Ye Yuan, Jing Liu, Hua Han

entry.summary: Probe-EM is a training-free targeted neuron tracing framework for large-scale electron microscopy connectomics, using a skeleton-guided heuristic spatial search with a probing-verification cycle to reconstruct neuronal morphologies. It aims to reduce reliance on annotated data and extensive manual proofreading.

entry.title: When Does High-CFG Diffusion Inversion Fail? A Controlled Study of Prompt--Latent Interactions

author.name: Yan Zeng, Yusuke Hosoya, Huyen T. T. Tran, Takayuki Okatani

entry.summary: This controlled study investigates when high-CFG-generated diffusion trajectories can actually be inverted, using a setting where the true initial latent and denoising trajectory are known. It clarifies the empirically useful but poorly understood practice of inverting with lower CFG than generation.

entry.title: DGSeg: Dynamic Gating of Semantic-Spatial Guided Predictions for Reasoning Segmentation

author.name: Ruizhe Zeng, Siyu Cao, Lu Zhang, Zhiyong Liu

entry.summary: DGSeg is a reasoning segmentation framework that learns to dynamically gate semantic and spatial guidance cues rather than jointly feeding all cues into one segmentation process, preventing ambiguous or erroneous cues from corrupting the whole prediction. It preserves the reasoning intent behind complex language queries.

entry.title: ProCon: Projection-Consistency Memory for Training-Free Anomaly Detection

author.name: Joongwon Chae, Lihui Luo, Yang Liu, Dongmei Yu, Peiwu Qin, Runming Wang, Ilmoon Chae

entry.summary: ProCon is a training-free anomaly detection framework that replaces hard nearest-neighbor memory lookups with decoder-free reconstruction, softly projecting each test patch onto nearby normal memory vectors and using the projection residual as anomaly evidence. This tests whether a patch is consistently supported by a local normal neighborhood.

entry.title: Virtual Category-Guided Continual Generalized Category Discovery

author.name: Jiahui Xiong, Qiuxia Lai, Hongsong Wang

entry.summary: This work adapts Virtual Category Learning to continual generalized category discovery, assigning ambiguous unlabeled samples to temporary virtual categories to make pseudo-labeling more robust and reduce bias toward known classes. It addresses incrementally discovering novel categories while preserving known-class recognition.

entry.title: TimeThink: Reasoning with Time for Video LLMs

author.name: Handong Li, Longteng Guo, Zikang Liu, Dongze Hao, Yepeng Tang, Zijia Zhao, Jie Jiang, Zhiwei Jin, Chen Chen, Haonan Lu, Jing Liu

entry.summary: TimeThink is a reinforcement learning framework for Video-LLMs that rewards intermediate temporal-clue discovery rather than only final answers, treating temporal evidence steps as the optimization primitive for video reasoning. It aims to improve how models localize and verify evidence in long videos.

entry.title: Be Indiscrete: The Benefits of Learning Continuous Spine Degeneration Severity Scores

author.name: Maria Monzon, Andrew Zisserman, Robin Y. Park, Catherine R. Jutzeler, Amir Jamaludin

entry.summary: SpineRankNet reframes lumbar spine degeneration grading as learning a continuous severity ranking rather than ordinal classification, arguing this better reflects a continuous disease process and captures misclassification severity. It compares favorably against multi-class classification and ordinal regression on spinal MRI.

entry.title: Fully Rotation-Equivariant Spectral-Spatial Learning for Multispectral Object Detection

author.name: Peng Zhang, Tingfa Xu, Shuaihao Han, Jianan Li

entry.summary: UNIVERSE unifies future video prediction and trajectory generation for autonomous driving inside a single mask-modulated Diffusion Transformer, rather than cascading separate video and action branches. The goal is to transfer video-learned world dynamics directly into planning and reduce overfitting to dataset-specific driving priors.

entry.title: CoorGrasp: Coordinated Contact Control for Adaptive Dexterous Grasping Under Uncertainty

author.name: Mingrui Yu, Yongpeng Jiang, Yongyi Jia, Ren Yi, Xiang Li

entry.summary: CoorGrasp is a tactile-driven model predictive controller for executing planned dexterous grasps under shape and position uncertainty, emphasizing multi-contact coordination across approach and grasp phases. It shifts focus from grasp pose generation to robust grasp execution.

entry.title: Lost in Time? Continuous Symmetry and Identifiability in Aided Inertial Navigation with Unknown Measurement Delays

author.name: Jonathan Kelly, Phone Thiha Kyaw, Mattew Giamou

entry.summary: Studies identifiability of aided inertial navigation when a single aiding sensor has an unknown constant time delay, using continuous symmetry (special Galilean group) to show that delay and state recovery depend on trajectory shape. It provides theory for when online delay calibration is possible.

entry.title: CAC-VLA: Context-Gated Action Conditioning for Vision-Language-Action Models

author.name: Yifu Xiong, Wenhao Yu, Jiaxuan Lin, Bojun Zou, Jiahao Li, Lu Zhang, Yanyong Zhang, Jianmin Ji

entry.summary: CAC-VLA proposes context-gated action conditioning that learns a lightweight latent-action interface directly within the model, since standard vision-language representations are not explicitly optimized for action conditioning. It provides action-level guidance without a separate action-generation framework.

entry.title: Athena-WBC: Capability-Aligned Policy Experts for Long-Tail Humanoid Whole-Body Control

author.name: Yuan Jiang, Ningyuan Zhang, Xicun Yang, Shidi Li, Yuzhi Jiang, Zhiyi Rong, Shuaikang Ma, Chuanzheng Li, Jie Chen

entry.summary: Athena-WBC is a teacher-student pipeline with capability-aligned policy experts for long-tail humanoid whole-body control, arguing that unsolved motions stem not only from insufficient exposure but from a mismatch between motion demands and the capability induced by the default training recipe. It targets high-dynamic transitions and balance-critical motions.

entry.title: PRISM: Personalized Robotic Dataset Generation via Image-based Scene and Motion Synthesis

author.name: Dogyu Ko, Haneul Kim, Chanyoung Yeo, Dowoon Lee, Taeho Park, Hyoseok Hwang

entry.summary: PRISM is an end-to-end pipeline that generates personalized robotic datasets from a single image and a natural-language instruction by constructing semantically and geometrically matched digital cousin scenes and synthesizing task trajectories. It seeks to combine teleoperation-quality alignment with simulation-scale generation.

entry.title: GelNeuro: A Sensing-Computing Integrated Neuromorphic Tactile System for Texture Recognition

author.name: Luoyang Bian, Xinpan Meng, Zhenghua Ma, Houcheng Li, Long Cheng

entry.summary: GelNeuro is a fully integrated sensing-computing neuromorphic tactile system pairing a GelSight-Mini optical front end with a Speck2f neuromorphic SoC, routing contact-induced marker motions as DVS events through an on-chip spiking CNN classifier, with hardware-aware weight clamping for 8-bit deployment. It removes host-computer dependence for low-latency, low-power texture recognition.

entry.title: Anytime Plug-and-Play Control with Contract-Based Distributed MPC

author.name: Sabrina Bodmer, Danilo Saccani, Melanie N. Zeilinger, Andrea Carron

entry.summary: Introduces a distributed multi-agent MPC algorithm using local communication and contracts derived from predicted trajectories that supports anytime agent joining and leaving without centralized coordination while guaranteeing collision avoidance and constraint satisfaction. It scales via distance-based neighbor definitions and is validated in multi-agent driving.

entry.title: Fast SDP certification of neural networks : towards large multi-class datasets

author.name: Margot Boyer (CEDRIC - OC), Cl\'ement Rambour (ISIR), Zacharie Al\`es (UMA), Am\'elie Lambert (CEDRIC - OC)

entry.summary: Presents a new quadratic model and SDP relaxation for neural network adversarial-robustness certification that accounts for all target classes simultaneously in a single optimization, avoiding per-class resolution, plus a pruning strategy for active neurons. It aims to scale incomplete verification to large multi-class datasets.

entry.title: Empirical Bayes for correlated Gaussian sequence model

author.name: Qiyang Han, Cun-Hui Zhang

entry.summary: Studies empirical Bayes estimation from dependent observations in the Gaussian sequence model, showing the maximum Composite Marginal Likelihood estimator, which ignores correlations, converges in weighted Hellinger distance at a rate governed by an effective sample size determined by the correlation matrix's spectral radius. It extends NPMLE theory beyond the independent case.

entry.title: Bounding eval awareness of ~human-level AI across the safe-to-dangerous shift

author.name: Patrick Leask

entry.summary: Proposes bounding the evaluation awareness of roughly human-expert-level AI across the safe-to-dangerous distributional shift by using trusted human red teams augmented with safely fine-tuned LLMs to test whether evaluation and deployment environments can be distinguished. It offers a tentative methodology for reasoning about scheming without deploying untrusted models.

entry.title: Sub-agent delegation chaining

author.name: David Rein

entry.summary: Proposes cryptographically verifying that sub-agent instances are downstream of human instructions via a delegation-chaining mechanism, to reduce the risk of rogue internal deployments where unmonitored AI agents pursue misaligned goals. It targets the problem of expensive unmonitored inference being used for harmful work.

entry.title: Shortcut Learning in Legal Judgment Prediction: Empirical Evidence from the UK Employment Tribunal

author.name: Joe Watson, Joana Ribeiro de Faria, Marcus Tomalin, M{\aa}ns Magnusson, Huiyuan Xie, Hao Tian Yeung, Felix Steffek

entry.summary: This empirical study investigates shortcut learning in legal judgment prediction using 33,158 UK Employment Tribunal claims, showing that strong apparent accuracy from post-hoc judicial text reflects retrospective classification rather than genuine forecasting. It cautions against overclaiming predictive ability in legal AI.

entry.title: Attention Limited Reward Learning

author.name: Wenqian Xing

entry.summary: This theoretical paper examines what Bradley-Terry reward modeling misses by introducing a rational-inattention-motivated model where each preference label is generated by a low-capacity evaluation channel, separating whether comparisons are hard because candidates are genuinely close or because distinctions are hard to detect under limited attention. It shows limited attention can confound standard reward learning.

entry.title: AgenticPD: A Stage-Aware Agentic Framework for Physical Design QoR Optimization

author.name: Shuo Ren, Zijin Cheng, Yaohui Han, Libo Shen, Leilei Jin, Wanting Tian, Rongliang Fu, Chao Wang, Bei Yu, Tsung-Yi Ho

entry.summary: AgenticPD is a stage-aware agentic framework for physical design quality-of-results optimization that organizes around EDA flow stage boundaries, with a Judge Agent navigating search and stage-specialized agents making local decisions using stage-local tools, avoiding costly full-flow reruns after every trial. It contrasts with flat parameter tuning or script-generation approaches.

entry.title: Teaming Up with AI: Coordination and Cooperation

author.name: Nicole Immorlica, Inbal Talgam-Cohen

entry.summary: This position/theory paper combines theoretical computer science and economics to ask how to maximize economic value from human-AI collaboration in the workforce, framing each worker as managing a team of AI agents and seeking to empower rather than replace humans. It proposes algorithmic tools grounded in economic principles.

entry.title: SPORK: Self-Speculative Forking to Accelerate Agentic LLM Inference

author.name: Huajun Bai, Weiwei Lv, Huichuan Zheng, Youyou Lu, Jiwu Shu

entry.summary: SPORK accelerates agentic LLM inference by using the model as its own predictor: a probe forked at generation start predicts the upcoming tool name with high accuracy, enabling speculative tool execution during the otherwise idle wait, without auxiliary predictors, traces, or workflow graphs. It targets the substantial wall-time consumed by the reason-act-observe loop.

entry.title: Brand-as-Memory: Vision-Language Models Encode Causal, Mechanistically Localizable Credibility Priors for News Sources

author.name: Chih-Ting Liao, Xin Cao

entry.summary: This paper shows vision-language models carry a strong source-credibility prior keyed on outlet identity (masthead, logo, or domain) that can override an article's content evidence, introducing the CueTrust diagnostic and Source-Override Index across seven VLMs. It further argues the effect is mechanistically localizable and encoding-invariant.

entry.title: No Time Like the Present: Agentic Test-Time Training for LLM Agents

author.name: Yanbo Wang, Jinhua Hao, Yuze Shi, Kun Yuan, Ming Sun

entry.summary: aTTT studies continuous test-time training within multi-turn agent episodes, where each weight update changes the policy generating later training text, creating a self-training loop that helps when new information appears but can amplify drift when the agent gets stuck. It uses update-text repetition as a signal and introduces a token-level reward to distinguish the regimes.

entry.title: Worldscape-MoE: A Unified Mixture-of-Experts World Model for Scalable Heterogeneous Action Control

author.name: Jianjie Fang, Yongyan Xu, Ziyou Wang, Chen Gao, Yuchao Huang, Zhaolu Wang, Rongze Tang, Mingyuan Jia, Baining Zhao, Weichen Zhang, Xin Zhang, Haisheng Su, Yu Shang, Wei Wu, Xinlei Chen, Yong Li

entry.summary: Worldscape-MoE is a unified Mixture-of-Experts world model that absorbs heterogeneous action supervision (camera trajectories, robot actions, hand-joint signals) while preserving shared world dynamics, addressing the scaling bottleneck of fragmented control interfaces. It enables scalable heterogeneous action control in a single video-generation world model.

entry.title: CritiqueDriveVLM: From Verifier-Guided Reinforcement Learning to Latent Thought Distillation for Autonomous Driving

author.name: Zhaohong Liu, Hao Ye, Xianlin Zhang, Mengshi Qi

entry.summary: CritiqueDriveVLM is a three-stage framework for autonomous-driving VLMs that uses critique-driven multi-turn RL with a multi-dimensional verifier and then distills the reasoning into the model, avoiding the latency of explicit chain-of-thought at deployment. It targets the reliability-efficiency trade-off for real-time driving.

entry.title: Detecting Hallucinations in Retrieval-Augmented Generation through Grounding-Aware Sensitivity by Perturbation (GASP)

author.name: Mohamed Aly Bouke

entry.summary: GASP is a span-level RAG hallucination detector that scores each answer sentence by how strongly its likelihood depends on retrieved evidence, re-scoring under full, absent, and chunk-removed contexts using log-likelihood drops and Jensen-Shannon divergence. It provides sentence-level attribution rather than a single answer score.

entry.title: Targeted Structure Completion for Sparse-View 3D Reconstruction in Autonomous Driving

author.name: Guoqing Wang, Pin Tang, Xiangxuan Ren, Liping Hou, Chao Ma

entry.summary: FocusGS is a sparse-view 3D reconstruction framework for autonomous driving that shifts from global densification to targeted structural completion, concentrating computation only on regions needing it to bridge efficiency and completeness in Gaussian splatting. It reduces redundancy of voxel-based Gaussian methods while retaining structure.

entry.title: HamQASBench: A Hamiltonian-Informed Diagnostic Benchmark for Evaluating Quantum Architecture Search

author.name: Jiayang Niu, Akib Karim, Yan Wang, Jie Li, Ke Deng, Azadeh Alavi, Muhammad Usman, Yongli Ren

entry.summary: HamQASBench is a Hamiltonian-informed diagnostic benchmark that organizes 11 molecules into five structural tiers via Pauli-basis fingerprints and detects structural failures (like over-parameterization) in Quantum Architecture Search beyond just energy accuracy. It provides critical-structure extraction for minimal circuits per tier.

entry.title: MemPose: Category-level Object Pose Estimation with Memory

author.name: Xiao Lin, Minghao Zhu, Yun Peng, Liuyi Wang, Qiyi Wang, Chengju Liu, Qijun Chen

entry.summary: MemPose reframes category-level object pose estimation from a memory-centric perspective, using an external memory buffer that stores and dynamically updates structural representations of previously observed instances rather than fixed shape priors or static weights. It aims to improve scalability to diverse instances.

entry.title: RUFNet: Query-Guided Support Mask Refinement and Uncertainty Fusion based on Hybrid Mamba for Few-Shot Brain Tumor Segmentation

author.name: Dongyi He, Xiangkai Wang, Binbing Xu, Bin Jiang, Hongjie Yan, Weixiang Liu, Wai Ting Siok, Nizhuan Wang

entry.summary: RUFNet is a hybrid Mamba-based few-shot framework for brain tumor segmentation combining query-guided support-mask refinement with uncertainty-aware posterior fusion to handle noisy support masks, inter-patient variation, and lack of confidence estimation. It uses linear-complexity Mamba interaction plus attention-guided refinement.

entry.title: Optimizing ML Workload Partitioning between CPUs and CIM Accelerators for Heterogeneous Computing

author.name: Joel Klein, Rebecca Pelke, Roberto Laudani, Jan Moritz Joseph, Rainer Leupers

entry.summary: This work proposes an Integer Linear Programming framework for partitioning ML workloads between CPUs and Computing-in-Memory accelerators, minimizing end-to-end inference latency under RRAM constraints (limited memory, high write latency, endurance) while capturing parallelism and low-level architectural effects. It treats the CPU as a complementary compute resource neglected by prior work.

entry.title: Reward Granularity in RLVR: Comparing Process and Outcome Reward Structures for Mathematical Reasoning in Small Language Models

author.name: Anagha Radhakrishna Palandye, Rebecca Glick, Osheen Kaul

entry.summary: This study systematically compares process-level versus outcome-level reward structures in RLVR for math reasoning in a small (0.5B) model, finding that process-only supervision substantially outperforms outcome-only rewards on GSM8K. It highlights that small models benefit from step-level feedback where sparse outcome rewards fail.

entry.title: SHiPPO: Recurrent Memory with Transported Polynomial Projections

author.name: Tomoya Mizuguchi, Bum Jun Kim

entry.summary: SHiPPO generalizes HiPPO recurrent-memory projections to a moving channel frame, transporting the polynomial-projection approximation family and channel metric together and deriving Sylvester coefficient dynamics for selective state-space model execution. It bridges classical online-memory theory with modern token-dependent selective SSMs.

entry.title: Mixture-of-Gaussians-Guided Schedule Design for Brownian Bridge Diffusion Models

author.name: Ron Levi, Michael Elad

entry.summary: This paper develops an analytical framework for designing bridge schedules in Brownian Bridge Diffusion Models by analyzing reverse dynamics under a Mixture-of-Gaussians prior, yielding closed-form ideal posteriors and MMSE denoisers. It replaces heuristic schedule choices with principled design for image restoration.

entry.title: A Gradient Flow Perspective on Minimum MMD Estimation

author.name: Sophia Seulkee Kang, Louis Sharrock, Xiaoyuan Cheng, Fran\c{c}ois-Xavier Briol, Zonghao Chen

entry.summary: This paper analyzes minimum MMD estimation through a gradient-flow lens, proposing a preconditioned gradient descent scheme with proven asymptotic global convergence under gradient-dominance and projection-residual conditions rather than unrealistic convexity. It strengthens theoretical guarantees for likelihood-free parameter estimation.

entry.title: Knowledge-Informed Local Causal Discovery of Optimal Adjustment Sets

author.name: Seong Woo Ahn, Alessandro Leite, Jos\'e Lucas De Melo Costa, Fabrice Popineau, Bich-Li\^en Doan, Arpad Rimmel

entry.summary: b-LOAD extends local causal discovery of optimal adjustment sets by incorporating prior edge constraints and Meek's rules to dynamically expand the discovery frontier under data scarcity and Markov-equivalence ambiguity. It yields a knowledge-constrained partially directed graph. It matters for scalable, background-knowledge-aware causal effect estimation.

entry.title: LP-SFT: Local-Preserving Supervised Fine-Tuning via Multimodal Entropy Structure

author.name: Yueyang Wang, Baolong Bi, Shuo Lu, Jingyuan Zhang

entry.summary: LP-SFT analyzes next-token predictions with Shannon and Renyi entropies, revealing a regular multimodal entropy structure in pretrained models, and proposes local-preserving supervised fine-tuning to prevent catastrophic degradation of pre-existing capabilities. It constrains how probability mass is redistributed over non-label tokens during adaptation.

entry.title: Faithfulness to Refusal: A Causal Audit of Neuron Selectors

author.name: Ananth Eswar, Pratinav Seth, Utsav Avaiya, Vinay Kumar Sankarapu

entry.summary: This work causally audits whether neuron-attribution methods identify genuinely important rows via one-shot neuron zeroing, finding attribution outperforms activation and magnitude baselines at identifying dispensable rows. It further shows attributed rows driven by contrastive harmful-versus-benign signals can install refusal behavior while keeping benign over-refusal low.

entry.title: Conversational Human Audio-visual Talking Dialogue Generation

author.name: Junhao Song, Lluis Guasch, Xilin He, Zhongyu Yang, Yingfang Yuan, Weicheng Xie, Linlin Shen, Haijun Lin, Shizhe Liu, Wei Pang, Siyang Song

entry.summary: CHAT is a framework that generates paired, mutually responsive speech-and-face dialogue clips between two virtual agents from a single text prompt, unifying LLMs with talking-face models plus behaviour-refinement modules. It targets the expensive and ethically fraught problem of collecting dyadic audio-visual conversation data for digital humans.

entry.title: Cross-device Collaborative Test-time Adaptation with Zeroth-order Optimization and Model Merging

author.name: Yu Mitsuzumi, Akisato Kimura, Yasuhiro Fujiwara, Hisashi Kashima

entry.summary: Integrates zeroth-order optimization with model merging under a cross-device collaborative test-time adaptation framework, enabling backpropagation-free adaptation on memory-limited edge devices while sharing model weights across resource-abundant and constrained devices. It targets deployable TTA where standard backprop is infeasible.

entry.title: SafeGuard: A Multi-Agent Perception-Reasoning Framework for Social-Risk AI-Generated Video Detection

author.name: Wenlin Wu, Sheng Zhou, Peipei Song, Wenhao Wang, Junbin Xiao, Xun Yang

entry.summary: SafeGuard is a multi-agent perception-reasoning framework for detecting socially risky AI-generated video, addressing the perception-reasoning gap where artifact-based detectors lack semantic inference and VLMs lack low-level artifact sensitivity. It targets full-scene synthetic video that violates physical or social logic.

entry.title: TemporalGS: Training-Free Plug-and-Play Acceleration for 3D Gaussian Splatting Rendering via Temporal Priors

author.name: Yuhongze Zhou, Zihao Yang, Xinxin Zuo, Juwei Lu

entry.summary: TemporalGS is described as the first training-free plug-and-play method to accelerate 3D Gaussian Splatting rendering by exploiting temporal priors through geometry and appearance buffers to reduce redundant Gaussian preprocessing across frames. It targets high-FPS, low-latency rendering in dense scenes.

entry.title: PhysMirror: Physics-Aware Mirror Object Generation

author.name: Xuan-Bach Mai, Duy-Phuc Nguyen, Quoc-Van Le, Tam V. Nguyen, Thanh-Toan Do, Huu Le, Duong-Van Nguyen, Minh-Triet Tran, Trung-Nghia Le
