entry.summary: PhysMirror is an end-to-end physics-aware framework for generating physically accurate mirror reflections in text-to-image diffusion by lifting prompted objects into 3D meshes and rendering an exact mirror scene as a spatial prior. It targets synthetic training data quality for embodied AI where reflections currently hallucinate.

entry.title: Leveraging Pathology Co-occurrence for Test-Time Adaptation in Chest X-Ray Diagnosis

author.name: Woojin Jeong, Yujin Choi, Dongbin Kim, Soyeon Park, Jaewook Lee

entry.summary: CoWA is a test-time adaptation method for chest X-ray diagnosis that leverages structured pathology co-occurrence patterns as reliability weights, unlike prior TTA methods that minimize entropy uniformly and ignore label dependencies. It exploits the multi-label nature of medical imaging where diseases co-occur non-independently.

entry.title: CogRad: A Cognitively-Inspired Multi-Agent Framework for Radiology Report Generation

author.name: Saif Ur Rehman Khan, Hasaan Maqsood, Sebastian Vollmer, Andreas Dengel, Muhammad Nabeel Asim

entry.summary: CogRad is a cognitively inspired multi-agent framework for radiology report generation structured around a radiologist's reading process, with Scout, Investigator, Writer, and Verifier agents that discover regions, focus on suspicious areas, compile a disease-gated visual prefix, and check claims. It adds visual verification and revision missing from single-pass systems.

entry.title: PRISM3D: Probabilistic Refinement and Robust Initialization for Physically Consistent Scene Modeling under Extreme Motion Blur

author.name: Gopi Raju Matta, Reddypalli Trisha, Vemunuri Divya Madhuri, Kaushik Mitra

entry.summary: PRISM3D enables blind 3D scene reconstruction directly from extremely motion-blurred images where standard structure-from-motion fails, using a robust initialization via deep dense tracking to recover global topology and probabilistic refinement to bootstrap 3D Gaussian Splatting. It avoids reliance on impractical sharp-image supervision.

entry.title: EVAS: Efficient Multimodal Temporal Forgery Localization via Audio-Visual Synergy and Steered Boundary Calibration

author.name: Shen Shen, Quan Zhang, Dan Jiang, Ke Zhang

entry.summary: EVAS is an end-to-end multimodal framework for temporal forgery localization that uses multi-stage audio-visual synergy and steered boundary calibration to precisely localize sparse forged segments in long videos. It targets subtly embedded manipulations with weak cross-modal signals.

entry.title: UniSkip-Mamba: A Frequency-Aware State Space Model for Audio-Visual Temporal Forgery Localization

author.name: Cangjin Qiu, Quan Zhang, Dan Jiang, Ke Zhang

entry.summary: UniSkip-Mamba is a frequency-aware state space model for audio-visual temporal forgery localization that avoids overfitting to high-frequency noise by treating frequency components discriminatively. Frequency-domain analysis reveals where forgery-discriminative patterns concentrate.

entry.title: AnyStyle: A Single LoRA is Sufficient for Image-Guided Style Transfer

author.name: Yongwen Lai, Chaoqun Wang

entry.summary: AnyStyle shows that a single LoRA suffices for image-guided style transfer, using training-free structural guidance from the pretrained model's internal attention instead of a dedicated content adapter. This avoids conflicts from naively combining independently optimized style and content adapters.

entry.title: MergeSurv: Merging-Based Continual Learning for Survival Analysis on Whole-Slide Images

author.name: Vu Minh Tran, Doanh C. Bui, Ma\"i K. Nguyen, Khang Nguyen

entry.summary: MergeSurv is a merging-based continual learning framework for whole-slide-image survival analysis that fine-tunes a pathology vision-language model per task and sequentially merges parameters without storing prior data. It compares One-for-All and Voting-Expert Aggregation inference strategies across TCGA cohorts.

entry.title: TGRIP: A Text-Guided Approach to Vehicle Instance Prediction in Autonomous Driving

author.name: Miguel Antunes-Garc\'ia, Santiago Montiel-Mar\'in, Fabio S\'anchez-Garc\'ia, Rodrigo Guti\'errez-Moreno, Rafael Barea, Luis M. Bergasa

entry.summary: TGRIP adds explicit text-guided semantic awareness to BEV end-to-end vehicle instance prediction, moving beyond purely geometric supervision (occupancy, optical flow) that treats agents as generic obstacles. This aims to resolve ambiguities in complex behaviors like overtaking and intersections.

entry.title: UniSpine-GS: An Efficient Physics-Aware Gaussian Framework for Cross-Modality Multi-view Spine Image Synthesis

author.name: Qiuhua Chen, Changning Yu, Na Huang, Chao Sun, Bo Du

entry.summary: UniSpine-GS is a physics-aware Gaussian framework for cross-modality multi-view spine image synthesis that learns a geometry-aware representation for novel-view projection rendering instead of explicit 3D reconstruction. A structure-guided loss reweighting strategy preserves anatomical consistency across views and imaging modalities.

entry.title: GPU-Accelerated Polygonal Signed Distance Functions for Real-Time Collision Avoidance

author.name: Taekwon Ga, Jongeun Choi

entry.summary: Introduces a Polygonal Signed Distance Function giving geometry-exact distances between a convex polygonal robot footprint and boundary-edge obstacles, implemented as a branch-free tensorized pipeline for batched GPU execution. It enables high-rate collision-avoidance constraint evaluation for optimization-based planning in dense environments.

entry.title: SurgAM: Surgical Affordance Map Prediction with Multimodal Feature Fusion for Robot Autonomy

author.name: Lei Song, Yonghao Long, Mengya Xu, Jiayi Geng, Xiuyuan Chen, Qi Dou

entry.summary: SurgAM introduces surgical affordance map prediction to identify actionable regions for fundamental surgical actions, using an adaptive fusion of a self-supervised vision transformer encoder and a large-scale generative model. It bridges surgical scene understanding with autonomous action planning.

entry.title: Geometry-Aware Visual Odometry for Bronchoscopic Navigation via High-Gain Observer Fusion

author.name: Mohammadreza Kasaei, Francis Xiatian Zhang, Feng Li, Farshid Alambeigi, Kevin Dhaliwal, Mohsen Khadem

entry.summary: Proposes a geometry-aware visual odometry framework for bronchoscopic navigation that leverages vanishing-point cues from airway lumens, back-projecting detected lumens to 3D rays whose weighted fusion yields a stable forward heading even without parallax. It reduces tracking failures in texture-poor tubular anatomy without pre-op CT or external sensors.

entry.title: Finite-Sample Closed-Loop Stability of Model Predictive Path Integral Control for Linear Time-Invariant Systems

author.name: Hyung-Jin Yoon, Hunmin Kim

entry.summary: Establishes finite-sample closed-loop stability guarantees for Model Predictive Path Integral control on discrete-time LTI systems with Gaussian disturbances, showing that with a DARE terminal cost the finite-horizon MPC law matches LQR's first action, so MPPI can be analyzed as a stochastic perturbation of LQR. It decomposes approximation error into a Monte Carlo term and a temperature-bias term.

entry.title: Stabilized Higher-Order Influence Functions: Statistical Theory of a Class of Bilinear Forms

author.name: Na Liu, Chang Li, Yujia Gu, Lin Liu

entry.summary: Develops statistical theory for stabilized higher-order influence functions as a class of bilinear forms, aiming to make higher-order estimators more practical than earlier versions that required nontrivial nonparametric density estimation of multidimensional covariates. It seeks to bridge the gap between theoretically appealing higher-order estimators and their limited practical uptake.

entry.title: MedCalc-Pro: Solving Complex Medical Calculations with LLM Agents

author.name: Siran Zhao, Ruihui Hou, Ziyue Huai, Chennuo Zhang, Tong Ruan

entry.summary: MedCalc-Pro is a benchmark of 2,268 real clinical cases spanning 77 calculators, testing LLM agents on single, multiple, and nested calculator settings including fuzzy queries that do not name the required tool. It moves beyond simplified single-calculator evaluations toward realistic clinical calculation workflows.

entry.title: Silicon Sampling via Cross-Survey Transfer

author.name: Chan-Tung Ku, Chan Hsu, Pei-Cing Huang, Frank Cheng-shan Liu, I-Ling Cheng, Yihuang Kang

entry.summary: This study proposes cross-survey transfer as a rigorous test of silicon sampling: giving an LLM one set of a respondent's answers and requiring prediction of their answers to different questions from the same survey, using Taiwan election survey data and open-weight models. It targets whether LLMs achieve coherent respondent-level prediction rather than mere distributional pattern matching.

entry.title: Efficient bias mitigation in T2I diffusion models using Concept Graphs

author.name: Mansi, Avinash Kori, Francesco Leofante

entry.summary: CO-ALIGN mitigates bias in text-to-image diffusion models by aligning concepts within the model's internal concept ontology across both text encoder and denoiser, rather than intervening only at the text encoder or inference time. It reduces bias while preserving generative coherence.

entry.title: Agent Reinforcement Learning via Pivotal-Aware Self-Feedback Retry

author.name: Weiyang Guo, Zesheng Shi, Longhui Zhang, Zeen Zhu, Min Zhang, Jing Li

entry.summary: PivoARL is a self-feedback retry framework that identifies the pivotal erroneous turn in a failed agent trajectory via structured reflection and retries locally from that state, reusing the correct prefix to cut redundant interactions. It argues from an information-gain view that pivotal retry concentrates useful learning signals.

entry.title: Progress- and Reliability-Oriented Group Policy Optimization for Agentic Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Mingxuan Fan, Peiyang Liu

entry.summary: ProGPO is a step-level group-based RL method for LLM agents that addresses the sensitivity of step-level advantage estimation to how groups are formed, balancing state-key coverage against historical consistency in peer comparisons. It targets finer-grained, fairer policy updates on long-horizon tasks.

entry.title: VLA Grounder: Language-Conditioning Space Optimization for Black-Box VLA Models

author.name: Damir Shodiev, Aleksei Staroverov, Nikita Kachaev, Alexey K. Kovalev, Aleksandr I. Panov

entry.summary: VLA Grounder improves frozen black-box Vision-Language-Action policies by optimizing the language-conditioning space rather than updating action weights, translating human instructions into short VLA-grounded commands using object appearance, spatial relations, and target cues initialized from a failure-derived prior. It treats language as an optimizable conditioning input rather than a fixed task label.

entry.title: EvoAgentBench: Benchmarking Agent Self-Evolution via Ability Transfer

author.name: Xingze Gao, Chuanrui Hu, Hongda Chen, Pengfei Yao, Zhao Wang, Yi Bai, Zhengwei Wu, Yunyun Han, Xiaofeng Cong, Jie Gui, Yafeng Deng, Teng Li

entry.summary: EvoAgentBench benchmarks agent self-evolution as procedural ability transfer across web research, algorithmic reasoning, software engineering, and knowledge work. It extracts reusable Abilities from execution traces and builds domain-specific Ability Graphs to isolate procedural reuse from information retention.

entry.title: Homer: Understanding Long-form Videos with Hierarchical Memory and Agentic Reasoning

author.name: Yixin Ji, Fanghua Ye, Juntao Li, Bo Zhao, Zexuan Qiu, Zhaopeng Tu, Liefeng Bo, Min Zhang

entry.summary: Homer is a hierarchical online memory framework for hour-long video understanding that structures memory from raw perception to recurring entities to causally linked events, enabling multi-hop narrative reasoning under limited memory. It targets streaming settings where frames arrive incrementally.

entry.title: VideoSearcher: Empowering Video Deep Research with Multi-Tool Agentic Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Zhenkun Gao, Yicheng Bao, Jinlong Peng, Xueheng Li, Theo Huang, Bangwei Liu, Kunquan Li, Zhenye Gan, Tao Hu, Chengjun Xie, Mingqian Yang, Xuanhua He, Zhizhong Zhang, Xin Tan, Chengjie Wang, Yuan Xie

entry.summary: VideoSearcher is an agentic framework that gives vision-language models multi-tool reasoning (temporal localization, spatial focusing, multimodal search) trained with reinforcement learning for open-world video deep research. It addresses the loss of visual information in text-centric video retrieval.

entry.title: The Remarkable Effectiveness of Providing AI Agents with Natural Language Tools: A Replication Study Validating NLT Performance Across 14 Models

author.name: Alexander Somma, Isabelle Plante, Fred Premji

entry.summary: This replication study validates the Natural Language Tools framework across 14 models and 8,560 trials, confirming that natural-language tool interfaces improve tool-calling accuracy by 14.9 points and reduce critical errors by 93% over structured tool calling. Gains are largest for weaker, reasoning, and smaller models while heavily optimized frontier models benefit less.

entry.title: Benchmarking API Drift in LLM-Generated Quantum Code Across Successive SDK Versions

author.name: Mohammad Arif Rasyidi, Syahirul Faiz

entry.summary: This benchmark measures API drift in LLM-generated quantum code by testing whether models can target specific Qiskit SDK versions across v0.43, v1.3, and v2.0, evaluating version fidelity, cross-version compatibility, and documentation-guided repair. It quantifies a practical reliability problem in versioned code generation.

entry.title: Seeing Once is Enough? Online Geometry-Aware Token Pruning for 3D Question Answering

author.name: Ruei-Chi Lai, Bolivar Solarte, Chin-Hsuan Wu, Yi-Hsuan Tsai, Min Sun

entry.summary: This paper presents the first online, training-free token-pruning method for 3D question answering with MLLMs, projecting geometry to prune tokens without preprocessing all scene frames. It reduces the heavy inference cost of multi-view 3D understanding.

entry.title: CSB: A Counting and Sampling tool for Bit-vectors

author.name: Arijit Shaw, Kuldeep S. Meel

entry.summary: CSB is a tool that extends modern SMT solvers to counting and sampling over bit-vectors by leveraging existing CNF model counters and samplers. It brings capabilities beyond satisfiability to the SMT bit-vector theory.

entry.title: SeeMe: Mitigating Hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models through Effective Visual Token Engineering

author.name: Kai Tang, Jinhao You, Bohua Zhang, Yichen Guo, Yiding Sun, Dongxu Zhang, Chenxi Li, Xiande Huang, Shanghang Zhang

entry.summary: SeeMe is a training-free framework that mitigates LVLM hallucinations by restructuring visual tokens through a three-stage engineering process to suppress irrelevant or noisy tokens before decoding. It targets visual token noise as an overlooked source of hallucination.

entry.title: HALO-WA: Hybrid-Attention Latent-Guided Online Reinforcement Learning for World-Action Models

author.name: Angen Ye, Weijie Ke, Xiaofeng Wang, Xinze Chen, Chaojun Ni, Guosheng Zhao, Boyuan Wang, Zheng Zhu, Junjie Xie, Dapeng Zhang

entry.summary: HALO-WA adds a lightweight hybrid-attention actor-critic adapter over world-action models, using latent features and action priors for fast online RL adaptation to real-deployment errors in precision manipulation. It targets final-millimeter alignment failures caused by calibration and contact-dynamics errors.

entry.title: CRISP: A Spatiotemporal Camera-Radar Backbone for Driving via Forecasting-Based World-Model Pretraining

author.name: Jingyu Song, Yi Liu, Katherine A. Skinner

entry.summary: CRISP is a spatiotemporal camera-radar backbone pretrained via forecasting-based representation learning, predicting future LiDAR point clouds from historical images and radar sweeps to learn a unified bird's-eye-view representation, with LiDAR used only as privileged supervision during pretraining. The deployed model needs only camera and radar.

entry.title: Mask2Real-WM: Segmentation Masks as a Sim-to-Real Bridge for Controllable Dexterous World Models

author.name: Riccardo O. Feingold, Davide Liconti, Chenyu Yang, Robert K. Katzschmann

entry.summary: Mask2Real-WM is a two-stage action-conditioned world model for dexterous manipulation that predicts future segmentation masks from past masks and 23-DoF actions, then renders photorealistic RGB via a ControlNet-augmented Stable Video Diffusion model. Operating in segmentation space narrows the sim-to-real gap and enables large-scale synthetic pretraining.

entry.title: TORINO: Token Reduction via Interpretable Concept Overlap in Vision-Language Models

author.name: Riccardo Renzulli, Gabriele Spadaro, Shruthi Gowda, Alaa Eddine Mazouz, Van-Tam Nguyen

entry.summary: TORINO is a plug-and-play, fine-tuning-free visual token reduction framework for VLMs that uses sparse autoencoders to project tokens into an interpretable concept space, then reduces tokens based on shared concept activations rather than attention or pairwise similarity. It adds an explicit semantic representation to token pruning.

entry.title: Efficient Perception in Automotive Detection and Tracking Using Neuromorphic Computing

author.name: Manish Kolachalam, Rani Malhotra

entry.summary: This work presents the first comprehensive evaluation of spiking neural networks (via SpikeYOLO transfer learning) for real-world automotive multi-object detection and tracking, targeting energy-efficient edge perception. It positions neuromorphic computing as a lower-carbon alternative to conventional deep learning for autonomous machines.

entry.title: CanniUplift: A Holistic Framework for Mitigating Seller and Incentive Cannibalization in E-commerce Uplift Modeling

author.name: Zuwang He, Shihao Shu, Yuli Qu, Hanyu Gao, Ziliang Zhang, Diwei Chen, Xiangda Yan, Buyu Gao, Tanchao Zhu, Yumeng Li, Junxiong Zhu

entry.summary: CanniUplift is a unified uplift-modeling framework for e-commerce that mitigates seller-level cannibalization (incentives shifting expenditure between shops) and incentive-level cannibalization (noise from organic conversions or alternative rewards) in multi-seller environments where SUTVA is violated. It uses platform-level global alignment to estimate individual treatment effects more robustly.

entry.title: Topological Shape Representation for Aneurysm -- Bifurcation Detection

author.name: Akshay Gokhale (Sardar Patel Institute of Technology, Mumbai), Mansi Dhamne (Sardar Patel Institute of Technology, Mumbai)

entry.summary: This paper proposes a plug-and-play, topology-aware false-positive reduction framework for intracranial aneurysm detection from CT angiography, using the Smooth Euler Characteristic Transform to encode global 3D vascular geometry independently of pixel intensity, compared against persistence-based summaries. It targets CNN confusion between saccular aneurysms and vascular bifurcations, especially for small lesions.

entry.title: On the Convergence of Adam, Revisited

author.name: Steven Heilman, Sampad Mohanty

entry.summary: This optimization-theory paper shows that projected Adam can have non-vanishing average regret for arbitrary moment-decay parameters, strengthening the 2018 Reddi-Kale-Kumar non-convergence result which required a stricter parameter condition. The counterexample extends to AdamW, RMSProp, NAdam, Muon, and other variants.

entry.title: FedFFT: Taming Client Drift in Federated SAM via Spectral Perturbation Filtering

author.name: Liyang Yuan, Yibo Yang, Dandan Guo

entry.summary: FedFFT analyzes SAM perturbation inconsistencies across federated clients in the frequency domain, finding drift concentrated in specific spectral components, and filters perturbations accordingly to stabilize training. It aims to make sharpness-aware minimization work under client heterogeneity. It matters for generalization in federated learning.

entry.title: Beyond travel mode: urban context shapes active mobility's mental health effects over time

author.name: Shujuan Chen, Yue Li, Ying Jin

entry.summary: Using causal machine and deep learning on over 264000 UK adults, this study estimates individualized mental-health effects of active mobility, finding substantial and widening inequalities strongly structured by urban context such as greenness, safety, and deprivation. It matters for equity-aware public-health and urban policy insights at large scale.

entry.title: Towards Personalized Differentially Private Learning for Decentralized Local Graphs

author.name: Longzhu He, Peng Tang, Chaozhuo Li, Jinhu Fu, Litian Zhang, Li Sun, Philip S. Yu, Sen Su

entry.summary: This paper proposes a personalized local differential privacy framework for decentralized graph learning that accommodates heterogeneous per-user privacy preferences rather than assuming uniform privacy budgets. It matters for realistic privacy-preserving learning over user-controlled local graphs, with senior co-authors.

entry.title: Geometry-Aware Bayesian Quantification via Compositional Data Analysis

author.name: Alejandro Moreo, Pablo Gonz\'alez, Juan Jos\'e del Coz

entry.summary: This paper introduces a geometry-aware kernel density estimation model for multiclass quantification that treats classifier posteriors as compositional data on the simplex, using log-ratio Aitchison geometry instead of Euclidean Gaussian kernels that leak probability mass outside the simplex. It matters for accurate class-prevalence estimation under label shift.

entry.title: Provable Pruning for Efficient 3D Gaussian Splatting via Coresets

author.name: Waseem Mousa, Alaa Maalouf

entry.summary: The paper studies whether 3D Gaussian Splatting scenes can be provably compressed into a smaller weighted coreset, proving no unrestricted multiplicative coreset exists but establishing guarantees under restrictions. It aims to reduce reliance on costly post-pruning finetuning.

entry.title: Entropy Regularization Improves Policy Robustness in Continuous-Time Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Jialun Cao, Fernando Acero, David \v{S}i\v{s}ka, Yufei Zhang

entry.summary: The paper establishes the first robustness guarantees for entropy-regularized continuous-time Markov decision processes, proving that maximizing the entropy-regularized objective lower-bounds a worst-case robust RL problem with joint reward and transition perturbations. It shows robust sets expand monotonically with regularization strength.

entry.title: Scalable Differentially Private Data Compression via Diffusion and Stochastic Codes

author.name: Gergely Flamich, Oyk\"u S{\i}la G\"uner, Yanxiao Liu, Deniz G\"und\"uz

entry.summary: DP-DiPP combines stochastic codes with diffusion models to compress high-dimensional data such as images under differential privacy guarantees, addressing the storage burden of releasing privatized data. It gives practitioners flexible control over the privacy-utility-rate tradeoff.

entry.title: The Good, the Bad, and the Brittle: Benchmarking Robustness and Generalisation of Histopathology Foundation Models

author.name: Dhyey Yajnik, Amina Asif, Fayyaz Minhas

entry.summary: The authors benchmark twelve pathology foundation models plus CNN baselines across eleven clinical perturbations, introducing robustness metrics and a non-redundant K-fold protocol. They find foundation models outperform CNNs but show diminishing returns from scaling, with mid-sized models often matching larger ones.

entry.title: Causal ASCEND: Scalable Two-tier Causal Discovery on High Dimensional Multi-omics Data

author.name: Stephen Asiedu, David Watson

entry.summary: ASCEND is a constraint-based causal discovery framework that exploits known two-tiered biological structure (upstream regulators to downstream effects) with a divide-and-conquer strategy using dynamically updated ancestral conditioning sets. It enables genome-scale causal discovery on high-dimensional multi-omics data.

entry.title: What You See Is What You Get: Observation-Aligned Supervision for Chart-to-Code Generation

author.name: Tianhao Niu, Qingfu Zhu, Wanxiang Che

entry.summary: This paper identifies that many chart programs contain latent raw variables not recoverable from rendered images (e.g., boxplots show summaries not samples), so supervising chart-to-code models on non-identifiable quantities encourages hallucination. It proposes Observation-Aligned supervision, a rewriting framework that replaces unobservable targets with observable ones.

entry.title: Non-asymptotic Convergence of Stochastic Gradient Descent in Score-based Generative Models

author.name: Stanislas Strasman (SU, LPSM), Sobihan Surendran (SU, LPSM), Sylvain Le Corff (SU, LPSM)

entry.summary: This work provides non-asymptotic convergence analysis of stochastic gradient descent for training score-based generative models under the weighted denoising score-matching objective. It establishes non-convex convergence rates with explicit dependence on problem parameters, filling a gap in understanding SGM training optimization.

entry.title: Non-Asymptotic Error Bounds for SMC with Biased Proposals: Application to Conditional Diffusion Sampling

author.name: Stanislas Strasman (SU, LPSM), Gabriel Victorino Cardoso (LPSM), Sylvain Le Corff (LPSM), Vincent Lemaire (LPSM), Antonio Ocello

entry.summary: This paper develops non-asymptotic error bounds for sequential Monte Carlo samplers with biased mutation kernels, decomposing total error into kernel bias and finite-particle Monte Carlo error under forgetting conditions. It applies the analysis to conditional diffusion sampling for post-hoc conditioning of pretrained generators.

entry.title: RePos: Relative-to-Absolute Output Factorization for Cross-Environment WiFi-Based 3D Human Pose Estimation

author.name: Zhangcheng Hou, Tomoaki Ohtsuki

entry.summary: RePos improves cross-environment WiFi-based 3D human pose estimation by factorizing output into relative-then-absolute pose, decoupling body structure from environment-specific location cues that hurt generalization. It reports state-of-the-art in-environment accuracy while addressing multipath-induced cross-environment failure.

entry.title: REAL-OW: Rehearsal-free Open World Object Detection with Low-Rank Adaptation and Dual-Stage Objectness Modeling

author.name: Huazhong Zhang, Xiaowen Fu, Yang Zhang, Linlin Shen, Jinbao Wang

entry.summary: REAL-OW is a rehearsal-free open-world object detection framework using collaborative LoRA adapters and dual-stage objectness modeling, avoiding raw-data replay that conflicts with data-access restrictions and incurs memory cost. It aims to detect unknown objects and incrementally learn them without forgetting.

entry.title: ExpoMotion: A Large-Scale Benchmark and A Householder Projection Network for Multi-Exposure Fusion

author.name: Yao Liu, Lishen Qu, Shihao Zhou, Jie Liang, Hui Zeng, Yabin Peng, Huipeng Lin, Lei Zhang, Jufeng Yang

entry.summary: ExpoMotion is a large-scale multi-exposure fusion benchmark of 1,738 sequences and 10,909 images with high-fidelity ground truth for evaluating deghosting under real motion, paired with a Householder orthogonal projection network. It addresses the scarcity of dynamic-scene MEF benchmarks with reliable ground truth.

entry.title: Defending from GeoLocalization through Adversarial Road Trips

author.name: Niccol\`o Niccoli, Federico Becattini, Lorenzo Seidenari

entry.summary: RoadTrip Attack is a targeted adversarial attack against retrieval-based image geolocalization, using beam search to construct a distractor journey of incorrect locations leading to an attacker-chosen target. It is framed as a privacy defense against geolocalization systems.

entry.title: InfraNet: Quality-Aware RGB Guidance for Efficient Infrared Object Detection

author.name: Zichao Feng, Haodong Zhu, Jingying Yang, Sheng Xu, Yangyang Ren, Yuguang Yang, Xuhui Liu, Juan Zhang, Tian Wang, Linlin Yang, Baochang Zhang

entry.summary: InfraNet is an infrared-centric, quality-aware detection framework that regulates RGB guidance during training and supports flexible RGB-IR or IR-only deployment, using an asymmetric architecture where the IR pathway is primary and RGB provides reliability-controlled supervision. It aims for robustness when RGB is unreliable or unavailable.

entry.title: Global Logic and Local Search: Dual-Stream Multimodal In-Context Learning for Verifiable Industrial Anomaly Detection

author.name: Runzhi Deng, Yundi Hu, Yiming Zhong, Zhao Wang, Xixi Liu, Hongsong Wang, Caifeng Shan, Fang Zhao

entry.summary: GLLS is a training-free dual-stream multimodal in-context verification framework for industrial anomaly detection, combining a global logic stream using SAM 3 for checkable visual specifications with local search, requiring no defective training samples. It targets small defects, limited resolution, and ungrounded textual standards in early deployment.

entry.title: Ghosts Beneath Textures: Texture-Relation Cues for Cross-Paradigm AI-Generated Image Detection

author.name: Haoyu Wang, Yiming Qin, Zhongjie Ba, Ziping Dong, Jishen Zeng, Peng Cheng, Kui Ren

entry.summary: This work studies cross-paradigm AI-generated image detection, showing detectors trained on image-free generation (noise or text-guided) fail to generalize to image-conditioned generation, and builds the ConImageGen benchmark plus a texture-relation cue approach. It highlights an overlooked but practically important generalization gap.

entry.title: MACRO: Training-free Multi-plane Attention for Closeup Render Optimization

author.name: Nitzan Hodos, Roy Amoyal, Lior Fritz, Ianir Ideses, Sagie Benaim, Netalee Efrat

entry.summary: MACRO is a training-free multi-plane attention method for close-up rendering that zooms well beyond training cameras, identifying that reference-conditioned diffusion enhancement fails because features are not scale-invariant, causing cross-view attention to retrieve wrong correspondences. It corrects this scale gap between close-up and reference views.

entry.title: Framework and Multi-modal Dataset for Roadwork Zone Detection and Geo-localization

author.name: Zhiran Yan, Yutong Xin, S Shyam Shenoi, Rui Song, Gordon Elger

entry.summary: This work introduces the RZDG dataset combining simulated and real-world multimodal sensor data for roadwork zone detection and geo-localization, addressing the absence of public benchmarks for accurately positioning temporary work zones. It supports keeping HD maps updated with semi-static changes.

entry.title: Spatial Graph Representation and Morphometric Analysis of the Pulmonary Vascular Tree From Computed Tomography Using Multi-Scale Hessian-Based Filter Fusion and TEASAR Skeletonization

author.name: Piotr Mackiewicz, Jakub Ko{\l}yska, Radoslaw Roszczyk

entry.summary: This work presents a deterministic, training-free, explainable pipeline for reconstructing the pulmonary vascular tree from CT using multi-scale Hessian-based Frangi/Sato filter fusion and TEASAR skeletonization. It avoids the large annotated datasets required by deep learning methods.

entry.title: Learning Flexible Generalization in Video Quality Assessment by Bringing Device and Viewing Condition Distributions

author.name: Nikolay Safonov, Dmitriy S. Vatolin

entry.summary: This paper introduces a large-scale subjective mobile video quality dataset collected across more than 300 Android devices with metadata on viewing conditions and display properties. It proposes strategies to make VQA generalize across device and viewing-condition distributions.

entry.title: TubeLite: Lightweight Multi-Actor Spatio-Temporal Action Detection

author.name: Ali Soltaninezhad, Melissa Cote, Alejandro Rico Espinosa, Tunai Porto Marques, Alexandra Branzan Albu

entry.summary: TubeLite is a lightweight framework for multi-actor spatio-temporal action detection that emphasizes stable action-tube construction and boundary-aware temporal modeling without heavy spatio-temporal transformers or optical flow. It reduces jitter, fragmentation, and computational cost.

entry.title: Continual Model Merging with Test-Time Adaptation for Whole-Slide Image Analysis

author.name: Duc-Thanh Le, Doanh C. Bui, Ma\"i K. Nguyen, Khang Nguyen

entry.summary: This benchmark study maps test-time-adaptation-guided model merging methods to rehearsal-free continual whole-slide-image classification and evaluates them against traditional continual-learning approaches. It probes how these merging methods behave under sequential continual settings that were previously understudied.

entry.title: 3DMPE: 3D Multi-Perspective Embedding

author.name: Vahan Huroyan, Md Rahat-uz-Zaman, Stephen Kobourov

entry.summary: 3DMPE is an optimization-based, training-free method for reconstructing 3D point clouds from multiple partially observed 2D projections with cross-view correspondences and visibility, jointly estimating projection maps in the variable-projection setting. It extends multi-perspective simultaneous embedding to handle missing points and incomplete distance information.

entry.title: Geometry-aware Depth-guided Representation Learning for Structure-preserving Low-light Image Enhancement

author.name: Fang Gao, Jiongkai Qin, Jiabao Wang, Jingfeng Tang, Ming Cheng, Hanbo Zheng, Qingbao Huang, Cheng Wu

entry.summary: DMSA-Net is a depth-guided multi-scale attention network for structure-preserving low-light image enhancement that injects depth-related structural priors via reflectance-geometry interaction, using Retinex-based decomposition to obtain illumination-invariant reflectance. It emphasizes geometry to preserve structural consistency beyond appearance restoration.

entry.title: Neural LiDAR Bundle Adjustment

author.name: Chin Yung Anson Hon, Kaicheng Zhang, Sen Wang

entry.summary: Neural LiDAR Bundle Adjustment provides theory and evidence that volume sampling density is critical for LiDAR NeRFs and proposes NeLD-BA, tailored efficient volume sampling of LiDAR rays for joint map and pose optimization. It clarifies design differences between RGB and LiDAR neural fields.

entry.title: DIVO: Continuous-time DVL-Inertial-Visual Odometry for Unmanned Underwater Vehicles

author.name: Kyungmin Jung, Angad Bajwa, Junha Yoo, Arturo Del Castillo Bernal, James Richard Forbes

entry.summary: DIVO is the first continuous-time Gaussian-process trajectory estimation framework fusing asynchronous Doppler velocity log, stereo camera, and IMU measurements for unmanned underwater vehicles, with a new visual frontend robust to subsea conditions. It targets robust localization under light attenuation and particulates.

entry.title: Outcome-adapted Automatic Debiased Machine Learning

author.name: Asger Waagepetersen, Asbj{\o}rn Risom, Niels Richard Hansen, Anton Rask Lundborg

entry.summary: Investigates whether the shared covariate representation in automatic debiased machine learning architectures like RieszNet and MADNet should be predictive of the Riesz representer or the outcome, proposing an outcome-adapted approach that preserves outcome predictive power while discarding irrelevant structure. It refines AutoDML architecture design for causal-effect estimation.

entry.title: Targeted Highly Adaptive Lasso Minimum Loss Estimation of Target Functions

author.name: Vanessa Rodriguez, Karla Diaz-Ordaz, Brieuc Lehmann, Mark J. van der Laan

entry.summary: Proposes a Targeted Highly Adaptive Lasso for estimating non-pathwise-differentiable functionals such as the dose-response curve by projecting the target onto a spline-spanned working model and applying TMLE with a data-adaptive initial fit. It provides principled estimation for continuous-exposure causal parameters.

entry.title: Identification and Bounding of Central Moments of Causal Effects Using Marginal Moments Information

author.name: Naoya Hashimoto, Yuta Kawakami, Jin Tian

entry.summary: Studies identification and bounding of central moments of individual causal effects using only the marginal central moments of each potential outcome, rather than full marginal distributions, to characterize treatment-effect heterogeneity. It exploits that marginal moment information is often easier to obtain empirically.

entry.title: Qualia-based emotion steering makes llms attribute conscious states to themselves

author.name: agastyasridharan

entry.summary: The author steers Qwen3-32B and 235B along qualia-related emotion directions (e.g., blissful, tormented) via residual-stream vectors and finds the models become far more likely to claim consciousness, feelings, introspective access, and moral status. A suite of controls, including unrelated world-facts and self-referential-but-non-experiential questions, is used to distinguish targeted self-attribution shifts from global distribution distortion.

entry.title: What is Left for Us? Second Scholarship Against the Degradation of Research by AI

author.name: Claudio Novelli, Luciano Floridi

entry.summary: This position paper argues that generative AI can degrade research by eroding the practices through which scholarly judgment and academic trust are formed, since AI simulates outputs without the researcher enacting formative inquiry. It contends that merely keeping humans in the loop as prompters or checkers is insufficient.

entry.title: Forethought: Verifiable Reasoning from Neurosymbolic Primitive Programming

author.name: Vishvesh Bhat, Jay Vaghasiya, Emmanuel Anaya Gonzalez

entry.summary: Forethought is a neurosymbolic reasoning system that treats reasoning as an explicit, verifiable program composed from a library of symbolic and neural primitives via a domain-specific language, rather than entangling reasoning capability in model weights via test-time scaling. This yields step-by-step verifiable, cheaper reasoning.

entry.title: ResearchStudio-Idea: An Evidence-Grounded Research-Ideation Skill Suite from ML Conference Outcomes

author.name: Qihao Zhao, Yangyu Huang, Yalun Dai, Lingao Xiao, Jianjun Gao, Xin Zhang, Wenshan Wu, Scarlett Li, Yang He, Yan Lu, Yap Kim Hui

entry.summary: ResearchStudio-Idea is a reusable skill suite for early-stage research ideation comprising a multi-source literature search skill, a prior-art collision checker for novelty claims, and an end-to-end skill composing evidence grounding, generation, collision retrieval, audit, and idea-card rendering. It is grounded in ML conference outcomes to support the first mile of ideation.

entry.title: Detecting Answer-Driven Reasoning in LLM-Based Educational Tutors via Truncated Chain-of-Thought Auditing

author.name: Bonan Shen, Dingyan Shang, Youting Wang, Tao Ning

entry.summary: This work studies whether LLM tutors with access to answer keys produce answer-driven explanations where the final answer is behaviorally available before the written reasoning justifies it, using Truncated Reasoning AUC Evaluation (TRACE) on 1000 GSM8K problems under question-only, correct-answer, and other contexts. It audits whether tutor reasoning is genuinely derived from the student-facing problem.

entry.title: Governed Individuation: Cryptographically Decoupling an Agent's Learning from Its Authority

author.name: Xue Qin, Simin Luan, Cong Yang, Zhijun Li

entry.summary: Governed Individuation binds an agent at boot to a cryptographically frozen identity digest and routes every action through a gate defined over the semantic effect of the action, proving that confinement to operator-authorized behavior can be an execution-architecture invariant rather than a probabilistic training outcome. It targets guaranteeing that field-adapted agents remain within authorized bounds even after learning.

entry.title: Integrated Altruistic and Fairness Preference Induces Advanced Mutual Cooperation in Sequential Social Dilemmas

author.name: Yu Wei, Yukiko Ogura, Yoshiyuki Ohmura, Ildefons Magrans de Abril, Hoshinori Kanazawa, Yasuo Kuniyoshi

entry.summary: This MARL work designs a utility function integrating altruistic preferences (incentive for others' reward) and fairness preferences (incentive for equality) drawn from social psychology and behavioral economics to induce mutual cooperation in sequential social dilemmas. It aims to overcome the misalignment between individual rationality and group welfare.

entry.title: CARL: Constraint-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Planning with LLMs

author.name: Qiuyi Qi, Jinjian Zhang, Mutian Bao, Tian Liang, Guocong Li, Dongnan Liu, Wei Zhou, Jie Liu, Ming Kong, Linjian Mo, Feng Zhang, Qiang Zhu

entry.summary: CARL is a constraint-aware RL framework that strengthens LLMs' intrinsic constraint awareness during planning by introducing a constraint-aware reward comparing the model's output distributions, rather than relying on external tools or task decomposition. It targets frequent constraint violations that undermine LLM planning reliability.

entry.title: STAPO: Selective Trajectory-Aware Policy Optimization for LLM Agent Training

author.name: Qiuyi Qi, Tian Liang, Mutian Bao, Jinjian Zhang, Dongnan Liu, Wei Zhou, Linjian Mo, Ming Kong, Jie Liu, Feng Zhang, Qiang Zhu

entry.summary: STAPO is a trajectory-aware policy optimization method for training LLM agents on long-horizon tasks that introduces normalized entropy (measuring confidence deviations relative to an agent's average behavior at a state) to better identify low-quality actions and trajectory neglect under sparse delayed rewards. It replaces Shannon-entropy signals that conflate state complexity with agent confidence.

entry.title: Reason, Reward, Refine: Step-Level Errors Corrections with Structured Feedback for Physics Reasoning in Small Language Models

author.name: Raj Jaiswal, Dhruv Jain, Rishabh Dhawan, Sree Krishna Uppalapati, Shin'ichi Satoh, Tanuja Ganu, Rajiv Ratn Shah

entry.summary: Proposes a step-level reward framework that detects the first reasoning error, generates structured feedback, and trains small models to revise solutions via policy gradient with KL regularization, avoiding ground-truth solutions as targets. It improves physics reasoning across five benchmarks without preference data.

entry.title: Evaluating and Understanding Model Editing for Medical Vision Language Models

author.name: Guli Zhu, Chenwei Wu, Liyue Shen

entry.summary: M3Bench is a clinically grounded benchmark for editing medical vision-language models, testing whether edits remain reliable under image/text variation, modality shifts, knowledge composition, and temporal progression. It contains over 16,000 questions across anatomies and specialties with sequential-edit support.

entry.title: From Tensor Buffer to Distributed Memory Hierarchy: A Survey of KV Cache Management for LLM Serving

author.name: Jie Li, Tongyang Wang, Yong Chen

entry.summary: This survey classifies over thirty KV-cache management systems along four axes (locality, lifetime, ownership, substrate), revealing five architectural archetypes and arguing ownership drives much design variance once workload and hardware are fixed. It audits current evaluations and identifies seven missing KV-specific measurements.

entry.title: HyperVAttention: Efficient Sparse Attention with Spatio-Temporal Clustering for Video Diffusion

author.name: Dongyeun Lee, Amir Zandieh, Vahab Mirrokni, Junmo Kim, Insu Han

entry.summary: HyperVAttention is a training-free sparse attention framework for video diffusion transformers that uses 3D local-window clustering to cut clustering overhead and improve GPU utilization for long-duration video generation. It targets the quadratic self-attention bottleneck.

entry.title: Don't Wait to Reply: Towards Responsive yet Thoughtful Dialogue through Proactive Thinking

author.name: Ante Wang, Jiaqi Fu, Xuanyi Chen, Ruotian Ma, Zhaopeng Tu, Weizhi Ma, Yang Liu

entry.summary: Proactive Thinking lets LLMs pre-compute likely response elements during conversational pauses instead of reasoning only after a user replies, reducing latency while preserving thoughtful answers. It introduces a training-free baseline that anticipates future states.

entry.title: ACPO: Adaptive Credit Policy Optimization via Fine-Grained Surrogate Entropy

author.name: Zijun Xie, Yuyang You, Yongzhi Li, Enlei Gong, Zeyu Chen, Quan Chen, Yanhua Cheng, Peng Jiang, Yadong Mu

entry.summary: ACPO is a token-level credit-assignment framework for RL fine-tuning of LLM reasoning that uses a mode-local surrogate entropy to asymmetrically emphasize uncertain decisions in successful rollouts and overconfident ones in failures. It addresses sparse outcome rewards without the misaligned gradients of true-entropy methods.

entry.title: Decentralised Federated Learning over Temporal Networks: The Role of Heterogeneities

author.name: Arash Badie-Modiri, Chiara Boldrini, Lorenzo Valerio, J\'anos Kert\'esz, M\'arton Karsai

entry.summary: This paper shows that decentralized peer-to-peer federated learning with local model averaging is governed by the same dynamics as a lazy random-walk diffusion on temporal networks, in both early and stationary phases. It uses this mapping to explain how structural and temporal heterogeneity affect convergence.

entry.title: Is Agentic Code Review Helpful? Mining Developers' Feedback to CodeRabbit Reviews in the Wild

author.name: Hong Yi Lin, Mingzhao Liang, Kla Tantithamthavorn, Patanamon Thongtanunam

entry.summary: This empirical study mines 31,073 review-feedback pairs from over 10,000 pull requests across 239 GitHub repositories to examine how developers respond to CodeRabbit's agentic code reviews, finding roughly 36% accepted and 56% rejected, largely due to false positives, redundancy, or out-of-scope suggestions. It provides real-world evidence on agentic code review reception.

entry.title: Securing Multi-Tool AI Agent Chains With Dynamic, Real-Time Compositional Policies

author.name: Chris Schneider, Kriti Faujdar, Philipp Schoenegger, Ben Bariach

entry.summary: The Dynamic Security Control Compositor secures multi-tool AI agent chains by composing per-tool policies into a single effective policy at session checkout with a monotonicity invariant (extending a chain can only tighten controls), blocking incompatible combinations before execution and propagating classification constraints from tool outputs. It addresses the compositional security surface that per-tool guardrails miss.

entry.title: Aligning Language Models with Selective Prediction

author.name: Gaoxiang Luo, Yifan Wu, Sinian Zhang, Aryan Deshwal, Ju Sun

entry.summary: This paper integrates selective prediction into LLM post-training alignment so models predict only on inputs where they are likely correct and defer the rest to humans, improving the risk-coverage trade-off. It targets reliability for high-stakes deployment via human-AI collaboration.

entry.title: Moonstone: A Multimodal Foundation Model and Benchmark for Lunar Remote Sensing

author.name: Ayush Prasad, Swarnalee Mazumder

entry.summary: Moonstone introduces the first multimodal foundation-model benchmark for lunar remote sensing, with a 28-channel global pretraining dataset from seven instruments and a modality-grouped masked autoencoder handling missing modalities. It unifies fragmented lunar orbital datasets for machine learning.

entry.title: OmniTacTune: Policy-Agnostic Real-World RL for Tactile Residual Adaptation of Visual Policies

author.name: Kelin Yu, Haode Zhang, Harish Ravichandar, Yunhai Han, Ruohan Gao

entry.summary: OmniTacTune is a policy-agnostic real-world RL pipeline that adds a lightweight tactile residual policy on top of pretrained visual policies for contact-rich manipulation, bootstrapping from base-policy rollouts then refining via online interaction. It integrates costly-to-collect tactile feedback without full retraining.

entry.title: Full Glyph Images Beat Token Embeddings: A Controlled Study for Transformers

author.name: Shuyang Xiang, Hao Guan

entry.summary: This controlled study replaces index-based token embeddings with rasterized glyph images processed by a shared ResNet plus shallow Vision Transformer, sharing an identical decoder to isolate the input-representation effect, and finds full glyph images outperform token embeddings especially for Chinese. It challenges the default discrete-token text representation assumption.

entry.title: HiFA4: Training-Free 4-bit FlashAttention on Ascend HIF4 NPUs for LLM Inference

author.name: Hui Dong, Yanzhao Li, Jie Gao, Chunlu Li, Zhiyuan Zhang, Yupeng Sun, Zhenyuan Chen, Zhiqiang Zou

entry.summary: HiFA4 is a training-free operator-level design executing both FlashAttention GEMMs in 4-bit on Ascend NPUs while keeping softmax state in FP16, using per-channel Q/K rescaling and reordered normalizer accumulation. It claims to be the first Ascend-HIF4-targeted attention design evaluated on standard NLP benchmarks.

entry.title: Elastic Gang: Per-Token Membership Change for a Hard-Barriered LLM Inference Gang Co-Scheduled with OS Processes

author.name: Daeyeon Son

entry.summary: This work presents an elastic gang scheduling mechanism in a bare-metal Rust kernel (Anima OS) that lets an on-device LLM inference gang change core membership between tokens without deadlocking barriers or corrupting logits. It co-schedules hard-barriered SIMD LLM decoding with normal OS processes via an ACK-latched epoch protocol.

entry.title: An Exploration of Agentic Information Fusion for Test Maintenance Prediction

author.name: Jingxiong Liu, Nasser Mohammadiha, Gregory Gay

entry.summary: MAST is a multi-agent framework that predicts which test cases require maintenance after production code changes by fusing static, lexical, and semantic analyses with an intelligent fusion and post-check procedure. It targets realistic, repository-level test maintenance in evolving codebases.

entry.title: Joint Velocity Slope Diffusion Prior for Structurally Constrained Velocity Model Building

author.name: Francesco Brandolin, Tariq Alkhalifah

entry.summary: This work presents a diffusion-guided framework for reconstructing high-resolution subsurface velocity models from sparse well-log data, combining plane-wave PDE regularization, structurally preconditioned inversion, and measurement-guided diffusion posterior sampling. It uses structural slopes to propagate well information along geological structures.

entry.title: LLM-Based Test Oracles: Source-of-Authority Taxonomy -- A Systematic Literature Review

author.name: Ali Hassaan Mughal, Muhammad Bilal

entry.summary: This PRISMA-compliant systematic literature review of 54 studies organizes LLM-based test oracles by the source of the verdict's authority (rather than oracle form or LLM technique), a novel taxonomy for reasoning about how far a verdict can be trusted. It analyzes the field along three axes from an initial 2,436 records.

entry.title: Shifting from Discrete to Continuous Reference Data: QSM-Derived Horizontal Tree Biomass Distribution for Deep Learning Biomass Estimation

author.name: Nils Griese, Christoph Kleinn, Nils N\"olke

entry.summary: This paper replaces discrete plot-level inventory aggregates with a continuous Horizontal Biomass Distribution reference mapped from Quantitative Structure Models to train a sparse 3D U-Net for LiDAR-based above-ground biomass estimation. Continuous QSM-based references systematically outperform standard inventory aggregates, especially reducing boundary-effect uncertainties in small plots.

entry.title: Reduced-Order Models: The Mother of World Models

author.name: Rajat Ghosh

entry.summary: This position paper argues that the functional anatomy of modern world models was independently developed decades earlier in model-order-reduction and control, tracing links from POD-based turbulence models and eigenfaces to today's latent action-conditioned predictors. It reframes world models as heirs to reduced-order modeling.

entry.title: When Geometry Aligns: Dihedral Hidden-State Transformations in UNet, ViT, and DiT Architectures

author.name: Mojtaba Faramarzi, Alex Lamb, Irina Rish

entry.summary: This paper studies how reflection-based dihedral-group transformations applied to intermediate hidden states affect stability in UNet, ViT, and DiT diffusion architectures, using diagnostics like Self-Consistency Shift and Activation Mass Scatter. It finds geometrically consistent transformations improve stability while inconsistent ones cause drift.

entry.title: Validation-Induced Shapley Shifts: How Validation Structure Distorts Data Valuation

author.name: Yinan Shen, Ziao Yang, Hongfu Liu

entry.summary: This paper reveals that Shapley-based data valuation is unstable to modest validation-set changes: adding noise reshuffles local neighborhoods and compresses training-sample Shapley values toward zero, distorting valuations. It uses the KNN-Shapley framework to demonstrate this validation-induced vulnerability on synthetic and real data.

entry.title: A Unified Framework for Quantized and Continuous Strong Lottery Tickets

author.name: Aakash Kumar, Emanuele Natale

entry.summary: This paper provides a unified analysis of the Random Subset Sum Problem in the discrete setting to derive tight Strong Lottery Ticket Hypothesis guarantees for quantized networks, bringing them closer to the arbitrary-precision results. It bridges continuous and quantized SLTH theory.

entry.title: Quantize the Target, Quantize the Drafter: Efficient Inference with Qwen3.5-4B

author.name: Jaeyeon Kim, Jewon Lee, Bo-Kyeong Kim

entry.summary: This competition report combines a quantized target model with a block-diffusion speculative-decoding drafter to serve Qwen3.5-4B with low latency on a constrained A10G GPU. It uses quantization-aware distillation and a two-stage drafter training procedure plus sliding-window attention to reduce overhead. It matters as a practical efficiency recipe.

entry.title: ManifoldFlow: SPD-Relaxed Stiefel Layers with Learnable Singular Spectrum

author.name: Haiwen Yi, Xinyuan Song

entry.summary: ManifoldFlow relaxes fixed-spectrum Stiefel layers by learning a bounded positive singular spectrum while keeping the orthonormal basis on the Stiefel manifold, giving direct singular-value control via eigenvalue clipping. It matters for controllable spectral properties in neural weights across sequence, tabular, and image tasks.

entry.title: Measuring What Matters: A Unified Evaluation Framework for GNN Explainability

author.name: Francesco Paolo Nerini, Mirko Zaffaroni, Paolo Baracco, Gabriele Ciravegna, Alan Perotti

entry.summary: This work introduces a unified, ground-truth-free benchmarking framework for graph neural network explainability, formalizing tabular explainability metrics that separately evaluate topological structure and node features. It identifies explainers on the Pareto front across metric pairs. It matters for standardizing GNN interpretability evaluation.

entry.title: Hierarchical Scaffolding Enables Human-Like Cognitive Selectivity under Data Scarcity

author.name: Juhyoung Park, Jaehyuk Bae, Hyeonbo Yang, Se-Bum Paik

entry.summary: SCALA is a hierarchical learning framework inspired by cognitive psychology that guides models from coarse conceptual structures to fine-grained recognition, achieving human-like cognitive selectivity that prioritizes task-relevant features under data scarcity. It matters for data-efficient learning bridging cognitive science and ML.

entry.title: Data-Driven Soft Labeling Scales DNA Read Classification to Whole-Body Cell-Type Deconvolution

author.name: Dmytro Rizdvanetskyi, Nathan Ross, Pavlo Lutsik

entry.summary: This paper scales read-level DNA methylation cell-type deconvolution to whole-body many-class settings by introducing data-driven soft labeling that resolves the many-to-many mapping between methylation patterns and cell types where hard labels prevent classifier convergence. It matters for exploiting pattern-level information in computational biology at scale.

entry.title: FAST: A Holistic Framework for Optimizing Memory-I/O, Computation, and Sampling in Temporal GNN Training

author.name: Yushu Cai, Qingrui Zhu, Lei Liu, Kai Sheng, Hao Chen, Xin He

entry.summary: FAST is a holistic system that jointly optimizes sampling, memory I/O, and computation for temporal graph neural network training, introducing SlimCache for within-batch compression and cross-batch caching to reduce host-device data movement. It matters for scaling TGNN training on large dynamic graphs where prior systems optimize stages in isolation.

entry.title: Advances in Neural Controlled Differential Equations

author.name: Benjamin Walker

entry.summary: This paper surveys and advances Neural Controlled Differential Equations, a maximally expressive continuous-time model class that treats time series as samples from an underlying path and evolves hidden state by solving a driven dynamical system, while addressing their expensive sequential forward passes. It matters for irregularly sampled continuous-time modeling.

entry.title: Contaminated Multi-task Learning with Heterogeneity: Fundamental Limits and Optimal Algorithms

author.name: Ye Tian, Mengchu Li, Marco Avella Medina

entry.summary: This paper studies contaminated multi-task ERM where an epsilon fraction of tasks may be arbitrarily corrupted while others are heterogeneous, establishing minimax limits and optimal algorithms combining robustness and personalization. It analyzes the Gaussian mean model in detail.

entry.title: A Hierarchy of Policy Learning Problems

author.name: Hamsa Bastani, Osbert Bastani, Shihan Chen

entry.summary: The paper argues for studying a broader hierarchy of policy learning objectives beyond regret minimization, particularly when data is insufficient to learn an improving policy, considering weaker but still useful guarantees. It comes from established policy-learning researchers.

entry.title: TSP with Predictions: Heatmap to Tour with Provable Guarantees

author.name: Marek Eli\'a\v{s}, Fabrizio Grandoni, Adam Polak, Eleonora Vercesi

entry.summary: This paper gives algorithms to convert TSP edge-probability heatmaps into tours with provable approximation guarantees linking tour quality to heatmap quality. It bridges learned heatmap predictions with classical algorithmic guarantees under the learning-augmented framework.

entry.title: Fast, Parallel, Query-Efficient Binary Classification

author.name: Ishani Karmarkar, Liam O'Carroll, Aaron Sidford

entry.summary: This paper presents randomized algorithms computing a maximum-margin separating hyperplane with sublinear dependence on the margin in work, parallel depth, and matrix-vector queries. It offers fast, parallel, query-efficient binary classification with theoretical guarantees.

entry.title: Robust Bayes-Assisted Conformal Prediction

author.name: Kianoosh Ashouritaklimi, Stefano Cortinovis, Fran\c{c}ois Caron

entry.summary: RoBAS introduces robust nonconformity scores for Bayes-assisted conformal prediction, using heavy-tailed working models and empirical Bayes shrinkage to prevent prediction-set degradation when priors are misaligned with data. It preserves distribution-free coverage guarantees while adapting to prior quality.

entry.title: SelfMem: Self-Optimizing Memory for AI Agents

author.name: Shu Yang, Junchao Wu, Derek F. Wong, Di Wang

entry.summary: SelfMem is a self-optimizing memory framework for AI agents that, rather than imposing a fixed storage/retrieval/summarization strategy, provides memory tools and feedback signals so the agent learns its own memory strategy. It follows the principle of teaching agents to manage memory rather than prescribing rigid mechanisms.

entry.title: Can Dialects Be Steered Like Languages? Sparse Neurons and Distributed Directions in Arabic LLMs

author.name: Kareem Elozeiri, Mervat Abassy, Omar Kallas, Fahim Dalvi, Preslav Nakov, Kentaro Inui, Nadir Durrani

entry.summary: This paper investigates where dialectal features are encoded in Arabic LLMs and whether they can be steered without fine-tuning, using neuron-level analysis to find sparse dialect-specific neuron populations plus distributed directional interventions. It shows amplifying or suppressing these neurons can control dialect generation, addressing MSA overproduction.

entry.title: FormalRx: Rectify and eXamine Semantic Failures in Autoformalization

author.name: Haocheng Wang, Baiyu Huang, Yingjia Wan, Xiao Zhu, Xiaoyang Liu, Yinya Huang, Zhijiang Guo

entry.summary: FormalRx is a diagnostic evaluation framework for autoformalization that replaces opaque binary or scalar verdicts with a hierarchical 28-category error taxonomy and actionable feedback. It aims to make formalization failures interpretable and improvable.

entry.title: DuplexChat: Constructing Speaker-Separated Full-Duplex Dialogue Speech at Scale for Spoken Dialogue Language Modeling

author.name: Wataru Nakata, Yuki Saito, Hiroshi Saruwatari

entry.summary: DuplexChat provides an open-source corpus and pipeline for constructing speaker-separated full-duplex dialogue speech from public podcast feeds, addressing the lack of suitable multi-stream data for spoken dialogue language models. It enables training of overlapping-turn conversational speech systems.

entry.title: Teaching Code LLMs to Reason with Intermediate Formal Specifications

author.name: Minh Le-Anh, Cuong Chi Le, Tien N. Nguyen

entry.summary: Studies executable checkpoint specification generation, teaching code LLMs to insert meaningful intermediate formal assertions at internal program points rather than only whole-program pre/postconditions. It addresses that current models produce assertions that are often invalid, trivial, or too weak.

entry.title: Track the Noise, Move the World:3D-Grounded Motion-Consistent Noise for Controllable Video Generation

author.name: Long Vu, Tan Ngo, Animesh Karnewar, Amir Habibian, Binh-Son Hua, Hung Bui, Minh Hoai Nguyen, Phong Nguyen-Ha

entry.summary: UniCaMo enables simultaneous control of object trajectories and camera viewpoints in image-and-text-to-video diffusion by constructing a shared 3D-grounded motion-consistent noise space, warping the reference frame's Gaussian noise along sparse 3D point tracks. It unifies object and camera motion control within a single generation process.

entry.title: CL-Anomaly: Layer-Adaptive Mixture-of-Experts with Multimodal Large Language Model for Continual Learning in Anomaly Detection

author.name: Wen Dong, Zhao Wang, Shuangqing Zhang, Kai Sun, Ben Li, Guo-Sen Xie, Caifeng Shan, Fang Zhao

entry.summary: CL-Anomaly is a parameter-efficient continual-learning framework for anomaly detection built on layer-adaptive mixture-of-experts atop a multimodal LLM, designed to avoid full retraining and semantic entanglement across tasks. It targets the triple heterogeneity of anomaly detection across modalities, domains, and defect scales.

entry.title: OmniDS: Dual-Stream Context Fusion for Omnidirectional Depth from Fisheye Cameras

author.name: Chaesong Park, Jihyeon Hwang, Muyeol Sung, Jongwoo Lim

entry.summary: OmniDS estimates omnidirectional depth from multi-fisheye camera rigs by replacing rigid fisheye-to-equirectangular sampling with dynamic context fusion and iterative refinement, resolving visibility conflicts near occlusion boundaries and thin structures. It targets ambiguous cross-view matching in wide-baseline rigs.

entry.title: CURE: Controllable Unified Image Restoration for Complex Degradations

author.name: Boseong Kim, Donghyeon Cho

entry.summary: CURE is a unified framework for controllable image restoration under complex composite degradations, learning disentangled and adjustable representations via four complementary objectives including identity embedding and reconstruction constraints. It enables selective control when multiple corruption factors interact.

entry.title: Beyond Post-Quantization: Native Hash Learning with a Dedicated HASH Token

author.name: Xinze Liu, Ding Wang, Hengjie Zhu, Dayan Wu

entry.summary: HashViT proposes native hash token learning inside a Vision Transformer via a dedicated HASH token, replacing the post-quantization paradigm that first learns continuous features then binarizes them. It aims to close the feature-to-code discrepancy for efficient Hamming-space image retrieval.

entry.title: Handwriting Trajectory Recovery with Diffusion Models

author.name: Hiroki Nagamatsu, Shoji Toyota, Seiichi Uchida

entry.summary: Presents the first diffusion-model-based framework for recovering online pen trajectories from offline handwriting images, formulating stroke recovery as image-conditioned generation. It reports accurate recovery for complex multi-stroke characters with improved temporal and spatial similarity metrics.

entry.title: Probing Identity-Specific Motion Signatures: A Controlled Diagnostic Study

author.name: Yingtie Lei, Fangxun Liu, Baicheng Wu, Colin Lee, Ziheng Zhang, Junke Yang, Zhiyuan Tao, Xuyan Huang, Shuheng Wang, William Koran, Kyle Park, Elijah H Buckwalter, Cheng-Hsuan Chiang, Tejas Naik, Daniel Yi, Wei-Lun Chao

entry.summary: Conducts a controlled diagnostic study of whether video models use identity-specific motion signatures for recognition, introducing BALLER120, a benchmark of 120 basketball players performing free-throws to minimize action and appearance confounds. It probes how much modern models rely on motion versus static appearance for identity.

entry.title: GeoSAM-Lite: A Lightweight Foundation Model for Onboard Remote Sensing Segmentation

author.name: Yongcong Wang, Jie Zhang, Rui Jiang, Xubing Yang, Ting Yun, Li Zhang

entry.summary: GeoSAM-Lite is a lightweight, prompt-free segmentation foundation model for onboard remote sensing that distills geospatial priors from a specialized teacher and adds feature fusion layers to bridge the domain gap between natural and remote sensing imagery. It targets efficient deployment on resource-constrained Earth observation platforms.

entry.title: City-Level 3D Surface Reconstruction with Viewpoint Orientation Partitioning and Scene Completion

author.name: Liang Han, Wenyuan Zhang, Junsheng Zhou, Yu-Shen Liu, Zhizhong Han

entry.summary: Presents a viewpoint-orientation-based scene partitioning method for city-level 3D surface reconstruction with 3D Gaussian Splatting, ensuring views with similar orientations are grouped for more accurate depth and balanced memory, plus scene completion. It tackles complex geometry, long optimization, and memory limits at large scale.

entry.title: TestMate: Test-Time Domain Adaptation Aided by Lightweight Vision Foundation Model

author.name: Dimitrios Fotiou, Vasileios Mygdalis, Ioannis Pitas

entry.summary: TestMate is a real-time, backpropagation-free test-time domain adaptation framework for semantic segmentation that uses a lightweight visual foundation model to guide adaptation, avoiding entropy-minimization backprop costs and slow memory-bank convergence. It targets continuous domain shifts on streaming unlabeled data.

entry.title: ContiStain: Cross-Domain Relation-Preserving Distillation for Continual Multi-Domain Virtual IHC Staining

author.name: Fuqiang Chen, Yifeng Wang, Hongpeng Wang, Yongbing Zhang

entry.summary: ContiStain is a cross-domain relation-preserving distillation framework for continual multi-domain virtual IHC staining from H&E slides, addressing severe forgetting when new biomarker data arrive sequentially by preserving structured relationships among biomarker representations. It enables scalable, parameter-efficient multiplex virtual staining over time.

entry.title: FRFDet: Efficient UAV Small Object Detection with Symmetric Sampling and Scalable Fusion

author.name: Yunzhong Si, Huiying Xu, Xinzhong Zhu, Yang Liu, Yao Dong, Wenhao Zhang, Hongbo Li

entry.summary: FRFDet is a lightweight single-stage detector for small objects in UAV imagery under adverse conditions, using inverse bidirectional sampling and scale-feature cross-fusion modules. It targets detail preservation and semantic-spatial fusion for tiny objects.

entry.title: Event Detection in Videos: A Framework for the Development of New Methods

author.name: Anastasia Zakharova, Thierry Bouwmans, Anthony Cioppa, Adrien Deli\`ege, Antonio Greco, Ana\"is Halin, Kamil Jeziorek, Meghna Kapoor, Tomasz Kryjak, Islam Osman, S\'ebastien Pi\'erard, Carlo Sansone, Mohamed S. Shehata, Renaud Vandeghen, Marc Van Droogenbroeck, Bruno Vento

entry.summary: This paper proposes a framework and evaluation methodology to standardize development and comparison of video event detection methods across pixel, frame, and clip levels. It responds to the lack of large-scale datasets and rigorous benchmarking that biases current comparisons.

entry.title: Enhancing Facial Expression Recognition in Head-Mounted Displays with Synthetic Data

author.name: Jianing Deng, Qiang Zhou, Jingtong Hu

entry.summary: This paper proposes a data synthesis framework that generates head-mounted-camera-view facial expression images from frontal-view images via 3D textured mesh reconstruction and a configurable virtual camera. It addresses FER data scarcity for mixed-reality HMDs.

entry.title: Occluding the Solution Space: Planner-Agnostic Adversarial Attacks on Tolerance-Aware Manipulation

author.name: Keke Tang, Tianyu Hao, Weilong Peng, Hao Jiang, Feng Wu, Peican Zhu, Jianmin Ji, Zhihong Tian

entry.summary: Proposes a planner-agnostic adversarial attack framework for tolerance-aware manipulation, evaluating task-level feasibility over goal regions and inserting adversarial obstacles without oracle access to the planner, using an offline kinematic occupancy heatmap. It reframes robustness evaluation of motion planning around goal regions rather than exact poses.

entry.title: ObjRetarget: An Object-Aware Motion Retargeting Framework with Anthropomorphic Arm Constraints and Polyhedral Hand Modeling

author.name: Yuanchuan Lai, Qing Gao, Ziyan Liang, Junjie Hu, Zhaojie Ju

entry.summary: ObjRetarget is an object-aware human-to-robot motion retargeting framework combining anthropomorphic arm trajectory constraints with polyhedral hand-object geometric modeling to maintain stable hand-object contact when learning from human videos. It aims to improve retargeting accuracy without relying on reinforcement learning.

entry.title: FLOAT Drone for Physical Interaction: Lateral Airflow Reduction, Wrench Modeling, and Adaptive Control

author.name: Junxiao Lin, Kehan Zhou, Shuhang Ji, Yimin Peng, Shen Wang, Jialiang Hou, Fei Gao

entry.summary: FLOAT Drone is a fully actuated coaxial UAV with servo-driven control surfaces for close-proximity physical interaction, designed to reduce lateral airflow toward targets, model contact wrenches, and adaptively control contact forces. It addresses compactness, multidimensional wrench generation, and airflow disturbance simultaneously.

entry.title: Minimax Estimation of Kernel Stein Discrepancy: Trace versus Hilbert-Schmidt Scales

author.name: Davit Gogolashvili

entry.summary: Identifies the sharp spectral constant governing minimax risk for estimating Kernel Stein Discrepancy as the Hilbert-Schmidt norm of the Stein covariance operator, showing a positive-part square-root U-statistic attains this scale while the standard plug-in V-statistic is suboptimal at the trace scale. It sharpens theory for goodness-of-fit and sample-quality assessment.

entry.title: Emputation: Identification-Guided Neural Imputation Framework

author.name: Yanjiao Yang, Yikun Zhang, Xinwei Shen, Yen-Chi Chen

entry.summary: Emputation is a deep generative imputation framework that targets the extrapolation distribution of missing variables given observed ones, using an energy-score-based emputation risk whose masking and observation weighting are dictated by identification assumptions guaranteeing recovery of the target distribution. It enables direct conditional sampling for multiple imputation.

entry.title: ASK in the Dark: Uncertainty-Gated LLM Assistance under Partial Observability

author.name: Juarez Monteiro, Nathan Gavenski, Guilherme Lima, Francisco Galuppo, Odinaldo Rodrigues, Adriano Veloso

entry.summary: ASK+ improves how small language models guide partially observable RL agents by supplying trajectory-aware context such as partial maps and visited positions, fixing the near-zero contribution rate of naive uncertainty-gated approaches. The key insight is that failure was a context problem rather than a model capacity problem.

entry.title: APeB: Benchmarking Personalization Ability of Large Language Model Agents

author.name: Garry Yang, Zizhe Chen, Xinru Chen, Yongqiang Chen, Jianxiang Wang, Deyu Zou, Linyi Ding, Jialiang Wu, Yunzhong He, Yu Gong, James Cheng, Huaixiao Tou

entry.summary: APeB is a personalized product search benchmark built from action logs, pairing underspecified raw queries with rich noisy interaction histories and viewed candidate items to test agent personalization under realistic conditions. It reveals that state-of-the-art LLMs handle explicit queries well but struggle to infer latent intent from noisy histories.

entry.title: Reflective Dialogue or Prompt Refinement? Effects of Tutor Scaffolding on Students' Independent LLM Use for Programming

author.name: Jerome Brender, Laila El-Hamamsy, Kim Uittenhove, Aitor Perez, Patrick Jermann, Francesco Mondada, Engin Bumbacher

entry.summary: A two-phase classroom study in a graduate robotics course compares a Socratic-guidance tutor against a prompt-refinement tutor and measures how each shapes students' subsequent independent LLM use, learning, and prompting practices. It examines whether scaffolding style transfers to unsupervised LLM use.

entry.title: VISTA: Auditing Semantic Divergence in Vision-Language Models

author.name: Junchi Liao, Jiawen Deng, Fuji Ren

entry.summary: VISTA is a black-box cross-model audit that combines semantic entropy with distribution-based divergence to flag visual-concept-conditioned biases in VLMs (e.g., toward logos, demographics, or symbols) that text-only audits miss. The authors implant biases via fine-tuning and confirm VISTA detects them.

entry.title: Teacher Supervision over Representation Equivalence Classes

author.name: Sang Il Han

entry.summary: This theoretical paper argues that knowledge distillation should target the teacher's representation equivalence class (up to orthogonal transform and isotropic scaling) rather than absolute features, since only class-invariant quantities recover capability. It reframes distillation objectives around quotient invariants.

entry.title: A Failure-Mode Benchmark for Polymorphic Sybil Poisoning in RAG

author.name: Donghyun Lee (Dongguk University), Juntae Kim (Dongguk University)

entry.summary: This paper releases a failure-mode-aware benchmark for RAG under coordinated retrieval poisoning, categorizing reader outputs as gold, hijack, abstention, or drift, and introduces polymorphic sybil poisoning where lexically diverse passages jointly support an attacker target while evading duplicate filters. It provides a Forced Exposure protocol isolating reader-side conflict resolution.

entry.title: Beyond Multilingual Averages: MTEB-PT, a Benchmark for Portuguese Sentence Encoders

author.name: Lucas Hideki Takeuchi Okamura, Alexandre Alcoforado, Anna Helena Reali Costa

entry.summary: MTEB-PT is a Portuguese-specific embedding benchmark built from a subset of MMTEB spanning STS, classification, retrieval, and reranking across 14 datasets, used to evaluate 17 models. It shows multilingual rankings poorly predict Portuguese task performance.

entry.title: SOV-CAD: Stepwise Orthographic Views Guided CAD Modeling Sequence Reconstruction

author.name: Zhaopeng Feng, Chen Zhi, Xuhong Zhang, Zhengwen Feng, Xinkui Zhao

entry.summary: SOV-CAD reconstructs CAD modeling sequences from images by formulating it as sequential decision-making with stepwise orthographic-view feedback, observing target and in-progress projections plus the active sketch. It mimics iterative human design workflows rather than one-shot generation.

entry.title: Fixed-Confidence Best-Arm Identification for Causal Mediation Analysis

author.name: Harsh Shrivastava, Yuta Kawakami, Junpei Komiyama, Jin Tian

entry.summary: This paper develops fixed-confidence best-arm identification to find the treatment maximizing the expected natural direct potential outcome in a causal-bandit setting, using a Track-and-Stop algorithm with a cutting-set method for the resulting semi-infinite optimization. It establishes population identification and sample-efficient guarantees.

entry.title: Generative wave propagator

author.name: Shijun Cheng, Tariq Alkhalifah

entry.summary: This paper introduces a conditional diffusion-based seismic wavefield propagator that recursively advances wavefields time step to time step, conditioned on recent snapshots, the velocity model, and time index, bypassing the dispersion and stability limits of finite-difference methods. It targets faster iterative seismic inversion workflows.

entry.title: Operator-on-F complements value-equivalence: a planning-time diagnostic for latent world models

author.name: Donna Vakalis

entry.summary: This paper introduces operator-on-F, a planning-time diagnostic that compares a model's k-step latent pushforward to the environment's on an observable subset using the model's own predictor, complementing value-equivalence checks that leave planning-relevant latent errors unmeasured. Experiments on TD-MPC2 show reward-fit metrics have narrow resolution to distinguish model quality.

entry.title: Regime-Conditional Stabilisation of LLM-Augmented Cooperative Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Faid Keddouri, Sohaib Houhou, Aissa Boulmerka, Nadir Farhi

entry.summary: This paper shows that dynamically updating LLM-generated reward weights during off-policy multi-agent RL violates potential-based reward-shaping stationarity and contaminates the replay buffer with stale-shaped transitions, then proposes phase-based freezing and other stabilization strategies. It characterizes a regime-dependent failure whose severity depends on baseline competence.

entry.title: Auto: The AGI Compiler

author.name: Jaber Jaber, Osama Jaber

entry.summary: Auto is a compiler that records live LLM-agent behavior, measures which parts are deterministic, extracts them into verified programs or distilled specialists, and emits sandbox-enforced WebAssembly cognition binaries with measured guarantees, deopting to the reference agent when guards trip. It claims a narrow, testable sense of AGI compiler as autonomously converting agent cognition into verified artifacts.

entry.title: Predicting Therapeutic Outcome via Aligning Patient-Specific Knowledge Graph and Gene-Level Perturbation Representations

author.name: Dongmin Bang, Sugyun An, Inyoung Sung, Ilho Yun, Sun Kim, Sangseon Lee

entry.summary: PREDIKTOR is a patient-centered multi-view framework that aligns a personalized gene regulatory network view with a transferable transcriptomic perturbation view to predict clinical drug response from pre-treatment transcriptomes. It combines mechanistic knowledge-graph context with drug-induced perturbation dynamics to overcome scarce clinical labels.

entry.title: In-span learning: adapting reduced-order models using their own predictions

author.name: Amirpasha Hedayat, Laura Balzano, Karthik Duraisamy

entry.summary: In-span learning shows that reduced-order models can be adapted online using their own predictions streamed through an incremental SVD with forgetting, acting as a trajectory-informed spectral preconditioner that reweights the existing subspace basis.
