This complements methods that require out-of-span corrections from full-order solves or sensors.

entry.title: Robustness Meets Uncertainty: Evidential Adversarial Training for Robust Selective Classification

author.name: Nicolas Sournac, Ahmed Baha Ben Jmaa, Bertrand Braeckeveldt

entry.summary: This paper builds a unified benchmark for the robustness-uncertainty trade-off in adversarial training, analyzed through selective classification, and proposes evidential adversarial training. It uncovers a recurring failure mode where adversarially trained models produce unreliable predictive uncertainty.

entry.title: WeightCLIP: Aligning Datasets and Models for Weight Space Learning

author.name: Aron Asefaw, Konstantinos Tzevelekakis, Damian Falk, L\'eo Meynent, Damian Borth

entry.summary: WeightCLIP learns a dataset-aligned latent space for neural networks by encoding model weights via an autoencoder and dataset samples via a dataset encoder, aligning them with a contrastive objective. This reshapes weight-space representations using information about training data, enabling new downstream applications.

entry.title: Directional Curvature from Armijo Backtracking: A Low-Cost Sharpness Probe and a Calibration-Free Learning-Rate Safeguard for Adam

author.name: Ashmitha R, J\"org Frochte

entry.summary: This paper shows that a single Armijo backtracking line search cheaply brackets the directional loss curvature, with the accepted step size tracking the top Hessian eigenvalue at Pearson correlations of -0.91 to -0.95 across several datasets. It yields a low-cost sharpness probe and a calibration-free learning-rate safeguard for Adam.

entry.title: Beyond Satisfaction: Learning Associations Between Content, Reviews, and Well-Being

author.name: Aaron Marker, Joel Lehman, H. Andrew Schwartz

entry.summary: This study examines whether common satisfaction signals (ratings, likes, sentiment) actually capture multidimensional well-being, using book consumption and long-form reviews as a testbed. It finds that ratings and sentiment only loosely correlate with well-being outcomes.

entry.title: Graph Neural Networks for the Graphical Bootstrap

author.name: Rigers Aliaj, Gabriele Dian, Reza Doobary, Paul Heslop

entry.summary: The paper benchmarks GNNs and graph transformers on over 20 million graphs from planar N=4 super-Yang-Mills perturbative correlator computations, achieving near-perfect classification and using models to shrink redundant graphical-bootstrap data. It also probes embedding interpretability.

entry.title: AI-RAN on NPUs: Baseband Processing Without Baseband Chips

author.name: Shilong Zhang, Luping Xiang, Jienan Chen, Kun Yang

entry.summary: This paper shows that Neural Processing Units, typically built for AI inference, can perform wireless baseband processing by exploiting a computational isomorphism between matrix/vector engines and physical-layer operations. It offers the first affirmative demonstration of AI-RAN on NPUs rather than GPUs.

entry.title: Deep Learning for Dynamic Programming with Recursive Utility

author.name: Xianhua Peng, Wu Guo

entry.summary: The Certainty Equivalent Learning algorithm is presented as the first deep learning method for high-dimensional dynamic programming with recursive utility, jointly learning value, policy, and certainty-equivalent functions. It is mesh-free and simulation-based, tackling problems intractable for classical Euler-equation methods.

entry.title: TACG: Trajectory-Aware Commit Gating for Diffusion Language Model Decoding

author.name: Chengcheng Wang, Tingzhang Luo, Wenhao Li, Jianyuan Guo, Chang Xu

entry.summary: TACG is a training-free decoder for diffusion language models that anchors token identities to the base posterior and uses trajectory-aware signals to decide commitment readiness rather than committing from a single snapshot. It combats premature locking-in of transient top-1 peaks by leveraging the full denoising trajectory.

entry.title: ReLo-IRR: Reflection-Guided LoRA Framework for Image Reflection Removal

author.name: Chaoqun Wang, Yuehuan Wei, Haoxiang Cao, Shaobo Min

entry.summary: ReLo-IRR is a reflection-guided LoRA framework for single-image reflection removal that first estimates a reflection-strength descriptor, then modulates LoRA adaptation per image based on reflection severity. It aims to fix the over- or under-suppression of image-agnostic diffusion adaptation strategies.

entry.title: Sparse-View Surface Reconstruction using Gaussian Splatting through High-Confidence Depth Propagation with Normal Priors

author.name: Liang Han, Bangcai Wei, Junsheng Zhou, Yu-Shen Liu, Zhizhong Han

entry.summary: Proposes a 3D Gaussian Splatting method for high-fidelity surface reconstruction from sparse views using normal-guided depth propagation that extends depth from high-confidence to low-confidence regions, plus abnormal depth edge-aware regularization. It addresses limited geometry cues and Gaussian discreteness in sparse-view settings.

entry.title: Probabilistic Robustness in Medical Image Classification

author.name: Yi Zhang, Siddartha Khastgir, Xingyu Zhao

entry.summary: Investigates probabilistic robustness as a more practical trustworthiness measure than worst-case adversarial robustness for medical image classification, constructing natural corruption settings and systematically evaluating common models on MedMNIST v2. It provides a statistically grounded assessment relevant to clinical safety.

entry.title: Green for Go, Red for No: Visual Grounding via Semantic Segmentation for VLA Navigation Policies

author.name: Adrian Szvoren, Dimitrios Kanoulas, Nilufer Tuptuk

entry.summary: This paper adds a real-time semantic-segmentation grounding layer to vision-language-action navigation policies, coloring traversable regions green and non-traversable red to reduce perceptual ambiguity. Applied to OmniVLA on the Grand Tour dataset, it reduces waypoint error by 27-44%, especially for long instructions.

entry.title: High-Precision Formation Control for Heterogeneous Multi-Robot Systems via Hierarchical Hybrid Physics-Informed Deep Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Yanzhou Li, Guangli Chen, Xiao-Meng Li, Wenjian Zhong, Yongkang Lu, Shenghuang He

entry.summary: Proposes a two-layer hierarchical hybrid physics-informed deep RL framework for high-precision formation control of heterogeneous multi-robot systems, combining SAC-based navigation with physics-informed lower control. It targets the sample efficiency and robustness gaps between classical control and end-to-end RL.

entry.title: Locally Private Online Quantile Regression: Estimation and Inference

author.name: Yi Liu, Qirui Hu

entry.summary: Studies online quantile regression estimation and inference under a strict one-report user-level epsilon-local differential privacy protocol, developing a finite-alphabet channel with support-aware stochastic quantization and randomized response plus a public decoder that corrects distortion. It enables private online updates where the standard estimating equation cannot be formed.

entry.title: Evaluating Generative Agents with Actions Grounded in Socially Distributed Task Environments using Incognita

author.name: Dan C. Hsu, Luke Lu

entry.summary: The paper defines socially distributed task environments where task-relevant knowledge is partitioned across role-isolated participants and consequential actions are only reachable through them, using the Incognita platform. Communication acts as exploration and grounded action as exploitation, combining grounded benchmarks with social simulation.

entry.title: Beyond Forecasting: The Belief-to-Trade Layer in Prediction-Market Agents

author.name: Yishu Wang, Yuxuan Wang, Jiaqi Deng, Hanyang Tang

entry.summary: Raven-Agent is presented as the first autonomous trading agent for prediction markets, adding a belief-to-trade decision layer on top of forecasting to address the gap between calibrated probabilities and trading returns. On an archived replay it is reported as the only policy with positive risk-adjusted return.

entry.title: Organizational Memory for Agentic Business Process Execution

author.name: Lukas Kirchdorfer, Adrian Rebmann, Christian Warmuth, Timotheus Kampik, Theiss Heilker, Gregor Berg

entry.summary: This position-and-system paper argues for organizational memory as a shared, governed substrate that consolidates fragmented enterprise knowledge (policies, process models, SOPs) for reliable agentic business process execution, avoiding knowledge silos and rule duplication. It targets scalable, consistently updatable knowledge across many agents.

entry.title: Can Conversational Temporal Dynamics Improve Depression Detection in Dyads? A Preliminary Investigation in Multi-Modality Perspectives

author.name: Hanie Kang, Huang-Cheng Chou, Sudarsana Reddy Kadiri, Shrikanth Narayanan

entry.summary: This preliminary study models dyadic turn-pair timing between clinician and participant as a distinct modality for depression detection, fusing a compact 24-dimensional timing module with WavLM and RoBERTa encoders on the DAIC-WOZ dataset. The timing module achieves the best single-modality development performance and improves late-fusion results.

entry.title: Compressing the Validation Bottleneck: An Agentic Self-Driving Lab for Scientific Discovery

author.name: Kyunghoon Hur, Chihun Lee

entry.summary: This work targets two physical bottlenecks in agentic self-driving labs: a prior-aware agentic design-of-experiments loop that uses domain knowledge and past results to reduce trials-to-target, and a cost-aware surrogate agent that predicts high-cost high-resolution measurements from cheap low-resolution ones. Together they compress the validation bottleneck in autonomous scientific discovery.

entry.title: FM-ChangeNet: Learning Change through Pathwise Feature Transport

author.name: Roie Kazoom, George Leifman, Genady Beryozkin

entry.summary: FM-ChangeNet reformulates bi-temporal change detection as continuous transport in feature space, constructing intermediate latent states and learning a time-conditioned velocity field along the transformation trajectory to provide denser supervision than endpoint-only comparison. The learned velocity field also serves as an interpretable representation of change.

entry.title: TacReasoner: A Dynamic Tactile-Language Framework for Interactive Reasoning in Real-World Scenarios

author.name: Kailin Lyu, Di Wu, Long Xiao, Jianning Zeng, Jianwei He, Chang Lin, Lianyu Hu, Lin Shu, Jie Hao, Ce Hao

entry.summary: TacReasoner is a dynamic tactile-language framework that models temporally evolving tactile signals and adds explicit reasoning to reduce hallucination in tactile foundation models. It targets multimodal reasoning over physical contact for real-world interaction.

entry.title: PEEK: Predictive Queue-Informed KV Cache Management for LLM Serving

author.name: Bing Xie, Zhipeng Wang, Masahiro Tanaka, Zheng Zhen

entry.summary: PEEK is a queue-informed KV-cache scheduling and eviction framework that maintains a radix tree over the pending queue to expose prefix-sharing clusters, admitting cluster pioneers first so siblings reuse cached prefixes. It delivers up to 3x improvements on SGLang and vLLM across workloads.

entry.title: DELTAVID: Enhancing Fine-Grained Spatiotemporal Perception with Cross-Video Differences

author.name: Yankai Yang, Yancheng Long, Bin Wen, Fan Yang, Tingting Gao, Han Li, Shuo Yang

entry.summary: DELTAVID enhances fine-grained spatiotemporal perception in video MLLMs by turning cross-video spot-the-difference into a trainable, verifiable perception signal for locating local changes and temporal boundaries. It addresses models' failure to detect subtle differences between near-identical videos.

entry.title: Signal from Space: Detecting Schools and Towers to Bridge the Digital Divide

author.name: Zakarya Elmimouni, Sandor Farkas, Fares Fourati, Vladimir Daigele, Walid Mathlouthi, Mohamed-Slim Alouini

entry.summary: Proposes a vision-only, transfer-learning framework using high-resolution satellite imagery to jointly detect schools and cell towers to map connectivity infrastructure for the Giga Initiative. It aims to bridge the digital divide without relying on sparse or noisy third-party datasets.

entry.title: Seduced by the Narrative: Assessing Rule Adherence in Semi-Open Textual Sandboxes

author.name: Weiying Chen, Junlong Shen, Zhanyuan Guo, Xiaoou Zhou

entry.summary: CoC-Seduce is a multi-agent adversarial benchmark built on tabletop RPG mechanics to test whether LLM adjudicators maintain rule adherence when users deploy Rhetorical Injection, exploiting narrative framing, pseudo-logic, and authoritative coercion to bypass adjudication logic. It exposes vulnerabilities from models' trained helpfulness and compliance.

entry.title: Vision Token Manipulation Attacks on Cloud-Edge Inference of Large Vision-Language Models

author.name: Zikai Zhang, Rui Hu, Olivera Kotevska, Jiahao Xu

entry.summary: Studies vision-token manipulation attacks in split cloud-edge vision-language inference, where an adversary intercepts and perturbs a subset of transmitted vision tokens under a budget in a black-box man-in-the-middle setting. Experiments across six LVLMs show manipulating only 10% of tokens can substantially reduce accuracy.

entry.title: PPE-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating MLLM Unlearning under Private-Public Entanglement

author.name: Xianren Zhang, Delvin Ce Zhang, Dongwon Lee, Suhang Wang

entry.summary: PPE-Bench is a benchmark for evaluating MLLM unlearning under private-public entanglement, using visually complex real-world photos where private individuals appear alongside public figures or backgrounds, rather than isolated single-target images. It relaxes the unrealistic assumption that forget and retain sets are fully separated.

entry.title: PromptPET: Privacy-Utility Optimized Prompt Obfuscation

author.name: Ke Yang, Olivia Figueira, Umar Iqbal, Athina Markopoulou

entry.summary: PromptPET is a user-side mechanism that obfuscates sensitive content in prompts (via redaction, abstraction, replacement, or a novel noising/denoising scheme) to prevent chatbot profiling while preserving response utility. It formalizes and empirically studies the privacy-utility tradeoff.

entry.title: Which Algorithm Specification Formats Help Language Models Implement Machine Learning Algorithms?

author.name: Masahiro Kato, Taka Kato

entry.summary: This empirical study tests how the written format of an algorithm specification (prose, LaTeX pseudocode, JSON/YAML, Python stubs, etc.) affects first-pass LLM implementation accuracy across five ML tasks and three models, with 4,020 generated implementations checked by hidden tests. It reveals which formats best convey implementation details.

entry.title: Self-Specializing Vision-Language Transmon Chip Calibration in a Physics-Grounded Environment

author.name: Animesh Tripathy, Aswanth Krishnan

entry.summary: This paper studies whether a vision-language agent can self-specialize to calibrate a single superconducting transmon chip without weight updates, using a physics-grounded simulation environment with realistic drift, distortion, and leakage. It frames calibration as a sequential decision problem under noise and budget.

entry.title: HiMe: Hierarchical Embodied Memory for Long-Horizon Vision-Language-Action Control

author.name: Li Ji, Siyin Wang, Pengfang Qian, Xiaopeng Yu, Yihai Tian, Zhaoye Fei, Jingjing Gong, Xipeng Qiu

entry.summary: HiMe is a hierarchical embodied memory framework for long-horizon vision-language-action control that decouples intelligence into a high-frequency Executor, a Sentry for working memory, and a Planner for long-term strategy, resolving the frequency-competence paradox between fast control and slow reasoning. It adds a dynamic knowledge system based on cross-modal semantic schemas.

entry.title: Attending to Multimodal Generation One Token at a Time

author.name: Varun Gupta, Vineet Gandhi, Makarand Tapaswi

entry.summary: This interpretability study (OTaT) tracks how multimodal LLMs shift attention across image, text, instruction, and generated tokens one token at a time during autoregressive generation, using tasks requiring explicit visual-textual switching. It reveals consistent temporal dynamics of multimodal computation across model families.

entry.title: Consistent but Miscalibrated: Evaluating LLM Limitations for Risk Communication in Natural Language

author.name: Diego Cerda-Mardini, Sarath Chandar, Sreenath Madathil

entry.summary: This study evaluates whether nine LLMs can reliably communicate probabilistic information verbally, requiring identical verbal descriptions for identical inputs and descriptors that match numerical magnitudes. It finds models are consistent but miscalibrated, undermining their use as post-hoc risk explainers.

entry.title: Probe, Don't Prompt: A Hidden-State Probe for Metadata Filtering in Multi-Meta-RAG

author.name: Mykhailo Poliakov, Nadiya Shvai

entry.summary: This work replaces the proprietary GPT-3.5 metadata extractor in Multi-Meta-RAG with a local deterministic probe trained on a small model's hidden states, reaching 90.9% set-exact accuracy versus 80.9% for GPT-3.5 with guaranteed output within the fixed source vocabulary. It shows a lightweight probe can outperform and constrain LLM-based metadata filtering.

entry.title: NormWorlds-CF: Solver-Verified Counterfactual Normative Reasoning with Metamorphic-Relation GRPO

author.name: Xinqi Zhang

entry.summary: NormWorlds-CF is a solver-verified environment for counterfactual normative reasoning in executable rule worlds, providing proof and falsification certificates and paired-world change labels for supervision without LLM judges. It shows answer-only supervision is an unsafe proxy while proof-plus-falsification training generalizes better.

entry.title: Parametric Memory Decoding for Zero-Shot Routing in LoRA-Based External Parametric Memory

author.name: Fengxian Ji, Zhuohan Xie, Jingpu Yang, Fan Zhang, Zirui Song, Xiuying Chen

entry.summary: This work studies zero-shot routing of a bank of LoRA modules used as external parametric memory without maintaining a separate routing component, introducing PMD-Bench spanning document, domain, and skill levels. It unifies scattered LoRA routing evaluations under an external-memory perspective.

entry.title: Conflict-Based Lazy Search for Fast Multi-Manipulator Planning

author.name: Dongliang Zheng, Zhipeng Wang, Siqi Wang, Yuxi Lu, Bin He, Hesheng Wang, Panagiotis Tsiotras

entry.summary: CBLS is a multi-manipulator motion planner that extends Conflict-Based Search with lazy single-agent search to speed real-time planning in cluttered workspaces. It targets the bottleneck of the single-agent solver within CBS.

entry.title: Hierarchical Multi-to-Single-Modal Knowledge Distillation for Disruption Prediction in EAST

author.name: Qiang Chen, Xiao Wang, Hao Si, Qingquan Yang, Meiwen Chen, Jianhua Yang, Xiaofeng Han, Yunhu Jia, Ran Chen, Liang Wang, Jin Tang, Guosheng Xu

entry.summary: This work uses hierarchical multi-to-single-modal knowledge distillation to transfer disruption-precursor knowledge from a multimodal (image plus time-series) teacher to a time-series-only student for tokamak plasma disruption prediction. It retains image-derived discriminative power while reducing inference cost.

entry.title: LBR: Towards Mitigating Length Bias in Large Language Models for Recommendation

author.name: Hongchen Li, Bohao Wang, Jingbang Chen, Weiqin Yang, Hang Pan, Bingde Hu, Can Wang, Jiawei Chen

entry.summary: LBR identifies and mitigates length bias in LLM-based recommenders, where longer item descriptions dominate attention on input and are disfavored in autoregressive scoring on output. It proposes corrections beyond naive length normalization.

entry.title: Risk-Constrained Freshness-Aware Semantic Caching for Open-Web Retrieval-Augmented LLMs

author.name: Muhammad Mansoor, Tahir Ahmad, Yeo-Chan Yoon

entry.summary: FreshCache is a three-tier semantic cache for open-web RAG that models the time-varying staleness of cached results via a fitted exponential-decay-plus-MLP estimator, approving reuse only when the estimated stale-probability falls below per-tier error budgets. It frames cache reuse as a risk-constrained temporal inference problem.

entry.title: IRIS: An Intelligent Vision-Language System for Ocular Surface Diseases via Topic Tree and Scene-Driven VQA Generation

author.name: Hao Wei, Wenjin Qi, Dasen Dai, Minqing Zhang, Wu Yuan

entry.summary: IRIS is a vision-language system for ocular surface diseases, introducing IRIS-120K, described as the largest OSD VQA dataset, generated via a topic-tree and scene-driven pipeline that injects clinical priors into image-caption pairs. It aims to overcome the data bottleneck for fine-grained clinical VLM reasoning.

entry.title: HASSL: Hierarchy-Aware Self-Supervised Learning Framework for Single Cell Microscopy

author.name: Julius Riel, Vishwa Mohan Singh, Sai Anirudh Aryasomayajula, Anuun Chinbat, Hannes Leonhard, Moritz Ladenburger, Frederik Alexander, Vishisht Choudhary, Fabio Laredo, Giacomo Masserdotti, Thorben Prein, Carsten Marr, Amirhossein Kardoost

entry.summary: HASSL is a hierarchy-aware self-supervised framework for single-cell microscopy that combines a segmentation-teacher distillation for morphological awareness with an HDBSCAN-based hierarchy-aware contrastive loss to separate closely related subtypes. It prevents coarse factors like imaging modality from suppressing fine morphological structure.

entry.title: Auto-AEG: Scalable Data Construction for Open-Vocabulary Audio Event Grounding

author.name: Zihan Zhang, Xize Cheng, Wenhao Yan, Tong Zhang, Dongjie Fu, Boyun Zhang, Yongbo He, Tao Jin

entry.summary: Auto-AEG is a scalable pipeline that automatically constructs open-vocabulary audio event grounding data with onset/offset supervision, addressing the scarcity of temporally annotated open-vocabulary resources for large audio-language models. It bridges free-form querying with frame-level localization.

entry.title: Failures and Successes to Learn a Core Conceptual Distinction from the Statistics of Language

author.name: Zhimin Hu, Jeroen van Paridon, Gary Lupyan

entry.summary: This paper tests whether the human-like distinction between principled properties (true by category membership) and merely statistical properties can be learned from language statistics alone, examining generic statements like tigers are striped versus cars have radios. It probes whether language experience can bootstrap a core conceptual capacity.

entry.title: Lyapunov-Guided Training for Hardware-Safe Neural Networks Under Fixed-Point Arithmetic

author.name: Anis Hamadouche, Amir Hussain

entry.summary: This paper proposes a Lyapunov-stabilized quantization framework for low-precision neural networks operating under two's-complement wrapping arithmetic, monitoring layerwise hidden-state energy and applying a monotone projection to enforce bounded, non-increasing state evolution. It addresses overflow-wrapping failure modes hidden by idealized quantization models.

entry.title: EEG-SpikeAgent: Agentic Closed-Loop Program Synthesis for Automated EEG Spike Detection

author.name: Sonali Santhosh, Kelly Shuhong Yu, Eugene Chang, Jonathan Kim, Kie Shidara, Danilo Bernardo

entry.summary: EEG-SpikeAgent is a closed-loop program-synthesis framework where an LLM agentic system iteratively proposes deterministic signal-processing feature modules for epileptiform spike detection in scalp EEG, executes them, evaluates via a tabular classifier, and refines based on structured diagnostics. It trades some accuracy for interpretability compared to opaque deep models.

entry.title: Do All Visual Tokens Matter Equally? Object-Evidence Preserving Token Merging for Vision-Language Retrieval

author.name: Suhyeong Park, Junha Jung, Jungwoo Park, Jaewoo Kang

entry.summary: SaMer is an object-aware token merging framework for multi-vector vision-language retrieval that compresses image-side post-projector tokens into K representative centroids while preserving the late-interaction interface, using object annotations only during training as a merge prior. It requires no bounding boxes or detectors at inference and adapts only a small shared component.

entry.title: Strategic Buying Agents

author.name: Mingyang Fu, Ming Hu

entry.summary: This paper formalizes the design of autonomous strategic buying agents that decide when to purchase within a finite shopping window across stationary, Bayesian, and robust information regimes. It derives optimal purchase policies as a policy menu for delegated agentic shopping.

entry.title: SynSFX: Multi-Model Sound Effects Synthesis Dataset for Deepfake Detection and Evaluation

author.name: Linxi Li, Yuncong Yu, Qianwei Guo, Liwei Jin, Yechen Wang, Carsten Maple

entry.summary: SynSFX is a large-scale corpus of 43,374 clips (synthetic and real) spanning seven text-to-audio models to support deepfake detection of synthetic sound effects, where existing detectors generalize poorly. It expands the scale and generation provenance available for isolated sound-effect deepfake research.

entry.title: Beyond Independent Labels: Schwartz-Geometry Decoding for Human Value Detection

author.name: V\'ictor Yeste, Paolo Rosso

entry.summary: This paper operationalizes Schwartz's circular motivational continuum of human values as an explicit output-space geometry for value detection, comparing training-time geometry-aware objectives with a post-hoc Schwartz-aware energy decoder on a DeBERTa classifier. It finds training-time geometry gives only limited gains across seeds.

entry.title: Dynamic Regret for Non-Stationary Linear Bandits via Misspecification Reductions

author.name: Zihao Hu, Yuan Yao, Jiheng Zhang, Zhengyuan Zhou

entry.summary: This theory paper studies non-stationary linear bandits with round-specific feasible action sets, using a misspecification-reduction viewpoint to relax a restrictive orthogonal-structure assumption while retaining near-optimal dynamic regret. It broadens the applicability of drifting-reward bandit methods to contextual settings.

entry.title: Do ECG Foundation Models Transfer to Rare Cardiac Diseases? Evidence from Brugada Syndrome Detection

author.name: Beatrice Zanchi, Giuliana Monachino, Alvise Dei Rossi, Luigi Fiorillo, Georgia Sarquella-Brugada, Giulio Conte, Francesca Dalia Faraci

entry.summary: This study evaluates nine public ECG foundation models for detecting rare Brugada syndrome across two cohorts and three training strategies, testing whether pretraining offers genuine clinical generalization beyond optimization benefits. It probes the limits of foundation-model transfer to rare cardiac diseases.

entry.title: From Global to Local: Efficient Regional Weather Downscaling with Global Weather Foundation Model

author.name: Wiktor Kamzela, Jakub Kubiak, Adam Dobosz, J\k{e}drzej Miczke, Anatol Kaczmarek, Piotr Wyrwi\'nski, Wojciech Stefaniak, Wojciech Kot{\l}owski

entry.summary: This paper proposes a foundation-model-driven weather downscaling framework that adds lightweight multi-scale prediction heads operating in the latent space of a pretrained global weather model to produce fine-scale regional forecasts. It reframes downscaling as latent-space refinement rather than image super-resolution.

entry.title: A Near-Linear-Time Solver for Graph $p$-Laplacian Semi-Supervised Learning via Continuation in $p$

author.name: Oren E. Livne

entry.summary: This paper presents a near-linear-time solver for graph p-Laplacian semi-supervised learning via continuation in p, addressing the degeneracy of the standard p=2 Laplacian when labels are scarce. It integrates near-linear Laplacian solvers where naive approaches fail at large p.

entry.title: CDCP: Conditional Diffusion Model with Contextual Prompts for Multi-task Offline Safe Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Jiayi Guan, Tianle Zhang, Li Shen, Ruiqi Zhang, Ao Zhou, Lusong Li, Guai Chen, Mengjie Li, Alois Knoll, Xiaodong He, Changjun Jiang

entry.summary: CDCP is a conditional diffusion model with contextual prompts for multi-task offline safe reinforcement learning, jointly addressing multi-tasking, safety constraints, and out-of-distribution actions to learn a shared safe policy from offline data. It targets high-risk, high-interaction-cost multi-task settings.

entry.title: GlacierCastAI: Predicting Glacier Retreat from Multi-Modal Satellite Imagery and Climate Signals

author.name: Arunkumar Ramachandran

entry.summary: GlacierCastAI reframes glacier boundary prediction as multimodal spatiotemporal forecasting, fusing Landsat imagery, ERA5 climate reanalysis, and terrain data via a ResNet-ConvLSTM cross-attention architecture. It shows climate signals add predictive value beyond imagery for forecasting future retreat. It matters for climate monitoring but is a domain application of standard components.

entry.title: Physics-Informed Graph Learning with Uncertainty Awareness for Open-Set Domain Generalization in Fault Diagnosis

author.name: Jinfeng Zhu, Shiyu Long, Ye Yuan

entry.summary: PGU-OD is a physics-informed graph learning framework with uncertainty awareness for open-set domain generalization in rotating-machinery fault diagnosis. It uses a spectral attention module for condition-robust features and models cascaded uncertainty propagation across the pipeline. It matters for industrial predictive maintenance under domain shift and unknown faults.

entry.title: Counterfactual Methods for Detecting Unfairness in Anti-Money Laundering Algorithms

author.name: Lea Multerer, Michele Inchingolo, David Kletz, Adrian Cosma, Alessandro Antonucci, Martina Gogova

entry.summary: This paper applies counterfactual methods to detect unfairness in anti-money-laundering algorithms, decomposing direct and indirect effects of sensitive features on model predictions to assess whether their influence is acceptable. It matters as a fairness audit for underexamined AML systems that use sensitive client data.

entry.title: GeoFlow: Geo-Aware Modeling of Inter-Area Relationships in Origin-Destination Flow Prediction and Generation

author.name: Zherui Huang, Guanjie Zheng, Hao Xue, Linghe Kong

entry.summary: GeoFlow augments origin-destination flow modeling with geospatial attributes like relative positions and geodesic distances, using a geometric-intrinsic fusion encoder and an axial-global attention decoder to capture long-range multi-area dependencies for both flow prediction and generation. It matters for urban planning and mobility analysis.

entry.title: H-OPD: Confidence Aware Heterogeneous Multi-Teacher Multimodal On-policy Distillation

author.name: Qixiang Yin, Huanjin Yao, Yuchen Cai, Jianghao Chen, Ziyi Wang, Min Yang, Fei Su, Zhicheng Zhao

entry.summary: H-OPD is a confidence-aware, heterogeneous multi-teacher on-policy distillation framework for multimodal reasoning, replacing static per-sample teacher routing with token-level routing across a trajectory. It exploits complementarity between visual-grounding and abstract-reasoning teachers.

entry.title: Scaling Weisfeiler-Leman Expressiveness Analysis to Massive Graphs with GPUs

author.name: Filippo Biondi, Mirco Tribastone, Max Tschaikowski

entry.summary: The paper accelerates Weisfeiler-Leman stable-coloring computation—an upper bound on GNN expressiveness—via a linear-algebraic formulation with a randomized refinement algorithm and correctness-preserving batching for GPUs. It scales expressiveness analysis to massive graphs.

entry.title: EvoOtter: Evolutionary Reproduction Test Generator

author.name: Toufique Ahmed, Jatin Ganhotra, Avraham Shinnar, Martin Hirzel

entry.summary: EvoOtter applies enhanced evolutionary programming to generate bug reproduction tests from informal issue descriptions, sharpening execution feedback while controlling cost compared to LLM inference-scaling approaches. It comes from an IBM Research team.

entry.title: Complexity of Normalized Persistence Problems for Topological Data Analysis and Local Hamiltonians

author.name: Dominic Lowe, M. S. Kim, Roberto Bondesan, Ryu Hayakawa

entry.summary: This paper introduces normalized persistence, an interpretable variant of persistent homology counting the fraction of holes that persist across lengthscales, and proves a variant is DQC1-hard and in BQP, giving evidence of exponential quantum speedup for topological data analysis. It also connects to local Hamiltonian complexity.

entry.title: Piecewise Dynamic Diffusion Regularization for Reconstruction of Cardiac Cine MRI

author.name: Florian F\"urnrohr, Reinhard Heckel

entry.summary: Piecewise Dynamic Diffusion Regularization integrates a spatiotemporal diffusion prior with dedicated spatial and temporal layers into a variational reconstruction framework for undersampled real-time cardiac cine MRI. It applies the dynamic prior piecewise for computational efficiency.

entry.title: ADP: Adversarial Dynamics Priors for Physically Grounded Humanoid Locomotion

author.name: Seokju Lee, Jeongtae Lee, Jeonghyeok Lim, Jeonguk Kang, Byungwook Lee, Seungho Han, Keun Ha Choi, Dongil Park, Kyung-Soo Kim

entry.summary: Adversarial Dynamics Priors regularize humanoid locomotion policies against dynamics features (center-of-mass motion, centroidal momentum, contact forces/states) rather than only kinematic style, using trajectory-optimization references and a discriminator. It targets perturbation-resilient control.

entry.title: Relevance-Based Embeddings: Lightweight Candidate Retrieval via Heavy-Ranker Calls

author.name: Kirill Shevkunov, Andrey Ploskonosov, Liudmila Prokhorenkova

entry.summary: This paper proposes relevance-based embeddings for candidate retrieval that use scores from an expensive heavy ranker to construct lightweight query/item representations, improving candidate selection over standard two-tower models. It targets efficient retrieval before expensive re-ranking.

entry.title: USE: A Unified Self-Ensembling Framework for Test-Time Prompt Tuning

author.name: Siru Jiang, Jian Liang, Ran He, Tieniu Tan

entry.summary: USE reinterprets test-time prompt tuning as implicitly learning from self-generated pseudo labels and introduces a self-ensembling strategy ensuring consistency between optimization and inference stages for CLIP-based test-time adaptation. It strengthens a strong TPT baseline.

entry.title: Legible-by-Construction: Attention and End-to-End Transformers

author.name: Mark Oskin

entry.summary: This work extends prior interpretable-transformer design from feed-forward layers to attention, passing head values through a sigmoid so each value channel becomes a readable feature detector without adding parameters. The goal is a transformer that is 'legible by construction' while maintaining language-model quality.

entry.title: Context-Constrained Transfer Learning for Tabular Foundation Models via Data Distillation

author.name: Yijun Lin, Sai Li

entry.summary: TL-ANDI is a transfer-learning framework for tabular foundation models that builds a compact source context via a budget-constrained optimal transport problem measuring target coverage and posterior compatibility, followed by distillation. It addresses TFMs' context-size limits and sensitivity to source-target distribution shift to prevent negative transfer.

entry.title: Canonical quantization of neurons

author.name: Alexander He, Nana Liu, Mark M. Wilde

entry.summary: This work applies canonical quantization to the artificial neuron, replacing the energy function with a quantum Hamiltonian and applying activation via matrix functional calculus to yield a quantum 'activation observable' measurable on input states. The quantized neurons are investigated for learning unknown observables from labeled quantum data.

entry.title: Distill Where the Student Goes: Teacher-Regularized RL for English-Evidence Cross-Lingual RAG

author.name: Haotian Zhou, Weiran Huang, Siqi Liu, Xiting Wang, Xin Zhang, Zhihao Wen

entry.summary: TR-RAG is a teacher-regularized reinforcement learning recipe for cross-lingual RAG in the English-evidence regime, addressing language drift and unreliable evidence use when users query in diverse languages but passages are English. It couples reward optimization with teacher regularization to handle prefix-dependent errors and noisy sequence-level reward credit assignment.

entry.title: ProACT: Towards Breakdown-Aware Proactive Agent in Multi-User Collaboration

author.name: Shu Yang, Difei Xu, Jiaxin Pei, Di Wang

entry.summary: ProACT is a breakdown-aware agent framework for multi-user collaboration that moves conversational agents from passive responders to proactive participants, recognizing moments (disagreements, ambiguous goals, forgotten constraints, discussion loops) when timely intervention would help. It is grounded in theories of collaboration breakdowns.

entry.title: CrossHallu: Do Hallucination Signals Generalize Across Languages and Domains in Large Language Model's Internals?

author.name: Aisha Alansari, Malak Alkhorasani, Hamzah Luqman

entry.summary: CrossHallu is the first study to evaluate whether internal-representation-based hallucination detectors generalize across languages and domains, using internal states from six LLMs on generative question answering. It systematically tests cross-lingual and cross-domain transfer of hallucination signals that have mostly been studied only in English.

entry.title: Evaluating Large Language Models for Antisemitic Incident Classification

author.name: Karina Halevy, Julia Mendelsohn, Chan Young Park, Yulia Tsvetkov, Maarten Sap

entry.summary: Introduces the task of hateful event detection and evaluates GPT-4o and Llama-3.2-3B on classifying antisemitic events from news, civil-society, and official records with fine-grained labels. It finds LLMs show potential but need substantial improvement, aided by clear definitions and in-context examples.

entry.title: Token-level Response-visual Attention Guidance for Multimodal LLMs Knowledge Distillation

author.name: Jaehyun Jang, Eunseop Yoon, Hee Suk Yoon, SooHwan Eom, Mark A. Hasegawa-Johnson, Chang D. Yoo

entry.summary: For multimodal LLM knowledge distillation, this work shows downstream performance correlates strongly with response-to-vision attention similarity to the teacher rather than the conventionally distilled prompt-to-vision attention. It proposes token-level response-visual attention guidance for compressing MLLMs.

entry.title: DELTA-TTS: Adapting Autoregressive Model into Diffusion Language Model for Text-to-Speech

author.name: Junwon Moon, Seungbeom Kim, Yejin Lee, Hoseong Ahn, Sewoong Park, Heeseung Kim, Kyuhong Shim

entry.summary: DELTA-TTS is a lightweight LoRA adaptation that converts a pretrained autoregressive TTS model into a discrete diffusion language model for confidence-ordered speech-token decoding, improving robustness and reducing error propagation. It leverages the fact that full text is available before synthesis.

entry.title: Autonomous Information Seeking: A Roadmap for Agentic Recommender Systems

author.name: Xinyu Lin, Yashar Deldjoo, Sunhao Dai, Honghui Bao, Xiaopeng Ye, Fatemeh Nazary, Wenjie Wang, Tommaso Di Noia, Jun Xu, Tat-Seng Chua

entry.summary: A survey and roadmap for agentic recommender systems, introducing a unified taxonomy based on autonomy level and three paradigms: agent-assisted recommendation, agent-as-recommender, and agent-as-user-simulator. It organizes the shift from static ranking pipelines toward autonomous, interactive systems.

entry.title: Coordinate Singularities Break Conformal Coverage for Gaze and Head Pose

author.name: Mohammadreza Jamalifard, Yaxiong Lei, Parastoo Azizinezhad, Javier Andreu-Perez

entry.summary: Shows that conformal prediction's coverage guarantees for gaze and head-pose tasks break near coordinate singularities (large pitch, gimbal lock) when scores are defined in flat charts like yaw-pitch or Euler angles. Across four datasets, slice-conditional coverage degrades, motivating geometry-aware scoring on curved output spaces.

entry.title: EmoteGPT: 3D Human Facial Expressions from Natural Language Descriptions

author.name: Haoran Wang, Mohit Mendiratta, Christian Theobalt, Adam Kortylewski

entry.summary: EmoteGPT formulates text-driven 3D facial expression synthesis as regression in the disentangled parameter space of a 3D Morphable Model, enabling fine-grained expression control separate from identity and texture. It introduces Txt2Emote, a benchmark pairing detailed language with precise expression parameters obtained from GPT-4o and a high-fidelity face tracker.

entry.title: VLRC: Vision-Language Reprojection Consistency as a scalable signal for better feed-forward 3D pretraining

author.name: Marwane Hariat, David Filliat, Antoine Manzanera

entry.summary: VLRC introduces Vision-Language Reprojection Consistency, an auxiliary objective that reprojects frozen vision-language features across views of a predicted 3D reconstruction and enforces feature consistency at corresponding locations, requiring no additional 3D annotations. It provides scalable semantic multi-view supervision for feed-forward 3D pretraining.

entry.title: When Does Resolution Help a Frozen Backbone? Global Attention at Resolution Predicts Scalable Adaptation for Camouflaged and Marine Animal Segmentation

author.name: Tyler Rust, Chandra Kambhamettu

entry.summary: Analyzes when higher resolution helps frozen vision backbones under low-rank adaptation for camouflaged and marine animal segmentation, finding that isotropic ViTs applying global attention over full-resolution tokens keep improving while hierarchical windowed backbones plateau. Backbone editing shows global attention, not pooling, is the causal factor.

entry.title: See the Emotion: A Facial Emoji Proxy Modeling for EEG Emotion Recognition

author.name: Jingjing Hu, Guo Dan, Haofan Cheng, Ying Zeng, Zhan Si, Jinxing Zhou, Meng Wang

entry.summary: Reframes EEG emotion recognition explainability as cross-modal generation, translating EEG signals into identity-anonymized facial emojis via a Facial Emoji Proxy Modeling framework grounded in neural-facial association. The goal is interpretable, human-readable visualization of otherwise opaque neural features.

entry.title: $C^3$ASD: Multi-Level Consistency-Driven Representation Learning

author.name: Jin Hong, Jisoo Park, Junseok Kwon

entry.summary: C3ASD is a multi-level consistency-driven framework for active speaker detection that adds embedding-level inter-modality consistency and other constraints to make audio-visual representations robust to noise, occlusion, and modality degradation. It aims to prevent fragile modality-specific shortcuts that fail under real-world corruptions.

entry.title: From General Actions to Domain-Specific Monitoring: Prior-Adaptive Transfer for Skeleton-Based Action Recognition

author.name: Hao Wang, Di Yang, Jiangtao Wang

entry.summary: PATS adapts general skeleton-based action recognition models to narrow domains like healthcare monitoring by selectively retaining task-relevant motion priors while filtering redundant ones during transfer. It addresses suboptimal direct transfer and the cost of retaining irrelevant pretrained patterns.

entry.title: From Geometric Labels to Semantic Understanding of Indoor Building Components Using Multimodal Large Language Models

author.name: Shuju Jing, Chao Yin

entry.summary: Building-MLLM is a point-cloud-centered multimodal LLM for indoor building components that moves beyond discrete labels to explain component functions and support recognition, complex captioning, and multi-engineering QA. It uses domain-specific mechanisms like a Point Information Enhancer and Geometry-Preserving Regularization.

entry.title: IPDiff: Diffusion-driven ORSI Salient Object Detection with Information Reconstruction and Multi-Prior Guidance

author.name: Gongyang Li, Zhen Bai, Runmin Cong, Dan Zeng, Weisi Lin, Xiao-Ping Zhang

entry.summary: IPDiff is a diffusion-driven salient object detection method for optical remote sensing images that replaces static inference with iterative dynamic optimization of saliency maps via a conditional diffusion process guided by reconstructed information and multiple priors. It aims to correct errors that fixed-parameter inference cannot.

entry.title: SharpSplat: Edge-Regularized 3D Gaussian Splatting for High Fidelity Urban Building Reconstruction from UAV images

author.name: Porus Vaid, Shivam Chopra, Vaibhav Kumar

entry.summary: SharpSplat is an edge-regularized 3D Gaussian Splatting framework for high-fidelity urban building reconstruction from UAV imagery, using SAM 3 to generate building masks from which architecturally significant edges are extracted and aligned with rendered image gradients to produce crisp facade geometry. It addresses 3DGS failure to capture sharp geometric transitions on facades.

entry.title: SGF-CDNet: A Consistency-Discrepancy Graph Network over Semantic-Geometric Fused Nodes for Face Forgery Detection

author.name: Jiayao Jiang, Bin Liu, Nenghai Yu

entry.summary: SGF-CDNet is a face forgery detection method using a consistency-discrepancy graph network over semantic-geometric fused nodes that combine face-parsing semantic regions with landmark geometry, then reason about consistency and discrepancy across facial components in parallel paths. It targets subtle disharmony among facial regions in deepfakes lacking obvious artifacts.

entry.title: NavEYE: Vision-Centered Multi-Sensor Fusion-Based Situational Awareness System for Intelligent Surface Vehicles

author.name: Ryan Wen Liua, Junxiong Lianga, Haoyu Wanga, Mengwei Baoa

entry.summary: NavEYE is a vision-centered multi-sensor fusion system for intelligent surface vehicles that combines AIS, radar, and RGB cameras for situational awareness. A gated data association method aligns low- and high-temporal-resolution sensor streams for maritime perception.

entry.title: ICME 2026 Grand Challenge on Cross-Scenario Defect Detection and Fine-Grained Severity Grading for High-Precision Manufacturing

author.name: Wei Sun, Weixia Zhang, Linhan Cao, Mingkai Lu, Xiongkuo Min, Xiaoping Zhang, Patrick Le Callet, Guangtao Zhai, Hongxing Chen, Wenqi Wu, Zhenhao Hu, Shanshan Lin, Guanjie Huang, Kai Xie, Rui Xin, Zilong Zhao, Runmin Cong, Ningjing Li, Siqi Ma, Yi Jin Ong, Tianfei Zhou, Shunzhou Wang, Zhiyang Chen, Hao Fang, Chen Zhang, Tze-Hsiang Tang, Dikai Li, Xianjin Wu, Avinash Kumar Sharma, Zhaoyang Wang, Haiyong Chen, Binyi Su, Atik Shahariar

entry.summary: This paper describes the ICME 2026 Grand Challenge on cross-scenario defect detection and fine-grained severity grading for high-precision manufacturing, with tracks for cross-scenario detection and severity-aware assessment. It motivates the need to handle unseen production scenarios and risk-relevant severity.

entry.title: Semantic-Guided Progressive Object Removal with Gaussian Splatting

author.name: Xianliang Huang, Chen Xiao, Yuanxiang Ni, Guanming Liu, Mingkai Liu, Dikai Fan, Xiao Liu, Hao Zhang

entry.summary: Presents a 3D object-removal framework for Gaussian Splatting scenes using semantic-guided block matching with DINOv2 features and region-wise progressive refinement, exploiting multi-view semantic information rather than single-step inpainting. It targets high-quality removal of complex geometry and textures for AR/VR and robotics.

entry.title: ECO: Incremental Ego-Centric Octree Update for Point Streams

author.name: Jaemin Yu, Seongyoon Jeong, Kang-Wook Chon, Duksu Kim

entry.summary: ECO is an ego-centric octree acting as a 3D sliding window that bounds mapping to the robot's immediate surroundings using an incremental update that categorizes shift-out, shift-in, and overlap regions to avoid redundant coordinate transforms. On KITTI it reduces octree update times by roughly 25% versus static reconstruction.

entry.title: On the Complexity of Entrywise Power Matrix Factorization

author.name: Nicolas Gillis, Subhayan Saha, Stefano Sicilia, Arnaud Vandaele

entry.summary: Analyzes the computational complexity of entrywise power matrix factorization, which seeks a low-rank matrix whose component-wise absolute power reproduces a given nonnegative matrix, establishing a complete complexity landscape for exact decision and Frobenius approximation problems and linking it to sign-flipping combinatorics. It generalizes modulus and component-wise square factorization.

entry.title: Automated Data Readiness for Scientific AI

author.name: Sean R. Wilkinson, Valentine G. Anantharaj, Jong Youl Choi, Ketan Maheshwari, Marshall McDonnell, Massimiliano Lupo Pasini, Polina Shpilker, Renan Souza, Patrick Widener, Sarp Oral, Wesley Brewer

entry.summary: REDI is an open-source five-stage pipeline that automates transformation, readiness assessment, provenance tracking, and agent-native deployment of large scientific datasets for AI training, with a companion tool automating FAIR compliance. It was evaluated across climate, proteomics, materials, and fusion datasets at leadership computing facilities.

entry.title: Personalized Causal Recourse: A Human-In-The-Loop Approach

author.name: Denise Tampieri, Giovanni De Toni, Paolo Giudici

entry.summary: This paper proposes a human-in-the-loop algorithmic recourse framework that iteratively approximates a user's structural causal model through interactive Bayesian queries before generating recommendations. It aims to personalize recourse rather than assume a fixed causal structure or use nearest counterfactuals.

entry.title: CoACT: Action-Preserving Observation Compression for Coding Agents

author.name: Haorui Chen, Yuancheng Zhu, Yitong Zhang, Jia Li

entry.summary: CoACT compresses the observations returned to LLM coding agents so that context stays short while the agent still chooses the same next action, using a next-action-preservation objective. This targets inference cost, a real bottleneck in iterative software-engineering agents.

entry.title: Punching Above Their Weight: Classification-Head Fine-Tuning of Tiny Language Models (TLMs) for Verifiable Multiple-Choice Tasks

author.name: Bhavesh Sood, Jaromir Savelka

entry.summary: This study shows that discriminative classification-head LoRA fine-tuning of tiny (<3B) language models on consumer devices reliably outperforms label-generation fine-tuning and makes 0.6B-1.7B Qwen3 models competitive with much larger models like GPT-3, PaLM, and GPT-4 on verifiable multiple-choice tasks. It highlights how small models can punch above their weight with the right adaptation.

entry.title: Observable- and Positional-Encoding-Dependent Symmetry Readout from Neural Network Weights

author.name: Naoya Chiba, Satoshi Sugiyama, Yuki Uranishi

entry.summary: This paper shows that the symmetry recoverable from trained neural-field weights is not the true symmetry group but an observable set determined by parameters, positional encoding, and readout observable, formalized through an observability hierarchy. It clarifies limits on post-hoc symmetry inference from weights.

entry.title: Transformers with Physics-Informed Encodings and Simulation-Based Inference for Robust Detection of Eccentric Binary Black Holes in Pulsar Timing Array Data

author.name: Subhajit Dandapat, Alvin J. K. Chua

entry.summary: This paper introduces a Transformer with physics-informed positional encodings and simulation-based inference to efficiently detect eccentric binary black holes in pulsar timing array data, embedding analytical gravitational-wave phase evolution directly into the model. It aims to replace costly Bayesian likelihood evaluations for high-dimensional GW inference.

entry.title: Knowing When to Stop: Predicting Execution-Consistency Convergence in Text-to-SQL

author.name: Yaron Anavi, Mor Aisenberg, Nadav Nesher, Elena Khabibullina, Isabella Cattinelli

entry.summary: This paper formulates deciding when to stop repeated LLM calls for Text-to-SQL confidence estimation as a convergence-prediction problem, training lightweight 1-D models on running-consistency trajectories and benchmarking against a Beta-Bernoulli stopping rule. On BIRD and production datasets it adapts stopping per question to save compute.

entry.title: Open-Weather Robust 3D Detection via Dual-Critic Diffusion Alignment

author.name: Shuyao Li, Chuanxing Geng, Heyang Sun, Qiang Zhou, Jingjing Gu

entry.summary: DCDA proposes a weather-agnostic dual-critic diffusion framework that recovers degraded LiDAR features toward a clean manifold for robust 3D detection under unseen adverse weather. It moves beyond closed-world assumptions in autonomous driving perception.

entry.title: CIPHER: Causal Intervention Pathways for Healthcare Equity and Robustness

author.name: Xinyu Jia, Weidong Guo, Wangyuan Zhao, Yi Guo, Zeju Li, Yuanyuan Wang

entry.summary: CIPHER formalizes medical image formation with a structural causal model revealing four distinct pathways by which sensitive attributes influence images, then uses causal generative augmentation to mitigate subgroup disparities. It targets fairness and robustness in diagnosis.

entry.title: XS-VLA: Coupling Coarse-grained Spatial Distillation with Latent Flow Matching for Lightweight Robotic Control

author.name: Lei Iok Tong, Qingchen Xie, Wei Huang, Ying Jie Yap, Yujie Zhang, Qianzhi Li, Xiaolong Liu, Zhidong Deng

entry.summary: XS-VLA distills spatial knowledge from a larger vision-language model into a tiny 0.25B backbone and adds latent flow matching to create an efficient vision-language-action model for edge robotic control. It targets the 'spatial blindness' of lightweight models while keeping compute low enough for real-time deployment.

entry.title: Fields of the Planet: Field Boundary Mapping Beyond 10m

author.name: Isaac Corley, Caleb Robinson, Jennifer Marcus, Hannah Kerner

entry.summary: Fields of the Planet is a 3m PlanetScope companion dataset to Fields of The World, providing 133k co-registered patches across 24 countries for agricultural field boundary mapping. The authors show that higher-resolution imagery substantially improves recovery of small smallholder parcels invisible at 10m Sentinel-2 resolution.

entry.title: MTEB-PT: A Text Embedding Benchmark for Brazilian Portuguese

author.name: Tardelli Ronan Coelho Stekel

entry.summary: MTEB-PT is the first dedicated text-embedding benchmark for Brazilian Portuguese, comprising 22 native tasks across seven categories and evaluating 93 models with statistical rigor (bootstrap confidence intervals). It fills a gap where Portuguese evaluation relied on translations or thin multilingual coverage.

entry.title: Breaking the One-Dimensional Expressibility-Trainability Tradeoff

author.name: Kyoungho Cho, Yu-Seong Jeon, Jinhyoung Lee, Jeongho Bang

entry.summary: This paper argues the expressibility-trainability tradeoff in parameterized quantum circuits is not one-dimensional, distinguishing parameter-ensemble coverage, fixed-circuit entangling response, and local gradient moments as inequivalent quantities. It challenges the conflated narrative linking expressibility to barren plateaus.

entry.title: Integrating Neural Encoders in Bayesian Generalized Linear Mixed Models for Multimodal Data

author.name: Yuankang Zhao, Youngsoo Baek, Felipe A. Medeiros, Samuel Berchuck, Matthew M. Engelhard

entry.summary: This work integrates modality-specific neural encoders into Bayesian generalized linear mixed models, enabling uncertainty-aware analysis of correlated longitudinal data that includes images and text. It combines supervised representation learning with variance-corrected stochastic-gradient MCMC for posterior inference.

entry.title: SleepBand: Single-Source Domain Generalization for Sleep Staging via Physiologically Structured Spectral Modeling

author.name: Zhi Lu, Yang Hu, Yan Chen

entry.summary: SleepBand tackles single-source domain generalization for sleep staging by embedding oscillatory physiological priors via a learnable Morlet filter bank and a structured integration-recalibration pipeline. This anchors representations to domain-invariant sleep rhythms, reducing reliance on dataset-specific artifacts across five public datasets.

entry.title: RIGS-Refiner: Risk-Guided Recursive Refinement in Prediction Space for Colonoscopy Polyp Segmentation

author.name: Jiachi Zhang, Zhuoyu Wu, Wenqi Fang

entry.summary: RIGS-Refiner is a lightweight risk-guided recursive refinement plugin that repairs colonoscopy polyp segmentation errors in prediction space, focusing selective local correction on ambiguous boundaries rather than reprocessing whole masks. It avoids extra correction heads or heavy multi-stage pipelines.

entry.title: DistillH-Mamba: A Hypergraph-Mamba-Based Knowledge Distillation Model for Efficient Impact Fall Detection

author.name: Tresor Y. Koffi, Youssef Mourchid, Mohammed Hindawi, Yohan Dupuis

entry.summary: DistillH-Mamba combines hypergraph modeling of higher-order joint relationships with Mamba and knowledge distillation for efficient impact-moment fall detection in the elderly. It aims to accurately identify when a person hits the ground while remaining lightweight for real-time deployment.

entry.title: Embodied Operators and Benchmarking: Toward Reusable and Deployable Embodied Intelligence Systems

author.name: Junwu Xiong, Jiaxuan Gao, Wei Chai, Renxing Chen, Yuzhen Li, Yu Guo, Yucheng Guo, Mingxi Luo, Wenyang Ma, Yiyun Mou, Yifei Zhang, Chen Zhou, Yongjian Guo

entry.summary: This work defines embodied operators as reusable, composable functional modules with standardized input-output contracts (detection, spatial localization, hand motion, etc.) and builds a taxonomy plus benchmarking toward deployable embodied intelligence pipelines. It emphasizes modularity beyond end-to-end policies.

entry.title: Biological Motifs for Agentic Control

author.name: Bogdan Banu

entry.summary: This paper argues that many agentic failure modes (hallucination cascades, infinite loops, prompt injection) can be analyzed using control motifs from systems biology, developing a typed-interface correspondence between gene regulatory networks and agentic software via polynomial functors and wiring diagrams. It maps five biological motifs to agent coordination structures.

entry.title: Agent Step Value: State-Transition Measurement with State-Grounded LLM Evaluators

author.name: Andrew Zhang, Chengzhan Li

entry.summary: Agent Step Value scores each observed agent action by the change it induces in a state-grounded evaluator's distribution over fixed candidate outcomes, providing per-step diagnostics rather than collapsing traces into a single success flag. It reports both gold-free belief diagnostics and offline oracle validation.

entry.title: MRMS: A Multi-Resolution Memory Substrate for Long-Lived AI Agents

author.name: Jizhizi Li, Amy Shi-Nash

entry.summary: MRMS is an architectural memory substrate for long-lived agents organized along a representational axis (structured records, vectors, graphs) and a temporal axis (short-term traces, medium-term abstractions, long-term commitments), with synchronized structured-vector-graph memory where structured records govern eligibility. It supports selective retrieval, separating personal context from external evidence, and memory revision.

entry.title: Neural-Network Inverse Design of SRF Cavities and Transmons for Bosonic Quantum Computation

author.name: Joseph Yaker, Jovan Markovic, Alessandro Reineri, Doga Murat Kurkcuoglu, Silvia Zorzetti

entry.summary: Presents two deep neural network approaches for inverse design of superconducting RF cavities and transmons for bosonic quantum computing, mapping desired electromagnetic and coupling targets back to device geometries. It addresses the prohibitive cost of iterative simulation for this one-to-many design problem.

entry.title: AutoResearch: An Execution-Grounded Multi-Agent Framework for Reliable Research Workflow Automation

author.name: Rajesh Kumar, Waqar Ali, Junaid Ahmed, Abdullah Aman Khan, Shaoning Zeng

entry.summary: AutoResearch is an execution-grounded multi-agent framework that couples sandboxed code execution, iterative repair, citation verification, and claim-support auditing to make automated research workflows reliable. It treats runtime errors and unsupported citations as filtering signals for generated artifacts.

entry.title: Not Every Sync Is Safe: Calibrated DiLoCo Scheduling for Shared AI Infrastructure

author.name: Maxwell Twelftree, David Lemphers, An-chi He, Yue Yang

entry.summary: This paper studies when DiLoCo-style local-then-synchronize training should defer outer merges on shared industrial AI infrastructure, introducing matched random deferral as a control to isolate timing from synchronization budget. It shows that which windows to defer genuinely matters via stress tests and vLLM sidecar replays.

entry.title: Fine-Grained Computation Offload for Off-the-Shelf Servers in Tens of Lines

author.name: Bojie Li

entry.summary: Observes that serving concurrent requests already provides the suspend/resume machinery needed to overlap fine-grained hardware offloads (microseconds to milliseconds), avoiding costly context switches or busy-waits. It enables lightweight computation offload for off-the-shelf servers in tens of lines of code.

entry.title: Metronome: Bound the Cache, Keep the Beat for Real-Time Interaction Model Serving

author.name: Jiaying Meng, Bojie Li

entry.summary: Metronome addresses a metastable collapse mode in real-time full-duplex interaction serving (Moshi, MiniCPM-o, Qwen-Omni) where monotonically growing per-session KV caches suddenly exhaust the pool and stall the engine despite healthy-looking metrics. It bounds the cache to keep frame deadlines under sustained load.

entry.title: Diagnosing Aerial-View Object Detectors with Foundational Image Generative Models

author.name: Stanislav Panev, Minhyek Jeon, Vaishnavi Khindkar, Ahish Deshpande, Celso M de Melo, Shuowen Hu, Shayok Chakraborty, Fernando De la Torre

entry.summary: Introduces a synthetic diagnostic framework using foundation image generative models with text-guided generation, attribute-controlled editing, and automated verification to stress-test aerial vehicle detectors under controllable scene conditions. It repositions generative models as diagnostic tools rather than just augmentation.

entry.title: A Preliminary Study on Explaining Risk of Code Changes using LLM-Based Prediction Models

author.name: Yalin Liu, Kosay Jabre, Rui Abreu, Zachariah J. Carmichael, Vijayaraghavan Murali, Akshay Patel, Jun Ge, Weiyan Sun, Cong Zhang, Audris Mockus, David Khavari, Peter C. Rigby, Nachiappan Nagappan

entry.summary: This preliminary study uses attention weights from an LLM-based Diff Risk Score model to highlight the parts of a code change most associated with defect risk, aggregating token-level attention into lines, hunks, and files for lightweight code-review guidance. Authors include Meta engineers working on just-in-time defect prediction.

entry.title: Differential Amplifier-Inspired AmpAttention for Multi-View Robotic Manipulation

author.name: Jin Yang, Ping Wei, Nanning Zheng

entry.summary: AmpAttention is an attention mechanism inspired by analog differential amplifiers that suppresses attention noise and captures high signal-to-noise signals to combat attention drift from redundancy, occlusion, and viewpoint dependency in multi-view robotic manipulation. The resulting RVAF model improves average success rates over prior state of the art.

entry.title: Where do LLMs Fall Short in CBT-Guided Affective Reasoning?

author.name: Vaishnavi Sinha, Pooja Guttal, Pranay Deep Reddy Katike, Vishal Sinha, Gerald Ndawula, Lira Yoon, Andrea Kleinsmith, Manas Gaur

entry.summary: Investigates why LLMs that score highly on CBT licensing-exam questions fail to apply CBT effectively, collapsing into validation and reflection regardless of user needs. It proposes a knowledge-guided framework decomposing narratives into Beck's cognitive conceptualization grounded in SNOMED CT concepts and multiple chain-of-thought affective reasoning.

entry.title: SPLIT: Training-Free AI-Generated and Partially Edited Video Detection via Spatial Patch-Level Incoherence and Temporal Roughness

author.name: Jongyeop Hyun, Hyounghun Kim

entry.summary: SPLIT is a training-free detector for AI-generated and partially edited videos that operates on frozen vision-encoder patch tokens, combining two-step temporal roughness and local spatial motion incoherence signals. It targets the ultra-low false-positive regime needed to avoid rejecting authentic content in real deployments.

entry.title: Learning Taxonomic Trees with Hierarchical Representation Regularization for Large Multimodal Models

author.name: Hulingxiao He, Zhi Tan, Yuxin Peng

entry.summary: Proposes Hierarchical Representation Regularization, a plug-and-play regularizer that injects taxonomic knowledge into large multimodal models to improve hierarchical visual recognition consistency, using a semantic-aware visual tree built from intermediate LLM features. It addresses LMMs' lack of taxonomy-aware training objectives.

entry.title: CONFLUX: A Latent Diusion Model for 3D Chest-CT Synthesis with RL Post-Training

author.name: Max Van Puyvelde, Halil Ibrahim Gulluk, Wim Van Criekinge, Olivier Gevaert

entry.summary: CONFLUX is a latent diffusion model for native 3D chest-CT synthesis using a 3D VAE and rectified-flow transformer conditioned on structured radiological metadata, with RL post-training, outperforming volumetric baselines on tri-planar FID. It enables controllable generation of clinically attributed CT volumes.

entry.title: AnchorVLA: Bridging Discrete Decisions and Continuous Trajectories for Vision-Language-Action Planning

author.name: Qi Liu, Yabei Li, Hongsong Wang, Heng Zhang, Lei He

entry.summary: AnchorVLA bridges discrete decisions and continuous trajectories for vision-language-action autonomous-driving planning, addressing weaknesses of both planning-head prediction (weak reasoning constraint) and full-trajectory token generation (discretization errors and low information density). It aims for better semantic-action alignment.

entry.title: Differentiate the Evaluator, Not the Program: An Efficient Runtime Representation for Neuro-Symbolic Learning

author.name: Lucas Sheneman

entry.summary: The Native Differentiable Virtual Machine (NDVM) is a runtime representation for neuro-symbolic learning that differentiates the evaluator rather than staging each candidate program into its own graph, keeping programs as data while enabling gradient-based parameter calibration. It targets the parameter-fitting bottleneck in program-and-parameter co-search.

entry.title: Token-Based Affordance Grounding with Large Vision-Language Models

author.name: Seung Il Lee, Qinqian Lei, Daguang Xu, Dong Yang, Robby T. Tan, Yixin Chen, Bo Wang

entry.summary: This work grounds action affordances in images using large vision-language models by extracting spatial cues from action-conditioned textual outputs via a token-based mechanism, addressing ambiguity in weakly supervised exocentric methods. It advances affordance localization for embodied perception.

entry.title: ELiTeFormer: An Efficient Transformer for FPGAs

author.name: Victor Agostinelli, Nicolas Bohm Agostini, Antonino Tumeo

entry.summary: ELiTeFormer is an FPGA-co-designed transformer architecture unifying hybrid linear attention with ternary linear projections, achieving 10x weight compression and 12.8x KV-cache compression. It jointly optimizes attention and feed-forward components for efficient hardware deployment.

entry.title: AutoCedar: An Agentic Framework for Verifier-Guided Access Control Policy Synthesis

author.name: Adarsh Vatsa, Sachi Shome, Yingming Zhou, William Eiers

entry.summary: AutoCedar is a verifier-guided agentic system that first turns natural-language access-control requirements into reviewable intent atoms and then synthesizes Cedar policies verified against that intent. It targets the danger of LLM-generated policies that compile correctly but grant unauthorized access.

entry.title: Self-Improving Diffusion Classifiers with Minority Preference Optimization

author.name: Hyunsoo Kim, Jungmyung Wi, Soobin Um, Donghyun Kim, Suhyun Kim

entry.summary: This paper reveals a direct link between minority sampling in diffusion generation and the perception capability of diffusion classifiers, then proposes minority preference optimization to self-improve classification accuracy in low-density data regions. It broadens coverage of underrepresented data manifold regions.

entry.title: Rethinking Depth Pruning for Vision Transformers: A Heterogeneity-Aware Perspective

author.name: Zhenfeng Su, Kang Zhao, Han Bao, Tao Yuan, Zhongzhe Hu, Xianzhi Yu, Wenxuan Wang

entry.summary: HetDPT is a heterogeneity-aware depth pruning method for vision transformers that addresses dimension mismatch across layers, enabling higher speedups than typical width-pruning-dominated approaches. It attributes the failure of prior depth pruning to neglecting inter-layer heterogeneity.

entry.title: Beyond Static Rules: Automated Discovery of Latent Vulnerabilities in Text-to-SQL

author.name: Hanqing Wang, Yongdong Chi, Jian Yang, Lei Yang, Jiehui Zhao, Yun Chen, Guanhua Chen

entry.summary: SAGE is a framework that autonomously discovers latent failure patterns in LLM-based Text-to-SQL by generating vulnerability hypotheses and referencing a continuously evolving Vulnerability Codex, moving beyond static expert-defined diagnostic rules. It enables systematic automated exploration of reliability weaknesses.

entry.title: High-Fidelity One-Step Generative Visuomotor Policy via Recursive Correction, Frequency Consistency, and Contrastive Flow Matching

author.name: Yuran Chen, Xinye Cai, Zhonglin Gong, Yang Huang

entry.summary: This paper proposes a high-fidelity one-step generative visuomotor policy that uses recursive consistent action flow, dual-timestep frequency consistency, and contrastive flow matching to avoid spatial deviation, frequency distortion, and mode averaging from naive one-step compression. It targets fast inference for diffusion/flow-based robot policies.

entry.title: Enhancing Implicit Neural Representations with Image Feature Embedding for Unsupervised Cardiac Cine MRI Reconstruction

author.name: Donghang Lyu, Marius Staring, Yiming Dong, Keupp Jochen, Hildo J. Lamb, Mariya Doneva

entry.summary: The paper augments implicit neural representations with image feature embeddings for unsupervised reconstruction of accelerated cardiac cine MRI, removing the need for fully sampled reference data. It aims to improve reconstruction where supervised references are unavailable.

entry.title: FedSPM: Routing-Enabled Federated Learning under Dual Heterogeneity via Semiparametric Mixture

author.name: Zijian Wang, Pengfei Li, Guangyu Yang, Qiong Zhang

entry.summary: FedSPM extends routing-prediction federated learning to handle dual heterogeneity by modeling latent subpopulations within each client via a semiparametric mixture, improving both routing and prediction. It addresses intra-client variability that prior methods ignore.

entry.title: ResearchStudio-Reel: Automate the Last Mile of Research from Paper to Poster, Video, and Blog

author.name: Lingao Xiao, Yalun Dai, Yangyu Huang, Qihao Zhao, Wenshan Wu, Hugo He, Ruishuo Chen, Jin Jiang, Qianli Ma, Jiahuan Zhang, Xin Zhang, Ying Xin, Yang Ou, Yan Xia, Scarlett Li, Longbo Huang, Zhipeng Zhang, Yang He, Yap Kim Hui, Yan Lu

entry.summary: ResearchStudio-Reel automates the last mile of research dissemination, composing skills that share one upstream paper extractor and wrap deterministic primitives to generate editable posters, talk videos, and blogs gated by hard pass/fail render checks. It aims to replace one-way renders and soft VLM-preference quality gates.

entry.title: From Regulation to Requirements: An Automated Requirement Derivation and Explanation Pipeline

author.name: Pavithra PM Nair, Preethu Rose Anish

entry.summary: This paper presents an automated pipeline that identifies requirement-bearing clauses in regulations like GDPR and the EU AI Act and derives system-agnostic software requirements with plain-language explanations traceable to legal sources. It is evaluated on the full clause sets of both regulations.

entry.title: A Deep Learning-based surrogate model for Severe Accidents in nuclear reactors using ASTEC

author.name: Alessandro Longhi, Danny Lathouwers, Zolt\'an Perk\'o

entry.summary: This work builds a deep-learning surrogate for nuclear-reactor severe-accident simulation using ASTEC data, combining an autoencoder for dimensionality reduction with a Neural ODE time-stepper to drastically speed up simulations for real-time operator training. It replaces multi-day simulations with fast surrogate inference.

entry.title: Two Black Boxes, One Solver: Encoder Probing and Decoder Attribution for Neural Multi-Attribute VRP under Hard-Mask and Recourse Decoders

author.name: Sohaib Afifi

entry.summary: This paper opens two black boxes of neural autoregressive multi-attribute vehicle-routing solvers, using encoder probing (linear probes, richness measures, discovered-direction analyses) and decoder attribution (gradient, integrated gradients, DeepLIFT) across abductive, contrastive, and counterfactual reading angles. It aims to provide per-step justifications dispatchers can validate.

entry.title: Explainable Novel Category Discovery in Semantic Concept Space

author.name: Ifrat Ikhtear Uddin, Yang Zhou, KC Santosh, Longwei Wang

entry.summary: xNCD is an explainable novel-category-discovery framework that performs both discovery and pseudo-labeling directly in a structured semantic concept space, learning label-free concept representations by aligning visual features with vision-language similarity priors. It provides semantic evidence for each discovered group rather than clustering opaque features.

entry.title: Simple-to-Complex Structured Demonstrations for Vision-Language-Action Learning

author.name: Xinchuan Qiu, Yi Yu

entry.summary: This paper identifies demonstration organization as an overlooked factor in imitation learning for vision-language-action models and proposes a simple-to-complex structured demonstration collection strategy on a dual-arm platform. It argues demonstration ordering affects learning efficiency, stability, and generalization independent of architecture or data scale.

entry.title: LCPNet: Latent Consistent Proximal Unfolding Network for Infrared Small Target Detection

author.name: Tianfang Zhang, Fengyi Wu, Lei Li, Chang Liu, Zhenming Peng, Huaping Zhang, Xiangyang Ji

entry.summary: LCPNet is a latent-consistent proximal unfolding network for infrared small target detection that embeds model-driven optimization iterations into a network operating in a latent domain, with memory mechanisms coupled to the underlying optimization process. It better exploits the physical target-background decomposition structure than feed-forward designs.

entry.title: Comparison of Loss Functions for Robust Deep Learning-based Echocardiography Segmentation when Learning with Partially Labelled Data from Multiple Domains

author.name: Iman Islam, Esther Puyol-Ant\'on, Bram Ruijsink, Andrew J. Reader, Andrew P. King

entry.summary: This study compares three loss functions (adaptive categorical cross entropy, marginal loss, adaptive binary cross entropy) for training echocardiography segmentation models on partially-labelled data from multiple domains. It provides a comprehensive comparison across scenarios and architectures for robust multi-structure cardiac segmentation.

entry.title: Less Tokens, Better Forecasts: Sparse Residual Routing for Efficient Weather Prediction

author.name: Janet Wang, Yunbei Zhang, Lin Zhao, Xi Xiao, Jihun Hamm, Xiao Wang

entry.summary: Sparse-Reslim is a parameter-free routing plug-in for ViT-based weather forecasting that reduces redundant per-token computation while keeping every grid cell physically meaningful for autoregressive rollout. It exploits the spatial smoothness of atmospheric fields to save compute without pruning tokens.

entry.title: Transfer Learning in High-dimensional Ising Models

author.name: Joonho Kim, Seyoung Park

entry.summary: Trans-Ising is a transfer-learning method for high-dimensional Ising model estimation that screens informative auxiliary binary datasets to avoid negative transfer, then uses a two-stage pooled-then-corrected estimation with a folded-concave penalty. It provides theoretical error bounds and graph-selection consistency.

entry.title: CSympNet-ID: conformal-symplectic map learning for linearly damped Hamiltonian systems

author.name: Jiale Gong (School of Mathematics), Pengzhan Jin (National Engineering Laboratory for Big Data Analysis and Applications, Peking University, Beijing, China), Dongyang Kuang (School of Mathematics), Lu Li (School of Mathematics), Yifa Tang (State Key Laboratory of Mathematical Sciences, Academy of Mathematics and Systems Science, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China)

entry.summary: CSympNet-ID learns discrete-time flow maps for linearly damped Hamiltonian systems while enforcing exact conformal symplecticity by construction, composing a symplectic core with explicit diagonal scaling to identify net dissipation. It enables reliable long-horizon prediction and physical parameter identification.

entry.title: Co-Adaptive Multi-Task LoRA: Transfer-Aware, Label-Free Control of Domain Participation

author.name: Wei Zhang, Lin Tang, Ming Zhao, Yuxuan Wang

entry.summary: Co-Adaptive Multi-Task LoRA reads off, label-free and cheaply, how much each domain should participate and which domains transfer positively versus interfere, using competence signals from forward passes over an unlabeled probe. It controls domain participation in multi-task LoRA fine-tuning without labels.

entry.title: AdaptiveSD A Stability-Aware, Runtime-Adaptive Speculative Decoding Framework with Multi-Policy Orchestration for CPU-Constrained LLM Inference

author.name: Sadra Saremi

entry.summary: AdaptiveSD is a runtime-adaptive, stability-aware speculative-decoding framework with multi-policy orchestration for CPU-constrained LLM inference, avoiding the bandwidth saturation and catastrophic resource exhaustion that plague fixed-depth speculation. It targets reliable on-device serving of small quantized GGUF models.

entry.title: Masked Generative-Contrastive Representation Learning for Cross-Dataset EEG-Based Emotion Recognition

author.name: Huqin Weng, Jiayang Huang, Yimin Wen, Jie Du, Chi-Man Vong, Chuangquan Chen

entry.summary: MGCRL is a self-supervised framework combining masked generative and contrastive objectives on a region-aware spatiotemporal encoder for cross-dataset EEG emotion recognition. It aims to handle varying channel configurations, noise robustness, and cross-subject generalization. It matters for transferable EEG representation learning.

entry.title: Channel-Adaptive Robust Aggregation for Over-the-Air Federated Learning in Heterogeneous Networks

author.name: Zubaida Fatima, Zubair Shaban, Yusuf Jamal, Nazreen Shah, Ranjitha Prasad, B. N. Bharath
