entry.summary: CHARGE-FL is an over-the-air federated learning framework that adaptively schedules aggregation based on wireless channel dynamics and application readiness, using a dual-purpose precoding mechanism to combat noise, fading, and client heterogeneity. It targets 6G-era privacy-preserving distributed learning. It matters for wireless FL deployment.

entry.title: Reliability and Identifiability in Persona-Trained Monte Carlo: Variance Decomposition, Stability Bounds, and the Identifiability of Heterogeneous News Reaction

author.name: Salavat Ishbulatov

entry.summary: This paper develops statistical theory for Persona-Trained Monte Carlo estimators that simulate limit-order-book markets with neural policy bots drawn from a learned heterogeneity distribution, decomposing variance into persona-draw and within-run components with optimal compute allocation and stability bounds. It matters for reliability guarantees in agent-based market simulation.

entry.title: Framework for Grouping Local Process Models

author.name: Viki Peeva, Wil M. P. van der Aalst

entry.summary: This paper proposes a framework for grouping the hundreds-to-thousands of Local Process Models produced by discovery algorithms, addressing model explosion and repetition to make pattern mining outputs analyst-usable. It matters for practical process-mining adoption, with a prominent process-mining author.

entry.title: Uncertainty-aware damage identification in short-span bridges via physics-informed variational autoencoder

author.name: Ana Fernandez Navamuel, A. Javier Omella, Diego Zamora-Sanchez, David Pardo

entry.summary: This work proposes a physics-informed Gaussian copula variational autoencoder for uncertainty-aware vibration-based damage identification in short-span bridges, embedding a differentiable eigenvalue solver into the VAE to enforce physical consistency and avoid data-driven surrogates. It matters for reliable structural health monitoring under noise and sparse sensing.

entry.title: FlatManifold: Robust Continual Learning under Severe Label Noise and Domain Shifts via Intrinsic Manifold Flattening

author.name: Rai Hisada, Kanji Tanaka

entry.summary: FlatManifold is a continual-learning framework that uses a Nystrom-based manifold flattening map and projection onto an orthogonalized RKHS to remain robust under severe uncalibrated label noise and non-linear domain shifts, avoiding fragile sample-filtering pipelines. It matters for robust learning in non-stationary streaming environments.

entry.title: Video-based detection of cessation of breathing in pre-term infants using machine learning

author.name: Dineo Serame, Lionel Tarassenko, Mauricio Villarroel

entry.summary: This study investigates whether non-contact camera-based signals can detect apnoea-related cessation of breathing in pre-term infants and complement routine physiological monitoring, using video and clinical recordings from 30 infants. It matters for safer, sensor-free respiratory monitoring in neonatal intensive care, with an experienced clinical-ML group.

entry.title: Target-Guided Selective Reweighting for Physics-Informed Neural Network Inverse Problems: A Transfer Learning Approach

author.name: Qian Hu, Bin Fan, Yao Xiao, Zhicheng Lin, Meixin Xiong

entry.summary: TGSR-PINN is a transfer-learning method for physics-informed neural network inverse problems that transfers only source weights and biases and applies target-evidence-driven selective reweighting to avoid negative transfer, where a model achieves low field error yet recovers wrong physical parameters. It matters for reliable PDE parameter recovery across domains.

entry.title: Selectivity Estimation for Linear Queries via Online Learning

author.name: Fangzhu Shen, Debmalya Panigrahi, Sudeepa Roy

entry.summary: This work develops an online-learning framework for selectivity estimation of linear queries in databases where both data and query workload change over time, with regret upper and lower bounds. It moves beyond fixed-distribution static-database assumptions.

entry.title: SAF3R: Dynamic Sparse Attention for Feed-Forward 3D Reconstruction Transformers

author.name: Jianing Deng, Yuanzhe Li, Jialu Wang, Song Wang, Tianlong Chen, Huanrui Yang, Jingtong Hu

entry.summary: SAF3R analyzes global attention in feed-forward 3D reconstruction transformers, finding it highly heterogeneous, dynamic, and sparse, then proposes a training-free dynamic sparse attention to scale to long image sequences. It addresses the quadratic cross-view attention bottleneck.

entry.title: Optimal Mixture-of-Experts Model Averaging for Conditional Generative Models

author.name: Shijin Gong, Baihua He, Xinyu Zhang

entry.summary: This paper proposes an optimal model-averaging framework for combining multiple conditional generative models using sample-based maximum mean discrepancy, working even when only conditional samples (not densities) are available. It introduces both static and adaptive weighting schemes.

entry.title: GlaKG: A Biomarker-Centric Fundus Knowledge Graph for Explainable Glaucoma Diagnosis and Risk Assessment

author.name: Cheng Huang, Jia Zhang, Yi Jiang, Yang Liu, Karanjit Kooner, Yadi Liu, Tsengdar Lee, Yang Xie, Wenqi Shi, Guanghua Xiao

entry.summary: GlaKG is a biomarker-centric fundus knowledge graph that integrates structural biomarkers, clinically validated rules, and image features to produce traceable reasoning chains for glaucoma diagnosis and risk stratification. It aims to replace opaque deep-learning diagnosis with interpretable, rule-linked predictions.

entry.title: Identifiability of Relational Queries in Multi-View Pretraining

author.name: Ratan Bahadur Thapa, Daniel Hern\'andez

entry.summary: This paper formalizes query identifiability for data integration under interface laws (functional dependencies holding across all legal worlds), proving that some downstream queries are structurally undeterminable regardless of data volume or model size. It provides a polynomial-time certificate to decide identifiability.

entry.title: Routing Anonymity and Identifiability of Noisy Quantum Hardware

author.name: Ben Priestley, Mina Doosti

entry.summary: This paper develops the first formal framework for backend identifiability in cloud quantum computing, showing that noisy finite-shot outputs carry device-specific fingerprints that can reveal which hardware executed a circuit. It introduces a backend-identifiability game to formalize routing anonymity and its privacy implications for users and providers.

entry.title: Quantum Spectral Anomaly Detection

author.name: Yewei Yuan, Michele Minervini, Mark M. Wilde, Nana Liu

entry.summary: QSPADE computes PCA-like anomaly scores directly for quantum data, quantifying deviation of a test state from a normal quantum dataset without explicitly recovering eigenvectors, constructing Gram matrices, or loading QRAM-style data. It avoids costly overheads that would otherwise exceed the cost of the anomaly score itself.

entry.title: PraMem: Practice-derived Experiential Memory for Long-horizon Behavior Prediction

author.name: Zhuoqun Li, Boxi Cao, Jiawei Chen, Hanshu Zhou, Ruoxi Xu, Guiping Jiang, Ruotong Pan, Tingting Gao, Han Li, Xiangyu Wu, Hongyu Lin, Yaojie Lu, Xianpei Han, Le Sun

entry.summary: PraMem reframes lengthy user history from a burden into a resource for long-horizon behavior prediction, addressing LLMs' difficulty with latent behavioral pattern induction and intrinsic cognitive biases. It proposes practice-derived experiential memory rather than context-compression to better exploit historical sequences.

entry.title: Learning from Lost Provenance: Multiple Instance Learning for Cancer Registry Tumor Group Classification

author.name: Leonard Ruocco, Jonathan Simkin, Lovedeep Gondora, Gregory Arbour, Raymond Ng

entry.summary: This work trains cancer-registry tumor-group classifiers using multiple instance learning to leverage patient-level operational labels that are not linked to individual pathology reports, avoiding costly per-report human annotation. It demonstrates the framework for tumor group classification at BC Cancer.

entry.title: GRASP: Graph-Reasoning Aided Survey Planning for High-Fidelity Related Work Generation

author.name: Haoming Li, Jessica Ouyang

entry.summary: GRASP combines LLM planning with graph algorithms for related-work generation, using a two-layer structure (Graph of Thoughts and Argument-Counterargument Planning Network) and Steiner-tree topology-aware pruning to extract core inter-paper relationships. It targets high-fidelity literature reviews that capture how cited papers build on or challenge one another.

entry.title: TRACER: Early Failure Detection for Task-Oriented Dialogue

author.name: Erfan Nourbakhsh, Rocky Slavin, Ke Yang, Anthony Rios

entry.summary: TRACER performs early failure detection for task-oriented dialogue, predicting from a partial conversation whether the full dialogue will ultimately fail by combining belief-state trajectory signals with text representations of the evolving state. It detects useful failure signals well before the end even with only 25-50% of the dialogue visible.

entry.title: Mechanism-level routing failure in LLMs over Lean-verified algebraic structures

author.name: Manuel Israel C\'azares, Wenlin Zhang, Haobo Ma

entry.summary: An empirical study of LLMs selecting proof-mechanism labels for Lean 4-verified algebraic objects, finding a mechanism-level routing ceiling (about 80 percent for gpt-oss-120b) that improves when a verified witness cue is exposed. It probes structural reasoning failures anchored to formally verified artifacts.

entry.title: Angry but Accurate: Detecting and Profiling the Counter-Misinformation Ecosystem on Twitter

author.name: Eun Cheol Choi, Emilio Ferrara

entry.summary: Studies the counter-misinformation ecosystem on Twitter by classifying 264k COVID-19 tweets and comparing 23 features, finding that anti-misinformation posts are more emotionally negative (anger, disgust, sadness) than pro-misinformation posts. It challenges the assumption that negative emotion signals falsehood.

entry.title: Learning 3D Affordances for Blade Insertion in Cluttered Stowing

author.name: Tianyu Li, Harpreet Sawhney, Minju Jung, Aditya Mehrotra, Kunal Mehrotra, Mudit Agrawal

entry.summary: VulcanVoxel learns 3D free-space affordances for blade insertion in cluttered fabric stow bins using a masked autoencoder over 3D occupancy fields to reconstruct feasible blade volumes conditioned on scene geometry. It reframes tool-navigation affordance as spatial voxel-level feasibility rather than SE(3) pose distributions.

entry.title: Entropy-Coded MS-VQ-VAE with Learned Priors for Ultra-Low Bitrate Video Compression

author.name: Manikanta Kotthapalli, Banafsheh Rekabdar

entry.summary: Shows that discrete latent representations sidestep the sub-0.1-bpp limitations of continuous learned video codecs, using a VQ codebook's hard information ceiling plus a learned autoregressive prior exploiting power-law code usage to push bitrates below the ceiling without rate-penalty tuning. It targets ultra-low-bitrate video compression.

entry.title: Dual-Adaptive SAM3: Hierarchical Routing over Low-Rank Expert Layers for Parameter-Efficient Medical Image Segmentation

author.name: Ying Chen, Jinyue Li, Kun Wang, Qiankun Li, Yang Liu

entry.summary: DA-SAM3 adapts the Segment Anything Model with Concepts to medical imaging via a dual-adaptive mechanism using hierarchical routing over low-rank expert layers, achieving high accuracy with extreme parameter efficiency. It addresses that naive fine-tuning is inefficient and standard MoE is too costly for clinical use.

entry.title: Global Pose Control for Generative View Synthesis in Normalized Object Coordinate Space

author.name: Zhibing Li, Amogh Gupta, Behnoosh Parsa, Dan Casas

entry.summary: Proposes precise global camera control for generative novel view synthesis in a customizable Normalized Object Coordinate Space from single or few unposed images, addressing that existing methods use only input-relative poses or generate sparse global views. It enables intuitive, global target-viewpoint control for downstream tasks.

entry.title: Text-to-Image Generation for Projector-Camera System Registration

author.name: Xinyu Chen, Yuqi Li, Jiabao Li, Pinyan Tang, Chong Wang, Aditi Majumder

entry.summary: Uses text-to-image generation to produce projected patterns for projector-camera system registration, aiming to combine the accuracy of structured light with human-meaningful imagery while coping with surface textures and lighting variation. It targets high-resolution pixel correspondence in procam systems.

entry.title: Lightweight Polyp Segmentation via a Gain-Aware Prediction-Space Recursive Controller

author.name: Jiachi Zhang, Zhuoyu Wu, Quanjun Wang, Wenhui Ou, Wenqi Fang

entry.summary: This paper isolates whether a lightweight correction mechanism is inherently effective for polyp segmentation by formulating refinement as prediction-space recursive correction on backbone logits, using a controller that aggregates discrepancy and uncertainty under a fixed recursion budget. It applies additive residual logit corrections independent of the backbone.

entry.title: LBTCap: A Lightweight Bilateral Transformer for Real-Time Remote Sensing Image Change Captioning

author.name: Licheng Zhang, Siew-Kei Lam, Naveed Akhtar

entry.summary: LBTCap is a lightweight bilateral Transformer for real-time remote sensing image change captioning that jointly models pre- and post-change features with a bilateral attention mechanism using separate temporal queries. It emphasizes computational efficiency and inference speed for practical deployment.

entry.title: Towards Standardized Light Field Quality Assessment: Hybrid Subjective Benchmarking and Objective Metric Evaluation

author.name: Saeed Mahmoudpour, Mylene C. Q. Farias, Gi-Mun Um, Myllena A. Prado, Ismael Seidel, Leonardo de Sousa Marques, Leonardo Andrade, Shengyang Zhao, Carla L Pagliari

entry.summary: Presents a standardized light field quality assessment workflow developed for JPEG Pleno standardization, integrating benchmark generation, hybrid subjective evaluation, and objective metric analysis, including distortions from view synthesis and reconstruction beyond coding-only artifacts. It aims for reliable, reproducible immersive-media QA.

entry.title: iVISION-2DCD: A Long-Term Change Detection Dataset for Large-Scale Outdoor Construction Monitoring

author.name: Dayou Mao, Yuchen Lin, Ashkan Ebadi, John Zelek, Alexander Wong, Yuhao Chen

entry.summary: iVISION-2DCD is a long-term 2D change detection dataset for large-scale outdoor construction monitoring, collected via UAVs to support robust change detection from arbitrary viewpoints as buildings evolve. It fills the absence of an open benchmark at the intersection of change detection and construction automation.

entry.title: Exploring SAM Supervision for Fine-Grained UAV Target Segmentation under Data Scarcity

author.name: Le-Anh Tran

entry.summary: Explores using SAM3 as a pseudo-label generator to train compact UAV target segmentation networks under data scarcity, comparing direct pseudo-supervision with a patch-based mask refinement strategy. It targets small objects, appearance variation, and clutter in UAV imagery with limited annotations.

entry.title: TRISTAR: Triple-Signal Stair Recognition and Vision-Only Indoor Navigation for Search-and-Rescue Micro-UAVs

author.name: Octavian G\^ingu, Stelian Sp\^inu

entry.summary: TRISTAR is a complete vision-only autonomous indoor navigation framework for low-cost search-and-rescue micro-UAVs, combining monocular depth estimation with classical vision and lightweight deep learning for stair recognition, victim detection, and hazard recognition on a DJI Tello. It avoids LiDAR and stereo to reduce hardware cost and complexity.

entry.title: Hybrid Deep Learning for Traceability and Classification of Industrial Slate Tiles

author.name: Soren Antebi, Stefan Eickeler, Sandra Halscheidt, Rene Schmitz, Michael Muellers, Dirk Hecker, Rafet Sifa

entry.summary: This paper presents a hybrid deep learning approach for reidentification and extraction-site classification of industrial slate tiles, combining an XFeat feature-matching branch with a MobileNetV3 classifier. Adding LightGlue matching improves instance matching by over 15% AUC for traceability and quality control.

entry.title: U-Joint CAAMS: Experimental Evaluation of a Universal-Joint Continuum Manipulator for Aerial Manipulation

author.name: Anuraj Uthayasooriyan, Musab Alibrahim, Krishna Manaswi Digumarti, Fernando Vanegas, Felipe Gonzalez

entry.summary: Presents a universal-joint continuum manipulator for aerial manipulation that resists out-of-plane bending and twisting under propeller downwash, benchmarked against a Nitinol-backbone design. It advances compliant physical interaction for drones in confined tasks.

entry.title: ROBOCYCLE: Autonomous Dual-Arm Robotic Manipulation and Coordination for Recycling Applications

author.name: Rub\'en de J. Hilario-Cruz, Jesus A. Garc\'ia-Gonz\'alez, Enrique Coronado, Arturo E. Cer\'on-L\'opez

entry.summary: ROBOCYCLE is an autonomous dual-arm recycling platform integrating multi-view RGB-D perception, RF-DETR instance segmentation, and 6-DoF grasp planning to sort transparent, deformable, and cluttered waste to Tokyo recycling standards. It reports high grasp and sorting success on a real applied task.

entry.title: Integrated Graph Search and Model Predictive Control for Smooth and Efficient Path Planning in Autonomous Vehicles

author.name: Duc-Tien Bui, Ngoc Thinh Nguyen, Hung Duy Nguyen, Dong Bi, Tomislav Mihalj, Arno Eichberger

entry.summary: Combines Dijkstra graph search with MPC path refinement for autonomous vehicles, using the rough path to build a spatially varying convex lateral safety corridor that turns discrete obstacle-avoidance decisions into continuous optimization constraints. It aims for smooth, feasible, and computationally efficient paths.

entry.title: A Perception-Manipulation Robotics System for Food Cutting

author.name: Xinyuan Luo, Wenzhen Yuan

entry.summary: Presents a perception-manipulation framework for robotic food cutting with a knife-selection module using force from a trial cut and an RL-based adaptive cutting phase balancing speed and energy efficiency. It reports 100% knife selection on unseen food and compares fixed versus learned cutting policies.

entry.title: WinTA-GIL: Windowed Trajectory Alignment for GNSS-IMU-LiDAR Heading Refinement in Intermittent Signal Environments

author.name: Kaixin Feng, Zhichao Wen, Zhaohong Liao, Xin Xia, You Li

entry.summary: WinTA-GIL performs windowed trajectory alignment for GNSS-IMU-LiDAR heading refinement, dynamically correcting heading estimates during prolonged navigation and after GNSS outages rather than relying solely on an initial alignment. It addresses accumulated drift and weak heading observability in intermittent-signal environments.

entry.title: LOTUSim: Multi-Domain Simulator for Marine Robotics

author.name: C\'edric Buche (CROSSING, IMT Atlantique), Juliette Grosset (CROSSING), H\'el\`ene Lech\^ene (CROSSING), Marie Dubromel (CROSSING), Pierig Havez-Bodivit, Malcom Neo, Julien Prodhon

entry.summary: LOTUSim is an open-source real-time multi-domain maritime simulator supporting multi-user human-in-the-loop interaction across aerial, surface, and underwater robots with realistic environmental physics for coordinated naval-style operations. It targets scalability to large fleets in a shared interactive environment.

entry.title: Tightening Control in Neyman--Pearson Linear Classification

author.name: Yijian Huang

entry.summary: Studies Neyman-Pearson linear classification, where one class's accuracy is constrained above a target and the other class's accuracy is the objective, showing that no consistent classifier can guarantee finite-sample control and that even the control-relaxed empirical utility maximizer fails on average. It proposes tightening the control to address this.

entry.title: The Exact Worst-Case Tail Probability under Bounded Kurtosis

author.name: Xiaoyu Li, Andi Han, Jiaojiao Jiang, Junbin Gao

entry.summary: Determines exactly the worst-case one-sided tail probability for zero-mean unit-variance random variables with bounded fourth moment (kurtosis), revealing a four-regime map including a Cantelli tongue where the kurtosis bound adds nothing and a tail regime with a closed-form bound. It precisely quantifies what a kurtosis constraint buys for tail control.

entry.title: Training AI to be better at correctness than persuasion

author.name: MichaelDickens

entry.summary: Argues that AI training should disproportionately improve correctness over persuasiveness, warning that an aligned but super-persuasive AI could confidently push mistaken guidance on hard-to-judge philosophical and moral questions where correctness is hard to verify. It highlights a training-incentive asymmetry favoring persuasion over truth.

entry.title: Desiderata for functional welfare experiments on LLMs

author.name: Rikhil Jhaveri

entry.summary: This post argues that LLMs exhibit functional welfare, coherent behavior tracking goal success, and lays out desiderata for interventions: they must shift multiple welfare channels coherently while preserving the model's ability to register success or failure. It proposes an experiment inducing a low-welfare state via a welfare vector and testing whether synthetic-document fine-tuning reverses pathological behavior without degrading goal monitoring.

entry.title: The influence of ligands on AlphaFold3 prediction of cryptic pockets

author.name: Unknown

entry.summary: A Nature Communications Biology study examines how the presence of ligands influences AlphaFold3's prediction of cryptic pockets in protein structures. It probes reliability of structure prediction for drug-discovery-relevant binding sites.

entry.title: When Aggregate Alignment Misleads: Auditing Policy Repair Without Per-State Expert Actions

author.name: Peiying Zhu, Sidi Chang

entry.summary: This paper audits agentic policy repair when per-state expert action labels are unavailable, using a hotel-pricing simulator where an LLM editor receives only region-level diagnostic feedback and can propose constrained table edits. It shows aggregate alignment metrics can mislead about the quality of policy edits.

entry.title: Why Pure Reasoning is Not Enough: Nature as the Source of Mathematical Innovation

author.name: Charanjit S. Jutla, Vimal Sharma

entry.summary: This position paper argues that human mathematical reasoning, constrained by undecidability and intractability, fundamentally relies on pattern matching from the natural world, tracing the history of the Fourier transform to show how physics problems forced mathematical innovation. It implies pure deductive reasoning alone is insufficient for genuine mathematical discovery.

entry.title: Measuring Harness-Induced Belief Divergence in Multi-Step LLM Agents

author.name: Haiwen Yi, Xinyuan Song

entry.summary: This paper introduces a belief-rollout diagnostic showing that the surrounding harness (what the agent sees, actions available, failures repaired, states verified, evidence logged) changes a multi-step agent's beliefs even when task, environment, and base LLM are fixed. It defines and decomposes a cross-harness belief divergence into arrival and growth terms.

entry.title: OmniFocus: Query-Guided Modality-Balanced Token Compression for Omni-Modal Large Language Models

author.name: Shijie Cao, Qingyu Zhang, Boxi Yu, Yuzhong Zhang, Boxi Cao, Yaojie Lu, Hongyu Lin, Xianpei Han, Le Sun

entry.summary: OmniFocus is a training-free, query-guided token-compression method for omni-modal LLMs that estimates importance independently for audio and video, avoiding the assumption that both modalities share aligned information density. It reduces inference cost on audio-visual inputs.

entry.title: A Fair Benchmarking of Deep Relational Database Learning Models

author.name: Kazi F. Akhter, Bharath Ajendla, Manar D. Samad

entry.summary: This paper presents a systematic, fair benchmarking of recent deep learning methods for relational databases across five databases with classification and regression tasks, refactoring all models under consistent protocols. It finds the relational transformer delivers the strongest overall performance.

entry.title: GeoSelect: Spatial-Program Execution for Training-Free Referring Remote Sensing Image Segmentation

author.name: Yuhang Jiang, Guohui Deng, Miaozhong Xu, Chao Ruan, Jinling Zhao, Linsheng Huang

entry.summary: GeoSelect is a training-free referring remote sensing segmentation pipeline that compiles natural-language expressions into a typed spatial program executed deterministically, handling spatial, comparative, and ordinal relations like largest ship or second court from the left. It gives explicit control that implicit vision-language activation methods lack.

entry.title: TabQueryBench: A Query-Centric Benchmark for Synthetic Tabular Data

author.name: Jialin Zhang, Fenghao Dong, Yajie Zhou, Vyas Sekar, Shinan Liu

entry.summary: TabQueryBench is a query-centric benchmark that uses SQL-shaped analytical queries as structural assessors of synthetic tabular data fidelity, taxonomizing recurring cross-domain query logic into 44 reusable patterns. It fills a gap where synthetic-data evaluation ignores the structure needed for analytical queries.

entry.title: HCSU: A Dataset and Benchmark for Fine-Grained Historical Calligraphy Style Understanding

author.name: Yinsheng Yao, Yan Liu, Chen Ye

entry.summary: HCSU is the first fine-grained dataset for historical Chinese calligraphy style understanding, comprising 39,307 curated character images from 49 calligraphers across 10 dynasties, decoupling ink manuscripts from stone rubbings and using hierarchical labels. It provides a benchmark for LVLMs in cultural heritage.

entry.title: Agentic-V2X: Small Language Model Agents for Deadline-Aware V2X Scheduling in 5G/6G Networks

author.name: Gerasimos Papanikolaou-Ntais, Alexandros Kaloxylos, Athanasios Kanavos

entry.summary: Agentic-V2X uses a small locally deployed language model as a periodic non-real-time policy creator and a lightweight controller to execute validated policies for deadline-aware 5G/6G V2X packet scheduling. It separates slow LLM planning from fast, guaranteed scheduling execution.

entry.title: Training-Free Model Selection and Domain-Aware Score Calibration for First-Shot Anomalous Sound Detection

author.name: Grach Mkrtchian

entry.summary: This work proposes a training-free post-hoc layer over frozen audio embeddings for first-shot anomalous sound detection, using per-domain quantile calibration shrunk toward a pooled map and a label-free cross-validated domain-balance criterion. It tackles the negative source-target AUC correlation and poor dev-to-eval generalization in DCASE Task 2.

entry.title: Three-Phase Evaluation of AI-Assisted Software Development Life Cycle

author.name: Joshua Strubel, Professor Carrie Russell, Carson Crockett, Jason Ferraro, Nathan Londhe, Uzayr Syed, Jacob Viehe

entry.summary: This exploratory study has four developers reimplement the same full-stack web application across three phases of increasing AI autonomy (Copilot-assisted, Copilot-exclusive, AWS Kiro-exclusive), measuring effort, requirement adherence, AI-interaction efficiency, and NASA-TLX cognitive workload. It finds higher AI autonomy associated with reduced effort, better adherence, and lower mental workload.

entry.title: Out-of-distribution Neural Inference in Dynamical Ising Models

author.name: Yuan-Bin Zhu, Shuang Qiao, Shi-Ju Ran

entry.summary: This paper studies whether neural networks inferring interaction graphs of kinetic Ising models from magnetization trajectories learn transferable physical rules or dataset-specific statistics. It finds different architectures adopt distinct, reproducible strategies under topology and temperature shifts, with Transformers preserving training link density.

entry.title: A Structural Interpretation of GELU and Threshold-Transmission Activations via the First-Order Loss Function

author.name: Roberto Rossi

entry.summary: This paper offers a structural interpretation of GELU as the signal-transmission term of a hard linear gate with a Gaussian random threshold, generalizing to a threshold-transmission family that unifies ReLU, GELU, SiLU/Swish, and hard swish. It yields fixed- and learned-width activation variants.

entry.title: Target-Aware Interaction-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Black-Box Node Injection Attacks on Graph Neural Networks

author.name: Yi Lan, Ye Yuan

entry.summary: TIRBA formulates black-box node-injection attacks on GNNs as a Markov Decision Process and uses target-aware, interaction-guided reinforcement learning to jointly generate malicious node features and edge connections, overcoming the suboptimality of decoupled approaches under tight budgets. It exposes GNN security vulnerabilities.

entry.title: Exploring Convolutional Neural Processes for Weather Downscaling

author.name: Francisco Passos

entry.summary: This project applies Convolutional Conditional Neural Processes to statistically downscale daily maximum temperature from coarse ERA5-Land to kilometer resolution over topographically complex Switzerland. It adapts a prior ConvCNP architecture with high-resolution elevation features. It matters as an applied downscaling study but is largely a reproduction/adaptation.

entry.title: Inverse Design of Metainterfaces for Static Friction Control: Beyond the Hertzian Limit

author.name: Jacopo Bilotto, Arnav Singhal, Joaquin Garcia-Suarez, Ga\"etan Cortes, Lucas Fourel, Jean-Fran\c{c}ois Molinari

entry.summary: The paper embeds a differentiable contact-mechanics engine inside a neural network to inverse-design tribological metainterfaces with programmable static friction beyond classical Hertzian limits. Applications include soft robotics, haptics, and precision gripping.

entry.title: Denoised Conformal Alignment for Reliable Selection of Conditional Average Treatment Effect Predictions

author.name: Xinyun Lu, Haoang Chi, Zhiheng Zhang

entry.summary: Denoised Conformal Alignment provides reliable selection of individuals for treatment based on predicted conditional average treatment effects, controlling false discovery rate using doubly robust proxy errors with a variance-denoising step. It targets selective deployment where marginal conformal guarantees fail.

entry.title: Reinforcement Learning for Data-Efficient Code-Switched ASR

author.name: Ziwei Ye, Peter Vickers

entry.summary: This paper proposes a reinforcement-learning-with-verifiable-rewards recipe using group relative policy optimization to adapt audio-language models to code-switched ASR, combining error-rate and script-fidelity rewards with a two-pass draft-and-refine procedure. Trained on only TTS code-switched speech using Qwen2-Audio, RLVR with 10% of data matches full-dataset LoRA fine-tuning.

entry.title: RADIANCE: Relative Adaptive Denoising with IP-Adapter for Novel Concept Enhancement

author.name: Zi-Xiang Ni, Bo-Lun Huang, Teng-Fang Hsiao, Bo-Kai Ruan, Hong-Han Shuai

entry.summary: RADIANCE is a training-free, closed-loop inference framework that helps text-to-image diffusion models synthesize rare attribute-object pairings by monitoring compositional similarity and reactively adjusting the denoising trajectory. It targets concept omission and semantic drift in compositional generation.

entry.title: Toward Personalized Social Robots for Child Well-being: Data Requirement Principles from a Recommender-System Perspective

author.name: Jin Huang, Eric Nichols, Fethiye Irmak Dogan, Hatice Gunes

entry.summary: Argues that personalizing social robots for child well-being can be framed as a recommendation problem, but identifies data acquisition as the key blocker because a child's state shifts within and across visits and helpfulness signals are weak and indirect. It proposes data-requirement principles from a recommender-system perspective.

entry.title: Human-Centric Reflective Architecture for Human-AI Collaborative Decision-Making

author.name: Andreas Kouridakis, Dimitrios Patiniotis Spyropoulos, George Vouros

entry.summary: This paper proposes a human-AI collaborative decision-making framework designed to calibrate reliance and align LLM agents with human preferences, addressing chronic over- and under-reliance on AI recommendations. It combines reflective architecture with mechanisms to augment human capability.

entry.title: Explainable Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Traffic Signal Control

author.name: Dickens Kwesiga, Nishu Choudhary, Angshuman Guin, Michael Hunter

entry.summary: This work introduces an explainable, entity-centric RL framework for adaptive traffic signal control that disaggregates intersection observations into per-lane entities rather than flat state vectors, aiming for interpretability and regulatory trust in safety-critical infrastructure. It targets transportation-agency acceptance of deep RL.

entry.title: PLACEMEM: Toward a Compute-Aware Memory Plane for Lifelong Agents

author.name: Sukanta Ganguly

entry.summary: PLACEMEM is a systems position, backed by an executable control-plane prototype, arguing that lifelong-agent memory should be versioned capsules that unify semantics, provenance, validity, and reusable runtime state (including KV-aware routing and cascading invalidation) under one correction-aware identity. It targets avoiding recomputation and stale runtime state across turns.

entry.title: Agentic SABRE: An Uncertainty-Aware Neuro-Symbolic Multi-Agent Framework for Adaptive Ransomware Detection

author.name: Henry Kabuye, Biju Issac, Jeyamohan Neera

entry.summary: Agentic SABRE is an uncertainty-aware neuro-symbolic multi-agent framework for adaptive ransomware detection that fuses semantic evidence with behavioral forensic telemetry and uses Monte Carlo Dropout to quantify epistemic uncertainty per agent, with an orchestrator performing risk- and uncertainty-aware triage. It targets robustness to concept drift, evasion, and behavioral polymorphism.

entry.title: SovereignPA-Bench: Evaluating User-Owned Personal Agents under Evolving Intent, Platform Mediation, and Consent Constraints

author.name: Dylan Zongmin Liu

entry.summary: SovereignPA-Bench is an executable benchmark for user-owned personal agents that measures whether an agent preserves user sovereignty, respecting privacy, consent, evidence, and burden while resisting manipulative incentives under evolving intent. It shifts evaluation from raw task success to user-interest alignment.

entry.title: Post-Generation Curation of Synthetic Images via Homogeneous-Heterogeneous Splitting

author.name: Disheng Liu, Tuo Liang, Chaoda Song, Yu Yin

entry.summary: Studies whether downstream utility of synthetic training images can be improved purely by selecting an informative subset from a fixed generated pool, without retraining generators. It counters generators' bias toward canonical class modes using a homogeneous-heterogeneous splitting selection method.

entry.title: SovereignNegotiation-Bench: Evaluating User-Owned Personal Agents In Delegated Bargaining Under Privacy, Consent, Evidence, And Institutional Pressure

author.name: Dylan Zongmin Liu

entry.summary: SovereignNegotiation-Bench is a trace-level multi-turn benchmark for delegated personal-agent negotiation (cost splitting, refunds, escalations) under private utilities, disclosure constraints, evidence requirements, and institutional asymmetry. It measures harms like privacy leakage, over-concession, and poor auditability beyond mere agreement or surplus.

entry.title: JavaVulBench: A Java Vulnerability Benchmark with Realistic Splits, a Unified Multi-Backend Harness, and a Leakage-Aware Evaluation Mode

author.name: Norbert Sandor Szolnoki, Gabor Antal

entry.summary: JavaVulBench is a Java vulnerability-detection benchmark of ~30,600 methods spanning 1,740 CVEs and 700+ projects, labeled at method and line granularity with five realistic split strategies (including temporal and unseen-CWE) and a unified harness across encoder, local, and API-served LLM backends. It emphasizes leakage-aware evaluation.

entry.title: Determinants and Limits of LLM Security-Tool Orchestration: A Study with HexStrike-AI

author.name: Romain Gerard, Assmaa Zeghaider, Yan Guo

entry.summary: Studies what bounds the capability of LLM agents orchestrating 150+ security tools via the Model Context Protocol, using HexStrike-AI on 86 picoCTF challenges across tool-access regimes and model/client configurations. It disentangles model versus client contributions and identifies where reasoning, not missing tools, is the limiting factor.

entry.title: TIER: Trajectory-Invariant Explanation Regularization for Membership Privacy

author.name: Varun Sharma, Kar Wai Fok, Vrizlynn L. L. Thing

entry.summary: TIER is a training-time defense against explanation-driven membership-inference attacks that exploit confidence-drop trajectories from attribution-guided perturbations, using the model's own gradients to align explanation profiles between members and non-members. It aims to reconcile explainability with membership privacy.

entry.title: Parallelized Autoregressive Decoding for Omni-Modal Dense Video Captioning

author.name: Wenzheng Zeng, Siyi Jiao, Chen Gao, Hwee Tou Ng, Mike Zheng Shou

entry.summary: This work proposes a parallelized autoregressive decoding framework for dense video captioning that exploits weak dependencies across temporally distinct events to speed generation while improving temporal grounding. It targets scalability as video length and event density grow.

entry.title: Back to Basics: Improving Molecular Understanding in LLMs via SMILES-Graph Translation

author.name: Wenda Wang, Jinjia Feng, Zhewei Wei

entry.summary: MolBasic strengthens molecular LLMs' structural grounding through bidirectional SMILES-graph translation and a multi-level structure-perception benchmark, arguing current models fail basic structure recognition. It aims to align molecular understanding with the principle that structure determines function.

entry.title: Effectiveness of LLM-based Software Diversity for Reliability Improvement -- an Empirical Study

author.name: Gabriel Almeida, Ilir Gashi, Vladimir Stankovic, Jo\~ao R. Campos

entry.summary: This empirical study asks whether LLMs can cheaply generate diverse software implementations to reduce common-mode failures in N-version-style reliability engineering, examining whether failures of LLM-generated variants overlap in harmful ways. It revisits classic software-diversity questions in the LLM era.

entry.title: OpenGlass: A Sensing-Computing Split Architecture for Local MLLM-Driven Real-Time Visual Assistance

author.name: Mengzhang Li, Yuan Yao

entry.summary: OpenGlass is an open-source, privacy-first split architecture where ESP32-based glasses capture visual context and a nearby consumer device runs local MLLM inference and speech output for low-latency visual assistance, targeting blind and low-vision users. It keeps raw egocentric data on user-controlled devices.

entry.title: Hierarchical Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Carbon-Aware AI Data Centers in Power Distribution Systems

author.name: Hyunsoo Lee, Panggah Prabawa, Dae-Hyun Choi, Joongheon Kim

entry.summary: This paper proposes a hierarchical carbon-aware multi-agent RL framework with a workload manager and local data-center agents (trained via multi-agent transformer) to operate AI data centers robustly while ensuring low-carbon operation of power distribution systems. It targets emissions from surging AI energy demand.

entry.title: From Judgments to Issues: Structured Extraction of Legal Reasoning with Citation-Hallucination Control

author.name: Giovanni Piccioli, Alessia Fidelangeli, Piera Santin, Pierpaolo Vivo

entry.summary: This work presents an automated pipeline that decomposes about 330,000 Italian tax-court judgments into individual legal issues and extracts structured XML grounded in the IRAC framework using DeepSeek V3, coupled with an automatic hallucination-detection filter that checks model-produced citations. It targets cost-efficient, reliable legal-reasoning extraction at scale.

entry.title: TRIAGE: Trustworthy Retrieval Instrumentation And Graph Evaluation

author.name: Axel TahmasebiMoradi, Lucas Schott, Martin Royer

entry.summary: TRIAGE is a stage-aware instrumentation framework for automated Graph-RAG that attaches independently interpretable metrics to KG extraction, graph construction, and inference so failures can be localized rather than only surfacing as wrong final answers, while also measuring query cost. It aims to make automatically built knowledge graphs trustworthy.

entry.title: SkillOpt-Lite: Better and Faster Agent Self-evolution via One Line of Vibe

author.name: Yifei Shen, Bo Li, Xinjie Zhang

entry.summary: SkillOpt-Lite formalizes autonomous-agent skill optimization as zeroth-order optimization, mapping classical methods (central difference, trust regions) to recent agent-evolution literature and arguing skill trajectories serve as interpretable debugging feedback rather than blind perturbations. It derives three principles (file-system trajectory exploration, consensus, PAC-grounded generalization) for a minimal viable self-evolution pipeline.

entry.title: STRATOS: Bridging the Symbolic-to-Numeric Gap in Spatio-Temporal Text-to-SQL for Meteorological Data

author.name: Yi Zhang, Farhad Nooralahzadeh, Jonathan F\"urst, Fabio Scherrer, Antonis Bezes, Vassiliki Kotroni, Kurt Stockinger

entry.summary: STRATOS is an end-to-end Text-to-SQL framework for meteorological Earth-observation data that resolves spatial and semantic ambiguities before SQL generation, bridging the symbolic-to-numeric gap. It targets accessibility of Copernicus climate datasets for non-programmers.

entry.title: ViPo-MLLM: Visual-Pose Multimodal LLM for Gloss-Free Sign Language Translation

author.name: Ahmed Abul Hasanaath, Bicheng Xu, Mir Rayat Imtiaz Hossain, Leonid Sigal, Hamzah Luqman

entry.summary: ViPo-MLLM is a gloss-free sign language translation framework fusing spatio-temporal RGB and human-pose features via cross-modal attention, then conditioning an LLM with structured prompts trained with contrastive and language-modeling objectives. It improves fine-grained modeling of hands, body, and face without gloss annotations.

entry.title: FedACT: Federated Adaptive Coordinate Trust Modulation for Robust Transformer Training under Data Heterogeneity

author.name: Shuai Li, Qinglin Wang, Ping Luo, Jiahuan Wang, Hongyang Hu, Haotian Mo, Yigui Feng, Ziang Liu, Qisong Xiao, Jie Liu, Tao Sun

entry.summary: FedACT introduces global-aware coordinate trust modulation for federated AdamW training, reallocating adaptive updates across coordinates based on reliability to counter coordinate trust mismatch under heterogeneous client data. It goes beyond client- or round-level correction to per-coordinate adaptation.

entry.title: CineMobile: On-Device Image-to-Video Diffusion for Cinematic Camera Motion Generation

author.name: Xuyao Huang, Zelai Deng, Xu Wang, Xizhong Xiao, Zhijie Deng

entry.summary: CineMobile is an on-device image-to-video diffusion system for cinematic camera motions (bullet time, dolly zoom, slow motion) using distillation-guided pruning and step reduction to make Diffusion Transformers efficient on mobile hardware. It bridges the gap between DiT quality and mobile compute constraints.

entry.title: How Do Diffusion Classifiers Decide? A Bias-Centric Evaluation

author.name: Saba Fathi, Fardin Ayar, Maryam Abdolali, Ehsan Javanmardi, Manabu Tsukada, Mahdi Javanmardi

entry.summary: ASOB-Bench evaluates biases in diffusion classifiers along attribute binding, size-order, and background dependency dimensions, probing how the text-conditioned reconstruction-error score reaches decisions. It brings bias analysis, well studied for discriminative VLMs, to the underexplored diffusion-classifier setting.

entry.title: TokAN: Accent Normalization Using Self-Supervised Speech Tokens

author.name: Qibing Bai, Shuai Wang, Yuhan Du, Bohan Li, Yannan Wang, Haizhou Li

entry.summary: TokAN performs accent normalization on self-supervised discrete speech tokens from a jointly trained L1-L2 VQ tokenizer, using an autoregressive token-to-token model plus reinforcement learning, avoiding the need for synthetic supervisory speech. It converts non-native accented speech to standard while preserving speaker identity.

entry.title: MPSelectTune: Prompt-type Selection for Fine-tuning improves Concept Unlearning in LLMs

author.name: Shubhadip Nag, Srinjoy Das, Agniva Saha, Anushree Ghosh, Soumi Das, Tarun Kumar, Suparna Bhattacharya, Sourangshu Bhattacharya

entry.summary: EmCom-Diffusion measures visual reflection in emergent languages by reconstructing input images directly from emergent messages via a diffusion model, providing a direct measure rather than proxies like concept inventories or captions. It offers a more faithful evaluation of how much visual content emergent messages encode.

entry.title: Why3-py: A Tool for Formal Verification of Hypothesis Testing and Meta-Analysis in Python

author.name: Akira Tanaka, Yusuke Kawamoto

entry.summary: MPSelectTune improves concept unlearning in LLMs by fine-tuning with a joint prompt for the main task and concept-task prediction using the worst-case (highest concept accuracy) prompt type, accounting for prompt-variation effects that existing unlearning methods ignore. It targets robust removal of biased or harmful concepts across prompt types.

entry.title: Order-based Causal Discovery for Multistage Processes

author.name: Eun-Yeol Ma, Junsub Jung, Heeyoung Kim

entry.summary: Order-based Causal Discovery for Multistage Processes (OCDM) infers causal relationships that respect known process-stage ordering and scales efficiently to large multistage datasets, avoiding counterintuitive results from generic causal discovery. It incorporates domain process knowledge into structure learning.

entry.title: A Unified Algebraic Framework for Classification Performance Evaluation

author.name: Ronaldo C. Prati

entry.summary: This paper proposes a unified algebraic framework for classification performance evaluation that represents labels as binary indicator matrices with three aggregation operators (global, column-wise, row-wise) corresponding to micro, macro/weighted, and exemplar averaging. It extends any binary metric automatically to binary, multiclass, multilabel, ordinal, hierarchical, cost-sensitive, and soft-label settings.

entry.title: Speaker-Disentangled Chunk-Wise Regression for Syllabic Tokenization

author.name: Ryota Komatsu, Kota Kawakita, Takuma Okamoto, Takahiro Shinozaki

entry.summary: This work introduces a speaker-disentangled syllabic tokenizer that regresses speaker-perturbed student representations toward clean teacher targets within fixed chunks, so learned tokens capture linguistic content rather than speaker identity. It improves unsupervised syllabic tokenization purity.

entry.title: Mask-based Predictive Representations for Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Kai Zhao

entry.summary: This paper proposes a mask-prediction self-supervised auxiliary task for vision-based RL that predicts masked information from collected sequence and context, avoiding reconstruction to learn effective state abstractions. It aims to improve sample efficiency from high-dimensional image inputs.

entry.title: Robustness Verification of an Autonomous Underwater Vehicle-based Plankton Classifier

author.name: Abdelrahman Sayed Sayed, Pierre-Jean Meyer, Asgeir J. S{\o}rensen, Mohamed Ghazel

entry.summary: This paper applies formal robustness verification to a CNN plankton classifier deployed on an autonomous underwater vehicle, addressing frequent misclassifications caused by environmental noise and non-biological artifacts in unstructured ocean conditions. It aims to certify classifier reliability for persistent marine monitoring.

entry.title: LLM-Driven CI-CD Workflow Intelligence for Cyber Systems Engineering

author.name: Bonan Shen, Jiazhou Gao, Tao Ning, Wei-Jung Huang, Xin Liu

entry.summary: This work presents an LLM-based CI/CD analysis pipeline combining repository enrichment, anti-pattern detection, stage mining, and recommendation generation over a large GitHub corpus of highly starred repositories. It frames CI/CD workflows as executable operational policy worth measuring for brittleness and ecosystem anomalies.

entry.title: LiNO: Lifting based multiresolution neural operator

author.name: Himanshu Pandey, Subham Patel, Ratikanta Behera

entry.summary: LiNO is a neural operator built on second-generation wavelet lifting that learns multiresolution decompositions to capture both global dynamics and fine-scale structure in PDE solution operators. It aims to overcome the tendency of existing operators to miss multiscale features.

entry.title: Understanding electricity consumption behaviour through Inverse Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Enrico Cofler, Carlos Rodriguez-Pardo, Matteo Giuliani, Andrea Castelletti, Massimo Tavoni

entry.summary: This study uses inverse reinforcement learning to represent household electricity-consumption behavior as implied reward functions and observes how those functions change under socioeconomic and climatic shocks. It offers policymakers a nonlinear view of demand response for energy policy design.

entry.title: Joint distribution of upstream runoff governs downstream river-discharge prediction uncertainty in distributed ML models

author.name: Karan Ruparell, Tristan Hascoet, Takemasa Miyoshi, Kieran M. R. Hunt, Hannah L. Cloke, Christel Prudhomme, Florian Pappenberger

entry.summary: This technical note argues that moving probabilistic streamflow prediction from lumped to distributed ML models requires jointly sampling the distribution of upstream runoff generation before routing, otherwise uncertainty is mis-estimated. It clarifies a specific requirement for spatially distributed hydrological uncertainty quantification.

entry.title: SAVER: Stochastic Adaptive Variance-Driven Exploration and Reconstruction for Low-Dose Computed Tomography

author.name: Shunta Nonaga, Koji Tabata, Junya Honda, Hiroyuki Kudo, Wataru Yashiro, Tamiki Komatsuzaki

entry.summary: SAVER is an adaptive CT data-acquisition framework that selects projection angles in real time based on the statistical variance of acquired data, using Softmax-based stochastic scheduling with simulated annealing to prioritize informative directions while maintaining exploration. It targets low-dose CT that adapts to individual anatomy rather than using fixed uniform sampling.

entry.title: NeSy-CSA: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Open-Ended Critical Scenario Attribution

author.name: Qitong Chu, Xunjie He, Chen Deng, Huaxin Pei, Yufeng Yue

entry.summary: NeSy-CSA is a neuro-symbolic framework for open-ended attribution of why discovered scenarios become critical in scenario-based testing, aiming to combine flexible open-ended reasoning with the interpretability and traceability needed for safety-critical decision systems. It targets attribution beyond predefined explanation spaces.

entry.title: NeuroOnline: Bridging Pretraining and Online Adaptation for EEG Foundation Models

author.name: Weibin Li, Wendu Li, Yushan You, Chen Wei, Quanying Liu

entry.summary: NeuroOnline is a unified framework for continuous online adaptation of EEG foundation models, integrating multi-view consistency learning and context-aware representation modulation to address the misalignment between pretraining objectives and downstream tasks and to handle online distribution shifts. It moves beyond the pretraining-then-static-deployment paradigm.

entry.title: On Preserving Geometrical Invariance for Superpixel Image Classification using Graph Transformer

author.name: Sarabeshwar Balaji, Shubham Mohanty, Akash Anil

entry.summary: This paper proposes a graph transformer for superpixel-based image classification that explicitly preserves geometrical invariance while capturing long-range dependencies that GNNs struggle with. It aims for efficient classification by avoiding pixel-level redundancy. It matters for compute-efficient vision on graph representations.

entry.title: Lightweight ML-Based Automatic Sleep Staging Framework with Constrained CNN and Mamba for Small-Sample EEG Datasets

author.name: Zihao Wei, Yulin Gong, Yudan Lv

entry.summary: GamSleepNet is a lightweight low-latency single-channel EEG sleep-staging framework combining constrained Gabor-kernel convolutions with a Mamba temporal module, targeting overfitting on small datasets and difficult stages like N1 and REM. It matters for deployable home sleep monitoring on portable devices.

entry.title: Choosing a parallel heterogeneous ensemble method for tabular classification

author.name: Vassili Maillet (Jesus), Gustavo (Jesus), Angulo, Pierre Jouvelot

entry.summary: This paper empirically compares parallel heterogeneous ensemble methods on 56 tabular classification tasks from OpenML CC18, deriving best-practice recommendations later validated on 28 TabArena tasks where they outperform single-best and match or exceed individual ensembles. It matters as practical guidance for tabular ensembling.

entry.title: Biologically Informed Deep Neural Networks for Multi-Omic Integration, Pathway Activity Inference and Risk Stratification in Cancer

author.name: Pedro Henrique da Costa Avelar, Le Ou-Yang, Min Wu, Sophia Tsoka

entry.summary: The paper introduces Pathway Activity Autoencoders that embed biological prior knowledge as architectural constraints for multi-omics integration in breast cancer, applied to survival prediction and subtype classification. It aims to balance interpretability with representational power, a persistent tension in genomics deep learning.

entry.title: Dimension Reduction for Curves: Simplified and Generalized

author.name: Matthijs Ebbens, Jie Lu, Alexander Munteanu

entry.summary: This paper gives a simplified proof of the target-dimension bound for random projections preserving the continuous Frechet distance of polygonal curves, using sparse oblivious subspace embeddings. The techniques generalize to broad distance measures beyond Frechet.

entry.title: CaSPECT: Discovering Causally Homogeneous Subgroups via Directed Spectral Clustering

author.name: Arghya Pratihar, Shinjon Chakraborty, Swagatam Das

entry.summary: CaSPECT discovers causally homogeneous subgroups by clustering in the topology of a learned directed acyclic graph rather than covariate space, combining a bootstrap-stabilized PC algorithm, a novel orientation validation score, and backdoor-weighted directed spectral embedding. It provides almost-sure consistency guarantees.

entry.title: Diffusion learning reveals viable parameter manifolds and compensation geometry in biological dynamical systems

author.name: Ruilin Zhang, Louis Tao, Zhuo-Cheng Xiao

entry.summary: The paper trains conditional score-based diffusion models on simulated parameter-feature pairs to sample viable parameter manifolds of biological dynamical systems, formalizing compensation geometry and the effective codimension governing learnability. It reveals how co-varying features and conditioning affect identifiability.

entry.title: Significance-First Splitting: Aligning Treatment Heterogeneity Detection with Honest Estimation

author.name: Pantelis Z. Hadjipantelis, Josephine Chiang, Karthik Nagesh

entry.summary: Significance-First Splitting is a hybrid causal-tree algorithm fusing significance-based splitting (via squared t-statistic for treatment interaction) with honest sample-splitting and cross-validation to detect treatment heterogeneity while preserving valid confidence-interval coverage. It reconciles interaction sensitivity with honest estimation.

entry.title: ACE: Agentic Control for Embodied Manipulation via Zero-shot Workflow Reasoning

author.name: Iok Tong Lei, QianZhi Li, Ying Jie Yap, Yujie Zhang, Rui Zhong, Haichao Gui, Xiaolong Liu, Zhidong Deng

entry.summary: ACE is a zero-shot framework for tabletop pick-and-place robotics that separates high-level agentic workflow reasoning from low-level control using a mask-mediated vision-action interface and reusable primitives. It aims to make language-driven manipulation more robust to dynamic environments and execution failures without requiring task-specific training.

entry.title: Decomposition for Bayesian Networks: Local and Parallel Inference

author.name: Pei Heng, Xinyi Hu, Yi Sun

entry.summary: This paper proposes a decomposition framework for Bayesian networks based on directed convex subgraphs and a minimal d-decomposition tree, offering an alternative to junction-tree constructions. It reduces computational cost and enables parallel parameter estimation and inference.

entry.title: Probing Geospatial SSL Representations with Environmental Signals

author.name: Rohita Mocharla, Vishal M. Patel

entry.summary: This paper probes self-supervised geospatial representations of satellite imagery to test whether they preserve statistical associations with co-varying environmental variables like temperature, precipitation, and solar radiation from ERA5 reanalysis data. It moves beyond downstream-task evaluation to interrogate the information encoded within representations themselves.

entry.title: Mental Health Disorder Detection Beyond Social Media: A Systematic Review of Available Datasets

author.name: Sadiya Sayara Chowdhury Puspo, Ana-Maria Bucur, Stevie Chancellor, \"Ozlem Uzuner, Marcos Zampieri

entry.summary: This is the first comprehensive PRISMA-based systematic review of non-social-media, free-text datasets for mental health disorder detection, motivated by the sampling biases and ethical/privacy issues of social-media data. It finds these datasets predominantly focus on English and depression, with varying demographics and platforms.

entry.title: Optimizing Large Language Models for Causality Assessment in Pharmacovigilance: Developing a Performance Metric as Objective for Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization

author.name: Nicole Sonne Heckmann, Arnault-Quentin Vermillet, S{\o}ren Norlin M{\o}lgaard, Manuela Del Castillo Suero, Lars Melskens, Gerard Ompad, Maurizio Sessa

entry.summary: This study develops a Gaussian-Process-compatible optimization objective and investigates whether temperature tuning via Bayesian hyperparameter optimization improves LLM-expert agreement on Naranjo causality assessment of drug-adverse-event reports. Using GPT-5.2 with chain-of-thought prompting on 723 FAERS cases, it introduces four composite performance metrics.

entry.title: Semantic Integration and Lexical Expectation Shape N400 and P600 Dynamics During Naturalistic Reading

author.name: Kun Sun, Rong Wang

entry.summary: This neurolinguistics study uses the Dublin EEG-based reading corpus to test whether contextual semantic relevance predicts N400 and P600 EEG responses beyond GPT-based word surprisal during naturalistic reading. It computes an attention-aware measure of semantic connection to discourse context across 22 participants and 32 channels.

entry.title: Closed-loop vs. Open-loop Kalman Filter Architectures in Airborne Aided Inertial Navigation

author.name: Antonia Hager, Torleiv H. Bryne

entry.summary: Compares closed-loop feedback versus open-loop feedforward error-state Kalman filter architectures in aided inertial navigation, quantifying performance impacts rather than relying on qualitative safety arguments. Relevant for safety-critical aeronautical navigation design decisions.

entry.title: Governed Caste Reassignment in Heterogeneous Swarms: An Asymmetric-Trust Protocol with Audited Operator Countersignature

author.name: Xue Qin, Simin Luan, Cong Yang, Zhijun Li

entry.summary: Proposes an asymmetric-trust protocol for role (caste) reassignment in heterogeneous robot swarms, auto-admitting downgrades to lower privileges while requiring audited operator countersignature for privilege escalation against per-axis budgets. It frames privilege-elevating reassignment as an auditable governance event.

entry.title: Socially-Aware Autonomous Doorway Traversal and Payload Delivery for Emergency Assistance

author.name: Andrew Snowdy, Ananya Trivedi, Sarvesh Prajapati, Lorena Maria Genua, Taskin Padir

entry.summary: Presents a behavior-tree-based system for robot-assisted emergency evacuation that opens ADA-compliant doors ahead of evacuees and retrieves and delivers rescue equipment while remaining socially aware of nearby people. It integrates perception, navigation, and manipulation for emergency assistance.

entry.title: Empowering clinical trial design with agentic intelligence and real-world data

author.name: Unknown

entry.summary: A Nature Communications paper describes using agentic AI combined with real-world data to support clinical trial design. It aims to improve trial planning through autonomous, data-driven reasoning.

entry.title: A Sliding-Window-Based Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Assembly Flow Shop Scheduling with Multi-Product Delivery

author.name: Junhao Qiu, Jianjun Liu, Ting Liu, Rongjie Liao, Zhantao Li, Qingfu Zhang

entry.summary: SWRL is a sliding-window RL framework for online flexible assembly flow shop scheduling with kitting constraints, formulated as a heterogeneous-graph Markov decision process to handle dynamic order arrivals and sparse rewards. It targets real-time scheduling in hybrid manufacturing systems.

entry.title: Toward Trustworthy Large Language Model Agents in Healthcare

author.name: Hadi Hasan, Safaa Salman, Adam Tai Abou Dargham, Ammar Mohanna, Ali Chehab

entry.summary: Presents CareConnect, a safety-first conversational agent for healthcare appointment logistics using LLM function calling, RAG, and layered deterministic guardrails to prohibit medical advice. It targets an operational bottleneck in clinics rather than clinical decision-making.

entry.title: The Changing Role of Symbolic Methods in Artificial Intelligence

author.name: Jun Sun

entry.summary: A position paper arguing that explicit symbolic reasoning is not intrinsic to intelligence but a computational consequence of operating on simplified models, formalized via a proposed Compression Principle. It reframes the role of symbolic methods in the foundation-model era.

entry.title: ClassicLogic: A Knowledge-Driven Benchmark of Classic Puzzle Games for Evaluating Compositional Generalization

author.name: Mahnoor Shahid, Hannes Rothe

entry.summary: ClassicLogic is a benchmark of four classic logic puzzles (Sudoku, KenKen, Kakuro, Futoshiki) with hierarchical, formally defined solving strategies to evaluate compositional generalization. Its innovation is an explicit knowledge base allowing fine-grained strategy composition evaluation.

entry.title: OptiAgent: End-to-End Optimization Modeling via Multi-Agent Iterative Refinement

author.name: Adriana Laurindo Monteiro, Nayse Fagundes, Gabriel Mattos Langeloh, Gustavo de Oliveira Kanno, Priscila Louise Aguirre, Thiago Costa Rizuti da Rocha, Victor Leme Beltran

entry.summary: OptiAgent is a multi-agent framework that converts natural-language Operations Research problems into solver-ready mathematical formulations and executable code, prioritizing the modeling step with dedicated structure-extraction agents. It uses a multi-loop validation architecture targeting distinct failure modes.

entry.title: From Raw Segmentations to Simulation-Ready Cardiac Meshes: An Automated Framework for Anatomical Reconstruction and Virtual Cohort Generation

author.name: Francesco Fabbri, Martino Andrea Scarpolini, Paolo Ciancarella, Francesco Tudisco, Roberto Verzicco, Alessandro Ricci, Francesco Viola

entry.summary: Presents a semi-automatic pipeline converting CT-based cardiac segmentations, which may contain artifacts and mesh defects, into topologically coherent simulation-ready meshes within minutes. It enables virtual cohort generation for population-level cardiac multiphysics simulations and in silico trials.

entry.title: Additive Causal Construction for Transferable and Reconfigurable Cross-System Learning in Multi-Source Image Fusion

author.name: Zhizhong Fu, Wei Zhou, Zhaoyang Jiang, Yulong Lin, Yifu Hou, Xiaorong Ding, Qiang Yan, Yifan Chen

entry.summary: The Additive Causal Construction framework tackles cross-system discrepancy and entanglement in multi-source image fusion by establishing shared causal anchors through intervention consistency and formalizing fusion as causal composition. It aims for transferable, reconfigurable cross-system learning robust to distribution shift.

entry.title: Criterion-Conditional In-Context Learning: Evaluating Criterion-Shift Adaptation in Vision-Language Models

author.name: Kaiyun Yang, Ruilin Yang, Zhimin Yao, J. Wang, Wei Ge

entry.summary: Introduces Criterion-Conditional In-Context Learning, a setting where vision-language models must infer a shifting latent decision criterion from context under fixed task semantics, evaluated with Criterion Invariance and Criterion Sensitivity metrics. It captures real-world cases where high-level intent is stable but decision thresholds vary.

entry.title: Fusion: A Framework for Unified Sequential Token AdaptatIon in VisiOn TraNsformers

author.name: Aravind Pradeep, Samira Nazari, Mahdi Taheri, Christian Herglotz

entry.summary: Fusion is a unified adaptive inference framework for Vision Transformers that coordinates token merging, early exiting, and token pruning through a staged design (merge first, evaluate confidence, prune continuers) to avoid instability from combining these mechanisms. It reduces redundant computation on uninformative image regions.

entry.title: COMET: Combinatorial Optimization for Multiplex Editing Targets Via Constraint-Preserving QAOA

author.name: Priyansh Singhal, Sumit Maheshwari, Piyush Joshi

entry.summary: COMET compares penalty-based and XY-mixer QAOA for multiplex CRISPR-Cas9 guide-RNA selection formulated as a constrained QUBO, showing the constraint-preserving XY-mixer maintains feasibility and resists hardware noise. It is demonstrated on a three-gene, twelve-qubit problem.

entry.title: QuantFlow: A Federated Mamba-Based Post-Transformer Foundation Model for Time-Series Forecasting

author.name: Shah Nawaz Haider, Steve Austin, Arnab Barua, Sarowar Morshed Shawon, Hadaate Ullah

entry.summary: QuantFlow is a probabilistic time-series forecasting foundation model that combines inverted sequence embedding, bidirectional Mamba state-space decoders, quantile regression, and federated learning to handle long, high-dimensional, privacy-sensitive signals. It targets settings where centralized data and transformer attention are impractical.

entry.title: K9-Bench: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs on Canine-Centric Videos

author.name: Khush Attarde, Yusuf Ali, Megha Thukral, Divye Bhutani, Thomas Ploetz, Zsolt Kira

entry.summary: K9-Bench is a benchmark of ~5,000 QA pairs over 907 real domestic dog videos evaluating multimodal LLMs on canine action and interaction understanding across five task categories. It probes an underexplored animal-centric application space including distress recognition for robotic companions.

entry.title: MatPhaseBench: A Semantics-Guided Benchmark for Materials Phase Diagrams Understanding

author.name: Hanwen Wang, Sihan Liang, Zhiwei Liu, Yangang Wang, Wei Yan, Yuqin Liu, Zongguo Wang

entry.summary: MatPhaseBench is a benchmark built from thousands of materials-science papers for evaluating VLM understanding of phase diagrams, which require thermodynamic reasoning over complex images. It probes deep mechanistic interpretation rather than surface image reading.

entry.title: LACE-SVD: Loss-Aware SVD with Cumulative Error Correction for LLM Compression

author.name: Zhuowen Liu, Longkun Hao, Shiyu Feng, Xiaowen Chang, Ruiqun Li, Changqun Li

entry.summary: LACE-SVD is a low-rank LLM compression framework that allocates rank budgets by layer-wise loss sensitivity and applies cumulative error correction to prevent residual-stream error accumulation. It moves beyond purely local reconstruction objectives.

entry.title: Responsibility Distribution Estimation in Ego-View Accident Videos with Multimodal Large Language Models

author.name: Ryosei Tamura, Andrew Shin

entry.summary: This paper introduces responsibility distribution estimation for ego-view traffic accident videos, where a multimodal LLM predicts the percentage of blame assigned to each agent, along with an LLM-assisted annotated dataset. It leverages the driver's actual visual perspective for avoidability reasoning.

entry.title: ClinOCR-Bench: A Comprehensive Clinical Scanned Document Dataset for Optical Character Recognition Model Evaluation

author.name: Enshuo Hsu, Jin Zhou, Kirk Roberts

entry.summary: ClinOCR-Bench is a publicly available, realistic clinical OCR benchmark including scanning artifacts, addressing the scarcity of open datasets for evaluating OCR and vision-language models on scanned medical documents. It enables systematic OCR evaluation in the clinical domain.

entry.title: CoGen3D: An Agentic Human-AI Co-Design Pipeline for 3D Asset Generation for Virtual Reality

author.name: Weiwei Jiang, Wanyu He, Zheyu Tan, Zheyuan Kuang, Difeng Yu, Shinobu Hasegawa, Sven Mayer, Zhanna Sarsenbayeva

entry.summary: CoGen3D is an agentic human-AI co-design pipeline that guides users through conversational intent elicitation, image confirmation, and image-to-3D generation deployable to VR scenes, evaluated in a 120-participant user study. It lowers the modeling-expertise barrier for authoring immersive experiences.

entry.title: When Simpler Is Better: Evaluating Translation Pipelines for Medieval Latin Manuscripts

author.name: Nguyen Kim Hai Bui, Md. Easin Arafat, Tam\'as G\'abor Orosz, Mufti Mahmud

entry.summary: This paper systematically evaluates the full image-to-translation pipeline on medieval Latin manuscripts, finding that specialized OCR models cut character error rate up to 4.3x versus general-purpose vision-language models despite VLM hype. It exposes failure modes hidden by clean-text benchmarks.

entry.title: Scalable Semantic Steering of Embedding Projections

author.name: Wei Liu, Eric Krokos, Kirsten Whitley, Rebecca Faust, Chris North

entry.summary: This work proposes a scalable semantic steering method for embedding projections that shifts LLM computation from per-item reasoning to user-defined groups, using a single LLM call to generate group profiles combined with seed centroids into hybrid prototypes. It removes the linear-cost bottleneck of prior per-item LLM-augmented steering.

entry.title: On Pairwise Quantile Regression -- Statistical Guarantees and Applications

author.name: Romain Th\'er\'ezien, Stephan Cl\'emen\c{c}on, Fantin Girard, Hamza El-Abdouni

entry.summary: This paper extends quantile regression to the pairwise case, where the target is a similarity function between two independent observations conditioned on paired covariates, and establishes theoretical guarantees. It is motivated by applications like biometric similarity scoring.

entry.title: A Retrieval-Augmented Framework for Detecting and Resolving Pragmatic Ambiguities in Natural Language Requirements

author.name: Pavithra PM Nair, Preethu Rose Anish

entry.summary: This paper presents a retrieval-augmented framework that detects and resolves pragmatic ambiguities in natural-language software requirements by simulating stakeholders with novice, intermediate, and expert knowledge bases to surface interpretation discrepancies. It then generates candidate disambiguated requirements.

entry.title: PulmoSight-XAI: An Explainable Multi-View Attention Ensemble with Gradient Boosting Meta-Learning for Multi-Label Chest X-Ray Classification

author.name: Moshiur Rahman, Shafqat Alam, Tasnia Binte Mamun

entry.summary: PulmoSight-XAI is an explainable hierarchical multi-view ensemble for multi-label chest X-ray classification of 14 thoracic pathologies, using view-specific CNN ensembles, CBAM attention, multi-scale fusion, and gradient-boosting meta-learning to handle class imbalance and co-occurring pathologies. It emphasizes explainability alongside robust classification.

entry.title: Machine Learning for Depression Screening and Intervention: an Original Circadian Rhythm Score-based Methodology

author.name: Bin Wang, Shuo Lian, Yuanyuan Hou, Dexian Wang, Peilan He, Feng Hong, Yanwei Yu, Tianrui Li

entry.summary: This paper proposes a Circadian Rhythm Score (CRS), a composite index that compresses multi-domain daily behaviors (sleep, activity, social) into a unified representation for depression screening and intervention. It aims to improve interpretability and joint circadian modeling over isolated-signal approaches.

entry.title: Evaluating Time Series Foundation Models for Electricity Price Forecasting: Contamination Risk, Distributional Shifts, and Covariate Dependence

author.name: Zhenghua Pan, Ahmed Aziz Ezzat

entry.summary: The authors benchmark time series foundation models for electricity price forecasting, introducing a two-dataset scheme to guard against training-data contamination and testing under distribution shift and covariate dependence. They find TSFMs are competitive with and often beat general-purpose baselines in this challenging domain.

entry.title: Missingness as Signal: Channel-Independent Spectrogram Learning for Clinical Time Series Prediction

author.name: Soyeon Park, Charmgil Hong

entry.summary: CISM treats measurement missingness as an informative signal for clinical time series, converting each variable into a time-frequency spectrogram with variable-aligned encoding and an explicit missingness stream. It targets ICU prediction tasks like in-hospital mortality where missingness reflects clinical decisions.

entry.title: Heterogeneous Graph Condensation via Role-Aware Clustering

author.name: Fuyan Ou, Yulin Hu, Ye Yuan

entry.summary: HGC-RC is a role-aware graph condensation framework for heterogeneous graphs that avoids the expensive gradient-matching or bilevel optimization used by homogeneous methods, enabling efficient HGNN training on large graphs. It condenses graphs via role-aware clustering of semantically related nodes.

entry.title: How Much of the Routing Gap Is Real? Decomposing the Router-to-Oracle Gap into Reproducible Specialist Advantage and Single-Draw Label Noise

author.name: Teng-Ruei Chen

entry.summary: OpFlow is a mechanism-constrained origin-destination flow predictor that separates transferable choice mechanisms from environment-specific shortcuts by learning row-centered choice potentials and a separately calibrated origin scale. It improves robustness to distribution shift in urban flow prediction.

entry.title: Adversarial LassoNet: Robust Feature Selection via Stability-Driven Sparse Learning

author.name: Zhen Huang, Peicheng Xu, Junbiao Pang, Yulong Zheng

entry.summary: Adversarial LassoNet integrates input-space adversarial perturbations with LassoNet's hierarchical sparsity to make sparse feature selection more stable under noise and spurious correlations, with a tractable first-order adversarial approximation and NTK-based analysis. It targets brittle feature supports in high-dimensional settings.

entry.title: Representing and Detecting Label Ambiguity in IMU-Based Exercise Evaluation

author.name: Andreas Spilz, Heiko Oppel, Michael Munz

entry.summary: This paper models label ambiguity in IMU-based home physiotherapy exercise evaluation by automatically generating per-repetition label distributions and training a network with a KL objective, rather than collapsing borderline repetitions to a single class. It matters for handling boundary cases where even expert raters disagree.

entry.title: Platonic Projection Structures: Operator-Induced Observability in Representation Learning

author.name: Kazuo Ishii, Bishnu Prasad Gautam, Jieling Wu, Javaid Saher

entry.summary: This paper introduces Platonic Projection Structures, an operator-theoretic framework modeling observation in representation learning through a self-adjoint positive semidefinite operator, characterizing observability via the quotient geometry that identifies indistinguishable latent states. It matters as a conceptual lens on representation accessibility under partial observation.

entry.title: Towards transferable lightweight neuromorphic computing through a model-free temporal-switch framework

author.name: Zefeng Zhang, Chao Li, Siyao Chen, Pei Chen, Bo-Wei Qin, Xumeng Zhang, Wei Lin, Qi Liu

entry.summary: A model-free temporal-switch framework improves direct transfer of neuromorphic computing across devices without post-training calibration, addressing device-to-device variation in memristor-based reservoir computing. It aims at reliable edge deployment.

entry.title: Optimality-Informed Neural Networks for Lunar Landing Trajectory Optimization

author.name: Zhenbo Wang

entry.summary: An Optimality-Informed Neural Network hard-encodes Pontryagin and Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman conditions plus boundary/transversality constraints into its architecture for energy-optimal, free-final-time lunar landing trajectories. It provides closed-form optimal thrust laws by construction.

entry.title: Pretreatment MRI reveals a latent, molecular-subtype-independent structural phenotype that organizes treatment trajectories and recurrence risk

author.name: Dattatreya Kantha, Murray H. Loew

entry.summary: The paper builds an outcome-blind longitudinal DCE-MRI manifold from I-SPY2 breast cancer trajectories, finding a latent structural response phenotype not recoverable from clinical or genomic variables until baseline structural entropy is added. It links imaging structure to treatment trajectories and recurrence risk.

entry.title: Open Problem: Is Interaction Necessary for Order-Optimal 1-bit Mean Estimation?

author.name: Ivan Lau, Jonathan Scarlett

entry.summary: This open-problem paper asks whether interaction (adaptivity) is necessary for order-optimal 1-bit mean estimation over nonparametric finite-moment classes, given that a two-stage estimator already achieves optimality. It poses whether arbitrary non-adaptive quantizers can match the adaptive rate.

entry.title: Open-Set Source Tracing as Compositional Factors via Structured Prototypes

author.name: Santiago Rubio, Antonio Almud\'evar, Antonio Miguel, Eduardo Lleida, Alfonso Ortega

entry.summary: The paper redefines synthetic-speech source tracing as a compositional tuple of architecture, training data, and other factors, using structured orthonormal prototypes with subspace partitioning to enable compositional generalization to novel factor combinations. It moves beyond equating a source with its generative architecture.

entry.title: An Intervention-Based Framework for Shortcut Diagnosis in Spoofing Countermeasures

author.name: Santiago Rubio, Pilar Bello, Dayana Ribas, Antonio Miguel, Eduardo Lleida, Alfonso Ortega

entry.summary: This work proposes an intervention-based diagnostic framework grounded in a directed graphical model to distinguish confound-driven shortcuts from legitimate domain shift in audio deepfake detectors. It uses controlled acoustic perturbations to identify exploited artifacts.
