entry.title: Air-Plan: Query-Optimized Topology Selection for Over-the-Air Decentralized Federated Learning

author.name: Kaushal Attaluri, Rebeca P. Diaz-Redondo, Manuel Fernandez Veiga

entry.summary: AIRPLAN establishes a formal equivalence between over-the-air decentralized federated learning and distributed query processing, enabling principled communication topology selection that reduces latency. It fills a gap where topology selection for OTA-DFL was previously ad hoc or absent.

entry.title: Quadrature-Aware Complex-Linear Neural Operator for Boundary-to-Field Prediction in Resonant Acoustics

author.name: Muhammad Idrees Khan, Hua-Dong Yao

entry.summary: This work introduces a quadrature-aware complex-linear boundary operator that maps surface velocity to acoustic pressure while preserving physical superposition and homogeneity by construction. It accelerates repeated acoustic field predictions that would otherwise require expensive wave simulations.

entry.title: Weakly Guided and Autoregressive Beamformer Parameterization for Generalizable Moving Speaker Extraction in Higher-Order Ambisonics

author.name: Jakob Kienegger, Tal Peer, Sina Khanagha, Timo Gerkmann

entry.summary: This paper proposes a data-driven beamforming pipeline for extracting moving speakers in higher-order ambisonics that only needs the target's initial direction, decoupling neural spectral processing from linear spatial filtering. It achieves generalizable, array-agnostic speech enhancement using autoregressive causal processing.

entry.title: Physically-Relevant Information Learning in High-Dimensional Time-Derivatives Spaces

author.name: Domiziano Doria, Matteo Becchi, Giovanni M. Pavan

entry.summary: This paper introduces high-dimensional Time-Derivative spaces to jointly learn and correlate structure and dynamics in many-body complex systems, arguing that positional or velocity information alone is insufficient for complete physical understanding. It demonstrates extraction of physically relevant information from such spaces.

entry.title: Jointly Improving Dialect Identification and ASR in Indian Languages using Multimodal Feature Fusion

author.name: Saurabh Kumar, Amartyaveer, Prasanta Kumar Ghosh

entry.summary: This paper proposes a multimodal framework that jointly improves ASR and dialect identification for Indian languages, using a bottleneck encoder for dialectal features and a RoBERTa encoder for CTC embeddings merged via a gating mechanism. It shows joint modeling avoids the trade-offs seen when optimizing ASR or DID separately across eight Indian languages.

entry.title: Alignment-Guided Largest Table Overlap Size Estimation

author.name: Ge Lee, Shixun Huang, Zhifeng Bao, Shazia Sadiq, Yanchang Zhao

entry.summary: This paper improves estimation of the largest overlap size between tables, addressing limitations of the state-of-the-art Armadillo estimator by modeling row-column structure and inter-table alignment signals explicitly. It uses alignment-guided encoding to improve accuracy in heterogeneous table repositories for blocking and query-by-table retrieval.

entry.title: S-DiverSe: Spanish Diverse Speech

author.name: Fernando L\'opez, Fernando Iba\~nez, Ana Mart\'inez, Iv\'an Alonso, Pablo G\'omez, Santosh Kesiraju, Jordi Luque

entry.summary: S-DiverSe is a corpus of 3.2 hours of in-the-wild Spanish speech from 22 speakers with ALS, Parkinson's disease, and stroke, with 444 transcribed segments and metadata on disease type and intelligibility. It supports ASR evaluation and adaptation for neurologically affected Spanish speech, a challenging and under-served area.

entry.title: Progressive Disclosure for LLM-Maintained Wiki Knowledge Bases: a Preregistered Ablation

author.name: Theodore O. Cochran

entry.summary: A preregistered ablation testing whether progressive disclosure (a compact catalog plus one-line page summaries) makes LLM agents cheaper and more accurate on a real 709-page wiki, using byte-identical frozen content across access-structure arms. It isolates the effect of retrieval access structure alone.

entry.title: You Frame It: How Conceptual Representations Shape LLM Detection and Reasoning about Antisemitism

author.name: Katharina Soemer, Helena Mihaljevi\'c

entry.summary: Compares definitional, taxonomic, example-augmented, and large-context conceptual groundings for LLM antisemitism detection across four models, finding fine-grained taxonomies raise recall but lower precision and that larger conceptual resources add no benefit. It probes how conceptual framing shapes detection and reasoning.

entry.title: Streaming Neural Speech Codecs through Time-Invariant Representations

author.name: K\'elian Est\`eve, Salima Mhdaffar, Mickael Rouvier, Richard Dufour, Yannick Est\`eve

entry.summary: Investigates the factorized time-invariant representations of the TiCodec neural speech codec via probing tasks, finding intermediate layers capture complementary speaker and environment information, and studies suitability for low-latency streaming. It informs codec design for codec-based speech generation.

entry.title: When Should Service Agents Reconsider? Difficulty-Routed Control in Customer-Service Operations

author.name: Qian Chen, Chengyuan Liu, Xin Yu

entry.summary: Proposes a difficulty-routed service-control architecture for autonomous customer-service agents that keeps routine sessions on a low-cost path while routing operationally risky requests (refunds, cancellations) to reconsider before executing backend writes. It targets balancing service speed against operational error prevention.

entry.title: Large-scale dataset of automatically classified rhetorical sections in scientific papers

author.name: Daniel Verdi, Jacob Aarup Dalsgaard, Roberta Sinatra

entry.summary: Releases section-level rhetorical annotations (Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion) for 15.6 million scientific papers from S2ORC using a rule-based classifier, validated against human and LLM annotators. It enables large-scale analysis of scientific writing patterns.

entry.title: CARD: Cross-component Audio Representation Distillation for Encoder-Free Audio Captioning

author.name: Ganesh Pavan Kartikeya Bharadwaj Kolluri, Yuchen Zhang, Michael Kampouridis, Ravi Shekhar

entry.summary: CARD is an encoder-free audio captioning model that removes the audio encoder at inference by distilling a pretrained audio teacher into a tiny projector and LoRA-adapted frozen LLM, routing perceptual stages to the projector and semantic stages to the LLM. It improves CIDEr-D while cutting inference cost.

entry.title: Curated retrieval versus open web search in public AI information services: a coverage-trust trade-off

author.name: Hafsteinn Einarsson, Hafsteinn Birgir Einarsson, J\'on Gunnar \'Olafsson, J\'on Gunnar {\TH}orsteinsson

entry.summary: A pre-launch expert evaluation of a government-funded Icelandic EU-information AI service, comparing curated retrieval versus live web search across 551 evaluations by five domain experts. It surfaces a coverage-trust tradeoff relevant to public-institution deployments during Iceland's EU referendum context.

entry.title: Do Diabetic Foot Ulcer Segmentation Models Generalize? A Cross-Dataset Benchmark of CNN and Transformer Architectures

author.name: Abderrahmane Benfatah

entry.summary: Benchmarks U-Net, DeepLabV3+, and SegFormer-B2 for diabetic foot ulcer segmentation under a leakage-screened cross-dataset protocol, showing all achieve strong in-domain results but degrade substantially on external datasets in architecture-dependent ways. It exposes overstated generalization in DFU segmentation.

entry.title: How many labels do you need? A decision framework for cross-habitat marine species recognition

author.name: Alzayat Saleh, Mostafa Rahimi Azghadi

entry.summary: Presents a decision framework quantifying the tradeoff between labelling effort and recognition accuracy when transferring vision systems across marine habitats, spanning five datasets, three oceans, and three taxonomic groups. It gives ecologists evidence-based guidance on labelling needs for new-site deployment.

entry.title: MAGE: View-guided Point Cloud Completion with Efficient Modality Alignment and Adaptive Geometry Enhancement

author.name: Weize Quan, Zhengwei Wu, Kai Wang, Dong-Ming Yan

entry.summary: MAGE is a view-guided point cloud completion framework using geometry-aware modality alignment via a shared self-attention transformer with cross-modal reconstruction supervision, plus adaptive geometry enhancement. It addresses weak image-point modality alignment and limited self-geometry enhancement in completing 3D shapes from a single-view image.

entry.title: CPR: Chained Perceptual Refinement for Coarse-to-Fine Medical Image Classification

author.name: Si-Yuan Lu, Hanruo Zhu, Ziquan Zhu, Gaojie Jin, Zeyu Fu, Lu Yin, Ke Li, Lu Liu, Tianjin Huang

entry.summary: CPR (Chained Perceptual Refinement) is a coarse-to-fine framework for high-resolution medical image classification that starts from a low-resolution global view, dynamically predicts refinement regions, and incrementally integrates high-resolution evidence while keeping backbone input size fixed. It preserves diagnostic detail while limiting compute.

entry.title: BiSLW: Bi-Spectral Latent Watermarking for Generative Diffusion Models

author.name: Aryan Pandit

entry.summary: BiSLW is a trainable bi-spectral latent watermarking framework for diffusion models that jointly embeds aligned identity signals across complementary spectral bands of the decoded latent using learned encoders and decoders. It targets attribution robustness against diffusion's stochasticity and regeneration attacks.

entry.title: GRCD: Grounded Region Change Detection for Multi-Finding Chest X-Ray Pairs

author.name: OFM Riaz Rahman Aranya, Peyman Najafirad, Kevin Desai

entry.summary: GRCD is a framework for grounded region change detection from chest X-ray pairs in the multi-finding setting, combining temporal reasoning across current and prior images with spatial grounding of multiple findings annotated by anatomical region and change status. It addresses the gap where prior methods handle only single findings or lack spatial grounding.

entry.title: Biomechanics-aware Multi-view Markerless Motion Capture of Dexterous Hand Movements

author.name: Pouyan Firouzabadi, J. D. Peiffer, Kunal Shah, Anton Sobinov, Lee E. Miller, R. James Cotton, Wendy M. Murray

entry.summary: Evaluates a biomechanics-aware markerless motion capture pipeline that uses gradient-based optimization with a biomechanical model in the loop to track dexterous unconstrained hand movements, validated against a custom 8-camera setup across 6 participants and 11 tasks. It advances markerless capture for the complex hand, which lags other musculoskeletal systems.

entry.title: Cancelable Biometric Template Protection Based on Multi-Instance Fusion: A Contralateral Iris Approach

author.name: Jittarin Chaivong, Nicha Vikromrotjananan, Teekatat Piriyapittaya, Waree Kongprawechnon, Suradej Duangpummet

entry.summary: Proposes a cancelable iris biometric template scheme that fuses left and right (contralateral) iris data without needing an external key or stored secret, aiming to meet ISO/IEC 24745 irreversibility, unlinkability, and confidentiality requirements. It uses majority-vote fusion and ID-derived bit permutation to protect against template theft.

entry.title: A Vision Based System for Guided and Collaborative Reconstruction of Fragmented Documents

author.name: Oliver Krumpek, Diana Leo

entry.summary: Presents a collaborative real-time system for reconstructing fragmented paper documents in cultural heritage preservation, using a cobot with a vacuum suction attachment for gentle, precise fragment positioning with sub-millimeter repeatability. Users can choose manual visually guided or fully automated positioning.

entry.title: Federated Learning for Object Detection: Enabling Collaborative Drone Learning Without Centralizing Data

author.name: Daniel M. Jimenez-Gutierrez, Enrique Zuazua, Georgios Kellaris, Joaquin del Rio, Oleksii Sliusarenko, Xabi Uribe-Etxebarria

entry.summary: Applies federated learning to drone-based object detection so models improve across distributed aerial deployments without centralizing privacy-sensitive imagery. It targets disaster response, infrastructure monitoring, and defense where transferring onboard visual data is impractical.

entry.title: LLMoxie: Exploring Agentic AI for Scientific Software Development

author.name: Landung Setiawan, Anant Mittal, Cordero Core, Anshul Tambay, Carlos Garcia Jurado Suarez, David A. C. Beck, Andrew J. Connolly, Vani Mandava

entry.summary: LLMoxie is an institutional AI platform with a three-tiered architecture for multi-cloud/on-prem inference, a control plane for auth, budgeting, PII masking, and observability, plus an RSE-Plugins ecosystem encoding research-software-engineering knowledge as a plugin-agent-skill hierarchy. It focuses on making agentic coding produce citable, reproducible scientific software.

entry.title: Echoes of Unrest: A Multimodal NLP Framework for Early Warning of Fake News and Violence-Driven Mob Activity

author.name: Md. Maruf Bangabashi, Tahmid Hasan, Golam Mahmud, Md. Mostafijur Rahman, Md. Toufiqur Rahman, Jahanur Biswas

entry.summary: Presents a multilingual, multimodal NLP framework for early detection of misinformation and violence-prone dynamics, using a fused dataset of over 138,000 Bangla and other items to flag fake news linked to mob activity. It responds to real-world harm from misinformation spreading faster than fact-checking in South Asia.

entry.title: A Precedent-Guided Co-Scientist for Side-Effect-Aware Drug Redesign

author.name: Yujin Kim, Charmgil Hong

entry.summary: PRECEDE is an LLM-orchestrated co-scientist that revises drug compounds to mitigate a specific side effect while preserving efficacy, grounding proposals in drug-side-effect associations, knowledge graphs, and safety-optimization precedents. It emphasizes auditable, human-supervised hypotheses.

entry.title: PixCon: Clean-Positive Contrastive Learning for Foundation-Model Semi-Supervised Segmentation

author.name: Ebenezer Tarubinga

entry.summary: PixCon is a clean-positive pixel-contrastive framework for semi-supervised segmentation that, using a DINOv2 teacher's already-clean pseudo-labels, maintains a per-class memory bank of only correctly classified labeled pixels for contamination-free positives. It argues remaining accuracy lies in embedding structure rather than confidence filtering.

entry.title: SNR-Adaptive Unified Diffusion for Multi-Task Medical Image Segmentation

author.name: Jiahao Liu, Hang Wei, Shuai Wu

entry.summary: UniT-Diff is a unified diffusion segmentation framework that consolidates semi-supervised learning, domain adaptation, and domain generalization for cardiac imaging into one model, resolving conflicting label semantics and gradient imbalance across tasks. It avoids maintaining separate per-dataset models.

entry.title: Conditional Diffusion Guided Knowledge Transfer for Multi-Domain Knowledge Graph Completion

author.name: Jiawei Sheng, Taoyu Su, Xixun Lin, Xiaodong Li, Tingwen Liu

entry.summary: DMKGC introduces a generation-based, conditional diffusion-guided knowledge transfer framework for multi-domain knowledge graph completion that treats each KG as a partial view and generates missing information rather than enforcing cross-KG entity consistency. It aims to preserve domain-specific context in low-resource settings.

entry.title: KARMA: Knowledge graph-based Automated Reasoning Materialization and Alignment

author.name: Jinkyeong Choi, Chaebin Jeong, Donghyeon Park

entry.summary: KARMA enumerates schema-constrained paths over domain knowledge graphs to generate slot-aligned contrastive candidates and uses a decoupled slot-level alignment objective to route preference supervision to discriminative entity slots. It targets the resolution-mismatch problem in template-based contrastive synthesis.

entry.title: CRRL: A Causality-Based Reinforcement Learning Framework for Autonomous System Recovery

author.name: Safia Fatima, Kai Olav Ellefsen, Leon Moonen

entry.summary: CRRL is a causal-guided RL framework that trains autonomous-driving recovery policies to coordinate with rule-based recovery, using causal relations from driving logs to shape learning and reduce time spent immobilized in failure states. It argues the problem is training policies to collaborate with interventions, not adding more recovery mechanisms.

entry.title: Builder, Defender, Breaker: The Case Against Removing the Human from the AI-Driven Security Lifecycle

author.name: Mohamed Chahine Ghanem

entry.summary: This position paper argues against fully removing humans from the AI-driven security lifecycle, warning that when the same model family builds, defends, and tests software, the three roles inherit shared blind spots and lose the independence needed for meaningful verification. It reframes full autonomy as collapsing the external oracle.

entry.title: Efficient Decentralized Multi-task Dataset Valuation via Model Merging

author.name: Mohammadsajad Alipour, Mohammad Mohammadi Amiri

entry.summary: DMVM is a decentralized, retraining-free framework for valuing datasets contributed for multi-task model training by leveraging model merging, avoiding data sharing and the compute cost of Shapley-based or retraining methods. It targets fair data marketplaces without a trusted central coordinator.

entry.title: LLM-Enhanced Hierarchical Heterogeneous Graph Representation Learning for Malicious Python Package Detection

author.name: Hang Gao, Xiaoyu Chen, Baoquan Cui, Zhen Tang, Peng Qiao, Fengge Wu, Jian Zhang

entry.summary: This paper proposes an LLM-enhanced hierarchical heterogeneous graph representation learning framework for detecting malicious Python packages in ecosystems like PyPI, combining structural program representations with LLM code understanding. It targets hierarchical organization and heterogeneous entity interactions that prior methods miss.

entry.title: DETECT-3B-Omni is Agnostic of Content and Demographics

author.name: Nicolas M. M\"uller, Aditya Tirumala Bukkapatnam, Dominik Schnieders, Zohaib Ahmed

entry.summary: This large-scale study tests whether Resemble AI's DETECT-3B-Omni deepfake audio detector bases decisions on acoustic artifacts rather than spoken content or speaker demographics, using 10,240 samples across US regions, genders, ages, and eight cloning systems with equivalence testing. It finds accuracy differences across groups at most two percentage points at 99% confidence.

entry.title: Latent Clarity: Bridging World-Model Kinematics to Semantic Manifolds for Video Anomaly Anticipation

author.name: Abu Anas Ibn Samad

entry.summary: PULS is a predictive world-model pipeline for video anomaly anticipation that maps V-JEPA 2 physical features into a text-aligned semantic hypersphere via a distillation bridge plus an anticipatory state predictor, achieving strong AUROC without multiple-instance learning. It reframes anomaly detection as continuous semantic forecasting.

entry.title: An Interpretable Deep Learning Framework for Discovery and Clinical Validation of Deep Radiomic Signatures in Tumor Classification

author.name: Chengkun Sun, Jinqian Pan, Renjie Liang, Zhengkang Fan, Xin Miao, Yi Guo, Mei Liu, Muxuan Liang, Russell Terry, Jie Xu

entry.summary: This framework integrates deep segmentation, explainable classification, and radiomic analysis to discover interpretable imaging signatures for tumor classification with clinical validation. It aims to overcome the interpretability barrier limiting clinical adoption of deep imaging biomarkers.

entry.title: A Step Towards Robust Unsupervised Domain Adaptation via Fine-Tuning and Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Sushant Dagaji Desale, Rahul Mishra, Ashutosh Kumar Sinha

entry.summary: SFT+RL is a two-stage robust unsupervised domain adaptation framework built on CLIP, using adversarial supervised fine-tuning of a linear classifier and reinforcement learning to improve the robustness-accuracy trade-off under adversarial target shifts. It tackles noisy pseudo-labels in adversarial UDA.

entry.title: Probing Low-Level Acoustic Attribute Encoding in CLAP Audio Embeddings

author.name: H\'ector Martel, Joe Hennessy-Priest, Taemin Cho

entry.summary: This probing study analyzes CLAP audio embeddings and finds that low-level perceptual attributes such as reverberation, loudness, spectral centroid, and relative pitch are reliably recoverable from frozen embeddings across five datasets. It clarifies what fundamental acoustic information audio foundation models encode.

entry.title: CGGS: Consistency-Augmented Geometric Gaussian Splatting for Ego-centric 3D Scene Generation

author.name: Zhenyu Sun, Xiaohan Zhang, Qi Liu, Huan Wang

entry.summary: CGGS is a text-to-3D framework for ego-centric scene generation that fine-tunes a multi-view latent diffusion model with a consistency-augmented loss and uses optical flow and point-based layout decoration to reduce geometric distortion. It targets viewpoint-consistent, semantically aligned generation under limited view overlap.

entry.title: Q-TriM: Question-Guided Tri-Modal Attention for Audio-Visual Question Answering

author.name: SungHun Kim, SeungJun Baek

entry.summary: Q-TriM performs shallow, parallel question-conditioned tri-modal fusion for audio-visual question answering, avoiding the information loss caused by deeply stacked sequential cross-attention layers. It conditions video and audio attention on the text query in a single fusion stage.

entry.title: NouveauVoice: Generating Novel Pseudo Speakers for Voice Anonymization

author.name: Meiying Melissa Chen, Anastasia Kuznetsova, Zhenyu Wang, Zhiyao Duan

entry.summary: NouveauVoice generates novel pseudo-speakers for voice anonymization using a hierarchical deep variational autoencoder as a plug-in module on top of FACodec and CosyVoice2, leveraging ELBO-based tractable sampling for greater identity variance. It targets stronger privacy protection with expressive synthesized voices.

entry.title: Predicting Drafted Deck Strength for "Magic: the Gathering"

author.name: Tomas Rigaux, Hisashi Kashima

entry.summary: This paper studies predicting drafted deck strength in Magic: the Gathering, where cards themselves define and change rules, using Draft as a tractable setting to study sequential card selection under partial information. It provides a benchmark for learning in open-ended, rule-mutating game environments.

entry.title: GRAFT: Grafted Reference Audio for Fine-grained Pronunciation in Zero-shot Text-to-Speech

author.name: Antonis Asonitis, Francesco Verdini, Aref Farhadipour, Vijeta Avijeet, Pierre-Edouard Honnet, Marzieh Razavi, Juan Pablo Zuluaga Gomez

entry.summary: GRAFT adds per-word pronunciation control to zero-shot neural-codec TTS by conditioning on a short spoken sample of the target word, encoded with the model's own tokenizer and voice-converted so the hint speaker differs from the output voice. It targets the common failure of mispronouncing rare proper nouns, loanwords, and technical terms.

entry.title: CoFEND: A Cross-Modal Fusion End-to-End Network for Cold-Start Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction

author.name: Di Wu, Hongyi Sun, Haichao Xu, Jia Chen, Zhong Chen, Jie Yang

entry.summary: CoFEND is an end-to-end cross-modal fusion network for cold-start drug-drug interaction prediction, jointly modeling relationships among drugs, enzymes, transporters, and molecular structures to compute new-vs-known drug similarity online. It targets safer prediction of adverse interactions for novel drugs.

entry.title: Sample-Efficient Pareto Front Modeling for Energy-Aware Reinforcement Learning Using Bayesian Optimization

author.name: Georg Sch\"afer, Jakob Rehrl, Stefan Huber, Simon Hirlaender

entry.summary: This paper automates the reward-weighting problem in energy-aware RL by using Bayesian optimization to efficiently model the Pareto front of trade-off policies, avoiding manual, biased scalarization of competing objectives. It targets industrial automation balancing performance and energy efficiency.

entry.title: Graph Classification via Network Usable Information: From Representation Evaluation to Structure Selection

author.name: Abdullah Shaik, Anwar Said

entry.summary: NetinfoGC extends the Network Usable Information paradigm to graph classification, constructing permutation-invariant representations from propagation and structural descriptors and using a training-free clustering-consistency estimate of representation quality. It selects informative structures with sparse-group LASSO rather than end-to-end black-box training.

entry.title: PIEFS: Physics-Informed Eigenfunction Features with Learnable Scaling

author.name: Varvara Nazarenkko, Timur Lidzhiev, Alexander Tarakanov

entry.summary: PIEFS is a supervised spectral representation-learning framework with a physics-informed inductive bias based on a modified Dirichlet energy, learning coordinate maps under Gram orthogonality with a learnable anisotropic metric. It aims to replace fixed kernels or manually chosen feature scalings in spectral methods.

entry.title: Stable Global Weighting of Flow Mixtures using Simplex Exponential Moving Average

author.name: Benjamin Wiriyapong, Oktay Karakus, Can Eyupoglu, Kirill Sidorov

entry.summary: AMF-VI-sEMA is a two-stage mixture-of-normalizing-flows framework where heterogeneous experts (RealNVP, MAF, RBIG) specialize in distinct structural regimes and are combined via a stable global-weighting mechanism using a Simplex Exponential Moving Average. It aims to generalize variational inference across heterogeneous posterior geometries.

entry.title: Binary Iterative Method for Non-targeted Adversarial Attack

author.name: Naman Goyal, Milan Chaudhari

entry.summary: This paper introduces a binary iterative optimization method for non-targeted adversarial attacks aimed at improving robustness testing and generating training data. It positions adversarial robustness as a broadly applicable differentiable optimization metric. It matters marginally as an incremental attack variant.

entry.title: F-ACVAE: A Federated Adaptive Conditional Variational Auto-Encoder for Privacy-Preserving Intrusion Detection in IoT Networks

author.name: Mohammad Ansarimehr, Somayeh Changiz, Ehsan Baghishani, Ali Mousavi

entry.summary: F-ACVAE is a federated adaptive conditional variational autoencoder for privacy-preserving IoT intrusion detection that uses selective parameter aggregation to handle high-dimensional traffic, class imbalance, and non-IID data across edge devices. It matters for distributed security but combines established techniques.

entry.title: SMART: A Machine Learning and Monte Carlo Framework for Rapid Analysis of Stochastic Transistor Aging and Process Variation in Digital Circuits

author.name: Arash Esshaghi, Siavash Es'haghi, Gholamreza Shahabadi, Alireza Moradi

entry.summary: SMART integrates Random Forest regression with Monte Carlo simulation to rapidly predict gate delay distributions under combined bias temperature instability and process variation, bypassing costly atomic-model parameter extraction for large digital circuits. It matters for scalable reliability analysis in deep-nanometer CMOS design.

entry.title: Schedulable Job-Level Dependencies for Cause-Effect Chains via Graph Neural Networks

author.name: Silviu S. Craciunas, Christian Hakert, Jian-Jia Chen, Zden\v{e}k Hanz\'alek, Paul Pop

entry.summary: This work proposes the first ML-based synthesis of job-level dependencies for automotive cause-effect chains using GNNs, checking whether produced dependencies are enforceable and jointly schedulable. It targets real-time timing guarantees on multi-core platforms.

entry.title: Quantum Kolmogorov--Arnold representation theorem for continuous unitary-valued maps

author.name: Sviatoslav V. Dzhenzher

entry.summary: This paper proves two quantum analogues of the Kolmogorov-Arnold representation theorem for continuous unitary-valued maps in a neighborhood of the identity, providing exact additive decompositions. It underpins quantum Kolmogorov-Arnold networks (QKANs).

entry.title: Deriving Benchmarking Datasets from Long-Form Recordings: Challenges and Opportunities

author.name: Kaveri K. Sheth, Lawrence Borst, Tarek Kunze, Marvin Lavechin, Okko R\"as\"anen, Sho Tsuji, Loann Peurey, Alix Bourr\'ee, Alejandrina Cristia

entry.summary: The paper presents a framework for deriving standardized benchmarks from child-centered long-form audio recordings, including a collection of 27 datasets, a replicable speech-processing benchmark pipeline, and a privacy-respecting role-based ecosystem. It addresses cross-corpus heterogeneity and consent constraints in early language development research.

entry.title: Missing Data Imputation under Manifold Hypothesis

author.name: Zelong Bi, Amuchechukwu Ibenegbu, Sarat Moka

entry.summary: The paper proposes model-based missing-data imputation under the manifold hypothesis using mixture VAEs and a sampling-importance-resampling procedure, optionally augmented with a latent-space diffusion model. It samples from the conditional distribution of missing given observed while respecting learned geometry.

entry.title: SABLE: An NDA-Safe Closed-Loop LLM Framework for Analog Circuit Optimization in Industrial EDA Flows

author.name: Xunqi Li, Chris H. Kim

entry.summary: SABLE is an NDA-safe closed-loop framework letting LLMs optimize analog circuits through Cadence tools while returning only scrubbed, numeric, scoped outputs under a stated threat model. It combines an explicit threat model with a whitelist of scoped operations for industrial EDA.

entry.title: A Policy Decomposition Framework for Dynamic Order Fulfillment Operations

author.name: Gal Neria, Michal Tzur, Marlin W. Ulmer

entry.summary: The paper introduces the Dynamic Order Fulfillment Problem unifying order preparation and delivery as a single Markov decision process with partitioned state/decision spaces, and proposes a policy-decomposition framework. It bridges logistics stages traditionally managed in isolation.

entry.title: Boundary-layer asymptotics for Gaussian-smoothed singular measures

author.name: Nicolas Brosse, Arnak S. Dalalyan

entry.summary: This mathematical paper studies small-noise asymptotics of Gaussian-smoothed probability measures supported on manifolds with corners, proving a two-term expansion for heat-regularized densities near boundary strata. It contributes fine geometric analysis relevant to diffusion regularization of singular distributions.

entry.title: Lacuna Inc. at SemEval-2026 Task 4: Structurally Gated State-Space Models for Disentangling Narrative Similarity

author.name: Aleksey Kudelya, Rafif Alshawi, Alexander Shirnin

entry.summary: IVD-SSM is a SemEval-2026 submission for narrative story similarity that uses a hybrid state-space model (Jamba-1.5-Mini) with a novel Structurally Gated Alignment head to disentangle invariant causal/plot structure from variant surface details. It aims to compare abstract narrative patterns while looking past specific names, actors, and settings.

entry.title: Consistent and Editable: A Balanced Framework for Text-Guided Video Editing

author.name: Tao Jin, Li Xiao

entry.summary: EquiEdit is a text-guided video editing framework that uses a temporal Mamba module with directional scanning to balance the usual trade-off between temporal consistency and editability in diffusion-based video edits. It matters as an incremental contribution to controllable video editing, though it follows a well-trodden line of diffusion editing work.

entry.title: Invisible Strings: Deriving Puppetry Principles and their Hidden Connections to Robot Behavior Design

author.name: Claire Lewis, Sawyer Collins, Alyssa Hanson, Johanna Smith, Tom Williams

entry.summary: This paper derives design principles from puppetry and connects them to robot nonverbal behavior design, arguing that puppetry offers richer guidance than Disney animation principles because it addresses physically embodied, interactive characters. It matters as a fresh arts-based lens for human-robot interaction design.

entry.title: Designing Touch for Trauma-Informed Social Robots: A Design Space for Direct and Indirect Actuation

author.name: Madeleine Rischer, Benedikt Bu{\ss}mann

entry.summary: Investigates how touch actuation in social robots can align with trauma-informed care for individuals with PTSD, distinguishing direct physical touch from indirect touch mediated through wearables or smart textiles and proposing a design space. It applies safety, transparency, autonomy, and trigger-avoidance principles to touch design.

entry.title: Robust Feasible Route Construction through Collaborative Partition Optimization

author.name: Oguzhan Karaahmetoglu, Hyong Kim

entry.summary: CoRC is a routing framework for large-scale capacitated vehicle routing that lets independently solved partitioned subproblems exchange customers and vehicles during optimization rather than relying on a fixed partition or a later global re-optimization. It scales to instances with up to 200,000 customers.

entry.title: Bootstrap Flow-Map Tree Sampling Enables Online Feedback Driven Search

author.name: Binglin Ji, Anindya Sarkar, Hengchang Lu, Jens Sj\"olund, Yevgeniy Vorobeychik

entry.summary: BFMT is a sampling framework that builds tree paths over a generative flow map to enable history-aware global exploration under a limited sampling budget with sequential feedback. It aims at scientific discovery settings where preferences are unknown a priori.

entry.title: STELLA: Efficient Sensor-to-LLM Translation for On-Device Human Activity Recognition

author.name: Nirhoshan Sivaroopan, Albert Zomaya, Kanchana Thilakarathna

entry.summary: STELLA shifts on-device human activity recognition burden from LLM adaptation to sensor tokenization: a lightweight hierarchical tokenizer compresses multi-channel inertial windows into compact latent tokens fed to a frozen LLM with a text prompt. It aims for privacy-preserving, low-latency edge deployment.

entry.title: Text as Partial Constraint: Core-Residual Alignment for Robust Vision-Language Learning

author.name: Chengzhen Yu, Canran Xiao, Siyuan Ma, Yang Liu

entry.summary: TPC treats underspecified captions as partial constraints, distilling a consensus semantic core as the alignment target and discouraging vision-language similarity from depending on omitted residual details, to make matching stable across paraphrases. It aims to improve robustness and calibrated confidence in vision-language alignment.

entry.title: Phase-Preserving Trimodal Transformer for Tropical Forest Biomass Estimation Using Optical and PolInSAR Data

author.name: Luiz Felipe Parente Santiago (Institute of Computing, Brazilian Army Research Institute in the Amazon), Rosiane Rodrigues de Freitas (Institute of Computing), Daniel Rodrigues dos Santos (Military Institute of Engineering), Felipe Ferrari (Military Institute of Engineering)

entry.summary: The Trimodal Coherent Co-attention Transformer fuses optical reflectance with complex-valued P- and L-band PolInSAR data using phase-preserving complex encoders and dynamic co-attention for tropical forest above-ground biomass estimation. It targets SAR saturation and cloud cover limitations in dense forests.

entry.title: Labeled-Data-Free Meta-Learning: Efficient Task Generation Using Pre-trained Models and Unlabeled Data

author.name: Lei Sun, Yusuke Tanaka, Tomoharu Iwata

entry.summary: This work proposes a data-free meta-learning setting that avoids expensive model inversion by combining pre-trained models with unlabeled data, generating meta-training tasks via soft labels. It targets scenarios where labeled datasets are costly or privacy-restricted.

entry.title: MABLE: Masked Autoencoding with Bi-Lipschitz Decoding for Embeddings and Graph Metric Learning

author.name: Yaniv Shulman, Shaghayegh Akbarpour, Jack B. Muir

entry.summary: MABLE is a self-supervised framework for node and graph embeddings on large heterogeneous graphs, combining masked reconstruction with a bi-Lipschitz feature decoder and Lipschitz-controlled pooling for stable graph-level representations. It is demonstrated on geospatial mineral-exploration data.

entry.title: Stacked LoRA for Subject-Adaptive EEG Foundation Models in Motor Imagery Decoding

author.name: Aymen Sarhane, Fouad Lbakali, Mouad Souissi, Jonathan Lys, Giulia Lioi

entry.summary: This work adapts EEG foundation models (REVE, LaBraM, LUNA) for motor-imagery decoding using a stacked LoRA approach to handle inter-subject variability without full recalibration. It targets subject-adaptive brain-computer interface decoding.

entry.title: PhenoNEST: A Neuro-Symbolic Framework for Ontology-Aware Multimodal Plant Phenotyping and Trait Discovery

author.name: Jayant Ghadge, Soumyashree Kar, Surya S. Durbha

entry.summary: PhenoNEST is a neuro-symbolic framework that constructs a multimodal knowledge graph from noisy field notes and RGB images for plant phenotyping, aligning graph nodes with standardized ontologies via PlantDeBERTa. It aims to bridge the semantic gap between unstructured phenotyping data and structured trait discovery.

entry.title: Modular Foundation Models for Time-Series Perception in Digital Twins

author.name: Quang Hung Pham, Ryad Zemouri, Martin Gagnon, Luc Vouligny

entry.summary: This paper proposes a modular time-series foundation model for digital twins and prognostics, built from a collection of self-supervised pretrained representation encoders that provide transferable, task-agnostic features across operating conditions. It targets robust perception in industrial health-monitoring pipelines.

entry.title: Conservative Subject Invariant EMG-based Gesture Recognition

author.name: Hamed Rafiei, Ali Mousavi

entry.summary: This paper proposes a conservative multi-objective learning framework for subject-invariant sEMG gesture recognition, combining gesture classification, adversarial subject confusion via gradient reversal, and triplet metric learning with Lipschitz-inspired adaptive weighting. It addresses cross-subject generalization without subject-specific data.

entry.title: MLSYSIM: First-Principles Infrastructure Modeling for Machine Learning Systems

author.name: Vijay Janapa Reddi

entry.summary: A Transformer-based fusion framework integrates ultrasonic guided-wave and fiber-Bragg-grating strain data operating at different sampling rates for multitask aerospace structural health monitoring. It addresses heterogeneous multisensor fusion of composite materials.

entry.title: Property-Constrained 3D Porous Media Reconstruction from 2D Images via Conditional Generative Adversarial Networks

author.name: Ali Sadeghkhani, Brandon Bennett, Arash Rabbani

entry.summary: A conditional GAN generates 3D porous media volumes with controlled porosity while trained only on 2D thin-section images, using a 3D generator and 2D discriminator with multi-axis slicing. It removes the need for expensive 3D training data in petrophysical modeling.

entry.title: From Interaction to Intent: Inferring User Objectives from Provenance Logs

author.name: Steffen Holter, Tobias St\"ahle, Arpit Narechania, Mennatallah El-Assady

entry.summary: This study investigates whether provenance logs of user interactions during exploratory data analysis can be used to automatically infer analytic intent, finding distinct behavioral signatures across task types. It points toward proactive AI assistants for visual data exploration.

entry.title: LuxSQA: Ask Me in Luxembourgish with TTS-Augmented Spoken Question Answering

author.name: Nina Hosseini-Kivanani, Marco Matassoni, Alessio Brutti

entry.summary: LuxSQA investigates whether text-to-speech can supply task-specific training data for Luxembourgish spoken question answering without a large human-recorded corpus, using a SLAM-style architecture connecting a frozen Whisper encoder to multilingual LLMs via a projector and LoRA. It compares multiple TTS systems for synthesizing spoken questions.

entry.title: Towards Digital Preservation of Efik: TTS for a Low-Resource African Language

author.name: Offiong Bassey Edet, Emmanuel Oyo-Ita, Archibong Okon Archibong, David Effanga Bassey, Mbuotidem Sunday Awak

entry.summary: Presents the first documented end-to-end TTS study for Efik, a low-resource tonal Nigerian language, building a three-hour single-speaker corpus and comparing four neural TTS models under low-resource conditions. MMS-TTS scored highest but tonal errors persisted.

entry.title: From Mobile Data to Business Insights: An End-to-End Analytics Framework for Large-Scale Urban Mobility Analysis and Decision Support

author.name: Thiago Andrade, Shazia Tabassum, Miguel E. P. Silva, Ricardo Dinis, Joao Gama

entry.summary: This study builds an end-to-end analytics platform using mobile location data to address urban challenges such as tourism planning, parking, bus route optimization, and retail behavioral profiling. It spans data platform development through decision support.

entry.title: Agentic IoT: Architectures, Applications, and Challenges Toward the Internet of Agents

author.name: R\"umeysa Hilal Sevin\c{c}, Bahaeddin T\"urko\u{g}lu, \.Ibrahim K\"ok

entry.summary: This survey examines Agentic IoT as a next-generation cognitive paradigm that integrates autonomous agents' perception, reasoning, planning, learning, and action with cyber-physical systems, moving beyond task-specific inference models toward system-wide autonomy. It reviews architectures, applications, and challenges toward an Internet of Agents.

entry.title: SWIFT: Spatio-temporal Wavelet Integrated Forecasting Framework for Workload Traces

author.name: Zeyuan Ding, Lingfeng Zheng, Dian Ding, Guangtao Xue

entry.summary: SWIFT is a convolutional cloud-workload forecasting framework using learnable cascaded wavelet paths and a multivariate interaction module to capture volatile, bursty patterns and spatial dependencies. It reframes fixed wavelet bases as adaptive convolutional operators.

entry.title: Attention Dynamics in Diffusion Models: A Visual Analytics Framework for Human-AI Collaboration

author.name: Yiran Xiao, George Legrady

entry.summary: This visual-analytics framework explores attention dynamics in diffusion text-to-image models, tracking step-indexed evolution of token-level cross-attention maps and their spatial-temporal concentration through an interactive workflow. It aims to make semantic-structure emergence interpretable for human-AI collaboration.

entry.title: DOSE-I: A Multimodal Biosignal Dataset of Procedural Sedation for Endoscopy -- Technical Report

author.name: Jakob Garbe, Jan W. Kantelhardt, Katja Seeliger, Thomas Schmid

entry.summary: DOSE-I is a multimodal biosignal dataset of procedural sedation for endoscopy, containing 78.5 hours across 171 records with over 1,100 consciousness transitions and 7,300 sedation-depth labels, plus preprocessed EEG features and metadata. It is a technical report describing a Zenodo-published resource.

entry.title: An automated method of identifying incorrectly labelled images based on the sequences of loss functions of deep learning networks

author.name: Zhipeng Zhang, Wenhui Shou, Wengting Ma, Dongjia Xing, Qingqing Xu, Li-Qun Xu, Qingxia Fan, Ling Xu

entry.summary: Proposes an automated method to detect mislabeled medical images by analyzing per-sample loss-function sequences across training epochs, flagging suspect labels for expert review. Experiments on a diabetic-retinopathy fundus dataset identified 75% of intentionally flipped labels with modest false positives.

entry.title: The agent creates, we validate: A Lightweight Framework for Agentic Artifact Generation

author.name: Yaniv Melamed, Yoni Zukerman, Michal Shechter, Miri Weissler, Ashwin Patil, Hani Neuvirth-Telem

entry.summary: Presents a lightweight framework for reliable agentic generation of structured artifacts (queries, schemas, threat mappings) built on a generate-then-validate principle using test-driven generation with informative error feedback plus deterministic and LLM-based tests. It shifts responsibility from generation quality to validation rigor.

entry.title: SMOCS: A Streaming Framework for Simplified Deployment, Monitoring, and Optimization of ML Systems in Production

author.name: Armen Kasparian, Kishansingh Rajput, Malachi Schram, John Vennekate

entry.summary: SMOCS is a Kafka-based containerized framework simplifying deployment, monitoring, and optimization of ML systems in scientific facilities, offering a layered abstraction over Kafka and a three-thread agent architecture that decouples data ingestion from inference. It targets the recurring engineering cost of rebuilding integration pipelines per facility.

entry.title: PosterHarness: Turning Scientific Poster Generation into an Auditable Instruction-Following Benchmark

author.name: Tianyi Yang, Dawei Fu, Youpeng Wu, Zixun Kou, Linrui Chen, Ruobing Jiang, Zijian Wang, Qiang Li

entry.summary: PosterHarness reframes scientific poster generation as auditable instruction-following, enforcing a placeholder-first contract that separates visual-summary design from data-bearing figures to prevent fabricated charts. It contributes a pilot benchmark and failure taxonomy.

entry.title: Flow-A11y: Flow-Aware Accessibility Testing

author.name: Nasr Eddine Fliti, Leisan Kokorina, Florian Tambon, Michael Papadakis

entry.summary: Flow-A11y is a flow-aware accessibility testing system that executes natural-language interaction scenarios in a real browser to detect dynamic WCAG violations (keyboard traps, focus loss, modal leakage) that static page audits miss. It automates checks usually done by manual inspection.

entry.title: CuBAS: Information Geometric Curvature-Based Adaptive Sampling for Supervised Classification

author.name: Alexandre L. M. Levada

entry.summary: CuBAS is an information-geometric adaptive sampling framework for supervised classification that scores local curvature via the ratio of second- to first-order Fisher information on a kNN graph, using a Potts MRF view of the data manifold. It selects informative training points based on distribution geometry.

entry.title: Agentic and Generative AI for Open-Source Intelligence and Cyber Investigations: Taxonomy, Evaluation, Challenges, and Future Directions

author.name: Eduardo Almeida Palmieri, Mohamed Chahine Ghanem, Dipo Dunsin, Zubair Baig, Ed de Quincey, Kim-Kwang Raymond Choo

entry.summary: This survey reviews 74 studies on agentic and generative AI for open-source intelligence and cyber investigations, establishing agentic AI as a distinct analytical category and organizing the literature via an 11-category taxonomy. It also covers evaluation gaps and future directions.

entry.title: SkillFab: An Agent-Native Skill Production Platform

author.name: Anjie Xu, Yifeng Cai, Yi Li, Zixing Wang, Zhiyu Zhang, Jingfan Chen, Ruohan Xu, Leye Wang

entry.summary: SkillFab is an agent-native platform that turns missing agent capabilities into reviewed, reusable skills through a demand-first lifecycle spanning search, repository development, commit-evidence ingestion, maintainer review, and registry publication, exposed via web, REST, and MCP. It gives humans, scripts, and external agents shared state over skill production.

entry.title: Next-Gen Sponsored Search: Crafting the Perfect Query with Inventory-Aware RAG (InvAwr-RAG) Based GenAI

author.name: Md Omar Faruk Rokon, Weizhi Du, Zhaodong Wang, Musen Wen

entry.summary: InvAwr-RAG is an inventory-aware retrieval-augmented generative model for sponsored search that integrates semantic retrieval with real-time inventory data to craft keyword queries for queries that otherwise return no ads. It targets ad revenue and engagement from unmatched search traffic.

entry.title: Finite Reliability Representations: Noise-Calibrated Belief-Space Covers for Reliable Decision-Making

author.name: Hyung-Jin Yoon, Hunmin Kim

entry.summary: This study benchmarks tabular foundation models against conventional ML for crowd-state classification at Hajj and Umrah in the very-few-label regime, characterizing accuracy and efficiency trade-offs across three real datasets. It probes when small-data foundation-model priors actually help versus tuned standard methods.

entry.title: Efficient Discovery of Conditional Dependencies with Desbordante

author.name: Ivan Kozhukov, Dmitry Fedoseev, Maksim Emelyanov, Artem Smola, Pyotr Senichenkov, Pavel Anosov, George Chernishev

entry.summary: This paper improves conditional functional dependency discovery by building on CFDFinder with algorithmic and parallelization improvements, yielding ParCFDFinder integrated into the open-source Desbordante data profiler. It accelerates a computationally demanding data-quality task with a Python-accessible C++ implementation.

entry.title: Submitted and Diagnostic Analysis of Full-Text Temporal Retrieval for LongEval-Sci

author.name: Yingdong Yang, Haijian Wu

entry.summary: This paper reports submissions and diagnostics for LongEval-Sci 2026 scientific retrieval under evolving corpora, comparing temporal full-text and citation-aware BM25 variants against dense baselines. Temporalized full-text runs achieve the strongest results across time snapshots.

entry.title: Poisson-Gamma Modeling of Inter-Relational Dependencies in Dynamic Knowledge Graphs

author.name: Nan Fang, Yijun Wang, Hao Liao, Sikun Yang

entry.summary: PGRE is a Poisson-Gamma probabilistic model for dynamic knowledge graphs that captures inter-relational and temporal dependencies via a Poisson-Bernoulli link formulation with Gamma latent variables for entity-factor and cross-relation associations. It targets noisy, incomplete, evolving graphs.

entry.title: Integrating Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Safe Reinforcement Learning in a 1-DoF Helicopter System

author.name: Georg Sch\"afer, Jakob Rehrl, Stefan Huber

entry.summary: This work-in-progress embeds a differentiable physics model into the PPO actor loss to penalize anticipated safety violations over short-horizon simulated trajectories, tested on a 1-DoF helicopter with strict pitch limits. It reduces constraint violations without relying solely on complex reward shaping.

entry.title: Anticipatory Reinforcement Learning for Trajectory Tracking

author.name: Georg Sch\"afer, Jakob Rehrl, Stefan Huber, Simon Hirlaender

entry.summary: This paper introduces an anticipatory RL formulation for trajectory tracking that augments the state with target velocities and future reference horizons, achieving a ninefold error reduction in simulation on a 1-DoF helicopter. It also documents a sim-to-real gap where simpler configurations transferred better to hardware.

entry.title: LLM-Guided Transportation Hub Capacity Planning with Textual Business Inputs

author.name: Xiaoyue Liu, Zheng Dong

entry.summary: This paper proposes an LLM-guided framework for transportation hub capacity planning where an LLM agent iteratively proposes capacity decisions from natural-language business context using a chain-of-thought decision table, validated through a feedback loop with an optimization model. It aims to inject qualitative business context into traditionally quantitative planning.

entry.title: Environmental Drivers of Respiratory Disease: A District Level Analysis

author.name: Rahim Iqbal, Asfi Ahamed, Izzath Nisfer, Shazan Shaheed, Muhammadu Ilham, Nathali Athukorala, Madara Mendis, Nisansa de Silva, Sandareka Wickramanayake

entry.summary: This study builds an 11-year district-level panel for Sri Lanka integrating satellite vegetation, fire, pollutant, and carbon-flux data to model respiratory admission rates and PM2.5 with XGBoost, addressing confounding from healthcare access. It matters for environmental epidemiology but is largely an applied regression study.

entry.title: RL-Ballast: Ship Ballast Water Path Planning and Clog Prediction via Reinforcement Learning

author.name: Ming-Kuan Lin, Yi-Chung Lai, Ming-Hsin Chiang, Tsung-Wei Pan, Jung-Hua Wang

entry.summary: RL-Ballast is a graph-based deep RL framework for adaptive ship ballast-water path planning and sensor-frugal blockage prediction, transforming valve permutations into feasible fluid-transfer routes and adapting to hydraulic anomalies like valve failures. It matters for autonomous vessel safety but is a highly specialized application.

entry.title: Privacy-Preserving Industrial Ergonomics: mmWave-Based Automated REBA Scoring and Pose Estimation

author.name: Xuhan Zhang, Zhuangzhuang Dai, Luis J. Mans, Victor Chang

entry.summary: The paper presents an end-to-end multi-task framework that reconstructs 3D skeletons from mmWave radar and regresses REBA ergonomic risk scores, enabling privacy-preserving industrial monitoring. Biomechanical and temporal-smoothness constraints handle sparse radar point clouds.

entry.title: Seeing Through WiFi: Lightweight Human Pose Estimation with Dynamic Kernel Attention

author.name: Toan D. Gian, Van-Dinh Nguyen, Vo Phi Son, Nhan Thanh Nguyen, Dinh Thai Hoang, Diep N. Nguyen, Nguyen Cong Luong, Symeon Chatzinotas

entry.summary: WiLHPE performs lightweight WiFi-CSI human pose estimation, trained with a camera teacher but inferring poses from raw WiFi signals alone, using dynamically learned convolutional kernels and channel-frequency attention. It preserves privacy on resource-constrained devices.

entry.title: LRX-PINN: A Layer-Resolving XNet Physics-Informed Neural Network with Integrated Cauchy Activations for Convection-Dominated Problems

author.name: Zihao Guo, Xin Li, Zhihong Xia

entry.summary: LRX-PINN introduces integrated Cauchy activations whose value profile is transition-type while its derivative recovers a localized Cauchy kernel, matching the scaling of thin layers in convection-dominated convection-diffusion problems. It targets the structural mismatch of standard PINNs for sharp layers.

entry.title: Smooth $\%$MinMax: A Differentiable Relaxation for Codon Harmonization

author.name: Yoonho Jeong, Hyunwoo Choi, Ryan Fernandez Medina Hariri, Eok Kyun Lee, Seung Seo Lee, Insung S. Choi

entry.summary: Smooth %MinMax is a differentiable relaxation of the discrete %MinMax codon-harmonization metric, replacing hard codon-usage values with probability-weighted synonymous-codon usage to enable gradient-based neural codon design. It preserves native-like rare/frequent codon patterns for heterologous expression.

entry.title: Cross-Modal Fusion of OCT and OCT angiography enface for Improved Diagnostics of Diabetic Retinopathy

author.name: Rashadul Hasan Badhon, Atalie Carina Thompson, Jennifer I. Lim, Theodore Leng, Minhaj Nur Alam

entry.summary: The paper fuses OCT B-scans with single-channel en face OCTA using a bidirectional cross-modal attention network for automated diabetic retinopathy classification, evaluated across within-, combined-, and cross-dataset settings. It leverages complementary structural and vascular information.

entry.title: Governing Generative AI Across Financial Institutions: An SR 26-2-Compatible Framework for Generative AI Risk Control

author.name: Yiqing Wang, Yixin Kang, Luyun Lin, Siqi Mao

entry.summary: The paper proposes a governance framework for generative and agentic AI in financial institutions that is compatible with the new SR 26-2 supervisory guidance, which itself excludes generative AI. It targets how generative outputs materially affect regulated decision control environments.

entry.title: Unsupervised Detection of Underground Tunnels in Ground-Penetrating Radar Using Depth-Restricted Reconstruction Scoring

author.name: Muhammad Junaid, Shoab A. Khan, Nisar Ahmed

entry.summary: This paper presents a fully unsupervised pipeline for detecting clandestine underground tunnels in ground-penetrating radar using a denoising autoencoder trained only on normal subsurface data, flagging tunnels via reconstruction error with depth restriction. It targets pipeline-security monitoring where labeled tunnel examples are scarce.

entry.title: msPCA: An R Package for Sparse PCA with Multiple Components

author.name: Ryan Cory-Wright, Jean Pauphilet

entry.summary: msPCA is an open-source R package implementing an alternating maximization algorithm for sparse principal component analysis with multiple components, supporting orthogonality or zero-correlation non-redundancy definitions. It scales to thousands of features with competitive runtimes and controlled variance explained.

entry.title: Candidate-Constrained Retrieval-Augmented Generation for LongEval-RAG: System Design and Empirical Analysis

author.name: Yingdong Yang, Haijian Wu

entry.summary: This paper presents a candidate-constrained RAG system for LongEval-RAG where retrieval and citations must stay within an organizer-provided candidate set, combining deterministic provenance tracking with query expansion, pseudo-relevance feedback, reciprocal rank fusion, reranking, and citation-aware aggregation. It evaluates ten pipeline variants and finds a rule-based-plus-MiniLM variant strongest.

entry.title: WPG-MoE: Weak-Prior-Guided Dense Mixture-of-Experts for User-Level Social Media Depression Detection

author.name: Xian Li, Yuanhe Tian, Yang Yang, Guoqing Wang, Yan Song

entry.summary: Proposes WPG-MoE, a weak-prior-guided dense mixture-of-experts on a shared LLM backbone for user-level social media depression screening, aiming to handle heterogeneous expressions of risk that a single classifier averages away. It matters for scalable mental-health screening, especially for non-self-disclosing users.

entry.title: Who's Behind It? Annotating and Extracting Conspiratorial Actors from German Telegram Posts

author.name: Helena Mihaljevi\'c, Jolanda Beer, Mareike Lisker, Katharina Soemer

entry.summary: Develops annotation guidelines and a span-annotated German Telegram corpus for identifying conspiratorial actors, then trains transformer models to extract them and applies the model to a large conspiracy-channel archive. It shifts conspiracy-theory analysis from document-level to actor-level extraction.

entry.title: Progressive Refinement: An Iterative Pseudo-Labeling Approach for Mandarin-English Code-Switching ASR

author.name: Qu Yang, Cakra Wardhana, Tim Ng

entry.summary: Applies iterative pseudo-labeling for the first time to Mandarin-English code-switching ASR, using a three-phase pipeline of pseudo-label generation, two-stage bilingual training, and iterative refinement to exploit unlabeled data. It improves CS-ASR under limited labeled data.

entry.title: Reliability-Aware Monocular Depth Supervision for Sparse-View Neural Reconstruction

author.name: Wei-Teng Chu, Yashasvini Gopalan, Changju Yuan

entry.summary: Studies monocular depth supervision for sparse-view neural reconstruction in outdoor driving scenes, aligning Depth Anything V2 predictions to metric depth and applying depth supervision selectively via photometric masks. It evaluates the strategy on Mip-NeRF-360 and a Splatfacto-style representation.

entry.title: CV-DCLR: Causal-Visual Dynamic Label Refinement for Robust Zero-Shot Learning

author.name: Can Wang, Jiangnan Li, Mingyu Li, Yining Song, Kangrui Ren, Min Gan, Jinfu Fan

entry.summary: CV-DCLR is a causal-visual dynamic label refinement framework for zero-shot learning that tackles semantic entanglement, where visual representations conflate visually similar concepts, by moving beyond superficial global visual-semantic correlation maximization. It aims to capture distinctive class identities rather than spurious similarities.

entry.title: Explainable AI for Screening Abuse-Related Trauma in Bangladeshi Children: A Training-Free Multimodal Framework Evaluated on Noise-Aware Synthetic Data

author.name: Salma Hoque Talukdar Koli, Fahima Haque Talukder Jely

entry.summary: ShishuRaksha AI is a training-free, clinically weighted multimodal decision-support framework fusing validated questionnaires, Bengali narrative text, House-Tree-Person drawing features, and facial affect to screen abuse-related trauma in children, evaluated on noise-aware synthetic data with explainable attributions. It targets a severe shortage of child mental-health professionals in Bangladesh.

entry.title: Pooling-Based Context Modeling for Convolution-Free Deep Image Prior

author.name: Gihyun Kim, Jong-Seok Lee

entry.summary: Pool-DIP replaces convolutions in Deep Image Prior with pooling-based context modeling, achieving competitive single-image denoising while cutting parameters and compute and reducing noise overfitting. It rethinks what primitive is needed for spatial context in DIP.

entry.title: Detecting Architectural Drift in Safety-Critical Firmware through Runtime Trace Analysis

author.name: Domenico Francesco De Angelis, Marco De Luca, Domenico Amalfitano, Pasquale Cimmino, Anna Rita Fasolino

entry.summary: This work detects architectural drift in ISO 26262 safety-critical firmware by abstracting hardware-assisted execution traces into component message exchanges and deterministically diffing them against design-time sequence diagrams, with a constrained LLM step producing human-readable reports. It targets consistency between design and runtime behavior.

entry.title: SE-UNet: Singular Equivariant Imaging for Real-World Constrained Generation

author.name: Kanishk Awadhiya

entry.summary: SE-UNet solves ill-posed imaging inverse problems using strong inductive biases—D4 geometric equivariance and singular-value gating—to standardize the solution space without extensive pretraining. It reports state-of-the-art zero-shot inpainting recovering 80 percent missing pixels on CIFAR-10.

entry.title: A simplex-based measure of symmetry

author.name: Egor Bakaev, Amir Yehudayoff

entry.summary: The paper defines a simplex-based measure of symmetry for compact convex sets, relates it to the classical Minkowski measure, improves stability analysis, and gives a novel characterization of simplices. It is a pure convex-geometry contribution.

entry.title: Semantic Video Communication via Multi-Scale Convolution and Dynamic Routing for Next-Generation Networks

author.name: Gengtian Shi, Jinze Yu, Chenhao Wu, Shaofei Wang, Eiji Fukuzawa, Junjie Tang, Hiroshi Onoda, Jiang Liu

entry.summary: This work proposes a generative semantic video communication framework that transmits meaning rather than raw bits, using a linear-complexity multi-scale temporal convolutional encoder and a capsule-based dynamic routing mechanism for edge devices. It targets bandwidth-constrained, IoT-oriented video transmission with query-content alignment.

entry.title: Applying Answer Set Programming with Fuzzy Membership Functions: a Case Study

author.name: Luca Ferragina, Ilenia Galati, Lorena Gullone, Francesco Scarcello

entry.summary: This case study applies a fuzzy-logic extension of Answer Set Programming with membership functions to bridge numerical inputs (including ML outputs) and qualitative symbolic reasoning over vague linguistic concepts. It demonstrates reasoning under vagueness without rigid thresholds.

entry.title: CP-WSP: A Declarative CP-SAT Framework for Configurable Multi-Constraint Workforce Scheduling

author.name: Vipul Patel, Anirudh Deodhar, Dagnachew Birru

entry.summary: CP-WSP is a declarative CP-SAT framework for workforce scheduling that enforces 14 hard constraints, including break placement, acuity-weighted equity, sub-shift granularity, and cross-midnight shifts. It extends beyond typical simplified CP formulations toward realistic 24-hour operations.

entry.title: GLM-5 Serving Parameter Tuning for OpenClaw: Single-Deployment MaaS Inference Optimization for Long-Context Agent Workloads

author.name: Minjie Hua, Ning Wang, Peijun Yang, Kai Wang, Shiguo Lian

entry.summary: A technical report on tuning GLM-5 serving parameters (chunked prefill, tensor/pipeline parallelism, concurrency) for long-context, tool-augmented agent workloads with ~28-30k input tokens. It focuses on single-node MaaS inference optimization for throughput, TTFT, and tail latency.

entry.title: Double-Helix Active Geometry: LiDAR-Anchored Multi-View Depth with Selective Abstention

author.name: Jinwen Wen

entry.summary: DH-Active is a training-free geometry back-end that treats consumer LiDAR (e.g., iPhone) as a metric ruler, anchoring relative pose via PnP on near-field returns and triangulating trackable samples, with a parallax gate that abstains when geometry is ill-conditioned. It leaves explicit holes rather than forcing unreliable depth estimates.

entry.title: The Role of Prompt Language and Translation-Theory-Driven Prompts in Large Language Models: A Case Study on Spanish-Chinese Journalistic Translation

author.name: Haohong Lai, Weijia Li

entry.summary: This case study evaluates how prompt language and translation-theory-driven prompt design affect GPT-5.2 Spanish-Chinese journalistic translation quality, comparing automated (BLEU, BERTScore) and human MQM evaluation across 48 conditions. It finds notable divergence between automated and human rankings.

entry.title: A Bayesian Framework for Evaluating Scenario Compatibility in Generative Population Synthesis

author.name: Zhenlin Qin, Leizhen Wang, Yancheng Ling, Zhenliang Ma

entry.summary: This work proposes an ensemble-based Bayesian updating framework to quantify whether scenario-level aggregate targets are compatible with the learned structural support of generative population-synthesis models, going beyond deterministic marginal calibration. It measures how scenario constraints distort structural uncertainty.

entry.title: Semantic Segmentation-Driven Image-Level Diagnosis of Liver Cancers in Hematoxylin and Eosin Histopathology Images

author.name: Ivica Kopriva, Dario Sitnik, Arijana Pacic, Karolina Krstanac, Irena Veliki Dalic, Marijana Popovic Hadzija

entry.summary: This work proposes a semantic-segmentation-based framework for image-level diagnosis of liver cancers from H&E histopathology, assigning image labels from the dominant pixel-level prediction under the assumption of one cancer type per image. It addresses staining and annotation variability.

entry.title: PedestrianDiffusion: Multimodal Generative Denoising and Dense State Estimation for Inertial Navigation

author.name: I-Hao Lu, Dongsoo Han

entry.summary: PedestrianDiffusion reformulates dense 6D inertial-navigation state estimation as a conditional denoising diffusion process operating in the frequency domain to bound spectral covariance and reduce estimation jitter, with a zero-shot semantic conditioning mechanism using vision-language embeddings. It targets MEMS-noise-limited consumer inertial navigation.

entry.title: EPRA U-Net: An Efficient Pyramid Residual Attention Framework for Accurate Infarct Segmentation in Diffusion-Weighted MRI

author.name: Hasan Ulutas, Muhammet Emin Sahin, Mustafa Fatih Erkoc, Esra Yuce, Turker Tuncer, Sengul Dogan, Serkan Kiranyaz

entry.summary: EPRA U-Net is a parameter-efficient segmentation architecture combining an EfficientNet encoder with residual-recurrent blocks and atrous spatial pyramid pooling for acute ischemic infarct segmentation in diffusion-weighted MRI. It targets accurate clinical lesion quantification with a compact model.

entry.title: Enhancement of E-commerce Sponsored Search Relevancy with LLM

author.name: Md Omar Faruk Rokon, Andrei Simion, Weizhi Du, Musen Wen, Hong Yao, Kuang-chih Lee

entry.summary: This paper develops an advanced ad relevance model within an e-commerce sponsored search framework using LLMs to better match keywords to user queries across a large, evolving keyword space. It aims to improve ad pertinence, user satisfaction, and business operations.

entry.title: BanglaMemeEvidence: A Multimodal Benchmark Dataset for Explanatory Evidence Detection in Bengali Memes

author.name: Fatema Tuj Johora Faria, Mukaffi Bin Moin, Md. Mahfuzur Rahman, Pronay Debnath, Asif Iftekher Fahim, Faisal Muhammad Shah

entry.summary: BanglaMemeEvidence is a curated dataset of 2,917 Bengali memes for the MemeEvidenceDetect task, which identifies sentences that explain a meme's meaning and humor via multimodal contextual understanding. It addresses low-resource multimodal understanding for Bengali social media content.

entry.title: Information-Geometric Superposed Vowel Evaluation: Part 1. Moraic Syllabary (Japanese)

author.name: Yusei Tamura, Shigekazu Ishihara, Ken Ito

entry.summary: This work outlines an information-geometric method to distinguish AI-synthesized from natural human speech by analyzing the limited diversity of vowel spectra in synthetic speech, using Japanese's five-vowel syllabary as a test case. It leverages articulatory flexibility as a discriminating cue.

entry.title: A Granularity-Aware EEG Feature Framework for Psychopathology Dimension Prediction

author.name: Haofan Cheng, Jingjing Hu, Jingrong Pei, Shuaiqi Fu, Meilun Shen, Shuai Fang, Meng Wang, Dan Guo, Jie Zhang

entry.summary: This work builds a multi-scale (global, regional, channel-level) EEG feature pipeline to predict dimensional psychopathology measures like the p-factor and internalizing/externalizing problems from the Healthy Brain Network cohort. It provides a systematic feasibility test of EEG-based prediction across paradigms and feature granularities.

entry.title: Trading Confidence: Comprehensive Uncertainty Estimation in Algorithmic Trading

author.name: Lin Li, Li Rong Wang, Hsuan Fu, Xiuyi Fan

entry.summary: This paper builds an uncertainty-aware reinforcement learning framework for algorithmic trading that integrates distributional, epistemic, and aleatoric uncertainty via SHAP-weighted reconstruction, MC Dropout, and LSTM technical indicators. It aims to make RL trading agents more robust to volatility and regime shifts.

entry.title: Physics-Informed Domain-Invariant Feature Learning with Autoencoder-Driven Gaussian Clustering for Robust Non-line-of-Sight Scenarios

author.name: Nisha L. Raichur, Lucas Heublein, Dominik Seu{\ss}, Frank Deinzer, Felix Ott

entry.summary: The paper fuses physics-informed constraints into deep networks with autoencoder-driven Gaussian clustering to make angle-of-arrival estimation robust under non-line-of-sight multipath, for detecting RF jamming and spoofing. It targets navigation and satellite signal integrity.

entry.title: RES-DARE: Failure-Aware Expert Adaptation and Rollback-Safe Self-Repair for Intrusion Detection

author.name: Rahil Aftab, Anyash Prasad, Soumya Mazumdar, Vineet Kumar Rakesh, Tapas Samanta

entry.summary: RES-DARE is a failure-aware continual intrusion detection framework with rollback-safe self-repair that treats uncertain and misclassified samples as signals for expert specialization under distribution shift. It combines contrastive encoding with two-pass expert routing.

entry.title: CodeJeNN: A simple C++ neural network generator for physics applications

author.name: Jay Arcities, Pavel Popov, Eric J Ching, Kamal Viswanath, Ryan F Johnson

entry.summary: CodeJeNN auto-generates self-contained C++ inference code from trained Keras models to eliminate Python-C++ integration bottlenecks in high-performance physics solvers. It demonstrates speedups on a CFD hydrogen-air mixing case without accuracy loss.

entry.title: Adaptive Loss Balancing for Multi-Task Bioacoustic Classification of Bird Species and Call Types

author.name: Paria Vali Zadeh, Sven Tomforde

entry.summary: The paper extends BirdCallNet to joint species and call-type classification on a long-tailed bioacoustic dataset, systematically comparing four pretrained encoders and multiple loss-balancing strategies across adaptation depths. It studies how task-loss balancing interacts with representations and fine-tuning.

entry.title: SalAngaBhava: A Sinhala Market Dataset for Aspect-based Sentiment Analysis

author.name: Lakshani Galwatta, Nisansa de Silva, Sarangi Aththanayake, Adithya Galwatta

entry.summary: SalAngaBhava is a new Sinhala market dataset for aspect-based sentiment analysis, addressing the lack of aspect-annotated resources for this low-resource Indo-Aryan language. It enables fine-grained ABSA research for Sinhala where prior work was limited to sentence-level sentiment.

entry.title: Can temporal article-level credibility signals improve domain-level credibility prediction?

author.name: Islam Eldifrawi, Shengrui Wang, Amine Trabelsi

entry.summary: Investigates whether temporal article-level credibility signals can improve domain-level credibility prediction for newly emerging web domains lacking reputation. It targets automated misinformation defense as LLM-generated content accelerates.

entry.title: Ossetic-COT: Designing a morphologically annotated corpus and morphological analyzer for Ossetic

author.name: Anna Shatskikh, Alexey Sorokin

entry.summary: Presents the first Universal Dependencies-conforming morphologically annotated corpus for Iron Ossetic (5,454 sentences, 74k tokens) and trains a BERT-based morphological analyzer reaching 95.6 percent tag accuracy. It advances NLP resources for an underrepresented language.

entry.title: Inpainting U-Net for seamless pedestrian-level wind prediction across urban morphologies

author.name: Jingzi Huang, Claire E. Heaney, Tao Li, Xinzhe Li, Graham O. Hughes, Maarten van Reeuwijk

entry.summary: Develops a two-stage inpainting U-Net framework for efficient prediction of time-averaged pedestrian-level wind speed over realistic urban morphologies, using patch-based inference with an inpainting stage to remove seams. It offers a fast surrogate for expensive LES simulations in urban design.

entry.title: Reliability-Aware CT-MRI Registration: A Quality Engineering Framework with Stability Analysis and Risk Classification

author.name: Nisreen Albzour

entry.summary: Proposes a reliability-aware CT-MRI registration framework that converts registration quality into Green/Yellow/Red risk categories using data-learned thresholds over metrics like Delta NMI, Dice, stability, and inverse consistency error. It provides per-case reliability signals rather than only aggregate metrics.

entry.title: An Automated Multimodal Glaucoma Detection Framework Using ViT and a Stacking-Based Ensemble

author.name: Ishrat Jahan, Muhammad E. H Chowdhury, Murugappan Murugappan, Kanchon Kanti Podder, Tawsifur Rahman, Shrestha Datta, Md Sakib Abrar Hossain, Md Mosarrof Hossen, Yosra Magdi Salih Mekki, Sanjiban Sekhar Roy

entry.summary: Proposes an automated multimodal glaucoma detection framework integrating fundus images with clinical data using ViT and a stacking-based ensemble, evaluated under both sample-wise and patient-wise settings. It aims to scale early glaucoma screening beyond time-consuming expert interpretation.

entry.title: Censoring-adjusted tree-based policy learning for estimating dynamic treatment regimes with censored outcomes

author.name: Unknown

entry.summary: A Nature Scientific Reports paper proposes a censoring-adjusted tree-based policy learning method for estimating dynamic treatment regimes when outcomes are censored. It targets personalized sequential decision-making in clinical/biostatistical settings.

entry.title: A Clustering-Based Framework for Identifying Suspicious Trading Patterns in Capital Market

author.name: Asif Zaman, Romona Magdalene Sarkar, Sabiha Khair Ohi, Iftekharul Mobin

entry.summary: This work implements an unsupervised fraud-detection pipeline using K-Means++ clustering on roughly one million financial transactions from 2012 to 2024 to flag suspicious trades and categorize them via market-practice heuristic thresholds. It reports 2.02 percent of trades as suspicious across categories like spoofing and pump-and-dump.

entry.title: CRODA-ST: Single-Target Cross-Receiver Open-Set Radio Fingerprint Recognition

author.name: Fengchong Yao, Jianbing Li, Qing Liu, Kefeng Song, Haitao Li, Song Wang, Feixiang Wang

entry.summary: CRODA-ST is a structure-first adaptation framework for single-source single-target cross-receiver open-set radio-frequency fingerprint identification, addressing threshold fragility when calibrated on one receiver and transferred to another. It uses discriminative structure anchoring to restore reliable rejection.

entry.title: Knowledge-Centric Information Systems

author.name: Mariano Garralda-Barrio

entry.summary: Argues that as LLMs make organizational knowledge into executable infrastructure, enterprises need a new architectural discipline (knowledge architecture) for representing, governing, and delivering knowledge. It offers a conceptual model and taxonomy extending data-engineering principles.

entry.title: Harmonic-Aware Transformer for Real-Time Catheter Localization in Interventional Procedures of Magnetic Particle Imaging

author.name: Abuobaida M. Khair, Wenjing Jiang, Xiaoli Yang, Moritz Wildgruber, Xiaopeng Ma

entry.summary: This work uses a harmonic-aware transformer to predict catheter tip positions directly from raw magnetic particle imaging voltage signals, skipping image reconstruction to cut latency. It targets real-time interventional radiology tracking.

entry.title: MambaLIE: Scene Light Intensity-Boosted Low-Light Image Enhancement with State Space Model

author.name: Wanshu Fan, Xiangyu Li, Cong Wang, Kin-man Lam, Xin Yang, Haiyan Zhang, Dongsheng Zhou

entry.summary: MambaLIE is a low-light image enhancement method built on a state space model with scene-light-intensity boosting, aiming for long-range context modeling without the compute cost of transformers. It targets consumer-device low-light degradation.

entry.title: FedAvg for HAR: Exploring the Tradeoff Between Personalized and Generalization Accuracy

author.name: Andrea De Luna, Susanna Peretti, Chiara Contoli, Alessandro Bogliolo

entry.summary: This paper designs testing scenarios to compare centralized, local, and federated (FedAvg) paradigms for human activity recognition, including a scenario emulating changing client data, and analyzes the tradeoff between personalization and generalization accuracy. It provides empirical guidance for FL in pervasive computing.

entry.title: CAGE-1: Control, Assurance, and Governance Evaluation for Enterprise Agentic AI

author.name: Roopam W. Sure

entry.summary: CAGE-1 proposes an evaluation framework for enterprise agentic AI focused on control, assurance, and governance questions such as authorization, policy application, memory validity, and stoppability. It reframes agent evaluation beyond accuracy toward auditable operational trust.

entry.title: PLGSA-Transformer: Periocular Landmark-Guided Attention with Occlusion-Adaptive Cosine Thresholding for Cross-Modal Masked and Unmasked Face Recognition

author.name: Dana A Abdullah

entry.summary: PLGSA-Transformer performs cross-modal masked/unmasked face recognition using periocular landmark-guided spatial attention over eye and forehead regions plus occlusion-adaptive cosine thresholding. It targets robustness when masks occlude much of the face.

entry.title: Advanced Topic Modeling Techniques for Categorizing Software Vulnerabilities

author.name: Utkarsh Tiwari, Spoorthi M, Anirudh S, Nidhin Prabhakar T. V

entry.summary: This study applies state-of-the-art topic modeling techniques powered by LLMs (BERTopic, Top2Vec, Llama2, Mixtral) with dimensionality reduction and clustering to categorize software vulnerabilities from unstructured threat descriptions.
