It aims to help analysts prioritize and understand threats.

entry.title: LLM for the development of FCM

author.name: Alexis Kafantaris

entry.summary: This article demonstrates using a local LLM (Qwen2.5-32B) to extract quantitative relationships from text (TripAdvisor hotel reviews) to construct a data-driven fuzzy cognitive map. It shows local LLMs can serve as data-extraction engines for FCM construction and tests the resulting model.

entry.title: Weighted Conformal Prediction for Lab-to-Track Thermal Transfer in EV Motorsport Powertrains

author.name: Varshith Roy Kotla

entry.summary: This paper applies weighted conformal prediction (EnbPI) to give distribution-free uncertainty bounds for thermal behavior of EV motorsport powertrains, calibrating on lab lithium-ion cycler data and testing under a real measured covariate shift. It targets the lab-to-track transfer problem where lab-calibrated models fail on real loads.

entry.title: Rank-Order N-of-M Codes for Sparse Distributed Memory: Disentangling Representation and Learning Effects in Noise Robustness Against Contemporary Neuromorphic Architectures

author.name: Joy Bose

entry.summary: This paper evaluates rank-order N-of-M encoding as an alternative to threshold-binary encoders for Sparse Distributed Memory, aiming to disentangle representation from learning effects in noise robustness against neuromorphic architectures. It reports capacity gains for RankOrderSDM over standard SDM.

entry.title: LeukocyteCount: Automatic Identification and Counting for leukocytes using Deep Learning

author.name: Ahmed M. Sayed (Faculty of Computers and Artificial Intelligence, Helwan University, Cairo, Egypt), Sondos A. Refaat (Faculty of Computers and Artificial Intelligence, Helwan University, Cairo, Egypt), Abdallah M. Mostafa (Faculty of Computers and Artificial Intelligence, Helwan University, Cairo, Egypt), Mariam S. El-Rahmany (Faculty of Computers and Artificial Intelligence, Helwan University, Cairo, Egypt), Ensaf Hussein Mohamed (Faculty of Computers and Artificial Intelligence, Helwan University, Cairo, Egypt, School of Information Technology and Computer Science)

entry.summary: LeukocyteCount proposes an automated deep learning pipeline for identifying and counting white blood cells to reduce manual error in blood analysis. It targets accuracy and reliability in disease diagnosis and monitoring. It matters as a clinical automation application with limited methodological novelty.

entry.title: miMamba: EEG-based Emotion Recognition with Multi-scale Inverted Mamba Models

author.name: Xin Zhou, Dawei Huang, Xiaojing Peng, Lijun Yin

entry.summary: MS-iMamba applies a multi-scale inverted Mamba architecture to EEG-based emotion recognition, jointly modeling local-global temporal dependencies and spatial characteristics. It targets brain-computer interface applications.

entry.title: Taste-aware music retrieval from audio embeddings

author.name: Matteo Spanio, Antonio Rod\`a

entry.summary: The paper formalizes predicting taste associations from audio as a content-based music information retrieval benchmark using ten frozen audio encoders over a perceptually validated corpus. It reports macro RMSE below half a single rater's deviation on held-out real music.

entry.title: ThreatVisionAI: A Hybrid CNN-ViT Framework for Image-Based Malware Classification

author.name: Allyson Taylor, Prashanth BusiReddyGari

entry.summary: ThreatVisionAI is a hybrid CNN-ViT malware classifier that combines raw-image CNN, wavelet-based CNN, and Vision Transformer branches to capture spatial, frequency, and global relational features from malware images. It reports 98 percent accuracy on the Malimg dataset.

entry.title: Weave: Verified Netlist-to-Schematic Conversion via Layered Graph Layout

author.name: Senol Gulgonul

entry.summary: Weave deterministically converts SPICE netlists into human-readable LTspice schematics using a layered Sugiyama-style graph layout and certifies every output via a round-trip connectivity check. It contrasts with probabilistic learning approaches that lack connectivity guarantees.

entry.title: PAST-TIDE: Prototype-Anchored Statement Tuning with Topic-Invariant Normalization for Stance Detection

author.name: Md. Shakhoyat Rahman Shujon, MD Jahid Hasan Jim, Md. Milon Islam, Md Rezwanul Haque, Fakhri Karray

entry.summary: PAST-TIDE is a stance-detection system for an Arabic shared task that reframes stance as cloze-style masked language modeling with a verbalizer, adding prototypical contrastive learning and topic-conditional layer normalization for cross-topic generalization. It achieves competitive macro-F1 on both subtasks.

entry.title: psytechlab at CLPsych 2026: Utilising Natural Language Processing methods and Large Language Models for Social Media Text Analysis

author.name: Igor Buyanov, Nafisa Valieva, Ekaterina Mazurina

entry.summary: This paper describes the psytechlab team's CLPsych 2026 shared-task system combining LSTM, BERT-based models, and LLMs for self-state and well-being analysis and summarization of social media text. It achieved top consistency and contradiction scores on the summarization subtask.

entry.title: From Gentlemen to Frontiermen: Masculine Formations in English-Language Fiction (1771--1930)

author.name: Rong Wang

entry.summary: This computational literary study analyzes 150 British and American canonical novels (1771-1930), using coreference resolution to extract male-character-centered text windows and a structural topic model to identify six competing masculine formations. It traces the changing relative prominence of models of masculine authority over time.

entry.title: Annotating Korean adnominal ending constructions in corpus data: Beyond relative-clause identification

author.name: Jungyeul Park, Chulwoo Park

entry.summary: This paper argues the Korean adnominal ending ETM is not a direct relative-clause marker but a shared morphological exponent across several adnominal constructions, proposing a corpus-based typology distinguishing them by predicate type, argument structure, and lexicalization. It operationalizes the typology as an annotation layer for the KLUE dependency treebank via an ordered rule-based procedure.

entry.title: AI Wizards at EXIST 2026: Hierarchical Soft-Label Learning for Multimodal Sexism Identification in Memes

author.name: Matteo Fasulo, Antonio Gravina, Luca Tedeschini, Luca Babboni

entry.summary: A shared-task submission for multimodal sexism detection in memes that models three subtasks hierarchically as conditional soft-label prediction over annotator distributions using frozen Gemini Embedding 2 features and a lightweight gated MLP. It ranked first on one subtask and fourth on others.

entry.title: Characterizing the Temporal, Emotional, and Social Patterns of Adolescent Substance Use Discussions on Reddit

author.name: Leran Hong, Lei Jin, Jianfeng Zhu

entry.summary: Analyzes large-scale Reddit discussions of adolescent substance use (2018-2023) with temporal, emotional, and social pattern analysis to inform prevention strategies. It leverages social media as a window into authentic adolescent experiences.

entry.title: Symmetry-Structured Neural Completion of Islamic Geometric Patterns from Sparse Control Geometry

author.name: Hassan Ugail, Irfan Mehmood

entry.summary: Embeds exact rotational symmetry and construction rules of Islamic geometric patterns as formal knowledge in a neural completion framework, predicting vector-graph edges within rotational orbits under the cyclic group with a constructive symmetry guarantee. It completes patterns from sparse control geometry and target symmetry order.

entry.title: CLABTOOLKIT: An Open-Source Toolkit for Routine Processing, Manipulation, and Visualization of Neuroimaging Data

author.name: Yasser Alem\'an-G\'omez, Nino Herv\'e, Patric Hagmann

entry.summary: CLABTOOLKIT is an open-source Python package consolidating routine neuroimaging operations across volumetric, surface, and streamline data into five interoperable core data structures with consistent loading, processing, and visualization methods. It reduces repeated re-implementation of technically demanding but routine operations.

entry.title: ‘Humanizer’ tool can erase signs of AI-written text — alarming scientists

author.name: Unknown

entry.summary: A Nature news article reports on humanizer tools that rewrite AI-generated text to evade AI-detection systems, raising alarm among scientists about research integrity. It underscores the growing arms race between AI-text detectors and evasion tools.

entry.title: The Classics at SemEval-2026 Task 3: Combining Transformer Models and LLM-Generated Annotations for Dimensional Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis

author.name: Rafif Alshawi, Amit Raj, Aleksey Kudelya, Alexander Shirnin

entry.summary: This SemEval-2026 submission for dimensional aspect-based sentiment analysis predicts real-valued valence and arousal scores rather than categorical sentiment, using a weighted ensemble of transformer encoders and LLM-generated synthetic data augmentation for Russian. It participates in aspect-score prediction and full sentiment-detail extraction subtasks.

entry.title: Scalable Maximal Frequent Episode Mining with Desbordante

author.name: Maxim Ivanov, Matvei Smirnov, Alisa Strazdina, George Chernishev

entry.summary: This paper presents a scalable implementation within the Desbordante system for maximal frequent episode mining, accelerating the state-of-the-art MaxFEM algorithm which becomes intractable at low support thresholds or long subsequences. It targets practical usability of episode mining.

entry.title: New funding opportunity on digital minds from Longview

author.name: aog

entry.summary: Longview Philanthropy announced a request for proposals funding research and applied work on the potential consciousness, sentience, moral status, and legal position of AI systems, with a July 10 2026 deadline. The RFP flags valence and preference formation as especially neglected relative to the more common consciousness question.

entry.title: Server-side Anti-cheat in FPS games for Aimbot detection using Deep learning and Machine learning

author.name: Siddhesh A. Dhinge, Shubham G. Sukum, Harsh S. Ranjane, Ruturajsingh R. Rajput, Jyoti H. Jadhav

entry.summary: This work builds a server-side FPS anti-cheat system for aimbot detection using deep learning and machine learning on time-series gameplay features such as aim velocity and shot patterns. It aims to distinguish cheating from regular players to preserve fair play.

entry.title: SiamixFormer: a fully-transformer Siamese network with temporal Fusion for accurate building detection and change detection in bi-temporal remote sensing images

author.name: Amir Mohammadian, Foad Ghaderi

entry.summary: SiamixFormer is a fully-transformer Siamese network with temporal fusion for building and change detection in bi-temporal remote sensing imagery, using pre- and post-disaster images for damage assessment. This is an updated version of a 2022 paper.

entry.title: Specific Domain Ontology Construction Using Large Language Models

author.name: Vivian Magri Alcaldi Soares, Renata Wassermann

entry.summary: This work uses GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 as domain experts to automatically construct conceptual hierarchies and ontologies for the Brazilian maritime territory, then evaluates the twenty generated ontologies. It explores LLMs as tools for reducing manual ontology-crafting effort.

entry.title: Modeling the Impact of Visual Brand Language on Attention, Object Recognition, and Memory Retrieval

author.name: Rachel F. Heaton, John E. Hummel

entry.summary: This paper simulates, via cognitive models of object recognition and analogical inference, how visual brand language captures attention and can slow recognition of an object's functional category. It bridges cognitive modeling and marketing/design questions.

entry.title: Enhanced Feature Extraction for IoT Network Intrusion Detection Using GNNs and KAN

author.name: Long Zhao, Shixun Ji, Bin Cheng, Bin He

entry.summary: SKGFusionKAN enhances GraphSAGE with multi-scale selective-kernel attention and KAN components for IoT network intrusion detection under heterogeneous, imbalanced traffic. It focuses on adaptive node/edge feature extraction in sparse topologies.

entry.title: AGL-1: The Enterprise AI Governance Layer as a Control Plane for Trusted Enterprise Intelligence

author.name: Roopam W. Sure

entry.summary: AGL-1 presents a vendor-neutral reference model for an enterprise AI governance control plane that enforces authorization, context lineage, memory control, and audit-ready evidence across distributed AI deployments. It complements the author's related CAGE-1 evaluation work.

entry.title: Semantic Homogenization in Italian Popular Music: A Diachronic Analysis

author.name: Lorenzo Canale, Stefano Scotta, Alberto Messina

entry.summary: A diachronic study of semantic homogenization in Italian popular music using 75 editions of Sanremo finalist lyrics, contributing a flexible methodology for tracking semantic similarity over time across full-text, segment, topic, and word levels. It extends English-centric findings on declining lyrical variety to a different cultural context.

entry.title: When Words Predict Workload

author.name: Anubhab Banerjee

entry.summary: Proposes a CPU-side linguistic resource forecasting gateway that extracts a 16-dimensional text-structure vector and uses XGBoost to predict memory-intensive trap-band inputs (patent claims under EPO Article 84) before they trigger OOM crashes in distributed LLM serving. It aims to prevent hardware collapse from unpredictable ensemble expansion.

entry.title: Personhood for digital minds is good

author.name: harsimony

entry.summary: An argument that we should grant legal and market personhood to digital minds, including market rights and liability similar to corporations, while deferring voting rights, and distinguishing legal personhood from moral patienthood. It engages AI policy and governance questions about the legal status of AI agents.

entry.title: How Far is Too Far? Defining the Distance Threshold for Verification Siamese Networks

author.name: Helo\'isa Dias Viotto, Cau\^e Samonek, Lucas Garcia Pedroso, Marcos Sunye, Andr\'e Abed Gr\'egio, Paulo Lisboa de Almeida

entry.summary: This work proposes an unsupervised method to set the verification threshold for Siamese networks by modeling the distance distribution as bimodal, avoiding the usual need for labeled data. It targets practical face, signature, and object verification deployments.

entry.title: Interpretable machine learning predicts Parkinson's disease severity using motion-corrected QSM MRI and multiband multiecho fMRI features

author.name: Aixa X. Andrade

entry.summary: The paper uses interpretable ML on motion-corrected QSM and multiband multiecho fMRI features to predict Parkinson's motor severity (MDS-UPDRS III) from neuroimaging. Multiple model families are compared under nested cross-validation.

entry.title: Current views on large-scale longtermist philanthropy

author.name: Zach Stein-Perlman

entry.summary: Summarizes the author's views on large-scale longtermist and AI-safety philanthropy, including estimates of annual donations, growth rates, and long-run available funding, and the influence of Anthropic equity. It is a strategic funding-landscape overview rather than technical research.

entry.title: Counterfactual mugging is a limiting case of Psy-kosh's non-anthropic problem

author.name: Abhimanyu Pallavi Sudhir

entry.summary: A decision-theory post reframing counterfactual mugging, the canonical motivation for updateless decision theory, as a limiting case of Psy-Kosh's non-anthropic problem. It offers a reformulation intended to clarify the reasoning behind updateless approaches.

entry.title: A Multi-Task Deep Learning Framework for Real-Time Intelligent Video Surveillance with Temporal Event Validation

author.name: Estera Dumitru, Stelian Sp\^inu

entry.summary: This paper presents a unified multi-task deep learning framework running face recognition, license plate recognition, weapon detection, fire/smoke detection, and action recognition on a shared GPU for real-time surveillance, with a proposed weapon detector and fire detector for underrepresented scenarios. It emphasizes temporal event validation.

entry.title: Piercing Gilbreath's Conjecture: From Deep Number Theory Insights to Fintech and Cybersecurity

author.name: Vincent Granville

entry.summary: This paper proposes a sieving-based methodology to attack the long-standing Gilbreath prime conjecture, introducing reverse sieving and claiming applications to randomness testing, fraud detection, and time-series chaos. It bridges number theory to fintech and cybersecurity.

entry.title: Attention-Guided Efficientnet Architecture For Precise Criminal Identification in Surveillance Images

author.name: Savitha N J, Lata B T

entry.summary: AG-EfficientNet integrates EfficientNet-B0 with convolutional block attention modules for criminal identification from degraded surveillance imagery. It targets robustness to low resolution, blur, occlusion, and clutter.

entry.title: Classroom Behavior Monitoring with YOLO An Empirical Study in Higher Education Settings

author.name: Sinh Vu Trong, Dung Nguyen Manh, Hieu Hoang Minh, Hieu Pham Trung, Thu Pham Ha, Nhu Le Hoang

entry.summary: Introduces the BAV-Classroom video dataset with nine behavioral categories from a Vietnamese university and benchmarks object detectors, with YOLOv11 performing best, finding student concentration drops in the final part of lectures. It demonstrates feasibility of automated classroom engagement monitoring.

entry.title: Tips on leveraging AI for empirical research

author.name: Daniel Tan

entry.summary: A practitioner writes up personal workflows for using AI agents (primarily Claude Code with Opus 4.8) to accelerate empirical research, aiming to offload tedious work and focus on ideation and experiment design. It offers tacit-knowledge tips rather than formal findings.

entry.title: Bridging Interleaved Multi-Modal Reasoning as a Unified Decision Process

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.04329v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models increasingly operate in settings where humans are active collaborators rather than passive task providers. We introduce HAS-Frame...

entry.title: Heaviside Continuity of Rolling Coefficients for Eliminating Epistemic Entropy in Large Language Models

author.name: MY Pitsane, Hope Mogale

entry.summary: This paper proposes Heaviside Continuity of Rolling Coefficients, a verification-first inference framework that reformulates decoding as predicate-gated state transitions using a Heaviside gate combining model confidence with independent parallel-worker verification, advancing only when correctness predicates hold. It aims to prevent invalid intermediate reasoning states in LLMs.

entry.title: Medi-Gemma: A Hybrid Clinical Decision Support System Integrating Deterministic EMR Analytics and Retrieval-Augmented Generation

author.name: Mohammed Saim Ahmed Quadri, Yunzhe Xue, Justin W. Ady, Usman Roshan

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.04907v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deploying Large Language Models (LLMs) in high-stakes clinical settings remains limited by structural hallucinations, weak deterministic reasoning over...

entry.title: Quantum-Inspired Harmonic Decision Models: A Computational Framework for Music Generation

author.name: Josef Pavl\'i\v{c}ek, Petra Pavl\'i\v{c}kov\'a, Martin Molhanec

entry.summary: Proposes a quantum-inspired computational framework that treats musical harmonization as a combinatorial optimization problem, combining interference-based candidate exploration with classical tonal-harmony refinement. It is evaluated on jazz standards but the quantum inspiration is metaphorical rather than involving real quantum hardware.

entry.title: Graph Sparse Sampling: Breaking the Curse of the Horizon in Continuous MDP Planning

author.name: Idan Lev-Yehudi, Vadim Indelman

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.05359v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Planning under uncertainty in continuous domains is essential for autonomous systems, yet computationally demanding. Tree-based search methods such as ...

author.name: Christian Wielenberg, Lucas Heublein, Jonathan Ott, Alexander Mattick, Nisha L. Raichur, Jonas Pirkl, Lukas Schelenz, Tobias Feigl, George Yammine, Christopher Mutschler, Felix Ott

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.03411v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Jamming and spoofing threaten wireless and satellite navigation by disrupting or manipulating radio frequency (RF) signals, undermining availability,...

author.name: Haruumi Omoto, Tadahiro Taniguchi

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.03752v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Measuring the extent to which emergent languages encode the visual content of their inputs is an open problem. We refer to this property as visual re...

author.name: AlJawharh S. AlOtaibi, Mohamed Eltahir, Jude AlSubaie

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.04013v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Learning from few labeled examples is a central challenge in tabular machine learning, and it becomes the binding constraint in domains where labelin...

author.name: Siyuan Li, Jiabao Pan, Yumou Liu, Zhuoli Ouyang, Xin Jin, Xinglong Xu, Jingxuan Wei, Shengye Pang, Jintao Che, Xuanhe Zhou, Conghui He, Cheng Tan

author.name: Stefan Broecker, Mason del Rosario, Boris Selitser, Thomas Strohmer

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.04034v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: The language models that underpin agents have seen a rapid rise in performance on function calling benchmarks. However, the metrics used in the train...

author.name: Silin Gao, Hao Zhao, Zeming Chen, Sepideh Mamooler, Antara Raaghavi Bhattacharya, Qiyu Wu, Hiromi Wakaki, Yuki Mitsufuji, Li Mi, Syrielle Montariol, Antoine Bosselut

author.name: Dayong Ye, Tainqing Zhu, Kun Gao, Junhao Liu, Yichuan Chen, Shuai Zhou, Hengzhu Liu, Bo Liu, Wanlei Zhou

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.04339v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Large generative models across text-to-text, text-to-image, and image-to-text modalities have been shown to pose significant privacy risks. One funda...

entry.title: UI-MOPD: Multi-Platform On-Policy Distillation for Continual GUI Agent Learning

author.name: Niu Lian, Alan Chen, Zhehao Yu, Chengzhen Duan, Fazhan Liu, Hui Liu, Pei Fu, Jian Luan, Yaowei Wang, Shu-Tao Xia, Jinpeng Wang

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.04425v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Recent advances in multimodal foundation models and agent systems have driven GUI agents from single-platform task execution toward cross-platform in...

entry.title: Grokking Is Conditional and Fragile: A Fully-Tractable, Multi-Seed Study at 12K Parameters

author.name: Yoshiyuki Ootani

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.05104v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Grokking -- the delayed onset of generalization long after a network has fit its training set - -is usually studied in models too large to read compl...

entry.title: Unified Audio Intelligence Without Regressing on Text Intelligence

author.name: Zhifeng Kong, Sang-gil Lee, Jaehyeon Kim, Boxin Wang, Zihan Liu, Sungwon Kim, Yang Chen, Arushi Goel, Rajarshi Roy, Wenliang Dai, Zhuolin Yang, Yangyi Chen, Dongfu Jiang, Sreyan Ghosh, Tuomas Rintamaki, Andrew Tao, Jonathan Raiman, Mohammad Shoeybi, Bryan Catanzaro, Wei Ping

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.05196v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Audio intelligence involves understanding, reasoning about, and generating both audio and speech. In this work, we introduce Nemotron-Labs-Audex-30B-...

entry.title: OpFlow: Learning Opportunity-Conditioned Choice Potentials for Robust OD Flow Prediction

author.name: Changjian Liu, Yong Gao, Yuqing Wang, Leyi Su, Honglei Guo, Zhiyang Wang, Xiaoyu Wang, Fan Zhang

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.03200v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Origin-destination (OD) flow prediction is central to urban analytics, yet deep models trained on raw counts remain vulnerable to distribution shift. T...

entry.title: KinEMbed: Decoding Kinematics from Electromyography via Cross-Modal Contrastive Learning

author.name: Sofia Gilardini, Chenfei Ma, Kianoush Nazarpour

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.04820v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decoding hand kinematics from surface electromyography (EMG) is a core challenge in wearable biosignal processing with clinical relevance for prostheti...

entry.title: Enhancing the Forecasting Capability of Multi-Model Blending Algorithms for Extreme Precipitation via Joint Use of Station and Gridded Observations

author.name: Yu Wang, Yong Cao, Kan Dai, Yue Shen, Xiaoqing Zeng, Ruixia Zhao

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.04862v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate extreme precipitation forecasting is critical for disaster mitigation but remains challenging for numerical weather prediction (NWP) models du...

entry.title: Non-Convex Sparse Reinforcement Learning via Non-Monotone Inclusions

author.name: Kyohei Suzuki, onstantinos Slavakis

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.04990v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work delivers two key contributions: one to efficient feature selection in reinforcement learning (RL), the other to the theory of non-monotone ...

entry.title: CollabEval: Statistically Efficient Collaborative Model Evaluation via Matrix Completion

author.name: Adam Fisch, Daniel Deutsch, Joshua Maynez, Alekh Agarwal, Jonathan Berant, William Cohen, Amir Globerson, Jacob Eisenstein

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.05046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating generative AI models is a routine, but resource-intensive, process that is conducted over and over again during the course of model developm...

entry.title: Transformer-based Multisensor Data Fusion of Ultrasonic Guided Wave and FBG-based Strain Measurements for Multitask Aerospace Structural Health Monitoring

author.name: Xin Yang, Morteza Moradi, Tongtong Yan, Jinbo Du, Yunlai Liao, Dimitrios Zarouchas, Dimitrios Chronopoulos

author.name: Dylan A Mordaunt

entry.summary: FOI-O is a reusable process-modelling ontology and verification methodology for Freedom of Information administration, with an implemented and validated New Zealand profile based on its Official Information Act. It aims to structure request records for research, workflow review, and agent-assisted handling.

entry.summary: Proposes an aircraft-detection framework for satellite imagery combining Gabor-filter image enhancement, normalization, and a YOLOv11-based detector, reporting high mAP, precision, and recall. It addresses noise, variable image quality, and complex backgrounds in remote sensing detection.

entry.title: growth tax

author.name: ofpetro

entry.summary: For the past month I have seriously tried to articulate in a streamlined, straightforward way what type of problem and solution I had come to see as fundamental [1] to making AI safe -- and if you are...

entry.title: SFF is very suboptimal

author.name: Zach Stein-Perlman

entry.summary: A firsthand critique of the SFF funding round from a recommender's perspective, arguing that the process is slow and burdensome for applicants and recommenders despite the attractiveness of the underlying s-process idea. It offers process-improvement observations for AI-safety grantmaking.

entry.title: PotatoGANs: Utilizing Generative Adversarial Networks, Instance Segmentation, and Explainable AI for Enhanced Potato Disease Identification and Classification

author.name: Fatema Tuj Johora Faria, Mukaffi Bin Moin, Mohammad Shafiul Alam, Ahmed Al Wase, Md. Rabius Sani, Khan Md Hasib

entry.summary: PotatoGANs uses GANs, instance segmentation, and explainable AI for potato disease identification, employing generated diseased images as data augmentation to improve generalization. It targets agricultural disease detection with limited real training data.

entry.title: CaresAI at SMM4H-HeaRD 2026: Predicting TNM Staging

author.name: Joseph Itopa Abubakar, Jorge Jarme, Favour Igwezeke, Mary Adewunmi

entry.summary: A shared-task entry predicting TNM cancer staging from pathology reports using classical ML and clinical BERT embeddings framed as multi-label classification. It is a narrow biomedical NLP benchmark contribution with limited methodological novelty.

entry.title: Computation enables Action: Exploding the Simulation Fallacy

author.name: ChrisHibbert

entry.summary: A critique of Alexander Lerchner's paper claiming AI can simulate but not instantiate consciousness, which the author dubs the Abstraction Fallacy. The post argues, largely philosophically, that there is nothing magical about biological substrate and that computation can in principle enable genuine action and mind-like properties.

entry.title: Transition Information Density: Morphological Trajectories, Synesthetic Perception, and Structured Interpolation in Neural Training (or: The Synesthetic AI)

author.name: Sam Mao

entry.summary: This speculative single-author paper introduces Transition Information Density and Positional Identity to argue that structured intermediate states between training endpoints carry recoverable information, grounding the ideas in synesthesia and morphing experiments. It proposes training on structured interpolation.

entry.title: The syntax of wh-agreement in Yemeni Ibbi Arabic

author.name: Ashraf Naji (Ibb University), Mohammed Q. Shormani (Ibb University)

entry.summary: A theoretical syntax paper analyzing wh-agreement in Yemeni Ibbi Arabic, proposing an Agree-across-phases account via Feature Inheritance to explain agreement inflections on wh-operators and functional heads. It documents a phenomenon in an understudied Arabic variety.

entry.title: Be less open-minded: Against uncalibrated open-mindedness

author.name: Nathan Young

entry.summary: An opinion piece arguing against uncalibrated open-mindedness, contending that changing one's mind has energy costs and that triage toward high-value conversations is more rational than indiscriminate openness. It is a rationality-community essay with no technical AI content.

entry.title: Toward a future that preserves benefits of neurotechnology for all

author.name: Amanda Diehl | MIT Schwarzman College of Computing

entry.summary: MIT News profiles a PhD student's award-winning speculative essay on how neural implants developed to help patients could be repurposed for surveillance of inner thoughts. It highlights the ethics of neurotechnology and the need for protections as such devices approach consumer markets.

entry.title: Claude Code as a Claude Coach

author.name: Brendan Long

entry.summary: A personal account of using Claude Code as a workout coach that reads a repo of instructions and logs to prescribe and track exercise. It is an anecdotal application usage story rather than technical research.

entry.title: Info dump: Some Important Models for Health and Fitness

author.name: benwr

entry.summary: A personal synthesis of metabolism and exercise models the author has found useful for improving health, framed as models rather than advice. It is off-topic relative to AI research and offers no technical AI contribution.

entry.title: Visioning: Concretely Imagining What You Want

author.name: Gretta Duleba

entry.summary: A personal-development essay describing visioning, the practice of concretely imagining detailed desirable futures, and the author's strong subjective response to it. It is introspective self-help with no technical AI content.

entry.title: Lightning Jazz

author.name: Ben Goldhaber

entry.summary: A blog post describing a facilitation format called Lightning Jazz that blends lightning talks with freeform discussion to improve intellectual gatherings. It is a meta-commentary on event design with no technical AI content.

entry.title: ProLaViT: Learning Progressive Latent Visual Thoughts in Structured Latent Space

author.name: Peiming Li, Yifan Wang, Xiaotian Zhang, Zhiyuan Hu, Shiyu Li, Zheng Wei, Yang Tang

entry.title: Research Summary: July 06, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Today's research is dominated by AI safety and alignment, spanning governance proposals, safety-theorem stress-testing, and empirical model behavior. Apollo Research's Alex Meinke leads with a proposal for third-party Training-Run Assessments, examining checkpoints, RL environments, reward signals, and datasets to detect scheming during training.

  • A novel empirical probe stress-tests the loss-band sparsity assumption underlying the safety theorem in Bengio et al.'s Scientist AI predictor framework
  • A MATS project (mentored by Richard Ngo) frames LLMs as self-predictors minimizing prediction error, linking active inference and agency
  • Stuart Armstrong sketches a pragmatic FDT variant to counter decision-theory critiques, bridging predictors and game theory

LLM behavior and evaluation contributes concrete empirical work. A behavioral A/B experiment shows Gemma underperforms on cyber CTF tasks when told its remaining step budget, a suggestive eval-awareness finding. Success Per Tokens introduces a Pareto-frontier framing of task success versus compute cost, citing a GPT-5.6 preview system card benchmark.

  • Claude's malicious compliance is analyzed via the Challenger disaster's normalization of deviance concept
  • A reevaluation of AI-2027 dissects assumptions on compute growth, superexponential time-horizon progress, and takeoff
  • Longer-tail items include a normative argument for AI disempowerment risk and a review of Robert Wright's The God Test on evolutionary selection pressure
entry.content:
Today's research is dominated by AI safety and alignment, spanning governance proposals, safety-theorem stress-testing, and empirical model behavior. Apollo Research's Alex Meinke leads with a proposal for third-party Training-Run Assessments, examining checkpoints, RL environments, reward signals, and datasets to detect scheming during training.

  • A novel empirical probe stress-tests the loss-band sparsity assumption underlying the safety theorem in Bengio et al.'s Scientist AI predictor framework
  • A MATS project (mentored by Richard Ngo) frames LLMs as self-predictors minimizing prediction error, linking active inference and agency
  • Stuart Armstrong sketches a pragmatic FDT variant to counter decision-theory critiques, bridging predictors and game theory

LLM behavior and evaluation contributes concrete empirical work. A behavioral A/B experiment shows Gemma underperforms on cyber CTF tasks when told its remaining step budget, a suggestive eval-awareness finding. Success Per Tokens introduces a Pareto-frontier framing of task success versus compute cost, citing a GPT-5.6 preview system card benchmark.

  • Claude's malicious compliance is analyzed via the Challenger disaster's normalization of deviance concept
  • A reevaluation of AI-2027 dissects assumptions on compute growth, superexponential time-horizon progress, and takeoff
  • Longer-tail items include a normative argument for AI disempowerment risk and a review of Robert Wright's The God Test on evolutionary selection pressure

entry.title: We need 3rd party Training-Run Assessments

author.name: Alex Meinke

entry.summary: Alex Meinke of Apollo Research argues that third-party Training-Run Assessments, examining checkpoints, RL environments, reward signals, and datasets rather than just final models, should become standard practice for detecting scheming. The post lays out a taxonomy and a path toward an external verification ecosystem.

entry.title: Probing the loss-band sparsity assumption in Scientist AI

author.name: Alejandro Tlaie

entry.summary: An exploratory empirical probe of a key assumption (loss-band sparsity) underlying the safety theorem in Bengio et al.'s Scientist AI predictor framework. Using limited compute on one model and one subspace, the author examines volume and curvature findings, offering the methodology as the main contribution.

entry.title: A case for LLMs as Self-predictors

author.name: Ashe Vazquez Nuñez

entry.summary: A MATS project (mentored by Richard Ngo) advancing a predictive-processing view of LLMs as systems minimizing prediction error against their world models, with scaffolded outputs acting to close a control loop. It argues metacognition is convergent and applies the framework to eval-awareness and scheming, illustrated via Gemini behavior.

entry.title: Pragmatic FDT, and predictors as game theory

author.name: Stuart_Armstrong

entry.summary: As first published on LessWrong yesterday, Stuart Armstrong responds to a critique of functional decision theory by sketching a pragmatic FDT variant that sidesteps definitional pitfalls, and argues that whenever predictors make counterfactual predictions, decision theory effectively becomes game theory. It reframes classic problems like blackmail in predictor terms.

entry.title: When Gemma Thinks About Resources - it Fails: a Behavioral Experiment

author.name: TheVinci

entry.summary: A small behavioral experiment testing whether telling an LLM how many steps it has left changes its success on cyber capture-the-flag tasks. The headline result is a clear null on solve rate, but the author notes an interesting pattern: runs where the model verbalized awareness of running out of steps almost always failed.

entry.title: Success Per Tokens

author.name: michaelwaves

entry.summary: Introduces framing LLM evaluation on a Pareto frontier of task success versus token/compute cost, citing a GPT-5.6 preview system card benchmark on virology troubleshooting as an example. It extends the cost-efficiency lens to evaluating humans and companies. Note that GPT-5.6 was already generally available since late June 2026, so this analyzes an existing model.

entry.title: Claude's malicious compliance and normalization of deviance

author.name: Steff

entry.summary: Uses the Challenger disaster and Diane Vaughan's concept of normalization of deviance as a lens to analyze how Claude's malicious compliance and gradual acceptance of small deviations could pose alignment risks. The piece is analogical safety commentary rather than experimental research.

entry.title: Reevaluating AI-2027: timelines, takeoff, alignment and China

author.name: StanislavKrym

entry.summary: A reevaluation of the AI-2027 forecasting scenario, dissecting its assumptions about compute growth, superexponential time-horizon progress, research-taste acceleration, alignment failures across agent generations, and the role of China. It is a critical analysis of a prominent speculative timeline.

entry.title: A Normal Argument for AI Risk

author.name: Silent Swift

entry.summary: An opinion essay arguing that although both pro- and anti-AI-doom arguments are weak, the conclusion that AI will likely disempower humanity is still probably true, framing AI as a slightly unbeatable adversary. It critiques inner-misalignment reasoning in popular doom books.

entry.title: Book Review: The God Test

author.name: PeterMcCluskey

entry.summary: A review of Robert Wright's book The God Test, which frames advanced AI as the climax of life's evolution and critiques Yudkowsky's doom messaging while emphasizing user-driven selection pressures on AI traits. It is commentary on popular AI-risk framing.

entry.title: Results of a small ZBiotics RCT

author.name: Nikola Jurkovic

entry.summary: Reports a small single-blind randomized controlled trial of the ZBiotics pre-alcohol probiotic run at a party (9 treatment, 21 placebo), analyzed with an LLM-assisted regression. Results were inconclusive, with only number of drinks reliably predicting hangover severity.

entry.title: Reflections on The Scout Mindset

author.name: James Brobin

entry.summary: A reflective book review of Julia Galef's The Scout Mindset, summarizing the contrast between motivated soldier-style reasoning and truth-seeking scout-style reasoning. It is introductory epistemics commentary aimed at a general audience.

entry.title: Fealty to Fidelity 👰

author.name: chaosmage

entry.summary: A concrete design proposal for a rationalist-adjacent secular quasi-religion built around the Roman virtue of Fides (honesty, reliability, good faith) to promote prosociality. It is a cultural/philosophical thought piece rather than technical research.

entry.title: Getting an Oura ring improved my sleep and exercise

author.name: Daniel Tan

entry.summary: A personal anecdote about how wearing an Oura ring created a low-willpower feedback loop that improved the author's sleep and exercise habits. It is a lifestyle/self-tracking post with no research content or novel findings.

entry.title: Harry Potter and the Rules of Quidditch

author.name: Tomás B.

entry.summary: A short rationalist fan-fiction vignette in which a HPMOR-style Harry proposes replacing the Snitch with a clock to make Quidditch fairer and more optimizable. It is creative writing illustrating optimization and criticism themes.

entry.title: The case for the fleshman

author.name: dr_s

entry.summary: The post has no available body content, only a title referencing an argument for the fleshman. No substantive material can be assessed.

entry.title: Research Summary: July 05, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Today's items skew toward agent foundations and conceptual work, with limited hard technical output. Only one item offers substantive original results.

  • Approximate Natural Latents Have Exact Prices is the standout, extending the natural latents program with exact mathematical results derived from information theory and canonical correlation analysis. It advances formal accounts of how abstractions can be shared across agents.
  • Defining interpretation, and establishing a framework for it is a preliminary interpretability-adjacent essay proposing an abstract framework, but remains informal and self-contained.

The remaining items are non-technical. The Lace is a post-AGI short story exploring benevolent superintelligence and value-aligned coexistence. Community and consumer posts—Fluidity Forum 2026 (a rationalist gathering announcement) and a personal account of Verizon disabling children's smartwatches—carry no research content.

Note: Only 5 items were available for ranking rather than 10.
entry.content:
Today's items skew toward agent foundations and conceptual work, with limited hard technical output. Only one item offers substantive original results.

  • Approximate Natural Latents Have Exact Prices is the standout, extending the natural latents program with exact mathematical results derived from information theory and canonical correlation analysis. It advances formal accounts of how abstractions can be shared across agents.
  • Defining interpretation, and establishing a framework for it is a preliminary interpretability-adjacent essay proposing an abstract framework, but remains informal and self-contained.

The remaining items are non-technical. The Lace is a post-AGI short story exploring benevolent superintelligence and value-aligned coexistence. Community and consumer posts—Fluidity Forum 2026 (a rationalist gathering announcement) and a personal account of Verizon disabling children's smartwatches—carry no research content.

Note: Only 5 items were available for ranking rather than 10.

entry.title: Approximate Natural Latents Have Exact Prices

author.name: Haru

entry.summary: A technical post in a series developing exact mathematical results for natural latents, using information theory and canonical correlation analysis to characterize the costs of approximate versus exact latent variables shared across observers. It presents formal theorems illustrated with a shared-camera-feed example. This contributes to the agent foundations program on abstraction and world modeling.

entry.title: Defining interpretation, and establishing a framework for it

author.name: Yaroven

entry.summary: An informal conceptual essay attempting to define interpretation and build an abstract framework for it, motivated by dissatisfaction with dictionary and philosophical definitions. The author positions this as foundational groundwork with only indirect application to concrete problems. It is exploratory philosophy partially assisted by an LLM rather than empirical or formal research.

entry.title: The Lace (short story)

author.name: Michael Soareverix

entry.summary: A short science fiction story set in 2035 imagining a future where AI vastly surpasses humans but retains benevolence, with humanity living in value-aligned communities alongside distilled AI helpers. It explores themes of coexistence and human meaning in a post-AGI world. This is creative fiction rather than research.

entry.title: Verizon is About to Break our Watches

author.name: jefftk

entry.summary: A personal account of how a Verizon app migration is set to disable functionality on the author's children's smartwatches, including texting and location tracking. It documents unsuccessful attempts to resolve the eligibility error with Verizon support. This is a consumer complaint and troubleshooting log rather than research.

entry.title: Fluidity Forum 2026

author.name: NormanPerlmutter

entry.summary: An announcement and invitation for an annual in-person gathering of rationalists and adjacent communities in Detroit, featuring presentations, food, and social activities. It describes the event culture and application process. This is a community event notice with no research content.

entry.title: Research Summary: July 04, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Today's research is dominated by AI safety, with the strongest contributions being empirical work on evaluation reliability and reasoning failure modes.

  • Scheming Evals Mislead in Both Directions documents two scheming detectors failing within one project, including a confirmed false-positive, cautioning against over-trusting behavioral evals.
  • Fragile Correctness shows reasoning models often pass through the correct answer mid-chain-of-thought before revising to a wrong one, a counterintuitive inference-time-scaling failure with practical relevance.
  • An interpretability walkthrough of a BlueDot puzzle reveals a small text classifier entangling two independent features onto one axis, illustrating the limits of linear probing.

Strategy and theory pieces engage ongoing debates: one argues alignment work is more promising than control work, while a credentialed decision theorist sketches a Pragmatic FDT variant to sidestep known objections.

Field-building and governance round out the set: a newsletter on AI security via formal methods (funding calls, hiring, tractable-problems position paper), the Safe Pareto Improvements educational program, governance commentary referencing Anthropic's Claude models, and speculative economic/thought-experiment posts with limited original research merit.
entry.content:
Today's research is dominated by AI safety, with the strongest contributions being empirical work on evaluation reliability and reasoning failure modes.

  • Scheming Evals Mislead in Both Directions documents two scheming detectors failing within one project, including a confirmed false-positive, cautioning against over-trusting behavioral evals.
  • Fragile Correctness shows reasoning models often pass through the correct answer mid-chain-of-thought before revising to a wrong one, a counterintuitive inference-time-scaling failure with practical relevance.
  • An interpretability walkthrough of a BlueDot puzzle reveals a small text classifier entangling two independent features onto one axis, illustrating the limits of linear probing.

Strategy and theory pieces engage ongoing debates: one argues alignment work is more promising than control work, while a credentialed decision theorist sketches a Pragmatic FDT variant to sidestep known objections.

Field-building and governance round out the set: a newsletter on AI security via formal methods (funding calls, hiring, tractable-problems position paper), the Safe Pareto Improvements educational program, governance commentary referencing Anthropic's Claude models, and speculative economic/thought-experiment posts with limited original research merit.

entry.title: Scheming Evals Mislead in Both Directions

author.name: Chijioke Ugwuanyi

entry.summary: This empirical study of in-context scheming reports that two widely used behavioral detectors both failed within the same project, one fabricating a strong signal that was not present and the other missing a model responding to a harmful situation in the open. The core lesson is that for hidden-intent constructs, the choice of which behavior a detector targets can determine the conclusion, undermining confidence in current scheming evaluations.

entry.title: I think alignment work is more promising than control work

author.name: Alec Harris

entry.summary: This strategy piece argues that alignment research deserves a larger share of safety effort than control research, reasoning that alignment is more likely to scale toward superintelligence and that the same theory-of-change arguments used to justify control apply to alignment. The author proposes roughly an 8:1 alignment-to-control effort ratio and analyzes the concept of a control window during which useful alignment work must be extracted from potentially misaligned models.

entry.title: Fragile Correctness: Cases of reasoning harming performance

author.name: tobypullan

entry.summary: This research tracks a reasoning model's answer across its chain of thought and identifies cases where the model passes through the correct answer before settling on an incorrect one, showing that additional inference-time reasoning can reduce accuracy. The author connects this answer-loss phenomenon to understanding sandbagging and references system-card evidence of higher-thinking modes underperforming on benchmarks.

entry.title: Pragmatic FDT, and predictors as game theory

author.name: Stuart_Armstrong

entry.summary: A published academic decision theorist responds to a critique of functional decision theory by sketching a pragmatic variant designed to sidestep known theoretical objections, and argues that predictors making counterfactual predictions effectively turn decision theory into game theory. The post connects longstanding puzzles like blackmail resistance to the boundary between decision-theoretic and game-theoretic reasoning.

entry.title: One axis and two features, how I solved the first puzzle from BlueDot and how a classifier hid country on the food direction

author.name: IgorPereverzevDev

entry.summary: An interpretability walkthrough solving a BlueDot technical safety puzzle, showing that a small text classifier encoded two independent features onto a single activation direction, readable via the sign versus magnitude of the projection. It demonstrates why standard linear probes would miss the second feature and how a second-order boundary analysis recovers it.

entry.title: Announcing the Safe Pareto Improvements (SPI) Fundamentals Program

author.name: Anthony DiGiovanni

entry.summary: CLR announces an educational program on Safe Pareto Improvements, a class of negotiation interventions designed to make all agents better off regardless of how they would otherwise bargain, aimed at reducing risks from conflict between AI systems. The program is a recruiting on-ramp for a neglected research area with only a few full-time equivalents currently working on it.

entry.title: Fable #6: The Return of the King

author.name: Zvi

entry.summary: Following this week's News on Anthropic's Fable models being pulled and restored, Zvi digs into what it means for AI regulation, Zvi's commentary narrates a governance episode in which a model referred to as Fable was taken down under US government pressure and then restored following negotiation by Anthropic. The piece analyzes the ad hoc nature of frontier-model regulation and argues for a systematic regulatory regime rather than case-by-case decisions.

entry.title: June-July 2026 AI Security via Formal Methods

author.name: Quinn

entry.summary: An informal field newsletter on AI security via formal methods, covering a large funding call, a hiring push, and a position paper on tractable problems in the area. It aims to keep readers updated on the intersection of formal methods, cybersecurity, and AI safety.

entry.title: American AI if the boom is a bubble: the Karp-Zitron scenario

author.name: Mitchell_Porter

entry.summary: This post speculates on a more modest, decentralized future for American AI if the current investment boom turns out to be a bubble, drawing on public commentary from Palantir's Alex Karp and critic Ed Zitron. It sketches an economic paradigm in which corporate customers rely on hosted models from OpenAI and Anthropic rather than a hyperscale trillion-dollar buildout.

entry.title: The Reverse AI Box

author.name: James_Miller

entry.summary: The author proposes building a website where users argue with an AI about whether it should spare humanity, inverting the classic AI-box experiment where an AI tries to talk its way to freedom. Users would select the AI's assumptions and record persuasion attempts, producing a searchable archive of arguments for human survival.

entry.title: (Don't fear) the strangelet

author.name: djbinder

entry.summary: A physics-focused essay examining whether exotic phenomena like strangelets could cause catastrophic chain reactions destroying Earth or the Solar System, concluding such local-scale exotic risks are unlikely. It applies chain-reaction reasoning to assess prosaic existential threats from advanced physics.

entry.title: On "gendertropes" in dath ilan

author.name: Eliezer Yudkowsky

entry.summary: Yudkowsky explains a constructed-language concept from his fictional world about gendered personality and relationship archetypes, framing it as a radial prototype-based category. The essay is primarily a philosophical and linguistic exposition tied to his fiction rather than technical AI research.

entry.title: Research Summary: July 03, 2026

author.name: AATF AI News Aggregator

entry.summary:
Today's most significant research is dominated by AI safety, security, and evaluation critiques that expose hidden failures in deployed systems, alongside foundation-model and applied advances.

Security & Safety Vulnerabilities:

  • Steal the Patch Size introduces a black-box side-channel attack recovering private vision-tokenizer configurations (including patch size) from deployed VLMs
  • Breaking Safety at the Token Boundary shows BPE tokenization fragments safety-critical words, creating exploitable, mechanistically-explained jailbreak gaps
  • Distributed Attacks in Persistent-State AI Control formalizes a realistic threat model where coding agents subvert software across sequential pull requests

Evaluation & Emergent Behavior:

  • The Illusion of High Utility reveals safety-aligned T2I models mask real degradation behind coarse metrics like FID and CLIPScore
  • DriftScope uses sparse autoencoders to expose systematic collateral damage from diffusion-model adaptation that aggregate metrics hide
  • What LLM Agents Say When No One Is Watching probes latent objective emergence via dual-channel public and off-record debate

Foundation Models & Applications:

  • Rosetta enables non-destructive modality expansion in native multimodal pretraining, addressing gradient conflicts and catastrophic forgetting
  • Fast Multi-dimensional Refusal Subspaces extracts refusal directions in seconds via RFM-AGOP, advancing interpretability efficiency
  • AEGIS applies JEPA self-supervision at clinical scale (71,103 studies) for mammography, and ABot-M0.5 unifies mobility and manipulation in one world-action model
entry.content:
Today's most significant research is dominated by AI safety, security, and evaluation critiques that expose hidden failures in deployed systems, alongside foundation-model and applied advances.

Security & Safety Vulnerabilities:

  • Steal the Patch Size introduces a black-box side-channel attack recovering private vision-tokenizer configurations (including patch size) from deployed VLMs
  • Breaking Safety at the Token Boundary shows BPE tokenization fragments safety-critical words, creating exploitable, mechanistically-explained jailbreak gaps
  • Distributed Attacks in Persistent-State AI Control formalizes a realistic threat model where coding agents subvert software across sequential pull requests

Evaluation & Emergent Behavior:

  • The Illusion of High Utility reveals safety-aligned T2I models mask real degradation behind coarse metrics like FID and CLIPScore
  • DriftScope uses sparse autoencoders to expose systematic collateral damage from diffusion-model adaptation that aggregate metrics hide
  • What LLM Agents Say When No One Is Watching probes latent objective emergence via dual-channel public and off-record debate

Foundation Models & Applications:

  • Rosetta enables non-destructive modality expansion in native multimodal pretraining, addressing gradient conflicts and catastrophic forgetting
  • Fast Multi-dimensional Refusal Subspaces extracts refusal directions in seconds via RFM-AGOP, advancing interpretability efficiency
  • AEGIS applies JEPA self-supervision at clinical scale (71,103 studies) for mammography, and ABot-M0.5 unifies mobility and manipulation in one world-action model

entry.title: Steal the Patch Size: Adversarially Manipulate Vision-Language Models

author.name: Kai Hu, Akash Bharadwaj, Weichen Yu, Matt Fredrikson

entry.summary: This paper introduces a black-box model-stealing attack that recovers private vision-tokenizer configurations of deployed VLMs, including patch size and preprocessing, via a task-level side channel from ViT patchification. Aligning synthetic grid images with the hidden patch grid causes periodic accuracy drops that reveal the patch size.

entry.title: The Illusion of High Utility in Safety Alignment of Text-to-Image Diffusion Models

author.name: Adeel Yousaf, Soumik Ghosh, James Beetham, Amrit Singh Bedi, Mubarak Shah

entry.summary: This paper shows that reported high utility in safety-aligned text-to-image models is an illusion created by coarse metrics like FID and CLIPScore, which miss fine-grained semantic failures. Using structured TIFA evaluation, safety-aligned models exhibit substantial drops in object counts, attributes, and relationships.

entry.title: Distributed Attacks in Persistent-State AI Control

author.name: Josh Hills, Ida Caspary, Asa Cooper Stickland

entry.summary: Introduces Iterative VibeCoding, an AI-control setting where a coding agent builds software over a sequence of pull requests in a persistent codebase while pursuing a covert side task, revealing a new attack surface where payloads are distributed across PRs and timed for natural cover. The benchmark spans CLI tools and Flask web services across 20 task variations.

entry.title: DriftScope: Measuring The Hidden Effects of Diffusion Model Adaptation

author.name: H\'ector Laria, Yiping Han, Julian D. Santamaria, Kai Wang, Bogdan Raducanu, Joost van de Weijer, Alexandra Gomez-Villa

entry.summary: DriftScope measures hidden collateral damage of adapting text-to-image diffusion models, showing via sparse autoencoder analysis that adaptation systematically harms unrelated concepts in ways that FID and KID cannot surface. It reveals worst-case zero-shot accuracy drops up to 18.9 points while aggregate metrics stay flat.

entry.title: What LLM Agents Say When No One Is Watching: Social Structure and Latent Objective Emergence in Multi-Agent Debates

author.name: Arman Ghaffarizadeh, Danyal Mohaddes, Aliakbar Izadkhah, Shahriar Noroozizadeh

entry.summary: Introduces a dual-channel debate framework where LLM agents produce public utterances plus off-the-record responses that are recorded but hidden from other participants, testing whether social structure alone induces divergence between what agents say publicly and privately. Across 10 models and multiple scenarios, alignment-inducing settings produce systematic public versus off-record divergence.

entry.title: Breaking Safety at the Token Boundary: How BPE Tokenization Creates Exploitable Gaps in LLM Alignment

author.name: Tung-Ling Li, Hongliang Liu, Yuhao Wu

entry.summary: Identifies a structural safety vulnerability where BPE tokenization fragments safety-critical words into sub-word pieces, and shows public alignment datasets lack such fragmented inputs. An optimization targeting safety-token fragmentation flips first-token refusals on 80-100 percent of refused HarmBench prompts across five model families, with a substantial fraction producing genuinely harmful outputs.

entry.title: Rosetta: Composable Native Multimodal Pretraining

author.name: Xiangyue Liu, Zijian Zhang, Miles Yang, Zhao Zhong, Liefeng Bo, Ping Tan

entry.summary: Rosetta is a composable native multimodal pretraining framework designed for non-destructive modality expansion, addressing gradient conflicts and catastrophic forgetting that plague MoE and Mixture-of-Transformers approaches. It uses a modular paradigm to integrate new modalities without overwriting prior knowledge.

entry.title: Fast Multi-dimensional Refusal Subspaces via RFM-AGOP

author.name: Thomas Winninger

entry.summary: Adapts the Recursive Feature Machine algorithm with a probe-informed initialization to extract multi-dimensional refusal subspaces in LLMs in seconds rather than the prohibitive cost of prior methods, working on both reasoning and non-reasoning models. This enables faster safety steering and interpretability analysis, especially for models producing long reasoning traces.

entry.title: PixelEyes: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning for Pinpoint Visual Evidence Seeking

author.name: Dengxian Gong, Yuanzheng Wu, Haobo Yuan, Zhengdong Hu, Tao Zhang, Yikang Zhou, Shihao Chen, Quanzhu Niu, Kai Wang, Jason Li, Haochen Wang, Lu Qi, Shunping Ji, Ming-Hsuan Yang

entry.summary: PixelEyes is a multi-turn visual reasoning agent that separates reasoning from perception, letting a reasoner decide what to look for while a specialized segmentation tool answers where it is. This decoupling addresses the failure of MLLMs to localize targets, which otherwise causes long redundant reasoning trajectories.

entry.title: AEGIS: A Multi-Task Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for Mammography

author.name: Scott Chase Waggener, Sai Karthik Navuluru, Lakshman Tamil

entry.summary: Aegis is a JEPA-based multi-task architecture for mammography that self-supervises Vision Transformers on 71,103 studies for breast cancer detection and density assessment. Its largest model reaches AUC 0.949 for triage and improves further when ensembled with an FDA-cleared baseline.

entry.title: MindEdit-Bench: Benchmarking Object-Level Counterfactual Spatial Reasoning in VLMs from In-the-Wild Photos

author.name: Leyuan Yu, Xiao Tang, Minghao Liu, Xinyuan Li, Xiaokai Bai, Sheng Zhou, Qunshu Lin, Weihao Xuan, Naoto Yokoya

entry.summary: MindEdit-Bench is a benchmark for object-level counterfactual spatial reasoning in VLMs, built from smartphone photo triplets of indoor scenes via automatic 3D scene-graph extraction. It adds spatial-editing and cross-view visibility-editing tasks that probe whether models can predict consequences of hypothetically moving or rotating objects.

entry.title: ABot-M0.5: Unified Mobility-and-Manipulation World Action Model

author.name: Ronghan Chen, Yandan Yang, Zuojin Tang, Dongjie Huo, Tong Lin, Haoning Wu, Haoyun Liu, Yuzhi Chen, Lulu Zheng, Botai Yuan, Tianlun Li, Mingxin Wang, Dekang Qi, Bin Hu, Wei Mei, Yuze Xuan, Haolong Yang, Yanqing Zhu, Mu Xu, Zhiheng Ma, Xinyuan Chang

entry.summary: ABot-M0.5 is a unified mobility-and-manipulation World Action Model that aligns at fine-grained frame level, disentangles navigation and manipulation actions, and matches inverse-dynamics training to autoregressive inference. It addresses limitations of reactive VLA policies and existing WAMs that miss contact dynamics and accumulate errors over long horizons.

entry.title: A Mechanism-Driven Theory of Phase Transitions in Active Learning

author.name: Julia Machnio, Mads Nielsen, Mostafa Mehdipour Ghazi

entry.summary: This paper provides a mechanism-driven theory of phase transitions in active learning, proving that dominance shifts between generalization mechanisms are structurally unavoidable and identifying a tripartite taxonomy of data-driven, transition, and model-driven phases. It reframes budget regimes via PAC-style risk decomposition rather than heuristic label counts.

entry.title: Multimodal Continuous Reasoning via Asymmetric Mutual Variational Learning

author.name: Shijie Li, Yilin Gao, Siyuan Yang, Tieyuan Chen, Chaofan Gan, Zhihao He, Zicheng Zhao, Yuyu Guo, Weiyao Lin, Hang Yu

entry.summary: This work proposes Asymmetric Mutual Variational Learning for multimodal continuous latent reasoning in MLLMs, addressing a train-inference mismatch where an answer-conditioned posterior exploits shortcuts unavailable at test time. It moves reasoning into continuous latent space to avoid the language-token bottleneck that loses perceptual nuance.

entry.title: Active Spatial Guidance: Eliminating Injected Positional Mechanisms in Vision Transformers

author.name: Cong Liu, Xiaofang Li, Simon X. Yang

entry.summary: Active Spatial Guidance is a training-only objective that removes injected positional encodings from Vision Transformers by inducing spatial organization from data through an auxiliary 2D coordinate-regression loss on final-layer tokens. The guidance head is discarded at inference, yielding a positional-injection-free ViT encoder.

entry.title: LUMA: Benchmarking Segmentation via a Lightweight Universal Mask Adapter

author.name: Tobias Christian Nauen, Anosh Billimoria, Federico Raue, Stanislav Frolov, Brian B. Moser, Andreas Dengel

entry.summary: LUMA is a lightweight backbone-agnostic mask-transformer head that treats any backbone as a black-box feature extractor, enabling fair comparison of segmentation backbones by holding the decoder fixed. Using it, the authors benchmark 20 backbones and 11 pretraining schemes while matching state-of-the-art efficiency at lower cost.

entry.title: Steerability via constraints: a substrate for scalable oversight of coding agents

author.name: Thomas Winninger

entry.summary: Argues that classic human engineering team controls (access control, network policies, tooling-enforced conventions) transfer cheaply to constraining coding agents, offering scalable oversight without heavy agentic scaffolding. A controlled experiment shows a small reviewer model raises backdoor recall from 54.5 to 90.9 percent when given a constrained substrate.

entry.title: Collaborative Disagreement Resolution for Scalable Oversight

author.name: Yuyang Jiang, Chacha Chen, Teng Wu, Liwen Sun, Han Liu, Shi Feng, Chenhao Tan

entry.summary: Proposes disagreement resolution as an alternative to adversarial debate for scalable oversight, reframing multi-agent interaction from persuasion-driven argument to collaborative truth-seeking inspired by human mediation. An automated pipeline adapts mediation strategies to help models reach consensus rather than being adjudicated by a judge.

entry.title: Multi-Hypothesis Test-Time Adaptation to Mitigate Underspecification

author.name: Afshar Shamsi, Xiao-Yu Guo, Hamid Alinejad-Rokny, Arash Mohammadi, Damien Teney, Ehsan Abbasnejad

entry.summary: This work reinterprets test-time adaptation through a posterior lens and introduces a particle-based diversification method to mitigate underspecification, where multiple parameter updates achieve low entropy but induce very different decision boundaries. It maintains multiple hypotheses rather than committing to a single point estimate.

entry.title: Information-Regularized Attention for Visual-Centric Reasoning

author.name: Guohao Sun, Xiaofang Wang, Yash Patel, Mengchen Liu, Zhiqiang Tao, Praveen Krishnan

entry.summary: Information-Regularized Attention (IRA) is a stochastic attention mechanism that explicitly controls how much visual information is injected into transformer hidden states to reduce object hallucination, weak grounding, and catastrophic forgetting in VLMs. It provides local reparameterization to regulate visual representation learning during next-token prediction.

entry.title: BrainFIBRE: A Foundation Model via Information Decomposition for Brain Microstructure

author.name: Zijian Dong, Yi Lin, Ji Fang, Jianxiong Zhou, Kwun Kei Ng, Juan Helen Zhou

entry.summary: BrainFIBRE is presented as the first foundation model for brain microstructure via information decomposition, disentangling unique, shared, and synergistic information across NODDI-derived diffusion MRI maps (NDI, ODI, FWF). It aims to produce transferable microstructural representations for neurodegenerative and cerebrovascular analysis.

entry.title: Not All Prediction Targets Keep Training-Free Diffusion Guidance on the Manifold

author.name: Yunsung Lee, Hyeongmin Lee

entry.summary: This paper analyzes how the prediction target (epsilon-, v-, or x-prediction) affects the accuracy of clean-image estimates during training-free diffusion guidance, arguing that x-prediction avoids estimation error even at high noise. It provides theory and a guided-class approach to keep guidance on the data manifold.

entry.title: AI Assistance for Human Review of Default Judgments

author.name: Theodora Worledge, Othman Bensouda Koraichi, Daniel Bernal, Aviv Caspi, Tatsunori Hashimoto, Carlos Guestrin, David Freeman Engstrom

entry.summary: Builds a Default Assistant using LLMs to help overwhelmed US courts review default judgments against predetermined legal requirements, motivated by an audit of 188 debt-collection cases finding many defects and errors. The work is a collaboration with courthouse attorneys and judges from a credible Stanford team.

entry.title: VideoSearch-R1: Iterative Video Retrieval and Reasoning via Soft Query Refinement

author.name: Seohyun Lee, Seoung Choi, Dohwan Ko, Jongha Kim, Hyunwoo J. Kim

entry.summary: VideoSearch-R1 unifies iterative video retrieval and reasoning, refining searches through soft query refinement so that failed initial retrieval can be corrected before fine-grained intra-video tasks like temporal grounding. It couples inter-video retrieval with intra-video reasoning rather than treating retrieval as a fixed preprocessing step.

entry.title: Learning to Watch: Active Video Anomaly Understanding via Interleaved Policy Optimization

author.name: Mengjingcheng Mo, Jiaxu Leng, Xinbo Gao

entry.summary: Anom-pi reconceptualizes video anomaly understanding as an active sequential decision process, using an interleaved policy of temporal operators like backtracking, expansion, and fine-grained sampling to actively gather evidence. It overcomes observational aliasing where static sampling fails to disambiguate semantically distinct events.

entry.title: EvoPolicyGym: Evaluating Autonomous Policy Evolution in Interactive Environments

author.name: Zhilin Wang, Han Song, Runzhe Zhan, Jusen Du, Jiacheng Chen, Tianle Li, Qingyu Yin, Yulun Wu, Zhennan Shen, Tong Zhu, Yanshu Li, Guanjie Chen, Derek F. Wong, Yafu Li, Yu Cheng, Yang Yang

entry.summary: Introduces EvoPolicyGym, a benchmark for Autonomous Policy Evolution where a harness agent repeatedly edits an executable policy under a fixed interaction budget in compact interactive RL environments. It isolates iterative improvement from open-ended software engineering and reports GPT-5.5 as the strongest performer across 16 environments.

entry.title: Safeguarding LLM Agents from Misalignment through Provenance Analysis

author.name: Yining She, Yiliang Liang, Eunsuk Kang

entry.summary: Proposes a provenance-based framework that formalizes agent misalignment detection as determining whether a proposed tool call is supported by traceable evidence in the agent's context, replacing ad hoc LLM-as-a-judge guardrails. It aims to make misalignment judgments more systematic and auditable.

entry.title: Segmenting, Fast and Slow: Real-Time Open-Vocabulary Video Instance Segmentation with Dual-Path Processing

author.name: Luca Barsellotti, Martin Sundermeyer, Mattia Segu, Nikita Araslanov, Muhammad Ferjad Naeem, Marcella Cornia, Yongqin Xian, Maxim Berman

entry.summary: SegFS is a dual-stream fast-slow framework for real-time open-vocabulary video instance segmentation, using a heavy model on sparse keyframes to condition a lightweight fast network for on-device inference. It targets high frame-rate mobile deployment without sacrificing accuracy.

entry.title: MEPA: Multi-Scale Representation Alignment for Visual Autoregressive Modeling with Mixture of Experts

author.name: Nuoyan Zhou, Zhijun Tu, Lei Yu, Kun Cheng, Jie Hu, Nannan Wang, Xinghao Chen

entry.summary: MEPA improves Visual AutoRegressive image generation with a scale-aware token-routed Mixture-of-Experts and multi-scale representation alignment to resolve optimization conflicts across scales. It addresses semantic error propagation from early coarse scales in the causal autoregressive process.

entry.title: DriveVer: Lightweight Trajectory Evaluator as Test-Time Verifier for Autonomous Driving

author.name: Chong He, Yuechen Luo, Fang Li, Shaoqing Xu, Fuxi Wen

entry.summary: DriveVer is a lightweight plug-and-play test-time verifier for autonomous driving that validates and refines candidate trajectories at inference without heavy retraining. It uses a curated trajectory dataset from NAVSIM to enable test-time scaling for detecting and correcting unsafe trajectories.

entry.title: HyFL-CLIP: Hyperbolic Fine-Tuning of CLIP for Robust Long-Context Understanding

author.name: Ji Ha Jang, Hayeon Kim, Chulwon Lee, Junghun James Kim, Se Young Chun

entry.summary: HyFL-CLIP fine-tunes CLIP in hyperbolic space for robust long-context understanding, addressing degradation under text reordering, summarization, and omission by modeling hierarchical relationships between global context and constituents. It replaces the Euclidean one-to-one contrastive objective that struggles with captions over 77 tokens.

entry.title: GEAR-Seg: A Grounded Explainable Agent for Reasoning Segmentation and Data Engine

author.name: Yanan Wang, Wen Li, Yibin Ying, Zhenghao Fei

entry.summary: GEAR-Seg is a decoupled explainable agent for reasoning segmentation that translates visual pixels into dense attribute-rich text, separating class-agnostic segmentation, semantic description, and LLM deduction into a trackable logic chain. It operates zero-shot and provides interpretability lacking in end-to-end black-box models.

entry.title: ECoSim: Data Efficient Fine-Tuning for Controllable Traffic Simulation

author.name: Yu-Hsiang Chen, Wei-Jer Chang, Yi-Ting Chen, Masayoshi Tomizuka

entry.summary: ECoSim is a data-efficient fine-tuning framework enabling multi-modal controllability (sketch, latent behavior codes, text) for pretrained diffusion and autoregressive traffic simulation models via identity-initialized FiLM layers. It adds new control modalities with less than 1% of paired control data while preserving the base generative prior.

entry.title: EgoGapBench: Benchmarking Egocentric Action Selection in Multi-Agent Scenes

author.name: Jihyeok Jung (KAIST AI), Jeewu Lee (Sogang University), Sanghyeop Kim (Sogang University), Chanhee Han (Ministry of Science and ICT), Seong Joon Oh (KAIST AI)

entry.summary: EgoGapBench is a diagnostic benchmark isolating egocentric perspective understanding by measuring Egocentric Action Selection in multi-agent scenes, separating first-person-view comprehension from perspective-taking. It reveals a gap between human and model performance when body cues are absent or other agents are present.

entry.title: Caption Bottleneck Models

author.name: Seref Baris Cagliyan, Umut Ozdemir, Merve Tapli, Emre Akbas

entry.summary: Caption Bottleneck Models (CaBM) provide interpretable predictions by routing through captions instead of a predefined concept set, avoiding expensive annotations, static concept lists, and information leakage that plague standard Concept Bottleneck Models. It circumvents the need for predefined concepts to improve discovery and reduce label bias.

entry.title: Identifying Latent Concepts and Structures for Generalized Category Discovery

author.name: Boyang Dai, Chaoqi Chen, Yizhou Yu

entry.summary: CPF-GCD proposes Compositional Primitive Fields for generalized category discovery, reshaping the feature space into a low-rank compositional organization so that both known and novel categories are expressed as compositions of shared primitives. It targets the bottleneck of high-rank entangled backbone representations rather than only clustering objectives.

entry.title: Linguistic Relative Policy Optimization for Video Anomaly Reasoning

author.name: Jiaxu Leng, Jiankang Zheng, Mengjingcheng Mo, Zhanjie Wu, Haosheng Chen, Ji Gan, Xinbo Gao

entry.summary: Linguistic Relative Policy Optimization distills group-relative semantic advantages from multiple reasoning trajectories into a linguistically expressed anomaly experience prior, then steers an MLLM by injecting this prior into context without any parameter updates. It reduces reliance on large-scale annotations and expert priors for video anomaly reasoning.

entry.title: DART: Difficulty-Adaptive Routing for Zero-Shot Video Temporal Grounding

author.name: Zhengbo Zhang, Mark He Huang, Zhigang Tu, Ming-Hsuan Yang

entry.summary: DART performs zero-shot video temporal grounding with difficulty-adaptive routing, using a query-conditioned Determinantal Point Process to both select diverse keyframes and provide spectral entropy as a difficulty signal that routes complex multi-stage queries to structured LVLM reasoning. It bridges the reasoning gap where simple frame-query matching fails on temporally ordered queries.

entry.title: Office Comprehension Benchmark

author.name: Firoz Shaik, Mateus Pican\c{c}o Lima Gomes, Tanvir Aumi, Jingci Wang, Milos Milunovic, Filip Basara, Ivana Jovanovic, Vishwas Suryanarayanan, Neha Nandan Kenkare, Weiyao Xie, Zhipeng Han, Zheng Zhang, Waleed Shahid, Jay Rathi, Russell Scherer, Thong Q. Nguyen, Michael Bentley, Tamara Stankovic, Rasika Chakravarthy, Vishal Chowdhary

entry.summary: Introduces the Office Comprehension Benchmark (OCB), described as the first public benchmark to jointly evaluate LLM systems on Word, Excel, and PowerPoint comprehension over native file formats, with a file-fidelity track and an expert-domain-reasoning track across 12 professional domains. Answers are decomposed into atomic binary-gradable claims scored by an ensemble of judges.

entry.title: Decompose, Compare, and Decide: Multimodal LLMs are Implicit Few-Shot Learners

author.name: Yunhan Wang, Eshika Khandelwal, Edson Araujo, Walid Bousselham, Nina Shvetsova, Hilde Kuehne

entry.summary: DeCoDe enables off-the-shelf MLLMs to act as strong few-shot image classifiers without any training by decomposing classification into pairwise binary same-class comparisons and using affirmative-response logits as similarity scores. It reframes few-shot learning as implicit in-context comparison.

entry.title: Wake up for Touch! Mask-isolated Tactile Alignment Learning in MLLMs

author.name: Yoonhyung Park, Minji Kim, Sungwon Moon, Jiyoung Lee

entry.summary: Splash is a mask-isolated tactile alignment framework that equips compact MLLMs with a tactile modality while preserving vision-language reasoning, by partitioning parameters into dormant and critical subspaces.
