The frozen critical subspace anchors general knowledge while dormant parameters learn touch.

entry.title: LIST3R: Long-sequence Instance-aware 3D Reconstruction

author.name: Jing Gao, Wei Wang, Feiran Wang, Yan Yan

entry.summary: LIST3R is an instance-aware framework for long-sequence 3D reconstruction that organizes reconstruction around persistent instance anchors to reconnect fragmented subsequences into a coherent global scene. It mimics how humans organize spatial memory around stable recognizable objects.

entry.title: EO-VGGT: Orbital Ray-Conditioned 3D Foundation Models for Satellite Multi-View Reconstruction

author.name: Qiyan Luo, Yingdong Pi, Lekang Wen, Jie Yang, Xiaoyu Wang, Haiming Zhang, Mi Wang

entry.summary: EO-VGGT adapts a frozen perspective-driven 3D foundation model to orbital satellite pushbroom geometry via explicit physical geometry embedding for multi-view satellite reconstruction and DSM generation. It handles the structural mismatch between implicit perspective assumptions and orbital imaging.

entry.title: Retrieved Images as Visual Thought: Training-Free Multimodal In-Context Learning for the Open-vs-Closed Gap

author.name: Bingchen Huang, Zhiling Wang, Yifu Chen, Yuanchao Du

entry.summary: ReVisIT is a train-free multimodal in-context learning framework that treats retrieved labeled exemplar images as units of visual thought, reasoning over them without generating imagery or invoking tools. It combines structured class definitions, per-query multimodal retrieval, and alternating injection to close the open-vs-closed model gap.

entry.title: Imprint: Online Memory Compression for Long-Horizon Egocentric QA

author.name: Kousik Das, Debaditya Roy

entry.summary: Imprint is an interaction-centric online memory compression framework for long-horizon egocentric question answering that preserves recurring interactions as explicit memory units rather than absorbing them into coarse textual summaries. It optimizes memory for retrieval effectiveness and scalability over days or weeks of recording.

entry.title: Does Your ViT Still Need U-Net for Segmentation?

author.name: Xin Li, Wenhui Zhu, Xuanzhao Dong, Xiwen Chen, Yanxi Chen, Yujian Xiong, Hao Wang, Oana M. Dumitrascu, Yalin Wang

entry.summary: This paper questions whether U-Net decoders are still needed for medical image segmentation given modern large-scale ViT pretraining, exploring encoder-only segmentation frameworks. It investigates how to design dense prediction without U-Net-style decoders.

entry.title: OnPoint: Offline-to-Online Multi-Level Distillation for Point-Supervised Online Temporal Action Localization

author.name: Sakib Reza, Gauri Jagatap, Mohsen Moghaddam, Octavia Camps, Andrea Fanelli

entry.summary: OnPoint introduces point-supervised online temporal action localization (POTAL), localizing streaming actions with only one temporal point per instance via offline-to-online multi-level distillation. It transfers knowledge through pseudo-segment, class-activation-sequence, and anticipatory window distillation.

entry.title: Vitality-Aware Compression for Efficient Image-to-Shape Diffusion Transformers

author.name: Jaeah Lee, Hyunjin Kim, Jaewoong Cho, Gihyun Kwon

entry.summary: This work presents the first compression approach for image-to-shape Diffusion Transformers, reducing model size while preserving geometric fidelity via a geometry-aware framework that exploits non-uniform layer importance. It targets backbone compression rather than only inference speed for 3D generation.

entry.title: MindAU: EEG-Conditioned Facial Action Unit Editing via Dual-Stream Manifold Alignment

author.name: Zhenhang Li, Xin Zhou, Hao Deng, Lijun Yin

entry.summary: MindAU is a framework for EEG-conditioned facial action unit editing, learning noise-robust AU-discriminative EEG representations and grounding them in localized identity-preserving expression edits via dual-stream manifold alignment. It explores editing facial expressions from brain signals rather than reconstructing perceived content.

entry.title: StochasT: Learning with Stochastic Turn Depth for Visual Instruction Tuning

author.name: Yuan Qing, Chengzhi Mao, Boqing Gong

entry.summary: StochasT introduces learning with stochastic turn depth for visual instruction tuning, randomly grouping same-image language tasks into clusters of varying sizes to counter visual attention decay and contextual overfitting from multi-turn training. It closes the mismatch between multi-turn training and single-turn evaluation of LVLMs.

entry.title: EPO: Boosting 3D Foundation Models with Edge-based Pose Optimization

author.name: Mattia D'Urso, Christian Sormann, Mattia Rossi, Friedrich Fraundorfer

entry.summary: EPO is a trackless edge-based pose optimization framework that boosts Structure-from-Motion reconstructions from 3D foundation models without extracting feature tracks, preserving their speed advantage. It uses a fully differentiable edge-based refinement to close the geometric accuracy gap with traditional bundle-adjustment pipelines.

entry.title: G-RRM: Guiding Symbolic Solvers with Recurrent Reasoning Models

author.name: Timo Bertram, Sidhant Bhavnani, Richard Freinschlag, Erich Kobler, Andreas Mayr, G\"unter Klambauer

entry.summary: Presents G-RRM, a neuro-symbolic method where symbol-equivariant recurrent reasoning models generate solution proposals to guide classical symbolic solvers (backtracking, SAT solvers) for constraint satisfaction problems. It investigates when neural guidance actually improves symbolic search efficiency.

entry.title: ReContext: Recursive Evidence Replay as LLM Harness for Long-Context Reasoning

author.name: Yanjun Zhao, Ruizhong Qiu, Tianxin Wei, Yuanchen Bei, Zhining Liu, Lingjie Chen, Ismini Lourentzou, Hanghang Tong, Jingrui He

entry.summary: Proposes RECONTEXT, a training-free inference method that uses model-internal relevance signals to build a query-conditioned evidence pool and replays it before final generation while preserving the full context. It targets the gap where LLMs have long context windows but fail to actually use relevant evidence already in the input.

entry.title: Online Safety Monitoring for LLMs

author.name: Mona Schirmer, Metod Jazbec, Alexander Timans, Christian Naesseth, Maja Waldron, Eric Nalisnick

entry.summary: Studies a simple real-time output monitor for LLMs that thresholds a verifier signal from an external model into an alarm decision, calibrating the threshold via risk control. On math reasoning and red-teaming datasets it matches more complex sequential hypothesis-testing monitors.

entry.title: Kara: Efficient Reasoning LLM Serving via Sliding-Window KV Cache Compression

author.name: Shen Han, Yuyang Wu

entry.summary: Introduces Kara, a reasoning-LLM serving system using sliding-window KV cache compression to reduce the massive KV cache accumulated during long chain-of-thought decoding. It addresses limitations of threshold-triggered compression that can hurt throughput and cause block-level information loss.

entry.title: Progressive Pose-Guided 4D Animal Reconstruction from Monocular Video

author.name: Siyuan Li, Weiying Chen, Yilin Wang, Xinxin Zuo, Xingyu Li, Li Cheng

entry.summary: This work reconstructs 4D animals from monocular video using a progressive test-time optimization framework built on 3D Gaussian Splatting, disentangling articulated pose from non-rigid deformation. A symmetry-aware temporal encoding exploits bilateral cues to work with only a coarse shape prior.

entry.title: VOCA: Visual Odometry with Codec Awareness

author.name: Nouri Alexander Hilscher, Mateo de Mayo, Dominik Muhle, Christoph Otten genannt Hermes, Daniel Cremers

entry.summary: VOCA is a causal stereo visual odometry method that exploits video codec information to improve tracking on compressed video streams, addressing artifacts from lossy compression that hinder traditional systems. It achieves state-of-the-art performance on codec-degraded data.

entry.title: CORGI: Consistency-Aware 3D Dog Reconstruction from a Single Image in the Wild

author.name: Yuxiao Wu, Weile Li, Boyi Zhu, Yumeng Liu, Youcheng Cai, Ligang Liu

entry.summary: CORGI reconstructs high-fidelity 3D dog models from a single in-the-wild image without 3D supervision, using canonical/orbit LoRAs to normalize poses and synthesize 360-degree observations plus a consistency-aware deformable Gaussian Splatting module. It targets highly articulated animals lacking reliable templates.

entry.title: Learning to Compose: Revisiting Proxy Task Design for Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval

author.name: Jingjing Zhang, Lei Zhang, Zheren Fu, Zhendong Mao

entry.summary: FoCo revisits proxy-task design for zero-shot composed image retrieval by making the composition function learnable through two coordinated stages of focusing on modification-relevant visual content and composing. It overcomes the limitation of prior methods that keep composition fixed via pseudo-word injection or feature arithmetic.

entry.title: GenSP: Consistent Spherical Parameterization via Learning Shape Generative Models

author.name: Sai Karthikey Pentapati, Shashank Gupta, Rajesh Sureddi, Yuezhi Yang, Alan C. Bovik, Qixing Huang

entry.summary: GenSP learns consistent spherical parameterizations across collections of genus-0 shapes via a neural generative model that maps the unit sphere to shapes, obtaining parameterizations through inverse mappings. This encourages similar shapes to share consistent parameterizations rather than optimizing each independently.

entry.title: Uncertainty-aware tree height change regression

author.name: Max Gaber, Dimitri Gominski, Jaime C. Revenga, Stefan Oehmcke, Rasmus Fensholt, Martin Brandt

entry.summary: This work presents the Canopy Height Change dataset with 3m-resolution continuous canopy height differences and spatially resolved uncertainties across over 10,000 square kilometers of Spain, paired with an uncertainty-aware regression approach. It reframes canopy monitoring from binary change detection to continuous uncertainty-aware regression.

entry.title: Hardware-Enforced Semantic Coordination for Safety-Critical Real-Time Autonomous Systems

author.name: Uwe M. Borghoff, Paolo Bottoni, Remo Pareschi

entry.summary: Proposes a hardware-enforced coordination architecture for safety-critical agentic AI systems, arguing that software-mediated coordination cannot guarantee the bounded latency and deterministic behavior required in real-time deployments. Selected coordination semantics are pushed into hardware to provide enforceable safety guarantees among heterogeneous components.

entry.title: PRISM-VO: Scale-Aware Visual Odometry Using Photometric Plenoptic Bundle Adjustment

author.name: Aymeric Fleith, Julian Zirbel, Daniel Cremers, Niclas Zeller

entry.summary: PRISM-VO is an optimization-based sparse photometric visual odometry framework for focused plenoptic cameras that jointly optimizes poses and inverse depths via plenoptic bundle adjustment. It achieves metric-scale reconstruction from a single plenoptic sensor, overcoming monocular scale ambiguity.

entry.title: RetailSMV: Exocentric vs. Egocentric Adaptation of Foundation Video World Models in Retail

author.name: Amirreza Rouhi, Rajat Aggarwal, Parikshit Sakurikar, Anoop M. Namboodiri, Sashi P. Reddi

entry.summary: RetailSMV studies parameter-efficient adaptation of foundation video world models to retail scenes, releasing a 32,105-clip corpus with synchronized egocentric and exocentric captures. It investigates which viewpoint of training data yields the strongest adapted world model.

entry.title: SFDATrack: Generalized Source-Free Domain Adaptive Tracking Under Adverse Weather Conditions

author.name: Siyuan Yao, Ziqi Wang, Ruiqi Yu, Junqi Huang, Wenqi Ren, Xiaochun Cao

entry.summary: SFDATrack is a source-free domain adaptive tracker for adverse weather that uses only target-domain samples via a mean-teacher backbone with Dual Interactive Mamba blocks to distill weather-resilient target tokens. It removes the need for source data under rigid resource constraints.

entry.title: GADA: Geometry-Aware Deformable Aggregation for Image-Based Gaussian Splatting

author.name: Siwoo Lim, Sunjae Yoon, Gwanhyeong Koo, Chang D. Yoo

entry.summary: GADA introduces geometry-aware deformable aggregation for image-based Gaussian Splatting, using iterative refinement with deformable offsets to correct spatial misalignments in warped images that disrupt residual learning. It recovers locally displaced visual cues to improve thin structures and high-frequency details.

entry.title: Creating Impactful Autonomous Driving Datasets: A Strategic Guide from Research Gap to Benchmark

author.name: Richard Schwarzkopf, Jonas Merkert, Frank Bieder, Annika B\"atz, Alexander Blumberg, Carlos Fernandez, Felix Hauser, Fabian Immel, Christian Kinzig, Hendrik K\"onigshof, Fabian Konstantinidis, Martin Lauer, Willi Poh, Nils Rack, Kevin R\"osch, Yinzhe Shen, Marlon Steiner, Gleb Stepanov, Dominik Strutz, \"Omer \c{S}ahin Ta\c{s}, Julian Truetsch, Kaiwen Wang, Royden Wagner, Jan-Hendrik Pauls, Christoph Stiller

entry.summary: This position and strategy paper offers a framework for creating impactful autonomous driving datasets by diagnosing whether a research question is blocked by a data problem or an evaluation problem, then selecting the minimal data operator that closes the gap. It analyzes the evolution of major AD datasets to guide resource-constrained labs and startups.

entry.title: Prompt Framing Distorts Count-Based Evaluation of LLM Error Detection: Evidence from Numeric Anchoring

author.name: Dekun Yang

entry.summary: Shows that count-based F1, widely used to measure LLM error-detection quality, can inflate substantially due to prompt framing (numeric anchoring) without any real improvement in span localization. It introduces ErrorBench, a controlled stress test, and reproduces the inflation pattern across six models using official ERRANT scoring.

entry.title: The Rising Unsustainability of AI Graphics Cards Production

author.name: Cl\'ement Morand, Aur\'elie N\'ev\'eol, Anne-Laure Ligozat

entry.summary: Estimates the environmental damages of GPU/graphics-card production over 2013-2025, analyzing energy consumption, carbon emissions, and resource depletion, and compiles a supporting dataset. It addresses the understudied manufacturing (not just operational) footprint of AI hardware.

entry.title: Scaling Laws for Grid-Based Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search in High Dimensions

author.name: Matthew J Liu, Wei Hang Zheng, Vidhan Purohit, Siqi Xie, Chieh-En Li, Jerry Li, Noah Flynn

entry.summary: Systematically characterizes a multiprobe grid algorithm for approximate nearest neighbor search across dataset size and dimensionality, revealing a previously unreported dimensional-scaling crossover on GloVe embeddings where grid search maintains stable scaling while graph, tree, and partitioning methods degrade. It also has lower indexing cost.

entry.title: DroneFINE: Domain-Aware Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning of Vision-Language Detectors for Drone Images

author.name: Ke Wu, Yanan Zhang, Yingjie Gao, Wenhao Li, Chenyu Zhou, XinZhu Ma, Jiaxin Chen, Di Huang

entry.summary: DroneFINE is a domain-aware parameter-efficient fine-tuning paradigm adapting vision-language detectors to drone imagery, addressing the mismatch between natural-scene VLM priors and aerial bird's-eye, small-object characteristics. It introduces two complementary domain-aware modules tailored for the aerial domain.

entry.title: NoPA: Non-Parametric Online 3D Scene Graph Generation

author.name: Qi Xun Yeo, Seungjun Lee, Yan Li, Gim Hee Lee

entry.summary: HieDG is a hierarchical discrete geometry-guided framework for multi-animal tracking that reformulates unstable continuous geometric embeddings into discrete representations to stabilize cross-frame attention and identity association. It addresses uniform appearance, high density, and irregular motion in wildlife monitoring.

entry.title: MG-SpaIR: Multi-grade Sparse-guided Implicit Representation for Training-Data-Free Image Restoration

author.name: Jianmin Liao, Lei Huang, Ronglong Fang, Ashley Prater-Bennette, Lixin Shen, Yuesheng Xu

entry.summary: MG-SpaIR is a training-data-free image restoration framework using implicit neural representations with a multi-grade coarse-to-fine residual hierarchy and sparse proximal regularization. It handles mixtures of blur, downsampling, noise, and missing pixels from a single observation.

entry.title: MVDGC: Joint 3D and 2D Multi-view Pedestrian Detection via Dual Geometric Constraints

author.name: Thinh Phan, Hao Vo, Khoa Vo, Thanh Ngo, Cuong Pham, Ngan Le

entry.summary: MVDGC performs multi-view pedestrian detection using dual geometric constraints that jointly leverage 3D BEV localization and 2D image bounding boxes to counter perspective-transformation distortion. It exploits the mutual relationship between BEV ground points and image boxes for robust occlusion reasoning.

entry.title: Personalized Object Identification and Localization via In-Context Inference with Vision-Language Models

author.name: Kensuke Nakamura, Byung-Woo Hong

entry.summary: This work formulates personalized object identification and localization (POIL), extending prior personalized localization to reject query images that lack the target object, using in-context inference with VLMs. It removes the unrealistic assumption that the target is always present.

entry.title: Diffusion-Based Multi-Class Normality for OOD Detection: An Application to CDP Authentication

author.name: Bolutife Atoki (imagine, LIRIS), Iuliia Tkachenko (imagine, LIRIS), Bertrand Kerautret (imagine, LIRIS), Carlos Crispim-Junior (imagine, LIRIS)

entry.summary: This work proposes a diffusion-based multi-class normality framework for out-of-distribution detection applied to copy detection pattern authentication, training a single class-conditional ControlNet only on authentic patterns across printing-and-digitisation classes. Counterfeits are detected via reconstruction error under authentic-class conditioning.

entry.title: ExPerT: Personalizing LLM Responses to Users' Domain Expertise via Query-Wise Semantic and Keystroke Behavioral Cues

author.name: Yeji Park, Jiwon Tark, Taesik Gong

entry.summary: Presents ExPerT, a query-wise personalization framework that infers a user's domain expertise from query text plus keystroke dynamics and conditions response detail, terminology, and conceptual complexity accordingly. A user study with 40 participants and 1270 queries supports its effectiveness.

entry.title: Structuring the Space of Sociotechnical Alignment

author.name: Esra D\"onmez, Agnieszka Falenska

entry.summary: Argues that sociotechnical alignment is inherently normative and that NLP research lacks a systematic way to specify what socially desirable AI behavior means. It introduces a human-centered framework drawing on social-scientific accounts to define, justify, and evaluate behavioral desirability.

entry.title: Trust the Prior (or Not): Uncertainty-Aware Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Segmentation

author.name: Erich Robbi, Daniele Ravanelli, Andrea Passerini

entry.summary: This work proposes an uncertainty-aware, patient-specific framework for abdominal aortic aneurysm thrombus segmentation, combining GMM-based intensity normalization with an uncertainty-gated anatomical attention module. It aims to improve multi-center generalization under CTA protocol domain shifts.

entry.title: Semantic-Guided Reading Order Reconstruction in Historical Armenian Newspapers with LLMs

author.name: Chahan Vidal-Gor\`ene (CJM, LIPN), Nadi Tomeh (LIPN), Victoria Khurshudyan (Inalco, SeDyL)

entry.summary: This paper reconstructs reading order in historical Armenian newspapers using a hybrid method that combines semantic zone detection with a generative LLM, releasing a new annotated dataset of 66 pages. The hybrid approach reduces ordering errors by up to 76% over the strongest geometric baseline and is designed as a data bootstrapping strategy for under-resourced scenarios.

entry.title: Text-Driven 3D Indoor Scene Synthesis in Non-Manhattan Environments

author.name: Xianhui Meng, Zirui Song, Yuchen Zhang, Li Zhang, Yongxuan Lv, Xiuying Chen, Kun Wang, Yan Luo, Kai Chen, Hangjun Ye, Long Chen, Jun Liu, Xiaoshuai Hao

entry.summary: Introduces SPG-Layout, a text-driven framework for generating physically plausible indoor 3D scenes in non-Manhattan (non-orthogonal) environments, using statistical priors and a human-inspired design workflow. It targets the failure of prior LLM-based methods to model non-orthogonal spatial relationships.

entry.title: Leveraging Phase Information to Boost Unrolled Network Learning for Image Deblurring

author.name: Samira Malek, Haichuan Zhang, Chul Lee, Vishal Monga

entry.summary: UPADNet is an unrolled network for image deblurring that decomposes restoration into amplitude and phase components, using LMMSE estimators and learned matrix parameters. It emphasizes accurate phase estimation for recovering sharp details.

entry.title: MedCAGD: Context-Aware Gated Decoder for Efficient Medical Image Segmentation

author.name: Saad Wazir, Patrick Dominique Vibild, Dinh Phu Tran, Seongah Kim, Daeyoung Kim

entry.summary: MedCAGD proposes a context-aware gated decoder for medical image segmentation that regulates feature fusion and contextual aggregation from a decoder-centric perspective. It targets cross-scale alignment, contextual integration, and boundary preservation under low contrast and scale variability.

entry.title: TokenScope: Token-Level Explainability and Interpretability for Code-Oriented Tasks in Large Language Models

author.name: Amirreza Esmaeili, Fatemeh Fard

entry.summary: Presents TokenScope, an interactive interpretability tool for decoder LLMs that exposes token-level metrics, attention patterns, and structural information during code generation, supporting token replacement, counterfactual branching, and AST-aware aggregation. It targets the lack of decoding-time signals in existing analysis tools.

entry.title: SPARCLE: SPeaker-aware Aligned Representations via Contrastive Language Embeddings

author.name: Priyam Mazumdar, Yurii Halychanskyi, Steven Guo, Mark Hasegawa-Johnson, Volodymyr Kindratenko

entry.summary: Proposes SPARCLE, a speaker-aware grapheme representation model trained with a contrastive objective to align characters with Wav2Vec2 acoustic representations conditioned on speaker identity. It aims to combine the scaling benefits of grapheme-based speech synthesis with speaker-specific acoustic detail, especially in low-resource settings.

entry.title: Mapping Text to Multiplex Graph: Prompt Compression as L\'evy Walk-Guided Graph Pruning

author.name: Yaxin Gao, Yao Lu, Jinhong Deng, Jiaqi Nie, Zhe Tang, Jian Zhang, Zhaowei Zhu, Shanqing Yu, Qi Xuan, Joey Tianyi Zhou

entry.summary: Proposes RAGP, which reformulates prompt compression as redundancy-aware graph pruning on a multiplex graph capturing both fine-grained attention dependencies and coarse semantic relations, using a Levy-walk-guided pruning strategy. It aims to capture the distributed nature of important information that flat-token compression methods miss.

entry.title: Adaptive Companionship for Group-Following Robots: Handling Dynamically Changing Group Formations

author.name: Cong-Thanh Vu, Yen-Chen Liu

entry.summary: Proposes an adaptive group-accompaniment method for social robots using vision-language models to infer companion positions and social distances amid dynamically changing human group formations, combined with a model-predictive path integral controller. It targets the challenge that real human groups do not hold fixed formations.

entry.title: A Synthetic-Driven Vision System for Assembly Step Recognition

author.name: Hui Zhang, Xuanang Lei, Rui Wang, Julian Ferchow, Mirko Meboldt

entry.summary: This work presents a synthetic-data-driven vision system for industrial assembly step recognition that auto-generates realistic assembly sequences from CAD models to train real-time inspection models. It can be deployed to a new task within an hour using only CAD models and step descriptions.

entry.title: Radial Interaction Tomography: Recognizing Non-Transitive Evolutionary Games from One Range-Expansion Image

author.name: Faruk Alpay, Baris Basaran

entry.summary: This paper formulates a computer-vision inverse problem to recognize non-transitive evolutionary games from a single microbial range-expansion image, recovering radius-indexed pairwise boundary-flow fields to test compatibility with a transitive fitness hierarchy. It provides theoretical guarantees on observability, stability, and cyclicity testing.

entry.title: Benchmarking Federated Learning and Knowledge Distillation for Point Cloud Classification

author.name: Aizierjiang Aiersilan

entry.summary: Provides a large multi-seed benchmark jointly evaluating 13 federated learning algorithms and 10 knowledge distillation objectives (a 130-pair cross-product, 504 runs) for privacy-preserving 3D point cloud classification. It finds FL degrades sharply under extreme non-IID skew and studies distillation as a remedy.

entry.title: CPG-PAD: Concept-Informed Prompts Guided Presentation Attack Detection

author.name: Haoyuan Zhang, Xiangyu Zhu, Li Gao, Ajian Liu, Siran Peng, Zhen Lei

entry.summary: Introduces CPG-PAD, which uses concept-informed prompts to guide vision-language-model-based presentation attack detection, aligning learned prompts with fine-grained attack-relevant visual semantics rather than overfitting domain artifacts. It aims to improve cross-domain generalization for face anti-spoofing.

entry.title: DroneIQA-VLE: Multi-Task Drone Image Quality Assessment via Vision-Language Ensemble

author.name: Wei Sun, Weixia Zhang, Hongjian Zhan, Mingkai Lu, Yixuan Gao, Guangtao Zhai

entry.summary: DroneIQA-VLE is a competition solution for target-aware drone image quality assessment that ensembles SigLIP2 vision encoders with a LoRA-adapted multimodal LLM to jointly predict global, target, and background quality scores. It achieved 2nd place in the ICME 2026 Drone-IQA challenge.

entry.title: Typography-Based Monocular Distance Estimation for Advanced Driver-Assistance Systems

author.name: Manognya Lokesh Reddy, Zheng Liu

entry.summary: This paper proposes monocular distance estimation for driver-assistance systems using the fixed regulatory size of rear license plate characters as a known-scale reference. The character height in the image directly measures distance given camera geometry, avoiding radar or stereo hardware.

entry.title: Domain Knowledge Based Temporal-Spatial Graph Convolution Network for ECG Recognition

author.name: Wenting Ma, Zhipeng Zhang, Xiaohang Yuan, Ningwei Xie, Yuxin Xie, Xiaolin Wang, Meng Guo, Xingang Chai, Zhenjie Yao

entry.summary: Introduces a domain-knowledge-based graph convolution network for ECG recognition that incorporates PRQST landmark points and uses double-stream directed graphs to model intra- and inter-cycle relationships. It aims to improve interpretability over end-to-end CNNs in healthcare.

entry.title: Learning When to Listen: Gated Affect Fusion for Human Motion Prediction

author.name: Jingni Huang

entry.summary: This single-author study investigates affect-conditioned human motion forecasting in unconstrained videos, combining MediaPipe pose trajectories with HSEmotion facial affect and a Gated Affect Transformer to regulate cross-modal flow. It examines the utility and temporal limits of facial affect cues for motion prediction.

entry.title: LLMs as Teaching Assistants for Mathematics Exam Grading: Reliability, and Practical Usability

author.name: Aastha Sapkota, M. G. Sarwar Murshed

entry.summary: Evaluates six frontier LLM configurations (Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude variants) as grading assistants for an undergraduate discrete mathematics exam, comparing a strict rubric-following policy against a more lenient one for reliability and feedback quality. It examines partial-credit consistency and misconception-repair feedback.

entry.title: Black-Box Inference of LLM Architectural Properties with Restrictive API Access

author.name: Christopher Ellis, Shreyas Chaudhari, Mei-Yu Wang, Leighton Barnes, Giulia Fanti, Jos\'e M. F. Moura

entry.summary: Surveys and extends generative AI and federated learning approaches for intrusion detection systems, covering synthetic traffic generation, data augmentation, imputation, and privacy-preserving distributed training. It positions these techniques against challenges like evolving attacks, class imbalance, and privacy constraints.

entry.title: Automated grading of Linux/bash examinations using large language models: a four-level cognitive taxonomy approach

author.name: Manuel Alonso-Carracedo, Ruben Fernandez-Boullon, Pedro Celard, Francisco J. Rodriguez-Martinez, Lorena Otero-Cerdeira

entry.summary: Evaluates whether four frontier LLMs can grade short Linux/bash command exam responses using a four-level cognitive taxonomy that combines cognitive complexity and operational impact. It examines how well models approximate expert partial-credit judgment across different task difficulty levels.

entry.title: A Practice Auditing Framework for Large Language Model Use: Collective Empiricism, Pseudo-Rational Cognition, and Governance of AI-Generated Content

author.name: Yang Zhao, Yingshuo Li, Zeyu Zhang

entry.summary: Proposes a practice-auditing framework for LLM use, introducing concepts of collective empiricism (how LLMs compress human experience into seemingly rational outputs) and pseudo-rational cognition (users mistaking AI structure for their own understanding). It analyzes governance of AI-generated content and the illusion of AI subjectivity.

entry.title: How Indian Dermatologists are Utilizing Artificial Intelligence for Clinical Practice and Workflow Management: A Nationwide Survey with a Special Focus on atopic dermatitis

author.name: Dipayan Sengupta, Saumya Panda, Sandipan Dhar, Dipankar De, Deepika Pandhi, Narayanan B

entry.summary: Reports a nationwide survey of 377 Indian dermatologists mapping routine clinical challenges and current AI use, with special focus on atopic dermatitis workflows and adoption barriers. It highlights that chronic-disease workflows receive less AI attention than image-based diagnosis.

entry.title: Beyond Detection: Redesigning Assessment and Governande of Generative AI at the Universidad Polit\'ecnica de Madrid (UPM)

author.name: Jessica D\'iaz, Sonia Linio, Fernando Pescador, Daniel Martin-Fabiani

entry.summary: Describes a university's approach to redesigning assessment and governance of generative AI, arguing that detection-focused, defensive policies are a dead end and advocating deeper adoption with redesigned assessment. It positions itself against integrity-and-plagiarism framings in favor of pedagogical redesign.

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence-Enabled Accounting Information Systems and Fraud Detection in Nigeria's Financial Services Sector: The Moderating Role of Natural Language Processing

author.name: Timothy Oluwapelumi Adeyemi, Abigail Omotola Ojogbede

entry.summary: Examines how AI-enabled accounting information systems influence fraud detection in Nigeria's financial services sector, testing the moderating role of natural language processing. It uses regression-style analysis on institutional data.

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.01366v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated learning (FL) research often depends on many small but consequential algorithmic choices: optimizer variants, server aggregation rules, local...

author.name: Aryuemaan Kumar Chowdhury, Afreen Shaik, Yaparla Bhargavi, Brahma Kumar

author.name: Qian Chen, Chengyuan Liu, Xin Yu

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.01426v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous customer-service agents are shifting from conversational interfaces toward operational execution roles: they retrieve firm records, apply se...

author.name: Samuel Schapiro, Core Francisco Park, Felix Sosa, Lav R. Varshney

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.01433v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Divergent thinking is a crucial aspect of creativity, yet large language models (LLMs) tend to consistently generate similar responses to open-ended qu...

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.01465v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are trained to predict the next token, not to act inside a specific API. In niche enterprise SaaS workflows -- where success mean...

author.name: Natalie Grace Brigham, Eugene Bagdasarian, Tadayoshi Kohno, Franziska Roesner

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.01510v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents that autonomously execute tool calls on a user's behalf raise pressing questions about permission management: what role could users play, and...

author.name: Hongyang He, Jiuming Liu, Victor Sanchez

author.name: Keita Kinjo, Takeshi Ebina

author.name: Xuqing Yang, Yi Yuan, Shanzhe Lei, Xuhong Wang

author.name: Yuante Li, Yicheng Tao, Kate Zhang, Taozhi Wang, Gefei Gu, Yaxin Zhou

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.01661v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems are increasingly used for forecasting future events, as deliberation among multiple LLMs is believed to improve reasoning and calib...

author.name: Yufan Lu, Xinhui Liu, Chenyang Xu, Yuxi Zhou, Hao Wang, Shenda Hong

author.name: Joshua Penman

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.01729v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep learning models for speech classification are vulnerable to backdoor attacks, where malicious triggers cause misclassification at inference time. ...

author.name: Simon Guilloud, Sankalp Gambhir, Samuel Chassot

entry.title: Meta-Benchmarks for Financial-Services LLM Evaluation

author.name: Blair Hudson

entry.summary: arXiv:2607.01740v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Public LLM leaderboards optimise for global average performance and do not capture the specific cognitive demands of financial-services work: a model t...
